description:
The exciting card game for young and old. Find out which values are hidden under your cards and cleverly secure the best supermallows to win. So fire away and don't let anything burn! Fun guaranteed!

Each player has six face-down cards in front of them, of which they only know two. The player whose turn it is draws a card, plays it as an action or swaps it with one of their face-down cards. The player with the fewest points at the end of the game wins the round. This is repeated for 6 rounds. The player with the fewest points is the overall winner.

boardgame: 大宋百商图：出招吧，少东家！(A Dream of Splendor in Song Dynasty: Make Your Move, Young Master!)
description:
出招吧，少东家！(Make Your Move, Young Master!) is an expansion of A Dream of Splendor in Song Dynasty.

It primarily adds elements like loans, auctions, and business tactics. This expansion introduces a character persona, making the game more strategic and refined.

boardgame: 西游斗妖行：飞龙在天 (Journey to the West: Dragon Soars)
description:
西游斗妖行：飞龙在天 (Journey to the West: Dragon Soars) is an expansion for 西游斗妖行 (Journey to the West).
It includes a new character, White Dragon Horse, and a new chapter, Eliminate the Giant Python on Seven Wonders Mountain.

boardgame: 西游斗妖行：妖魔当道 (Journey to the West: Demons Reign)
description:
西游斗妖行：妖魔当道 (Journey to the West: Demons Reign) is an expansion for 西游斗妖行 (Journey to the West).

It includes two new chapters:

     Subdue the White Rat in the Bottomless Cave
     Secret Passage through Lion Camel Ridge

Batalla Elemental (“Elemental Battle”) is a collectible trading card game based on the Reaper Chronicles universe, fusing strategic elements of Magic: The Gathering with Pokémon and Legends of Runeterra style character evolution. It is a physical card game based on tactical strategy to reduce the opponent's life points to zero using summoning units and algorithmic magic cards linking with an APP to complement the game.

—description from the designer
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
"Senpai, have you heard of the urban legend called 'Lost Child'...?

When your hand only has the cards 'Lost Child' and 'Darkness,' you will be taken away by the lost child...
Can you make it safely home, avoiding encounters with the lost child in places where there is no light...?

A horror game of an unusual Old Maid, where the fear lies in the 'Lost Child.'"

Welcome to the pitch! We are proud to bring you Inside the Net Soccer. The game provides a great feel of the sport and captures soccer’s end-to-end flow. Each timed action (using Fast Action Cards) will compare an offensive and defensive player based upon the zone the ball is in. The offensive player will try to pass or dribble, and the defender will have the opportunity to make a play. The more skilled your offensive or defensive player, the more likely you are to succeed.

Speaking of dribbling and passing, each player is rated for these and other categories. In addition, the game captures those players that can make deep passes and even connect with a ‘through ball’ that could provide an open shot opportunity for a teammate. The players have unique shooting frequency, accuracy, and scoring percentage traits. They are even rated for their ability to create corner kicks and to generate goal scoring opportunities from those kicks.

On defense, players are rated for their ability to tackle, defend passes, block shots and of course, commit fouls. Fouls can become yellow cards or red cards or generate a penalty kick for the opponent. Even those players who have the misfortune of scoring ‘own goals’, will have that same chance with this game.

Goalies are rated for the ability to make saves, cough up rebounds, intercept crosses, and deal with Penalty Kicks. They also are rated for the ability to distribute the ball and can help the defense by using their ‘sweeper keeper’ abilities to deal with balls put over the top of the defenders.

One of the key factors is how your strategy can dictate the outcomes. Starting with the formation you play. The game is broken down into three zones. Defensive Zone, Midfield, and Offensive Zone. If you play a 4-4-2 formation, where you have 4 ‘defenders’, 4 ‘midfielders’, and 2 ‘forwards’, this places 4 players in the defensive zone, 4 in the midfield, and 2 in the offensive zone. Naturally that doesn’t mean a forward would never come in to play in the defensive zone. It just means that they would feature less in the defensive zone than a defender or even a midfielder. Consequently, a defender may appear in the offensive zone, but that will occur less likely than it would for a forward.

What this means is that as you play a game, you must decide based on the score, what type of formation you want to use. In addition, if you are protecting a lead late in the game, you may substitute an offensive minded player for one with better defensive skills. If you need a goal, you make go with ‘three in the back’ and put on an extra striker or another offensive minded mid fielder.

Just like the real manager, you may deal with fatigue and injuries, which are optional. And how to handle the player who picked up an early yellow card. Since he will have to play conservatively to keep from picking up a second yellow, you might want to consider bringing in a replacement.

Do you love amazing buildings? This little game is full of well-known and also more recent architectural wonders. Each card has a picture of a famous building, a question about that building and a short fact about it's fascinating history. No-one needs to wait for their turn as everyone is answering the questions at the same time. This handy-sized game contains 50 cards and fits both a suitcase and a game night!

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Of the World (Tactic small square box)

description:
Roll up your sleeves and tie up your apron strings! MasterChef challenges all skilled and, well, not-so-skilled home cooks
to a delicious Indian food fight! Players collect ingredient cards from the fun mini-games and use them to assemble their own culinary masterpiece at the end of the game. Creativity is sometimes necessary however, as all flavor combinations may not be too convincing at first glance!

In Telebomb, or 穿越拆弹 (Time-Travel Bomb Defusal), players are thrust into a tense scenario where they either play as couriers in a bustling mall or as a disguised bomber. After a catastrophic explosion occurs, a scientist equips the players with time-travel devices, giving them the chance to go back in time to prevent the disaster.

Gameplay

Players are divided into two roles: couriers who must travel back in time to find and defuse the bomb, and the bomber who also travels back to stop them and potentially plant new bombs. The game plays out over a series of time points, represented by cards, where players interact with the environment by searching for tools, manipulating time, and outmaneuvering their opponents.

Objective


     Couriers win if they successfully defuse the bomb by the end of the day. 
     The bomber wins if the bomb detonates or if they can outlast the couriers.
publishers: 星空桌游 Starry Sky Games

description:
The American soldiers of the Vietnam War, often young, diverse, and inexperienced in war, were thrust into a complex and divisive conflict that tested their resilience and commitment. Issued with new, and sometimes even experimental weapons and equipment, these soldiers often had to think on their feet to help protect both their friends, squadmates, and their own lives.  These young troops faced a challenging environment marked by guerrilla warfare, ambushes, harsh and unforgiving  jungle terrain, and an elusive, deadly enemy. US Soldiers exhibited remarkable bravery in the face of danger, carrying out missions of all different types.
-description from kickstarter campaign

This Warfighter Vietnam Expansion adds 56 Cards to your arsenal!

This Expansion gives you new Soldiers, Weapons, Equipment, Skills, Missions, Locations and Objectives!
-description from the back of the box

8 Non Player Soldiers
7 Squad Soldiers
3 Weapons
9 Equipment 
5 Skills
4 Missions (Vietnam)
4 Objectives (Vietnam)
6 Locations (Vietnam)

boardgame: Warfighter: Vietnam Expansion #4 – VC and NVA Hostiles
description:
The North Vietnamese Army (NVA) and the Viet Cong, communist forces aligned with the Democratic Republic of Vietnam, played crucial roles in the Vietnam War. The NVA, consisting of well-trained regular troops, operated primarily in North Vietnam and infiltrated the South through the Ho Chi Minh Trail, engaging in conventional warfare during major offensives. The Viet Cong, on the other hand, were guerrilla fighters and local insurgents embedded within South Vietnamese society, employing hit-and-run tactics and underground networks to challenge U.S. and South Vietnamese forces.
-description from kickstarter campaign

This Warfighter Vietnam Expansion adds 56 Cards to your arsenal!

This Expansion gives you new Elite and Frontline Hostiles as well as new Events and Weapons.

boardgame: The Shackleton Series: Interactive Puzzle Adventure
description:
Solve an epic adventure.

The Shackleton Series is a 6-episode (1-2 hours/episode) puzzle game that blends escape rooms and board games with historical fiction. The storyline follows the true account of how 28 men got trapped in Antarctica...for 2 years.

As the player, you will get transported to 1914 where you join Ernest Shackleton's historic crew. This voyage is often described as one of the greatest survival stories of all time. Will you survive the harsh landscape of Antarctica in this 6-episode interactive puzzle adventure?

—description from the publisher

Embark on a daring journey into the shadows of the Vietnam War with 'Warfighter Vietnam: MACV-SOG.  Take control of Special forces Operators, and use authentic MACV-SOG weapons and gear in this immersive expansion.  Players will enter the covert world of the Military Assistance Command, Vietnam - Studies and Observations Group (MACV-SOG), where strategic thinking, precise execution, and resilience are paramount. As you navigate treacherous jungles and fight through complex missions, you'll take out enemy supply lines, capture and destroy NVA strongholds, and assassinate key enemy leaders.  Inspired by real-life operations, this expansion captures the essence of MACV-SOG's contributions to the Vietnam war, including search and destroy, deep strikes behind enemy lines, sabotage, and other critical special forces objectives.
-description from kickstarter campaign

The Tunnel Rats of Vietnam were a specialized group of soldiers who courageously navigated and cleared the underground tunnel networks used by the Viet Cong and North Vietnamese forces during the Vietnam War. Armed with their wits, and a host of smaller weapons, they faced constant dangers such as booby traps and ambushes. Their mission was vital in disrupting enemy operations, extracting intelligence, and saving lives. Operating from the mid-1960s to the early 1970s, the Tunnel Rats demonstrated exceptional courage, adaptability, and mental resilience as they confronted the physical and psychological challenges of tunnel warfare.
—description from the publisher

The Battle of Khe Sanh, a critical episode during the Vietnam War, unfolded from January to April 1968 as U.S. Marines and allied forces defended the Khe Sanh Combat Base against a prolonged siege by North Vietnamese troops. Situated near the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ), the base became a focal point of intense conflict, marked by heavy artillery bombardments, ground assaults, and aerial engagements. The siege, reminiscent of Dien Bien Phu, raised concerns of a repeat scenario and strained American resources.

Employing innovative tactics and utilizing the mobility of helicopters, U.S. troops managed to repel North Vietnamese advances and inflict significant casualties.

description:
Lead your squad into the Vietnamese heart of darkness on a top secret kill mission to take down an Army Colonel that has gone rogue.

This is the first of our "Campaign Packs". In it, you'll find Soldier cards of levels 1 to 6 (like PMC), Campaign specific Hostiles, Missions, Locations, Objectives, and a Campaign Map!

A Campaign Pack is roughly a dozen missions linked to a central story. You start with a team of inexperienced soldiers, and with each mission, you gain experience and gear. The missions become more difficult as you fight your way through the campaign. At the end of the campaign, you'll face the ultimate encounter!

This Campaign Pack uses the Campaign system found in the latest Warfighter Universal Rulebook (2023).

Campaign Pack includes: 3 decks of cards, 1 counter sheet, and 1 map sheet
—description from the publisher

Campaign Map, Soldiers Level 1 to 6, New Gear and Skills, New Ranks of Hostiles!

Be the fastest player to catch the stick indicated by the two cards in play. Every round you win you get a card, so get more cards than anyone else to win! This game develops the areas of: Concentration / Attention / Psychomotor skills.

—description from the publisher

Be the fastest player by getting the stick that matches all the cards or the one that doesn't appear on any of the cards. For every round you win, you get a card - get more cards than anyone else to win! This game develops the areas of: Concentration / Attention / Psychomotor skills.

—description from the publisher

Command & Cohesion World War II is the first historical title by Echidna Games.

Designed for two players, Command & Cohesion pits squads of 5-15 soldiers against each other on the battlegrounds of the 1940s.

Pick from one of five warring nations – or play as brave resistance operatives – and customise your squad to take on your opponent in one of four exciting scenarios.  And use figures from any manufacturer to bring those games to life!

Command & Cohesion is easy to learn and fast to play but also rewards good tactical decisions, making it perfect for beginners and tabletop veterans alike.

So grab your miniatures and a handful of ten-sided dice and dive in to Command & Cohesion World War II.

-description from designer

Join forces to annihilate the monsters to regain the fragment of the mystic crystals called "IGNITOR", and use its power to strengthen your weapons!

This is a cooperative bag-building board game. By defeating monsters, players gain reward chips to strengthen their weapons.

The unique effects of the weapons and skills, the power of crystals, and cooperation with allies will help you to survive.

Ultimately, if all the monsters are defeated while all the players are still alive, you all win.

—description from the designer

The school of herrings always swims happily in a very special formation in the same direction and arranged by color. This way they form a large unit and protect themselves from attackers. However, due to some special predatory fish, the herrings have inadvertently changed the formation slightly out of fear.
Help them and find the mistake(s), being careful not to make a mistake yourself, as this could put you out of the game.
Look closely, but don't get confused and always try to collect three herring friends.

Print & Play version: Papierspiele.at

—description from the publisher

It's the mid-1980s, full of neon lights, synth pop and mullets. In the village disco "Fruchtig", everyone is looking forward to Friday night to dance to awesome music and forget the worries of the week.

But first a fire alarm spoils the exuberant party mood, and then in the early hours of the morning the body of Andi, aka DJ Pommes, is found in the ladies' toilet.

The question that remains is: can you solve the crime together?

Mord bei Tisch is a crimi dinner game in which 4-6 players can participate to find out who among them is the murderer of the found victim. In addition to that, every player gets a personal task to fulfil during the evening, which can include finding certain information via asking, hiding certain information during the game, convince other players to join them in certain acts or act in a very specific way during the game.

While it’s impossible to include everything, we jammed all the Clarenville we could into this box. It’s full of outdoor adventures, family attractions, rich traditions and of course…beautiful harbours- a little something for everyone! Looking for a fun way to experience The Hub of the East Coast? Buy your favourite Clarenville properties, increase your rent by collecting City Blocks and trading them in for Keys to the City. Sound easy? Well, add in some traffic jams, property tax and parking fines and it gets a little more difficult and a lot more fun! So choose your token and advance to GO Clarenville! You may soon be the Mayor…or out of the game for eating too much fish & chips at Rod’s Restaurant!

•Contents: High five, Big Smile, Heart, The Dog, Pretzel and Gym Shoe, Money, Two Dice, 12 Keys to the City, 28 Hotels, 28 Property Cards, 28 Action Cards.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Similar to the Panjango Trumps card game that "explores the world of work", this game depicts fanciful jobs that might arise in the future.

Players are dealt out the deck and plays their top card by choosing from one of six categories that help describe the card.  The highest value wins that "trump" and wins the other players card.
Just like other "top trump" card games you work through the deck and the player with all or the most cards wins.

What separates this game from other similar trump games is that one is expected to discuss the card and this can be a springboard to discuss wider themes regarding the future, what is good for the community and the individual.  This is further explored in the mini games.


    WHATS THE JOB*  One player selects a card and the other players take turns asking a question about what the job is like and then tries to guess what it is.  The first person to guess correctly wins.



    STORY IMPROV* One player selects 10 cards at random and together with the other players takes turns improvising a story that weaves all the future jobs together.



    ROBOT RISK*  Each player takes a turn to debate with the others what the "robot risk" is for each job.  How likely is it that this future job will be automated and taken over by an AI of the future?



    GOOD OR EVIL*  Players have to quickly choose three cards and argue if the job has the potential for the greatest good or evil in society.


The Panjango cards are aimed to help players explore the world of work as well as to open up critical thinking about the environment, society and what work would be best for individuals.

Chess on Hexagons!
The rules work the same as chess, but with more angles!
This take on the global classic features less space, and more opponents!
This means: More action, more trading pieces, and countless new scenarios.
With no unguarded spaces in the opening of the game, you are immediately embroiled in a multi-front battle.
Two ways to win: Both opponents are checkmated or if your king can reach it’s outer orbit!
A new game Piece called the Twin Star can explode in two different ways: ‘Nova’ and ‘Hypernova’.

—description from the designer

It's 9 AM on a sunny summer Sunday. After a fun-filled party last night, everyone is waking up, thinking about the mess of bottles and cans left behind.

But don't worry! You call everyone to the kitchen for breakfast, and together, you'll turn cleaning the house into a fun and exciting game!

You borrowed a Qanqash machine too. It's a machine that you put cans and bottles in it and give money receipts from it.

Game includes:
 * 8 room cards, representing rooms in the house.
 * 40 wooden cubes, representing cans and bottles
 * 5 special action cards
 * 40 action cards
 * and a standard die

The game is played room by room, and each room is played in rounds until all 40 cans in that room are collected.

Each round is played in two phases:
 1. Planing phase; players play simultaneously by arranging 4 action cards
 2. Execution phase: players play in turn executing action cards

Example of action cards:
 1. Find action card: when you play this action card, you roll a die and move that many wooden cubes from box on to the room card
 2. Collect action card: when you play this, you move maximum of 3 wooden cubes into your bag
 3. Hug action card: You hug next player to prevent his/her from playing next action.

There are other action cards such as steal, loot, sneak, ....

Each room also have an effect on the game, for instance when your cleaning pool, hug action cards have no effect, probably because it's slippery in the pool.

The game ends when you clean all 8 rooms in the house. If you're in a hurry you can randomly select rooms to clean at the start of the game.

If you want to win, you have to be the one that collects and qanqashes the most cans among all other players.

-description from designer

A set of simple spaceship miniatures combat rules. The rules proper fit on a single A4 page, but pretty much assume you know the basics of "how this sort of thing works".

There's a miniatures line for it, but it would be trivial to use any other spaceship minis you have lying about.

boardgame: Squidbeard Island: A Treasure Hunting Game
description:
You and your crew have found the location of Squidbeard’s Treasure. The bad news is that it’s on a Kraken Island and Squidbeard and his skeleton crew take “over my dead body” quite literally. Find and open the three treasure chests before the Kraken wakes up!

Squidbeard Island is a family game with a fun twist to the classic memory game, where players flip over tiles to match treasures and keys, while also flipping over tiles to defend themselves against Squidbeard, his crew, and the Kraken itself.
Each flip of the tile becomes an adventure of its own!

Players win if they remove all three treasures and keys from the island, but lose when all three Kraken tiles are removed from play.

-description from designer

It's all just about making those toasties.

In this card game, toss the right fillings on the toast and smash your palm on the right pile to claim that toastie. The first to claim three toasties wins.

-description from designer

boardgame: At the Mesas of Madness: a game of dares and dexterity
description:
How does a boot even pursue an ant? If the boot gives the ant any form of head start.
In the End Times, the Great Old Ones allow puny mortals their cardio exercise in futility.

In this game, players do low bids how many legitimate moves it will take to get a poker-sized card through a particular path. The lowest bidder then attempts to move the card through the path within the limit of the bid.

—description from the designer

In this poker-card sized wargame, players take turns secretly moving through the building's grid, guessing where their opponent currently is, before their opponent guesses where they are first.

The only clues that the players get is the sounds their opponents have to make as they move around the building.

—description from the designer

Tatamgram is a folding puzzle that can be played solitaire or as a speed puzzle against others. 
It consistes of 15 double-sided task cards (30 tasks) that show a shape and one or more colored dots, plus four copies each of origami-like papers in three colors. The papers contain different shapes. The goal is to fold each paper marked by a colored dot on the task card exactly twice and then see the shape shown on the task card. Whoever manages this fastest, gets a point; first player to 3 points wins.

boardgame: Next Station: The Singapore MRT Game
description:
In Next Station The Singapore MRT Game, players are commuters racing against one another to be the first to travel across Singapore's MRT lines! They are given an initial hand of 7 cards comprising of Station cards, Interchange Station cards and Special Pass cards.

Players must play a card matching the previous metro line, or draw from the deck. If they draw 10 cards without playing a card, they skip their turns. But that's not all! The game also has an interesting combo mechanic that can enable a player to quickly clear the cards in their hands.

The game ends when a player is the first to clear their hand, or when the deck is empty, a player has the least number of cards on hand.

-description from publisher

A silly game for adults where you draw something dirty and then change it to be something else.

Each player, in turn order, will secretly take one card from the "Dirty" pile and one from the "not dirty" pile.
Then the player will draw the dirty one and let the other players guess what it is. a point for each correct guess.
part 2: draw on top of the dirty drawing and make it to be the not dirty one. again let the other players guess what it is. a point for each correct guess.

After two rounds count up the points and whoever has the most points wins!

—description from the designer

The Backrooms is an eerie and otherworldly realm that exists just beneath the surface of our own mundane world. This strange and fearsome place can only be accessed through a phenomenon known as no-clipping, where one seems to fall through the world into this bizarre liminal space.

In this solitaire trading card game, you are a Wanderer, one of the unexpected explorers of the initial Lobby Level you've fallen into. You play through a deck of cards called the Pull Pile, which houses all manner of strange creatures, twisting pathways, and special items that can help you survive in this impossible place.

The ultimate goal: Escape. You must go from room to room, finding unfriendly entities to defeat for a chance to flip the Position Token and possibly gain a precious Painite Gem that will allow you to enter the Golden Stairs and escape.

The Wanderer will perform an Action Round to pick up an item and engage with entities both Friendly and Unfriendly, and then perform a Movement Round where they will travel the Backrooms to uncover new rooms, items, and entities. This continues until one of the following occurs: the player runs out of their Deck in Hand which represents their stamina, runs out of Sanity points and goes insane, or gathers the Painite and finds the Golden Stairs to escape for the win.

Mon Repos Print & Play is a strategic game filled with excitement and tough decisions, transporting players to a beautiful yet challenging beach. Throughout the game, you’ll be tasked with guiding sea turtles from their nesting grounds, across the sandy beach, and into the safety of the ocean’s waters. Every move is crucial, and your choices can determine the fate of these magnificent creatures.

Your turtles will face numerous threats along the way—crabs lurking on the shore, predatory birds swooping from above, and sharks awaiting in the water. To win, you’ll need to devise a well-thought-out strategy to navigate these dangers and successfully lead the turtles to the ocean. Each turn brings new challenges, and skillfully using bonuses and special actions can make all the difference. Do you have what it takes to guide the turtles across the perilous beach and ensure their safe passage to the ocean?

Mon Repos Print & Play offers an exhilarating gameplay experience while also highlighting the importance of protecting endangered sea turtles. With its print & play format, you can quickly set up the game and enjoy it anywhere.

This game is currently live on Kickstarter: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/mageekgames/mon-repos-printandplay

—description from the designer

A trick-taking skirmish game set in the fantasy world of Zanziar. You’re an orc chieftain winning competitions where each point scored allows you to choose the best spot for placing the sacred totems. Proving grounds reside between villages and are jointly populated and scored. In the end, only one village will honor the ancestral spirits the best!

In The Proving Ground deck you'll find six suits, all the suits are used regardless of player count. Each card has two values (shown on both ends of the card), representing different suits and ranks. Each suit and rank is thus represented twice in the deck. Players can use either value at any time. Ranks form a hexagon, where each rank has adjacent and opposite ranks. Playmat hexagons have two sides, one for 3 player, one for 4-5 players. All playmats have to use the same side in the game.

The lead player plays a single card at a time. In clockwise order, other players need to play a single card, doing one of the following:
a) Play a card of the led suit of any value.
b) Play a card of one of the two adjacent suits with a value greater than any previously played card. 
c) Pass. You can only pass if you have played something to this trick previously.

So, each time a suit is led, there are three legal suits to play. If a player cannot play any of the three legal suits even once, they must discard a card of their choice and take a Penalty. A game lasts 3 rounds. The closer the end of the game reaches, the more restricting the board states are for the players - forcing them to think which suit of tricks you can try to win and which suits you just would need to give a pass.

The Math Game Set 4-IN-1, also known as 大数学套装 (Ultimate Math Set), is a "big box" collection featuring four redesigned educational math games aimed at children aged 7 and up. Each game focuses on different aspects of mathematical learning, providing both educational and recreational value.

The set includes four distinct games:


     争强斗数 (Expanders): Basic addition and subtraction 
     逻辑九九 (Logic 99): Prime numbers
     速算大师 (Mix Match): Mixed operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division)
     逻辑对决 (TAGIRON): Overall mathematical and logical skills

boardgame: Wayfarers of the South Tigris: Tides of Trade
description:
In Wayfarers of the South Tigris: Tides of Trade, new opportunities for commerce have opened up beyond Mesopotamia. We must document new lands, establish trading posts, and oversee a greater increase in advanced technologies for the work ahead.

Tides of Trade gives players the means to upgrade their workers, recruit traders, and compete for influence over the new red guild. The expansion also includes a flurry of additional cards for each draw pile.

Main features of the expansion:

A board to upgrade your workers in a similar way to upgrading dice
    Adds a 4th (Red) minaret, and therefore a new area control spot and a new way to spend influence
    Trader Tiles and new Overseer Townsfolk which bring powerful new actions into the game
    New cards for each of the various decks

-description from designer
family: Series: The South Tigris Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Scholars of the South Tigris: Body of Books
description:
In Scholars of the South Tigris: Body of Books, the Caliph has called for a greater focus towards the study of medicine. Doctors from near and far bring books and scrolls, wishing to share their latest discoveries. With this recent influx of knowledge and understanding, there seems no better time to start publishing our latest findings across all fields of science.

Body of Books introduces a new black Medicine Research Track, black Dice, and the concept of publishing books. The expansion brings in more of a focus towards engine building and creating interesting combinations of retired translators and published works.

—description from the designer
family: Series: The South Tigris Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Inventors of the South Tigris: Era of Experts
description:
In Inventors of the South Tigris: Era of Experts, great minds from across the known world continue to make the House of Wisdom their home. It is an age of specialists. Brilliant thinkers and masters of design bring with them new inventions to wow the inhabitants of our great city. For those who wish to leave their mark, the time to create their greatest devices yet, has come.

Era of Experts provides 12 unique playable characters to the game. Each character comes with its own special abilities, starting bonuses, and motivations.

-description from designer
family: Series: The South Tigris Trilogy (Garphill Games)

description:
Embark on an epic journey of scientific discovery in The South Tigris Moonsaga. This expansion provides players with the means to play all 3 South Tigris games back-to-back, with special goals and abilities across each game.

Moonsaga adds a solo-only 6-chapter campaign with branching decisions between each game. This campaign requires the expansions, as it will have you first play the base game then add the expansion in the following chapter.

-description from designer
family: Series: The South Tigris Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Cascadia: Rolling Hills – 2024 Big Game Night Promo Environment Sheet Pack
description:
Two new environments for use with the Cascadia: Rolling series of games. Thirty-two double-sided sheets are included.

The first environment, Mima Mounds, plays similarly to the Zumwalt Prarie in Cascadia: Rolling Hills. There are four quadrants of five hexagons (one of each habitat type.) Fill in a hexagon to gain a bonus. Scoring is the total of all values written in the hexagons, with bonus points for completing all four hexagons of habitat type.

The Painted Hills environment is similar to Okanagan in Cascadia: Rolling Hills. The hexagons of each habitat type are arranged in a spiral pattern. When a Habitat Card is completed, outline hexagons equal to the number of the Habitat Card just completed. When a Landmark Icon is earned, cross off the topmost matching Landmark Icon of that habitat type. Bonuses are earned when when hexagons connected by lines are completed. Scoring is based on the number of Landmark Icons earned for each habitat type.

E.L.E Crisis on Asteroid SV1 is a single-player or semi-cooperative game for 1 to 4 players inspired by the classic Sci-Fi B-movies from the 50s/60s. In this game, each player will control 3 S&R Rangers who are on a daring mission to save the Earth from a potential extinction-level event. The semi-co-op play is that while the Rangers are all teammates, they also have friends, family, and/or rivalries with other Rangers and their own objectives to complete, in addition to saving the earth. Each Ranger is very unique and has special actions and abilities, and objectives that make them unique to the other Rangers.

The goal of the game is to find and determine WHY the asteroid is on a collision course with Earth. In this initial free release, the goal is to simply destroy the AI Brain controlling the Advanced Weapons and Research Station. As the AI is the reason the satellite space station is targeting Earth. Doing this while avoiding the death of Enslaved crew and fellow rangers while accomplishing the mission to save Earth AND completing the objectives of the individual Rangers will be the challenge of this grand adventure. Relationships among the Rangers also have a meaningful impact on play, and your Rangers could even have the possibility of even finding true love on this mission!

E.L.E Crisis on Asteroid SV1 is played in turns, first, the events and Robot spawns are completed, and then each Ranger moves starting with the first player and first ranger and proceeding clockwise from there. Then the AI moves according to programmed rules, and combat is resolved simultaneously. Each Ranger gets 2 Actions, 1 Free action per turn, and may be able to use a Surge ability if they have a Surge token. The success of attacks or Skill checks are determined and resolved by rolling dice.

Build your city, sabotage your opponents

Collect the highest score by placing the structure cards in your hand around the town center. 
You can place low-scoring structures in your opponents' cities to lower their score.

After the first round, each player draws cards from the deck at the beginning of the round until they have 4 structure  cards in their hand and chooses to build or sabotage. Each player completes their city by placing a pair of 5  different types of building cards around the town center. The  game ends when all players have completed their city.  Points are tallied. The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 史记：楚汉相争 (Shiji: The Chu–Han Contention)
description:
史记：楚汉相争 (Shiji: The Chu-Han Contention) is a strategic war game set during the historical Chu-Han Contention period in ancient China. The game allows players to reenact the famous power struggle between Liu Bang and Xiang Yu, as well as other significant figures such as Han Xin, in a bid to unify China under one rule.

				
				
					Game Flow1. New Round

Based on the value of the Monarch Card, gain Tiger Tally tokens and replenish your hand.

2. Event Phase


     Flip over Event Cards equal to the number of events for this round and trigger Historical Events.
     Add the Heaven, Earth, and Human aspects on the Event Cards to the Fate Event table and trigger Fate Events.


3 Action Phase
Players take turns executing the Action Phase, which consists of two parts: Movement and Combat.

Movement
As long as the conditions are met, you can perform any of the following actions:

     Play a Hero Card onto the historical stage.
     Play a Strategy Card and execute the card's effects.
     Use the active effects of Hero Cards on the historical stage.
     Discard a hand card and convert it into a Tiger Tally.
     Use Tiger Tally to move armies, launch an assault, or declare combat after moving.


Combat
After the Movement Segment ends, resolve all declared combats.

4. Maintenance Phase

     Units on the road can advance to corresponding city tiles.
     Check if any units on the board exceed supply limits or are surrounded. If so, apply penalties.


5. Recruitment and Recovery

     Check the ownership of each commandery; each commandery provides 1 recruitment point. Controlling key areas can also increase your hand limit.


  For example, occupying the 4 commanderies in the [Jiangdong] region increases the hand limit by +1.

     Spend recruitment points to recruit units onto the board and recover damaged units.


6. End of Round

     Reset all tapped Hero Cards.
     The number of cards you can keep cannot exceed the hand limit.
     You can keep up to 5 Tiger Tally tokens.


				
				
					Victory
     Eliminate the opponent's Monarch piece or achieve other victory conditions to win!

Players gets questions in 4 categories: Universe, Ocean & Water, Air & Sky and Earth. Questions touching fossiles, insects, birds, plants, voyages of discovery,vulcans and planets. Players are going to collect one card from each category. If the player has the card allready and answer correctly he/she can move the gaming piece. Those are been using to get from startet from the stoneage to the spaceage. You have to plan well and get a bit lucky so you don't have to move back in time.

First player to reach the spaceage is the winner.

Happy Birthday To Me is part of the Stop the Killer Hex Dex Series of games themed to the 1981 horror film. To win this solitaire game, you must correctly match the edges of the hexagonal cards before the sixth Kill card appears. Rule variations allow for easier or more difficult gameplay.

The Hex Deck Includes 6 Logo cards, 60 cake cards, and 6 Kill Cards with 6 brand new killer. illustrations by Yannick Bouchard.

—description from the publisher

You've heard of the legend... Stand in front of a mirror, gaze into it deeply, say his name 5 times, and then... CANDYMAN appears in a swarm of buzzing bees to end your life!!

This is an edge-matching solitaire game where you try to assemble a giant beehive before you say Candyman's name 5 times. If you can complete your hive before then, you live. But if you can't... you are Candyman's next victim!

—description from the publisher

This is a high-risk-reward card game where 2-6 players compete for victory in chaos by collecting cards "worth over a thousand" through a battle of wits and courage.

Players will obtain and accumulate "valuable" cards while playing and drawing cards. The abilities of cards will greatly increase the randomness and strategic elements of the game.

Without compromising the fun of the game, the process constantly challenges players to compete with each other until one of them meets the conditions for victory.

這是一款高風險回報卡牌遊戲，2-6名玩家透過鬥智斗勇，收集「價值過千」的卡牌，在混戰中爭奪勝利。

玩家在出牌和抽牌的行動中會獲得和累積「有價值」的卡牌。卡牌的能力會令到遊戲中隨機性和策略成分大增。

在不損遊戲的趣味性時，過程不斷挑戰玩家互相競爭，直到其申一位達到勝利的條件。

boardgame: 投骰写写: 聪明厨房 (Roll and Write: Smart Kitchen)
description:
You are picking from the 5 dice to match the ingredients in your recipe to serve the dinner.
—description from the back of the box (translated)

從5顆骰子中進行選擇，將圖案填到菜譜上以制作料理，完成屬於你自己的聰明廚房吧！
—description from the back of the box

A unique addition to your kid games, Bird Bash lets you test your strategic skills and compete with other players in a friendly and challenging environment. The game is easy to learn and can be played in just 40 minutes, making it ideal for quick gameplay sessions. A fun addition to any classroom games collection, teachers can use this board game to engage students in interactive and educational gameplay, promoting critical thinking, strategic planning and problem-solving skills. Crafted with high-quality materials, this family board game ensures durability and longevity, making it a great investment for years to come. The colorful and engaging artwork adds to the overall appeal, capturing the attention of kids and adults alike. Whether you're a board game enthusiast or new to the world of classic games, Bird Bash is sure to captivate your attention. Designed for 2 to 4 players, the goal is simple: earn points by playing the right action cards to strategically attract birds to your feeder. Collect birds to earn points so you can become the player with the most points at the end and rule the roost. Sure to be added to your list of best games for family game night, Bird Bash offers thrilling entertainment for the whole family!

How to Play: Strategize your moves and earn points by playing action cards to attract birds to your feeder; the player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

—description from the publisher

Your favorite neighbors are back as spy trainees in our new game, SpyOuts! SpyOuts is fun, strategic 10-15 minute card game for 2-4 players.

Your mission? To outsmart your rivals and become the top spy trainee by cracking three of your assigned codes. Complete codes by collecting number cards and arranging them in the precise order shown on your codes.

But watch out! Along the way, you'll encounter special agents with unique abilities that could either help or hinder your mission. Stay sharp and stay fast!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Guerra de Mitos VII: La segunda Gran Guerra
description:
Guerra de Mitos V (La segunda Gran Guerra) is a card game set in a fantastic world where different mythologies coexist. It is a different, dynamic game designed to be able to build different decks with very distinct and varied strategies, and each expansion increases both the number of available mythologies and the game possibilities.

The game contains 150 cards to boost each and every mythology published so far.

Each player needs to build his own deck of at least 51 cards (1 pantheon / 20 gods / 30 designs) of his choice, and each installment increases the diversity of characters, actions and strategies you can use in order to achieve victory.

Each player has to bring into play the cards his oracles offer him and play actions from the designs deck both in the influence phase (where the gods plan from their pantheon) and in the confrontation phase (where the characters who decide to go to battle fight to decrease the opponent’s power) to obtain supremacy or consign the opponent to oblivion.

Standalone set for Heroclix inspired by the movie Iron Man 3. Can be played alone or combined with other HeroClix sets. Contains minis for Iron Man MK 42, Iron Patriot, Killian and Mandarin + character cards + 1 map + rules.

Discover the Ultimate level of entertainment and take on the iconic quiz Trivial Pursuit: House Party in a new, board version! There are 1800 questions waiting for you, divided into 6 categories: Music, Travel, Lifestyle, World Cuisine, Movies and TV series and Who Am I?. To win, you have to demonstrate your knowledge in each of them. So roll the dice, answer the question and collect points, and when you get 6 - you win!

Bob has a peculiar appetite and doesn’t eat everything that’s put in front of him. Can you keep track of all the objects he’s swallowed?
A fun little game that will put your memory to the test!

The players put the tokens on Bob’s table and close the box. The box is shaken... and then opened up again! In the meantime, Bob has swallowed some of the tokens (they have fallen down his throat to the bottom of the box). The players work together to identify which tokens have disappeared.
Aim of the game: Identify all the objects that Bob has swallowed.

Set-up: Empty the box and position the Bob board on top. Place the object tokens to one side.

—description from the publisher

Havoc F1 is a card-driven racing game, where you select cards to accelerate or brake for corners.

Complete the agreed laps first without losing all your racecards and crashing out. 
You get all played cards to hand every time you brake but You only get cards your lost cards back by making a pitstop.

—description from the designer

"Evoloosen," a last-hope simulation world created by the soon-to-be underfunded biotechnologist Dr. Carlwin to validate his accelerated evolution hypothesis, descends into chaos due to his rogue AI, Eidolia.

As brilliant, competitive, yet lighthearted colleagues facing unemployment, you must evolve a species that can survive, prevail, and escape to a safe planet while preserving its genetic information for research.

Gameplay
Evoloosen is a dynamic card game for 3-5 players where you become scientists, manipulating evolution to dominate with unique inclination powers. Collaborate, fight, and sabotage as you compete to adapt and evolve.

—description from the designer

In June of 1798 British forces converged on the United Irish camp on Vinegar Hill to crush the Irish Rebellion. A major battle ensued which the British won though many Irish soldiers escaped. The United Irish were superior in numbers (and perhaps religious and ideological fervor) but the British were better equipped and more of a regular, disciplined force.

The game is won by capturing a majority of the objective hexes and not suffering too many casualties in doing so. Cards govern movement, combat and unique events, and vary game play.

A new entry in the Battle Series by The Historical Game Company, LLC.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mouse Cheese Cat Cucumber: Portrait of a Master Mind
description:
There are 5 major influences in the evil master mind Kaiser Cucumber! Each is depicted in this expansion for folks who want to feel like a super villain.

Adds match play with a single use meta power usable once a match! Also, expands the solo mode to the solo campaign rules

Card driven gameplay for 3-5 players where the aim of the game is to keep as many Pandas in your hand before the end of the game. Cards utilized will trash pandas, given them to another player or even avoid players from playing their turn altogether.

How fast can you remember what 7 x 8 is? It’s tricky, isn’t it? With this game, you will learn the products to multiplication operations that have always made your head spin.

Turn all cards over the table with numbers side up. Everyone plays at the same time. Take one card and flip it. Look at the operation that it shows now. Search for product of operation on your card among cards on the table. Pick the card with correct product and find new operation on the other side. Have the most correct cards and win!

拆弹对决 (Telebomb: Duel) is a 2-player variant of the popular identity deduction game 穿越拆弹 (Telebomb).

Two players take on the roles of a Bomb Disposal Expert and a Bomber, competing in a high-stakes battle of wits. The game is set across various time points, each represented by a different card deck. Players must manage their actions, paying energy to travel through time, search for critical tools, and either defuse bombs or plant them to outmaneuver their opponent.

Victory Condition

     The Bomb Disposal Expert wins by successfully defusing all bombs before time runs out.
     The Bomber wins if they can plant new bombs and prevent the Bomb Disposal Expert from defusing them in time.
publishers: 星空桌游 Starry Sky Games

description:
Get ready to relive the nostalgic snake game, but with a whole new twist! Snake Papel transforms the classic snake experience into an exciting roll and write game, where every dice roll and decision counts. Across 15 unique sheets, you'll enjoy an epic campaign full of challenges that will test your strategy and skill.

Each campaign is a unique adventure that will take you through intricate paths, avoiding obstacles and collecting points on your way to victory. With simple but profound rules, Snake Papel is an incredibly fun and addictive game, perfect for players of all ages.

Whether you're looking to relive the magic of the classic game or discover a new way to enjoy the snake, Snake Papel is the perfect choice. Get out your dice, grab your pencil, and get ready to write your way to glory in this vibrant and creative version of the game we all know and love!

boardgame: Time's Up!: Festival International des Jeux
description:
This edition of Time's Up was available during the 2023 Festival des Jeux de Cannes and its cards focused on board game titles and other concepts related to the world of board games and the Festival des Jeux de Cannes.

Time's Up! is a charades-based party game for teams of two or more players (and is best with teams of two). Before the game begins, each player looks at several cards featuring boardgame concepts and chooses some of them. Each player's cards are shuffled to form a deck, and this deck is used for each of the game's three rounds.

In each round, team members take turns trying to get their teammates to guess as many names as possible in 30 seconds. In round 1, almost any kind of clue is allowed, and the cluegiver cannot pass on a card. In round 2, no more than one word can be used in each clue (but unlimited sounds and gestures are permitted); the cluegiver can pass on any card he likes, and the teammates can give only a single answer. In round 3, the cluegiver can use no words at all and can pass as often as he likes; again, teammates are allowed only a single guess. Give good physical clues in round 1, and they'll pay dividends down the road when you need to keep your mouth shut and gesture like a maniac before time's up!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Pictionary Sketch Squad is a new way to play the classic drawing game! Players work together as one team in this cooperative game. Gameplay is simple, but always hilarious. Players sketch secret hints at the same time and then discuss the drawings with each other to guess the Big Clue that ties them all together. Figuring out the drawings and their connection can get heated and hysterical! Pictionary Sketch Squad is a great party game for any game night with adults and teens!

Introducing Pictionary Sketch Squad, a cooperative drawing party game where teamwork is key!

Includes a specially designed clue case that features windows with sliders so each player can secretly view their hint.
All players draw their secret hints using dry erase sketchpads and markers.
When time is up, players discuss the hint drawings and try to guess the Big Clue that ties them all together.
Big Clues range from objects and places to movies and TV shows!
Solve 6 Big Clues before getting 3 wrong and win as a group!

—description from the publisher

Free expansion for the game ‘Destroy BCN!’ obtained during the 2016 Dau Barcelona Fair.

The expansion has 4 red cubes that replace the green cubes that would be placed in the 4 corners of the board. Each red cube has a value of 3 green cubes during the game.

Players must guess the bookmaker's BIFFETON card.

Deal to each player: 
- 10 BIFFETON cards (from 1 to 10),
- A BLACK CARD
- A PERSONAL card (to be drawn at random and not shown to others).

The bookmaker draws a card FOR HOW MUCH, reads it out loud and places a BIFFETON card face down that matches his bet. With his BIFFETON card, the bookmaker indicates for how much he would be willing to do what the card says.

Each bettor must try to guess this bet by placing their own BIFFETON card face down and can ask the bookmaker a question in order to refine their bet.

Before that, the bookmaker must define a price scale for BIFFETON cards. Everyone then turns over their BIRETON card to compare it to the bookmaker's.

- Same number as the bookmaker = 2 points.
- One figure difference with the bookmaker = 1 point,
- More than two digits difference with the bookmaker = 0 points (nothing!).

You win the game when you reach 10 points

You’re a river fisherman on a boat, and all you have is a net and a paddle. Your goal is to collect sets of fish to earn the most points, while navigating the environment.

River Rush is a card-drafting game with a market that doubles as a map! There are two rows of cards that form "The River" where your pawn moves from card to card. The location of your pawn determines which cards you can draft (1 space away from your pawn). Your pawn also blocks the card it’s on from being drafted by your opponent.

—description from the designer

Conan: Shadows Over Luxur (a crossover with Monolith's "Mythic Battles: Isfet" game) will once again explore the dusky lands of Stygia, where wicked customs and foul sorceries rule over an ancient people. Players will travel lush riverbeds and ancient temples to discover dark secrets in a mysterious land of cruel men and crueler creatures.

This small Kickstarter only expansion will contain: 
• A 20+ page softcover booklet with 3 brand new Adventure Mode scenarios and 3 brand new standalone solo/co-op scenarios* 
• More than 20 Overlord tiles associated with the Mythic Battles: Isfet miniatures  
• 5 Hero Sheets associated with the Mythic Battles: Isfet miniatures 
• 9 new equipment cards exploring the powerful world of alchemy

Publisher Note: * players will require this add-on, a copy of the MB:I game, the MB:I Duat expansion, and Conan base board game to play these scenarios.

Enlist to fight alongside the cunning Shamrock mercenary group to track down and collect on bounties for game changing rewards and bonuses!

REDLINE: Payday adds new mechanics like fight, bounty hunter and rotation to the mix along with new tokens and bounty card types! Build your decks and develop new strategies around powerful new cards in your fight to control an ever changing set of missions. No two games will ever play the same!

Clock in to your shift and battle alongside the Drihl Co Industrial Corp as you mine for valuable ore and use close combat to protect your mining operations!

REDLINE: Payday adds new mechanics like fight, bounty hunter and rotation to the mix along with new tokens and bounty card types! Build your decks and develop new strategies around powerful new cards in your fight to control an ever changing set of missions. No two games will ever play the same!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Once upon a time, horrifying Adventurers raided the Dungeon, where Slime Monsters live. Slime Monsters must defend their territory from the attack.

Calculate and place all of your Slime Monsters! Slime Monsters can be divided and combined


Players will build a Pyramid with Slime Monsters. You can only play a card if you calculate correctly.

On your turn, you must play one of following actions:

1. Slime Monster Card Play: play one or more Slime Monster Cards
- Cards can only be placed in a higher row, whenever two cards are already in place right below.
- You can play a card only when its value is the result of addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division of each value of the cards right below.
- You can play additional cards, when the conditions meet.

2. Divide or Combine: Exchange your card with a card in the middle.
- The card's value must be the result of addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division of each value of the cards right below.
- Also, there must no other cards right above the card, you want to exchange.
- After that, turn over the card you've just exchanged and put it down in front of you.

3. Pass: Turn over one of your cards and get a pass card (only once)
- When you cannot or don't want to play other actions, you can pass.

If you already passed, and cannot choose any other actions, you lose the game.

If you don't have any cards left or if all the other players except you lose the game, you win the game.

-description from publisher

Expansion for Gun and Gun Core Set II: W SHOUT

Adds two new gunners, three new guns, and a solo play mode versus a super-powered version of the character Ran from the original Gun and Gun.

Expansion to Gun and Gun.

Adds two gunners, three guns, and a new overheat mechanic to the game.

As the leader of a medieval town, you face a dire challenge: a terrifying outbreak of pestilence is sweeping through your people.

In this roll-and-write game, you'll navigate two pages filled with four thematic game areas and one town map grid. Strategically fill rows and columns of asymmetric action grids, record imported resources, mark off crates of used goods, and secure the four exotic ingredients needed to create the cure.

Race against time as the plague infects new houses every turn. Slow it down by setting quarantines, and ultimately defeat the pestilence by concocting the cure. But if the sickness spreads too far, death will be inevitable.

To gather the four essential ingredients, you'll need to use your actions wisely and adapt to ever-changing circumstances. Each of the four game areas offers unique bonuses, risks, and possibilities, so careful planning is key.

Three-phase turns:

     Locate the infection coordinates by rolling 4 D6 dice.
     Set a quarantines.
     Use one of the dice results for actions, such as exporting goods through workshops, importing necessities at the harbor, burying the dead in the graveyard, and funding the church's research into the cure.


Act quickly and make flexible decisions. Learn to prioritize resources, defend against outbreaks, and push toward cure research to save your town from the plague.

—description from the publisher

The goal is to earn the most points by defeating your opponents in battle!

Take your cards, build a 4x4 grid with cards on the table.  Attempt to make a match memory style.  If a match of the faction is made, collect on card/pile of cards diagonally adjacent, NOT the match.  Cover the match with 2 new face down cards.  If no match is made, go to the battle phase.

Choose who to battle, lay down one or more cards. Opponent must defend with at least one card, whoever plays the most cards wins.  If tied add up the points on the cards.  
If you win, collect all cards, set aside these are your points.

Play till the deck is empty, most points wins!

Minos is a solo print-and-play maze-solving game of logic and deduction rooted in Greek mythology. Escape Daedalus’ labyrinth by deciphering Ariadne’s clues and uncovering the meaning behind Gods’ symbols you encounter along the way. The maze will soon reveal itself far more intricate than it first appears; only those who heed the whispers within its walls will will find their way out.

Minos guides players through its mechanics and concepts organically, allowing them to get immersed in the maze, experiment with its secrets, and navigate their path to freedom.

—description from the designer
Bonus missions 9-card expansion for Famous Missions.

It’s your team against the clock – how many orders can you fill before time runs out?

Work as a team to play fish cards and complete customer orders.  Hold the electronic flopping fish to place a card, then quickly toss it to another player.

With three levels of difficulty, see if your group can earn the rank of Master Fishmonger or if you’re just a bunch of lousy Landlubbers!

—description from the publisher

Alle mot Alle is a quiz game you've hardly ever seen before. To reach the goal on this demanding quiz board, you have to deal with time pressure and questions that you hardly saw coming. Do you, or your team, have what it takes to be a finalist and winner of ALL AGAINST ALL?

boardgame: Three Kobolds in a Trench Coat
description:
Three Kobolds in a Trench Coat is a bluffing, party game for 2-4 players. After spending too long in the dark, the Kobolds are ready to see what life is like in the village of Crumblebatch. But there's a problem: the villagers aren’t exactly welcoming to monsters.
To blend in, the Kobolds have come up with a cunning plan - to dress up in a trench coat and pretend to be a regular person.

Now, as a “citizen”, players must avoid suspicion.
Each player must keep their Kobolds hidden by completing Quests, collecting Gold, and defeating opponents in Challenges.
Pay the Guards to redirect attention toward others and use Items to protect themselves or expose their rivals’ Kobolds one by one. The last player with hidden Kobolds wins the game.

This is a dexterity + set collection game where you make a big pile of meeples and use a single finger to push specific colors into your play area, trying to create sets without knocking the pile over and making noise.

On their turn, a player player places their finger on a meeple and silently slides it into their play area without knocking any other meeples off the pile or otherwise causing noise. Once the meeple enters their play area, the player places it at the front of the queue on their food stall sheet.

If they disturb the pile and make a noise, the player must send one of the meeples in their queue to the toilet queue, where they may be dropped back into the pool as the game continues.

The game ends once one player has placed their 8th meeple on their food stall sheet. At this point, every player will identify sets of meeples within their queue and add up the points for completed sets. For example, a red meeple + white meeple + green meeple, in that order, makes the 'pizza' set, which is worth 3 points. Players can also earn bonus points for every meeple which matches the color of the meeple at the front of the toilet line.

The player with the most points is the winner!

-description from publisher

Set sail with Swashbuckle, where piracy meets strategy and showmanship. Gather your finest mates and duel using dynamic Action and Treasure cards to claim the richest treasures. Who will get the most Treasure points? Who will find themselves stranded on the Island? Your crew will be clamoring for more! Ready yourselves for an epic journey, take the helm, and Chart Your Fate like never before in this thrilling pirate card game.

In Swashbuckle, you will compete with fellow pirates to be the first to gain five Treasure points. Treasure points are earned by purchasing Treasure cards using Coin cards, while Action cards help you obtain more Coin cards and attack other pirates.

Each pirate’s turn has three phases. The first is the Draw phase, where you will draw either a Coin card or two Action cards. The next phase is the Market phase, where you can cash in your Coin cards if you have enough to purchase Treasure cards. The final phase of each turn is the Action phase, where you can play up to two Action cards. You cannot return to the Market phase on your turn once you begin playing Action cards, unless you have a Ship card that allows it.

Treasure cards gain you points required to win the game and may also grant you powers to boost your Action cards. Treasure card powers may be one-time or enduring for as long as you hold that card. But beware, as fellow pirates might steal your powers to take them for their own...

The final danger that you must navigate throughout the game is the Deserted Island. If you run out of Action Cards, you will be forced to the Island. Once on the Deserted Island, you cannot play cards until you have rolled the dice and earned your way back into the game!

The game ends when one pirate has gained five Treasure points on their turn to become the ultimate Swashbuckler. To compete and win on the high seas, a pirate must combine the flair, bravado, and strategy of a true buccaneer to chart their fate into pirate legend.

-description from designer

The time has finally come for the wild young wolves to go into the village at night, cause mischief, and grab sleeping villagers — but these villagers are also extremely defensive and will plan together how they can track down the young wolves in their hiding places during the day.

Werwölfe Kids is an introductory version of Werewolf that pits two teams of werewolves and villagers against one another. Who is hiding in which house? Where are the dangerous werewolves?

Werwölfe Kids can be played with or without the accompanying app, and no moderator is required to play.

Make 'n' Break: Around the World features the same frantic gameplay of earlier editions of Make 'n' Break, but with players now using blocks to construct the most famous buildings on the globe as quickly as they can.

Players can build co-operatively or play against one another in teams depending on the game variant you choose.

The disappearing drawing game! Draw your picture before it gets wiped out! Try to guess each others doodles based on what is on your Challenge card. You must draw on the Scribble Scramble board before the board disappears. The quicker you guess – the more points for your team. You can choose either a short, medium or long game whereby a short game is the first team to 5 points, medium is 10 points, and long is 15 points.

Bocals to Bocals is a party game for bassoon enthusiasts from the Breaking Winds Bassoon Quartet.

Each player draws 7 yellow cards into their hand.  One person will serve as "judge" and is referred to as "The Maestro".  They will select a random black card with a question.  Each player then passes one yellow card facedown to the Maestro.  They are shuffled and then one is chosen as the best response.  The Maestro role passes to the person on the left.

The game is a bit Cards Against Humanity and Apples to Apples.

boardgame: Aetherial War: Untold Adventures – Stranglehold Expansion
description:
This new expansion is perfect for even more on-the-go solo adventures in the Aetherial War! Contained within this new tin-sized game is the following:

A new faction: the Galactic Imperium
Three new quests
A new species to choose: The science-oriented Praug
Three new classes to mix and match
Eight new items to plunder
Many new achievements to earn!

This expansion requires the Aetherial War: Untold Adventures Game and is compatible with all other expansions in the series!

The object of the game is to collect GARLIC HEADS that represent the real world, and FOLLOWERS that represent the online world. The final score at the end of the game represents the number of resources (garlic heads or followers) the player has collected the least of. This means that in order to win, players have to collect a balanced amount of both resources. Players can also collect coins, which they can use to purchase various items and resources. However, these funds do not contribute to the final score, because, much like in real life, money should be a means to an end, rather than the goal.

In the game players have several options or moves they can perform during their turn. Here's a summary of the key actions available:

Move and Activate Location: Players can move their main piece to a different location and then choose to activate that location. Activating a location allows the player to interact with the specific areas available there, such as exploring the location for resources or shopping for items.

Switch Locations: Players have the option to move their piece to a different location without necessarily activating it.

Upload Video: Players can choose to upload a video by moving one of their pieces to the online zone in the same location. This action is tied to scoring points based on the card drawn and the color of the zone.

Use Smartphone Boost: By using a battery bar from their smartphone card, players can boost a specific resource they receive, effectively doubling its value.

Prank: This move allows a player to choose another player and draw three cards. The prankster can then collect resources based on the drawn cards' symbols from the selected player.

Explore Location: This action involves turning over a card from the draw pile and gaining resources based on the card's color and the active area within the location.

Purchase Resources: Players can use their coins to buy different resources at their current location. The cost and type of resources available vary by location.

Rate Videos: At specific intervals, the game pauses for players to rate videos. This involves turning over cards from the draw pile and rewarding players based on their pieces' positions in online zones.

These actions form the core gameplay, allowing players to gather resources, interact with other players, and ultimately compete for the highest score.

The iFluence Land game is based on the Baron Bite cartoon series, which is available on YouTube.

boardgame: 大宋百商图：过年好！(A Dream of Splendor in Song Dynasty: Happy New Year!)
description:
大宋百商图：过年好！ (A Dream of Splendor in Song Dynasty: Happy New Year!) contains :


     大宋百商图 (A Dream of Splendor in Song Dynasty)
     大宋百商图：出招吧，少东家！(A Dream of Splendor in Song Dynasty: Make Your Move, Young Master!).


The new version is redesigned with a New Year's theme, capturing the festive atmosphere of the Spring Festival.

Description

The Song Dynasty was an era of unprecedented economic prosperity. In this game, players find themselves immersed in the bustling city of Bianjing, witnessing the grandeur of this flourishing period firsthand. As the newly appointed young master of a business located in the busy street markets, you face endless possibilities, but you must also repay a 50 tael silver debt. Your task is to use your wit to rebuild the streets, repay the entire debt, and restore the street to its former glory under your management!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Fast, fun and stylish, Myth Mash is a two-player card game born from a love of trick-taking, deck-building, and card-battling. Bid, bluff, and best your opponent in battle after battle as you cleverly combine the power of your heroes to win new cards and sweet, sweet treasure. Be careful, though; these heroes favor Peace above all else, so if you push too hard they may switch sides and join your opponent!

Each round you'll draw a hand of seven cards from your deck, which you'll blindly bid across three battles. The winner of each battle is rewarded with a card--either a new hero to strengthen your deck, or a shiny treasure putting you one step closer to victory. The first player to collect 15 points of treasure wins!

Mixed amongst these cards you'll also find Mash-Ups: a special third type of card that gives the Peaceful player (that's whoever lost the last battle) an important choice. You'll either trade hands, decks, or discard piles with your opponent. Sound crazy? It is! Don't worry, though; clever players will find creative ways to play around this, even from ahead.

Welcome brave workers!

You have admired and envied them for a long time... But from today you can impersonate your heroes! Choose which of the 8 mythical contemporary Italian workers represents you the most, challenge the others and try to free yourself from all your working hours. Who cares if it's a working hell, job security is the best! Let the massacre begin!

Benvenuti coraggiosi pretendenti! Li avete ammirati e invidiati per tanto tempo.

Ma da oggi potrete impersonare i vostri eroi! Scegli tra 8 miti del lavoro contemporaneo italico quale ti rappresenta di più, sfida gli altri e cerca di liberarti di tutte le ore di lavoro che ti toccherebbero, ma che alla fine, sticazzi ho il posto fisso, fotte sega! Che il massacro cominci!

boardgame: American Pursuit: Dream On! Expansion Pack
description:
Can you fulfill the dream of early retirement, or will you be wiped from history by the secret societies that run America behind the scenes? Dream On! Expansion pack adds even more fun and friendly rivalry to the American Pursuit base game.


     Play with up to 9 players and 4 teams! 
     3 hilarious new characters and 12 dream cards
     With a total of 42 additional cards you supercharge your party experience


—description from the publisher

What was the greatest warrior of the Medieval age?

Prepare to answer that question once and for all.
Choose your favourite warrior, and go head to head with other warriors. Prove to the world that you are the greatest.

Warriors of Legend pits warriors from differing centuries and cultures against each other.

You could be the greatest, only by playing will you prove it!

Historically accurate in terms of arms, armour, and combat manoeuvres, this 1v1 dueller is fast paced, and quick to learn, with a surprising strategic depth.

Iron Future is a two-player game with deckbuilding and dice elements. It takes place in a dystopian near-future where the financial incentives for the military-industrial complex have collapsed, and factions have arisen to seize what resources they can using hardware sold to the highest bidders. The art style evokes 16-bit video games of the 1990s.

The overall goal of the game is to win three Conquest rounds, done by controlling more locations in a given round than their opponent (which usually means controlling two of the three available locations).

During the conquest round players draw a hand of five cards from their personal deck and alternate deploying units from their hands to one of the three locations. Units have between zero and three weapons, and when deployed, may make attacks against deployed enemy units that are in range of those weapons. Enemy units at the same location are considered to be in short range, units in adjacent locations are at medium range, and two locations away is long range. Attacks are made with die rolls, and each roll of 3 or better reduces the armor of the enemy unit. Units with no armor remaining are destroyed and placed in their players discard pile. Units ONLY attack when they are deployed, but continue to hold the location using their Capture value until they themselves are destroyed. When each player has played five cards, the remaining capture value at each location in tallied, and the player with the highest wins that location.

Winning locations is beneficial for two reasons - winning more locations than your opponent wins you the round, and locations provide resources that can be exchanged for better cards. Between each Conquest round players have an opportunity to purchase units to add to their deck, which are placed in the discard pile and shuffled into the deck when it runs out.

In addition to weapon loadouts, units have abilities that impact what effect they have on the game, and support cards can be acquired to provide a small but consistent benefit each round. Infantry units tend to be weak, but provide higher capture values than vehicles. Vehicles have heavy weapons and armor but low capture values, and mechs are strong overall, but tend to cost more to acquire than the other unit types.

—description from the designer

This is a strategic wargame suitable for 2-4 players, based on the Central Plains War (a war between Kuomintang warlords) that broke out in 1930.
In the complete war scenario for 4 players, each player plays one faction in Kuomintang at that time. The four factions are: 

     Central army, led by Chiang Kai-shek；
     New Guangxi Clique, led by Li Zongren;
     Northwest Army, led by Feng Yuxiang；
     Shanxi-Suiyuan Army, led by Yan Xishan.


Cards are used in this game to influence the combat, downgrade the level of disorder and recruit the army.
New Avatars, new ICE and all other Streatch Goals unlocked during the Kickstarter crowfunding campaign.

boardgame: 恐龍家族快樂西餐廳 (Happy Western Restaurant)
description:
This is an educational game for cultivating financial intelligence management. The game comes with different complexities to provide various experiences to children ages 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7+.

You are running a western restaurant. By effectively purchasing food ingredients, completing recipes, and serving customers, you develop a happy western restaurant.

—user summary

是一款財商管理遊戲。遊戲中小朋友扮演一位西餐廳老板，需西好好經營自己的店鋪，購買食物，服務顧客，打造一家快樂西餐廳！

提升孩子的觀察能力。
鍛鍊孩子的計算能力。
培養孩子理財經營能力，財商管理知識學習。
強化孩子的規劃能力和競爭意識。

—description from the back of the box

Our little kittens were so fascinated after the Cat Horror Festival, that they have decided to dress up as their favourite characters for the next Halloween. But you know how they are! They’ve made a mess of everything...

Tile placement game, where the player who scores the most points in their costume combinations wins.

Help them organise the costumes to win!


Nuestros queridos gatitos quedaron tan fascinados tras el Festival de Horror que han decidido disfrazarse de sus personajes favoritos para el próximo Halloween. ¡Pero ya sabéis cómo son! Lo han dejado todo hecho un desastre..

Juego de colocación de losetas, donde gana el jugador que sume más puntos en sus combinaciones de los disfraces.

¡Ayúdales a organizar los disfraces para ganar!
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description: Tactical placement game (inspired by the famous ‘Pont des Trous’ in Tournay, Belgium). Two players or two teams of 2 players try to line up 5 pieces first, horizontally, vertically or diagonally (different types of diagonals are accepted). In certain critical situations (once per round), a player can ask his opponent to roll a die to add a constraint on the placement of his piece. A game is played in 1 to 3 sets of 5 winning rounds.

The Command Protocols book includes new content for existing players, plus the latest rules and updated force lists. And if you’re new to Firefight – start here!

Firefight is a medium-scale skirmish wargame set in the same exciting universe of Warpath. It goes deeper into the action, zooming in and allowing you to decide the fate of your soldiers in smaller conflicts. The game focuses on squad-based action and individual soldiers, where the stakes are desperate and every bullet counts.
Firefight uses the Warpath miniatures range and shares some core mechanics, however the gameplay is more focused on the fate of individual units to ensure you have a totally different sci-fi gaming experience.
This rulebook contains everything you need to know to play Firefight’s strategic skirmishes.

Inside you will find:

     a new take on the Warpath rules, presenting a new challenge with a familiar starting point.
     detail-oriented mechanics to make every soldier count.
     comprehensive terrain rules that tie in with Mantic’s Battlezones scenery range.
     customizable character equipment to make your unit leaders stand out.
