—description from the publisher

Miramix is a strategic game that immerses you in the wonders of the marine world, allowing you to create and manage your own underwater ecosystem. Set in a rich and diverse ocean environment, players must connect environment cards to build a balanced ecosystem in a 4x4 grid and populate it with native species tiles. During the game, each player selects and places environment cards to expand their ecosystem while strategically deciding which species to introduce based on habitat compatibility and available space.

Players face challenges as they balance between populating their ecosystem early with smaller species for guaranteed points or expanding habitats to host larger species with higher rewards. Environmental threats, like pollution and invasive species, add tension and force tough decisions. The game plays over three rounds, with each round consisting of five turns. The player with the most points at the end, considering species diversity and effective management of threats, wins the game.

Miramix is an eco-friendly game, designed with sustainable materials and a strong educational focus on marine conservation, making it both fun and meaningful.

-description from designer

Replica is a two player game played using a chessboard and 12 black and 12 white flippable checkers. Players setup the game by placing the checkers on the board in opposite corners (with a 2x2 square in the corner, flanked by a 2x2 square on each side). The pieces in the very corner start the game flipped over (indicating a king). Players make one move per turn, starting with White.

On each turn, players either step, jump, or transform. All moves (even captures) must go "forward" (1 of the 3 directions towards the opponent corner). For steps and for jumps, if there is already a piece on the destination square, it is captured by replacement. For a step, the piece moves forward one square. For a jump, the piece moves in a straight line forward over friendly pieces until it reaches a square not occupied by a friendly piece. For a transform, a friendly non-king piece in line-of-sight of a friendly king gets flipped (this creates another friendly king). Only enemy pieces block line of sight.

The game is over if a player wins by getting any friendly king into the opposite corner, or wins by capturing any enemy king.

-description from designer

Lay the path of the elements and find the elemental spirits in a head-scratching memory game. Lure them into your path and place them in the right place to gain the most influence and points and become the new leader in the end. Watch out! For every new elemental spirit on your path, an opponent also receives one. And be careful: both you and your fellow players can bribe the spirits - so pay close attention and act wisely to win the game!

This competitive all-age board game combines an exciting story with innovative game mechanics. Who will get the most points and become the new leader?

The world has shattered! Our two-dimensional universe has splintered into dozens of shards, and heroes like us need to knit it back together! As dimension-hopping wizards in a flat world, you will struggle to reunite your mutiverse and score some points along the way, in The Fratured Flat.

The Fractured Flat is a simple area-control board game, in very early development at Crab Fragment Labs. You will need 16 small tokens for each player, and the Fractured Flat game board.

Players take turns placing or moving their tokens on the board. You score points by occupying regions with dots (“cities”), and you also score points if neighboring regions are owned by your opponent. Each game takes 10 to 15 minutes.

—description from the publisher

TWISTED TALES: The story telling party card game that will leave you twisted. Simply put together the best tale with 175 Original Story Cards, detailed comic book style illustration and 50 voting cards.

PARTY GAME: Perfect for Kids, Tweens, and the family! Ideal for Sleepovers and Birthdays gifts!

HOW TO PLAY: Simply deal out 7 Story Cards to each player and 3 Voting Cards. Then take turns telling the most twisted tale prompted by 7 of the Story Cards. Draw replacement cards after each round to experience all of the super silly stories Twisted Tales has to offer. At the end of the predetermined number of round, total up the voting cards for each story, whoever has the most votes wins. Can you hold your title with the most Twisted Tale?

HIGHLY RATED: Recipient of The National Parenting Center’s Seal of Approval, best for players aged 8+.

—description from the publisher

Mattergy is a strategic deck-building card game with direct player interaction and powerful card synergies.

The existence of everything is on the brink. The fall of the first shaper, escalated the fight for succession. Defend your standing as an aspiring shaper and fight the herds of rivaling pretenders.

The main resource in the game is matter, which you will transform into mighty creatures to build your following. All players have access to an updating market of creatures in the middle to build their deck. In your turn you will spend matter to create and play creatures of four unique factions, use their special abilities, combine and attack your opponents. 

Playing intelligent strategies will give you resources to obtain powerful progression cards that will strengthen you the rest of the game.

But be aware – every turn is a battle, and you have to defend yourself from the attacks of the other players! If you can't defend yourself, and you have no energy left, you lose.

To win, you will need to handle your resources wisely and launch devastating attacks to erase your opponents.

Mattergy's distinctive artworks and characters will open up a fantastical new world to explore.

-description from designer

ALZH3IM3R, the game where you...uhm, I forgot!!
The game plays like Memory with funny controversial illustrations, but when you find two identical cards, the browser app will give you another challenge for you to test your brain! If you solve it, you get points, if not, you get zilch!

60 cards featuring advice, insults and meaningless phrases.
Different texts for each card and images that aim to lighten and entertain, containing a peculiarity: short exercises that keep the brain trained.

When a pair is found, a symbol appears on the corner of the cards:
Gold brain
Silver brain
Bronze brain

Each symbol corresponds to a type of test which presents these possibilities:
Riddles
Words spelled backwards
Scattergories

Through a Web App programmed for the game, which can be accessed with the QR Code shown on the instructions, the screen will present you with 3 cards identified as the tests to be done.

Il gioco che ti fa fare qualcosa che adesso non ricordo

60 carte, consigli, insulti e frasi senza senso in 30 carte che hanno 30 gemelle da ritrovare.
Testi diversi per ogni carta e immagini che vogliono alleggerire e divertire, contenendo una particolarità: dei brevi esercizi che tengono allenato il cervello.

Nel momento in cui la coppia viene trovata, sull’angolo delle carte si nota un simbolo:
Cervello oro
Cervello argento
Cervello bronzo

Ogni simbolo corrisponde ad un tipo di test che si prefigura in queste possibilità:
Indovinelli
Parole al contrario
Nomi/cose/citta/etc

Tramite una Web App programmata per il gioco a cui si arriva con QR Code riportato sulle istruzioni, la schermata ti presenterà 3 carte identificate come i test da fare.!

An Old Kingdom stronghold lies buried in the twisting catacombs beneath the Wilds: a treasure trove of relics, knowledge, and secrets best left forgotten.

Deepvale expands Leviathan Wilds with 7 thrilling new leviathans, including the ever-changing Flux, the brutal Ravager, the unsettling Infestation, and more! The new climber Edge and the new class Harvester increase character and class shuffle-build combinations from the current 64 to an impressive 81.

-description from designer

boardgame: A Hard Day's Work:  The Battle of Droop Mountain, November 6, 1863
description:
“It was a hard day’s work, but officers and men worked with a will, and did their duty—no flinching, no shirking.” –Correspondent “Irwin” comments about the conduct of the Union attack at Droop Mountain.

A Hard Day’s Work is a moderate complexity level game on the largest battle fought in West Virginia during the American Civil War. On November 1st, 1863, General William Averell left Beverly, West Virginia intent upon destroying the Virginia and Tennessee Railroad at Salem, Virginia. The railway was a vital railway for the Confederacy, and following the battle of Chickamauga, it was imperative that Averell’s force sever the link to prevent any more support or reinforcements going west from General Lee’s Army of Northern Virginia to Bragg’s Army of Tennessee. Opposing the Yankees were varied Confederate forces that were outnumbered by the nearly four thousand Union troops under Averell’s command. Falling back in the face of the Union onslaught, over seventeen hundred Confederates, under the command of Brigadier General John Echols, occupied a strong position at Droop Mountain that they believed would stop the Yankees in their tracks. Can you do as well or better?

Players will also need a six-sided die (D6) and ten-sided (D10) dice to play the game (although two D6, one for each player, will help speed combat resolution).

Each turn in A Hard Day’s Work represents one half hour of time. Infantry and Cavalry units represent Companies or 80-100 men per combat factor. Artillery units represent 2-3 guns (cannon and howitzers). A hex on the map is approximately 150 yards across.

A Hard Day's Work is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh and features graphics by Tim Allen.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Reign of Chaos: The Israeli-Hamas War, October-December, 2023
description:
“Raise the banner of Allah over every inch of Palestine” –excerpt from the 1988 Covenant of Islamic Resistance, the founding charter of Hamas.

“We are moving from Iron Dome to an iron fist.” –Israeli Economy Minister, Naftali Bennet, about an Israeli invasion of Gaza.

A Reign of Chaos is a low-complexity, solitaire simulation game of the Israeli-Hamas War of October 2023.  The game is an expansion of A Reign of Missiles: The Gaza Crisis, November 2012. The player takes on the role of the Israeli military high command as it attempts to fend off the missile strikes launched by Hamas from Gaza and rescue the hostages taken by the Hamas commandoes and terrorists on the first day of the conflict. The player is also attempting to manage the crisis in order to keep it from escalating into a larger and bloodier conflict that could engulf the entire Middle East. The game’s rules control the actions of the Hamas forces in Gaza.

Each inch on the Gaza map is about 40 kilometers. Each turn represents a week of time. A Reign of Chaos is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh and features graphics by Bruce Yearian.

Each copy of A Reign of Choas is composed of the following:

•    One 11 by 17 inch area map of Gaza.
•    One sheet of 64 un-mounted, double-sided counters.
•    Twelve pages of rules.

Players will also need the following:

•    The Reign of Missiles game. Note: You cannot play A Reign of Chaos without A Reign of Missiles.
•    An opaque container such as a mug or envelope.
•    One six-sided die (D6) and one ten-sided die (D10) to play the game.

-description from designer

Every soul needs a guide. Usher spirits to the afterlife in this spooky trick-taking game for three to five players. Face off in a strategic duel for ghosts, each with unique modifiers you need the most. But beware of the powerful Rook lurking in each player’s hand, for they have the ultimate power to upset your plans.

How to Play
1. Determine Trump
2. Deal Cards
3. Reveal a Ghost
4. Play Tricks
- You must follow suit
- High card wins, unless trump is played
- Highest trump wins the trick, unless a Rook is played. Rooks have unique powers and copy the previously played suit.
5. The Winner Takes the Trick and the Ghost Card
- Ghost cards can be positive or negative, and have special abilities
6. Play a Round – Each round consists of 12 tricks. 
7. Count Scores and Reset – The Highest Score after 3 Rounds wins!

Nexus Rift: Battle of Dimensions is a strategic tabletop game where players command powerful factions from different dimensions, all connected by the mysterious Nexus. The game combines deck-building with tactical board-based combat as players aim to destroy their opponent's castle.

Each player leads a unique faction with legendary heroes and units, employing strategic positioning and abilities to outmaneuver their enemies. With a dynamic game board and evolving strategies, Nexus Rift offers endless replayability, especially as expansions introduce new dimensions and factions. Players must carefully manage energy resources, plan their moves, and dominate key positions to control the Nexus Rift and gain victory.

—description from the designer

Control the ice, collect pebbles, and find love in this chilly area-control game of the heart for two to four players. Play cards to both win hands AND strategically outmaneuver your foes on the frozen tundra board. Will your clever play win you the love and pebbles needed for victory, or will you be pushed aside by a more worthy rival?

How to Play

On Your Turn
Play a card to try and win a trick and gain a love token.
- Starting player establishes trump
- Highest value trump card wins the trick
- You do DON’T have to follow suit!

Take two actions …
- Place a unit on a space matching your played card
- Use a power of your played card
- Go Fishing for a new card and bonus fish token

Gain victory points by …
- Controlling spaces and territories
- Owning Love Tokens and Fish Tokens

One tree. A bunch of birds. What could go wrong? Find out in this light-hearted soirée of balancing adorably difficult birds for one to four players. Take turns selecting from your pools of oddly shaped birds, carefully balancing them on the rocking nesting tree. If the birds hold firm, you’re in the clear. The higher you climb, the more you score!

How to Play
- Select a bird from your supply to place (with one hand only!) 
- Balance your bird on the tree (seriously, with just one hand!
- Say, “Silly little squirrel, stay away from me!”
- If no birds have fallen, you did it! Score your points and end your turn.
- However – if any bird falls, add the fallen birds to your supply and end your turn.
- First player to run out of birds or reach 20 points wins!

Ultramons is an exciting monster combat game that brings the thrill of mythical monster battles, right to your table. The objective of the game is to eliminate players in a battle of mythical monsters, called Ultramons, and emerge as the legendary champion.

Each player gets a battlefield to strategize their attack, and 12 cards to start. Players then strategically position Ultramons within "Attack" and "Reserve" zones on their battlefield, and attack Ultramons of other players during their turn. Players can also use special powers to boost their attack, protect their Ultramons when attacked or revive their Ultramons that were killed in battle.

The outcome of the game depends on how players position their Ultramons and strategically utilize their special powers during each turn. It's a fast paced game with twists and turns all the way through the last move. The last player standing with Ultramons on the battlefield wins the game.

Designed by a 10-year old, Ultramons is kid friendly, highly strategic and takes only 5 mins to learn. It's perfect for playing with friends, family or on a game night!

Gillers from around the world come to compete in the most famous (and dangerous) fishing contest in the land—The Ogrefish Fishing Tournament! Seasoned anglers compete by fishing-up and fighting monsters using swords, brass knuckles and magic items. Defeat monsters to gain trophies. First Giller to 4 trophies wins and receives the most coveted title in all of the fishing sport—Master Giller! Have what it takes to be crowned the world’s greatest fisher?

Gameplay proceeds with a Giller (active player) and a Second (player to their left) going through the 5 phases of a turn—Bait, Fishing, Fighting, Rewards, End.

Like any good fisherman, selecting the right bait is key to increase your chances of success. The Giller rolls a die to determine the free bait type—cyclops shrimp, bony spider or armored grub. Don’t like the free bait? You may also play an action card to “chum the water” to attract a different type of monster.

Next, the Giller and the Second fish by each rolling one die and then adding in a fishing pole (and other equipment) value to “reel-in” a monster. Giller gains rewards (action cards, magic items, etc.) for fishing based on the risk involved. With the monster now revealed, weapons are decided and fighting begins. Giller and Second each roll two dice (three if the monster is weak against their equipped weapon), play explosive weapons and action cards. If the monster is defeated the Giller gains a trophy, if it is their fourth trophy they immediately win. But Beware! The other Gillers (or even your own Second) might turn cutthroat and take action against you to try to deny you trophies and keep you from winning the title of Master Giller!

-description from developer

Prolong Your Dreams...they are fleeting. The longer you drift within them, the more you can take advantage of their possibilities.  Escape Your Nightmares or they will consume you! Are you able to awaken from them and gain new clarity from the experience.

Vindication: Dreams & Nightmares has 30 new cards that influence the rest mechanism, adding additional benefits (dreams) or consequences (nightmares) for your resting companion. These cards give new significance to the rest and empowered draw mechanisms in Vindication.

When a player reveals a Dream or Nightmare card for any reason, that player must attach the card to their Character and announce that they have an active Dream or Nightmare (you may place a miniature on the card for clarity). There is a maximum of 1 Dream or Nightmare per player at a time - if you draw others, you may choose which to keep and shuffle the others back into the deck.

Each Dream/Nightmare has a special ability that only occurs when the player takes a Rest action, and has a specific criterion for an Awaken effect (removing the Dream/Nightmare from the game and gaining the listed bonuses).

—description from the publisher

The adventurers, exploring the newly discovered Goblin Cave and defeating goblins in search of treasure, finally reached the deepest part of the cave. However, awaiting them was a huge and ferocious troll which had been asleep.
The troll awakened, and now the adventurers must flee toward the escape portal outside the cave, avoiding the troll and grabbing as many treasures as they can while escaping!
Each player's party consists of three adventurers (classes).
Your goal is to make sure that your party escapes ahead of others. Classes closer to the exit score higher. Points are also awarded for treasures collected during the escape. The player with the highest combined score from their party's classes and treasures emerges victorious.

—description from the rulebook

Prize Fighter is a 2-player card game where players are martial arts fighters, competing to be the last one standing!

Each player has a set of identical Skill cards and Health cards. The game is played over a series of Rounds until one player has no remaining cards in their Health Bar. At the beginning of each Round, both players choose a Skill card from their hand and reveal them to each other at the same time. The Skill cards played are then resolved, with the fastest Skill card activating first and cancelling the other. If a player took Damage, they draw cards from their Health Bar equal to the Damage received, and then play one of the drawn cards as a bonus effect for their fighter next round.
At the end of each round, if your opponent has no cards remaining in their Health Bar, you win. Otherwise, play another Round.

-description from designer

This party game revolves around the excitement of shouting out famous or favorite lines that match a given theme. Players decide on a memorable phrase or quote that aligns with the theme of a drawn card. When that card appears again, everyone races to be the first to accurately shout out their chosen line.

The game combines quick reflexes with a fun and lighthearted atmosphere, making it accessible and enjoyable for players of all ages. The cards feature a mix of suggested phrases and cute, friendly characters, adding to the charm and humor of the experience.

The goal of the game is to be the fastest and most accurate in recalling and shouting out your chosen lines, which leads to lots of laughter and excitement as players compete to outwit each other. With simple rules and high flexibility, the game is perfect for casual gatherings, offering a fun and engaging way to bring people together.

—description from designer

Players become managers of the idol group Toriteita 4to6, organizing the formations for live performances and deciding on the Center and Captain. Idols graduate when they reach 30 points. Earns the most points and show everyone you have what it takes to be the best manager!

The game of toriteita-46 (鳥手板4to6) is a must-follow trick-taker. When a player plays a card, it determines where they place their acrylic standee in front of a numbered lineup. Additional cards are revealed called Cadet Cards that determine the location of any unselected standees.

Players compete for the Center position in that lineup or to be the Captain by playing the strongest card. The Center moves their card to their scoring area face-up and will lead the next trick, and the Captain moves their card and any Cadet Cards that match it face-down to their scoring area. If a player takes a seventh or higher face-down card, they pass all of their face-up cards to the player with the least face-down cards.

When players have one card left in each of their hands, they all place that last card face-up or face-down in their scoring area and the round ends. A player scores a point for each face-down card and a point for each suit symbol on their face-up cards. If they have between 4 and 6 face-down cards, they receive the score from their face-up cards a second time. Once a player scores 30 or more points at the end of a round, the player with the highest total score wins the game.

Additional rules include variants for players to draft their hands, ways that trump can change suit, or a mix of the two.
publishers: 金沢小立野ゲームズ（Kanazawa Kodachi Games）

description: No Dachi No Dice is a fun, dynamic and exciting game for 2 to 10 or more players that will run for 1 to 4 hours fitting within a club night or equally a full day of gaming! A game that relies on base sizes rather than figure scale allowing it to be played with any figure sizes from 6mm to 54mm! Amazing looking at 6mm but Spectacular at 10mm and 28mm scales and only needing 15 bases for a full army but no upper limit for those mega games! Just 60 28mm figures will see you with enough to start this epic adventure.

Due to an ancient spell, a young prince is turned into a fierce Dragon. From that moment on, he keeps the princess prisoner in the dungeons of a ghostly tower.

Princesses from all the kingdoms, combining their cunning, their skills and their courage, will come to help her escape... Throughout the adventure you will have to play as a team, unspell knights trapped in their armor, overcome endless dangers and reach time to support the princess in her final duel against the dragon.

But do not be discouraged, because you will have the help of magic (if you are able to obtain and exchange the necessary elements to prepare potions) and a brave group of princesses who will join forces in these 4 fun forms of card games, cards and dice, of different difficulty and duration.

Will you join their adventure so that the princess can free herself from her confinement and free the young prince from her enchantment?

4 WAYS TO PLAY!

Pincesas to the rescue!
Climbing to the tower
The curse of the 3 knights
Princesses search for monsters

Por culpa de un hechizo milenario, un joven príncipe queda convertido en un fiero Dragón. A partir de ese momento, mantiene prisionera a la princesa en las mazmorras de un fantasmagórico torreón.

Princesas de todos los reinos, sumando su astucia, sus destrezas y su coraje, acudirán para ayudarla a escapar… A lo largo de la aventura tendréis que jugar en equipo, deshechizar a caballeros atrapados en sus armaduras, sortear un sinfín de peligros y llegar a tiempo para apoyar a la princesa en su duelo final contra el dragón.

Pero no caigáis en el desaliento, pues contaréis con la ayuda de la magia (si sois capaces de conseguir e intercambiar los elementos necesarios para preparar pociones) y de un valiente grupo de princesas que sumarán sus fuerzas en estas 4 divertidas formas de juego de cartas, tarjetas y dados, de diferente dificultad y duración.

¿Os uniréis a su aventura para que la princesa pueda liberarse de su encierro y liberar al joven príncipe de su encantamiento?

¡4 FORMAS DE JUEGO!

¡Pincesas al rescate!
Escalada al torreón
La maldición de los 3 caballeros
Princesas busca monstruos

The Powerlineboard analoge Spielkonsole is an analog gameconsol or gamesystem with you can played countless games with an mechanical random board and a few game material that the basic material builds, 27 games and few more variations are in the basic box included, the board has 66 fielddices one fielddice has 4 sides with colours and numbers and the fielddices are rotatable beetween there are fixed by a gum, and you can mixed all 66 fielddices also the complete board with the hand and than you place the board at a surface and you have a random maked playing area at the board

Ferocious boarding actions rage through the corridors and strobe-lit chambers of voidships. The fires of this age of war burn just as fiercely amidst the stars as they do on any planet's surface. Fighting for each hatchway, every corridor junction and vital data console, your boarding parties will be tested against the enemy, and their mission parameters.

Warhammer 40,000: Boarding Actions brings the action into the tight confines of space-faring vessels, represented by the Boarding Actions terrain kits. In the cold void of space, the missions are critical and the fighting is up-close and brutal. This book brings Boarding Actions missions into the current edition, and adds loads of Boarding Actions Detachments to enhance your experience. You'll find updated versions of the missions from the Arks of Omen series, including options for narrative, matched play, and multiplayer games.

Inside this 296-page softcover supplement, you'll find:

     Updated rules for playing intense Boarding Actions games in Warhammer 40,000
     55 thematic new Boarding Actions Detachments
     Stratagems and Enhancements unique to Boarding Actions
     54 updated missions for matched play, narrative games, and multiplayer games


—description from the publisher

Pubmill is a strategic two-player game where you must outmaneuver your opponent on a 3x3 grid. The goal is simple: move your pieces in such a way that your opponent can't make a legal move on their turn. With straightforward rules but deep tactical possibilities, Pubmill is a quick and engaging game perfect for both casual and competitive play. Can you master the grid and leave your opponent without a move?

—description from the publisher

A Kocsmalom egy stratégiai, két játékos játék, ahol az a célod, hogy túljárj az ellenfeled eszén egy 3x3-as rácson. A cél egyszerű: mozgasd úgy a bábuidat, hogy az ellenfeled ne tudjon érvényes lépést tenni a saját körében. Az egyszerű szabályok mellett mély taktikai lehetőségeket kínáló Kocsmalom egy gyors és izgalmas játék, amely tökéletes mind alkalmi, mind versengő játékosok számára. Vajon képes leszel uralni a pályát, és lépéskényszerbe hozni az ellenfeledet?

—description from the Hungarian

This micro mini Shogi is played on a 3x3 compact board.  The rules are very limited but you can use all 8 of the pieces.  So it is the best way to master each piece’s movement.

Pieces: Each player has own 8 Shogi pieces as stock. They are any one of 8 different pieces. 王将、金将、銀将、角行、飛車、桂馬、香車、歩兵

Starting position: There are no piece on the board.

Play:

     Decide first player and turns are alternately by two players. Turn player must place a piece on the board or move any one of piece(s) on the board.
     Player may place any one piece on the open own side three spaces but he may place his pawn(歩兵) on any open space on the board. If player moves his piece onto the space where opponent piece exists, he must capture it. 
     Players can’t re-use the captured opponent piece and no pieces are  promoted. Players can’t pass their turn, if player doesn’t have legal  turn, he lose the game.


Goal: To capture opponent King(王将) or to move own King(王将) onto opponent side three spaces.

Optional rule: Second player has two starting option below. 

     1: Second player may indicate the first player’s placing piece at his first turn.
     2: Second Player may select starting pie rule. Pie rule is second player  may switch to first player when first player’s first placement is too  disfavor for him. First player’s first placement will be second player’s  first placement. Then game starts.

boardgame: In the Footsteps of Darwin: Correspondence
description:
England, 1856: Charles Darwin finishes writing On the Origin of Species while you are on your way home. Your correspondence with the illustrious scientist during your journey has paid off as his book will soon be published — but you're not done yet.

In the In the Footsteps of Darwin: Correspondence expansion, you will gain the support of historical figures and make fascinating new discoveries thanks to several new features for the base game: publication tokens, classification tokens, envelope tokens, and new characters such as Emma Darwin and Queen Victoria.

—description from the publisher

Take on the role of 2-5 rival plutocrats vying to grab the wealth of the world before your actions trigger a revolution and you all lose… a lot more than your assets.

Will you play the media baron or the property speculator? The aristocrat or the tech overlord or the war profiteer? Whoever you play, you will be trying to buy a collection of five extravagant assets prized by the super-rich (a mega yacht, a celebrity spouse, a disaster bunker, an art masterpiece and more!) and prevent your opponents from achieving their dreams first.

But watch out - as you gobble up ever more resources, crises start to cascade out of control: wildfires and floods, pandemics and militias, killer robots and doomsday cults… Will you collaborate to suppress the growing rebellions, or will you try to get off the planet with your toys?

Billionaires and Guillotines is designed to get you playing right away with a simple set of rules. But once you’re ready, you can add more chaotic complexity and wicked fun.

You'll bribe politicians to ensure that government policy favours you over your rivals, and you'll take on a secret role that offers special powers and ways to win. Maybe you’ll be the Banker, who gets to break the rules? Or the Disaster Speculator, who is betting on the crisis?  Or the Elite Prepper, decorating your deluxe bunker? Or the Celebrity, who hopes to be spared by the revolution?

And yes, you can play the revolutionaries! In an optional expansion of the game, one player gets to be an agitator. But are you a well-meaning reformist, trying to work with the billionaires to solve the crises and suppress the rebellion, or are you a diabolical revolutionary, eager to wheel out the guillotine?

Billionaires and Guillotines is designed by Max Haiven and will be published in 2025 by London-based Pluto Press. The crowdfunding campaign for the game will launch on March 26 with a party in London on April 13. Games should be in stores and available to order worldwide by October. The game artwork is by Berlin-based artist Amanda Priebe.

Puppies with Powers is a fast-paced, dog-centric card game for 2-6 players, perfect for a family game night or a gathering with friends!

The first person to fill their Kennel and Power up at least 3 Pups wins the game. Each Pup has a unique power that can only be unleashed when they are Powered up. These powers can be used to advance your own Kennel or wreck havoc on your opponents. Power up your Pups quicker by matching their type with the corresponding Power Point cards. Action cards and Impact cards can also have game-changing impacts, so beware, as their bite can sometimes be more powerful than their bark!

—description from the designer

Tranquil Sky is a quiet tile-laying game about creating a night sky full of constellations.

Each turn, choose one of three tiles and place it next to one already in play, making sure that each side matches the adjoining side of every tile it touches, line to line or space to space. If every constellation in your sky is complete when you place the last tile, you win the game.

Tranquil Sky is the kind of game you play when you need a little time to rest. But it's not just about solving a puzzle - it's about enjoying the creative process and admiring what comes out of it.

—description from the designer

Race with Lighting McQueen and his friends over the race-track of the World Grand-Prix. But watch out! During the race you meet friends and foes. Gather the right characters like a real spy and reach the finishline first.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Race met Bliksem McQueen, Takel, Francesco of Finn over het circuit van de World Grand Prix. Maar let op: tijdens de race loop je vriend en vijand tegen het lijf. Verzamel als echte spion de juiste karakters bij elkaar en race als eerste over de finish

—description from the publisher

In Mixart, you will play in competition against each other. You will place cards in front of you to complete Graffiti using Spray Cans.

A game is played in 4 timed rounds. The goal of the game is to have the most points at the end of the four rounds in order to become the most renowned street artist in the city.

—description from the publisher

Night Thirst is a game of bloody competition in a world in which Vampires of all generations and clans put differences aside to participate in a long-honored tradition of battle royale, called the Night Thirst. Vampires representing their clans utilize various strategies and special abilities in an attempt to outhunt their fellow Vampires and consume the most blood before the sun rises! Overseen by the secretive S.T.A.K.E Agents, Vampires will attempt to eliminate their Prey before their fear and commotion rises to a level that gets S.T.A.K.E involved!

While Night Thirst is intended to take place in near modern times, players are encouraged to utilize terrain and models they already have or would like to have, the Night Thirst has existed for centuries and shows no signs of stopping! Night Thirst can take place in the Old West, a Medieval Castle, or a Cyberpunk future! Why not? Vampires in space!

A game within a game, Night Thirst will have players strategizing the best way to drink the most blood while dealing with the rising panic of their Prey as well as potential attacks from S.T.A.K.E Agents and each other!

—description from the publisher

Find your flock and a home to roost in this exciting new take on Rummy for two to five players. Compete to have the most birds gathered on your island. But beware, these fowls are quite fickle, as other players can play cards to attract birds from your flocks to theirs. Will you play it safe and send your birds home to roost, or risk it all in a bid for more points?


     Draw a Card
     Take Two Play Actions
         Flock (Play a Set of Three Matching Birds)
             Matching Birds Join Your Flock
             Birds from Your Flock Can Fly Away!
        
         Roost (Play a Run of Three Birds)
             Play from Hand or Board
             Roosted Birds No Longer Fly Away! 
        
    
     Take Free Actions
         Use Your Island Power
             Reset the Flap [discard pile]
        
         Use a Feather Token
             Draw a Card, Change a Suit, or Take a Third Action
        
    
     Discard 1 Card to the Flap
     Continue Until a Player Runs Out of Cards – Highest Score Wins!

In this game you can become the owner of your own horse by correctly answering questions from the world of horses!

Which horse will be yours?
The Shetland pony, the Fjord or perhaps the Arab? On the game board, 10 different horse breeds are depicted, and each horse has five cards with its special characteristics. By answering the questions correctly, the players get to place a card on the horse's picture on the game board. Once the horse has received all five of its cards, you can try to win the horse by reproducing all of its attributes. You are of course allowed to look at the cards along the way.

Embark on a unique adventure as you accompany the characters of the PAW Patrol series in pursuit of treats. Thanks to a special cube with paws, you will play as 4 different characters looking for delicious dice. Watch out for obstacles that will hinder the completion of the task.

Target of the game is to go through the obstacles and collect all the treats by rolling the dice

—description from the publisher

In the early fifteen century, artillery and the hand held firearm were rapidly superseding the bow and edged weapons and for the next 250 years pike-and-shot prevailed.  Gunpower so dominated the battlefield that armour, except for the helmet and breastplate worn by heavy cavalry and pikemen, was discarded.  It was an era that mage many positive forward steps in the art of warfare and is particularly attractive for simulation in miniature because of its colour, tactical innovations and brilliant commanders.

Each battle described in this book is a significant and outstanding example of the art of warfare and the development of of military tactics in the period.  They are eminently suitable for scaling-down to realistic table-top proportions because of the size of the armies and the area of the battlefield.  By re-fighting them, the wargamer can transform himself into an historical commander, savour his excitement and triumph and suffer his misjudgments and disappointments.

Each of the fifteen battles covered in the book is illustrated with two maps.

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Anti-Desertification Techniques Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Anti-Desertification Techniques is a Prelude card with Microbe and Plant tags.

Increase your plant and steel production 1 step each. Gain 3 M€.

Promo Card X49

boardgame: Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid – Allies Pack #4
description:
Step into the boots of five new heroes: Blue Senturion, the intergalactic law enforcer; Hunter Bradley and Blake Bradley, a.k.a. the Thunder Brothers, who showcase their elite teamwork skills; Levi Weston, the guitar-slinging cowboy; and Orion, who uses the all-new key deck mechanism to represent the power of the Ranger Keys!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid

description:
Tokyo might be full of Heroes and Villains, but it's also full of everyday people like our incredible Kickstarter backers! Without the help of our hundreds of sidekicks, the city would remain in turmoil and World Creator would never find his way home. Add these 4 Kickstarter-exclusive characters to your very own game, and use the power of everyday people to save the day.

—description from the back of the box
publishers: Little Future (リトルフューチャー)

boardgame: Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid – Forever Blue Pack
description: Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid Forever Blue Pack features 20 all new Combat Cards which can be used with any Blue Ranger in Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid!
family: Game: Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid

description: Race Day is a racing-themed puzzle game that challenges you to lay down tracks for three races, aiming to connect the start and end characters for the perfect finish. With every card you play, you'll piece together thrilling racecourses, stacking and strategizing to keep the momentum going. It’s all about maximizing points and creating the most exciting tracks possible before the final flag waves! Ready to take on the challenge and see how far your tracks can go?

Step into the turbulent waters of the Central Sea, where a dark and sinister force threatens to engulf the entire realm in chaos. Pirates and sailors alike are losing their minds, driven mad by an ancient evil rising from the depths. In this 1-4 player epic pirate adventure, you are the most feared pirate of the seas, the only hope against an upcoming doom that seeks to drown the world in corruption and despair.

This crawler offers one of the most exciting and strategic experiences, blending the depth of classic dungeon-crawlers with the dynamic excitement of roguelite mechanics. As you sail through perilous quests, your hero will grow stronger both during and between adventures, allowing you to create devastating combos that turn the tide of battle in your favor.

Customize your Sea Lord with unique powers and strategies, creating powerful synergies. With engaging, fast-paced gameplay and a richly immersive narrative, every turn brings new challenges and opportunities to prove your dominance over the seas and reshape the fate of the world.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Battlezones: Scenarios for Ultra Modern Period
description:
The scenarios are in two parts. The first, a historical section based on actual battles. Most of the battles had much larger forces involved and the battlefield has therefore been compressed to give the feel for the forces involved.

The second section is a series of fictional battles of the Third Word War based on the many excellent fictional books on the subject which have been linked together to give a logical history.

The scenarios have been constructed to be used with either WRG or Challenger rules and in fact several of the earlier historical battles can be fought with Cambrai to Sinai.

—description from the rulebook

It is a time of great disturbance in Landsraad, House Atreides is no more, destroyed by the House Harkonnen on planet Arakkis, also know as DUNE. But, in this chaos, there is an opportunity. Many small houses are now in the spotlight as there is a hole in the fabrics of politics of the Imperium that must be filled. Emperor, Spacing Guild and Bene Gesserit sisterhood are on the lookout for their own shadowy representative in Landsraad. And there are talks of another faction of Fremen rising from Arakkis led by a mysterious, religious fanatic Muad'Dib. All of them are converging toward Imperium board of intrigue, looking for their Shadow Emissary.

Dune - Shadow Emissary is a game for 1 to 4 players set in the Dune universe following the timeline in between games of Dune Imperium and Dune Uprising.

Players will be representing small houses waging secret war on multiple planets and location using their agents to send troops into conflict or gain some resources.
Objective of the game is to win conflicts and establish your rule on multiple locations until you have enough of them to trigger the end game.
Player that scores the most points in Political power, location cards and house tokens is the winner.

—description from the designer

This game uses components from, and therefore requires that you own, Dune Imperium.

Battles! is a free miniatures war game with a unique combination of features:


     Aimed at adults with kids, (ca 5-10 years) that want to participate in the complex games we adults play, but are still too young. 
     This streamlined miniatures war game can be played by kids with any random mix of miniatures (tin soldiers, toy soldiers, sci-fi, historical or fantasy), and on any scale. 
     The game is co-operative, so you play alongside your child (or children play by solo by themselves or with siblings), united against a fully automated evil Warlord. 


Because we all know how heartbreaking it can be to crush the kid's in battle, much more enjoyable to play with them side by side!

Rules free to download and print out. Use whatever miniatures you (or your child) have available, of any scale and any setting.

—description from the designer

boardgame: So, You've Been Cursed.: Magickal Implements of the Future
description:
Tools that seem out of this world appear in the village.

If the Wizard or the Monster picks them up, they can gain coins or energy, teleport, move villagers around, manipulate panic levels or refresh held spells or powers.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: So, You've Been Eaten.: Mystickal Potables of the Past promo
description:
Strange potions appear on the digestive tract.

If the Miner picks them up, they can reroll their action dice or decrease bacteria damage.

If the Beast gets to them first, they can use them as energy or play additional mutation cards.

Either player can use a set of them to force an immediate Ending Number Two and end the game, if they can win.

—description from the publisher

A new sushi restaurant has opened! You need to act quickly using the number sticks in your hand, tasting more delicious sushi than the other players. Of course, you must stay alert and make the right choices at critical turning points to secure your victory.

Number Rods features two distinct game modes that focus on number recognition, quantity comparison, and basic arithmetic operations.

Sushi Conveyor Mode

Players take turns drawing number sticks from a blind bag, placing them adjacent to their game piece, and moving their piece to the end of the stick. 
The first player to cross the finish line wins.

Head Chef Mode

Players roll dice to determine which number stick to place on the game board. The goal is to fill a row with number sticks adding up to ten. If a player's row exceeds the limit, all sticks in that row are removed.
The first player to complete his board wins.

Components


     Game Board x1
     Number Tiles x20
     Cloth Bag x1
     Dice Stickers x1
     Number Rods x60
     Dice x2
     Game Pieces x4
     Instruction Manual x1

A dice-based abstract strategy game featuring Custodial Capture as a main mechanic.

Played on a 5x5 board, both players grab five dice in their own colour, roll them onto the table, and set them up along their edge.

On their turn, a player then moves one die according to the number on top, trying to pinch the other players' dice.

The game ends once one player is reduced down to one die.

This is a deduction game where players look for clues to solve the case.

Murder in the Pines is an immersive detective game set in the Pinewood Woods. The tranquility of a camping trip in Pinewood Woods is shattered when Chloe Bennett, an investigative journalist, is found dead. What starts as a weekend to commemorate the anniversary of a friend’s tragic death quickly turns into a sinister whodunit. With suspicion cast upon her closest friends, each with their own hidden motives, it’s up to a player to piece together the clues and identify the true killer.

Players will examine authentic-looking documents, forensic reports, personal journals, and more. Every piece of evidence is designed to provide players with the real experience of being a detective, making the investigation as close to reality as possible. Narrative is filled with twists, turns, and red herrings. Each character is crafted with detailed backstories and potential motives.

Rokeeto is a 1960s space game of skill. The object of the game is to knock over plastic-colored cones positioned on a space themed board. To knock down the cones a player holds and tilts a small board with a groove.

Each player rolls a colored dice to select a colored target cone on the board. Then Resting a larger cone in the groove the player blows into the cone and sends it down the groove, onto the board, knocking over the colored cones. If a player knocks down all their target cones they receive a marker of that same color. When a player collects a marker of all 6 colors they are the winner.

—user summary

Cartaventura's range of narrative games is expanding with Cartaventura: Cosmologia!

In this adventure, the action takes place in 1633 in Padua, Italy.

You are one of the disciples of Galileo, an illustrious scientist who has already revolutionized astronomy and mechanics several times. Your master calls on you for help because he is about to be tried in Rome for heresy. The publication of his treatise on astronomy has indeed attracted the wrath of the Inquisition.

Will you manage to gather enough elements to convince the actors in this trial to change their minds?

La gamme de jeux narratifs Cartaventura s'agrandit avec Cartaventura : Cosmologia !

Dans cette aventure, l'action prend place en 1633 à Padoue en Italie.

Vous êtes l'un des disciples de Galilée, illustre scientifique qui a déjà révolutionné plusieurs fois l'astronomie et la mécanique. Votre maître vous appelle à l'aide car il est sur le point d'être jugé à Rome pour hérésie. La publication de son traité d'astronomie lui a en effet attiré les foudres de l'Inquisition.

Parviendrez-vous à réunir suffisamment d'éléments pour convaincre les acteurs de ce procès de changer d'avis ?

Drag Racing always creates a fun quick and exciting board game within the racing genre. Drag Racing Max uses a unique dice rolling mechanic of risk and reward for each turn hoping to maximize full movement without complications. DRM is a wonderful simple game to print and play with your children, grandchildren, or even a friend maximizing the enjoyment of drag racing on your table top.

—description from the designer

Fear of the Dark is a solo push-your-luck board game with tile placement and a horror theme.

Inspired by one of the primal fears, it places you in an unknown location without any tangible threat except being alone in the dark with only a limited number of matches in your pocket. The only other resource is your common sense, but like the matches, it is in short supply and tends to deplete too fast.

To win, you cannot be too cautious, as all your matches will run out, and soon after, the loss of common sense will leave you stranded in the dark, and unable to move (losing the game). You must take risks and move forward until you find that last door that does not have any room behind it, leading you to freedom.

The gameplay is divided into two modes:
- Playing with a lit match, you can use your cards to perform actions (moving, exploring or searching) or use their more advanced abilities that will use up that card permanently (loss of common sense).
- Playing without a lit match is much worse, as each card played is used up permanently as you slowly lose the ability for stable thought in the dark.

-description from designer

This is a mini-expansion for Gardlings.  Includes 7 mission tiles and 1 rules tile.

At the beginning of the game, place one mission tile in front of each player. When playing with 2 or 3 players, also place one tile to the right of each player.

Your mission tile will be the first tile of your garden. Also, each tile provides a task or special ability for that round. For example, you can gain 2 extra gems by building a certain shape, or you get the one-time ability to draw a replacement tile from the bag.

At the end of the round, each player pass their mission tile left and takes the tile to their right. This way, the missions rotate throughout the game.

-description from designer

In a world dominated by huge corporations, a small start-up called S.Mall has decided to change the rules of the game. Instead of focusing on size, S.Mall has revolutionized the concept of a shopping centre, demonstrating that it is the intelligent use of space that makes the difference. In this card game, building smaller shopping centers is not only possible, but also the key to getting more points and winning the game.

In S.Mall, two players compete to build the most efficient shopping mall and earn the most points. Over 4 rounds, players choose cards to expand their mall, adding floors with luxury, clothing, food, and tech shops. Each card has an objective that, if completed, awards victory points. At the end of the game, points are tallied based on the shops present and the objectives completed.

The game also includes a solo mode where players face off against the MegaMax Corp. Bot, an opponent known for its aggressive market strategy. The rules are similar to the two-player game, with some exceptions for the Bot. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins, while the Bot does not incur penalties for shops under construction or lack of shop types.

-description from designer

In a remote corner of the planet, there lives a friendly community of geckos, each with a unique character and very specific food preferences. Some love brightly colored mosquitoes, others prefer more muted ones. The competition between geckos is increasingly heated, and only the most astute are able to satisfy their refined tastes, using their skill to prevail over others and secure the much-desired meal. However, they must be careful to avoid predators, which can jeopardize their plans.

In Feed the Geckos, players compete to collect cards of specific colors to accumulate points and win the game. The cards are divided into four colors: Yellow, Red, Blue and Green, numbered 1 to 9. Additionally, there are black cards that function as wild cards, adding a touch of unpredictability to the game.

Each player has a goal: to collect as many cards of two specific colors as possible. During the game, players take turns playing a card from their hand, trying to follow the color or number of the card played first that round. If they can't follow the color or number, they can play a black card as a wild card, changing the fate of the round.

But be careful! Predator cards, marked with the number 5, are traps to avoid, as they can cause you to lose valuable points. At the end of the game, players count the collected cards of their target's colors to determine the final score.

Who will have the best strategy? Who will be able to avoid the predators and collect the most cards of their target? Find out who will be the winner in this exciting and fun challenge!

-description from designer
Players work together in this unique game-and-puzzle combination to spin/roll and add puzzle pieces to the board. Put the forest animal puzzles together before the rain comes and everyone wins!

数棒题卡01 (Exercise Cards 01) is an expansion for Number Rods.

It includes 31 exercise cards with 62 exercises designed for children aged 3-4, covering :


     Flat Puzzles
     Quantity Matching
     Level 1 Measurement
     Level 1 Creative Drawing
     Level 1 Maze

数棒题卡02 (Exercise Cards 02) is an expansion for Number Rods.

It includes 33 exercise cards with 66 exercises designed for children aged 4-5, covering :


     Number Combining
     Number Decomposition
     Symmetry
     Number Rods Filling
     Level 2 Creative Drawing
     Level 2 Maze

数棒题卡03 (Exercise Cards 03) is an expansion for Number Rods.

It includes 34 exercise cards with 68 exercises designed for children aged 5-6, covering :


     Rotation
     Spatial Awareness
     Concrete Addition and Subtraction
     3D Shapes
     Level 2 Measurement
     Level 3 Creative Drawing

Guildmasters of Aurelia is an epic board game that immerses you in a magical world, where you write your own adventures as a guildmaster. In this game, you will hire new adventurers, equip them with the best gear, and send them on dangerous quests.

The rewards you earn from quests will help you enhance your guild by building new structures, increasing your power, or acquiring more skilled adventurers and superior equipment.

Your goal is to craft a successful strategy and take risks at the right time to complete all the quests needed to defeat the great danger that threatens the world—before rival guildmasters do. Lead your guild to the pinnacle of glory!

—description from the designer

Sketch Artist is a print-and-play drawing game about making terrible wanted posters. It came from our desire to make more drawing games that celebrated poor quality and hasty decisions, and that put less pressure on players to be ‘perfect’ in their task. Players will, one at a time, act as witnesses of strange and ridiculous crimes, with the appearance, accessories, and the crime itself randomized by decks of cards. While describing the fugitive for about a minute, the remaining players will draw wanted posters without being able to look while they draw, ensuring low-quality and silly results. The Witness will then choose the most accurate poster, while the other players gain ‘voting points’ to influence a final selection at the end of the game: Most Wanted. While there is, in a sense, a winner, Sketch Artist is mainly for those who want to have fun making something dumb together.

The print-and-play includes rules and cards, and optional wanted posters to print and laminate.

-description from designer

Promo card given out at BGG Con 2023.

Skellie Cost: 5
Skellie Amount: 1
Unlock Amount: 1
Card Name: Blu
Card Type: Unluckable
Ability Type: Instant
Ability Effect: Reshuffle the discard pile into the Luck deck and draw up to your hand size.

boardgame: 古战：三国志 (Ancient War: Heroes and the Three Kingdoms)
description:
Ancient War: Heroes and the Three Kingdoms is a historical wargame set in the Three Kingdoms period of ancient China.

Players command their armies under the leadership of generals. The gameplay includes deploying reinforcements, secretly issuing and revealing military orders, and managing momentum (战势), which directly affects morale. Keeping morale high is essential, as the first side to reach zero morale loses the battle.

Tactical elements such as fire attacks, plagues, poisoned arrows, and siege towers are introduced based on the specific scenarios.

Scenarios


     界桥之战 (Battle of Jieqiao)
     下邳郊外之战 (Battle of Xiapi Outskirts)
     南郡之战 (Battle of Nanjun)
     长坂坡之战 (Battle of Changban)
     逍遥津之战 (Battle of Xiaoyao Ford)
     渭南之战 (Battle of Weinan)
     定军山之战 (Battle of Mount Dingjun)
     猇亭之战 (Battle of Xiaoting)
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Dive into the wacky world of Who Murdered the Turtle?, the hilarious and suspenseful card game where your friends are your biggest suspects!

Who Murdered the Turtle? is a game of asymmetric roles where one player is the murderer and the rest turtles.  On each turn, players play any number of action cards from their hand, ending with picking a card from the draw pile. Turtles can accuse other players of being the murderer, but lose half their cards if their wrong.

if all the turtles are killed before the deck runs out, the murderer wins.  If the turtles guess the identity of the murderer, they win.

—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll with the vip-dice-automatic and move one of their figures. The object of the game is to capture all four enemy figures or to get all your own figures into the enemy camp.

Captured figures are taken to the center space, were they may be liberated by other friendly figures.

The game ends, when one player achieves the game objective.

—user summary

Snatching Pears is a memory-matching reaction party game where you match pairs of pears from 70 cards on the table.

A matching pair of pears gains you 2 points but if a player turns over matching backgrounds then all players race to grab the pears in the middle of the table.

Everyone who grabs one (there is one less than the number of players) gains 1 point.

Once all pairs of pears have been found you add up the score to see who wins.

It's the classic UNO card game players know and love themed to the universe of Star Wars.

The deck features graphics with schematics of the most iconic starships, space stations and droids in the Star Wars universe. The special 'That Was One in a Million' rule will have players tossing cards at the Discard Pile as if it were a thermal exhaust port no more than 2 meters wide! Don't forget, when players get down to only one card, they must yell 'UNO!'
Makes a great gift for collectors and fans of Star Wars for teens, adults and game nights with 2 to 10 players ages 7 years and older.
Expand the basic Cities game with a new achievement board. That includes the main attractions of the cities of Essen and Indianapolis for even more variety.
A Gen Con exclusive promo expansion containing variant rules and joker cards. Included with limited early release of the base game at Gen Con 2024.

A promo micro expansion for Café Baras, included with purchases of the base game at Gen Con 2024.
Also part of Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign (Pack A).

This expansion includes eight new equipment cards which add ongoing abilities to your café. When used as customers, the new cards do not have a preferred decor and will not become regulars but may be served up to four foods from your café.

boardgame: Target for Tonight: The Italian Campaign Expansion
description:
A expansion for Steve Dixon's solitaire game 'Target for Tonight'.

A narrative driven, dice rolling and table reference game placing the player in charge of RAF Bombers during the Italian campaign. The game introduces a new bomber - the Armstrong Whitley and extends the original game to mainland Italy and you will be up against Italian Nightfighters. Will the dice be on your side and will you make the correct choices to get your crew and aircraft safely back to base? Can you last a full campaign?

-user summary

boardgame: Adventure Party: The Role-Playing Party Game Mimic Promo Pack
description: Add new tales to your adventure with two additional encounter cards, Mercenary and Weirding Woods, and a mimic jail for uncooperative dice!

Promo card given away by Brotherwise Games at Gen Con 2024.

Klonos, Xenomorph Mimic copy the abilities of unused racer cards by randomly drawing them. This ability may be used multiple times during a game by discarding the racer card you are currently copying and drawing a new one.
A promo pack containing three copies of a new necklace Handicraft tile for Doggerland. Each tile is worth 4 Tribe points at the end of the game.

Jolly up your box of Herd Mentality with 100 new questions all about Christmas.

Includes Moodolph the red-nosed cow.

Players act as Cult Leaders 'Serving' the Great Old Ones, specifically, by trying to prepare the Best Snack-Rifices for them while keeping their own hidden followers from becoming lunch.

Players garnish and season potential 'Entrees' with Ingredient cards, making them more appetizing, and more valuable when served to the Old Ones. Placing ingredient cards on opposing Cultists serves to increase their value, while simultaneously directing attention away from their own.

Players take turns 'Serving' the Old Ones, choosing which Cultists to Snack-Rifice and gaining rewards depending on how delectable their choices were. The more ingredients played on a Cultist, the greater the rewards!

Victory goes to the player who has managed to gain the most favor with the Old Ones, both through keeping their followers alive the longest and gaining the most Gold by serving up the most delectable ‘Dishes’ to their Dark Masters.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Caribbean (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride: Rails and Sails)
description:
This is a fan made expansion for the game Ticket to Ride: Rails and Sails. The subject matter for the expansion is the Caribbean Sea and Gulf of Mexico.

This expansion follows the basic rules of the game, but adds several features that address many of the criticisms of the original game. Included is a document that describes the original rules as well as the rules of this expansion.

The additional features for the expansion include "non-traditional" routes that allow players to travel greater distances which simultaneously keeps more train and ship cards in circulation. The expansion rules also tweak the number of cards and vehicles you begin the game with. The expansion rules present an option for trading cards and drawing 2 wild card to further keep boat and train cards in circulation.

The expansion rules streamline the harbor process that better keeps harbor cards in circulation.

Finally the expansion rules streamline the scoring process.

Includes a map to be printed, train and ship cards that can be printed and used (though players may use the train and ship cards that come with the original game if they choose,) 120 ticket cards to be printed, and the rules pdf.

A real challenge for all who like fast, dynamic entertainment. Fun for everyone regardless of age.

The game mechanism is designed in such a way that children, parents and seniors have equal chances. It doesn't matter how old you are, it doesn't matter what knowledge you have.

In this crazy race, the one with the sharpest eye and the fastest hands will win. Play and see how fast and exciting a board game can be.

Ahead of us is a real emotional rollercoaster and lots of laughter!

The rules of the game are very simple. Each player gets a dice with 6 different emoticons. Players are presented with a card showing a "combination" of emoticons to roll onto the dice, for example: 6 smiling faces and 2 sad faces. We all roll the dice at the same time, set aside the matching emoticons, and roll the remaining dice until we get the result. The first player who succeeds takes the highest value token (e.g. 4 players make it 4), and so on until everyone has rolled their tokens. The value of the marker is the number of squares you have to move your pawn across the board. The first one to finish wins.

Zipper is a domino climbing-shedding game for 2 players.  It uses a single set of double six dominoes.

Zipper has only two combination types - singles and pairs.  Playing combinations manipulates a zipper which changes what ranks can be played on top of each other.

Win by shedding out!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Race to Escape: The Escape Room Board Game
description:
This game is a 3D escape room game in which players work their way around a series of rooms on the game board solving puzzles and clues as they go one room at a time. To escape the rooms you must solve the room passwords you must use the clue cards.

The game includes tools such as mirrors and magnifying glasses to help you solve the clues.

Classic Surf is an exhilarating board game that captures the thrill of competitive surfing, where every wave is a new challenge and every decision could make or break your score. Designed for 1 to 4 players, this game combines strategy, skill, and a bit of luck as you paddle, catch waves, and perform radical maneuvers to outscore your opponents.

In Classic Surf, each player takes on the role of a surfer, navigating through the shifting conditions of the ocean. With a deck of Paddle, Board, and Maneuver cards, you’ll need to make quick decisions on when to paddle out, which waves to catch, and how to execute the perfect trick. But beware—catching the biggest wave isn’t always the best strategy, as the wind and wave direction can turn against you at any moment!

The game’s unique priority system keeps everyone on their toes, as players vie for the best waves while managing their resources carefully. The dice rolls add a layer of suspense to each maneuver—will you pull off that daring aerial, or will you wipe out and lose your chance at victory? Only those who can adapt to the changing surf conditions and master their board will earn the top scores.

Classic Surf offers endless replayability with its dynamic wave and wind systems, making each game a unique surfing experience. Whether you’re a seasoned surfer or just a fan of the sport, this game will have you feeling the rush of the ocean as you compete to become the ultimate surf champion!

—description from the designer

Players race across the world (board) to be the first to reach the final destination.  You will constantly lose and gain money as you navigate through accommodation stops, chance spaces and job stops reaching check points as you go.  
Players will choose from several modes of transport as they had for success

Players take turns to be the judge.  The judge must draw a judgement card and secretly vote for the play that is guilty, Players score points by guessing who the judge found guilty. The player to reach 10 points first wins.

The animals are sick! But the hospital entrance is crowded with people. What’s the best way to ensure your pets see the doctor first? How can you ensure your little pets see the doctor first? It all depends on your clever maneuvering, and... Sometimes cut in line!

The main mindset of this game is a bit like "In Front of the Elevators". Players will take turns playing 1 hand card, which maybe a patient in different severity(critical to minor) or a patient's relative, to line up in front of the hospital at different levels. The patients must line up in the order of  severity, however each player has limited chances to "Queue jump" with the card played face down. The queue length is limited, and while any card in the queue surpasses the limit will be immediately "downgrade referral" to a lower level hospital.

The triage card, which is also a component of player's hand and can also be played, will let a limited number of patients in the queue enter each hospital and get points.  Each card entered the best hospital scores 3 points, and counts as 1 point while it ends up merely entering a local clinic. Triage will be administered in every hospital after the card is played, so it often helps other players to get points.

Players must make clever decisions on whether to TREAT (perform triage) or CHEAT (Queue jump)!

Use a buzzer to bid for the least number of works possible to describe random items.  This fast paced game keeps all players involved but the tables can turn quickly.  Errors can cause other teams to steal your points.

Use chips to vote on whether you think 50% of the public agree or disagree with statements written on the cards included. If you have a particularly strong opinion you can use additional chip to strengthen your bet.

If you guess correctly you win you chips back plus the same number of voting chips extra. However if you bet 2 chips you must guess within a 10% accuracy.

If you lose your bet, you lose all the chips you used when placing your bet.

The game ends after 10 rounds and the player with the most chips wins.

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Astra Mechanica Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Astra Mechanica is an automated card with a Science tag.

Choose 2 project cards from your event pile and take them to hand.  It may not be cards that place special tiles.

Promo Card X51

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Aqueduct Systems Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Aqueduct Systems is an automated card with a Building Tag.

Requires that you have a city next to an ocean. Draw 3 building cards. (1 Victory Point).

Promo Card X50

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Carbon Nanosystems Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions or from FryxGames.

Carbon Nanosystems is an active card with Science and Building tags.

Effect: When you play a science tag, including this, add a graphene resource here.
Effect: When playing a space or city tag, graphenes may be used as 4 M€ each.

Promo Card X52
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Escape from New York: Stretch Goal Pack
description:
Much more than just 3D plastic bits, the box contains new tiles, Horror cards, and a game variant for crossing the bridge. Horror tiles for Chinatown, UFO, Trick-or-Treat District, and Mist District provide new positive and negative effects.  Broadway tiles create the avenue for quick travel to those willing to brave it.  New item and event cards expand your equipment inventory, and the color rings help you easily distinguish your cars and heroes.

This box contains all the exclusive content unlocked during the GAMEFOUND campaign.

Contents:
1 Stretch Goals rulebook (2-sided sheet)
4 Suitcase plastic figures
15 Ammo plastic tokens
15 Item plastic crates
25 3D plastic Roadblocks
4 Cars plastic figures
1 Gullfire plastic figure
1 Helicopter plastic figure
1 Cabbie's Taxi plastic figure
1 Event Token plastic figure
3 Broadway Tiles 
1 Horror Tiles Pack
1 Horror Cards Pack (4 cards in each of 5 languages)
2 Special dice
8 Colored plastic player rings
"The Bridge" with 6 tokens

Apparition is the world’s first board game exclusively focused on the Blessed Virgin Mary. The game features three card categories: Apparitions (gold ringed), Rosary themes (silver ringed), and Saints (red ringed).

How to Play: On their turn, players draw all of the gold, silver or red-ringed cards from the seven cards shown face-up in the revealed card pile. They use these cards to build matches to claim any of the 10 apparitions, 5 Rosary themes or Saints. When achieving a match, players take the scoring token from the game board that represents that card subject (e.g, Our Lady of Lourdes token).  Everyone's cards are displayed face up for all to see during the game, which allows players on their turn to determine whether to take cards to help themselves or potentially block others. The game ends when the draw deck and revealed card pile are exhausted.

The game's educational value is found in QR codes on all 168 cards that provide additional information about each card topic. Players can read the stories behind more than two dozen apparitions, 18 saints who were profoundly impacted by Our Lady, as well as learn the history and meaning behind Mary's most ancient names, Marian epithets and invocations, and Mary's 10 Evangelical Virtues.

For ages 8+. Simplified instructions provide rule adjustments for younger players. Players don't need to know anything about the Blessed Virgin Mary or Catholicism to play and enjoy this game.

-description from designer

A game in the universe of the web series Le Visiteur du Futur.

Cooperative mode from 1 to 4 players

The Lombardis try to find a time machine called the Tempusfugitron. The players play together against one of the Lombardis. Collect Wiz and buy the Tempusfugitron before the Lombardis to win the game.

Competitive mode from 4 to 9 players

Néo-Versailles, 2550. A defective temporal portal sent people from Néo-Versailles to diverse eras. To find them, Queen Clothilde IV calls two teams of time travellers to save them. The team who finds more lost citizens wins the game.

Open the portal, to collect a portal card and try to find one of the lost citizens.
Use your powers and the effects of your cards to recover citizens from the temporal zones of the other team.

There’s enough excitement to pack the stadium inside this Officially Licensed UNO Elite NFL 2024 Core Edition card game! The action starts with 112 UNO cards, plus 56 randomized NFL Player Cards and 4 collectible Foil Cards. UNO Elite NFL plays like Classic UNO, but actions like Skip and Reverse are found on NFL Player Cards. You use these cards when a game player plays a card with an Elite Icon on it -- there are 2,112 of these collectible UNO cards to take UNO card games to the next level. Colors and decorations may vary. Guide to Collecting Officially Licensed UNO Elite NFL Player Cards: Each NFL Player has 11 different cards to collect: four Base Player Cards and four Base Color Foil Cards (one of each in red, blue, green and yellow), two Dual Color Foil Cards and one Wild Foil Card. Player Cards come in Starter Packs and Draft Packs. Starter Packs and Draft Packs each include 60 player cards: 56 non-foil Player Cards (all unique players) and one Foil Pack containing four Foil Player Cards that include three Base Color Foil Cards (all unique players) and one Dual Color Foil Card or Wild Foil Card (1 per case pack). We may create special Promo Cards for certain events, so keep an eye out for those if you want to complete your collection!


     Draft NFL players into the classic UNO card game with the Officially Licensed UNO Elite NFL 2024 Core Edition!
     The deck comes with 112 UNO cards, 56 Player Cards and 4 collectible Foil UNO Cards!
     Gameplay matches the classic UNO card game with the action and excitement of NFL football!
     Play a card with an Elite Icon on it to unlock an NFL Player Card’s special power!
     Part of the UNO Elite series, this game can be expanded with cards from the Draft Pack -- 60 in total (each sold separately, subject to availability).
     Use your UNO Elite NFL Core Edition box to store your UNO Elite NFL collection as you add more teams – more than 2,000 Player Cards to collect!
     Designed for 2 to 7 players ages 7 years and older, this collectible UNO card game makes a great gift for fans of the NFL!


—description from the publisher

UNO Elite Officially Licensed NFL Draft Pack Cards let players expand their roster of NFL players when playing UNO Elite NFL Core Edition (sold separately and required to play). Each pack includes 56 NFL Player cards, plus 4 Collectible Foil Cards -- which ones will you get? There are more than 2,000 NFL Player Cards to collect! Each Player Card comes with its own special power that activates when a card with an Elite Icon is played. And when down to the last card, don’t forget to yell “Hut, hut, UNO!” Makes a great gift for NFL fans and card game lovers ages 7 years old and up. Colors and decorations may vary. Guide to Collecting Officially Licensed UNO Elite NFL Player Cards: Each NFL Player has 11 different cards to collect: four Base Player Cards and four Base Color Foil Cards (one of each in red, blue, green and yellow), two Dual Color Foil Cards and one Wild Foil Card. Player Cards come in Starter Packs and Draft Packs. Starter Packs and Draft Packs each include 60 player cards: 56 non-foil Player Cards (all unique players) and one Foil Pack containing four Foil Player Cards that include three Base Color Foil Cards (all unique players) and one Dual Color Foil Card or Wild Foil Card (1 per case pack). We may create special Promo Cards for certain events, so keep an eye out for those if you want to complete your collection!

Players can expand their Officially Licensed UNO Elite NFL roster with UNO Elite NFL Draft Packs (the UNO Elite Core Edition, sold separately, is required to play).
Each Officially Licensed Draft Pack includes 56 Player Cards and 4 Collectible Foil Cards -- all randomized so the Player Cards are a surprise! Which ones will you get?
Players build their own Lineup from their UNO Elite Player Card collection -- Draft Packs expand the options with more players!
