Player Cards each have a special power that is unlocked when a card with an Elite Icon is played!
Designed for 2 to 7 players ages 7 years and older, the collectible card game makes a great gift for fans of the NFL!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Cyberia Systems Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Cyberia Systems is a automated card with no tags.

Increase your steel production by 1 step. Copy the PRODUCTION BOXES of 2 of your cards with building tags.

Promo Card X53

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Hermetic Order of Mars Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Hermetic Order of Mars is an automated card with no tags.

Oxygen must be 4% or lower.  Increase your M€ production 2 steps.  Gain 1 M€ per empty area adjacent to your tiles.

Promo Card X56

For many plentiful harvests, the Rock of Misfortune sat peacefully in the valley between two friendly villages, a solemn reminder of their once-troubled past.

Then came a year of drought, a time of fear and whispers. In their desperation to save their people, the leaders of each village betrayed each other attempting to push the accursed stone into the other village away from their own.

While this rivalry tested their endurance, cunning, and determination, the true challenge was not just moving the rock but overcoming fear and rediscovering the unity that once bound them together.

In Rokku, two Daimyos use their spells to engage in an epic battle working to outwit each other with the goal of pushing the Rock of Misfortune into their opponents village or bringing both Rocks of Prosperity into their own. Players start the game with their Daimyo on the board and 6 spells in reserve. Take 2 actions per turn choosing from moving, spawning, activating spells, or passing. Move any spell piece one space on the hexagonal grid, spawn one of your reserve spells onto your gate bringing it into the game, or activate a spell which allows you to push, pull, flip, swap, and other actions interacting with the other spell pieces and rocks on the board.

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Homeostasis Bureau Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Homeostasis Bureau is an active card with a Building tag.

Effect: When you raise temperature, gain 3 M€.
Increase your heat production two steps.

Promo Card X57

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Kaguya Tech Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Kaguya Tech is an automated card with City and Plant tags.

Increase your M€ production 2 steps. Draw 1 card. Remove 1 of your greenery tiles (does not affect oxygen). Place a city tile there, regardless of other restrictions. Gain placement bonuses as usual.

Promo Card X58

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Mars Nomads Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Mars Nomads is an active card with no tags.

Action: Move the Nomads to adjacent non-reserved, empty area, and GAIN PLACEMENT BONSUSES as if placing a special tile there. No tiles may be placed on the Nomad area.

     PLACE THE NOMADS (a gold cube) on a non-reserved, empty area on the game board.


Promo Card X59

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Martian Lumber Corp Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Martian Lumber Corp is an active card with Building and Plant tags.

Effect: When playing a building tag, plants may be used as 3 M€ each.
Requires that you have 2 greenery tiles. Increase your plant production by 1 step.

Promo Card X60

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Neptunian Power Consultants Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Neptunian Power Consultants is an active card with a Power tag.

Effect: When any ocean is placed, you MAY spend 5 M€ (steel may be used), to raise your energy production 1 step and add 1 hydroelectric resource here.
1 VP per hydroelectric resource on this card.

Promo Card X61

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Red Ships Promo Card
description:
Promo Card, which was included in the Kickstarter for Prelude 2, but will also be available in limited quantities at select board game conventions.

Red Ships is an active card with no tags.

Action: Gain 1 M€ for each CITY and SPECIAL TILE ADJACENT TO OCEAN, regardless of owner.
Requires 4% oxygen or more.

Promo Card X62

A card game where from 2 to 6 players draw a character card, draw a hand of cards, reveal a monster card, and then take turns attacking and taking damage from the monster until either the party kills it or they die trying. The game is played in a series of rounds. If a character dies, they draw another character card and are back in it for the next round. After 8 rounds, the player with the most gold value in treasure wins the game.

Meanwhile, players are playing cards on each other and on the monsters to manipulate who lives, dies, or gets the all important kill-shot, which determines who gets first pick of all the loot.

-description from designer

Starfire is a game of battles of between fleets of space warships. Each player commands a fleet of starships, moves their starships, and fires their weapons.

Originally published in 1984, this is the second edition of the Starfire board game. This edition of Starfire was boxed and included a combination of the original Starfire and Starfire II with rules editing and corrections; several rules changes were made to fighters. As such, 2nd Edition Starfire contains the tactical combat rules for both starship and fighter combat in a single book.  Furthermore, it includes 19 scenarios that introduces the first interstellar conflict between the Terran Federation and the Khanate of Orion, and the third interstellar conflict between the Terrans, Khanate, and the Rigelians.

It also had two expansions: Starfire New Empires which added a campaign system (replacing Starfire III, Empires) and Starfire, The Gorm-Khanate War.

Who will survive? Who will thrive? And who will not make it out of the wilderness alive?

The New World, promising freedom and prosperity to those who dare brave its desperate expanse, has quickly become a savage free for all. And yet wagons full of pioneers, fuelled by a dangerous combination of optimism, naivety and idealism, continue the march West.

In We Forlorn Few, players take control of pioneers and must race across the country to be the first to reach the city of Newhaven. On their turn, players must take risks to gather and manage their resources in order to travel, cure ailments, buy items, and trade with others. If they fail to do so, they will find themselves turned ravenous by hunger, and their survival is now based on one thing: hunting down remaining players.

The game ends when players have either reached Newhaven, or when all players have turned ravenous and there is no one left in the wild to satisfy the hunger.

Quick to learn and impossible to predict, We Forlorn Few balances simple and satisfying mechanics with heart-in-mouth risk and seemingly impossible odds, creating a game perfect for players with all levels of experience.

Effect: When playing this card defeat all enemy creatures with the power of 4 or less.

Clarification: This was not originally a promo card. But the German version was misprinted at first to read "When playing this card defeat all creatures with the power of 4 or less" Thus a corrected version was sent out to German backers.

This game consists of nine typographer's quads (the Danish word for quad is geviert) - small lead pieces for letterpress printing, each being a four-sided stick with a nick on one side.
On your turn you throw the nine quads three times, each time counting the number of nicks up, and adding the three throws.
The player with the lowest sum loses.

A variant has the sum of the two first throws multiplied by the third.

Introducing Frozen Darkness, the new expansion for the  Crystal Age board game.

Enjoy two new biomes (Swamp and Tundra), each with their own creature decks. Two new heroes, 5 new abilities, and two corruption lords, each with a unique mechanic to use in cooperative mode.

Dare to venture into the Swamp, where you'll encounter creatures with sacrifice and weakening abilities. Survive in the Tundra to gain abilities that strengthen and counterattack enemies.

Welcome to Frozen Darkness!

—description from the publisher

Step into the shoes of a daring adventurer, following in the footsteps of archeology legends like Jones and Croft. Your expedition will lead you to China, where you’ll unravel the mystery surrounding the Terracotta Army of Emperor Qin Shi Huang. Within the ancient Qin’s Tomb, rows of terracotta army units await, just as they stood divided in history. Terracotta Army Quest is a standalone game from the Dice Raiders Series.

Strategic Choices: Solve the mysteries hidden within this tomb by strategically selecting which units to mark as your own. Each unit can score you in three distinct ways, making every choice crucial for your success. Anticipate opponent's movements to prevent them from undermining your plans.

Solo Challenge: Solo players can test their skills with the smooth to use but hard to beat "Independent Explorer."

Dice Raiders: Terracotta Army Quest adds a captivating layer to the series, inviting you to explore this remarkable historical setting. Dive into the adventure and experience the intrigue of ancient China!

—description from the publisher

Try to communicate with the spirits without breaking the circle you've formed with the other guests and falling into the madness that lurks around the corner.

Form a word together, without consulting each other, using the cards in each player's hand. The first player who cannot continue the word loses the round, unless they can bluff without being exposed.

Red Mist is a miniatures skirmish game set in the world of the Thinning Veil. Each player controls the fate of their warband, fighting for honour and to elevate their status to "Known Men", and eventually to become Heroes of legend! Guided by their Druid, the Chiefs of these warbands lead their followers into battle, for glory and riches. This narrative-based miniatures skirmish game with a twist develops round by round, weaving about your deeds, as you fight your way through the story, but who will have the ultimate victory, and who will literally lose their heads?

Each Warband is comprised of a Chief, a spiritual advisor, and their warriors. All of these powerful combatants can gain skills and increase attributes by spending Skulls.  These carry over game to game. Skulls are gained by killing enemies, and may be spent on your activation.  Be wary, for accumulating too many unspent Skulls will make you a target for enemies, who will wish to claim them from you, to take them for themselves! Red Mist comes with the Headhunters and the Berserkers, and highly challenging, varied, scenario-based encounters, unleashing both allies and enemies into play.

At the conclusion of each scenario the winner of the game is the player who completed the most of their objectives! Be wary though, for the game itself will throw enemies at you, and if you aren't careful you can suffer the ignoble defeat of being beaten by the Crypt Ghouls, the dreaded Shachran, or even the Ghoul King himself!

Red Mist can be played solo in 3 scenarios, or with up to 10 players taking control of two rival warbands, in the 6 Rage mode scenarios and 3 Grudge matches.

—description from the publisher

Hive Ultimate is the very same wonderful game but now includes all the Expansions: Ladybug & Mosquito and Pillbug. This edition also includes a Strategy Tips booklet. Everything in one zipper bag, take it anywhere and play. The Pocket version is available as well.

Competitive Hive players consider Hive PLM (Hive with Pillbug, Ladybug and Mosquito) the standard to play in official tournaments. The game is much more fun and deep. Try it, you will never play the old Hive again!

Stay updated on events via WorldHiveTournaments.com and play online on the new official open-source platform Hivegame.com!
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games
Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description: This game is a card game about Thailand dish and variety of ingredient of Special food in each region of Thailand. Its consisting of 40 famous dish in Thailand. The goal of this game is to be the fastest player to collect dish card that matching with the right ingredient card.

Every year, the town of Otterdam hosts the grand and spirited Wild Boar Parade, a lively festival celebrating the pride of the wild pigs as they descend en masse from the forests surrounding Boarcelona. This year, the festivities were more boisterous than ever, leaving the city in a state of joyful chaos. As coordinators, your mission is to lead your teams of patient and ingenious raccoons to clean up the aftermath of the festivities. Strategize and manage your teams efficiently to tackle various challenges and obstacles, from scattered trash to displaced items, ensuring Otterdam is restored to its pristine state as quickly as possible.

in Trash Rush, players coordinate a team of raccoons to clean up the city. In each round, players choose an action sequence for their team. Steering your garbage trucks around the city, gather as much rubbish as possible over four rounds.  Make the most of your rubbish: use it to repair the city to gain bonuses and victory points. Alternatively, deliver it to recycling plants to score big! After 4 rounds, the game ends and the player with the most victory points wins.

Trash Rush comes with solo, competitive, and cooperative modes.

—description from the publisher

ColorFit is an abstract board game for two players. ColorFit is a game created by a group of researchers from the University of Milan.

Colorfit aims to test the ability of players to correctly recognize colors by allowing to perform an early screening of the visual abilities of the players and recognize if there may be hypothetical colorblind subjects among them

In ColorFit, players take turns placing colored tiles on a board with a graph defined by zones with different colors. A player can only place a tile in zones that have the same color as the chosen tile and that are already connected to other zones on which a tile has been placed.

If a player makes a mistake in placing a tile or does not have the opportunity to place any tile, he passes the turn without making any move. The first player to place all the tiles is the winner.

Colorfit has multiple setup variations regarding the shape of the game board, the color used as the background of the game board and the colors of the tiles given to the players. These variants exist with the aim of testing various types of color deficiencies.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Himmapan: The Story of Agni Mani
description:
When the peaceful Himmapan Forest faces a great danger Himmapan creatures are in dangerous. Guardians must protect and help Himmapan creatures from foreign invaders, who want to steal the Himmapan creatures and use their power to do more evil things! If the invaders succeed Himmapan forests will eventually die. Which side will difinitely survive? Has this forest disappeared? Come be a part of this story ‘Himmapan The Story of Agni Mani’

This game is divide the players to 2 teams, Guardian and invader. Goal of this game for guardian team is protect Himmapan creatures by use the card and steal creature from invader bag. The invader team need to steal all of creature, keep it secret and invader will win!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This funny and creative party game is all about improv storytelling. Inside the box are 101 cards, each with an absurd, funny, or far out solution printed on it. When a player reads the solution prompt card, every other player needs to invent a unique problem that could be solved by the solution on the card. The player that read the prompt picks their favorite made-up problem and that storyteller gets to keep the solution card as a point.

For example, if the solution is “High I.Q. Seagulls” ...what’s your problem? A player might answer: “There’s not enough money in the city budget to pay for lifeguards, so they're planning to bring in high I.Q. seagulls to patrol the beach!” This game can be learned in 60 seconds and used for icebreakers, writing prompts, road trips, and more.

You are an apprentice of the all-powerful Arch-Wizard with the task of crafting the most vibrant, lively micro-Realm. It should be a Realm of wonderful fantasies and high adventures; filled with a myriad of Citizens, Monsters, and Heroes! However, it is not as easy as it sounds. Every class group must go hand-in-hand. Fail to do so, and it will lead the Realm to unbalance and destruction. The Monsters should terrorize the Citizen, the Citizen should pay for the Heroes, and the Heroes should slay the Monsters; one must always follow the other. That is the core-principle rule, and what makes for a healthy social-ecological-economical codependency cycle in any fantasy Realm! So roll up your sleeves, and get working!

Pocket Realm Crafter is a challenging solo game of realm crafting. You are trying to collect as many Character cards as you can by adding them to your realm according to their particular suit order or value, while trying to avoid a “bust” by using their different Powers. Its quick and short, yet full of strategic challenges, that will have you keep on playing!

—description from the publisher

This expansion features brand new Crew and Crew Cards! Get ready for a thrilling experience as each Crew Card boasts its own unique one-type ability. Unleash your creativity and strategize by combining a Crew Card with your Captain!

—description from the publisher

Prepare for an expansion of powerful gargantuan Beasts! While they may require significant preparation and investments, they harness the power to reshape the game's outcome dramatically. Are you ready for the challenge?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tiny Epic Game of Thrones: Deluxe Edition
description:
The Deluxe Edition includes the base game and The Hand of the King Mini-Expansion. It introduces The Hand of the King, a new playable character, Tyrion Lannister, and a beautifully crafted Iron Throne Mini for Tiny Epic Game of Thrones. Also includes solo content tailored for Tyrion Lannister.

Due to licensing agreement, this content is only available in the Deluxe edition and won't be found in retail stores.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Spy City is a funny espionage game for the whole family. One of your agents – your spymaster - need to steal a secret device from your opponent's base and bring it back to your safehouse. To win the game, you must accomplish one of the missions shown on the spywheel. The missions on the spywheel can change during the game, so you need to swap devices between your agents and turn the wheel with an action-card. You will also get other powerful action cards that can change the game. Its fun, because you need to remember where the devices are among your enemy’s agent, you have to beware of your opponent’s chance to accomplish the mission on the wheel and you also need a bit of luck on the dice. Seek, steal and secure. Seek for the device in your opponent’s bases, steal them and bring it back to your safehouse. Its fun.

Spy City takes around 45 minutes to play, and its easy. If you can play Cluedo you can play Spy City.

—description from the designer
Jurassic Feud: Pangea Expansion adds new dino-weapons, more rubber boulders, more warriors, more bricks, more gates, more action cards, as well as bushes to help fortify and beautify your fortress.

You are one of the famous resistance fighters from Belgrade, codename: Otpisani, trying to topple German occupation forces during World War II, from the beginning of 1941 to the end of 1945.
Your adversaries, Gestapo and their lackeys from the Special Police, will try everything to arrest and execute you, while your objective is to sabotage, assassinate and obstruct the occupiers, and liberate Belgrade from their evil reign.
Using items and allies you will go on various missions throughout the city, getting closer and closer to the Nazi high command, until you manage to remove them from their offices and bring freedom to your loved city - Belgrade.
Game will end in 1945, so you must hurry to bring justice for the victims, before people that were responsible for their suffering escape to Argentina.

—description from the designer

Delve into the mysterious Kingdom of Opalstone, where strategy and excitement intertwine in epic 1v1 or 2v2 duels! With a unique day-night cycle and diverse card mechanics, every game is an exciting journey of domination. Gather your forces, capture towers, and outwit your opponents with double-sided cards and powerful stash cards. Opalstone promises endless replayability and a captivating gaming experience for both experienced strategists and newbies. Reduce your opponent's health to zero with dynamic combat, or win by dominating the towers!

Features:

     Diverse card mechanics: Double-sided cards and stash cards provide tactical depth.
     Innovative tower control: Capture towers to gain significant advantages.
     High replayability: Endless combinations ensure that every game is unique.
     No pay-to-win: Fair play, all powerful cards are equally accessible.


Rise to the challenge, master your strategy, and conquer the Opalstone Kingdom!

—description from the publisher

"I'm on a boa-oh no!"

Get ready to rock as a pod of Orcas who love Yachts. Singing the song of the seas, the pod travels the waves getting down and having a good time. Wicked fun can be had in these cards and dice. Win by scoring or stealing the best yachts and shark livers, but watch out for bombs!

Rolling takes me away to where I've always wanted to be, just the cards and waves to carry me. Roll the dice, then find some yachts to rock, did you find a valuable one? That's great as long as you can hang on to it. 
 Roll your dice up to three times, then decide which ocean to visit, or steal from one of your opponents.

The amount of a dice face indicates which action a player can choose, they could choose a lesser action if they desire. Each column of cards is randomly set with Yachts, Bombs, and Shark Livers. Looking at cards will help players determine which is the best play, but they will also have to remember what they saw. 
 Yachts are worth their printed values, Shark Livers are worth an amount based on the number of them a player has collected with 4 available each game. Bombs will lose you points, try to pass them to your opponents when they steal or bluff about their locations in the columns. 
 In this game of pressing your luck with dice and cards, watching opponents and how they react is a game within itself. Bluffing may help encourage your opponents to steal your bombs though, so be a witty whale!

—description from the designer

If you've already raked up all the cards from the original a few times and haven't had enough of coming up with silly answers to silly questions, this box contains 100 questions to put your poker face back on. The base game “Sounds Fishy” is required to play with this expansion!

—description from the publisher (translated)

This easy board game is an expanded version of the old party game known as Celebrities, Hats, Lunchbox, Fishbowl, Saladbowl. The game consists of 200 cards with the names of well-known characters, dead or alive, fictional or real. 
Team members take turns in describing to their teammates, who have to guess who is being described.
It is played in 5 possible rounds - Round 1 describing the character, Round 2 using only one word, Round 3 acting it out, Round 4 drawing it, Round 5 acting out under a sheet with sound effects. It is optional which rounds are played.
The fun is in the antics giving the clues. Loads of fun, with some good laughs.
There will be additional card decks in the near future.

Far Away: Corporate EspionageContinue Far Away's two-player, cooperative planet exploration with a new set of missions. Subvert megacorporations on behalf of your mediocre space government.

Far Away: Corporate Espionage (FA:CE) adds brand new missions and plenty of new challenges. The primary addition is that of nanobots: a new AI system to control the creatures. Explorers can swap in and out the ROLEPLAY system from the base game with a new dice-based AI system.

Other new additions and challenges include:


     New terrain: Hollow. Explore underneath the planet surface to discover rare resources and exotic deathtraps.
     New game mode: GREEN Act. Embrace sustainability through limited resources and limited species extinction.
     New landmarks, injuries, artifacts, and ways to disappoint your manager.
     Things Legal doesn’t want to commit to.


				
				
					The Corporate Espionage DivisionThe Federation Alliance’s Corporate Espionage (FA:CE) division is being developed after the unexpected success of the Far Away program. We need more options for those thousands of explorers who signed up to explore the cosmos, despite safety warnings and several negative reviews that may or may not have missed the point of the Far Away program.

You are now eligible to participate in trial missions that advance Federation interests that the expense of autonomous super-corporations. Embrace the growing wave of economic populism and combat the growing extra-governmental authority exuded by corporate entities.

				
				
					New MissionsMembers of the Corporate Espionage program have access to nine new missions. These will test your abilities beyond the expectations of replacement-level contractors. New challenges include:


     Controlling explorers simultaneously in physical and digital worlds.
     Corralling flying pods with manual dexterity.
     Outmaneuvering genetic monstrosities to save ecosystems.
     Avoiding catastrophic weather such as gas winds and volcanic waves.
     Designing a better AI system than a major tech corporation.


				
				
					AI ControlsFar Away creatures traditionally use the ROLEPLAY system to provide the counterpart to the explorers’ actions. FA:CE includes nanobots that provide AI-driven systems for creatures. Simply replace the creatures’ free will with a new AI CARD and roll a DIE to determine what they do. Use this new purely deterministic system or hybridize it with the ROLEPLAY system to boost your morale for the next mission.

				
				
					Corporate CompetitionFA:CE introduces a pair of missions that pit pairs of explorers against each other. Explorer teams from the Federation Alliance and Conglomerated Enterprises will be given separate missions to accomplish on the same planet. Can both groups succeed? Quite possibly, but given the precision and tactics required, it’s far more likely both will fail.

				
				
					HollowsFA:CE explorers are frequently discovering cave networks on new planets. Exploration of these systems requires specialized DICE and bravery. Your expansion module comes with the DICE. Push your luck to gain an advantage above ground, possibly through the new resource of GEMs. GEMs combine with standard resources to exceed the limits of default explorers.

				
				
					Expanded UniverseDiscover new landmarks, anomalies, creatures, and horrific injuries as you continue your mission to find steady employment.

Mirianth Pets is a thematic adaptation of the shedding game Ambiente Abissal/Planet etuC, set in the captivating world of Mirianth. In this game for 2 or 3 players, you will have the opportunity to control six fantastical animals from this magical universe, using their unique strength to win the game by getting rid of their hand.

The cards in the game represent the six mystical animals of Mirianth, each with different strengths and weaknesses, depending on the number of players. Each animal has a rank and power, allowing for various ways to outmaneuver your opponents' plays.

The game proceeds in turns, with players playing 1 or 2 cards to beat the current strongest play or passing. When all but one player has passed, the round ends, and the last player to play starts a new round.

When a player successfully plays all their cards, they earn points. Once a player accumulates a certain number of points, they are declared the winner of the game.

VLKNO is a 2 player abstract strategy game played on a 5x5 grid covered in 25 stackable stones.

Players each have 2 pawns in their color, which are placed on stones in diagonally opposite corners of the grid at the start of the game.

Each turn, you do the following actions:


     Move either of your pawns one space in any direction to a stack of 1+ stones that's the same height, or 1 stone taller, or 1 stone shorter than yours.
     Pick up a stone from the smallest unoccupied stack on the board, except the one you just came from (if there's a tie for shortest stack, you choose which one).
     Place it on any unoccupied stack of 1+ stones except the one you just came from.


You win when your opponent cannot complete each step of their turn.

The lava grid is used for thematic purposes; the game can be played without a board.

—description from the designer

This game was entered in the BGG 2024 Children & Family Game Design Contest, where it was voted #2 Best Children’s Game and #3 Best Family Game

In this game it is all about seeing which object is on the card twice. 
All players are looking at the same card at the same time, so you have to be fast. 
Search as fast as possible and call out the correct answer, before anybody else does is.

This game can be played in 2 levels and also has an option for a different play;
Instead of looking for 2 identical objects, look for two objects that are the same, but in a different color.

boardgame: Between Light & Shadow: Chapter II – Twilight
description:
Immerse yourself in the mystical desert world where everything takes place between light and shadow. In a world threatened by darkness, you take on the role of Mara, a brave adventurer who is on a journey that will determine both her fate and that of the world itself.

Your path is determined by dice and clever resource management. Use your dice strategically to activate powerful abilities and perform decisive actions as you fight your way through the challenges of the desert world. You have both roll-and-write mechanics and tactical planning at your disposal to master the various tasks. Always pay attention to Mara's health and energy, because if one of these resources drops to 0, the adventure ends abruptly.

Mara moves between light and shadow, and each of your decisions will change the balance in the world. Will you choose the light and defy the darkness, or will you give in to the shadow and unleash its power? The world around you reacts to your choices and adapts to your journey, making each game a unique experience.

—description from the designer

Question: If a child in Afghanistan loses his burqa-wearing mother at the market, would he be able to find her again?
My response was to create yet another game through which people can give themselves their own personal answer.
Reflection on such a serious topic could emerge between the discomfort of having to approach something so important in an extremely frivolous way, through a card game.

Inside the box, the deck is made up of 75 cards, divided into three different sub-decks representing the two GAME TEAMS and a third of the CHOICE of the mysterious character to play with. Having chosen the team and placed the cards on the table, each player places in front of them  25 women in burqas and must ask the correct questions to guess the opponent's character.

Domanda: se un bambino in Afghanistan perde sua madre che indossa il burqa al mercato, riuscirebbe a ritrovarla?
La mia risposta è stata creare l’ennesimo gioco in attraverso il quale la gente possa darsi la propria personale risposta.
La riflessione su un argomento talmente serio potrebbe sbucare tra il disagio del doversi approcciare a qualcosa di così importante in modo estremamente frivolo, attraverso un gioco di carte, e la presa di coscienza di come l’identità pubblica delle donne che indossano il burqa sia effettivamente e totalmente appiattita e portata allo stato di zero. O è solo un pensiero occidentalocentrico e chi ci gioca scoprirà di poter aiutare il bambino a ritrovare la madre?
Burqì lo sa.

All’interno del pack, il mazzo è composto da 75 carte, divise in tre diversi sotto-mazzi rappresentanti i due TEAM DI GIOCO e un terzo di SCELTA del personaggio misterioso con cui giocare. Scelto il team e posizionate le carte sul tavolo, ogni giocatore ha davanti a sé 25 donne in burqa su cui fare le domande corrette per arrivare ad indovinare il personaggio avversario.

A glamorous veneer envelops what in reality everyone calls degradation and it transports us lightly towards exciting competitive races. Who said sport has to be healthy? On the other hand, society has never disdained decadence when it is painted like a high-class harlot.
Trainspotting? A cult film that has worked on the collective imagination for decades, making junkies disarmingly appealing.

The title of the game is a reference to a South Park episode, Guitar Queer-o.

And so: six heroin addicts at the starting lines, ready
like never before in pursuit of a large and colorful imaginary dragon.
Each player can bet whatever he wants on their favorite champion , taking part in what will be a race filled with excitement to the finish line.

Each racer has a color, reveal a colored card from the deck and move the corresponding racer down the track!

Una patina rosa avvolge quello che nella realtà tutti
chiamano degrado e ci trasporta con leggerezza verso
avvincenti gare agonistiche. Chi l’ha detto che lo sport
debba essere sano? D’altronde la società non ha mai disdegnato la decadenza quando è imbellettata come una puttana d’alto bordo.
Trainspotting? Un cult del cinema che ha lavorato per decenni sull’immaginario collettivo rendendo i tossici di un disarmante appeal.

E allora: sei eroinomani alle griglie di partenza, pronti
come non mai all’inseguimento di un grande e colorato drago immaginario.
Ogni giocatore può puntare ciò che vuole sul campione
preferito, prendendo parte a quella che sarà una corsa
entusiasmante fino al traguardo.

SSG Basketball is a possession by possession simulation of basketball.  Every nuance of the sport of basketball is captured in the game’s ratings and game mechanics.  Players are rated for shooting, passing, drawing fouls, turnovers, rebounding, steals, committing fouls, blocked shots and how often they control the action on both offense and defense.  Teams are rated for pace of play, home court advantage and defense.

Spice Ships takes place in the 16th century, during the height of the global spice trade. Players take on the role of rival spice merchants, racing to discover new spice islands, establish lucrative trade routes, and fend off challenges such as storms and pirates. The game blends quick rounds with a mix of strategy and luck, making it approachable for casual players.

Overview:
The goal of Spice Ships is to be the first player to complete a set number of trade routes by collecting the right combination of cards. The game can be played with 2-6 players, and each game round takes approximately 5-15 minutes.

Gameplay:
Players are dealt 3 cards each. On their turn a player takes one action and draws a card from the draw pile.

The actions they can take are:

Play an Island Card: Players can place an island card face-up in front of them to represent the discovery of a new spice island. A player can only have one island of each spice type at a time.

Play a Ship Card: If the player already has an island, they may place a corresponding ship card on it. Each island requires two matching ship cards to complete a trade route.

Play an Event Card: Event cards create opportunities to disrupt opponents. For example the “Redirection" card allows a player to move one ship on the table another matching island (yours or someone else’s). The “Pirate” event lets a player steal an opponent's island and its ships.

Discard a Card: If none of the above actions are possible or advantageous, a player may choose to discard a card from their hand.

After taking an action, the player must draw one card to end their turn. The game continues clockwise, with each player taking turns in sequence.

Game End and Winning Conditions:
The game ends when a player completes the required number of trade routes. In a 2-player game, the first player to complete three trade routes wins. In a game with 3 or more players, the winner is the first to complete two trade routes.

Four brand new alternate shapes to change up your Hamster Roll experience.

Substitute in one or more of these pieces in place of any pieces from the originals.

Or for a more difficult and longer game, try adding one or more to the original set.

Set includes 4 each of 4 unique shapes for a total of 16 pieces.

-description from publisher

Whisker Wars is a whimsically serious card battler, inviting 2-4 players to build the ultimate hand of heroic animal warriors and deploying cutthroat critters to battle for the throne. The twist... the flow of battle can change at any time, making this contest a unique blend of risk, timing, and strategy.

A player scores Victory Points (Crown Tokens) by forcing another player's Morale to zero (0). The first player to reach 2 Crown Tokens, wins!

—description from the publisher

The War of 1812 may have been determined by the battles on the periphery of the new United States and on the high seas, but on the far (Old) Northwest Frontier in the Illinois Territory (which then included Wisconsin) it was just as desperate if not moreso.
The British ... and the local American Indian tribes ... got word of the war starting a full month before the Americans did, and in Illinois the war finally started with the 15 August 1812 Fort Dearborn Massacre which, among many others, claimed the life of young Ensign (2nd Lieutenant) George Ronan, West Point Class of 1811, the US Military Academy's first graduate Killed in Action (KIA).

The game has no conventional movement grid.  The rivers are the highways - keelboats *could* go/sail upstream, if slower - or cross-country to the nearest opposing fort or town or village.
Combat is very simple, but losses are permanent for Indians, so if they do badly they might be neutralized for the next season: Hostile, ?? (Neutral), and Friendly. The Indians naturally looked to the British as protectors against oncoming American settlers and both they and the British wanted the territory north and west of the Ohio River to be their neutral reserve.

The first year, the British have Strategic Superiority, but things can then reverse in land and Great Lakes naval battles as well as in the East and on the oceans.  There are victory point compensations either way.

Tribes represented are the Winnebago/Ho-Chunk in the North, the (warrior) Sauk and (farmer) Fox along the Mississippi an Rock Rivers and around Rock Rapids and Island, the Potawatomis along the central Illinois River, the Kickapoo south along them, and the Plankashaw/Wea in the southeast near Vincennes and Fort Patrick Henry.
Both British and Americans can build forts, and there is a chance Fort Dearborn can be safely evacuated.

Agents competing for (Indian loyalties) influence are spy Thomas Forsyth for the US and agent Robert Dickson for the British.
Leaders (who can be caughtkilled) include Governor Edwards, Ensign Ronan, and chiefs like Black Hawk, Bad Sturgeon, and others.
Massacres by either side can occur and have strategic negative impact.

Victory is determined by who controls forts, settlements, and/or villages.

This is right now a 2-player game with hidden deployment, but I may be able to devise simple solitaire mechanics.
It plays very quickly.  Hostile tribes cannot attack through neutral or opposing tribes' territories.

—description from the publisher

description:
That's You! Adult Pack is an expansions for use with the That's You! base game. Each expansion is added to the basic game and they should be played separately.

Who' most likely to love blindfolded surprises?
Or to never pay for drinks at the bar? Or who's always receiving noise complaints from their neighbors? 
Get ready to point fingers and argue who's the naughtiest out of you with this ADULT PACK edition of THAT'S YOU! And remember, if it doesn't fit anyone else... THAT'S YOU!

—description from the publisher

Go even faster, higher, and stronger than ever, and embark on a daring quest to reach the moons of Jupiter first. Use R&D to develop the most sophisticated rocket and hire advisors to gain an edge over your competition. Success is in your hands!

Target: Jupiter is an expansion for Rocketmen, quickening the game's pace and expanding its horizons. It offers three new features: a new Destination with five Locations (Jupiter and its Galilean moons), a new action (Research & Development) granting bonuses to help you complete even the most challenging missions, and two new types of cards (Benefits and Advisors) offering alternative options and more variety to Rocketmen.

Target: Jupiter also introduces a new solo mode, new rules around removing cards from the display, and a new board and components to support all these features and changes.

—description from the publisher

Mighty Shot! A unique ARENA/MOBA asymmetrical board game that blends tactics and dexterity in which players compete in a immersive, fun, and tactical duel.

As a Champion of Sorceline, during your turn you deploy your minions to control areas of your home field and collect Arcana to unleash your Arcane Strike directly damaging your opponent, cleverly play your Champion's cards to implement your strategy or counter your opponent's, but above all shoot your opponents minions to deal Arcane Link damage in your quest to knock your opponent out. You must knock out your opponent to win the game.

New scenario for the game.
During an energy shortage, energy production must be increased to compensate for the increased demand. The global footprint is an indicator of what type of energy is being produced on Catan. A low global footprint indicates renewable energy production has been a viable investment. Players who have invested heavily in this area benefit from a need for increased production. A high global footprint indicates that fossil fuels still dominate investment. Players who have invested heavily in that industry now benefit from the sudden increase in demand.

You will plunge into the atmosphere of uncertainty and piece by piece collect knowledge about the life of the colony, which led the local population to split. You will be able to lead one of three factions, each of which pursues its own goals and lives by its own truth.

The game takes place in two interconnected planes:

     The playing field. The events of the game take place inside the old tree, so the architecture of the Lignum consists of 7 floors with a central staircase, which makes communication between floors difficult.
     The player's tablet, on which the supporters hired by you will be located.


There are more than 30 characters in total, which will bring you victory points, unique properties and combat strength for battles on the field.

The game Bierfischen (English: "Beer fishing") has the same rules as the game Haifischen and reimplements it for a different audience.

Angle the treasures from the beer lake: Competition is at the heart of the game - the players fish in a race to pull the most treasures from the beer lake. They not only have to watch out for floating beer barrels, but also make sure that no competitor steals their hard-won treasures.

25 picture cards: The Bierfischen card game contains 25 entertaining picture cards depicting various treasures and presenting players with the challenge of fishing them out of a beer lake.

Drinking game mode: Bierfischen offers the option of playing the game in a drinking game mode, which provides additional fun and a relaxed atmosphere - perfect for sociable evenings with friends.

Age rating from 16 years: Due to the fun and potentially competitive nature of the game, Bierfischen is suitable for players aged 16 and over. The game mechanics and humor are geared towards an adult audience.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Promo cards for Legion: The Siberian Story. The pack contains 4 character cards (1 new character for each faction).

     Antonín Kalvoda


Add the promo Characters to the regular Characters.

These Characters introduce a new ability called Toughness. A Character with this ability dies only upon receiving a third Wound. The second Wound card should be place into the Character's card sleeve upside down to indicate that it has two Wounds.

description:
Exclusive promo cards for the pre-order of Legion: The Siberian Story.

The pack contains 4 new locations cards in Czech and 4 cards of the same locations in English.


     Watch rotation (L06) / Střídání (L02)

Add the promo Locations to the deck of Location cards in the game. The game rules stay as usual.

Every season seems to bring a new challenge that complicates your normal routine. Addressing it head-on may help you feel better, as long as you don't ignore your other concerns!

Seasonal Stresses 4-card expansion introduces unique Stress cards. Add one random Stress card above your Tea line at the start of the game. Should you complete the card’s requirement, such as having 4 Distracted Tea cards at once, you may immediately use its bonus effect then remove it from play. If you’re unable to eliminate your Stress, you’ll suffer negative points at the end of the game.

boardgame: A Nice Cuppa: A Good Book
description:
Few things pair together as nicely as a warm cup of tea and a good book. Let the worries of the world go as you settle in for a nice relaxing read.

A Good Book 5-card expansion adds 2 new Tea cards and 3 new Worries to manage. Use a full set of 9 Tea Cards (and correspondingly 9 Worries) to play through a longer, more challenging game. Can you pay attention to both your tea and book through a full 10 turns?

Thick fog covers the sea, sowing doubt and confusion wherever you look. Illuminate these obscured areas if you hope to survive.

Fearsome Fog 4-card expansion adds unique Fog cards, all of which are placed above the starting sea. These cards do not move, but remain face-down until a Nightmare underneath one is defeated. Once this happens, reveal the Fog card’s unique effect and discard it. These effects rearrange your Traps and Nightmares, potentially wrecking your plans for survival. All Fog cards must be discarded before the end of the game or the Nightmares win.

The deepest recesses of the ocean hide nightmares even more terrible than those you’ve seen. As the tides carry them to shore, you must steel yourself for a desperate fight.

Terrible Tides 6-card expansion introduces Tide cards, which increase the health and range of existing Nightmares to create nearly unstoppable horrors. One Tide card is added to the rightmost Nightmare at setup and each time a Nightmare is moved to the right side of the sea, it also receives a Tide card. When Nightmares are defeated, their Tide cards are discarded – but they may return if the deck runs out. With the possibility of some Nightmares acquiring multiple Tide cards, careful management is critical to surviving the worst night of your life.

A collection of 4 expansions for The Last Lighthouse:


     The Last Lighthouse: Strange Shores
     The Last Lighthouse: Long Nights
     The Last Lighthouse: Fearsome Fog
     The Last Lighthouse: Terrible Tides

boardgame: Limits of Glory: Donning the Sacred Heart
description:
This is a standalone game using the Limits of Glory system to examine the impact of events on the Civil War in the Vendee in 1793. Will you fight for the Republic or For God and the King?

The French Revolution was not welcomed by all in France. The staunchly Catholic and Royalist leaning Vendee Militaire was a region unwilling to sacrifice its youth to the voracious appetite of the Republic's military conscription machine, and the people of the Vendee were prepared to fight to defend their beliefs.

In Donning the Sacred Heart you take the role of either the Vendee Catholic Royalists, or the Republicans.

The Limits of Glory series examines the effect of 'skill and luck' on the commanders present in a campaign. A Glory rating is attributed to all the significant participants in the campaign, made up from the skill they exhibited and the luck from which they benefited. Players must make difficult decisions as to when to use their commanders Glory to mitigate the situation on the board to their advantage by attempting to change unfavourable events as the campaign unfolds.

—description from the designer
