Don't Be A Sheep is a baaaa-rilliant party game for herds of 5 to 25 players.

In each round, players answer five questions to category cards like "Basketball shoe". They score points based on the number of shared answers, but watch out! If just one - and only one person - goes rogue, they steal all the flock's points on that question for themselves.

The game usually lasts three rounds, but can be played in fewer rounds for a faster game. The player with the most points wins and is crowned the G.O.A.T. (Greatest of All Time).

boardgame: Last One Laughing: Das Spiel – Mini Edition
description:
Your challenge in Last One Laughing: Das Spiel – Mini Edition is clear from the game title: Be the last one to laugh. More specifically, be the last one to laugh twice since the second laugh eliminates you from play.

Once the round starts, if you get someone else to laugh, whether by using the suggestions on the 170 included cards or by doing anything else, you can hit the buzzer and win a point for their lack of restraint.

Whoever remains in the game the longest or who scores the most points wins.

Last One Laughing: Das Spiel – Mini Edition can be played on its own or combined with Last One Laughing: Das Spiel.

Apiary: Expanding the Hive adds to the variety of the core game with components all designed to fit inside the original insert and a comprehensive appendix that covers the core game and the expansion. It features a few bigger additions (7 new hive mats, 15 unique frames, and 15 new seed cards), along with a greater variety of tiles and tokens, plus a few updated components.

—description from publisher

Are you ready to be Keeper of the Royal Menagerie? We'll find out in Lorepets when you compete against other contenders to find out who can catch the most critters.

To set up, deal ten different lorepets from the deck of eighty tiles face up into the central menagerie, shuffling duplicates back into the deck. Each of these tiles counts as a stack. Remove ten tiles from the game without looking at them, then deal each player a hand of six tiles, setting the rest down as a deck.

On a turn, each player chooses and reveals a tile from their hand simultaneously. If more than one player reveals the same type of lorepet, take all of these revealed tiles and start a new stack in the menagerie (or add them to an existing stack). If you are the only player to reveal a type of lorepet, take a stack of this type from either the menagerie or another player, add your tile to that stack, then place it in front of you. Refill players' hands to six tiles.

Keeping playing until no one has tiles in hand. All players compile their stacks into a single pile. Whoever has the tallest pile wins.

boardgame: Tsukuyumi: Full Moon Down (Second Edition) – Amaterasu Rising
description:
Reclaim your territory with the new faction ‘The First Guardians’ and strengthen all your factions with the ‘Amaterasu's Blessing’ module to take your experience to the next level!

Amaterasu Rising introduces new factions, mechanics, and story elements, expanding the game's universe. Like the original, it emphasizes area control and asymmetrical gameplay, but with new twists that add fresh challenges and depth to the experience. Fans of the original game will find this expansion adds even more complexity and excitement to the already rich and immersive world of Tsukuyumi.

It consists of a special deck with five suits of eleven cards each, numbered from 1 to 10 plus a joker. In the first round, players receive a card and try to guess whether they will win or lose that battle, after which they compare their cards.

In subsequent rounds, players will receive the equivalent number of cards (2 for the second round, 3 for the third, and so on) and must guess how many battles they will win or lose in that round. Players who get it right will receive 10+the round number in positive points (11 points in the first round, 12 in the second and so on), those who get it wrong receive an equivalent negative score.

The winner is the player who scores the most positive points or the fewest negative points, if everyone scores negatively.

Cheesed Off! is a fast-paced, hilarious, and action-packed party game for the whole family!

Players take turns playing direction cards to send a rubber wedge of cheese zooming around the table. But beware! If the cheese lands in front of you and you can't play a card, you'll have to take a dreaded "stinky" token!

The game continues until all the "stinky" tokens have been handed out. The player with the fewest tokens is crowned the winner! (ties are friendly)

However, if one crafty player manages to collect all the tokens, they instantly snatch victory for themselves!

—description from the designer

There’s a new Chinese restaurant in town and the dumpster out back is up for grabs. Who will be the last varmint standing to claim the turf as their own, the honorable RACOON or the loathsome POSSUM?

“Critter Rasslin” is a 2 or 4 player card game based on hilarious wrestling moves to see who will gain dominance over the new territory! Take turns dealing damage to your opponent or defending against mortal blows. Once enough damage is accrued you can pin your opponent for the victory, or go for the TKO!

ATTACK CARDS are given a damage level of 1 through 4. They are played in the opponent’s DAMAGE PILE. They are the only cards placed in the opponent’s damage pile, Others are discarded face up beside the player’s deck. As ATTACK CARDS are played, they should be placed fanned out in front of their opponent in a manner so damage can be easily added up.

The first player to dole out 21 (or more) damage points to their opponent AND play a PIN CARD on them is declared the winner. After a player reaches 21 or more damage they cannot play any cards after being pinned. A player may also win by knockout - inflicting 30 or more damage on an opponent with no PIN CARD required.

There is a four player tag-team mode for more incredible critter chaos!

In Label Legends, you're a record label executive who's trying to get bands under contract, but can you focus your recruiting efforts enough to land more fans than anyone else?

Label Legends features gameplay similar to Uwe Rosenberg's Klunker, but with a larger player count and slightly different rules. To set-up, each player has 1 fan card (a random face-down card) in their "band area" and a hand of six cards from a deck that contains seven genres of musicians.

On a turn, each player in clockwise order from the round's starting player places 0-6 musician cards from their hand into their waiting room. (If a player's waiting room is empty, they must place at least one card.) Next, in clockwise order players can place one card face up in their band area or pass; when a player passes, they take the lowest numbered "hype" token, and players continue placing cards one by one until they all pass.

Starting with the player who has the lowest hype token, a player can sign all of the musicians in someone's waiting room — including their own — by giving 1 fan card to the player who owns that waiting room; the player then places these cards in their band area. (If you have cards in your waiting room, you must acquire musicians.)

As soon as a player has four of the same type of musician in their band area, whether from playing cards there directly or from acquiring musicians from someone's waiting room, they must cash in those cards to gain fans. If you have only one type of musician in your band area, you flip all four cards for 4 fans. For each extra type of musician in your band area, you flip one fewer card, e.g., with three types of musicians (including the one you're scoring), you flip only 2 cards to fans, discarding the other two cards from play.

If a player declines to acquire musicians, the round ends immediately, then players refill their hand to six cards. If this isn't possible because the deck has run out, the game ends. Whoever has the most fans wins.

During set-up, you adjust the number of cards in the Label Legends deck based on the player count, and if you wish, you can add the Uwe Rosenberg conductor card as a joker who can be assigned to any genre of music. Uwe's so versatile!

In Aquarium Trading, you and another amateur aquarist are competing with each other to have the best collection of fish after 1 month. You will buy fish from the local store, breed them, and sell them.


Gameplay:

On your turn, You will first breed your fish by rotating your fish cards 90 degrees clockwise, then you will choose a card from your hand and play it either as a goal or as fish. After you have played a fish or goal, you will pass one of your cards to your opponent, and finally keep one for yourself.

If you play the card as a goal, rotate the fish cards indicated 90 degrees counterclockwise and put the goal off to the side. If you play it as more fish, rotate fish of your choosing until the coin amounts equal out, then flip the card with the fish you are buying and rotate it to the amount shown on the front of the card.

Game End and Scoring:

When the deck runs out, the game ends and you score your points by counting up the goals that you have completed, and adding up how many fish you have. That is your score.

—description from the designer

A system for playing large fleet actions using 1/2400 to 1/6000 scale ships. Full colour 56 pages. Includes QRS and sample OoB Roster sheets.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
A competitive action game for 2~8 players, played in teams!

The winners are the first teamplayer to fill all capsules of their color with Gems of theirhis/her own color by controlling their blindfolded partner with the Joy Stick!

—description from the publisher

The Atomic Builders is a strategy and point-gathering card game inspired by chemistry. It aims to teach and inspire players about scientific concepts while having fun. Suitable for children, teenagers, and families, the game is easy to learn and turns players into hobby chemists after a few rounds.

Be the first player to reach 24 points by creating larger molecules (products) from smaller building blocks (reactants) and other actions in the game.

You play cards and combine them in order to create the products and you special and radioactive cards in order to disrupt your opponents. But beware of the Acid cards that mess with your gameplan!

—description from the designer

Welcome to Mythoria: Clash of Legends, a print-and-play card game where you command powerful forces to shape the fate of a mystical realm. In Mythoria, heroes and villains from various mythologies and legends clash in epic battles, each striving to collect story points and achieve ultimate victory. Your objective is to gather either 20 Good Story Points or 20 Evil Story Points, guiding your chosen path towards triumph.

Each player selects one of the elemental decks: Thunder, Ice, or Fire. You then draw cards, use them for mana to pay for other cards, deploy your creatures on the board and use them for their special abilities and to attack your opponent in order to gain the Story Points and win the battle.

—description from the designer

Shadow & Blade is a PnP R&W game of stealth, action and heists with a dice management system.

Print out the maps, campaign and your character sheets and get ready to roll in the world of Valathria, the city of cathedrals.

Use your 4 dice rolls to move through the map and activate your skills and actions but beware of the noise track so as not to gather unwanted attraction and have your enemies arrest you. Use strategy to complete your missions.

Race against time
You are visiting your old friend Sara in Munich. You're having a wonderful time together. But on the third day of your visit, Sara suddenly disappears. You open her laptop and find a blackmail letter. What has happened? Is your friend in danger?

In this escape room card game, you have to show skill, creativity and teamwork to avert all dangers and rescue your friend. You have to solve puzzles, decipher codes and never lose sight of the big picture. An exciting escape adventure that takes you to the most popular sights of the Bavarian metropolis - through the picturesque old town, past the major construction project for the second S-Bahn main line to the Eisbachwelle.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Wettlauf gegen die Zeit
Du bist bei deiner alten Freundin Sara in München zu Besuch. Gemeinsam verbringt ihr eine wunderbare Zeit. Doch am dritten Tag deines Besuchs ist Sara plötzlich verschwunden. Auf ihrem geöffneten Laptop findest du ein Erpresserschreiben. Was ist passiert? Ist deine Freundin in Gefahr?

In diesem Escape-Room-Kartenspiel musst du Geschick, Kreativität und Teamwork beweisen, um alle Gefahren abzuwenden und deine Freundin zu retten. Es gilt, Rätsel zu lösen, Codes zu entziffern und dabei nie den Überblick zu verlieren. Ein spannendes Escape-Abenteuer, das dich zu den beliebtesten Sehenswürdigkeiten der bayerischen Metropole - durch die malerische Altstadt, vorbei am Großbauprojekt der zweiten S-Bahn-Stammstrecke bis zur Eisbachwelle - führt.

—description from the publisher

Save Germany's Lost Places
The lost place ‘Hotel Waldlust’ in the Black Forest has been blown up and an ominous letter reveals that further attacks will follow if you don't solve all the puzzles quickly enough to save the fascinating old buildings from destruction. According to the unknown sender, you will have to use your detective skills to defuse the bombs - which will definitely not be easy. In a race against time, a hunt begins throughout Germany. Only if you are clever enough can you find a way out and save the popular and incredible Lost Places. Are you ready?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Rettet Deutschlands Lost Places
Der Lost Place "Hotel Waldlust" im Schwarzwald wurde gesprengt und ein ominöser Brief verrät, dass weitere Angriffe folgen werden, wenn du nicht schnell genug alle Rätsel löst, um die faszinierenden alten Bauwerke vor der Zerstörung zu retten. Laut dem unbekannten Absender musst du Bomben mit detektivischem Fachwissen entschärfen - leicht wird das auf keinen Fall. In einem Wettlauf gegen die Zeit beginnt eine Jagd durch ganz Deutschland. Nur wenn du clever genug bist, kannst du einen Ausweg finden und die beliebten und unglaublichen Lost Places retten. Bist du bereit?

—description from the publisher

Firelock 198X is a tabletop wargame about nuclear-age fantasy warfare. It attempts to recreate the broad strokes of Cold War military tactics with unearthly troops. Its rules are meant to provide fast, lethal, authentic gameplay, supporting realistic tactics without clunky simulationism.

—description from the rulebook

boardgame: War is Our Homeland: Wargame Rules for Big Battles During the Age of the Tercio
description:
War is Our Homeland is a set of Historical miniature wargame rules that will allow you to play the big battles of the Thirty Years War, English Civil War, Dutch Revolt, The Deluge, and more. The game is meant for 2 or more players to be able to learn in a few minutes, play in an evening, without a ridiculous amount of miniatures, and do all of those things without sacrificing historical fidelity.


    Brand new players can finish a game in under 3 hours.
    Most scenarios can be played on a 6×4′ or 4×4′ table with 8-20 units per side.
    Players feel like the great leaders of the period such as Wallenstein or Mansfeld.
    4 Historical scenarios included, Mingolshiem 1622, Lutzen 1632, Zusmarshausen 1648, and Edgehill 1642.


-description from publisher's website

6 cards with value 11 that replace the original 11’s from the base game.

Each 11 has now 2 types of fruit. When you win a trick with such an 11, you have to decide as which type of fruit it will count.

boardgame: Eternal Mist: Cursed of the Forsaken Forest
description:
Eternal Mist: Curse of the Forsaken Forest is a 2 to 5 Co-op survival game.

Someone has released the seal that binds the accursed demon. Work together to navigate through the treacherous depths of the cursed forest and collect all three artifacts. Only when all three artifacts have reached the Shrine of Restoration card shall the Heroes will succeed in imprisoning the accursed demon known as Malivora, Queen of Eternal Night.

Each player can choose among 6 different heroes with special abilities. You need to work together as a group to maximize the potential of each role. One wrong move could be the end of the group.

Will you survive or be trapped in the cursed forest?

—description from the designer

Get 5 points at the end of a game of Tang Garden for the rare (unique, in fact!) Golden Cat. You can add this exciting, opportunistic decoration to any game mode.

Components:

     1 Golden Cat decoration card
     1 Golden Cat decoration

boardgame: The Legend of Korra: Pro-Bending Arena Deluxe Edition
description:
A Kickstarter Deluxe version of Legend of Korra: Pro-Bending Arena.

This version has significantly more content, with many more teams to choose from.

Exclusive, Limited Deluxe Edition Packaging. 
A limited edition championship promo-poster.
7 mystery expansion teams, totaling an additional 15 miniatures.
30 trick cards separated into 5 themed packs. Tricks are usable by all teams in the game.
30 punch-to-plastic upgrade components.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada
Players: Games with expansions that add solo play
TV Shows: Avatar The Last Airbender/Legend of Korra

description:
Rebus Mix is a party game in which players guess words, names and expressions by making collective rebuses. In each round, one player is designated "Rébulogue". He or she will have to put the drawings back in the right order and guess the meaning of the rebus made by the other players.

The aim varies according to the game mode chosen. 
There are 2 of them:

- Cooperative (between 4 and 7 players): the aim is to score as many points as possible by :
- Interpreting the rebus correctly if you are a "Rébulogue" 
- Drawing as clearly as possible if you are a draughtsman.

- Competitive (between 8 and 14 players): the aim is to find the rebus before the other team.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Old School Tactical: Volume 4 Bonus – Americans at Anzio
description:
This is a five scenario booklet that was developed as a stretch goal for the Old School Tactical Volume 4 kickstarter campaign. It represents US participation in the Battle for Anzio:

Scenario 1: Surprise Party - January 22, 1944
Scenario 2: Too Little, Too Late - January 31, 1944
Scenario 3: Two Bridges - February 15, 1944
Scenario 4: Fischfang - February 18, 1944
Scenario 5: Wet and Wild - March 27, 1944

Not that players must also own Old School Tactical Volume 2 to play these scenarios.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Old School Tactical (Flying Pig Games)

boardgame: Beyond Humanity: Astrominers – Mining Unions Game Expansion
description:
This game expansion is an expansion to the basic game. It is included as part of the Collector's Edition game or was available by itself as an add-on during the Kickstarter campaign.

This expansion includes Mining Crews where you can start fulfilling Corporate Permissions by preparing containers packed full of resources and haul them with you on a transport ship. This optional expansion makes the game more complex and rewarding for hardcore players.

This expansion contains a 55 card deck, several markers to tag action slots, and a four page Player's Manual

Crown & Consequences is a fast-paced, strategic card game where teams compete to empty their hands first, claiming victory and avoiding the hilarious, cringe-worthy consequences that await the losers.

Gameplay Overview:
Objective: The primary goal of Crowns & Consequences is to be the first team to empty your hand of cards, securing the crown and avoiding the consequences.

On Your Turn:
Play a Card Combination: The starting player will play a combination of cards from their hand—this can be singles, pairs, triples, or sequences. Play continues with players going around playing higher cards of the same combination. You can choose to pass or continue to play a higher combination. The player with the highest combination when everyone else passes takes control.

Take Control: If you win control, you get to lay a new combination from your hand of your choosing. Play then continues like previously. Strategic play is essential—each card combination played empties your hand and gets you closer to victory.

Use Action Cards: Beware the Action Cards! These special cards have the power to disrupt your opponents’ strategies, allowing you to discard cards, or give additional cards to your opponent. Be careful these can completely shake up the game dynamics.

Unleash Booms: Playing four of a kind activates a Boom, Booms can be played on any combo, and become the highest combo on the table. Only a higher boom can beat it. These are  game-changing events that can turn the tide in your favor or throw everyone into chaos. Timing is everything with Booms, as they can be the key to your victory.

Endgame: The game ends when one team successfully empties their hand. The victorious team claims the crown, while the losing team faces the dreaded Consequence Cards. These cards challenge the losing team with hilarious and sometimes embarrassing tasks, ensuring that no one escapes the game without a memorable experience.

Winning: To win in Crowns & Consequences, you must outplay your opponents, make the best use of your cards, and navigate the ever-present threat of Booms and Action Cards. But remember, the real fun is in the consequences—whether you win or lose, Crowns & Consequences guarantees an unforgettable game night filled with laughter and surprises.

-description from designer

Harbor of Blight is a standalone game that takes you on a campaign to unravel the mysteries of the exotic world of Blight. You start as a new member of the Hunter's Guild by creating a custom or premade character based on 4 professions, each with 2 unique specialty routes. You battle with a unique skillset that levels up with you from 1-10. Skills replenish via various means such as cooldowns or by sacrificing your Life Force. Your character may also persist into your next playthrough (and future games) with the New Game+ feature.

The core gameplay of each scenario includes a narrative prologue, followed by an Exploration Phase where you search the map via exploration tokens for loot, lay traps, other surprises and your target. The Combat Phase then begins in a fight with 1-3 enemies in a boss battler / dungeon crawler hybrid system. After successfully completing the Combat Phase you continue to the scenario epilogue, completion rewards, and the optional Social Phase in which you can level up friendship levels with your fellow NPCs and unlock other secrets. At the end of each scenario is also when you are allowed to shop with your Barter Points for new gear.

Harbor of Blight is designed for solo or 2 player co-op as an introduction to campaign and RPG gaming for players interested in the genre but don't know where to start, and for existing players to have a new world to explore without the need for finding a large group. The setup is fast via campaign and map books. The learning curve is slight and introduced over the first three scenarios. Embark on an unforgettable experience in Harbor of Blight's unique blend of gameplay, art and lore.

—description from the designer

Chemistry Set is a quick, low-complexity card game about building molecules out of atoms to complete experiments before your opponent. It is primarily a competitive game for two players, but can also be played as a solitaire game.

There are two main types of cards in this game - Atom Cards and Experiment Cards, which dictate how molecules are to be constructed. Players take one or two actions on their turn. Actions include playing a single Carbon / Oxygen / Nitrogen (C/O/N) Atom Card to start or continue the construction of a molecule, playing up to four Hydrogen Atom Cards adjacent to a single C/O/N Atom Card already in play, playing a Double Bond Card between adjacent C/O/N cards, and discarding any number of Atom Cards to immediately draw back up to a hand size of five. At the end of their turn, Atom Cards are drawn to a hand size of five.

Some flexibility is permitted as certain molecular rearrangements are allowed. However, players will also have to work with restrictions, such as only being allowed to build their own molecules and not their opponent's, a construction limit of two molecules at a time, and not being allowed to use two actions of the same turn on the same molecule.

When a player completes the molecule on an Experiment Card, they take that card and score the number of points listed on it. Neither player may complete that Experiment again for the rest of the game.

The endgame is triggered when a player has scored at least 10 points or when all experiments have been completed. The other player gets to take one last action. The player with the higher score wins!

Everyone's outrageous underpants are the top-secret target in this ridiculous and fast paced social deduction game.

In Top Secret Briefs it's a race to expose your fellow secret agents' embarrassing choice of underpants, before they expose yours ! Players place cards to gather information, whilst trying not to reveal too much about themselves, before making accusations in a high-speed chase to correctly reveal another player's briefs and win the game.

—description from the designer

Pitch Out - Under Vs Aquilies is a box containing new teams for the Pitch Out system, with unique playstyles to refresh strategies. Each Pitch Out box can be played separately (2 players) or combined (3 and 4 players).

It is a disk-flicking game with tactical elements in which players try to take out or isolate the opponent's leader.

The game is played with a bunch of disks (5-8 for the base sets) and five "walls". In turns, players place their pieces and two walls on their side of the field. Then, they start flicking one disk per turn, trying to push the opponent's disks out of the field.
Each disk has a special power, for example the Assassin just needs to touch an opponent's piece to take it out, so players need to play tactically.
The first player to take out the opponent's leader or all their other pieces wins the game.

—description from the publisher

New Greenland is a strategy war game where you control a country's military forces, competing with others to capture an unknown island and score the most points. The key to winning is through negotiation with other players, where you can form alliances, deceive, or even betray them to gain an advantage.

Each faction in the game has unique strengths, like nuclear power or air force, which can be used to threaten or help others during negotiations. While working with others is important, only one player can win, so you'll need to outmaneuver all your opponents to come out on top.

—description from the publisher (English)

New Greenland ist ein strategisches Kriegsspiel, bei dem du die militärischen Kräfte eines Landes steuerst und gegen andere antrittst, um eine unbekannte Insel zu erobern und die meisten Punkte zu sammeln. Der Schlüssel zum Sieg liegt in den Verhandlungen mit den anderen Spielern, bei denen du Allianzen schmieden, täuschen oder sogar verraten kannst, um dir einen Vorteil zu verschaffen.

Jede Fraktion im Spiel hat einzigartige Stärken, wie nukleare Macht oder Luftstreitkräfte, die in den Verhandlungen eingesetzt werden können, um andere zu bedrohen oder zu unterstützen. Obwohl Zusammenarbeit wichtig ist, kann nur ein Spieler gewinnen, daher musst du alle Gegner geschickt überlisten, um als Sieger hervorzugehen.

—description from the publisher (German)

language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: A set of 20 maze cards allow for infinite number of mazes to be created.  Players compete to find their characters' way home by marking it with the dry-erase pens, or play solo to complete the longest maze.

Explore the Dixit Universe with Dixit Universe Access+, an accesible variant of Dixit and Stella-Dixit. You can play alone or cooperative and choose the complexity, there are two levels. 
Use your voting cards to choose the 3 best matching cards for a word. 
This is part of the Access+-series for easy gaming for people with special needs.
—description from the publisher (translated from German)

The game contains 70 cards from different past Dixit sets, the card size is the same, but the card back is different because it has a special function in the game.

Dungeon Up is a card game in print & play format. Your quest for treasures and fame has led you to be captured in the depths of the dungeon. Now you will have to escape from the dungeon by climbing to the surface, collecting weapons, gems, treasures and potions to defeat the monsters on your way.

Our goal when we have escaped after ascending 12 rooms is to have earned as many victory points as possible. We get these points in the rooms we visit, by confronting bosses from level 1 to 4, in the treasures, by completing instant or majority missions at the end of the game or with character skills in its advanced mode.

Players will have to choose whether to take a room from the room market to add it to their dungeon, or confront a boss when they have the necessary equipment to do so. The rooms can be connected in two ways, choosing to confront one of the two monsters in our current room and getting potions, gems or treasures by entering through one of the entrances to the new room. Potions allow us to confront bosses more easily, steal treasures from rivals or get wild resources. The gems allow us to activate our hero's skills and the treasures will provide us with victory points and additional equipment.

Dungeon Up has a solo mode, where we must confront the final bosses, which we get some requirements. In this mode, the amount of cards in the deck are limited, we will have 3 cards in hand and every time we play 1 we must discard another. It is up to us to make the right decision to win.

-description from publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
In Cats & Dragons, players are cats leading their armies against hordes of dragons that infest their land. But these cats are devious and always out for themselves as well, scheming to take the glory and the spoils of every battle. Armies are deployed stealthily, hiding their strength from the other cat leaders. Will this lead to glory and victory, or will all this deviousness hand the victory to the invading dragons?

On each player's turn, they add a new tile to the landscape and possibly deploy new army units to this tile with their strength hidden from the other players. They may even hire the big wild cats to help them win the battle and best their peers. When a region closes, all army units are revealed, and players see whether they have defeated the dragons. If they did, the strongest armies get the most spoils, and all players earn glory. Spoils and glory are the path to victory over the other players.

Player choices in Cats & Dragons are relatively simple, but the board becomes very dynamic. Will you try to jump into a battle underway to earn spoils and glory? Or will you work hard to dominate a battle you know you can win and best all your rivals? The combination of blind bidding for area control and tile placement means that your interaction with the other players — as well as the dragons coming onto the board — will determine your fate.

-description from writer

Featuring New Cards and Mechanics:


     Blighted Forgeborn - Discover rare alternate Forgeborn, shadowed by the Blight, who wield incredible new powers!
     Legendary Monster 100+ Variations - A new legendary creature emerges with over 100 distinct artistic and mechanical twists!
     Exalted Creatures - When paired with exalted lands, these new creatures unleash astonishing powers!

FOOLS' FUEL is a semi-fantasy racing adventure for 1 to 4 players, filled with high-speed action, thrilling detours, and unexpected surprises at every turn.

In this game, each player takes control of a unique, heavily modified vehicle, competing in a chaotic race. The courses are constructed from modular track tiles, ensuring that every race offers a fresh and distinct experience. Throughout the game, players can purchase upgrades for their vehicles, attack rivals through ramming or using a wide variety of weapons, shift gears to gain speed, and take daring detours to fill their pockets with money.

The game ends when a player crosses the finish line, however, finishing the race first doesn’t guarantee victory. The ultimate winner is the player who amasses the most money throughout the race.

Scale a skyscraper and experience the thrills of free solo urban climbing on a rotating 3D board. Meticulously plan and coordinate your ascent and avoid being detained by security forces. Your climb is not just a test of physical prowess but also a strategic campaign to rally public support from onlookers below and above.

Free Solo is a tactical racing game for 2-6 players with a playtime of around 40 minutes. Each player controls a team of urban climbers, racing to the top of a skyscraper. To win the game, you must move all your climbers to the roof of the skyscraper whilst avoiding the police helicopters - the “Choppers”. The Choppers’ movement is woven into each move your climbers make. Your climbers work better if they climb in tandem as a team. Window squares offer your climbers respite from the looming threat of the Choppers whilst supporters can be deployed to propel your team to victory.

—description from the designer

Boulder Bluff is a fast-paced card game in a bouldering (climbing) theme, where climbers enter in a one-day competition to win a free trip around the world!

On your turn, you can either 'Prep' which means taking an extra Move card or 'Climb' which means attempting to climb one of the three Bouldering routes that are revealed, by placing your Move cards face-down in front of you, with at least one revealed.

Each player joining the climb then draws a random token from the chalk bag, which ranges from -1 to +2, and adds this to the power to climb the route.

The player with the highest power wins the bouldering route, and everyone (including the winner) who didn't have at least the number displayed on the bouldering route now has to take an injury token. This injury token is  -1 or -2 victory points at the end of the game (depeding on how you toss it).

But there's more! If you initiate a climb so confidently that nobody dares to join, you don't have to reveal your cards, and simply get the Boulder route without having to do anything! That's real Boulder Bluff right there..

Spice up your game by using 'pure colour' combos and using action cards at the correct time to maximize your power and hinder your opponents.

After the 15 bouldering routes have been climbed, the game is finished. The number on the bouldering route is the number of points you scored. Add up all your bouldering routes, minus your injuries, and the winner is the climber with the most points!

-description from designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Deep Sea Adventure, you and up to five other explorers are searching for riches under the sea, and while you're all rivals, you're also all poor, so you've pooled resources to rent a shared submarine...which means you're sharing the air supply too. If you don't return to the sub before air runs out, you have to drop everything in order to make it to safety.

In each of three rounds, players take turns rolling dice and moving their diver along a trail of face-down treasure tokens. The deeper you go, the more valuable the tokens are!

At the start of each turn, decide whether to dive deeper or head back toward the sub, lower the air supply, then roll the dice to move. On the space where you land, you can do nothing, pick up treasure (not looking at it and replacing it with an X token), or dropping off treasure in an X space.

Why would you drop the object of your dreams? Because you need to make it back to the sub in order to score your treasure collection, and for each object you hold, you subtract 1 from the movement dice. Pick up too much, and you might not move at all. Glub.

Deep Sea Adventure Boost features the same gameplay and components as Deep Sea Adventure, but it adds three new dice for a new way to play. These dice feature the digits 0 & 3, 1 & 3, and 1 & 2. When you roll, you now roll these three dice, then choose two of them to determine your movement, although you still subtract points for the treasure you hold.

What's more, at the start of your turn, you can decide to "boost" your movement by using the sum of all three dice — but doing so uses an addition unit of air. Will your need for speed leave everyone gasping?

(The original dice for Deep Sea Adventure are also included.)

Dungeon Up Below is the expansion of Dungeon Up. This expansion extends the experience with new heroes, enemies, goals, treasures, rooms and secret chambers.

In addition, it allows up to 6 players to play.

-description from publisher

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Iron Victory Crew
description:
Any coastal settlement fears the day that the Kraken’s banners are seen over the water’s horizon. But when it’s found out that they’re specifically the Iron Victory’s banners, things go from bad to worse.

The Iron Victory Crew unit box for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives House Greyjoy players a new melee-centric unit for their forces. These decently-defended raiders hit hard with their boarding axes, and get even more vicious when they’ve accrued a pair of Pillage tokens.

Contents:
12 Miniatures
1 Unit Card (For Each Language)
1 Movement Tray
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Crownland Scouts
description:
Knowledge is power, and having knowledge of enemy movements and formations can win a battle before it’s even begun. That’s why having reliable scouts is so important. For House Baratheon, they use the Crownland Scouts cavalry unit to perform this task.

The Crownland Scouts unit box for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives House Baratheon players of all alliances a new light cavalry unit for their forces. The unit is adept at ranging ahead, causing problems for enemy forces, as well as helping other friendly units reposition at key moments during the battle.

Contents:
4 Miniatures
1 Movement Tray
1 Unit Card per Language ( (EN, DE, FR, ES, PL, HU)
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Harma's Vanguard
description:
Many creatures dwell north of The Wall. However, horses are fairly rare in those frozen lands. As such, Harma’s Vanguard, a unit of horse-riding cavalry is a strange sight. However, these warriors give hope to other Free Folk units, bolstering their morale on the battlefield.

Harma’s Vanguard for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives Free Folk commanders a new cavalry unit for their forces. Though lightly armed and even more lightly armored, the unit boasts impressive speed. They also can bolster allies, purring them to shun enemy tricks and rouse their combat spirit.

Contents:
4 Miniatures (50mm Bases)
1 Unit Card per Language (EN, FR, PL, DE, ES, HU)
