description:
Vilnius is one of the few capitals of countries in the world, above which you can rise in a hot air balloon. Therefore, during the game Vilnius you will immerse yourself in a breathtaking adventure - you will become a balloon pilot yourself. "Flying" over the enchanting Vilnius, you will try to see as many unique places of the city as possible. But don't relax - an unfavorable wind can disrupt all plans, and clever competitors - make the path to victory difficult.

During the game, a die is rolled - its number determines the direction of the wind. The rolled die is placed on the "wind rose" on the board, which indicates which direction the players should move. However, each player has three action cards in their hands with which they can: change the direction of their balloon by 45 degrees; stop the movement of the balloon or speed it up by flying through 2 cells according to the direction of the wind; can restart or change the direction of the wind for all players by rolling the dice again. The first one to scatter rubies on the sights of Vilnius and finish at the tower of Gediminas Castle wins the game.

This is a promotional set of three mini-expansions, given out at SPIEL '23.

Asset Manager Of The Year
During the game setup, place the asset manager next to the discard pile. Whenever property cards are discarded, they are immediately moved face-up to the asset manager.

At the end of the game, after all cards in the betting office have been revealed, shuffle all cards on the asset manager into a facedown stack. Starting with the Don who triggered the end of the game, each Don (if possible) draws a card from the facedown stack and places it face down in front of them. In the same order, each Don secretly looks at one of these cards and either moves it into their safe or returns it and selects another card. The chosen card must then be moved into their safe.

Diamond Heist
Place the diamond heist during game setup next to the bank.

At the end of the game, after the owner of the key has taken the contents from the bank, all Dons count their $ 10K property cards (alcohol, weapons and money bags) in front of and in their safe. The Don with the most $ 10K property cards receives the diamond heist and may score $ 150K for it. In the case of a tie, the diamond heist is not scored and discarded instead.

Gambling Ring Busted!

Place the gambling ring during game setup underneath the draw pile at the very bottom.
Once the last card has been drawn from the draw pile, immediately pause the game (similar to an event card). The player who drew the last card flips over the gambling ring and reads its effect out loud.

Each player secretly plays one card from their hand face down. Then, reveal the cards. The player with the lowest unique ownership card moves all the cards played under their safe. If there is no winner, discard all cards played. Then continue the game as usual.

To play this optional mini-expansion, the Unfinished Business base game is required. The asset manager is compatible with all other expansions.

Bangarang in the Gutterlands is an easy to learn, small, fast and fun game of robot tabletop battle. Each player builds their single and individual miniature robot and controls it in a free for all skirmish competition. Set up in a science fiction environment the game is designed for a head-to-head competition as also suitable for a multiple player count.

The web-published book features all the rules to design and build your own robot (or to use the provided generator). You play in rounds using actions to move, to shoot and to bash in melee.

The skirmish game features different factions, lots of customization, fun upgrades and weapon combos. Gameplay provides easy movement rules including three dimensional movement as also light weight combat rules using RPG dice. The book also provides game aids like a 'Bot Sheet' and the 'Junk Bot Generator'.

The design philosophy is driven by the following concepts:


     creativity, artwork and some background story
     design your own miniatures
     create and build your own environment
     easy, fast and fun rules


The 42 page rulebook is available as a free digital download edition as also a print version via the game website. It includes game aids like reference sheets and wonderful artwork by the game designer himself.

2 Player memory based card game. Players race to find all the PEARS while simultaneously trying to sabotage each other. In this game all 52 cards are shuffled and set to constitute the game board.

Players then take turns turning over two of the cards trying their best to memorise them. when two identical cards are found they are removed from the game board and placed in the respective pear piles.

Some of these pears are worth more points than others and will impact the way the game is played, giving some players advantages when looking for pears.

Be careful you don't find the Rotten Pears.

The winner is the player with the most points at the end of the game.

This card game also doubles as premium deck of cards.

boardgame: Μέτρο Μέτρο στο Μετρό (Metro Metro in the Metro)
description: Travel by subway on the three wonderful routes (green red and blue). Combine fun and knowledge in a game you will never forget Use the technology of 2000 starting from the atmosphere of ancient Athens, cross the culture of the modern capital of Greece to end up at your table. The aim of the game is for the players to travel the three routes of the subway reaching the end of the line

Welcome to the neighborhood! Battle your neighbors to be the ultimate partier. Compete to gather an array of seasonal decorations and unique party essentials to win the favor of the Holiday Spirits.

Players roll resource dice to complete and claim cards representing holiday items. Resources include people, money, time, weather, and music. Each turn you will roll and assign the dice until you get your target card or run out of dice. But if you can’t place at least one die on the target card, you must send a die to the dice grave. Once the last item is claimed, the game ends. Each card has a value and sets give you a bonus. The player with the most points wins.

Add some holiday excitement by utilizing the Holiday Spirits to grant each player a unique power.

—description from designer

A pantomime game with two different ways to play. 
Teams: Make 2 groups, put a card on your head without looking at it and try to find more than the other team. 
Solo: For 2-6 players. One player guesses, all the others do pantomime

Attack the kingdom? Defend the kingdom? Why not both – in the same game?

You’re all governors, and while reports of a dragon approaching have been confirmed, you’ve still got a village to grow!

In this play-and-pass print-and-play, players play simultaneously on individual map sheets. You'll have two actions - choose to raise a tower (draw a circle) to defend the village, build a house near some towers (draw a square), and/or open a shop near some houses (draw a triangle). You can also attack the dragon from your towers in range by rolling a die. When you're done, score points on your scoresheet, then pass your sheet clockwise and move to a different section of your kingdom.

On one sheet, you’re the dragon instead! As the dragon, use your two actions to move or attack the buildings everyone else built to score points.

The game ends when a dragon has 0 health remaining or when a dragon has destroyed enough buildings (based on player count). Count up your points, both from creating and destroying, and the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

Enter a whimsical world of Pixies! In Pixie Trails, you play as a Pixie foraging for snacks, collecting feathers, building friendships and playing with charms. Pixies are playful albeit an innately competitive species. Will you emerge the smartest Pixie with the most snacks or the social butterfly with the most Friendship accumulated?

On your turn, play direction cards face down simultaneously with other players and reveal to move your pixie around. Take actions at your location depending on the number of pixies at that location. Collecting Charms grant you unique powers to use to your advantage. Collect the most amount of Food during the four rounds of play to win the game. Or be the first Pixie to collect 7 Friendship to snatch an instant victory!

Outwit other players to seize the win in this cute but strategic game!

—description from the designer

“Bunker Down!” is a survival game for 3-4 players set in a fantasy world where towns of peasants must survive against the Dark Army’s attacks, where even the smallest mistake can lead to ruin. As the Dark Army grows and resources dwindle, players will have to make tough decisions on whether to be collaborative or competitive to ensure their own survival.

Each of the games 10 rounds represents a day with the players sole objective being to survive 10 nights of raids. Players draft resource cards from a central deck of 160 cards which can may run out early forcing players to survive on reserves.  As a result, players must manage their resources between building up their defenses represented by the Army units and Fortifications they have built or sustaining their towns morale represented by the town’s Hunger and Upkeep meters. Each town will have to do whatever is necessary with regards to their neighbours... spend gold to hire couriers to lend aid while being ever-prepared to use that same gold to hire bandits when you need to backstab and steal! But players must never believe that they are safe as each night brings random events drawn from the central event deck which will end each round by triggering potentially game-altering miracles.... or catastrophes.

-description from publisher

Rescate Animal is a Chilean card game set collection and push your luck, in which you must rescue animals in different levels of preservation of the Chilean fauna. To do so, you must discover, preserve and conserve different species in order to obtain preservation points and help the local fauna. 
The board game is played in three phases 

     Preserve: In this phase you can rescue only once animals that you have in your ecosystem, but they must be part of the same collection of animals.
     Discover: You can do one of these two actions.
         From the central deck you reveal cards one by one but be careful if you have 3 of the same type or 5 of different types, the animals will run and fall in a danger zone.
         Preserve: Here you can rescue animals from the danger zone using your preservation points.
    
     Preserve: If you didn't do it in point 1 you can do it now.


Reaching 15 points triggers the end of the round and finally a round of Preservation only.

Come and help us to take care of the Chilean fauna.

Whiskers and Fins is a 2-4+ player deduction game that challenges players to strategically uncover their opponents' fish while avoiding their mischievous cats. Each player has a set of 25 cards (13 fish and 12 cats) and 6 tokens, aiming to collect the most fish by the end of the game.

Players arrange their 25 cards face down in a 5x5 grid, creating a continuous path of fish that must be logically deduced by opponents. On your turn, you flip over at least two cards to find fish. Revealing a fish gives clues about where the next fish might be since all fish must be connected. You can keep flipping to uncover more, but if you reveal a cat after your second pick, your turn ends, and you lose a fish.

The core of the game is logic and deduction, not just guessing. As fish and cats are revealed, players gather crucial information to map out the likely positions of the remaining fish. By analyzing the placement of revealed cards and applying deduction, you can make informed choices that increase your chances of success while minimizing the risk of hitting a cat.

Players are sidelined after revealing 6 cats, and the game continues until all players are sidelined or no more valid moves remain. The player with the most fish at the end wins, making every deduction and decision vital in your pursuit to outsmart your opponents and become the ultimate fisher in Whiskers and Fins!

—description from designer

Jogo Exploradores is a board game where the objective is to create exploratory campaigns, each with an initial cost. Players collect numerical cards ranging from 2 to 10, and any campaign that accumulates more than 20 points starts generating profit. Special cards, such as Funding and Great Treasure, are aimed at maximizing this profit.

The campaigns begin with an archaeological excavation and progress through scientific environments until they reach international media, culminating in fame, prestige, and fortune. In this highly competitive environment, setbacks like blockades or even earthquakes can occur, potentially jeopardizing your campaign. To protect themselves, players must obtain the Protection card, and to gain more points, the Federal Certificate card.

The player with the most points at the end is the winner.

—description from the designer

Rate My Friends is the hilarious game of rating yourself – and figuring out how your friends rate themselves!

Rate: Each round, one player grabs the RATING DIAL and rates themselves on two different attributes, each on a scale of 1-10.

Guess: Once the rating is locked in, the rest of the players try to guess what the total number is for the two attributes.

Score: The numbers on the Dial are revealed, and players score points for matching (or being closest to) the combined number.

Win: The first player to score 7 points is the WINNER!

In this math education game by WLW Ent., players have to compete placing down 9 cards onto their mat to achieve a product of composite numbers drawn from a deck.

One side of the mat requires the player to come up with 3 multiplication problems to arrive at the solution of their drawn composite number.

The other side of the mat requires the player to come up with 2 multiplication problems, and 1 mixed problem involving the sum of 1 number and a product to arrive at the solution of their drawn composite number.

—user summary

In this math education game by WLW Ent., players have to compete placing down 7 cards onto their mat to achieve a sum of 1 in 3 different ways.

One side of the mat requires the player to come up with 3 addition problems to arrive at the solution of 1.

The other side of the mat requires the player to come up with 1 addition problem, 1 reciprocal-only multiplication problem, and 1 subtraction problem to arrive at the solution of 1.

—user summary

In this math education game by WLW Ent., players have to compete placing down 9 cards onto their mat to achieve a sum of 10 in 3 different ways.

One side of the mat requires the player to come up with 2 addition problems and 1 subtraction problem to arrive at the solution of 10.

The other side of the mat requires the player to come up with 1 addition problem, 1 subtraction problem, and 1 mixed problem where a sum is being subtracted to arrive at the solution of 10.

—user summary

Be the first player to play all of your cards. The winner is the player with the lowest score at the end of the game. Each player may play cards from their Board and/or from their Hand when it is their turn. Alternatively, a Mystery Card not covered by a Board Card can be played. When a player turns over a Mystery Card, it MUST be played.

Play starts with the player selected to start the round. That player plays any quantity of matching cards from their Hand and/or their Board into the Center Pile. Any matching number card(s) or a SWOOP Card can be played to start the game. Play then proceeds to the next player. The next player can play any quantity of matching cards with a number value equal to or less than the card(s) last played into the Center Pile.

A SWOOP occurs when 4 or more of the same numbered cards are played into the Center Pile by a single player or consecutive players. Upon a SWOOP, the entire Center Pile is moved from the center of the table and the player's turn continues. As at the start of their turn, the player can again play any quantity of matching Hand and/or Board Cards, an uncovered Mystery Card, or a SWOOP Card. A player must take the entire Center Pile if they are unable to play a card equal to or lower in value to the last card played in the Center Pile or a SWOOP Card. The round is over when a player has no more cards remaining in their Hand, on their Board, or Mystery Cards

A game starts with each player having a score of zero (0) points. For each round, the player who goes out gets zero (0) points. Each of the other players total the points of the cards remaining in their Hand, on their Board, and their Mystery Cards using the point values printed in black text on each card. These point totals get added to each player’s running total score. Number cards 1-9 are 5 points, 10-12 are 10 points, and SWOOP cards are 50 points.

—description from publisher

Vestiges of the Ancients is an immersive board game set in a world where ancient mechanisms hold the key to survival. Players take on the role of wizards exploring mysterious locations, including the Vaelstrom Castle, in search of powerful artifacts and knowledge. The gameplay combines elements of role-playing, puzzle-solving, and strategy as players interact with the environment, activate ancient mechanisms, and battle enemies that move autonomously.The game offers modular components, such as traps and devices, that players can physically activate to alter the course of the game. With a mix of cards, spells, and evolving challenges, each playthrough offers new interactions and outcomes. The goal is to uncover the secrets of the ancient mechanisms while defeating enemies and unlocking hidden paths, all while adapting your strategy as your wizard's powers evolve. Objective: Explore, solve puzzles, activate mechanisms, and defeat enemies to unlock new areas and progress through the story, ultimately mastering the ancient forces.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Brass Birmingham Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Brass: Birmingham.

To play this realm you use dice to mark checkboxes on routes. Canals can only be checked in the first half of the game, and railroads in the second half. You score resources and stars for connected cities. Can you build the best network?

—description from the publisher

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Genotype.

To play this realm you fill die symbols in the headers of three Punnett squares. You score intersecting stars and benefits. Can you discover the 6-star genotype?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Stamp Swap Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Stamp Swap.

To play this realm you use dice to draw stamps in your collector grid. Your stamp depends on whether you use the high or low rolled die. Can you optimally fill your grid to earn 6 stars?

—description from the publisher

Between the 9th and 17th centuries, trade in the Mediterranean was characterized by the presence of the Maritime Republics, Italian port cities that, thanks to their commercial influence, were able not only to prosper but also to maintain a certain political autonomy. 
Among them, the oldest is the Duchy of Amalfi, which reached its peak between the 9th and 10th centuries.

Amalfi is a competitive game in which players, taking on the role of the Dukes of Amalfi, must distribute their fleet among the ports of the Mediterranean. 
At the end of each of the 5 rounds, players who have deployed the most ships in a single port earn a doubloon. But beware: anyone familiar with the sea knows that you must always reckon with the wind, which can change at any moment. In fact, during each round of the game, only the cards associated with the "active" winds are in effect.
Additionally, only one player per round has the ability to control the winds, so pay close attention to the cards other players play face up, and try to anticipate the ones that will be played face down. This way, you'll be able to better plan your strategy, accumulate more wealth than anyone else, and become the most powerful merchant in Amalfi!

—description from the publisher

A game of BONO starts with each player being dealt 7 cards, the remaining stack is placed on the table (reverse facing up), and top card is turned.

The player to go first, puts down a card in same colour or with same number (or a special card) as the card on top of the stack. The player will then have to say what's pictured on the card he/she/they put down.

If the player doesn't know the word, is unsure or says the wrong word, card could be scanned with the (absolutely free) app, to get the correct word - with sound for pronunciation! Player will then have to take back the card and memorize the word, plus an extra card from the stack - and pass the turn to the next player around the table. Next time the player gets to play, hopefully he/she/they has memorized the card/word from last round effectively and can say a word they have now just learned!

The winner is the first player to get rid of all cards.

BONO comes in a number of different themes, takes 3 minutes to learn, and either you win or you lose: All the players end up having learned - and said - plenty of new words!

The main rule in BONO: Say It to Play It!

—description from the publisher

That's My Cat is an innovative card game that invites players to compete in a playful battle of feline charm. By strategically stacking transparent cards over their cat cards, players enhance their cat's traits—cuteness, playfulness, laziness, and sneakiness—to score points and outsmart their opponents.
29 new cards and rules. These cards does not change the game in other ways that you learn more about Friedrich Ebert and his time. And of course the game take a little bit longer to play to the end.

In this board game you will use your cards, tokens, and wit to manipulate your way into buying all the happiness keys and winning the game.
Split up couples, find a sugar daddy, steal someone’s career, earn money, make someone marry you.  In this game you do what it takes to be the first to collect all the happiness and win!

How to play and win:
At the start of the game players are given a status card to see if they are married to someone at the table or single.  Then through the cards in their hand they use the cards to earn money or attack people to make them lose their money.  Everyone is trying to earn tokens (money) to buy happiness keys.  HEALTH, CAREER, HOUSE, FAMILY, FRIENDS, MONEY.  The first married couple or single person to have all six of these keys wins the games.
The catch is, throughout the game people can steal your keys, divorce a happy married couple, force someone to marry them and combine assets, etc.  The cards drive the game as long as wit and manipulation.

—description from the designer

boardgame: CHEF! The Most Un-Balanced Card Game
description:
It is the most Un-Balanced card game. You must complete your plate before your opponents bother you, impede or steal you!

CHEF! is an hilarious card game in which to win you must be the first to prepare a balanced dish. Use your wits to avoid rival´s attacks and sabotages. Do not be too confident since in this kitchen nothing is what it seems to be...

¡Chef! is a fun card game for up to 5 players who want to enjoy wild battles lasting about 30 minutes.
 To help Chef Flo, you need to gather the four ingredients from each group, and once you’ve got them, you’ll need to cook the dish to win the game. Use every resource at your disposal to be the first to finish your dish. But beware—nothing in this kitchen is what it seems! Protect yourself from your rivals while challenging them with annoying cards that will make them waste time.

The way to play is simple but very addictive.

On each turn you must draw cards until you have 7 in your hand.

Once done, you will only have to execute one of the actions that you have available, or that you want to do. The actions to be carried out are many, attack, steal, disturb, protect,...

If you don't do anything, you only discard one card or more depending on what you can. and to continue.

It is a fast, dynamic and above all very competitive game with friends to know who to attack and how to defend yourself.

-description from developer

O-Zone is an exciting team dice game combining skill and luck.

Any flat surface becomes the playing area. Just place out the targets and foul lines, then let the fun begin!

Players toss dice toward the targets. They score points in three ways--by landing on the targets, being closest to a target, or by being closest to the far edge of the playing area.

The team game offers plenty of high-five moments and the occasional chorus of groans. The first player or team to 100 points wins!

—description from the designer

Good evening! Welcome to La Ruina!

"La Ruina" is a hugely successful podcast in Spain, and now it has its own board game. Tonight, thanks to this game, you're going to be part of the show. We want to hear your most pathetically funny story: that date that went wrong, the time you threw up during a work meeting, or that night you thought a taxi driver had kidnapped you. Don't be shy, because in *La Ruina*, misery wins prizes.

In La Ruina, each player receives 3 scoring cards numbered 1 to 3, and a Chair card with a letter. The Concept deck is shuffled, and 4 cards are revealed to inspire the stories. Players who wish to participate place their Chair card in the game box. The Chair cards are shuffled, and the first card revealed indicates who will be the first to share their "Ruina", followed by the others. Only three people will share their stories, even if more Chair cards are in the box.

After hearing the three stories, everyone votes by giving a Scoring card to those who shared their anecdotes, without being able to vote for themselves. The player with the most points wins the game. If there's a tie, it’s simply accepted. After the voting, another round can be played to let others share their Ruinas.

Laughter is guaranteed!

—description from the publisher

¡Buenas noches! ¡Bienvenidos y bienvenidas a La Ruina!

Hoy, gracias a este juego, vais a formar parte del show. Queremos vuestra anécdota más patético-divertida: esa cita que salió mal, la vez que vomitasteis en una reunión de trabajo o aquella noche en que creísteis que os secuestró un taxista. No tengáis vergüenza, porque en La Ruina la miseria tiene premio.

En *La Ruina*, cada jugador recibe 3 cartas de Puntuación numeradas y una carta de Silla con una letra. Se baraja el mazo de Conceptos y se revelan 4 cartas que servirán de inspiración para las anécdotas. Los jugadores que deseen participar colocan su carta de Silla en la caja del juego. Se barajan las cartas de Silla, y la primera carta revelada indica quién será el primero en contar su "Ruina", seguida de las demás personas. Solo tres personas narrarán sus anécdotas, aunque haya más cartas en la caja.

Después de escuchar las tres historias, todos votan entregando una carta de Puntuación a quienes contaron sus anécdotas, sin poder votar por uno mismo. El jugador con más puntos gana la partida. Si hay un empate, simplemente se acepta. Tras la votación, se puede jugar otra partida para que otros jugadores compartan sus Ruinas.

¡Las risas están aseguradas!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Före eller efter? is a quiz game. One players says a number between 1 and 6. Another opponent do then read about an event on a card. After that the opponent draws another card and read about an event on the second card. The player should now guess if the second event did happen before or after the first one when it comes to chronological order. If the player guess right the player can keep the first card or get another event read aloud, and take a similar guess. The player can continue like this as long as the player want, and gets one card for each right guess when the player choose to stop. But if the player answer wrong the player gets no card at all.

The first player to reach 20 cards is the winner.

Blizzard Blitz is a fast-paced card game of toboggan racing. Outmaneuver rivals and race to victory in this frosty showdown!

In Blizzard Blitz, you and your friends are brave toboggan racers hurtling down a treacherous, snow-covered track. But it’s not just about speed; strategy, snowballs, and sneaky maneuvers are essential to your success as you race to the finish line. Outmaneuver your opponents, unleash snowball attacks, and collect powerful boosts to claim victory!

How It Plays
Each game of Blizzard Blitz takes you through a series of quick, intense turns where you have to push your luck. Draw cards from the Push deck; each card drawn brings you one space closer to the finish line, but if you draw two (or more) hazard cards, you wipe out and don't move at all!

Dragonlings is a family-friendly card game where you play as a Dragon Trainer, competing to grow your dragon herd and amass the largest treasure hoard! It is for 1-6 players, ages 7+, with options for solo play and teams.

Players start by hatching eggs into Level 1 Hatchlings, and then stack Dragon cards to create Towers, with the goal of becoming Level 5 legendary Dragons. As Dragons grow, they learn abilities, which chain together to do amazing things! Treasures, Towers, Eggs, and Trainer bonuses are all worth victory points, so there are lots of ways to win!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Andromeda's Edge: Exotic Matter Promo Kit
description:
Seek out new life in Exotic Matter, the promotional pack for Andromeda’s Edge. Contents of this pack were all originally offered on the gamefound campaign and bundled in this retail edition.

Discover three exciting new factions: the Astrodrakus, Drakanaran Flamekeepers and Wolfguardian Howlers! Get ready to battle deadly new raiders: the Hive full of Hornets, and the Ancient Brain. Build new modules in the flexible Metamorphosis, double-damaged Obliterated and risky Explosive varieties!

Astrodrakus and Drakanoran Flamekeepers are illustrated by guest artist Sandara Tang.

—description from the publisher

Steel Psalm is an old-school, d20-friendly rules of MÖRKBORG and dip them headfirst into post-apocalyptic, mechanical, MEK-KNIGHT mayhem, drawing inspiration from robot-on-robot combat, doom metal aesthetics, and more. Fight over the scraps of what little remains in an easy-to-learn campaign setting that puts aesthetics and mood before rules and crunch.

STEEL PSALM gives you command of a FULL UNIT of MEK-KNIGHTS in a minis-agnostic skirmish game. Your unit will join (or fight) other Mek-knights, fend off invasion by unspeakable horrors from the undercooked realms of the universe's expanding edge, or simply loot and plunder those who've failed to succumb to extortion to one of the four great hegemonies.

This stand alone game includes all the cards from Timeline: Classic, Timeline: Events, and Timeline: Inventions. The game can also be used as an expansion for Bezzerwizzer Timeline or any of the other Timeline games for extra variety.

boardgame: Mad Dogs, Mughals, and Marathas:  Volume I – The Mughal-Maratha Wars
description:
Large format all colour scenarios for Mad Dogs & Englishmen: India. 36 scenarios, maps, suggested orbats.

—description from the publisher

Centerpede is a simple game with a slinky movement mechanic and potential for deep strategy. Centerpedes, an Escherian sort of creature, unfold themselves as the game progresses, growing larger and larger to attain more freedom of movement and constricting power to defeat the enemy colony. Joining them are insects called "Workers" that freeload off the Centerpede storehouses in times of peace but are put on the frontline during battle. Centerpede is a perfectly cute game for kids and adults alike. The rules are simple... well... for a chess variant, and the mechanics inspire the imagination. Corey L. Clark designed Centerpede in a particularly productive late summer of 2024. A game of Centerpede cannot end in a draw.

Materials: a 169 cell hex hex board (side 8), chess queens,  2 rooks, and 2 knights (depicted as 4 rooks here due to lack of limitation on materials in virtual space) in two colors representing the Centerpedes, and 6 pawns representing the Workers. Set them up according to the game image. You will also need about 30 pieces in two colors like stones for the bodies of the Centerpedes.

Gameplay: Players draw ten stones to begin the game, these are the initial reserves the Centerpedes may be grown with. White makes the first move, a single action, from then  on turns alternate with any two actions per turn (whether moving 1 cell, growing a Centerpede or removing an immobile enemy Centerpede or Worker). Workers may spend an action to progress 1 cell in any of the three forward directions. Centerpedes start out as just single pieces and begin their lives with the same forward movement as the Worker pawns. However Centerpedes can obtain a lot more degrees of movement and wrap themselves around enemy pieces to capture them.

Growing Centerpedes: When a Centerpede grows, it consists of a  chess piece head and a stone tail. You may grow any Centerpede initially by appending a tail to the head, then each additional stone added must only contact the previous tail of that particular Centerpede, no other stones or the head, nor any part of any other Centerpede.

Moving Centerpedes: This is where things get real fun! A centerpede moves by projecting its head in any direction, so it is completely disconnected from its body, then the tail takes the place of where the head was previously linking it back to its body. The move is legal if either the head or the tail were moved forward in this process and as long as the Centerpede is not contacting any friendly Centerpedes; contacting Workers is allowed.

Capturing Centerpedes and Workers: If any color Centerpede or Worker is stuck and cannot move anywhere (even though its your turn) you may spend an action removing it from the board (even your own!). After you remove an enemy Centerpede or Worker you get a store of stones in your color equal to the number of pieces captured (chess pieces and stones), if you capture one of your own centerpedes or workers, you must reimburse your opponent likewise.

Game End: You win Centerpede either by capturing the enemy Queen or by occupying the enemy Queen's home with your Queen, The point at the end of the board.

—description from the designer

Sandpile Panic, also known as Sandpile, is an abstract strategy game based off the abelian sandpile model that involves making piles of pieces. When these piles get too tall, they topple over and cover the area around them. The goal of the game is to cover the board with the largest contiguous area of your color -- and the game ends when a piece topples over the edge of the board.

Sandpile Panic may be played on any grid board with solid-colored stacking objects (such as checkers pieces or building blocks). No specialized pieces are otherwise required. The grid may be as large or as small as desired to affect the length of the game or to accommodate up to 8 players.

—description from the publisher

A modern skirmish wargame in a techno-punk fantasy style will transport you to a unique techno-punk fantasy world. This game combines elements of science fiction, magic, and a post-apocalyptic world to create an immersive gaming experience. If you like the battles in Heroes of Sword and Magic, Disaples, King's Bounty, if you want fast-paced combat and large beautiful miniatures, then you will love this game!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Современный скирмиш-варгейм в стиле фэнтези-техно-панк перенесет вас в уникальный мир техно-панк фэнтези. Эта игра сочетает в себе элементы научной фантастики, магии и постапокалиптического мира, создавая захватывающий игровой опыт. Если вам нравятся битвы в Героях меча и магии, Дисайплз, Кингз баунти, если вы хотите быстрых схваток и крупных красивых миниатюр, то вам обязательно понравится эта игра!

—description from the publisher

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Felix der Kater auf der Weltreise
description:
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The fists to reach space 90 wins the game.

The Game was published as a supplement in a chocolate box between 1922 and 1937, printed by CaWar Halle (Verlag Carl Warnecke).

—user summary

A roll&write take on classic travelling games, A Tour of the Realm sees two to four players compete to visit all locations on a map and make it back to the start.

Each turn, you first roll for possible moves and then reveal your possibilities to other players one by one, committing specific values to routes to use or hinder others.

—description from the designer

Each player receives a pawn, which they place on the starting position of the same color. The first player to roll a 6 begins to march in the direction of the arrow according to the respective dice result. For each fitness space, they must perform the exercise indicated there 5 times. As a reward, they receive a point chip, which they place on one of the circles of their large F. If their physical condition does not allow it, they may not perform the respective exercise. No point chip is given for this. The first player to fill their F completely and thus form the word "Fit" wins.
For each question space, each player must answer a health question. If the answer is correct, the player receives a point chip, which they place on their F. Any game penalties are indicated on the question cards.

—user summary

When was the last time you looked under the bed? You'd be surprised what oddities you find there. Monsters, Wraiths or a stinging moustache? The only winner in this game is the first one to fall asleep with no fear, but be careful because you can easily get in each other's way.

In Mumusok, a player can draw as many cards as he wants in his turn to get sleep points and advance on the scoreboard. But if he encounters 3 Mumus from the deck he will be caught and lose the points he scored in that round. However, the deck also contains action cards and event cards that you can use to either advance yourself or to advance your opponent.

The scoreboard consists of 20 pillows, but the colours of the pillows have different properties that either make it harder or easier for the player to advance in that round.

—description from designer

This is a single stand-alone case in the Suspects series based in Liverpool, during the English Football League.

Claire Harper was born in 1910. She is an avid reader of Agatha Christie, a world traveller, and more British than 5 o’clock tea. Oxford University admitted her after implementing quotas for female students in 1927. She followed in her father’s footsteps and graduated with the key to unlocking her dreams: a degree in criminal law. Now Claire Harper investigates, observes, discovers, and thwarts. There’s no mystery that is too twisted for her brilliant mind, no case that’s too hard for her to crack. Claire Harper isn’t just a detective. She’s the very best.

In each case of Suspects, you will read Clue cards to progress through the plot like a book, discuss and reason amongst yourselves, and form hypotheses. Your goal is to score the most points when you answer the questions about the case. The "faster" you answer (i.e. having drawn and read as few Clue cards as possible), the more points you will score.

Richter the Seventh is dead…

…And as is tradition, the six Goddesses of Aetherica have summoned champions across the continent to battle for his empty throne.

Warriors, vanguards, spellstrikes and even royal sovereigns from Aetherica’s Six Realms have answered the call of battle and are prepared to fight until only one is left standing.

Who will best the competition, claim the title of “The Seventh” and become Aetherica’s next Almighty ruler?

Steel yourselves… the Battle Requiem has begun!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Trails of Tucana with Ferry Expansion
description:
Trails of Tucana is a flip-and-write game from the creators of Avenue and Doodle City that is quick and easy to play, but has enough depth to entertain gamers.

This new edition includes the Ferry expansion, which adds two new island maps: Isla Bahía and Isla Habita. On both maps, ferries will help players cross the bays.
On Isla Habita, 3 additional villages provide new challenges and opportunities.

Each player is given a map of the island Tucana, showing its villages and important sights. The positions of the villages are randomized for each player, so every game will provide a unique puzzle.

Each turn, one player flips over two terrain cards. Each player must draw — on their own map — a trail between two neighboring spaces of the shown terrains. Gradually, the trails will grow into a network of roads. Players score points by connecting matching harbors, and by connecting sights to harbors. Being the first to connect a pair of harbors provides bonus points.

To add depth to the game, there are varying distribution of the different terrain cards. Mountain cards are for instance rarer than desert cards, so it would be harder to build a trail through a mountain range or over water than through desert or forest. Players need to take this into account when planning their routes. And they should maximize the probability that they will be able to make use of any combination of terrain cards that may come up.

—description from designer

TimemiT is a board game for two players where you will immerse yourself in an epic time battle in which the fate of humanity is at stake.

Choose who you want to be: Nora, an engineer determined to save the world, or Aron, an AI intent on destroying it. As you engage in an intense race against time, you'll need to wisely use Kronos, energy packs that alter space-time and form small wormholes.

The game unfolds in two phases: Combat and Bidding. During the Combat phase, both players reveal cards to move the ship on the time board, but be careful—revealing the same symbol three times may trigger a time paradox. Then, during the Bidding phase, Kronos cards are used to bid and obtain power cards and time events that can change the course of the game.

Use your Kronos Cards to obtain Power Cards and build a deck to defeat your opponent. Move the Ship forward on the Time Board until you reach the other side. Nora must go to the past, to 1980, and Aron to the future, to 2220. If neither player has reached the end of the Board when all the Event Cards have been used, the winner will be the one who has progressed the farthest. But beware! If your character sheet contains 6 Paradox Triangles, you will land in a parallel dimension and immediately lose the game.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the land of Oz! Get ready to have thean amazafying time in the Monopoly Wicked Edition game! Play as a student at Shiz University, where you receive the very best education and make outstandiful friends. But there’s a whisper of bad things happening in Oz. Gain the Wizard’s invitation by acquiring locations, transports, and commodities throughout the land—and help the animals and fellow Ozians! The more a player owns, the more Ozian coins they can collect from other players. The last one with money when all others have gone bankrupt wins!

BASED ON THE WICKED MOVIE: Explore the amazafying land of Oz! The Monopoly Wicked Edition game combines classic Monopoly gameplay with art, storylines, and characters from the Wicked film
6 THEMED GOLDEN TOKENS: Includes 6 golden zinc Monopoly tokens: Elphaba’s hat, Elphaba’s Broom, Elphaba’s Bottle, Glinda’s Crown, Glinda’s Trunks, and The Grimmerie
AMAZAFYING LOCATIONS FROM THE MOVIE: The board features properties from the land of Oz—from Shiz University to Emerald City! The more a player owns, the more Ozian coins they can collect. The last player with coins when all other have gone bankrupt wins
MUNCHKIN HOMES AND EMERALD CASTLES: In this Monopoly game, Munchkin Homes and Emerald Castles replace houses and hotels. Players who own them can collect more Ozian coins
DEFY GRAVITY AND TOTALLY OZMOPOLITAN CARDS: “Rejoicify! Madame Morrible gives you a training wand. Collect 20 Ozian coins.” These cards, featuring moments from the film, can bring good—or something bad
THRILLIFY FAMILY GAME NIGHT: Add some magic to game nights, vacations, and more! This indoor game for kids and adults is a fun family board game for ages 8 and up
FANTABULOUS GIFT FOR WICKED FANS: Stunningly designed with fan-favorite details, this Wicked-themed Monopoly board game is an ideal gift for kids and adults who love the Wicked movie and the Monopoly games

A thrilling game of balance and dexterity where players try to stack edamame without spilling the bowl!

Each turn, roll the selection die to see which edamame will be stacked. There are different configurations of shape and weight for each bean. Then try to place the selected edamame on the steamer bowl. If a player successfully places their piece, pass the turn to the next player. But be careful, if the steamer bowl topples, you've lost the game!

A game similar to Clue, one card from each of the suspect, target, weapon, location, and motive decks is drawn to form the Murder Plot that players must discover. Then the remainder of the decks are shuffled with the action cards and players dealt three cards. Players take turns drawing cards and using actions to look at other players' hands, trying to find a match for one of their cards (each card type only has two copies), if not, the card is placed next to the draw pile. When players think they've deduced the Murder Plot, they write down their suspicions and hand them to the dealer to verify. The person who correctly guessed saves the day.

In many trick-taking games, you're dealt a random hand and then bid on how many tricks you think will take with that hand. In Draught Trick, you are assigned a specific bid (different for each player), and you need to draft cards into your hand to achieve your bid exactly.

Serve your table of fantasy creatures the exact right number of drinks (i.e. win the correct number of tricks) to win!

—description from the designer

The sun is shining, the air is crisp, and the festival you've been waiting for is finally here. But just as you’re about to dive into the day’s celebrations, a mischievous creature grabs your attention, begging for your help in a quest for a lost treasure. Strange happenings swirl around you, and you can’t shake the feeling that something magical is at play. Will you follow the clues, face the dangers ahead, and claim the gold hidden at the end of the rainbow before time runs out?

The Leprechaun Larceny, title #10 in the Holiday Hijinks line, is an escape room-style game in just 18 cards! As a group, you will solve a series of puzzles to unravel a mystery. Puzzles may test your perception, wordplay, and lateral thinking. English language fluency is required.

When you have solved a puzzle on the cards, you will enter the answer into the companion website (compatible with all smart phones and computers) and it will check whether you are correct. If you are, the website will tell you which card or cards to take next. The website also contains a graduated hint system, so if you get stuck you can get just a small hint about the current puzzle to get moving again without giving too much away.

This system is non-destructible: some groups may choose to write on cards, but this is not required. A group cannot re-play the game, but can pass it on to another group for their use. This entry in the series takes about an hour to solve and is rated 2 out of 3 in difficulty.

boardgame: Holiday Hijinks #11: The New Year Nightmare
description:
It's the cusp of a new year and you find yourself in Prof Timely‘s manor. You never thought you'd return after your February adventure, but it’s funny how time has a way of repeating itself. With the clock ticking down to midnight and the professor's experiments going haywire, you prepare yourself for the calamity to come. Only your New Year resolve will ensure a bright future, instead of dwelling in the past…

Join forces with the Krampus as he steps up to the plate and keeps the North Pole operation running. If all goes well, nobody will be the wiser!

Pantheon is a simultaneous deck builder for two players by Good Nerd Bad Nerd Games.

Pick your Pantheon, collect FOLLOWERS and ARTIFACTS, use ACTIONS and special DEITY powers, all to gain the most FAVOR among the people during the year. During the SUMMER season, FAVOR is tallied and points are received. The first Pantheon to reach 5 points reigns supreme.

All game phases are done in unison (simultaneously), and each player is able to buy cards from their own unique deck or a shared common deck. Cards are purchased with SILVER. SILVER is generated by particular cards and BONUS SILVER is collected each round depending on where you fall on the tracker board.

Find the balance between SILVER and FAVOR generating cards, as well as ACTION cards that may give you an advantage or hinder your opponent. Depending on your strategy, DEITY CARD abilities may be the advantage you need to win the most FAVOR that year.

—description from the designer

Sting Like a Bee…Strike Like a Dragon!

Two of the greatest martial artists to ever throw a punch face off in the latest set for Unmatched, the critically acclaimed, best-selling game of tactical combat between unlikely opponents. Muhammad Ali floats like a butterfly and stings like a bee. Ali’s deck boasts all-new art from Rob Zilla III. This set sees the return of the out-of-print and highly demanded Bruce Lee deck from the solo pack published at the game’s launch in 2019, illustrated by Unmatched veteran Oliver Barrett.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Conquest: A Dark Ages Supplement for the Barons' War
description:
The stage is set for skirmishes featuring Vikings raiding the coasts of Europe and the Normans invading England in 1066. This supplement also enables Viking on Viking action, Later Saxon rival armies coming to blows to settle their respective Thegn or Æthling's dynastic aims, or William's ten years of conquest taking place after crowning himself King of England.

Written to be used with The Barons' War rulebook, this supplement lets us play games set during the early medieval period.  It facilitates the ever-popular Vikings, the Later Saxons, and the Normans taking to the tabletop.

With the choices provided, players can create very different retinues even from the same faction lists. Having options is always a good thing for tabletop gaming, and we have added more with the introduction of Champions, specific faction traits and new abilities.

This digital supplement contains 92 pages of rules for playing Dark Ages skirmishes using The Barons' War rules, with faction lists for Vikings, Later Saxons and Normans. These lists are by no means exhaustive, and if there is a desire for more, other factions will be added as time permits. As additional factions are made available, anyone who purchases the Conquest PDF will receive a digital update for free.

—description from the publisher

The Welsh had a warrior society, and Wales was made up of small kingdoms constantly vying for power and influence over each other. Unlike 'Saxon/Norman' society, any royal child, illegitimate or otherwise, was eligible for the crown-making for many contenders for leadership and a fractious ruling elite well capable of murdering or mutilating their competition to get the power they wanted.

This retinue list allows you to make a 'pure' Welsh retinue or mixed retinue from the Early Medieval period to the High Medieval Period. The Welsh would ally with the Saxons or Vikings if it suited their purpose. After 1066 old enemies could be allies, disenfranchised Saxons would strike back at the Normans across the border with Wales.

This digital supplement contains 30 pages of rules for playing the Welsh in The Barons' War.

—description from the publisher

A promo deck containing a set of cards in English or German for several DDP games

The Promo Deck contains:
• 4 x Perdition’s Mouth Treasure cards
• 5 x Perdition’s Mouth Wound cards
• 2 x Perdition’s Mouth Hero cards
• 2 x Justice Accused card
• 3 x Factory 42 Inventor’s Guild cards

All of the new cards are designed to add intriguing new strategies and even more replay value. You can use the Perdition's Mouth cards with the Perdition's Mouth: Abyssal Rift or Revised Edition and with all existing and upcoming content.

In a time when, Rome's elite wield brutal gangs as instruments of their will, Gangs of Rome invites you to forge your path to power. As a Dominus, you will recruit and command a motley crew of ruthless fighters, manipulating them to achieve your goals. With their savagery tempered by your cunning, you will rise through the ranks of Roman society, crushing all who stand in your way.

This book provides a comprehensive set of rules for controlling and equipping your gang and engaging in cinematic skirmishes among the city's teeming populace. The innovative action stack mechanic allows you to create unique and varied activations for your gang fighters, ensuring that no two games are the same.

Gangs of Rome is a different approach to ancient tabletop gaming, focusing on the gritty underbelly of Roman society. With a playstyle emphasis on the narrative, Gangs of Rome is perfect for players who want to experience one-off games or run a campaign in ancient Rome's dark and dangerous streets. Whether you seek to amass wealth, gain influence, or carve out a place for yourself in the cutthroat world of the gangs, Gangs of Rome offers a unique and engaging gaming experience. Memento Mori.

The new rulebook makes it easier to get started while at the same time offering plenty of options. once you have learnt the core rules, add depth via advanced and campaign rules,

Core Rules: 20-30 pages provide all the rules needed to play a game, including gang sheets for 2 beginner gangs and three starter scenarios.

Advanced Rules: Optional rules adding the Incola, creating your own fighters, different fighter types, night fighting, didgy scaffolding, ROmes Most Wanted, Vigiles, a scenario builder, equipment and abilities.

Campaign Rules: Everything you need to play an ongoing campaign, including random events, injuries, territories and progression. It Can be played with a definitive goal or endless play, FIght and bribe your way to the Senate to advantage over your foes.

—description from the publisher

You and other participants are brought to a dimly lit room. You all take your seats and are each given 21 coins. “What is going on?” you think “am I multiplying the weight of life by 21 grams?”

Suddenly, you hear a voice...

It’s time for some fun and games. There are costs for you to live. Yes, I think 1 coin per turn sounds good. Only one card can be used at that time, and if you can’t pay....well...game over for you.

Don’t worry, I don’t mind taking change if you have it. I’ll keep you alive as long as you can pay....

Each time it’s your turn, you can play only 1 card and pay 1 coin to keep you alive until your next time. And maybe there’s something about available change...?
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Match Word is a word game like no other! Your creativity and ability to make quick associations will be put to a real test. In each round, 2 letters will be revealed, and the players' task will be to come up with words starting with them that match the given categories. Success will not come easy, because in addition to coming up with the words themselves, you will have to take a risk and decide whether to try at all costs to create original and non-obvious words, or maybe try to follow the first association, hoping for an answer consistent with the suggestions of other players. What strategy will you choose to get as many points as possible?

Słowo daję to słowna gra, jakiej jeszcze nie było! Wasza kreatywność i umiejętność szybkiego kojarzenia zostaną poddane prawdziwej próbie. W każdej rundzie ujawniane będą 2 litery, a zadaniem graczy będzie wymyślenie rozpoczynających się na nie słów pasujących do zadanych kategorii. Sukces nie przyjdzie łatwo, gdyż poza wymyśleniem samych słów będziecie musieli zaryzykować i zdecydować, czy starać się za wszelką cenę tworzyć słowa oryginalne i nieoczywiste, czy może jednak spróbować pójść za pierwszym skojarzeniem, mając nadzieję na odpowiedź zbieżną z propozycjami innych graczy. Jaką strategię obierzecie, by zdobyć jak najwięcej punktów? Słowo daję, to nie takie proste!

-descriptions from publisher

EN:
Ready your magic cauldrons for an unusual challenge – one that requires exceptional skills! You’re studying at a world-renowned Witch Academy, where you find yourselves in Potions class. As novices, somewhat unsure, you start adding ingredients to your steaming cauldrons. But be careful – one wrong component, and the whole mixture might blow up spectacularly, leaving you with nothing more than clouds of smoke and a pile of penalty points!
You must remember that brewing magic potions is a complex art. The contents of your cauldron may explode, earning you penalty points, while you’re doing everything in your power not to be the clumsiest witch in your school… Have fun brewing magic potions!

Choose the starting player – they can be the person who last burnt something in the kitchen. Then, the players take their turns clockwise. During their turn, the active player plays one of their cards into one of the potions being brewed. They may try to avoid or, conversely, cause an explosion on purpose or out of necessity.

A potion won’t explode if two of the following conditions are met:
1. The added ingredient card is either of the same colour as the potion (or is a black, wild card). AND 2. The sum of all ingredient card values for the potion is smaller than or equal to the potion card value.

A potion will blow up in any of these two situations:
1. The added ingredient card matches the potion colour (or is a wild card) but the sum of ingredient card values in this row (after adding the card) exceeds the potion card value. OR 2. The added ingredient card doesn’t match the colour of the potion (and is not a wild card).

Players have starting hand of cards.

The game ends whenever any of these two circumstances arises:
1. During their turn, one of the players places their last owned ingredient card on the board without causing an explosion. If that’s the case, all players complete their turns. Then, their points are calculated
OR
2. All potion cards have been taken by the players. In that case the game is immediately over."

PL:
Przygotujcie magiczne kociołki, bo czeka Was nie lada wyzwanie, które wymagać od Was będzie wyjątkowych umiejętności! Znaleźliście się w renomowanej i znanej na całym świecie Szkole dla Wiedźm, gdzie trafiliście prosto na lekcję eliksirów. Jako nowicjusze, nieco po omacku zaczynacie wspólnie dodawać składniki do buzujących kociołków, ale musicie być ostrożni – jedna niewłaściwa ingrediencja i mikstura może wybuchnąć w spektakularny sposób, pozostawiając w tle tylko chmury dymu i punkty ujemne na koniec gry!
Nie miejcie jednak złudzeń, opanowanie sztuki warzenia magicznych mikstur to nie lada wyzwanie! Eliksiry wybuchają, punkty ujemne trafiają na Wasze konto, a Wy robicie wszystko, by nie okazać się najgorszą wiedźmą na roku… Różdżki w dłoń i do dzieła!

Ninja Lite is a cooperative board game where you control ninjas from the Crow Clan, tasked with infiltrating a samurai compound to eliminate a traitorous warlord, the Daimyo. The game emphasizes stealth, strategy, and teamwork as players navigate through a heavily guarded area. Each player takes turns moving their ninja, using action cards to attack, evade detection, or collect critical information.

Gameplay:

1. Event Phase: Draw event cards that can introduce new enemies or challenges.
2. Ninja Phase: Players decide the order in which ninjas move, taking actions like moving, attacking, or retrieving information.
3. Enemy Phase: Enemies patrol and respond to player actions. Alerted enemies attack ninjas, making the mission more challenging.

The game features both melee and ranged combat. When an enemy becomes alert, they raise the difficulty by summoning reinforcements and chasing ninjas. Players must carefully manage noise and avoid detection to succeed.

Victory Conditions:

Players win by gathering incriminating evidence against the Daimyo and eliminating him. The game ends in failure if time runs out, all ninjas die, or if the Daimyo is killed before gathering enough data.

Ninja Lite includes varying difficulty modes, optional event cards, and additional missions to enhance replayability.

You were given the name Geoffrey the Sentinel, a paladin from the order of Eternal Gem. You are just a simple mercenary, for years you were patrolling empty dungeons, sometimes bringing home treasure that someone forgot about. But recently monsters started to appear again, you’ve tried to warn the Ten, but you’ve never even got the audience. You have decided that if no one will believe you then you might as well clear those dungeons yourself.

The first dungeon you’ve decided to clear is the old crypt of king Cedric the Boar. The crypt deep in the ruins of the old town of Amberwell. Walls were decorated by marble prepared by master stone masons of the past, inscriptions above the gateway to the crypt already faded. Magical crystals, embedded in the walls, dimly illuminate the crypt even after all this time. As you arrived at the crypt you saw that it’s filled by undead awoken by the undying rage of King Cedric. You will finish the job only when the old king returns to eternal rest.

In Die!geon you will defeat monsters and make your way towards the final boss by using your dice. You will start weak but as you defeat stronger and stronger monsters you will earn new abilities that give you more dice as well as help you with your monster fighting.

Explore dungeons, defeat the strongest monsters and grow in power. Travel through twisted corridors and make your own path to the hearth of the dungeon. Grab your sword, string your bows and gather your mana. You are not the first that tried to save this world but you might as well finish the job.

-description from publisher

Imagine a box of keepsakes you collected in memory of a person close to you. Each of the keepsakes represents a memory and together they tell a story of your relationship with this person. In Memory Of is a storytelling card game about a fictional loved one who passed away. Create a story about a character using specific keepsakes at specific moments in their lives.

It starts with a picture of an anonymous person and a name. Draw a moment card that represents periods in the character's life, turning points, or notable events. Pair them with a keepsake card that represents the loved one's possessions they've come to own. They were perhaps given, perhaps inherited, perhaps once lost and only now found.

When it's your turn, create a story around you, the keepsake, the moment, and the character. Who was this person? What did they leave behind? And most importantly, what did they mean to you? What did they mean...to everyone?

After everyone has shared their stories, the game ends with everyone giving a eulogy to the character they collectively created.

This experience can be used as a tool for understanding grief and loss together as a group. As players gradually develop the person's life that never existed, they can draw parallels and reflect on their own experience with the loss of someone in their life. Every culture and person perceives death differently, and this game gives players the unique opportunity to learn, discover, and reflect safely on grief through play.

In Memory Of is for 1-5 players and plays in about 45 minutes.

Rediscover the Woodland with three brand-new factions and two new maps for Root!

Lilypad Diaspora
Scattered long ago and suppressed ever since, the Lilypad Diaspora now hopes to call the Woodland home. While they work to integrate peacefully, peace is rare in the midst of civil war. As they train their warriors, they must ensure that their desire for safety does not tip into outright aggression. With weapons at the ready, a simple misunderstanding between the Diaspora and the Woodland can flare into vicious reprisals, hardening the Diaspora’s militancy and spreading resentment against their cause.

Twilight Council
Sickened by the enduring conflict, the Twilight Council hosts assemblies to end the war, bringing together all the Woodland from the lowliest mouse-in-a-sack to the mightiest hawk with a royal claim. The assemblies emphasize political connections over pure numbers of warriors, pushing the factions away from bloody battle and toward heated debate. As the Council progresses in their mission, they can declare edicts to change how the assemblies work, manipulating their enemies’ incentives and actions.

Knaves of the Deepwood
Nobody really knows how the Knaves of the Deepwood got together, but the myths are many. From the treeline these miscreants and ne’er-do-wells sally forth, raiding barracks and baggage trains and making off with warriors in tow as hostages. Why? For ransom, that’s why. Some say they give the proceeds to the needy. Some say they spend it lavishly. Either way, their celebrations ring out through the night, the music swelling from the forest depths where few dare to go.

In the mid-1970s, a popular newspaper in São Paulo, Brazil was responsible for one of the most widely circulated urban legends of the time, which allowed the original story to continue for more than 30 editions: the “Bebê Diabo” (Devil Baby) was born. Coming from a hospital in the ABC region of São Paulo, the child was born with horns, a tail and could talk! This is bizarre!

Of course, many were suspicious of the “saga” that was told, as it never included a real photo of the little creature.

In this game, your mission as a journalist is to obtain proof that the Devil Baby really exists, with the help of a news agency for photographs!

Rumors lead those involved (including you) through the neighborhood, in pursuit of the scoop.

Get your typewriter ready and don’t miss any tips to get the story first-hand!

To win, the player must publish two editions of the newspaper that prove the existence of the little devil. To do this, he will need a snapshot and a headline. The headline comes from cards in the player's hand, while the snapshot is obtained from the neighborhood blocks on the board, when one of the two gossipers, the photographer and the Devil Baby himself are grouped together. It is worth noting that the gossipers and the photographer move along tracks and the baby orthogonally between adjacent blocks. On the player's turn, he must buy a card and make his move, laying down as many cards as he wants. The cards have numbers that can be added up and divided among the actors on the board.

There are three types of cards: 
- RUMOR, whose intrinsic action is to allow the Devil Baby to move.
- PHOTOGRAPHER, which allows the snapshot to be taken.
- HEADLINE, used in conjunction with the snapshot that has the Devil Baby's image to obtain the publication of an edition of the newspaper.

There is a limited number of times that players can take photos during the game (10 in a game with up to 2 players and 12 for 3 or more players). Each time a player "takes a photo", a marker is placed on the block where the photo was taken and it is no longer allowed to take photos in that location.

When "taking a photo", the player reveals one of the 6 snapshot cards that are placed face down. Two of them show the image of the Devil Baby with the city in the background, while the others show only the city, but allow the player to perform other types of actions.

A perfect turn would be: player draws a card, plays "X" number of cards, groups a gossip, the baby and the photographer on a block, reveals a snapshot of the Devil Baby, plays a headline card and publishes an edition of the newspaper, taking one of the newspaper cards for himself.

-description from designer

boardgame: Into the Pumpkin Patch We Go
description: A pumpkin deck is laid out in a 10x5 grid, with an item deck shuffled and players dealt three cards, then taking turns exploring the pumpkin field, flipping over one card, taking any pumpkins into their stash, trying not to uncover a witch or ghost card that curses a player by stealing pumpkins/items/next turns. Item cards can be used to defeat witches/ghosts, reveal more cards, take more pumpkins, or sabotage other players. After every card in the grid has been flipped, the player with the most pumpkins wins.

In a retro-futuristic reality, renowned scientist James Watt, known for his revolutionary inventions, embarked on a new and mysterious journey. In his laboratory, full of gears and glass tubes, Watt developed an extraordinary machine capable of exploring the deepest recesses of the human mind during sleep: the "Máquina dos Sonhos" (Dream Machine)!

A complex contraption that had a helmet adorned with electrodes and allowed people's dreams and nightmares to be guided and observed in real time. The dreamlike images were projected onto a tube screen, revealing surreal landscapes and fantastic creatures. With each experiment, Watt came closer to unraveling the secrets of dreams, navigating between the unknown and the impossible.

To win, the player must reproduce on the matrix (a board made up of 9 cards in a 3X3 matrix) the positioning of the elements of two cards from the machine. To do this, players will have a number of actions that will increase (2 to 6) with each new round, returning to the lowest number of actions at the end of the trail on the card responsible for this count. As actions, players can add and subtract elements in the matrix (sheep and fences) or move them if they are already in the matrix. Sheep are positioned and/or moved on the cells of the matrix, while fences are positioned and/or moved in the spaces between the cells. There are two types of cards that can be completed by players: dream cards (which give the player additional actions) or nightmare cards (which give the opponent additional actions). In addition, the cells of the matrix can be turned over, showing their other side (grass/stones). To turn over a cell of the matrix, the player must make a sheep "jump" over a fence between 2 cells. With this, he can choose whether or not to turn one of the cells (never both) that were part of the action.
Players also have an impediment marker that restricts the manipulation of a cell during the opponent's next turn. After spending, this marker returns when the player uses an action.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Холст: Набор делюкс-компонентов для базовой игры и дополнений (Canvas: Deluxe Upgrade Pack)
description:
Холст: Набор делюкс-компонентов для базовой игры и дополнений ("Canvas: Deluxe Upgrade Pack") is the upgrade pack for Russian edition of Canvas, Canvas: Reflections and Canvas: Finishing Touches and it includes:

Canvas: Premium Components Pack:

     20 wooden inspiration tokens
     100 wooden ribbon tokens


For Canvas:

     10 more transparent art cards
     8 award cards
     rules card for awards expansion


For Canvas: Reflections:

     10 transparent reversible art cards
     5 signature style cards
     2 ribbon pins
     16 wood gold ribbons
     10 wood inspiration tokens
     rules card for signature styles expansion


For Canvas: Finishing Touches:

     10 more transparent art cards
     12 painter cards
     15 holofoil wood platinum ribbons
     rules card for painters expansion
     score pad



Набор делюкс-компонентов для базовой игры «Холст», а также дополнений «Холст: Отражения» и «Холст: Финальные штрихи».

Включает в себя:

Делюкс-пак для базовой игры «Холст»

     10 прозрачных карт живописи
     8 карт наград — дополнительный соревновательный элемент 
     Карта правил для игры с наградами
     20 деревянных жетонов вдохновения (замена картонных)
     100 деревянных жетонов лент (замена картонных)


Делюкс-пак для дополнения «Холст. Отражения»

     10 прозрачных двусторонних карт живописи
     5 карт авторского стиля — дополнительные победные очки за выполнение персональных условий 
     Карта правил для игры в авторском стиле
     2 особых жетона лент на булавке
     16 деревянных жетонов золотых лент
     10 деревянных жетонов вдохновения


Делюкс-пак для дополнения «Холст. Финальные штрихи»

     10 прозрачных карт живописи
     12 карт художников — великие художники дадут вам вдохновение и новые игровые возможности 
     Карта правил для игры с художниками
     Блокнот для подсчёта очков
     15 деревянных жетонов платиновых лент

You are a junior scientist curing wacky diseases like Noodle Neck and Irritable Beard Syndrome to earn grants and awards. Win by collecting the most awards over three rounds.

To play, draw compounds from your bag into your flask and then pour the compounds into experiment tubes to complete sets. Completed sets earn you awards and grant money to buy more tubes and conduct more experiments. Players may choose to press their luck when they encounter contamination tokens that could shut down their laboratory for the rest of the round.

Do you want to play it safe or risk it all for scientific glory? Easy to learn and fast to play The Best Medicine uses simultaneous play to maximize excitement and player engagement.

—description from the designer

JAZZ is a 3- to 4-player cooperative 18-card game. Players take on the roles of a jazz trio or quartet, playing either piano, bass, drums, or the saxophone. JAZZ is equal parts musicality and the ability to read what your bandmates need. It’s a game of give and take, about staying in the pocket at times, laying low during others, and popping sparks of brilliance when the perfect time arises.

Players simultaneously choose and play number cards matching their specific instrument. However, cards must be played in a certain manner, according to the instrument and the section of the song the band is playing. Measure your band up to the amount of applause you receive at the end of the night to see how well you do.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mistborn Deckbuilding Game: Vin The Prodigy Gen Con Promo Card
description:
VIN
The Prodigy

IF YOU ARE BURNING... BRASS
(I) ONCE PER TURN
Eliminate a card.

(II) ONCE PER TURN
When you buy a card you may resolve its top effect immediately, then eliminate it.

IF YOU ARE BURNING... ATIUM
(III) 3 Combat Points 3 Mission Points

A short game of trying to determine table combinations based on the number of players that show up to a game day.

Over a series of rounds, players will play cards on rows to develop different combinations of game tables.

boardgame: Clank!: Catacombs – Mister Wizkers Promo Card
description:
A promo card to add to the game, first distributed with the publisher direct preorder of the Lairs and Lost Chambers Catacombs expansion.

Rules:
Companion

+ 1 Skill +1 Sword +1 Boot +1 Victory Point

Arrive: Put 3 dragon cubes back in the bag.
If you placed one of your cubes in the dungeon this turn, draw a card.
4 Skill Cost
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Misc: Watch It Played How To Videos

description:
Originally available from Tantrum House Season 10 Kickstarter campaign.

A new building tile.
Gain a fertilzer, roll the forest die, gain a coin

Can you be unique or are you just part of the flock? Don't Flock Up is a family party game with a simple mission: create as many connections between category cards as possible, without making the same connections as anyone else. Six cards are played in each round and players will have 60 seconds to try to find as many connections as possible among the cards and write them down. (You can make connections using just 2 of the cards, 3 of the cards, 4 of the cards or 4+ for bonus points). Then, everyone shares the connections they made. Score points for any unique connections that you were able to find. If your connection(s) are the same as anyone elses, you don't score and have to cross off a number from the bonus track which gives you more points at the end of the game. After 5 rounds, the player with the most points, wins.

—description from the publisher

Includes 200 cards, a score pad and rules.

In Blütentanz, you want to dance your figures across the blossoms to reach your opponent's side of the field.

To set up the game, place the sixteen discs onto the game board at random; each disc features a blossom in the two player colors — orange and blue — as well as a neutral gray blossom.

On a turn, rotate all the tiles in a row or column 90º, with any player figures on those tiles rotating as well. Next, you have up to three movement points that you can use on 1-3 of your figures. Your five figures start off the edge of the game board, and by spending a movement point, you can place a figure on a gray blossom or a blossom of your color on the edge of the board. You can spend points to keep moving a figure orthogonally to gray blossoms or blossoms of your color.

Your goal is to move four of your five figures off the edge of the game board near your opponent. Whoever does this first wins.

A black hole has appeared in Sector 15! The sector's High Consul has issued a desperate distress call, reaching out to your respective fleets. He's asking for your help to save his fellow citizens, but old grudges between you and the sector are seriously eroding your motivation. However, he adds that you'll be able to get back whatever you want as a reward for your heroic deed.
And so, as if by magic, your hero's soul awakens...
As leader of your faction, guide your fleet through the 6 planets of Sector 15! Use your energy wisely, because although you'll optimize it by surfing the planets' orbits to get around, you'll need to save some to explore them!
Each planet is unique and offers different resources, so don't just focus on materials, save Carbonids in distress too. Not only will they make you look like heroes, they can also be very useful!
On some planets, you'll also discover unique technologies developed by the Sector, perfect for upgrading your ships.
Don't waste any time, because the High Consul is right: our sensors indicate that the star is on the verge of collapse. When that happens, you don't want to be on the planets that are about to be absorbed!

The Goal
Be the faction that has benefited most from the situation, determined by your number of Opportunism points!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Operation Biting, a solitaire World War II wargame. In this game you are in command of a group of British Airborne on a daring raid in enemy-occupied France to steal some new top secret radar. Also known as the Bruneval Raid the radar is defended by an enemy garrison, set against a snow laden cliff top.
