You must lead your squads, after their parachute drop, across the cliff tops and engage the enemy. On finding the radar you must then take the critical parts before escaping off the cliff tops to the beach below before being rescued by the Royal Navy.

As the player you have very difficult decisions to make - you have several simultaneous objectives with having to contend with the both the German forces you know about and those you do not. Lady luck plays a part, as with any military operation but your decisions and tactics are crucial, especially in how you maximise your squads and hold back the German forces. The game is not static - as you move through the map to your objectives you will be hounded by the German forces for all directions.

Getting the radar parts and getting to the beach and holding at the beach is nerve racking as you await the Navy to make good your escape, all the while being assaulted by the German defences. You must choose to what extent a fighting rear guard should be used at the cost of getting all your squads off the beach.

—description from the designer

Play with Legendary Heroes like the mighty Samson! Beat the Giants with King David! Equip your Heroes with majestic artifacts like the Sword of the Spirit or the Staff of Moses! Embark on an epic adventure fighting thrilling battles! Make good use of your miracles and better strategize to defeat your opponents with the Power of Faith! (from publisher's website)

Faith Battle is a collectible card game with a Christian bible theme, but you know exactly what you're buying, because each box contains the same cards, but by buying different boxes you can combine them in any way you like, forming your own decks.

In each game, players cast heroes, equip them with artifacts and summon miracles using wisdom (equivalent to Magic's mana), and put the heroes into battle to try to take away the opponent's faith points, with mechanisms very similar to other collectible card games (like Magic or Pokemon).
The game ends as soon as the opponent has no faith points or when the players have played 10 rounds.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: 23 Wards of Tōkyō, Ōita Hot Spring Town & The Lost City of Dwarka
description:
Age of Steam Expansion: 23 Wards of Tokyo, Oita Hot Spring Town & The Lost City of Dwarka features a trio of expansions for Age of Steam":

• 23 Wards of Tokyo is a map for 3-4 players. Players' actions are limited by the amount of Power they've stored up. In this dice-drafting variant for Special Action selection, Locomotive is powerful as always, but it comes at a steep cost, limiting your available action points.

• Oita Hot Spring Town is a map for 3-5 players. Nestled in a valley off the eastern shores of Kyushu, Oita boasts the country's highest concentration of hot spring resorts. Player's work to unearth six hidden hot springs, each with their own unique blend of natural resources, attempting to visit all of them for a special bonus.

• The Lost City of Dwarka is a map for 4-6 players. In the multilevel map, players embark on an underwater expedition to excavate artifacts from the long-lost civilization in the sunken city of Dwarka.

Cerro Gordo Silver Mines, is a game based on the iconic youtube channel @ghosttownliving, and the true story of the mining town from the 1860s that extracted enough silver to build Los Angeles.

In this game, you will co-build Cerro Gordo in its heyday, with individual and shared objectives. You'll be receiving silver as you dig further into the mine, and use that silver to build the town in your vision.

Watch out as catastrophic events can set you back, and you may cross paths with other characters that have their own agendas.

Can you complete all your objectives and gain the most silver, to win the game?

—description from the publisher

Bastille is a simple and accessible card combo game with a time management on the wire. Rediscover the protagonists of the time: Louis XVI, Beaumarchais, Marie-Antoinette, Olympes de Gouges… Live the year 1789 from the Tennis Court Oath to the Declaration of the Rights of Women in 1791!

Aim of the game:
To have the most victory points.

How:
Play an equipment for free or a character by sacrificing cards from your hand. Each character has victory points corresponding to their cost of putting them into play… But be careful: the discard pile is visible and serves as a common draw pile!

—description from the publisher

Bastille est un jeu de combo de cartes simple et accessible avec une gestion du temps sur le fil. Redécouvrez les protagonistes de l’époque : Louis XVI, Beaumarchais, Marie-Antoinette, Olympes de Gouges… Vivez l’année 1789 du Serment du Jeu de Paume jusqu’à la Déclaration des Droits de la Femme de 1791 !

But du jeu :
Avoir le plus de points de victoire.

Comment :
Jouez un équipement gratuitement ou un personnage en sacrifiant des cartes de votre main. Chaque personnage possède des points de victoire correspondant à son coût de mise en jeu… Mais attention : la défausse est visible et sert de pioche commune !

—description from the publisher (French)
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Roads & Boats: Book of Scenarios Volume 1
description:
This companion book for Roads & Boats and Roads & Boats: &Cetera includes:

     Foreword by Joris Wiersinga
     Table of Contents
     Introduction with photos of Joris's hand-made wooden Roads & Boats implementation
     A detailed How to Use this Book section
     A Jeroen-approved variant to the &Cetera Jumpstart rules
     A short treatise in defense of Artwork
     A new Secondary Producer building for Polder tiles, the "Splotter Autter"
     160+ unique scenarios for Roads & Boats by a number of contributors
     6 Sticker sheets containing tokens for the new building and extra tiles for scenarios that use more tiles than that which comes with the game/&Cetera
     Tile Manifest(s)
     Sterling Babcock's Player Aid (as a full, two-page spread and as a small, single-page image)
     Blank pages for notes


The scenarios contained in the book originate from diverse places such as:

     The Roads & Boats Scenario Book from Splotter (included with base game)
     The &Cetera rulebook
     Board Game Geek (Forum Threads, Photos, Files)
     Aaron Marriner's old website for playing Roads & Boats online
     A physical paper-release promo from Gen Con 2018
     Those created by the author and other contributors, not posted elsewhere online


Each scenario lists the credited designer(s), as well as the conditions for the scenario and any special rules associated with it.
All scenarios are presented in a graphically uniform fashion, using artwork from the most recent printing of Roads & Boats.
Solitaire scenarios also include Current Records and Difficulty Levels.
Each scenario is accompanied by a unique QR code - these each link to a unique item on the Board Game Geek Geeklist, Roads & Boats Scenario Discussion Space (ID: 341324) conditioned specifically to a given scenario.

This book was given as a freebie gift to attendees at the inaugural Splotter Con convention in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania on August 30, 2024. The book is available on the Neon Comet Games website as Roads & Boats Book of Scenarios Volume 1. The book is set to be available for sale at Essen Spiel 2024 (via the Spielbar booth - Hall 2, Booth 2A102) and Tokyo Game Market 2024 (via Neon Comet Games @ the AllPlay booth)


Introducing a new Secondary Producer: The Splotter Autter

Splotter Autters may only be built on Polder tiles. The cost to build a Splotter Autter is two Boards, one Stone, and one Gold. Only one Splotter Autter may be built on each Polder tile. This building works slightly differently from other Secondary Producers of Goods in that its output is dependent on the presence of a transporter on its tile and, if present, what that transporter is carrying.

During the Production phase, if a transporter carrying one or two Gold is on the tile of a Splotter Autter and has the carrying capacity to accept its output, the Splotter Autter will consume one Gold carried by the transporter and output two Gold, loading them directly back onto that same transporter. If the transporter is instead carrying three or more Gold, the Splotter Autter will consume the three Gold and output one Coins back onto that same transporter.If the active player has multiple transporters that satisfy the prioritized condition on the same tile, that player may choose which transporter provides the consumed Gold and accepts the building’s output. If instead no transporter is present, or if Gold, Coins, or Stock are on the tile but not held by a transporter, the Splotter Autter will consume any Gold, Coins, or Stock on the tile that is not held by a transporter. The production capacity of each Splotter Autter is one, though a Manager can be used to double its capacity, of course.

—description from the publisher

The shape of space is new. Sprawling empires used to rise, spread to different worlds, before crumbling into ruin or slouching toward oblivion, but even those regular rhythms of change themselves… change. There have been no galactic empires in countless lives, not since the devastation wrought by the misuse of the most volatile of all technologies: the Faster-than-Light drive. The device that allowed worlds to fall to conquest with unaccountable speed, far in excess of light, bending fabric between worlds into jungle of deadly paradox, making each world an isolated island on the black seas with no aspirations beyond their solar system.

But the shape of space isn’t impermeable, not with the newest means to navigate it: the probability drive. Chance is the one means by which one might leap through the deadly void, pushing the probable to the surface of the ocean of the improbable. A vessel might leap across an entire sector, or it might blink from existence entirely. Even if you end up where you hope to go, the drive ensures you can never go back and cause a new tsunami of paradox.

Using the probability drive holds terrible risk, but it is a risk taken by those desperate, ravenous, or mad with some grand design. Even now, the spiders spread, autonomous vessels that spin their webs through the paradox-riddled void. They are the vanguard of the Void Vestige, those willing to walk between worlds, to brave the VAST COSMOS.

Vast Cosmos is a campaign of different solitaire games all about charting and learning about quirky worlds across a galactic map. As these games progress, you will venture across sectors of the galaxy, lay threads that can ease future travels, discover disconnected worlds, and slowly uncover their true nature. Vast Cosmos inherits elements of roll-and-write board games and world-building tools for role-playing games but is its own unique experience. Indeed, the game is all about the ability to customize and expand a galaxy from your imagination as many times as you like! The secrets you discover are conveniently indexed in a notebook, meaning Vast Cosmos produces a galactic atlas replete with places you might choose to explore and define more deeply in more traditional space-faring role-playing games.

Moreover, Vast Cosmos is a “Make-as-You-Play” game. This means that the game starts with a few materials (a map, printouts, notebook, and wooden cubes). These rules include tables and guidelines for modifying the components to build out a deep and unexpected galaxy, and this makes every copy of Vast Cosmos distinct from any other and uniquely your own. It also means that each time you play a game of Vast Cosmos, you build on the galaxy as you discovered it in the previous game, with an endless potential for new discoveries.

—description from the designer

Zama is a strategic card and board game that reenacts the historic Battle of Zama between Rome and Carthage. Players choose to lead either the Roman legions under Scipio Africanus or the Carthaginian forces led by Hannibal Barca. The game features beautiful artwork, cards, dice, and a game mat, offering an immersive experience. The goal is to outmaneuver your opponent using tactical decisions to win the ultimate showdown between these ancient superpowers. It blends historical accuracy with engaging gameplay, set on the open plains of North Africa.

Introducing Shapes of Play: Battle, a stylish board game created by the team behind the design of PlayStation consoles in celebration of PlayStation’s 30th Anniversary.

Challenge a friend to line up four different shapes of the same color on the board to win. Take turns in placing one shape on the board at a time. Win by placing four different shapes of the same color in a straight line.

—description from the publisher

Explore a 60 minute game of Land vs Sea with 24 new tiles with new shapes, new artwork and 3 new scoring mechanisms. This expansion takes the modular Land vs Sea game from light to medium weight.

Menhirs - Score the bonus points on tiles in 'ley lines' you form between distant Menhirs. Can you triangulate Menhirs to score more?

Cities - Connect City tiles to land and sea areas to double the bonus point values in them, and the end game scoring value of trade routes connected to them. All the more reason to complete rivals' areas!

Bandits and Pirates - Score by raiding trade routes (pirates raid ships bandits raid caravans) throughout the game. Do you lower your risk by making a variety of smaller trade routes, or push trade routes longer to increase their risk and rewards?

Start scoring immediately with 1 of 4 large starting tile faces pre-filled with different scoring elements.

—description from the publisher

Help! These puffins were on vacation exploring the far north but got caught in a blizzard. Arrange the puffins into specific patterns and help them build igloos to stay warm.

In Pocket Puffins you will have 3 pattern cards and a row of 9 puffins. You will use your movement cards to help move the puffins into the correct position while also helping them build igloos to gain points. Be swift with your movements because time is limited and all 3 patterns need to be fulfilled before you run out of movement cards.

A blend of strategy with a little luck of the draw will keep you striving to get 12 points and gain the Perfect Puffin rank.

—description from the designer

In Survival: Scandinavia, each player must navigate the harsh landscapes by gathering resources, building strongholds, and equipping gear. You can choose to be a hero, trading with neighbors to help everyone survive—or a villain, raiding and betraying those around you to secure your own victory. The choice is yours.

At the end of each turn, every player must eat one food card to survive. But beware—random events can strike at any moment, and they can be unforgiving unless you’re prepared. Our testing sessions have shown that as the game progresses, many players find themselves forced to turn on their friends just to make it through the final round. With over 50% of players typically eliminated by the end, only the player with the most Artifacts will emerge victorious.

—description from the designer

Eastern front, 1944...

After the Battle of Kursk saw the Red Army defeat the Wehrmacht's last great offensive on the Eastern Front, the Russians began a series of large attacks and began to rapidly drive the invaders back west. The German's retreated, but their Army Group Centre put up a stubborn fight.

A 173 page Battlegroup campaign supplement primarily covering the Soviet summer offensive in 1944 that all but destroyed Army Group Centre. Can also be used for the 1943/44 Winter fighting and other actions in ’44 from the Finns around Leningrad to the southern Ukraine. A number of new army lists including German Security Division, Finnish Infantry Division, Soviet Mechanised Cavalry and Soviet Partisans. Also includes new scenarios and a Borisov bridgehead campaign.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The Mega Empires: Upgrade & 3-4 Players Pack enhances the experience of playing Mega Empires: The West (2019) or Mega Civilization (2015) by adding new and upgraded components plus rules and components for playing with 3 or 4 players.

The main feature of this pack is the Market Board, which is used for trading when playing with 3 or 4 players. In addition to the Market Board, a set of rules, dedicated A.S.T. Boards and Calamity Quick Charts for 3 or 4 players are included in this pack. These components are by default included in Mega Empires: The East (2021).

The new and upgraded components include City Count Boards and full color Player Mats that were developed after the release of Mega Civilization.

—description from the designers

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Cardboard Map Box Big Box Extension
description:
Adds the 4 new maps on 2 cardboard maps (Utopia Planitia, Terra Cimmeria, Vastitas Borealis, Amazonis Planitia) and an extension for the big box to store everything in one box again.

Contains:

     Terraforming Mars: Amazonis & Vastitas
     Terraforming Mars: Utopia & Cimmeria
     Terraforming Mars: Big Box Extension


—user summary

boardgame: ドラえもん ひみつ道具 ナイ！ナイ！ナ～イ！ゲーム (Doraemon Himitsu Dougu Nai! Nai! Na~i! Game)
description: Doraemon Himitsu Dougu Nai! Nai! Na~i! Game (ドラえもん ひみつ道具 ナイ！ナイ！ナ～イ！ゲーム) is a card game where you collect the secret gadgets that Doraemon has misplaced. Flip over two cards to find matching pairs. There are also hidden "effect cards" that assist gameplay and can make the game even more exciting...!? The player who collects the most secret gadget cards wins. The rules are simple so the game can be enjoyed both by adults and children.

Vita Luna is a board game played using cards. There is no die and randomness is kept to a minimum. This means the game is all about tactics and skill and requires you to think ahead.

As you fight battles, the winner gets to add powerful 'blue' coloured cards whilst the loser has to make do with the less powerful 'black' cards. This way your deck is built upon and, if you get far enough, you can even add the all powerful 'red' creature cards.

As well as the creatures, you have singular cards that are powerful one off's that can change the course of a battle. Once used, they're gone forever so you must use them wisely.

For some board gamers, player counts can really make or break a game. If you're in a position where you play a lot of games with just you and a partner, then Vita Luna is going to be a great choice for you. With each battle lasting approximately 10 minutes each, games can last about 30-40 minutes in total. And, considering its high replayability factor, it fits beautifully into that category of ‘go on then, one more game…’

—description from the designer

boardgame: 新米オーナー、見習いスタッフたちとレストランを救う。 (Save New Owners, Apprentice Staff and Restaurants)
description:
Story
Famous British Restaurant "Cypsophyla".
This is a new owner that suddenly became a parent owned restaurant.
As an added bonus, staff almost quit and the rest is just apprentes...
VIP is coming to "Sypsophila".
Can new owners and apprentice staff overcome this pinch?

Save restaurants with new owners, apprentary staffs. (New Sta)
A game that takes about 15 minutes to play and can be easily played by 2 to 5 players.

However, the difficulty is not low.
The strategy and speed of serving the food with a planned amount of time is pushed to the limit.
Best of all, teamwork is required for each other.

This game is perfect for enhancing your relationship as it creates conversation between nature and staff through the game.
Once you have cleared, you can also challenge yourself with additional rules.

Players are part of "Sypsophila" and cooperate with other staff to serve their clients.
The game is clear if you can order successfully, and complete your VIP order card.
The game over if the order deck is out of place or 3 complaints tokens are placed before completing the VIP order card.

boardgame: Violet and the meddlings of Madame Kamila
description:
In the expansion, Violet visits the fortune teller Madame Kamila before embarking on her nightly adventure to meet Ordep the friendly troll and her best friend. Hoping to obtain guidance, Violet accepts a card reading that will change her journey in unpredictable ways.

"It seems you have a difficult path ahead of you young girl. Here, let me read into it so that you may be prepared for the challenges ahead."

Violet observes Madame Kamila shuffle her Tarot cards and fan them out on the table. "Take your pick little girl" Uncertain about what the cards will reveal, Violet picks the one card that seems to call for her fingertip. Madame Kamila reveals it and after some highly ambiguous words that to Violet sound wise and ominous at the same time, Madame Kamila finally says: "in order to succeed in your journey, you must open your heart to the Path of...."

Violet and the meddlings of Madame Kamila is the upcoming expansion for Violet and the Grumpy Nisse, in which Violet draws a Tarot Card that determines the path she will walk. There are 12 unique tarot cards and each will determine the way Ordep's helpers assist Violet on her path.

The expansion adds:

     12 beautifully illustrated custom Tarot cards
     6 new Torech Event Tiles
     12 new Advantage Tokens (two copies each)
     5 Trust Tiles (for the path of Trust, with Fortitude on the reverse)
     1 Ordep Tile (for the path of Love)
     18 Path Tiles (for the path of Darkness)
     4 Danger Tiles (for the path of Silence)


—description from the publisher

A game that can be played by 2/4 players or teams of players, 'Zoo Logical' offers a challenge to the participants both young and old. Each player travels around the board collecting animals by successfully answering questions put to you by one of your opponents.

There are 600 questions to test your ability and increase your knowledge of animals and nature from the lethal Tarantula to the magnificent Tiger, from the ferocious Piranha to the soaring Eagle.

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Dungeon Doors: Big Bad Evil Guy
description:
In this expansion pack for Dungeon Doors you’ll take door mastering to another leve! Throw something more than creatures, curses and death-traps at the hapless heroes; something horrific and final: YOU! Don the mantle of a Big Bad Evil Guy and give your Dungeon Doors heroes a final boss fight they’ll never forget (if they survive at all to tell).

-description from publisher's website

Promo Card for Vampire Nights.

Rules:

Push 1 Guard exactly 1 space forward or sideways into an adjacent (empty) Room. The Hayfork can be used from the Entrance or into the Garden (space #2), even if space #1 is empty.

—description from the publisher

Vampire Nights Promo.
Rules:
Reveal up to 2 Rooms where in the Mansion.
These will be revealed for all players.

—description from the publisher

The Ants & The Grasshopper is a solo worker placement game for children.

In this game you play as a colony of ants that is preparing for the coming winter. Each turn you place your ant workers on cards in your colony in order to take actions. You will collect food and dig tunnels in order to build additional cards and expand your colony further.

Each turn you will also draw from a deck of grasshopper AI cards, and the pesky grasshopper will show up to block your cards, distract your workers, and steal some of your food.

To win, you need to enter winter with 30 food in your stockpile and with 4 new cards built in your colony.

-description from designer

This game was designed for the 2024 BGG Solitaire Print & Play Design Contest, where it was voted 9th Best Overall Game.

You step into the shoes of a daring alchemist apprentice, standing on the brink of her ultimate trial. The fate of her future rests on crafting a legendary elixir, forged from five distinct potions. To succeed, you must venture into mysterious realms, uncover ancient brewing techniques, and face formidable creatures lurking in the shadows. Set in the time before Scenario 1, this expansion weaves your choices into the fabric of the world itself, shaping the course of the campaign. 


This expansion brings a completely new scenario to the base game, consisting of 1 city card with tasks, 1 event card and another card that allows for the brewing of potions. This brewing card brings a new mechanic for using potions into the game. This scenario can be played either as a standalone scenario or as the new first part of the campaign.

-description from designer

boardgame: Battlegroup Centag: New Army Lists for Battlegroup Northag
description: Includes new army lists for US, West Germans, Dutch, Canadian, Danish, Warsaw Pact (East Germans, Poles and Czechs), Soviet Naval Infantry and Soviet Airborne, plus a new Airborne Assault scenario.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
War of the Arcane is a two-player strategy card game set in a mystical world where powerful sorcerers command armies, cast spells, and set traps to protect their Mystic Stones. The game’s theme blends magic and tactical warfare, with players taking on the role of sorcerers battling to defend their own Mystic Stone while trying to destroy their opponent’s.

The game is played on a 5x5 grid, where players strategically place units, traps, and spells to outmaneuver their opponent. Hidden tactics play a big role, as cards can be placed face down, adding mystery and bluffing to the strategy. The persistent damage system means that every point of damage counts, creating tension throughout the game as players carefully manage their units’ health and positioning.

Each turn, players manage action points to deploy units, move cards, attack, or cast spells. The flow of battle is dynamic, with adjacent units engaging in combat and special abilities influencing the outcome. The key to victory lies in protecting your Mystic Stone while setting up opportunities to strike at your opponent’s.

The ultimate goal is to destroy your opponent's Mystic Stone while keeping your own safe. With grid-based combat, persistent damage, and hidden tactics, War of the Arcane offers an engaging blend of strategy and magic, where every move matters and the battle for the Mystic Stone can turn at any moment.

—description from the publisher

Dragon Tricks is a trick-taking game with a unique twist, designed to be accessible to kids and families while being deep enough to engage and challenge adult players too. The beautiful artwork complements a fun trick-taking and set collection mechanism to create an exciting and engaging experience for all ages.

Like most trick-taking games, Dragon Tricks is played over a series of rounds. Unlike most trick-taking games, players in Dragon Tricks don't have to follow suit. Tricks are won by the highest card in the suit that was played the most.

After each round, players will take turns trading in the tricks they won to hatch dragon eggs. The player who assembles the highest-scoring collection of baby dragons wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Draft a team of any subject-- Actresses, Fairy Tales, Junk Food, and more! Draft'd offers two different ways to play.

The first way to play is by Popular Vote. At the start of the game, players choose a Topic Card and then write their picks for that subject-- however, each pick can only be written once. Players also must choose a "Bust", which is a pick they believe is terrible. Then, players vote on the best picks. The player who earns the most votes wins the round and earns the Topic Card. The first player to earn 3 Topic Cards wins!

boardgame: Pampero: Promo Action Card and Scoring Card
description:
A two card promo (plus a rules card) available as a $5.00 add-on during the 2022 Kickstarter campaign for Pampero, with proceeds going to BoardGameGeek (hence the BGG logo on the rules card).

Consists of one action card, one scoring card, and a rules card, with rules for each card on opposite sides of it.

Rules for the action card:
Receive $2 for each action card on your company mat, including this one.

Rules for the scoring card:
Receive $1 for each action and scoring card in your control, including this one.

You’re the mayor of a small fishing town in this medium weight family-friendly board game. Build and send out fishing boats, sell your fish at the market, build houses, and make your town a top tourist destination. You’ll compete with other players for the best fishing spots, but beware of storms! With a fun twist on a classic resource collection mechanic, Terra del Mar is great for casual and serious gamers alike, and will keep you inspired until the final throw of the dice. Grab your copy today and head out to sea!

The main engine of Terra del Mar is a dice-based resource collection mechanism. Players collect fish by placing their boat meeples on the corners of intersecting hex tiles, and gaining fish when the tile’s corresponding number is rolled. Terra del Mar uses two four-sided dice, and maps out the probability of each sum of two dice being rolled using dots, which players can use to identify the best fishing spots. Anyone familiar with the Catan dice-roll resource collection will be able to easily pick up this game, and it offers a very different gameplay experience while piggybacking on players’ existing knowledge of the Catan mechanic.

Players must regularly bring their boats back into harbor, meaning the best spots are regularly shifted around from player to player. This adds a “push your luck” element to the game, where players must decide whether to stay out and fish more but risk being caught in a storm, or to return to harbor but risk losing their fishing spots. Fish are used as an in-game resource by selling them for gold to purchase new boats, new houses, or to upgrade “tourism levels.” These “tourism levels” depend on other prerequisites being met and are used as the victory condition – first to level 6 – thus avoiding the cumbersome tracking of victory points throughout the game.

In HACK: players take on the roles of elite hackers racing through a virtual network. The game blends competitive strategy with fast-paced dice rolling as players navigate a dynamic, player-created board filled with dangerous hexes, opponents, and powerful upgrades. Each hacker must outwit their rivals, engage in real-time hacking battles, and overcome the advanced MINDHEIST Firewall to steal the coveted Brain token.

Gameplay revolves around moving across a modular board by rolling dice, collecting upgrades, and engaging in PvP combat. Players also have access to unique asymmetric abilities based on their chosen HACKNET USER card, which allows for specialized control over dice, combat strategies, and movement. Minigames, portals, and monitor pawns add layers of challenge and unpredictability, keeping the game dynamic and engaging.

The ultimate goal is to be the first player to successfully complete the MINDHEIST, steal the Brain token, and return to the START hex before your opponents hack you or cause you to burnout. Only one hacker will emerge victorious, while the rest are left to experience burnout in the unforgiving world of the Net.

Outmaneuver your opponent in this quick-playing strategy card game for two players. Build fleets, place stations in orbit, and claim planets – but watch out for spies and black holes. The first player to claim five planets wins. Blast off with Stax Galaxies and launch your fleet today!

Stax is a robust and flexible game system for head-to-head duels that can be adapted to a variety of settings. (more versions coming soon!)

As a highly portable small-box game with strategic depth, Stax Galaxies is ideal for casual gamers or hobbyists looking for a fast and fun game to play during downtime or in between longer gaming sessions. At the beginning of the game, a set of cards are laid from the planets deck and then players compete to build stacks to claim the planets. A few tricky mechanics add to the challenge and the fun, such as the Spy, which can displace an opponent's claim.

The simple rule set and strategic depth will keep players coming back for more, while making the game accessible to a wide range of age groups and skill levels.

—description from the publisher

TROVE is a dungeon crawl card game where players take on the role of adventurers who have fought their way through perilous dangers and now face the ultimate challenge: dividing the treasure. Inspired by classic games like Talisman and Citadels, TROVE combines strategic decision-making with the excitement of treasure hunting.

Each player selects an adventurer with unique abilities, engaging in a dynamic series of rounds. The game progresses through three phases: Hiring, where players draft adventurers; Looting, where they claim treasure from the central pool; and Skills, where adventurers use their abilities to gain an advantage, steal from others, or protect themselves from curses and traps.

With a mixture of relics, treasure, and cursed items, every turn offers surprises. The goal is simple: by the end of six rounds, the player with the most valuable trove of treasures wins the game. Balancing risk, luck, and strategy is key to emerging victorious.

Biscuits is a partnership climbing card game for four players playable with a standard 52-card deck.

The object of the game is to play all of your cards to the table, preferably while helping your partner to shed all of their cards in the process. 
If you cannot be the first to play all your cards, then your aim is to have as few cards as possible when an opponent finishes.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Beginner Set
description:
Welcome to the Age of Sigmar, an era of ceaseless conflict in which gods and monsters clash upon the battlefields of the Mortal Realms. Take to the field and lead your troops into battle to defend civilisation – or tear it down.

This set contains everything you need to get started with all aspects of this creative and engaging hobby, including 25 push-fit plastic miniatures representing the immortal Stormcast Eternals and the devious ratmen of the Skaven, as well as all the rules and accessories for up to two players to face off in games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar that last under an hour!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Walking Dead: All Out War – The Commonwealth Collection
description:
8 Resin Survivor Miniatures: Pamela, Lance, Princess, Mercer, 4 X Commonwealth Soldiers
     8 Survivor Cards
     Equipment Cards
     Event Cards
family: Game: The Walking Dead – All Out War

description:
A highly strategic financial game (inspired by The Intelligent Investor book) that realistically simulates the stock market with 10 real-life companies. It’s designed to make investing fun while teaching the basics of the investment world. Experienced investors can also test their wits and strategies.

As an investor, you must observe and respond to both market trends and your opponents' moves, compare stocks, and anticipate other players' actions.

The goal is simple: start with a budget of $200M and compete with your friends to become the first billionaire!

Each investor creates their own investment strategy and manages a portfolio of stocks and bonds. Players receive dividends (cash) and bond rewards, which can be reinvested at the end of each round.

Each turn offers opportunities to:

     Buy and sell stocks, causing market prices to fluctuate
     Trade stocks with other players
     Adjust leverage (borrowed money) to maximize your investments
     Use "Power Plays" to protect your strategy with clever financial moves or weaken your opponents' positions


Are you ready to become a Wise Investor?

—description from the publisher

In this cooperative game:

     Stack cards on a board while telling a short, crazy, funny, wacky story... all within 2 minutes!
     Together, take on the challenge of remembering the story in the correct sequence! 


And that’s when the magic happens: Surprises and Laughter! Because, with the help of all players, having a good memory has never been easier!

Each card correctly recalled in sequence earns the players 2 points. If a player seek assistance, the group still earns 1 point for each card, encouraging teamwork and collaboration. However, be cautious - an incorrect guess results in the group losing 1 point. Aim to continually improve your collective score from game to game, or to climb higher on the success ladder.

The basic rules remain the same across different versions of Story Box, but each version offers a Themed Expert Variant.

—description from the publisher

Trophies & Bonuses expands Emberleaf by introducing new trophies and variable score track bonuses to the game.

Trophies:
During setup, you can now randomly select 6 of 15 trophies to include in your game. The new advanced trophies offer a range of unique options, bringing fresh challenges and opportunities that weren’t available before.

Score Track Bonuses:
You will now randomly place score track bonuses around the game board during setup. With 18 double-sided tiles to choose from, each game will feel different as the flow changes, offering new shared objectives for players to pursue alongside their personal goals.

Favour Cards:
The expansion also adds 24 new favour cards, introducing a variety of unique objectives to work towards as you plan your strategy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Emberleaf: Hero Pack 1 – Starter Deck F
description:
Hero Pack 1 adds an intermediate starter deck to Emberleaf, introducing new rules for players to explore.

In addition, it includes special favour cards, offering the unique option to sacrifice points in exchange for powerful benefits, adding a fresh layer of strategy to your gameplay.

—description from the publisher

Hero Pack 2 brings new seasonally themed heroes to Emberleaf, offering exciting new options for players to explore.

Alongside the heroes, this pack also includes seasonally themed favour cards, introducing a variety of new objectives to work towards as you plan your strategy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Carcassonne: The Wonders of Humanity Set II
description:
A second set of Wonders of Humanity. This time containing: Abu Simbel, Tikal, Angkor Wat, Terracotta Army.

At the end of the game, you first score the wonders that score at the end of the game. After that, you continue with normal final scoring. Each wonder tile counts as 5 individual normal tiles. Accordingly, the roads and cities can consist of more than one title. You must take this into account during the scoring.

Bloodline Legend is a collectible card game in a fantastic world with creatures of all kinds such as warriors, dragons, wild animals, ancestral beings, angels among others, with them you can create a winning game strategy to beat your rivals in a game, thus reducing their 20 life points to zero, to achieve this each player takes turns playing cards from their hand and paying their cost to be able to perform their ability.  
Build your field with various races, objects and spells to make incredible combinations.

—description from the designer

This is a Promo Pack of 6 new Dream cards. There are 2 cards of each type – Lucid, Neodream and Recursive Dream cards.

Exclusively available at SPIEL Essen 2024 together with a Neodreams game copy.

Friends 'n' Foes is a 2 player handheld card battle game, where neither friends nor foes are "forever".

Each game the 2 players take turns in challenging the other player's team to fight, flight, freeze or fawn. 
You attack, run, hide or use magic to trick and outwit the other team - all in the palm of your hand.
The first player, who runs the other player's team out of spoons or out of friends wins the game.

Friends 'n' Foes is played with 6 cards (a team) per player plus 1 spoon card to track the energy available to each player. Each round you choose which challenge you want to play, but you have to play with the first card in your hand. Some challenges are for free while some have a cost. Some challenges you play out in the open while others are hidden and leave room for bluffing.
The other player can choose from the first 2 cards in their hand how they want to react to a challenge. Each challenge has different options to choose from as well as different kinds of possible outcomes. A challenge with a cost may result in winning a member of the other team over while a hidden challenge may be more effective in running the other team out of spoons. 
You may also choose to recharge your spoon card, but you have to sacrifice one of your own friends in order to do so. Too bad, that sacrificed team-members are exhausted and out of play for the rest of the game.

The rookie mode is useful to familiarize the players with the game mechanics, but the pro-mode is, when the game design really comes to full play.

The game comes with 36 friends/team-member cards and lets you choose to play with randomised team decks, with teams by color or even build/choose your own deck.

(All cards are randomly selected and distributed to each game out of a total of 216, so no game contains the same set of cards.)

Both spoon cards are double-sided to suit both, right- and left-handed people alike.

—description from the designer
family: Series: Circle of Friends (Lonely Kid Games)

boardgame: Space Bureau: Captain Ability Promo Tiles
description:
Available at SPIEL Essen 2024.

These promo captain ability tiles can be added to the other abilites of the base game.

All abilities are used before the cell action (i.e. immediately after placing the trade agent tile in the cell). Players are not required to use an ability or may only receive a partial ability bonus.

All promo captain abilities are related to the concepts of ship areas. Each ship consists of 3 visually separated areas (2 vertical columns in each) and 4 rows — horizontal cell lines.

Captain ability – C7:

Whenever you place a third trade agent within one ship area, you may spend one fuel to place an office or get a sample from a planet or megamall.

Captain ability – C8:

Whenever the connectors of trade become adjusted on the border of the ship areas:
> in the 1st or 2nd row – get 2 fuel;
> in the 3rd or 4th row – start a flight or move bureau marker 2 rooms further.

Captain ability – C9:

At the start of the game receive 2 special tiles with bonuses and place them in different areas of the ship – one in the 2nd row, the other in the 3rd row.
Whenever you place a trade agent next to a special tile with bonuses, get one of the bonuses shown on the tile.

boardgame: Montrose Triumphs: The Battles of Auldearn and Alford, 1645
description:
Fought on July 2nd, 1645, Lord Montrose deployed the bulk of his force on the reverse slope concealing them from Royalists. Due to this, General Baillie believed he was opposed only by a Covenanter rear guard. Montrose waited for the Royalists to cross the river and then launched his attack, that quickly routed the Royalist cavalry that crossed first and sought to cover the infantry. When the Royalist infantry proved unable to reform and step the Covenanter assault, the King’s army broke and fled the field. The Royalists lost 1,500 to 2,000 men, while the Scots lost a tenth of that number.

Fought on May 9th, 1645, the battle of Auldearnt was another fierce, if small, battle. Once again, Montrose was able to conceal a portion of this cavalry from the Royalists in a hollow, and their sudden appearance and attack on the Royalist left decided the battle. As with the other battles the vast majority of the Royalist losses were suffered after their army broke, and Montrose’s cavalry ruthlessly cut them down as the rank and file attempted to flee.

Each copy of Montrose Triumphs is composed of the following:


     Two 8.5 by 11 inch hex maps.
     One sheet of 168 single-sided, un-mounted units.
     One Players’ Aid Sheet with game tables and Turn Record Tracks.
     6 pages of rules.


Montrose Triumphs is designed by Roberto Chiavini, developed by Paul Rohrbaugh, and features graphics by Ilya Kudriashov. It is the eighth game of ten by Roberto on famous battles of the English Civil War.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: English Civil War (High Flying Dice)

boardgame: Like Totally 80s Pop Culture Trivia Game
description:
Flash back to the most radical decade of all time! With Like Totally 80s Pop Culture Trivia Game teams battle each other over who rules the 80s in a series of turns between teams! Play over 400 pop culture questions crammed with pictures, trivia and various game challenges. Relive the gnarly fashions, trends, movies, TV and the tunes! You can even listen to the soundtrack on Spotify!

If a player answers a question correctly they score 1 point for their team. The first team to score 7 points is the winner and will bask in 80s glory (cue the music)!

The Tortoise & The Hare is a solo push-your-luck game for children. It is based on Aesop's Fable about a race between a slow and steady Tortoise, and a quick yet careless Hare.

This game uses a Blackjack style push-your-luck mechanism. You play as the Hare and race through a town along a track with numbered spaces. Each turn you draw cards and add up the numbers on them to determine how far you move your Hare pawn.

Move as quickly as you can to reach the end of the racetrack before the Tortoise. Move too quickly however, and you will Trip and miss your turn!

The racetrack also contains several hazard spaces that will slow you down if you land on them.

—description from designer
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Tortoise and the Hare

description:
In a magical forest, hidden from the world, several stray animals were welcomed and protected. This forest is supported by the action of six magical bones that are guarded by the brave dog 'Abreu'.
But the forest is threatened, because the cat 'Malevolent' thinks the bones belong to him. Clever, he entered Abreu's little house and hid all the bones in the woods...
Time passes and if the bones are not found and reunited again, the magic that protects the forest will end and the animals will be in serious danger.
A frantic game of rolling dice, perform searches and scoring points.

Roll the dice, look for combinations, but be very careful with the cat 'Malevolent' and, above all, find the magic bones before your friends. Also help 'Abreu', the dog, and restore the magic that protects the forest! The animals are counting on you!

In "Where did my bone go?" (Onde foi parar meu osso?), your objective is to accumulate as many points as possible by collecting bones that are scattered and hidden throughout the forest.

Each turn, the player rolls and select dices in order to make combinations (pairs of symbols) and perform actions, which could be 'reveal hidden tokens on the forest' or 'take bones from other players', for example. Some actions are immediate and others can be done in next rounds.

The game ends when 6 magical bones were found and the player with most points is the winner!

Magical bone = worth 4 points
Common bone = worth 1 point

In Arctic Armies, two teams of penguins throw snowballs at each other to score points. It's part area control, part deck builder in which your 4x4 group of penguin troops first throw snowballs in a ranged attack phase and then go head to head in melee attack. You use cards to buff both ranged and melee attacks, or use special ability cards to get an edge on your opponent. Switch out your cards and arrange your penguins every round to outwit your opponent.

It's small enough to throw in your pocket for coffee shops, restaurants, or pubs. It plays in three 5 minutes rounds for a 15 minute, two player game that is simple enough for new gamers but different enough for veteran games who need a quick game fix.

ON YOUR MARKS, GET SET, GO!

BUM i BRUM. Rajd Malucha is the first puzzle-racing game in a new series featuring characters from a Polish bestselling book series for choldren.

Players participate in an exciting rally full of unpleasant and pleasant surprises. Choose your car, plan the race route, and… speed straight to the finish line! Bird droppings (BUM) or nitro (BRUM)? You never know what you’ll encounter on the road to victory. One thing is certain: you must be the first to cross the finish line!

—description from the publisher

Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Sunspot & Warlock welcomes two of the "New Mutants" to this tabletop game for the first time. Sunspot is known for his ability to absorb and manipulate solar energy while fighting foes, and the techno-organic alien life form known as Warlock allies with his friends to bring new defensive abilities to the table.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Weapon X & Maverick
description:
The Weapon X Program is coming to Marvel: Crisis Protocol in the Weapon X & Maverick expansion!

This product includes a new version of Wolverine: the savage, indestructible, and uncontrollable Weapon X. In addition, it features the player's first chance to paint and field Maverick, a former freedom fighter turned mercenary whose painful past led him to the Weapon X Program.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Innsmouth Conspiracy: Investigator Expansion
description:
Under the waves, an ancient civilization thrives, and a conspiracy brews. Only those outsiders who have heard the whispers of the deep can brave the maelstrom and discover the secrets of the abyss.

The Innsmouth Conspiracy: Investigator Expansion for Arkham Horror: The Card Game features five investigators and contains all of the player cards and customization options featured in the original "The Innsmouth Conspiracy" cycle of products. Grant blessings as the nun, Sister Mary; learn and practice various fields of study as the student, Amanda Sharpe; watch from the shadows as the spy, Trish Scarborough; perform arcane tricks as the magician, Dexter Drake; and brave the open seas as the sailor, Silas Marsh.

The Innsmouth Conspiracy: Investigator Expansion can be used to build or enhance investigator decks for any Arkham Horror: The Card Game scenario or campaign.
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – The Innsmouth Conspiracy
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Shrine to En Sabah Nur Terrain Pack
description:
With the Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Shrine to En Sabah Nur Terrain Pack, players can now add a shrine to En Sabah Nur — a.k.a., the Egyptian mutant Apocalypse — to their Marvel: Crisis Protocol collection. This thematic terrain provides a shrine and chests, as well as a one-shot for new play opportunities when using this terrain.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Dark Future Terrain Pack
description:
With mutants and humanity on the run, the Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Dark Future Terrain Pack brings a world to life where its strongest defenders were never able to stand against Apocalypse. This pack provides regeneration vats, energy siphons, and more to populate your table with thematic terrain. Plus, it offers new opportunities for play with two new one-shot cards.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Rejuvenation Chamber Ultimate Encounter
description:
The Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Rejuvenation Chamber Ultimate Encounter terrain adds more Apocalypse themed terrain to players' collection! This product also comes with a new Ultimate Encounter that introduces special rules and comes with all the gaming components needed to start playing.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Angel & Archangel introduces two new playables, Angel and his future self, Archangel.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Lands of the Mesozoic: Kickstarter Exclusive Cards
description:
A set of 15 Kickstarter exclusive cards (numbered 168-182) included with pledges of the game during the 2023 Kickstarter campaign. These were packaged outside of the game box and are not included inside any copies of the game.

This set consists of 9 herbivore and 6 carnivore cards not included in the game box or the rulebook components.

Get ready to unlock your imagination and make the impossible connections! In  Brain Line, your goal is to link two seemingly unrelated concepts with just one clever word that wows the crowd. But be careful—if everyone figures out your thoughts, you get nothing. With simple rules and endless twists, it’s the perfect party challenge that keeps everyone guessing!

① There will be a symbol between every two words on the table.
② Everyone secretly draws a designated symbol token and then writes a word on his or her answer card.

③ After everyone writes his word, face it up.

④ Everyone makes the guess and gets the points for every correct answer. But, if everyone figures out your symbol, you get nothing.

A new round: Change the table card and draw the designated token again.

After 5 rounds, the player with the most points wins the game.
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Change the course of history in your very own 15 Minute Cold War.

Choose from Fidel Castro's Republic of Cuba, the French Republic under the guidance of Charles de Gaulle, Walter Ulbricht's East Germany or the Commonwealth of Australia under Robert Menzies.

Build up your unique technology and culture, your military defences and special abilities while balancing the need for protection with the urge for aggression.

Form new alliances with your fellow countries and use all of your skill to remain the last leader standing and the next great superpower!

—description from the designer

boardgame: 15 Minute Cold War: Six Day War Edition
description:
Change the course of history in your very own 15 Minute Cold War.

Choose from the State of Israel under David Ben-Gurion or Egypt (as the United Arab Republic) guided by Gamal Abdul Nasser.

Build up your unique technology and culture, your military defences and special abilities while balancing the need for protection with the urge for aggression.

Form new alliances with your fellow countries and use all of your skill to remain the last leader standing and the next great superpower!

—description from the designer

Begin your Global War 1914 game before the conflict allowing the various Major Powers input into the Mexican Revolution, The First and Second Balkan Wars and the Italo-Turkish War as well as a series of new events to further change and impact the Great War beginning from 1912 instead of the usual 1914 start in the core game.

Endgame — Destroyevsky vs. Zerofearskyi is played between two opponents who move their pieces according to their turn on a board called the Battlefield.

The goal for each player is to eliminate the opponent’s HQ with its military leader, the general, present. The player who first achieves this goal has won the game.

The two opponents will have to move their pieces, acquire, distribute and deploy weaponry to eliminate the opponent’s pieces. They must also react to unforeseen events which may lead to the need for quick operational and tactical adjustments.

The game provides almost perfect information, with a randomizer adding a certain amount of luck.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Krycí jména: Bonusové karty – 10 let MINDOK
description:
Exclusive expansion with additional cards featuring Czech words, specifically designed for the Czech version of Codenames. 
 This limited-edition set of 10 bonus cards was released to celebrate the 10th anniversary of the Czech publisher MINDOK. 
 The deck contains the following cards: 
 
     (deck title card) 
     tanec / Zima mrtvých 
     ohňostroj / Carcassonne 
     dort / Citadela 
     pivo / Černé historky 
     balónek / Krycí jména 
     výročí / Vládci podzemí 
     zpěv / Pandemic 
     špunt / Agricola 
     chlebíček / Tučňáci na ledu 
     párty / Příběhy z kostek

A competitive turn-based dexterity racing game using flicking to advance discs on a modular track with jumps.
Similar to: PitchCar - Chirlito RACE differentiates itself with a unique configurable barrier system allowing extremely scaleable difficulty, splits, chicanes, run-offs etc.
It also features a solo mode and variant rules for novice/expert players.

—description from the designer

The Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Apocalypse expansion pack provides players with everything they need to add Apocalypse to their game, including the miniature, cards, two statues, and a terrain dais.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
A double-sided game board presenting two new maps for Terraforming Mars, each with new milestones and awards:


     Terra Cimmeria - Terraform the cratered highland region of Mars.
     Utopia Planitia - Terraform the northern lava plain of Mars.

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Good Soldiers Follow Orders Squad Pack
description:
Fans of The Bad Batch on Disney+ will enjoy bringing Crosshair and his villainous squad to the table in the Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Good Soldiers Follow Orders Squad Pack. This squad is lead by Crosshair, with secondary unit ES-04, and Elite Squad Troopers as support units.

—description from the publisher

Dive into a drop of water and experience the world of plankton! Equip yourself with traits. Fight microscopic monsters. Survive environmental challenges. Cooperate with others... But remember, there can only be one winner!

In this munchkin-like game you spend your turn upgrading your plankton, or by facing a threat. Overcome 3 of these threats, and win the evolutionary arm's race! You can always choose to help others (for the right price), but you can also make it harder for them by playing the right cards at the right time.

Two new and original Varia classes battle in this exciting, accessible stand-alone dueling game.

Varia is a game of tactical card play, powerful fantasy-themed classes, and unique player-controlled RNG.

A fast-paced 2+ player combat game, Varia has you select from a variety of classes that play and feel completely distinct from one another. Attack your opponent or defend against their blows with unique action cards that represent your chosen class. This box contains two such classes - the Zeal Angel and the Wrath Demon.

Zeal Angel - Spear in hand, you'll fly across the heavens, channel divine powers, and smite evil with angelic light. If your opponent won't back down, swoop in and make them regret challenging a Zealot of the Sixth Host!

Wrath Demon - Chains in hand, you'll fly through the sky, smash your enemies into the ground and drag them through the dirt. As they plead for mercy, you'll tempt them with power before claiming their soul for the First Legion of Hell!

This is a stand-alone release supporting 2 players. Additional classes are required to play with more than 2 players.

—description from the publisher
Expand the basic Cities game with a new achievement board. That includes the main attractions of the cities of Prague and Brno for even more variety.

Slither into the world of Snakers: A venomous new gaming experience!

Prepare to shed your old gaming habits and slide into the coiled excitement of Snakers, a game where the thrill of the hunt is just a hiss away! This serpentine adventure wraps you in its colorful embrace.

Snakers is more than just a game; it’s a test of your instincts. Are you ready to take on the challenge, or will you let it slither away?

Snakers is best described a snake themed shedding game where the goal is to empty your hand or have the least amount of cards in your hand when there are four complete snakes on the table.

You can achieve this by playing the card(s) from your hand where you connect to the card to the last played card of a snake using the correct colors. And of course you can shake things up by playing the hiss cards with varying results! Connect a large snake bite to make your opponent take three cards. Or block/reflect bites. Switch your full hand with another player and more... It's a game full of laughter and frustration.

The game was made for kids, families and students to have fun and interact!

Some of the main characteristics: 

     Fun for the whole family 2-7 players
     Endless replayability
     For the young ones as well, 4+
     Laughs and frustration
     Not too long, not too short 15-30 min
     Easy to learn
     Happy snakes , happy vibes


—description from the publisher

The Civic Game is a product of the Erasmus KA2 Project - ICE Informal Civic Education - aiming at disseminating Civic Education issues among adults.

The game is a simple roll and move with questions drawing from a guide on basic adult competencies on Justice, Economy, Environment and Health, with an additional category of questions on general adult learning methods.

Breakthrough Russia (“BR”) is a WWII strategy game that takes players back to the crucial period from Summer 1941 to Summer 1943, when the outcome of the war in the East hung in the balance.

A unique chit-pull system determines each player’s resources rather than which units may activate.

The game’s tempo is entirely up to the players, who decide on Pass, Attrition, or Offensive options each turn.
Historical features such as partisans, industrial production, armor superiority, weather, air transport, and Lend-Lease enhance the game’s realism.

Each game is guaranteed to be different due to the presence of variants such as General Vlasov's army,recruits from the Gulags, Turkish entry into the war, Katyusha rockets, and Vichy volunteers.

Breakthrough Russia, a game for two players with a low to moderate complexity level, is also suitable for solitaire play.

Given the low unit count of corps and armies, BR can be easily played in an afternoon, along with shorter scenarios for Barbarossa, Fall Blau, and Kharkov-Kursk.
The movement and combat systems are straightforward, quick to learn and easy to administer.

BR is a game that is fun, balanced, and offers a challenge to both newcomers and veteran war gamers.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Betrayal at House on the Hill: 3rd Edition – Deck of Lost Souls Promo Card
description:
Deck of Lost Souls Promo Event Card from Gencon 2024.

Text on the card is:

DECK OF LOST SOULS
A deck of cards sits on a nearby table.  You're inexplicably drawn towards it, compelled to play.  Dread grips you as the cards unveil glimpses of the future.

Each player in this room makes a sanity roll.

5+  Gain 2 Sanity.  Your gamble with fate proved fortunate - this time.

0- 4 Take 2 Mental damage.  You cannot unsee what has been seen.  A piece of your soul is forever lost.

boardgame: Betrayal at House on the Hill: 3rd Edition – Twitching Doll Promo
description:
Available at PAX West 2024.

card text is:

TWITCHING DOLL

Your come across a well-loved doll that makes you yearn for simpler times.  It's twitching unnervingly, but you still feel the desire to clutch it for comfort.

Make a Sanity roll.

6+  Gain 2 Sanity.  The mysterious life force within the toy calms as you press it against your cheek.  It likes you!

0 - 5 Take 2 Physical damage and 1 Mental damage.  As you hug the doll, you feel...  itchy.  That protruding black stuffing was spiders!  You try to brush them off but they're inescapable.

Zü, the city's hottest new zoo, needs animals for its new exhibits, and you're the agent for the job!
Players are representatives for animals seeking gigs at different exhibits around the zoo. Show off your animal talent and win the residency at Zü!

Through the Zoo is a meta game system that combines a variety of standard deck games. Hearts, Spades, Bridge, you name it! Players play hands of standard deck games and see who performs best in this card game system.

—description from the designer

A 5 card game compendium with 4 decks of custom cards comes with rules for several games:


     Chai Tea Jazz: Modern version of Coiffeur-Jass with all the fixings.
     Hodge Podge Dodgeball: Team Avoidance game as you play a must-not-follow trick-taking
     Bug House Millionaire: Climbing/shedding game about passing your partner cards in real-time
     Short Zoot Suit: Trick-taking game about short-suiting.
     Through the Zoo: Meta game about standard deck games


—description from the designer

Dreidel Dash is an 18 card Hanukkah race. Spin your dreidel card to move down the menorah and collect the most gelt to win. Plays in 10-15 minutes and is enjoyable by ages 8 and up. Go head to head or play with up to 4 using the 6 card expansion.

—description from the designer

Skybirds is a game for 2-4 players, where you’ll fight for dominance in the skies over the battlefields of the Great War (which later came to be known as World War I). Aerial combat is simulated by – and this is a little weird but work with us – winning rounds of a ladder-climbing, empty-your-hand card game. If only real war could be so civilized!

Players – known as Aces – start out even in skill, but as play progresses the winner of a given round of cards will be able to improve their  squadron incrementally more than the second-place finisher, both of whom will improve more than third place, etc.  Except in a 2-player match, the Ace left with cards at the end of the round gets no improvement at all.

After each victory, the more skillful Aces gain more powerful weapons (relative to those who finish the round behind them), and these weapons, represented by tokens, can be deployed for better bonuses during future rounds.  These tokens also unlock further progression down the path to victory, but if you use the most recently won bonus, you cannot make further progress along that path until you reclaim the spent token. The game ends when one player unlocks the final “Armistice” token, and the winner is the player who amassed the most victory points (VPs) during the War to End All Wars.

The game doesn’t have too many rules, and game play is quick, with each hand (a “Dogfight”) lasting about a minute, each round (a “Campaign Season”) lasting a few minutes and the overall game (“The Great War”) taking 20-30 minutes depending on players’ choices.

Dogfight = one full hand/trick
Campaign Season = one full round of tricks, ending when all but one of the players are out of cards
The Great War = a series of Campaign Seasons ended by a player acquiring their Armistice token

—description from the designer

400 questions from Matthew Kamm and renowned author and bird artist David Allen Sibley to test players' knowledge of birds and birding. Questions are arranged on 200 cards divided into five categories: Bird Identification, Bird Anatomy and Physiology, Bird Names, Birds in Culture, and Bird Behavior. Each card has one easy and one challenging question to choose from. Instructions are included for playing with teams, as a group, or pub trivia style.

An 18 card game of deduction and influence. Take on the role of either Santa or Krampus, playing positive or negative influence cards to win over children to your side. Santa knows if you've been bad or good, but Krampus has the power to change things...

This game was a finalist in the 2023 Buttonshy Holiday Design Challenge.

Utilizing hidden knowledge and deduction, you must play your opponent as much as you play the cards.

—description from the designer

Tír na nÓg: Blessings & Burdens expands your journey into the Otherworld with five powerful new modules. Mix and match them however you wish—each brings fresh twists, daring choices, and unexpected challenges. Every module reshapes setup, rules, and scoring, and several include solo play so you can test your fate alone.

Module 1 – New Geas Cards

New destinies await! Six fresh Geas cards (two per Saga row) open up bold new paths. Use them on their own, combine them with the base set, or dare to play asymmetrically with different cards for each player—if you’re ready, the Morrigan herself awaits you in Module 5.

Module 2 – Blessed Realms

What will you sacrifice? Each card you discard sways the fate of the Realms, shifting their value for endgame scoring. Do you burn a color to make a region thrive—or keep it to build your own legend?

Module 3 – The Lore of Places

Rise as a Mormaer, a ruler of regions. Claim dominance in a color to unlock special once-per-round powers and bend the story of your Saga to your will.

Module 4 – Triquetra

Honor the sacred unity of all things! Complete Triquetra goals to claim blessings—divine gifts that let you change a card’s rank, swap cards in your Saga, or even place a Bridge, a mystical new tool that bends space to connect cards diagonally or across gaps. Every player who reaches the goal that round is blessed, but only the swiftest earns the glory of being first.

Module 5 – Followers of the Morrigan (2+ players)

The Morrigan whispers… Draft and command your own unique set of Geas cards, giving each player a singular fate. You were going to try it anyway—now the rules of chaos have been written.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Roll dice to pull frogs along three card tracks to your side. Have two frogs at your end at the same time to win, but move fast because your opponent is pulling them too. To move a frog, you must match your dice roll to the next closest card. 18 cards, dice, and frogs included.

—description from the designer

Trader's Journey is a strategic retro-inspired adventure card game for 1-4 players, ages 13 and up, that runs for about 60 minutes for solo players, and 90-120 minutes for 2 or more players. Collect and manage coins, buy and trade precious loot, battle ferocious monsters, and explore the land of Arden as you attempt to make a name for yourself as a Trader... or fall trying.

Inspired by retro video games like The Legend of Zelda and Dragon Quest, Trader's Journey is set in a fantastical fantasy world brimming with ancient ruins, spooky dungeons, fearsome monsters, and awesome loot. You'll move your trader across the game board, complete journey cards, engage in trade rounds, and battle bosses in semi-cooperative gameplay as you attempt to hoard the most amount of coins in your pocket by the time the final boss has been defeated.

—description from the designer
family: Video Game Theme: The Oregon Trail

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Heroes For Hire
description:
In a last-ditch effort to drive the Heroes of Barcadia out of the dungeon, the Grand Drink Guardian has unleashed an ancient and powerful weapon, The Die of Barcane Chaos. The heroes flee from this new magical threat and seek refuge at the Plastered Pony, their favorite tavern in all of Barcadia.

The surly barkeep, Brewtus Pintsmasher, sends a plea far across the land. More heroes are needed to reclaim the kingdom's stolen drinks! Rewards abound for those who will take up the call!

Meet the new Heroes for Hire:


    Bigswigg (The Buzzkiller) - Do you know how much dynamite it takes to disintegrate a dragon? Biggswigg does! Beware high fives from this tinkerer.
    Sip-Jin (The Drunken Fist) - Hi-yah! This master of martial arts is ready to nunchuck her way to victory. Every drink makes her fighting style more unpredictable!
    Ontaprielle (The Alechemist) - Top of her class at the Academy of Mystical Mixology, she can brew a solution to any problem. She also holds the school's 100m dash record.
    Dram (The Aged & Oaken) - Do you know how much dynamite it takes to disintegrate a dragon? Biggswigg does! Beware high fives from this tinkerer!


When playing Heroes of Barcadia (the base game) simply swap in one of the Heroes for Hire for one of the base game or Party Pack heroes. Shuffle the new Power-Ups and Loot Cards into their respective decks and you're ready to dungeon crawl! Huzzah!

Mensa Select winner in 2025!

Radioactive waste from local power plants has granted you and your fellow farm animals both sentience and an insatiable hunger... for power. Now you must take up the weapons of your former masters, the humans, to fight both them and each other for control of the countryside.

Farms Race is a 1-2 hour strategy game that parodies peaceful Eurogames like Catan and Agricola. Players play as the Supreme Leader of a species of farm animal racing to accumulate 10 Victory Points by building Barns and Armories, recruiting Spies, building the most nukes, and conquering opponents' regions with their herds.

The Deluxe Edition contains everything you need to play Farms Race plus an expansion's worth of content, pieces for a 5th player, higher quality components, and bonus goodies to build out your game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Die of Barcane Chaos
description:
Roll at your own risk with the Die of Barcane Chaos! Get ready to toss strategy out the tavern window—this magical mayhem-maker changes the rules by reworking how Power-Up Cards behave. Whether you’re boosting your own abilities or throwing a wrench into your rival’s plans, one roll of this die can shift the entire dungeon crawl.

Each side triggers a different Barcadian effect, decoded using the included Chaos Scroll. It’s unpredictable, it’s unfair, and it’s exactly the kind of spicy nonsense your dungeon run’s been thirsting for.

Product Specifications:
- Heroes of Barcadia Die of Barcane Chaos
- Requires Heroes of Barcadia to play (sold separately)! 
- Mechanic: Dungeon crawling, procedurally generated, player elimination, roll/spin and move, collection

Includes:
- 1 Die of Barcane Chaos
- 1 15" x 8.5" inch fabric Chaos Scroll reference
- Barcadian unpredictability at its finest

Includes Heroes of Barcadia Die of Barcane Chaos
Full game NOT included

—description from the publisher

The Deluxe edition include the base game and Creature Caravan: Wanderers packaged inside of the base game's box. It also comes with metal coins and wooden fruit, bag, food, and zombie pieces.

A new threat lurks in the land of Arzium. Ember zombies swarm from volcanic lands to the south, their charcoal bodies the walking hives of evil fire fairies. The closest haven is the city of Eastrey, where a powerful artifact protects the inhabitants. You must travel across the desert and the plains, over mountains and through red canyons, helping wandering creatures of every shape and size to the safety of the city.

In Creature Caravan, players build a card tableau of creatures while traveling through a magnificent and dangerous land. Players take turns simultaneously, placing dice on actions on their cards, moving their caravan on the map, and playing new creature cards. Players also compete to trade goods and rare coins in a shared market, search the mysterious white towers, and fight ember zombies as they travel. The game ends when one player reaches Eastrey. The player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
In this game, players become alchemists, combining elemental cards to reach 26 points. Strategic card management and unique special abilities create dynamic gameplay, where clever combinations are key to victory. Simple mechanics with layers of depth make it accessible for all players.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Five Leagues From The Borderlands: 3rd Edition – Paths in the Wilderness
description:
Paths in the Wilderness is a major new expansion that provides you with an array of different options for your Five Leagues from the Borderlands games. Where the first expansion (The Ancient Land of Dust) was aimed at one type of adventure in a specific region of the world, this expansion is all about the adventure you want to create, brimming with options for almost every aspect of the game system, whether it is characters to create, activities to perform, creatures to fight (or recruit), or new ways to use the game rules.

All the material contained within is optional, you might want to use some of these rules for one campaign, and different rules for another. Pick and choose however you want to play!

This huge 97-page PDF expansion is full of new options for your Five Leagues from the Borderlands games:

New origins! Deep Kin, Dwarfs, Elriki, Felids, Gnawlings, Kobolds, and Skysworn.
New mystical paths! Stone Mage, Shade, Trickster, Prescient, and Path Watcher.
Optional rules including Heroic Mode, Hit Points, Enemy Infiltration, and Weather. 
New Enemies! Dare you face vampiric Bloodstained, lawless Outlaw Bands, the deathless Silent Battalion, or the Retinue of the Wolf Prince!
Optional World Rules including Journeys, Inhospitable Map Terrain, Roaming Aberrations, and Sea Travel. 
New Activities such as Fishing, Bounties, Caravans, and Lore Research, 
And a whole chapter on Scenario Creation for players who enjoy creating their own adventures either for their solo games or as Game Master for another player.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Five Leagues From the Borderland

description:
Race to 15 victory points to win the game! Time of War is a game of bidding, trading, alliance building, resource management, and combat. All actions are simultaneous so there is no waiting for your turn!

Bidding Phase    - Bid for control of resource boards
   Battle Phase     - Use battle cards and dice in combat
   Voting Phase     - Vote for control of the white flag
   Collection Phase - Collect resources from cities and banks

Repeat the phases until a player buys their 15th victory point.

—description from the designer

Betray your friends. Rule the arena in this card game.

Enter the arena of Brutus: each player embodies a Ludus, a house of gladiators whose survival depends on the wise choices of its leader, the Lanista. Use their unique ability to your advantage. Bet wisely on which fight you'll win: whether your gladiators win the laurels of victory or lose miserably, each of them will be one more step toward your rise.

—description from the designer

Two modules to flavor your Splendor game, from "cities of splendor", with minor adjustments.

Dominate Trade within the Cities and discover new victory conditions for each game. The Cities replaces the noble tiles with 3 different city tiles (randomly taken from a pool of 14). The city tiles are objectives (in prestige points and/or development cards) and you need to fulfill one of them in order to win.

Create Trading Posts and leverage their unique powers to maximize your prestige. The Trading Posts are special bonuses you earn by acquiring an array of development cards: more prestige points from the 1st noble tile you receive, an extra token when you choose the "Take 2 gem tokens of the same color" action, and so on.

THE AIM OF THE GAME - SH!THEAD isn’t about winning, it’s not to lose! We’ve mixed things up a bit and created a hilarious card-based game with dares. It’s a great game to play with friends and will provide hours of entertainment.
