DARES TO MAKE YOU TENSE – all players risk having to do one of the many silly and fun dares included in the pack. Don’t worry, if you’re 14 or older they’re suitable for you but the tension among players WILL rise as who knows what the dare will be?

FUN TO PLAY AGAIN AND AGAIN – you’ll never know what the sequence of situations will be but one thing’s for sure, this is one of those card games that’s guaranteed to cause laughter! You’ll want to play all night, just be careful not to wake the neighbours.

CAN YOU PLAN NOT TO LOSE? – no exceptional knowledge of any specialist subject is needed to enjoy SH!THEAD, but a perfect combination of skill and luck will help. You’ll never know what your dare is going to be, so you can’t prepare in advance, sorry!

SUITABLE FOR TEENAGERS AND ADULTS – players aged 14 years and over can play this unique and fun card game, packed with fun filled dares. An average game takes between 2 and 7 minutes to complete and can be enjoyed with 2 to 5 players.

—description from the publisher

Kalologos Bible Board Game is a board game designed to effectively test and expand your knowledge of the Bible. At the same time, players can enjoy fellowship and deepen their understanding of the Bible.

Kalologos also provides a practical expression of Christians’ interaction with the Bible and God. This is done through the allocation of talents and burdens, as well as forgiveness and opportunities to invest.

The goal of the game is to reinforce the message that people should make the most of their God-given talents and that you can always bring your burdens to the Cross of Jesus Christ.

In Kalologos, players journey through the Old and New Testament, collecting talents by answering questions and completing action tasks, while also gaining burdens as they progress. There are opportunities to invest your talents, and at the foot of the Cross, all burdens are unconditionally taken away. First to fill their talent board wins.

Kalologos is fun for believers and a great game for faith-based gatherings for young and old alike.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Kalologos Bybel-bordspel is ’n bordspeletjie wat gemik is daarop om jou Bybelkennis doeltreffend te toets en uit te brei. Terselfdertyd kan spelers die samesyn geniet en die Bybel beter leer ken.

Kalologos gee ook praktiese uiting aan Christene se interaksie met die Bybel en God. Dit word gedoen deur die toekenning van talente en laste, asook vergifnis en Woekergeleenthede.

Die doel van die spel is om die boodskap tuis te bring dat mense moet woeker met hul God-gegewe talente, en ook dat jy altyd jou laste na die Kruis van Jesus Christus kan bring.

Met Kalologos toer spelers deur die Ou- en Nuwe Testament en versamel in die proses talente deur die beantwoording van vrae en aksie-opdragte , maar kry ook laste by; soos spelers aanbeweeg. Daar is ook geleenthede om met jou talente te woeker. Aan die voete van die Kruis word al jou Laste onvoorwaardelik weggeneem.

Kalologos is pret vir gelowiges en ‘n lekker spel vir geloofsbyeenskomste vir oud en jonk.

—description from the publisher

Lucky Diamonds, jack of all trades, stood outside the doors of the Red Wyvern with two others – the first Myndara, an enchantress of the Black - not black sorcery but the color black; as well as Griff, a knight of the Old Ways in gleaming steel plate.  Soon they would start their quest – to rescue the four daughters of Good King Redthorne – stolen by Malkrit, the Evil Sorcerer, and twin brother to the King, but they needed to hire a few fighters first, which is why they were outside the inn.  They soon found three adequate fighters and the six of them left town, en-route to whatever fate had in store for them.

Four Suit Dungeon Delve is an attempt to play the classic dungeon adventure with a deck of playing cards.  No dice are used in the game.   Once the basic rules are learned, a game takes about 10 minutes.

Your party of six must defeat foul monsters, dodge fiendish traps and the powers of Darkness itself in order to rescue the king’s lovely daughters.  The four young ladies (the four Queen cards) were stolen by the evil sorcerer and are imprisoned somewhere in his lair.  If the party finds all four, they gain the reward of some additional luck, but they must make their way to the exit and escape.  The game is played until the deck runs out.

If at least one of your party survives the adventure, the game is won.  The princesses can’t be harmed while in your delvers’ care, unless they fail in their quest.  If so, the unfortunate girls are recaptured by the monsters that serve the evil sorcerer and the king will need to send another party to save them.

Rules are included for parties ranging in size from 1 to 12, as well as several scenarios including Double Decker Delving and The Quest to Save the World.   Other sections include Expanded Characters, Expanded Monsters as well as an optional rule you will probably need to use – Fleeing from Monsters.

To play, you will need a deck of playing cards with jokers and a piece of paper to keep track of party strength values.

—description from the designer

Take a walk on the Wild Side as you compete with other players to get rid of your cards first in this exciting card-shedding game, for 2 to 8 players, that combines skill and luck. The goal is to be the first player to play all 20 of your cards while trying to catch other players with unplayed cards, which count as penalty points against them.

Play multiples of cards of the same number, straights of the same color or the all powerful honey badger card to make your turns as lasting and effective as possible! If you can't play a card of equal or lesser value than what is on top of the stack you are forced to add the stack to your hand, which is usually a bad thing...or is it??? Wild Side is quick to learn but has many strategic nuances that will keep players coming back for more. This addictive game is fast, fun and feral!

All you want to do is make some new friends.

You are in the CoA - the Center of Attention - and there are plenty of potential new friends to choose from, but you only have space for six.
You have to choose wisely which friends you want to have in your circle and which ones better not.
In order to make a new friend, you can either use any advantages you have or invest some of your resources - your Interest, Time, Energy and a little bit of magic.
But you still cannot become friends with just anyone you invested your ITEMs in, you also have to have some common ground with them or - even better - find out if you are a really good match.
Only then you bring your new friends to your circle, where you can deepen the friendships you already have, become close friends with at least some of them, but also might have to deal with some "issues".
Because "life" - in the form of the other players - will also "happen". 
You don't just (have to) have friends, you also have to keep them. 
Friends affect each other and sometimes you might even want to play a friend in order to mess around with the other circles as well.

The goal of the game is to have six close friends as well as to make your own circle of friends the best possible version of itself. This means, the more different colors, and the more different numbers your friends have, the better.

The Game can be played in a competitive or a cooperativ mode and is played in two phases per round.

In the first phase, the social phase, you have to place the advantage token and invest all of the 4 resources you have per round. You may split them between meeting new friends, getting your hands on a special ability or working on some of the friendships you already have. 
In case you're meeting a new friend or are interested in a special ability you'll also roll your die in order to determine any matches or some common ground.

In the second phase you play cards/your friends in order to bring new friends to your circle, work on friendships you already have, play an affect or use a special ability.
 The competitive game ends when the first player has six close friends in their circle. You then score your points to determine which player won.

The cooperative game ends after a preset number of rounds. You only win, if all players have six close friends in their own circle after the last round. The scoring can further determine how well you've done.

The game also includes ways to modify your game in order to make it easier or harder to play or more simplier to learn.

(Each game comes with 72 friends, which have been randomly selected and distributed to each copy of the game out of a total of 216 friends. And since most of the 216 friends have their own unique affect and no game contains the same set of cards each copy of the games provides players with a different game experience.)

—description from the designer

Originally available at Pax Unplugged 2024.

The Confusing Lands Promo set adds 6 new cards to the world of Confusing Lands, these cards have new scoring conditions and special double symbol spaces.

These Promo cards will add variety to your game as well as extend the game by one round!

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Classic Kickstarter Pack

Expand your adventures with the original Kickstarter-exclusive promos from both the Heroes of Barcadia Base Game and Party Pack - unlocked during the the first Heroes of Barcadia Kickstarter campaign. (This package only available through the Heroes for Hire Kickstarter campaign.)

16x Loot Cards
12x Power-ups
4x Room Tiles

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: House Rules Booster Pack
description:
Completionists, this one's for you. Unleash your inner chaos goblin with the Heroes of Barcadia DIY Booster Pack! Add a new layer of strategy and absurdity to your dungeon experience with all the House Rules from the first Heroes of Barcadia Kickstarter and more!

Whether you're spicing up game night or unleashing your punniest creations, this pack expands the way you play the game with 20 House Rules Cards, 8 DIY House Rules Cards, and 1 Reference Card.

Product Specifications:

Heroes of Barcadia House Rules Card Booster Pack
Includes:

100% waterproof

Move your pawns strategically around the playing area to either evade your opponent’s pucks or STRIKE DOWN THEIR  PAWNS. You will take turns moving and flicking discs at each other’s pawns. A pawn that is knocked over will not get to act that round. Choose your next target carefully, dexterity is important, but strategic strikes will be just as valuable. Hit each of your opponent’s pawns 3 times for victory!

En un futuro apocalíptico, un virus devastador se ha propagado, acabando con la humanidad y convirtiéndola en seres vacíos y sin vida que solo desean devorarse unos a otros. En medio de este caos, debes cooperar con los pocos sobrevivientes restantes y retomar el control de la ciudad antes de que los muertos marchen hacia ti y te devoren.
Se dice que el virus se transmite por mordeduras o heridas abiertas, causando un dolor intenso y un deseo insaciable de devorar a aquellos que estén cerca. Sin embargo, en el ambiente tiene un efecto diferente: la vegetación crece de manera desmesurada, especialmente las flores de las plantas, creando un paisaje desolador pero colorido en el horizonte.
En este juego, asumirás el papel de los sobrevivientes que protegen una localidad de una gran ciudad. Deberás trabajar en equipo para cubrir la mayor cantidad de espacio posible, escapar de los zombies e intercambiar armas y recursos para ayudar a controlar las hordas de muertos vivientes. Además, tendrás que aprovechar al máximo la poca gasolina disponible para distribuir estas ayudas.

In an apocalyptic future, a devastating virus has spread, wiping out humanity and turning it into empty, lifeless beings who only want to devour each other. Amidst this chaos, you must cooperate with the few remaining survivors and take back control of the city before the dead march towards you and devour you.
In this game, you will take on the role of survivors protecting a town in a large city. You will need to work as a team to cover as much space as possible, escape from zombies, and trade weapons and resources to help control the hordes of the undead. In addition, you will need to make the most of the little gasoline available to distribute these aids.
Make trios of cards to attack zombies in your town, or make pairs of symbols to help your team, eliminate special zombies to make the boss zombie appear and finish him off to obtain victory with your entire team.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: Duel for Middle-earth – The Shire Promo Tile
description:
The Shire - Play a visible card, from the current chapter, to your play area, whether it’s available or not.
Unlike other Landmarks, do not place a Fortress on the central board.

Book Surfers is a strategic adventure board game for 2-4 players (ages 8+), with a playtime of 60-90 minutes. In the game, players take on the role of students at the Academy of Professor Joshua Bookheart, who invented a technology to travel through the pages of books. After the Professor disappears into a mysterious dimension called the Archive, players must journey through these literary worlds, gather resources, and solve challenges to rescue him from the terrifying Bookworm. With a blend of resource management and strategy, players will compete to gain victory points and find the missing professor.

—description from the designer

description:
Zachraň poklad ("Save the Treasure") is unique children's game with a double-layered board and moving parts. A ship carrying precious treasure has wrecked in Kvido, and its coins have scattered across the ocean floor. Fortunately, the ship's captain survived and asked his diver friends to retrieve his gold coins from the sea bed before the pirates arrive. Can you help?

You will navigate the sea using five means of transportation: a whale, an anchor, a basket, an underwater whirlpool, and a submarine. Let’s embark on an adventure!

—description from the publisher

Lap of the Gods is a set of rules for fighting ancient and medieval battles using tabletop miniatures. They can be used with any scale of figures, and with any basing method, provided that the figures are grouped into units of equal frontage.

A typical game has around 15 units on each side and should  take under 3 hours to play. The aim of the rules has been to limit the detail and focus on the choices available to the players to make the game challenging. Both players are involved at all times, avoiding the “my turn, your turn” philosophy of other rules.

Games are designed to be played on an area of 180cm by 120cm, but smaller or larger tables or boards can be used. If using 15mm figures a typical unit will be 8cm wide.

—description from the rulebook

The Battle of Lodz was to become one of the largest and most complex battles of the manoeuver-period of World War 1. Deep operations, flank manoeuvers and encirclements under harsh conditions made command and control chaotic.

Mud & Blood recreates this setting making it a perfect re-encounter battle where both players need to carefully plan their moves in order not to create a disaster.

description:
Goblin Party is a quick tactical hero-crushing card game where players are underdogs of fantasy and take revenge on those pesky Heroes for years of being the favorite punching bags.

The first Player to satisfy the appetite of 3 Warchiefs around the cooking pot wins the game! Each hero slain is used as an ingredient for the cooking pot to please the Warchiefs. Defeating the most Heroes is the key to winning!

Before each game begins, players choose a Warchief. This Warchief changes the way you play with your deck each game.

Each turn you play 1 Goblin to create Parties together with your fellow Goblin Clans to attract Heroes. 3 Heroes are placed next to the draw pile in the middle of the table. There can be multiple Parties that consist of multiple clans.

Each player has over 20 unique Goblins in their deck. The trick is to play your Goblins in the best way possible. Every Goblin card has a unique skill that changes the playing field drastically.  No game is the same as there are more than 30+ Heroes that are always randomized and you will have a different Warchief each game, completely altering your playstyle.

—description from the designer

Can you rock the game board in Wippe-lig! to line up your discs?

To set up, place the wooden seesaw game board on the table, then place one white disc in each of the four grooves. Each player takes five discs in a color of their choice. On a turn, you either:


    Place one of the discs in your supply into any groove on the raised side of the game board, or
    Remove any disc already in a groove, then place it into a groove on the raised side of the game board.


To end your turn, tilt the game board. If three or four of a player's discs are now in a line, whether in the same groove or across multiple grooves with no other discs between them, that player wins, regardless of whose turn it is.

If 3-4 white discs are in a line after tilting the game board, remove these discs from play for the remainder of the game. Note that removing these discs might cause other discs to roll and set up a winning line.

In this game you get a Topic card and a Phrase card. You will be talking about the topic for 30 seconds. Your goal is to mention at least one of the phrases, while the other players try to guess which phrase(s) you used. Each player who guesses correctly gets a point, while you get a point for each opponent who didn't guess correctly. If you choose to include the harder of the two phrases on the Phrase card, you get an extra point because they are generally harder to sneak in.

Prepare to lead the forces of the Army of the Republic of Vietnam (ARVN) in **Drawn Swords**, the highly anticipated expansion to *'65 Squad Battles in the Jungles of Vietnam*. Featuring a rich array of new units, this expansion introduces the pseudo-powerful M24 Chaffee, M41 Walker Bulldog, and the formidable T-55 tanks, giving you new tactical possibilities and strategic depth.

Included in the box is a full sheet of beautifully designed counters, adding new ARVN and NVA units to your forces. Also, experience the immersive action on a stunning new map board, masterfully illustrated by acclaimed artist **Marc von Martial**. Additionally, the expansion includes 9 new action cards to add to your possibilities.

o 6 new scenarios.
o A new, geomorphic mounted map.
o 1 sheet of ARVN and NVA counters.
o 9 new action cards.
o A box to keep it safe.

description:
Experience the intensity of Vietnam’s most iconic battles with **Kickin' Ass**, the newest scenario expansion for *'65 Squad Battles in the Jungles of Vietnam*. This gripping magazine features **eight historically accurate scenarios**, bringing together the combined might of the United States Army, Marine Corps, and ANZAC forces as they face off against the determined North Vietnamese Army (NVA) and Viet Cong (VC).

Each scenario plunges you into the heat of battle, where strategic thinking and tactical prowess are essential. From jungle ambushes to full-scale assaults, every scenario is crafted to reflect the real-world engagements that shaped the Vietnam War.


        Note:** You must own *'65 Squad Battles in the Jungles of Vietnam* and the USMC/ANZAC expansion to play **Kickin' Ass**. Prepare for an intense test of your battlefield command!
    


Components:
•    1 x 12-page scenario magazine.

description:
Payoff Pitch Baseball: 2023 Cards is a set of 1,500 cards — including players and parks — printed on high-gloss heavy cardstock for use with the Payoff Pitch Baseball base game.

L/R splits on batter cards as with all Payoff Pitch Baseball sets.

Doomsayers is a three-dimensional area control game where you play as a deity trying to convert followers without bringing about the end of the world. Throughout the game, waves of fireballs and asteroids fall through the four-tiered board, dealing destruction wherever they land. Move your prophets on the ground to convert followers by warning cities of their impending doom and convincing them that only you can save them. Move your seraphs three-dimensionally to deflect the fireballs and asteroids… protect your followers or destroy your opponent’s. But if players allow too many asteroids to crash into the ground, the world will be demolished and all players lose. If you survive the storm, whichever team has the most followers wins.

KEY FEATURES:


     Intuitive Three-Dimensional Gameplay: Get immersed in the world and strategize in ways you never have before with fully three-dimensional movement on a four-tiered board.
     Alternate Game Modes: Play solo, 1v1, 1v2, or 2v2 with each mode providing a unique experience while using the same intuitive ruleset.
     Variable Setup: City locations, deities, and storm cards are randomized at the beginning of each game, ensuring maximum replayability.


—description from the designer

Partygame in which words and phrases are described by the players. The twist of the game is that all words in that description have to start with a certain, random letter. That letter is decided by rolling a die. You can either use words starting with that letter or prepend it to other words. The game is played in teams.

—user summary

description:
Świętuj z Portalem (Celebrate with Portal Games) is a small, simple 2 player game, added as a surprise to orders during the celebration of the 25th anniversary of the publishing house (September 5-8, 2024).

It consists of only 4 tiles and a score sheet with 8 goals and symbols to cross off. It doesn't even have a box.

Draw 2 tiles and cross out the symbols visible on them on your sheet, then choose one category in which you expect to score the most points. Remember, however, that this category will be unavailable to you until the end of the game.

The winner will be the player who collects more points after 8 rounds, i.e. in each of the available categories... if one of them does not exceed 10 madness points before, thus winning the game before the end of 8 rounds.

description:
Earn your way to the top by getting rid of your cards Fastest.
You are also trying to get "The Best Seat in the House"
After 7 hands, the person with the most points, wins.

Before you start, designate one seat as the MVP seat. You compete for that seat in each hand, to the right is #2, and so on, all the way to the last seat, the rookie.

Rookie starts the deal, deals all cards out starting with the MVP.  Once dealt, check your cards for some special cards(Home Run, Strike out, Wild Pitch, etc.) Certain combinations can make you the immediate winner of the hand or help you get rid of your cards faster.

At the start, the Rookie must give their 2 best cards to the MVP, who then gives them any 2 cards. Then if even number of players, the 2nd seat and 2nd to last exchange 1 card.  in odd number, the middle seat plays what they are dealt.

Whoever has the Play Ball card, leads, then you can either play or pass, but you must play a card lower than the top card.  Play continues until everyone Passes.  Last card played wins the round, then starts the next round.  in the rest of the hands whoever leads can also play a set of cards, and then all players have to play a set of same number of cards, of a lower value.

When you have played all your cards, you have "gone out" and collect a 5 pt coin, and become the MVP for the next hand.  The last player with cards becomes the Rookie for the next hand.

After 7 rounds, the person with the most points wins the game.
An old Portuguese Monopoly-copy game created and published by the now extinct Portuguese publisher, Karto

The faction that plays completely differently from the other four! With a unique approach to Development, Conflicts, and Presence, this expansion focuses on assassination, diplomacy, and scoring Victory Points in an entirely new way!

boardgame: Danger the Game: Whack a Moley
description:
A standalone expansion for the game designed to prepare you for every dangerous situation imaginable and unimaginable! Use your skills, tools, and imagination to devise a foolproof plan to save the day. Compete to convince the Victim your rescue plan is best. Worried someone will steal your thunder? Play a Plot Twist and trip them up!

162 cards divided into Danger, Skill, Tool, and Plot Twists. Family-friendly, party game for 3+ players. Introduces ALL-NEW round modifying Wild Plot Twists!

—description from the publisher

Cats and caverns is a dungeon and dragons inspired cat themed board game! This is a one to four player game for ages 8 and up. It is a tile laying dungeon crawl where you explore the dungeon together or solo to find treasure and fight monsters! Once the board has been fully explored you have to face off with the final boss! There are multiple boss options and this game has a kid friendly version and harder version for the more seasoned players. The four classes you can choose from are currently ranger, mage, barbarian and rogue. Each class has a custom miniature you can paint, and you can insert your own sets of dice for all of the dice hoarders out there, otherwise a base set of dice will be included.

Game overview

Game set up
Roll out the mat and place the three decks in their corresponding spots on the end of the board, do the same with the cave and surface tiles being separate.
Every player will pick a class and will each have a player mat, a figure and a colored set of dice.

Turn overview
Start your turn by rolling for speed, this will determine how many spaces you can move orthogonally. 
After rolling you now have three choices, you may pick up to one

     Flip up and resolve a new tile and move onto it
     Summon the shop
     Fight a wandering monster


If you pass over or land on another space as another player you may choose to trade, you may trade anything except xp and stress.
If you roll low on movement you exhaust yourself and gain one stress for each additional tile you move past your initial roll.

Game play
When you flip up a new tile, resolve the corresponding iconography. There are three separate symbols that will match one of the decks of cards. Monsters, events, and loot. If there is more than one symbol on the tile you may resolve them in which ever order you choose.
If the tile reveals the monster symbol you draw a monster card and initiate combat.
When it comes to  combat you may choose to solo or ask for help.
For combat Solo, you need to roll for both stats, the three combat skills are DX, dexterity, ST strength and MG magic. Every cat will have different dice for each stat depending on your class. (as marked on your player mat) You choose one of your weapons per combat. You also add any bonuses from your level or companions. You then roll your corresponding die to each stat and with your bonuses you need to meet or beat the numbers on the card.
For combat together you may receive the help of one other cat that is within 1 tiles space away from you orthogonally or diagonally. You both roll for each stat with bonuses and take the higher of the two rolls for each.

Results
If you roll the same number or higher you win and gain two xp tokens and draw however many loot cards are listed on the card
If you fought together you split the two xp, one each. And the one who initiated the fight has the negotiation power for the loot. You may not trade xp or stress but you can trade anything else (including starter weapons.)
If you lost a roll you still have to roll for the other stat and for each loss gain one stress.

End game
The end game will trigger when you fill every tile on the board, remaining monsters do not effect this.
You will resolve the last tile as normal before starting the boss phase.
After the last tile has been resolved the player who placed that tile gets to choose
You can fight the boss alone
You can fight with those adjacent to you
You can collectively decide to fight it all together regardless of where everyone is on the board.
The boss will gain the board bonus according to where it was summoned
You must roll for all three stats
If you lose a roll to the boss it gains a death token, this is per player so one player losing one roll is one token but if more than one player rolls and you all fail it will gain one death token per player who lost a roll.
If you all beat the rolls against the boss you win!
If you wish the player with the highest level can be the winner of the winners. :)

Special rules
If you draw the cat door tile you place that door on your space and the other door on any other space explored or not, if you place it somewhere not explored place a new tile face down underneath it. If that tile is a wall draw a new one.
If a tile at any point in the game prevents you from moving or finishing the board draw a new tile.
If you have the ball thrower and your fighting the happy dog you gain +3 instead of the +2 to your strength
If a weapon gives you reach, this is for helping other players. +1 reach allows you to help up to two tiles away instead of one, and you do not need to use the weapon that gave you reach to assist in that combat.

For advanced play use the characters special abilities and add the bonuses listed on the side of the mat to the monsters stats.

—description from the designer

A tableau building game where everyone is racing to invest in technologies that give abilities, profit, points, or patents. Each turn a player either uses a card or refreshes all used cards.
Cards may be played using an already extant card power, and paid for with cards in hand as cash or face down cards that are cash. Patents (with points) are gained via card powers, and there are a set of achievements for "most of" that are randomly selected each game.
First to 12 points (10 in the tutorial mode) wins.

—user summary

Imwah is an abstract strategy game that combines the classic card play of trick-taking games with Chess-like movements to create a simple, yet satisfying strategic and tactical experience for 2 players.

The goal of the game is to move 2 of your pawns to your opponent's base.

Each player has an identical hand of 11 cards that consists of 3 suits and 2 special cards. The board is a 6x6 grid with squares corresponding to the suits.

Turns are very simple. Starting with the leader, players play 1 card. The winner of the trick is the player who played the highest card of the lead suit. Then, starting with the winner, each player will move 1 pawn.

The choice of which card you play is very important because you may only move a pawn from a space corresponding to the suit of your played card. In addition, the rank of the played card will determine how you can move that pawn. The possible movements are similar to the various pieces in Chess.

The game will end if someone reaches their opponent's base twice, if someone captures all of their opponent's pawns, or at the end of 2 rounds (11 tricks each), at which point there will be a final scoring to determine the winner.

—description from the publisher

It's a communication game where everyone can enjoy talking with each other about real problems.
Tarot reading has been developed as a fun game to play!
You can consult your worries and feel like a fortune teller with the original tarot cards that can be loosely enjoyed even for the first time, while maintaining the deep meaning of tarot cards.
The game can be played face-to-face, online, or alone as an easy way to enjoy tarot reading.
One person is the questioner, and the others are the fortune tellers, and they draw tarot cards and get inspiration from the cards or connect them to keywords or patterns.
The person who gives the best fortune-telling wins.
Let's find out what kind of fortune-telling will make the person think "I like that!

This is a full deck version of Loose Tarot Readings (2021).

—description from the designer

Two additional dystopian scenarios that can be added to the core game, Dryland and Cyber Zombies.
In the Dryland dystopia, players go on expeditions to discover water resources hidden beneath the surface that may survive and be used in the future. The basic game mechanic used is bag management.
In the Cyber Zombies dystopia, you stand at ground zero, killing zombies and trying to help as many people escape as possible. The basic game mechanics of this dystopia are area movement and dice rolling.

—description from the publisher

The year is 425 AD. The Roman Empire, once the world's greatest superpower, is crumbling under relentless barbarian invasions, internal strife, and dwindling resources. As Emperor, the weight of civilization rests squarely on your shoulders. Can you hold the empire together, or will Rome be overrun?

In Rome Must Fall, every roll of the dice determines the fate of your empire. You'll allocate your precious resources to defend six key regions: Gaul, Hispania, Illyria, Africa, Italia, and the vital Mediterranean sea lanes. Each turn, you must carefully decide where to send your Imperial dice to stop the advance of marauding barbarian tribes, from the fearsome Visigoths and Vandals to the Huns and Franks.

This solitaire Roll & Write wargame challenges you with tough decisions each round. The barbarians will never stop advancing, and your resources will never be enough to defend everywhere at once. You’ll need to manage your defenses, rally your legions, and fortify your most critical regions to stand any chance of survival.

With fast-paced dice allocation, escalating threats, and constant pressure, every decision could mean the difference between holding back the tide or watching your empire collapse. Will you safeguard the Eternal City, or will history witness Rome's final days?

—description from the publisher

Each player positions his ship, loaded with a sledge, at one of the starting spaces of a longitude with the blue-dotted Arctic Circle in the sea. Anyone who only wants to travel with the sledge can start with just that in Greenland or Siberia at the Arctic Circle. Each player only rolls the dice once. The sailors roll the blue sea die, the sledge drivers roll the green ice die.
On the sea and ice dice, points 1 or 2 mean forward on the longitude, the points in a circle mean sideways (right or left) on the latitude.
White for the ships: collision with an iceberg, damage to the ship, which starts again.
White for the sledges: snowstorm. The sledge cannot move any further until the next roll.
Black: the ship stops once due to adverse winds.
If the ships reach the ice zone (white-dotted latitude), the ship stops and the journey continues with the sledge. If a sledge has moved 3 spaces away from its departure space(ship or Arctic Circle), the traveler is forced by a lack of provisions to shoot a polar bear with the red hunting die in order to be able to continue.
The red hunting die means:
Black = miss:
Red = the shooter is wounded by the bear, the player must miss a turn;
White = killed bear, which is placed next to the sleigh.
If a bear is killed, the traveler continues the sleigh ride to the pole with the ice die on the next roll until he is 3 spaces away from the bear (green meeple), then he must go hunting again and so on until the pole is reached.
The first player to reach the pole wins the game.

—user summary

In this game you get a Topic card and a Phrase card. You will be talking about the topic for 30 seconds. Your goal is to mention at least one of the phrases, while the other players try to guess which phrase(s) you used. Each player who guesses correctly gets a point, while you get a point for each opponent who didn't guess correctly. If you choose to include the harder of the two phrases on the Phrase card, you get an extra point because they are generally harder to sneak in.

Water animals play hide-and-seek among the coral reef plants. Players take on the role of seahorses, whose task is to find all the animals hidden behind the reef plants and get a shell with a pearl. In your turn, place your seahorse on a resource and/or ship to get resources. If you have resources at the end of your turn, discover a water animal and enrich the coral reef. Whoever reaches the white pearl first is the winner.

-description from designer

CLANS is a family card game, at the start of each round, 4 rows of Reward cards are laid out on the table. Players simultaneously and secretly place 4 of their 5 Animal cards face down, one for each row.

During their turn, each player tries to remove their opponents' cards by guessing their value.

Once all the Animal cards have been revealed, the rewards obtained are assigned to each player.

The game continues until a player gets three Reward cards of the same type or accumulates 8 Exploration tokens (8 VP), thus becoming the winner of CLANS.

—description from the publisher

Echo Team: Spearhead is a  card-driven, tactical wargame where battles take place in and around urban terrain. You take command of a Delta Force unit that conducts operations around the world. The opponents are, for example, Somali warlords, members of the Mexican Minola cartel or units of ISIS West Africa.

Two players play one or more "Command" cards from their hand to activate their units on the game board for various military functions or to trigger events. The players try to achieve victory by moving their combat units around the game board to attack common combat units, rescue hostages, destroy objectives or repel attacks. Variable turn events such as weather, reinforcements or the number of command points can vary and occur at random intervals to add a little uncertainty to each player's perfect plan.

SCALE: Each hex on an Echo Team: Spearhead map is approximately 150 feet long. Each full player phase abstractly represents several minutes of real time. Each full player phase abstractly represents 10-15 minutes of real time.

Each Delta Team unit is represented in-game as a single entity/person, along with their individual primary and secondary weapons. Enemy units are represented as a 2-man team or a 5-man team.

—description from the designer

Players compete to build the strongest clan by recruiting new members from the Dojo or the Suburb.

On your turn, you can either recruit new members to your clan or challenge the other clans.

When recruiting new members, you must choose whether to take them from the Dojo or the Suburb.

If a player is ready to challenge the other clans, instead of recruiting, they can call out “Banzai!” to pause the game. Players who participate in the call-out will compare their clans to see who is strongest.

The winner takes all cards from the Suburb that share the same attribute (color or number). Each card collected is worth 1 victory point.

The first player to reach 5 points is declared the winner.

—description from the designer

Thirty days of vacation on the beautiful island of Spiekeroog lie ahead of you! What are you going to do? Sunbathing on the beach, shopping in the village or visiting one of the countless sights of the North Sea island. The choice is yours. All you need is a game sheet, a pencil and three dice and your vacation can begin!

SPIEKEROOG: The Roll + Write Game is played over thirty turns. On each turn, you have three dice at your disposal, which you can re-roll a maximum of two times. You enter your dice result in one of the five areas of your game sheet: The beach, the island village, the sailing race, the campsite or the boarding school.

Finally, you check whether you have achieved bonus or sun symbols in your turn. If you are the first to complete one of the five game areas, all other players receive a cloud. Whenever you receive a bonus symbol, you have to choose one out of two actions: either you enter an additional number of your choice in any area or you use the bonus to visit a sight.

Then it's the next person's turn. After thirty vacation days the game ends. You add up your sun symbols and then subtract the number of clouds you received in the game. The winner is the player with the most victory points. If there is a tie, the number of sights visited decides who wins the game.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Asara is an acclaimed, award-nominated worker placement game created by the brilliant designers Wolfgang Kramer and Michael Kiesling. In this 2-4 player game, you assume the role of an architect competing to shape the city skyline by constructing fantastical towers and earning prestige points.

Now returning with all-new artwork, setting, and components, This new edition of Asara retains its classic gameplay that made it a fan favorite. Set in a distant future where the city of Asara soars into the clouds, its skyline is graced with vibrant turrets, gilded accents, and plumes of prismatic smoke.

Step into the role of an architect and engage in this timeless worker placement game as you compete to build the most magnificent towers.

The premium edition includes the two expansions, Das Haus des Flaschengeistes and Asara: Die Gaben des Kalifen, though they have been renamed and re-themed. However, the gameplay remains the same as in the base game.

—description from publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
It is the turn of the 19th century, and the world is surging forward into the Second Industrial Revolution. Mechanization, electrification, and bold innovation are reshaping society at an unprecedented pace. Nations rally their greatest minds to craft inventions that will define the future. To celebrate this new age of discovery, the most prestigious International Invention Exhibition has been established—an extraordinary event where visionaries from around the world gather to showcase their ingenuity. Here, only the most daring ideas will earn their place in history. You have been invited to compete, and the prize is a future of your own design.

Unvention is a competitive roll-and-write game where players take part in a prestigious weekend-long invention competition. Over the course of three days —Friday through Sunday— players journal their designs, gather parts, and construct mechanisms to bring their most ambitious inventions to life. At the end of each day, players present their inventions to the judges. The earlier an invention is unveiled, the more impressive it is — but timing is everything. Will your designs capture the spotlight?

At its core, Unvention is a polyomino puzzle game, where players build the polyomino shapes themselves. There are three core game systems, all heavily interlocked towards a single goal of creating elaborate inventions:
1. Journals: Mini sudoku puzzles where players try to gather wrenches; the resource required to build mechanisms.
2. Workshops: Players gather parts here, and use wrenches to connect them together and build polyomino-shaped mechanisms.
3. Inventions: The polyomino shapes of mechanisms are used in invention patterns, giving players full control to puzzle them in for most victory points, with each invention’s unique scoring criteria.

boardgame: Epic Cat Christmas: Win the Holidays!
description:
A holiday-themed board game where players must navigate between what’s right and what’s funny in a quest to earn twelve holiday spirit bells and buy twelve presents before the twelve days of Christmas is up!

Players roll the dice and move their playing piece around the board. Spaces for cleaning out the cash kitty, roll again, and holiday bonuses help players win. Side Hustles get players singing the second verse of their favorite carol or throwing down a “Yo Snowman” joke, all to earn some extra cash. Fate plays her hand in the fearful Vortex, where players pull cards that can make or break their bank.

The core of the gameplay is when players land on the Scenario Card spaces. These questions test players’ ability to navigate the real-world holiday season of aggressive charity bell-ringers, obnoxious relatives, and the pressure to provide the perfect holiday for the entire family. Sass and sarcasm create a darkly humorous, but ultimately uplifting, holiday game for adults or families with teens.

Win or Lose? You Choose.

—description from the publisher

An action shooting board game where you compete for points by knocking your pieces with a knock-type ballpoint pen and placing them in your opponent's scoring spaces!

A party game that can be enjoyed by everyone with shooting techniques and a little strategy on a three-dimensional board with jump platforms, holes, and obstacle stones.

Each player flicks a glass ball piece with a knock-type ballpoint pen into a depression in the opponent's schoolyard. The pieces represent the gang leader and his minions, and if one piece falls off the board it is eliminated. The gang leader gets a high score, but if one piece falls off the board, another piece takes over as the gang leader.

In addition, in the 4x4 special effect square in the center, you can use a ballpoint pen with a strong spring that can flick the ball far away, place obstacles, or turn the ball into a poop top that is difficult to move, making it quite crazy.

A relatively fast and simple air, sea, and land battle game focusing on the 2 decisive aircraft carrier battles just east of Guadalcanal: Eastern Solomons (24-25Aug42) and Santa Cruz (Islands, 25-27Oct42).

For the Eastern Solomons scenario, 1,2, or all 3 U.S. carriers may instead be absent due to Admiral Fletcher's concern about refueling. And Japanese have a possible night surface battle advantage - lost thanks to radar by the time of Santa Cruz.

"Campaign game" possibility with Eastern Solomons carrier losses subtracted. Japanese submarines have a free (if low-chance) Torpedo Junction shot at each U.S. carrier in the interim.

U.S. "Magic" code-breaking can give a game-turn sequence advantage ... or not, if the Imperial Japanese Navy code cannot be broken in time for the battle.

5 day turns with 4 phases - Night, AM, Noon, PM - per day. Hexagonal grid: hexes 105 miles/169 kilometers wide.
3 U.S. and 4 Japanese task forces, plus a dummy or 2.

Individual carrier and squadron - scout bomber, divebomber, and torpedo bomber for U.S. - unit level for naval air assets, task forces for surface units, subs hiding in rules, and general U.S. Marine vs. Imperial Japanese Army strengths on turn track.
Ground combat (at night) depends on how many of his units the attacker is willing to bet.
Hidden movement vs. recon.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Temperance is played with a standard 78-card Tarot deck. Place Minor Arcana (water) cards beneath either one of the Major Arcana (chalice) cards, "filling" the chalice by totaling the value of the water cards until they are equal to the value of the chalice cards. Cards in the Suit of Cups can have a positive or negative value. Cards in the other suits only have a positive value. When a chalice card is filled, the Temperance card is moved to the other chalice, which indicates that this new chalice is the target chalice. A player is not required to fill the target chalice, but it is advantageous to the player to do so. In this way, the player should go back and forth between the two chalices, filling one chalice and then the next.

The player is aided by the Temperance card, which has two special abilities, as long as it is active:
 
     The player may clear the target card by reaching Temperance's value, 14.
     The player may move up to two cards in play to a different chalice.


The Temperance card becomes inactive whenever its ability is used, or whenever the non-target chalice is filled.

A player can activate the Temperance card by either 1) filling a target chalice, 2) discarding one of the four court cards from hand, or 3) adding another chalice to the chalice deck.

To win, fill all chalice cards before the water deck runs out.

—description from the designer

Dog’s Best Friend Game is a fun and engaging game designed for both humans and their dogs. Created by pet behavioralists, seasoned pet experts, and game design professionals, this game aims to make your pup the life of the party during family game nights.

Game-play involves simple and silly challenges that encourage laughter and bonding. The game is designed for real-life play, ensuring safety and enjoyment for both pets and their owners. If a card isn’t entertaining, players can easily pass and move on to the next challenge.

Goal of the game is to have fun while sneaking in a bit of training for your dog. The game emphasizes breaks and flexibility, making it a relaxed and enjoyable experience for everyone involved.

—description from the designer

A game master is chosen who gives each player a hat of their color, which the player places on the space of the same color. The game master then shuffles the 24 cards and places them face up in front of him. These cards contain the tasks; each player has 4 tasks to complete, which are announced to them in turn by the game master after each task has been completed.
The game master takes a card, reads out the task - and places a hat in the color of the player to carry out the task on the space that has the same number as the card. The player to the left of the game master starts, i.e. he receives the first task. Whoever has an task can immediately move forward as far as he wants, but only in a straight line, never around a corner. So everyone can always make a straight move when it is their turn. If a player has reached his destination space, i.e. completed his task, he puts his hat on the hat that is already there. Then the game master reveals a new card and places another hat of his color on his next destination space. He then tries to reach this hat using the quickest route with the two hats from his last location, and so on.
Everyone has to move when it is his turn. If he cannot move forwards or backwards, he must move to the next free picture until his path is clear. If this picture is occupied by the game master before his next turn because of a new task, the player has to return to his starting space or to the space of his last completed task.
If a player has completed his 4 tasks, he moves his 5 hats home as quickly as possible, i.e. to his colored starting space to win the game.

A game all about sustainable fishing in Tasmania.

In the Big Fish Card Game, players take on the role of a recreational fisher who has to catch fish from their fishing spot but also manage the fish population. They do this by selecting which fish to catch and which to leave. Actions are based on the number and size of your fish and so its up to the players to makes sure they can keep fishing on future turns.

The game has three types of cards, fish cards, wild cards and event cards. Fish cards are the main card in the game and represent many local Tasmanian fish species. Wild Cards include things like seaweeds which help you grow fish and oyster reefs that help with fish spawning. There are also wild cards that affect your opponents fishing spot by causing chaos. Lastly event cards happen at the end of each round and can either positively or negatively affect the players fishing spot.

The player who fishes and looks after their spot the best and thus earns the most points becomes the #1 fisher!

There's been a big storm in Moomin Valley! The entire Moomin House is modeled after Moomin and full of things that need to be put away. 
Spin the spinner, walk up the stairs and explain to the other players what kind of things you find on the cards. 
Every item can also be found on the game board, so guessing and explaining is now easier than ever before!

—description from the publisher

DEFACTOS – Istoria României I is a historical trivia game that invites players to explore key moments from Romanian history by organizing them chronologically. Players take turns placing event cards, which feature significant historical events, into a shared timeline. The game requires a strong grasp of history, as well as strategic thinking, to correctly position the events in relation to one another.

The objective is to collect six tokens by placing event cards in the correct sequence. Tokens are earned when a player successfully adds an event to the timeline without error. Special effect cards offer unique advantages, allowing players to modify the gameplay and enhance their chances of success. The game’s mechanics focus on decision-making, as players must assess whether their card’s event occurred before or after the cards already on the timeline.

Winning the game requires a balance of knowledge and strategy. As the timeline grows, so does the difficulty, making each move more critical. The game ends when one player collects six tokens by correctly placing events, with tokens forming three pairs from distinct categories. Special effect cards can also be used after the first round to tip the balance of the game.

—description from the designer

In Boa Boa, players try to be the fastest to cross the jungle with the help of boas, orchids, secret tunnels, compasses and magical insects, while trying to avoid deep ponds, poisonous toads and most importantly, the tarantula!

Boa Boa is played on a board with 18 tiles randomly placed inside a frame. The tiles may be positioned horizontally or vertically, creating a different board each game with more than 3000 possible combinations.

Players take turns rolling the die and moving their pawn the number they rolled. If they land on a PASSIVE square, their turn ends and play passes to the next player. But if they land on an ACTIVE square, they must perform the action the square symbolizes. There are 8 types of ACTIVE squares, each one requiring a specific action.

There is a variant of the game in which players use two pawns instead of one and choose which of the two they will move after they roll the die.

Play ends when a player lands on the WINNER square by exact count.

—description from the designer

il Gioco dell'Iliade is a Tactical level board-wargame which represents 4 days of harsh fighting under the Walls of Troy.
Every turn in the game represents 1 hour of movements and combats between Greeks and Trojan warriors and Heroes.

The first board-wargame of Marco Donadoni, self-published in 100 copies in Milan in 1976.

—user summary
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Players work together to create the most beautiful vertical garden. However, the most skillful gardeners will receive greater rewards for their contributions.

The garden is made up of a 5x4 grid of spaces where flower pots will be placed.

On your turn, you must take items from the market to build the vertical garden, and you may optionally place them in the garden. Timing your placements well is key. Additionally, each player will have a personal objective card that might grant them extra points at the end of the game.

When placing a pot, you will earn points based on the group of connected pots. When planting seeds, they will turn into flowers, and you’ll earn points according to the number of flowers in the plant.

There are also special actions available from the market instead of taking seeds or pots. These allow you to collect water and water plants to earn extra points.

The game ends when the vertical garden is full of pots.
Then personal objectives are revealed and scored.

The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

It’s a hard life being a crooked politician…

Manipulate the masses to build your wealth with the ultimate aim of taking control of the government.

However there may be traitors amongst you! Unrest is building and anarchists could be plotting to start a revolution, destroying all of your hard earned wealth.

Austerity is a party game pitting you against your friends to accumulate enough wealth to take control of the government, while balancing the scales of societal abuse to the point that the people rebel.

If everyone is too greedy in accumulating wealth, too much unrest will build and an anarchist can overthrow the government.  Learn which of your friends are the greediest, which are trying to keep the masses peaceful, and who are secret revolutionaries!

—description from the designer

The Pay Day Rivals Edition board game has 2 players going head-to-head in a battle to end up with the most money to win! In this fun game about making and spending money, each player moves their pawn around the game board, finding bargains and unexpected windfalls. Deal with bills that come in the Mail cards such as having to pay library fines or to cover a pedicure. Event cards can bring surprises such as winning cash at Bingo, or paying for a night on the town. Who is the better money manager? The player with the most cash at the end of the game wins.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Mentalista ("The Mentalist") is a social deduction game in which the challenge is to guess what comes to the minds of other players when a topic is brought to the table. Each of the 60 Topic Cards has 3 questions - for example: "A movie that won an Oscar"; "A movie with the word "Love* in the title"; "An action movie". The two people chosen for the round must write their answers on a sheet of paper and reveal it at the same time. The more equivalent answers between the two players, the more points they earn! A light game, with fun and gentle questions, with beautiful metallic packaging. It promises to be a hit with teenagers and an ideal gift for family and friends.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In REF The Reference Game, the challenge is to convince the crowd that a series of characteristics such as "In Love" and "Ordinary" fit the references given by the players. You must use all your creativity to combine references, which range from family friends to famous characters and personalities, with the characteristics present in the game cards. So, are you up for it

This a a 2 player game by the designer Shane "Bogue" Bogardus. This read and write 1 v 1 competitive game is an easy way to interact with our companion on a dinner date or at any restaurant table. You both simultaneously read the menu searching out specific words that match the random matching card given to each player. The first player to score a row, column, or diagonal wins and the other pays the check.  The tip, well, that is up to the roll of the die! This is apart of the dining out series that focuses on that time between ordering and receiving your meal. An extra bit of fun at your next intimate gathering with a spouse, partner, friend, or family.

—description from the publisher

BOOM BOOM is a team-based survival party game, where players will bring weapons such as rifles, grenades, or even pans to the battlefield amidst gunfire and bullets and engage in a chaotic melee of mutual slaughter, and collaborate with teammates to confront the enemy. The one that persists to the last will prevail and sing "winner winner chicken dinner."

The core mechanism of the game is one card with three uses. The number of cards in hand represents life, the number of cards in hand represents position, and the card in hand represents action.

Each round, the player chooses one of the following actions to execute:
1. Action: Play a card in hand to activate the effect
2. Escape: discard one and draw one or discard two and draw two
Snipe by guessing the opponent's number by playing attack cards. Those who are hit need to discard the specified number of cards. After a successful hit, the attacker can draw a card.

When the central deck (eight cards) is exhausted, a card will be randomly drawn from the remaining deck, which means that this number does not exist in the next game.

Remove all cards from your opponent's hand to win a hit token. The first one to get three hit tokens win the game!

BOOM BOOM supports below there modes for players.

a. Duel mode: 1v1/2v2/3v3 when playing with 2/4/6 players; players from different teams need to sit alternately, and then each player takes their corresponding team aid. The team that gets 2/2/3 hit tokens first wins.
b. Death Match: team up as “Duel Mode”. The team that eliminates the other wins.
c. Free-for-All: each player takes a team aid regardless of their team. The player who survives all others wins.

Become the last one who lives!

Join our great mushroom hunt!
The great mushroom hunt is an exciting forest adventure for the whole family, where the best mushroom picker takes their winning basket to their home cottage. Along the way, players will find many fun surprises in the sunny scenery, which require a strategic approach.

Who is the first to fill their basket? Does the luckiest or most cunning player win? A fun game is guaranteed!

—description from the publisher

A roll-and-write game where players take on the role of a rabbit alchemist. The objective is to create 3 potions within a given time frame. To achieve this, players use 2 boards: a map where they move around to collect ingredients, and a laboratory where they mix the ingredients. After finding a location on the map, players place all the ingredients from that spot into one of the bottles, stacking them from top to bottom.

Before the game starts, 3 goal cards are drawn, which specify the required characteristics of the 3 potions to be created. Each card describes the required properties of the potion, such as specific ingredient patterns and levels. Each potion can be completed at 3 levels: meeting the minimum, standard, or maximum requirements.

During their turn, a player rolls four D6 dice and can allocate them freely between moving and collecting ingredients or working in the laboratory and creating potions. These actions correspond to specific tasks on the respective boards. The game lasts for 12 turns, after which the player counts the points for their potions by comparing them to the goal cards' requirements.

—description from the designer

Dope is a competitive game for 2 to 4 players.

You'll lead a gang of New York criminals trying to dominate the city's dope market.

Expand your gang, take control of key neighborhoods, and traffic illegal goods while keeping an eye on shifting market prices.
Face off against rival gangs, gamble your earnings, and bribe the police to protect your turf.

But beware: the more your empire grows, the more you attract attention. Police raids will hit hard, putting your criminal reputation to the test.

The goal is to carry on your business through escalating police raids, keeping your gang’s reputation high.

In Common Earth you build your own nation, trying to score as many victory points as you can. At the same time you will have to take care of the planet in co-operation with other players. Cards that harm the planet are cheap, but also move the risk-meter towards the extreme to simulate the impact on planet earth. If players do not take care of the planet, extreme disasters will strike them round after round.

Gameplay consists of multiple rounds. At the start of the round players draw cards in turn order. After that players can play their cards either in their own tableau to build their nation, or to the middle of the game board to boost all cards of the same colour. Playing cards in your own tableau costs money, whereas playing cards to the middle forces you to discard other cards from your hand.

In the third step of the round players can turn cards in their tableau in for points. Cards of similar colour in the middle may offer them extra points. In the fourth step players gather income from the cards remaining in their tableau. In the fifth step the risk-meter is moved based on the cards players have placed in their own tableaus. The sixth and final step includes resolving a disaster and drawing a new one for players to see. The severity of the disaster depends on the position of the risk-meter marker.

Play is continued until a set number of disasters have been resolved. At the end, the player with the most victory points wins.

-description from designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This game simulates road traffic by integrating pedestrians, cyclists, moped riders, motorists and motorists. Players must use the light signals to display cards representing these different actors.

Each player chooses a signal places a token on it and plays a card corresponding to this signal. They must also follow traffic signals and rules while trying to expose the most cards to accumulate points. The cards are valued as follows: Pedestrians (200 pts), Bicycles (150 pts), Mopeds (100 pts), Cars-motorcycles (50 pts).

“Traffic Officers” can influence the game by replacing faulty signals. The game continues until a player has eliminated all their cards or all the tokens have been placed. The winner is the one who has the most points after deduction of penalties, with a victory in the event of a tie for the one with the most “Pedestrians” cards.

—user summary
New Temporal Paradoxes come to change the rules between each mission.

In this game, children aged 7 and over, with the mediation of an adult, will learn about problem situations that will playfully teach them to recognize and defend themselves against sexual abuse.

Mechanically, the children will cooperatively move the bunny Ritoca in her attempt to save her little friends from Uncle Popcorn, a wolf in sheep's clothing who is trying to take everyone to his house.
Each round, the current player rolls a die, the result of which determines whether Ritoca or Uncle Popcorn moves. Depending on where the character ends up moving, various situations can arise: a little friend could move away from or towards Uncle Popcorn's house or a problem situation must be discussed by the child.
Problem situations are cards that describe situations to the child, with a question at the end: "what should be done?". The child must then answer this and the expected correct answer is on the back, as well as a text so that this situation and its answer can, with the help of the mediator, be discussed with the players, explaining why.
Depending on the child's response to the problem situation, there may be a beneficial or harmful effect on the game (such as a little friend moving closer or further away from Tip Popcorn's house).
The game ends with the players losing if Uncle Popcorn reaches Ritoca the rabbit, or winning if all the little friends are safe in Ritoca's house.

The game is based on a children's book of the same name published in Brazil.

Explore the world of diversity, equity, and inclusion in a hilarious party game that will leave you asking "Am I Racist?" Each round, one player draws a stereotype or debate card, then everyone has to do the work to decide if it's right or racist.

Comes with 200+ cards
Play with 3 to 10+ people
Includes a helpful scoring chart to determine just how racist everyone is
Ages 18+

Armor Up is an 18 card game where players compete to build the best suit of armor!

You draw 2 cards, select one and discard the other. If there are cards in the discard, you can select to take a card from there instead. Continue until each player has 6 cards in hand, then you score the cards, hoping to get armor combinations that are extra valuable. Watch out for the Dragon and the Mimic with extra abilities!

You can add more sets of 18 cards to increase the player count.

PopSavvy: After Dark is the version of PopSavvy created for adults who love provocative card games. This is a game that challenges each player's social awareness. It is played by a couple sharing a bottle of wine, by friends and family groups at a dinner table, by classrooms of kids getting to know each other, and by pub crowds demonstrating their social savvy.

Players win the game by being the best at predicting how individuals or groups of people will answer thought-provoking, fun, and/or revealing YES/NO questions. The goal is to "know the crowd" better than your opponents.

Gameplay is very simple. A player selects a PopSavvy card and chooses one question from different categories. Each player votes YES or NO on a scoresheet, predicts how many players will vote YES, discovers how many actually voted YES, and earns a score based on how accurate their prediction was. After several questions, the most socially savvy person will reveal themselves.

Players work together to defeat monsters from Ticino folklore.

With some cards you create a random play area and you defeat monsters along the path by applying a force on a disk and moving it on the goal.

“Commander! The green country dropped paratroopers near our border and also landed tanks on nearby beaches for support . What shall we do?” “Rally our units!” You order, “We must make a Front Line!” Front Line is for 2 players, ages 12 & up, taking place in 4 rounds (September to December) of 3 turns per player. Objective: Have the most Occupation Points (OP) at the end of December or occupy your opponent’s capital to immediately win.  This is the not the same game as Front Line Strategy Edition, due to, the new Battlefields greatly alters the game-state.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Battletech: Inner Sphere Battle Armor Platoon
description:
The Clans changed the nature of warfare forever when they unleashed their giant, genetically modified warriors in powered battlesuits capable of inflicting significant damage against BattleMechs. The Inner Sphere rushed to respond, and soon a new battlesuit accompanied Great House armies into the field. This suit soon became known as the Inner Sphere Standard, field by a host of militaries and mercenaries alike.

The Inner Sphere Battle Armor Platoon includes four squads of battle armor—no assembly required—along with four Battlefield Support cards and four Alpha Strike cards. Perfect for BattleTech and Alpha Strike action!

The tread of these 'Mechs shakes the very ground — and the determination of any defenders willing to face these monsters. Boasting maximum armor and withering firepower, these 'Mechs usually are the true "kings of the battlefield."

Unleash the Inner Sphere Assault Lance on your enemies! Included inside are the Pillager, Goliath, Shogun and Hoplite, along with four MechWarrior pilot cards and for Alpha Strike cards.

— blurb from back of box

Compete in yacht races, utilizing strategy, movement tokens and chance cards to optimize your position and finish the race.

Start from your position, sail around the chosen mark, and cross the finish line. 
Move around the field in a zigzag pattern according to the token value and the rules.
Adjust movement based on obstacles and boundaries.
Play a chance card after movement, or allow opponents to play one against you.
Plan your moves carefully, but be ready for unexpected challenges.

SOLO GAME
Compete for the least moves, following the same rules.

—description from the designer

MARVEL-THEMED MONOPOLY GAME: Step into the Marvel Universe with the Monopoly Flip Edition: Marvel board game! It features favorite Marvel heroes and villains, familiar locations, and exciting themes

END THE CHAOS OF KANG: Time-traveling menace Kang is on the brink of conquering the Marvel Universe, and other Super Villains are capitalizing on the chaos! Stop their attacks on key locations to save the day

CHOOSE A FAVORITE SUPER HERO: Includes 4 molded plastic character tokens. Play as Captain America, Ms. Marvel, Spider-Man (Miles Morales), or Wanda Maximoff. Each has a unique power to lend players an edge

BATTLE AND FLIP TILES TO CHANGE BOARD: On Battle Space tiles, roll the dice to take on Super Villains, such as Venom, Red Hulk, and Hela! Players who win flip over the tile to change the dynamic gameboard

TEAM UP TO SAVE THE MARVEL UNIVERSE: Draw Team-Up cards to join forces with other Super Heroes, such as Black Panther and Wolverine. Each hero has its own power to help players beat Super Villains in battles.

End the chaos of Kang in the Monopoly Flip Edition: Marvel board game! Time-traveling menace Kang is on the brink of conquering the Marvel Universe, and other Super Villains are capitalizing on the chaos. Playing as Captain America, Ms. Marvel, Spider-Man (Miles Morales), or Wanda Maximoff, players must team up with other Super Heroes to battle the villains, then flip gameboard tiles and race to secure the locations under attack. In this fun kids game, instead of earning money, players can earn cardboard Battle Point chips by collecting protection fees for their secured locations, teaming up with other heroes, and more. The player with the most Battle Points when all the locations have been secured wins!

Contents:
- Gameboard
- 4 Plastic Character Tokens
- 4 Double-Sided Character Mats
- 8 Cardboard Location Tiles
- 16 Plastic Tile Brackets
- 16 Location Cards
- 16 Team-Up Cards
- 16 Event Cards (The Chaos of Kang)
- 8 Super Villain Cards
- 1 Numbered Die
- 1 Action Die
- 90 Cardboard Battle Point Chips
- Game Guide

description: Be the first to get rid of all your cards on your Italian camping trip. Play a single card or activate the card event by playing multiple of the same card type. Try to avoid the No Camping spots.

boardgame: Deadly Whisper: Feast of the Gods
description:
Feast of the Gods is the first in the Deadly Whisper series of murder mystery dinner games. In this game, each player is assigned one of the roles that they will assume on the day of the game. One of the players will take on the role of the killer. The goal of the game is for the players to work together to reveal the killer, and the killer's goal is to remain hidden.

The story takes place during the decline of ancient Greece. People have already forgotten the importance of the gods, and the heroic deeds of heroes such as Achilles and Perseus echo only in fairy tales. For centuries, humans have mocked the gods and were destroying their temples. Zeus had enough. The God of Thunder, therefore, summoned the most powerful gods to decide the fate of humanity.

Instead of deciding the fate of humanity, Zeus's fate was decided. The king of the gods has been murdered!

Can you find the killer?

—description from the publisher

Asylum of Death is the second murder mystery dinner game in Deadly Whisper series. In a murder mystery dinner game, each player is assigned one of the roles that they will assume on the day of the game. One of the players will take on the role of the killer. The goal of the game is for the players to work together to reveal the killer, and the killer's goal is to remain hidden.

The game has three chapters. After each chapter, you discuss what you experienced and try to figure out the dark secrets of the other players. At the end of the game, each player votes for who they think is the killer.

It was the year 1995 when a joyful birthday party at the asylum turned into a nightmare. Before one of the patients, Christine, could blow out the candles on her cake, the candle of her life was extinguished.

Can you find the killer?

—description from the publisher

It's lights, camera, action in this buzzer game all about the big screen! Use the die to select your movie. Then go head-to-head to bet the fewest number of words you can use to describe the answer on the card. The player who bids the fewest words plays the round The other players race to be the first to press the buzzer and give the right answer. If they're correct, they win a point - but beware- a wrong answer will freeze them out for the round! Will your description make the directer's cut, or will the credits roll on your commentary?

How do you say thank you in German? How many sides do stop signs have? Who was the goddess of love in Greek mythology? Is gold magnetic?

The Hygge Game – Trivia Edition has more than 400 entertaining trivia questions that give that extra boost to any game night or dinner with friends and family.

Hygge is a Scandinavian word that evokes a sense of deep comfort and togetherness, and there’s nothing more hygge than slowing down and spending time with the people you love. These feel-good trivia questions are just the thing to conjure up that warm and snuggly hygge glow!

It makes a great gift and is the perfect game for your next party or get-together.

This is a low-complexity, solitaire, tactical-level (single ship versus aircraft formations) game, with three waves of incoming aircraft broken down into four sub-waves each, with each aircraft marker representing roughly 20 attacking aircraft.

The player controls the decisions on starting preparations, facing the larger gun turrets, firing decisions of the multitude of AA guns, movement and use of damage control teams, and when to counterflood to prevent capsizing.  There are options for a historical or random setup and a hypothetical scenario with additional U.S. aircraft.  The rules cover fires and fire spreading, counterflooding, strafing, damage control radar warning, and spare gun crews.

The player’s objective is to get further along the ship’s final mission than was done historically.

One 34x22” small-hex map and 117 medium (9/16”) counters.

This expansion comes with 24 pieces and is compatible with STAKD and STAKD ELITE.

Each player removes 3 basic pieces and adds 3 expansion pieces, i.e. 
Trio -> Expansion Trio (3 nodes)
Quad -> Expansion Quad (4 nodes)
Base -> Expansion Base (5 nodes)

The expansion pieces are treated as their basic counterparts, e.g. a trio and an expansion trio cannot be placed on the same piece, etc. and all rules remain the same.

This exclusive limited edition was only offered during the pledge manager of the KS campaign.

description:
Sometimes the defensive positions are too solid, or the distance to cover too far, from standard recon 'Mechs. In those situation, these specialized 'Mechs and their pilots comes to the fore, possessing the speed to range ahead and the firepower to come home.

Unleash the Inner Sphere Heavy Recon Lance on your enemies! Included inside are the Charger, Ostroc, Merlin and Assassin, along with four MechWarrior pilot cards and four Alpha Strike cards.
