The successor of the original BattleTech: Legendary MechWarriors Pack, the Legendary MechWarriors II Pack is a collection of five plastic miniatures for use in the classic BattleTech tabletop and Alpha Strike games. The box also provides five dry-erase cards, which are intended to directly support Alpha Strike gameplay, and five new Pilot Cards.

As with the original Legendary Pack, Legendary MechWarriors II features five miniatures, four BattleMechs and one Combat Vehicle, associated with a famous MechWarrior or character. The 'Mechs are all unique reposed and/or alternate variants from the standard versions in the BattleTech: Mercenaries, BattleTech: Inner Sphere Command Lance or BattleTech: Inner Sphere Heavy Recon Lance boxsets, with the Marauder most notably including additional dorsal gun options.

The SM1 Tank Destroyer is, so far, exclusive to this ForcePack.

In Worldwide Decathlon, you play as an athlete competing in the most demanding athletics event: the decathlon.

The decathlon consists of a series of events over two days.
On the first day, athletes compete in the 100m, long jump, shot put, high jump and 400m. On the second day, they compete in the 110m hurdles, discus throw, pole vault, javelin throw and 1500m.

In the game, the athletes' performances are determined by the combinations of cards that players manage to obtain (mechanics similar to 5-card draw poker). To win, you will need to accumulate the most gold medals (Initiation and Competition modes) or the most points (Simulation mode).

-description from designer

Hockey Heroes is a head-to-head game where each player controls a team competing for the Hockey Heroes League championship title.  Each player has a unique deck of 9 skaters and 2 goalies.  In this 3-on-3 hockey game, players will try to score more goals than their opponent by the end of the third and final period.

In the game, players use their skaters' skills to gain dice and roll them in an attempt to skate or pass the puck up the ice into the offensive zone to shoot and try to score, earning momentum dice for every successful play.  Defending players will use their skaters to body check and stick check their opponents and their goalie to make saves.

Spending players’ energy gains extra dice and players can be changed "on the fly" to keep linemates fresh.

Players can use penalty dice to try to get an edge on their opponent, but risk getting a penalty in the process.

Play continues for 20 minutes (plays) until the period ends.  The winner is the team who scores the most goals.

Hockey Heroes contains several dice to chuck, a hockey rink game board, a hockey puck token, and 8 teams - each with completely unique players and abilities to explore.

-description from designer

In the core game mode you and your opponent will take turns drawing cards then splitting the draw while your opponent chooses which pile they would like. the draws will be used to build up your forces till tensions spill over and weapons are exchanged. will you build up the forces to defend your people and have the most survivors.

In the advanced mode take this to the next level using deckbuilding to simulate scientific development and political moves. can you use your prowess to keep your opponent from attacking till you can attain a political victory or will weapons fly.

Is a MAD situation inevitable?

Only you can decide.

Want to take it to the next level play it with four players, either as allies or as a free for all.

Legions of Kadmon is a deathly strategic card game themed around necromancy. Enter Morbidia, a cursed realm where the line between life and death is no longer certain. Become a Death Summoner, tainted creatures born out of Kadmon's teachings. A group of talented individuals capable of wielding the Death Esa — the ability to control the Dead.

Players will face a gauntlet of bosses called the Sovereigns. These special units are hosts to Kadmon's Ichor. Players deal damage to the Sovereign to collect Kadmon's Ichor. At the end of the game, the player with the most Ichor wins.

Every turn, a unit will ambush each player. These units are a part of Kadmon's Legion. As a Death Summoner, you can summon your own unit or cast a spell. Every card in the game can act as both a unit or a spell in an intricate duality. You must choose carefully on how to use each card.

In this card game, players do not obtain cards in a conventional manner. In order to obtain cards, a player must kill a unit on the board. Then, those players put the killed units in their hands. Every unit on the table are valid targets, including those belonging to other players. As players progress further in the gauntlet, Sovereigns will be killed and obtained by players. The BattleTech Salvage Box: Visigoth is a plastic miniature for use in the classic BattleTech tabletop and Alpha Strike games. The box also provides one dry-erase card, which are intended to directly support Alpha Strike gameplay, and one new Pilot Card.

It’s party time for the alpacas! The more they dance, the more mixed up their wool gets! Be the quickest player in Alpaca Fiesta to identify the right colours. But watch out for the naughty sheep trying to gatecrash the party!

The player who does the best alpaca impression starts the game. If you are the first player, turn over the top card of your stack and put it in the middle of the table. From now on, players need to look at the alpacas on top of the other players’ stacks.
Check if the colour of the yarn on the card in the middle of the table can be made by mixing the colours of at least 2 of the visible alpacas.
If your alpaca can be used to make this colour, put your hand on the yarn in the middle of the table as quickly as possible. 
If the colour cannot be made from the visible alpacas, the next player (in clockwise order) turns over the top card of their stack and puts it in the middle of the table.

If you are the slowest to put your hand on the yarn card, you must pick up all the cards in the middle of the table and put them at the bottom of your stack (yarn side up). If 1 (or more) player(s) mistakenly puts their hand on the yarn card, the first player to do so picks up the pile of cards.

If you pick up the cards in the middle of the table, you start the next round. Turn over the card on top of your stack and put it in the middle of the table.

The game ends when all but 2 players have revealed a party time card. For example, in a 5-player game, the game ends when 3 party time cards have been revealed. The remaining players count the number of yarn cards they have picked up during the game. The player who has picked up the fewest yarn cards during the game is declared the winner.

Catastrophic is a game of community, conservation and catastrophe. Cultivate and support the development of the most robust community of plants and animals, weaken those of your opponents, and adapt to survive the events the planet and your opponents continually throw at you.

The game is designed to help players make connections across large spatial and temporal scales in biology. Plant and Animal Trait cards (e.g. thick waxy cuticles and evaporative heat loss) help Species to withstand environmental Conditions that they must face in an onslaught of Events such as global warming, drought and habitat fragmentation. Interaction cards help players to support their community of Species through reproduction (by using the pollination card, or perhaps cross-breeding), protection (e.g. with the Doomsday Vault), collaboration (e.g. reciprocal altruism and obligate mutualism) or stab other players in the back with cheating, brood parasitism, predator ecesis and more.

Version 2.0 is a reimagined and redeveloped version of the original Catastrophic, building in content from the expansions into a unique standalone game. Individual species of plants and animals use the new population tokens to track their growth and extinction. Event and interaction cards have been streamlined and balanced, and new mechanics give players more control over their strategies to build different kinds of ecosystems. The game also features brand new custom artwork throughout all the cards and rules.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Battletech: Alpha Strike FieldTech Battlemat – Grasslands/Desert
description:
'Alpha Strike Hexless BattleMat: FieldTech - Grasslands/Desert is a double-sized 34” x 22" BattleMat, two-sided and printed on neoprene, which includes grasslands and desert biome maps.

Alpha Strike FieldTech maps include only the background terrain, with other features and hex markings removed, to allow players to add their own 3D terrain and buildings.

boardgame: Battletech: Alpha Strike FieldTech Battlemat – Lunar/City
description: This neoprene map is the first for Alpha Strike, showcasing Alpha Strike City and Alpha Strike Lunar without the hex grid and terrain indicators.  Each map is 34" x 22".

“Only Love” is a “heart game” for two to four people aged eight and over.

A special feature is the individual design of the large wooden heart, as it is printed with a desired image. Each copy is unique and only made by hand after ordering.

The heart-shaped game board consists of 30 running fields on which a common game figure, the heart, moves. On each of these running spaces there is a specific heart's desire, for which there is also a matching card. Each player has five of these cards in his hand. When it is the players turn, he rolls the dice in hope that the heart reaches exactly a suitable field in order to put down as many cards as possible. But all other players also pay attention, because whoever has the card from the field where the heart lands can put it down, even if it is not their turn. And if you sacrifice your own heartblood, you can also fulfill the wish of a neighboring field. If you have fulfilled enough wishes, a weight will be lifted from the heart by symbolically taking a pebble from the game board. The goal is to collect as many stones as possible.

Escape into the enchanting world of LORE, where every illustration tells a tale and traditional myths breathe life into your adventures. Perfect for those captivated by fairy tales and folklore.

LORE offers an RPG-light framework, letting you assume the guise of a classic fantasy archetype. When you escape into the world of Lore, you'll embark on quests, uncover lore, battle mythical monsters, find treasure, get into fantastical predicaments, and more.

Lore: Second edition makes the following changes:

     Actions are chosen, not mitigated to action cards.
     Turn phases have been restructured to Movement/train, action, and event.
     The archetypes now have unique abilities instead of auras.
     A single player and co-op version of play.
     Unique character pieces for each archetype.
     A world map with various locations and a more robust movement system.
     Enhances positive player interaction in events and quests.
     A simplified dueling system for monster battles.


The second edition still follows the day system, and the game ends on the third day. In a competitive game, players will collect victory points based on the success of their adventures, adding up quests completed, monsters defeated, lore uncovered, treasure collected, and renown. The player with the highest total wins, but it's about the journey, not the destination.

In single-player and co-op variants, players will go toe to toe with the Dark Paladin, the evil force plaguing the land of Lore. Work together to destroy his curses, take back relics, save the people from monstrous fates & ultimately team up to battle the Dark Paladin.

Experience inventive symbolic game play and timeless narratives by yourself or with friends.

—description from the designer

Folk horror skirmish game that sets 16th or 17th century heroes against the darkest imaginings of the early modern mind.

Co-operatively control self-righteous but flawed human monster hunters against the forces of darkness controlled by the game's action and reaction mechanics. From redcaps and revenants, to witches and werewolves, each of the thirteen monster types profiled in the rules are rooted in European (and North American) superstition and folklore form the period.

With thirteen example scenarios and open-ended campaign rules, follow the rise and demise of your characters over a series of linked games against ever fiercer foes.

Devilry Afoot is playable with as few as 6-12 miniatures on a 2’x2’ (60cmx60cm) table. Games can be played in less than an hour allowing multiple games to be played in a single sitting.

boardgame: Jelly Belly BeanBoozled: Taste the Truth Game
description:
Find out how well you know your friends and family in this game of ultimate flavor consequence… Get the answer right and taste yummy victory. Get it wrong and taste yucky defeat

On your turn, ask the other players a question about yourself. Put the tasty beans on the right answer and the weird & wild beans on the wrong answer. They will find out how well they know you through the taste of the jelly beans they pick! Will they get the answer right and get the Top Banana flavor, or get it wrong and get a Wet Dog tasting jelly bean? Only one way to find out!

Includes 100 fun question prompts.
Includes flavors: 

     Licorice/Burnt Rubber (NEW)
     Top Banana/Wet Dog (NEW)
     Juicy Pear/Booger
     Peach/Barf
     Tutti-Fruitti/Stinky Socks
     Cappuccino/Liver & Onions
     Toasted Marshmallow/Stink Bug
     Pomegranate/Old Bandage
     Berry Blue/Toothpaste
     Strawberry Banana Smoothie/Dead Fish


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Trick Market players compete collecting cards from a shared market and manipulating the trump suit to gain the most points at the of the game.

Each round, players play cards to win tricks, with the trump suit determined by the lowest total value of cards in the market.

After each trick, the winner adds the card that won the trick to the market, affecting the trump suit for the next trick.

Players aim to collect suits in their play area, but can only keep up to three suits. At the end of each round, players score points by multiplying the number of cards in their hand by the number of matching cards in the market, but exceeding the number of cards in the market for any suit results in a score of zero for that suit.

—description from the designer

Step into the shoes (or paws) of legendary heroes like Sadie Cat and take on a trio of terrifying monsters in this brain-busting, dice-rolling adventure! Armed with your trusty dice and a pocket full of Brain Tokens, you'll need to outsmart, out-roll, and out-math your monstrous foes. Use cunning calculations and clever combos to match the Monster’s Heart value and emerge victorious.

HOW TO PLAY

Flip Over the Monster

Flip over the top card from the Monster deck. Note the Heart value on the Monster card. This is your goal. Now it is time to begin the Battle Phase.

Battle Phase

Roll all of the dice that match your Hero card once. You must use the values on all of your dice. You may use any arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) to modify the values on the dice to exactly equal the Heart value on the Monster. Example: You roll a 3, 5, 8, and 9. The Monster’s heart is 16. You do 9 - 8, which gives you 1. Then you do 3 x 5, which gives you 15. Then you add the 1 to the 15 to get 16. Success!

—description from the publisher

You might have thought that whoever smelt it dealt it, but in this game, you have to sniff out the stinker to win! Once the Fart Containment Unit starts its tooting, it’s up to you to figure out whodunnit (or put on your best poker face if you’re the gas-passer). Stock up enough points in the Stank Bank and you’ll be the champion cheese-cutter!


     It doesn’t matter if it's silent but deadly or a little cheek squeaker, it’s up to you to figure out who let her rip!
     Find the farter (or keep your own toots tight-lipped) before the FARTSPLOSION takes over
     The Fart Containment Unit also doubles as a classic fart machine, so you’ll be ready to prank friends and family (and farters) alike


—description from the publisher

This sample pack for Cross Clues lets you play a 3x3 version of the game, and it also doubles as a mini-expansion. It contains six new double-sided word cards, a set of letter and number cards for creating the grid (A, B, C, 1, 2, 3), and a set of coordinate cards (A1 through C3). Gameplay is the same as the base game.
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet – Stellar Crown
description:
Pokémon first discovered in Pokémon Scarlet and Pokémon Violet's DLC adventure, The Hidden Treasure of Area Zero, are making their way into the Pokémon Trading Card Game. Pick up Paradox Pokémon like Raging Bolt and Iron Boulder, alloy yourself with Archaludon, and take a bite of the big Hydrapple ex.

—description from the publisher

Discover a new version of the classic card game - San Juan! The cards have been given new layouts and artwork, with some buildings renamed to better align with the Puerto Rico 1897 Special Edition.

The game revolves around building production and special buildings, with cards used both as currency and goods.

This edition was originally announced as containing all previously released cards, including The Hut [first introduced in San Juan (Second Edition)], and the first San Juan edition”s expansion, San Juan: The New Buildings & The Events, [whose buildings had also been included in San Juan (Second Edition)]. However, though all the previously released buildings were included in the final product (with The Hut being renamed to Bohio), the event cards were not.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
It’s time for the baby bears to collect their winter snacks! Work together to help the bears race through the forest and find the snacks they need. But you better be quick! Park Ranger Pete is on the lookout and if the baby bears don’t collect all three snacks and make it back to their cave before he catches them.

In Baby Bears Adventure the bears race through the national park to collect their winter snacks before Park Ranger Pete catches them. Players work as a team to move the bears across the board using image-based cards and collect all three snack tokens at the matching snack locations.

Baby Bears Adventure uses a cute and colorful setting that makes learning new skills fun. This cooperative game is the perfect introduction to visual matching, strategic thinking, and teamwork. Easy to learn, and image-based play makes this game perfect for kids ages 4 and up.

—description from the publisher

It’s time to build some robots! The factory is having a friendly robot building competition and the little engineers must race around and collect all the spare parts and robot pieces spread around the factory. The first engineer to collect all their parts and assemble their new robot wins the competition!

In Robot Builders the little engineers race around both sides of the factory to collect their missing spare parts and robot pieces to win the competition. Players move their engineer around the factory using number-based cards and collect their spare parts by landing on specialty spaces.

Robot Builders uses a bright and colorful setting for an exciting robot building competition that helps kids learn basic counting, visual matching, and strategic thinking skills. Limited reading and easy counting makes this game perfect for kids ages 5 and up.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Awaken the Ancients: Blight and Bother
description:
The peaceful grove has become a bit more lively! Troublesome Blightbloom flowers have begun to sprout, making it trickier to perform awakening rituals. Curious Seed Stalkers scuttle about, looking for seeds to snatch. Prickly Thornweavers have settled in, blocking paths with their thorny spines and making it harder to collect seeds. Fortunately, a loyal Sentinel Owl has been called upon to help patrol the grove against the small critters. As Warden of the Grove, it is up to you to manage these pesky visitors and safeguard the grove’s harmony.

In this expansion, you have the flexibility to play with Blightbloom, Seed Stalkers, or Thornweavers, or increase the challenge by using any combination of them together. Additionally, you can adjust the number of Blightbloom in play, and/or choose to use just one Seed Stalker or Thornweaver instead of two. Customize your gameplay experience by mixing and matching these elements to suit your preferred difficulty level and strategy.

—description from the designer

This interactive card game revolves around acquiring teapots and adding teabags and water to these teapots to make tea. Each flavor - chamomile, oolong, mint, green, & earl grey - has its own points formula that will give you more points the more water you can add. Sugar and honey add a little extra sweetness to the deal by giving some bonus points. You can also mess with your opponents by stealing their cards, dumping their water, and of course. . .spilling their tea!  Try to make the best tea party you can without getting your tea spilled.

—description from the publisher

Combat! Eastern Front is a stand-alone game pioneering a new epoch in the Combat! series.

The game features the ability to play either side.  You can now be the Soviets or the Germans!

The game also features a new series rulebook.  The actual changes will be minimal; it is being streamlined and clarified as a foundation for future games

Eastern Front has four new maps, new rules for fighting in the East, new scenarios, and a random scenario generator.

—description from the publisher

Test your knowledge of astronomy and space exploration in this new version of classic card game Timeline.

Published in collaboration with Agenzia Spaziale Italiana and European Space Agency.

In your turn, you make up a rule without disclosing it. You divide your cards into two groups according to your rule. Every other tries to guess your rule in mind from your grouping, and plays a card into either group. Players score if their played card satisfies the actual rule. 
The main player scores only if the rule is not too simple or difficult.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

理性和感性並用的聚會遊戲《奇異法則》第一彈，將以《夢想方塊動物園》為主題，大量可爱的動物插畫一併奉上！
發動你的想像力、觀察力和創意，用一條奇異的規則將手中的卡牌分為兩類！這條規則既要天馬行空、出人意料；又不能於亂無章法、無跡可依。用你的法則迷惑的對手，並參透別人的規則吧！

—description from the back of the box

The annual Bake-Off is about to kick off in Cookiestead! As the town's famous dessert chef, you have been invited to the event. Whoever bakes the most delicious cookies with limited ingredients in limited time will be crowned the Dessert King!

In this game, players may choose to take a cookie from the supply or place their cookies on the baking pan to gain more cookies if the condition is satisfied in turn. If a cookie placed completes a regular triangle with two adjacent cookies, and the sum of numbers on two of these three cookies in the triangle equals to the number on the other one, that player can take two cookies of the same number as those adjacent cookies from the supply. You can achieve a combo if there are multiple triangles meet those condition. When cookies in the supply is less than players, game will end after players complete the last round. Cookies taken determine the final score, and the player with the highest score is the winner "Dessert King"!

Welcome to the Numerologists Academy! As an apprentice you will have to prove yourself before you can become a true Math Wizard through a series of challenges in the Dungeons of Numera.

Dungeons of Numera is a dungeon crawler, roll and write puzzle game. The goal of the game is to explore the dungeon and claim all of the treasures without dying. Each turn you roll three 6-sided dice and move through the dungeon placing numbers with each step.

—description from the designer

We find ourselves in Vallescamas, a world inhabited by 13 dragon tribes, each of which protects and guards an element. These dragons have been lost throughout the valley, and our goal is to gather them to restore balance. We will do this by stealing cards from one another and earning points in the process. The player with the most points when all the Drak tribes are reunited will be the winner.

—description from the designer

Nos encontramos en Vallescamas un mundo donde habitan 13 tribus de dragones, cada tribu protege y custodia un elemento. Estos dragones se han perdido por el valle y nuestro objetivo es reunirlos para restaurar el equilibrio. Lo haremos robándonos las cartas entre nosotros y así consiguiendo puntos. Ganará el que más puntos tenga cuando reunamos a todas las tribus de Drak al completo.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

The world’s richest man is going to exhibit the world’s  best diamond, and all the kaitos are coming here. Even if you try your best, you can’t lose to the other thieves, so steal as much as you can, and if you can’t steal it, then try to make noise to attract the police to come and find out who can steal the diamond, and who will be caught by the police!

The person who plays the biggest card in each round will not only steal the diamond, but will also be ambushed by the police who have been lured to follow you this round... Whether or not you can steal perfectly in full view of the public is up to you!

—description from the designer

Kaito & Diamond is a trick-taking game where every player takes on the role of a daring Kaito, competing to steal valuable diamonds each round. The player who plays the highest-valued card wins the diamond but also catches the attention of the guard.

At the end of the game, the Kaito with the most attention is immediately disqualified, and the remaining players add up their diamond points to decide the ultimate winner.

Pixel Forts is a family board game of building a base while flicking destruction on the enemies. The pixels can be used as both weapons & barriers, giving each player the opportunity to knock other forts while keeping theirs intact. Each board game contains 4 forts, 60 pixels, 28+ power-up cards, and a player board. After building each player’s bases, they can either flick a pixel towards the enemy, fortify their knocked down pixels, or purchase a power-up.

—description from the publisher

Prepare to dive into Warleague, a revolutionary tactical board game. Combining the strategic depth of wargaming with the immersive interactivity of dungeon crawlers, Warleague is set in an AI-controlled arena where players craft and customize heroes and units. Players will navigate through dynamic, grid-based interconnected rooms to engage in strategic battles and explore an evolving storyline. Offering modes for solo, co-op, and competitive PvP gameplay, Warleague caters to all types of gamers.

Developed to provide a unique gaming experience, Warleague fuses detailed customization with dynamic game mechanics, tailored for enthusiasts of deep strategic planning and interactive storytelling. Unlike traditional dungeon crawlers that demand extensive time commitments, Warleague offers tactical depth with reduced playtime. Furthermore, Warleague sets itself apart with a light-hearted, humor-infused adventure world that draws inspiration from childhood animation series of the 90s and energetic speed metal music, creating a nostalgic yet exhilarating environment.

Players can engage in Warleague through various game modes, including challenging solo campaigns and thrilling competitive PvP battles. The game focuses on strategic planning and adaptability, with customizable heroes and units allowing for diverse tactical approaches. Gameplay features include navigating complex rooms, avoiding traps, and engaging in combat with both AI and player-controlled adversaries.

Warleague is designed for a broad audience:

- Veteran wargamers seeking new challenges with a fresh blend of mechanics.
- Fans of dungeon crawlers and story-driven games desiring interactive, rich narrative experiences.
- New players attracted to tactical and strategic board games looking for an engaging alternative to conventional options.
- Competitive players who thrive on PvP dynamics and strategic confrontations.

—description from the designer

In this wild battle for bones, your goal is to score big and grab the most bones!
Start with 9 dog cards and face off over 6 intense rounds. Each round forces a critical choice: should you go for points, or build a stronger hand for later? 
You can bet anytime to go for a bigger score—or use your chips to bluff and bait out your opponents‘ best cards

But be careful!
The highest score each round earns points—but picks cards last. The lowest? They choose first, possibly snatching the best cards from the pile!
Watch your opponents’ expressions, guess their plans, and play the right card at the right time to become the Top Dog!

—description from the designer

Syluna, the Moon Goddess, empowers the Wildseeds with her magic, and the strength of her Blessing depends on how much of her Favor you have gained from your Offerings.

The Awaken The Ancients: Syluna's Blessing is an expansion that completely reverses the strategy of avoiding seed removal! The faster you remove seeds from the Grove, the more Syluna's Favor you earn. However, be careful not to remove too many too soon, as it may leave you vulnerable. It's recommended to get familiar with the base game before diving into this expansion.

How it Works: Whenever a seed would be removed from the game, place it on the current Offering instead. Gain Syluna’s Favor by counting the number of slumbering Ancients in the Grove when the Offering is presented with 2 seeds on it. If you win the game, your score is now equal to the total  Syluna’s Favor!

Grandmaster is a two-player chess-adjacent game. It uses a modular board composed of 64 square tiles that are used to create an 8x8 board.

Each player has 16 pieces. 8 pawns and 8 Grandmasters, which are valued 1-8. The Grandmasters move based on the tiles on which they start the turn. Their value is only used to determine win conditions. Otherwise the Grandmasters are equal.

The player who captures 21 points worth of their opponent's Grandmasters is the winner.

Grandmaster was inspired by the Parker Brothers Game All the King's Men.

—description from the designer

You are a crime lord in the world of Dark Heists. Do petty larcenies and choose from monsters, hardened criminals and crooked cops to build your gang. When you’re ready, it’s time to do a heists: ambush a funeral, collect a bounty on a dangerous ghosts or pull a honey pot scam. Then you’re ready for the big time; kill a vampire, sell some hypnotic dust or get paid to resurrect a recently deceased client. But hurry, other crime lords in the city are trying to do the same thing, and they’ll stab you in the back to stop your ascent if you don’t get them first. The crime lord with the most notoriety at the end of the game, wins.

Dark Heists is a strategic deck builder game for 1 to 4 players. Ages 14 and up. Contains Mature themes.

Recruit monstrous criminals and criminal monsters to do high stakes heists in this spooky deck builder game. The first player to complete three heists triggers the final round. When the last player has taken their turn, everyone counts their notoriety from crew, heists and coins and the player with the most notoriety wins.

Dark Heists is a game of deck building and heists with crooked cops, horrific monsters and other criminals.

Heists challenge your entire crew, requiring you to draw multiple hands and meeting thematic challenges by amassing more crime or sway in your hand, than the challenge threshold. Want zombie minions to terrify the people? generate 7 Sway. Want to kill the vampire? Generate 9 Crime.

The game ends when one player completes three heists.

The player with the most notoriety, gained from heists, coins and recruits, wins.

—description from THE designer

Peking: 55 Days of Fury is a tactical wargame that places players in the heart of the 1900 Boxer Rebellion. Players choose to command either the besieged Eight-Nation Alliance or the attacking Chinese forces, each with unique objectives and strategies.

The game is designed for 1-2 players and offers a tense, immersive experience lasting approximately 1.5 hours. In solo play, players take control of the beleaguered Eight-Nation Alliance.

Each turn represents roughly 11 days of the siege. The game board depicts the International Legations, divided into four main areas. Players use a combination of event cards and operation points to execute actions, such as firefights, raids, artillery attacks, and barricade repairs.

The game begins with an initiative phase, determining the order of play. The player with the initiative initiates a mandatory firefight, followed by the other player. Players then play event cards to trigger various effects, supported by additional cards if conditions are met.

Next, the eight nation alliance player must manage supplies, with shortages potentially leading to epidemics or desertions. In the operation phase, players use remaining cards as operation points to perform actions. Finally, the maintenance phase involves checking victory conditions, handling fog of war cards, and preparing for the next turn.

A distinctive fog-of-war mechanic sets this games apart. Mastering this element is key to outmaneuvering opponents.

Will you be able to withstand the siege and protect the International Legations?

Experience the intensity of the Boxer Rebellion in this tactical wargame.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In an ancient, wild orchard The Froots live a happy and carefree life; dancing frolicking and enjoying the Sun, Rain, Soil and Bees! Until the evil Dr. Blenda and his cronies arrive and discover them! Realizing the Froots are special, they quickly set about constructing a laboratory in an old oak tree, where they plan to abduct the Froots and squeeze them into the world’s largest smoothie! It’s up to you to rescue the Froots from this awful fate!

You’ll be playing collection cards that match The Froot tiles in the Laboratory. Dr. Blenda will be sending his cronies to the orchard to collect more Froots, but the players will get the opportunity to prevent them!

Players will take turns playing 1 collection card and rescuing Froot Tiles from the laboratory. They will draw one collection card at the end of their turn to replace the one they played. The game ends when either there are no more Froots in the Laboratory or if all of the collection cards have been played.

Each Froot tile is worth points, but watch out! There are some Bad Froots that will cost you points! You’ll earn points by collecting tiles, completing a Secret Mission, and collecting sets of Sun, Rain, Soil and Bee icons. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins!

This game is a two-player game with deduction and push your luck elements.
Players play a row of season cards facedown to their play areas and try to play all of their cards before their opponent does so, or to correctly guess the position of the opponent's "Four Seasons" card.

Each player takes three "Spring", two "Summer", two "Autumn", two "Winter", and one "Four Seasons" card.
Then, each player plays a row of three cards face down to their play area. Cards must be placed according to the placement rules (from left to right Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter, repetions allowed, while the "Four Seasons Card" may be placed in any position).

During the game, the players alternate turns playing a new card into their row, and guessing a card from their opponent's row.
If they guess correctly, they may guess again or pass.
If they guess incorrectly, they have to reveal their own played card and the opponent starts their turn playing and guessing.

A player wins if they successfully and guess and reveal all opponent's cards, or if they find the opponent's "Four Seasons" card.

boardgame: Explorers of the Woodlands: Wildscale Swamps
description:
This expansion for Explorers of the Woodlands takes you into the Wildscale Swamps, which bring new dangers, challenges, and rewards!

The Wildscale Swamps are known among Woodlanders as the home of many somewhat unnerving, but harmless wetland residents. It is an eerie place on the best of days, and mysterious events have recently rendered it dangerous to travelers and heroes alike. The Woodland Elder has tasked you with investigating the area, but thankfully a sprightly new hero will be joining your party - the legendary river otter, Athu. Get on your wet boots, and don't forget a paddle on your way out, because you're heading for the Swamps!

"Wildscale Swamps" is an expansion for "Explorers of the Woodlands", featuring a new Hero, swamp tiles with rivers to explore by rowboat, along with fresh loot, events, monsters, bosses, and two new card types: amulets and swamp fever cards.
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

description:
Evergreen: Giant Trees and Mushrooms is a set of two modular expansions for Evergreen.

This set introduces giant trees, an additional growth step beyond big trees, and mushrooms, which thrive in the shadows. Discover new forest-planning strategies to get the most points out of them!

DinoGenics: New Arrivals is both an expansion and a gentle rework of systems within the DinoGenics core game and Controlled Chaos expansion.

New Arrivals includes a new gameboard, new dinosaur species, facilities, manipulation cards, and specialists. New Arrivals also introduces enhancements to the DNA Market, Site B, Time Share, Intelligent Designs action spaces and rebalances numerous core game facilities and specialists from Controlled Chaos.

—description from the publisher

This new expansion for The Ferryman introduces a host of daunting and thrilling missions, a new colony named Gasket featuring elite companions for recruitment, and a variety of optional rules and side quests to enhance your adventure!

The Ferryman is a solo journey through a post-apocalyptic world where you'll explore zones, gather resources, enlist companions, embark on quests, confront dangers, and make impactful decisions for survival.

To win, you must gather enough resources and allies for the perilous exodus towards an uncertain, yet hopeful tomorrow.

—description from the publisher

Alien Sanctuary is a print and play, roll and write, solo player game design by Toby Lancaster from DR Games. You manage a sanctuary where aliens come for treatment and rehabilitation before being returned to their home world.

There has been a breakthrough in Jump Gate technology and the International Federation of Humans (IFH) has opened a jump gate to a distant star system known as Eridanus. It has three  inhabited planets, one of which has two inhabited moons. The bounty has been beyond imagination. The land is rich and  the aliens encountered are not sentient. These new  worlds have been surveyed, are considered non-threatening and are being colonised despite your opposition. The human race is expanding and is now a two system species. You know there are problems with such advancement not to mention that during colonisation some alien species can be injured.

As part of the Galactic Responsibility Treaty (GRT), these aliens must be looked after and need a place to recover before being released back onto their planet. Seeing what is happening you approached the IFH with a proposal. You could set up an Alien Sanctuary where these aliens, injured as a consequence of human colonization, could be housed, healed and then released back to their home world. You were given the go ahead and the initial funding but told the venture would have to be a self-sustaining enterprise. Overheads of the colonization efforts are mounting up. Your venture has caught the public attention and now people are interested to see what the wonders of the Eridanus system has to offer.

—description from the designer

Entrenched in the jungle, multiple strongholds have kept your army from gaining ground in the war. In place is a plan to send an elite group of soldiers to locate and eradicate these strongholds. Along with a couple of medics and a local villager, you will break up into teams and scour the jungle trying to find the strongholds. As you progress deeper into the jungle, you will have skirmishes with the enemy and skirmishes that hinder your progress.

Once you locate these strongholds, you will engage in an extended firefight. Will you be able to hold out and defeat each of the strongholds to conquer the enemy? Time is of the essence; your units will have to get the job done by the end of the day to avoid having the enemy regroup. If you eradicate all four strongholds of the enemy, you win!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Bardsung: Tale of the Forsaken Glade
description:
Bardsung: Tale of the Forsaken Glade remasters the gameplay of the first ENNIE award-winning Bardsung game for an all-new adventure.

The world of Bardsung is filled with tales of legendary heroes, sung by bards who travel from tavern to tavern. Players live out their versions of these fables, exploring branching paths to create their own legends.

Now, far from the Ancient Forge of the first game, a new tale unfolds. Forsaken Glade is the song of a sprawling forest realm where gods once walked, yet now withers from the corrupting influence of a vile black dragon. Abandoned by their gods, the elves waged war against the creature, yet only sacrifices have held the decay at bay.

There, a group of heroes must help bring the forest back from the brink of annihilation. Not only will they have to navigate the labyrinthine woods, but the corrupted threats and clashing peoples that lurk within. Twisted fae creatures like the Overseer; wretched, many-limbed cultists who feed on the dragon’s dark aura; hostile Rustblood tribes with ever-burning furnaces, and uneasy treaties between the elves themselves.

Embark on a grand campaign spanning ten chapters with deep, evolving story arcs and expansive quests. Along the way, you'll face stunning and grotesque enemies, encounter compelling NPCs, and uncover tempting side quests.

Once again, the story is yours to shape. Each choice you make could alter the path of your campaign, leaving a lasting impact on future events.

The trails and clearings are procedurally generated, starting from a single tile, so no two campaigns are the same. As you draw new tiles, the forest will unfold, revealing dangerous enemies, wandering monsters, challenging puzzles, and valuable treasure.

Your character is also yours to create. As you level up, you’ll unlock a wide range of possibilities, giving you the option to follow a set path or build a unique, multiclass hero. Explore stat upgrades, enhance existing skills, and access powerful new abilities.

From grunts to bosses, combat in the Forsaken Glade is more than just a battle—it’s a puzzle. Encounters unfold across multiple tiles, with heroes battling on several fronts. Expect advanced puzzles and more varied enemies with distinct behaviours. Enemies adapt to the battlefield, ensuring no two fights are the same. Positioning and teamwork are key, and just like your hero, enemies can level up, evolve their attacks, and even hold grudges.

The marching order system keeps gameplay flowing in a fast-paced, natural way, letting you assist friends at critical moments and keeping everyone at the table engaged. But not all challenges require a sword—the new Challenge system tests your party in non-combat encounters, where resource management and hero coordination are vital.

Bardsung: Tale of the Forsaken Glade can be played as a cooperative campaign for up to five players or in true solo mode. Explore the forest in standalone sessions or as part of a far-reaching campaign that evolves based on your decisions.

Inspire the songs. Live your legend. Become Bardsung.

—description from the publisher

"I feel lucky today!"
Have you ever felt that way?
When that happens, you're probably lucky with a lucky character!
Lucky characters are there to bring good luck to people!
They compete every day for the top spot in the "Good Luck Coliseum" with their luck values ​​(poop)!
Kaiun Coliseum(that means good luck coliseum) is a trading card game in which players use lucky characters to fight with each other, the character will play rock-paper-scissors with the opposite character, the winner will use the the power on the left and the loser will use the power on the right. The character with lower power will lose the battle and make the player lose a shield. The player defeat the opponent with no shield will win the game.

A timed game where you try to name items in a category given by a card. Noisy timer to add to the stress!
The number of items increases for each round up to 7 items for each category. Players keep score of success on a paper pad. Player with the highest score after 5 rounds wins

Chirlito ROLL is a competitive dexterity game, where players take turns rolling their dice on a stepped board. The lower steps multiply the value of your dice more and more, but be careful, if you go too far your die will be worth zero!
BE CAREFUL, your dice have different values, and to win you have to think about when and how to use each one: A single die can advance a previous one of yours, knock out a more valuable die of your competition, go for the highest score, or any combination of these.

Similar to:

     Tumblin' Dice - Chirlito ROLL differentiates itself with a portable form factor, custom dice with less score variation, and the ability to combine up to 4 copies of the game to support up to 16 players.


—description from the designer

A game of observation, speed and syllables! Charly, Luly, Charlu … what dog’s name will have you as top dog? What syllables do you use? And in what order? You’ll need to be careful not to get your tail in a twist to win this battle of names!

Specifications: ages 8 and up, 2 to 3 players, lasts about 20 minutes

A new case for Sherlock Cookie

Soon the glitter world will be arching the traditional rain and like every year, a big biscuit baking competition will take place again this time!

So far, Pummelomi has always cleared the first prize with her glitter glitter cookies. But now someone has the secret recipe of her

Borrowed to finally bake these pummeltastic biscuits. Smart, the perpetrator has commissioned the forgetful unicorn Rini to bring the recipe back to the Pummelomi. But unfortunately, Rini has not only forgotten who gave her the recipe, but also where it should be taken back on the way.

Now it's up to you to find out who has stibitzed the recipe, how it happened and, of course, where the recipe has now landed! Because a rainbow festival without glitter glitter biscuits is completely unthinkable!

Sherlock Cookie, your criminal sense is in demand!

boardgame: Dead Man's Hand Redux: The Good
description:
Inside this edition of The Good you will a collection of different factions vying to control the town of Dead Man's Hand. So are these the "Good" guys? You may ask this question and the answer is a resounding No. There's nothing as simple as Good and Bad, and Ugly, even in Dead Man's Hand. Each gang trying to wrestle control of the town is doing it "for the good of the town", others are a lot more cynical and are just out for what's best for them. If the townsfolk benefit from them taking control then that's a happy coincidence.

Details:
This 40 page source book includes a 52 card deck (13 x 4 faction cards) and contains all the information you need to bring 13 new Factions to the streets of Dead Man’s Hand. A copy of Dead Man’s Hand Redux rulebook is needed to use this product.

Buffalo Soldiers, The Stranger, Daughters of the West, The Seven, Mountain Men, Plains Indians, State Police, Texas Rangers, The Boys, The Bucks, The Other Seven, The Tong and Pioneers.

New Legends of the West including: The Good, John Bridges, Wild Bill, Calamity Jane, Rev. Johnson, Roy Magnum, Tex Miller, The Professor, Doc Amos, Cheveyo “Death Hunter” and El Rayo de Dead Man’s Hand.

Also includes 12 new scenes in 4 new Acts.

Groopic Monster's Style revolutionizes rummy with its innovative grouping mechanic. Instead of traditional sets and runs, players create dynamic groups using special Groopic Cards, matching them with whimsical Monster Cards. This unique approach offers a perfect blend of strategy and accessibility.

—description from the publisher

DIDAYO is a Fast-Paced Fun Game. Players must quickly say the correct word or color, not always matching what's written on the cards.
The game's core mechanic revolves around the interplay between the color and text on the cards, with the Mode Card determining how players should respond. The addition of Action Cards adds variety and unpredictability to the gameplay.

—description from the publisher

TaleOrMem is a hilarious game of storytelling and memory challenges. You win by telling unforgettable imaginary stories and remembering specific details from other players' stories. 
Create engaging stories in 60 seconds! Players use random word cards to build memorable tales. All other players try to recall the words from the cards to win points. 
A unique game that is very easy to explain. It's a great game for family night and friends gathering.

—description from the publisher

Two fast agility games in one box! No more waiting for turns - everyone plays simultaneously! No reading is required! Easy to learn.

Game 1: Monsters Go Home 
Engage in a thrilling race to gather four monster cards of the same type and their corresponding house.

Game 2: Pile Panic
A spectacular and improved version of the well-known card game Speed. The goal is to be the fastest player to play all your cards by matching the monster or the activity.

—description from the publisher

Tyrannical is a Card game for 2 to 6 players. It comes with the cards 180 total and a set of 6 20 sided dice which act as hit dice. Players draw from the same deck and play to see who can get all other players hit die to 0 or Lay down 5 land cards to win.

Play starts when all cards are shuffled into one deck. Each player gets 5 cards dealt to them, and a 20 die. The deck is placed within reach of all players and a discard spot is reserved to the side of the deck. Each player rolls their 20 sided dice to see who goes first. Highest roll wins. All players move their die to 20. Play moves clockwise. Each player will take a turn playing 1 action, one mp multiplier card, one D defense card or one L land card. They then follow the instructions on the card. Action cards are discarded once the rule on the card has been satisfied against an opponent. All other cards go in their places as assigned by the rules. The player then draws a card from the deck back to 5. Always keep 5 cards in your hand. Play continues like this until 5 land cards are played, or one player gets all others hit dice to zero.

—description from the designer

Battle of Mekaverse is an intense, strategic board game for two to four players.  Inspired by Meka, a genre of Japanese animated stories involving giant robots, the game allows you to attend the tournament to join your meka team and battle it out with opponents.  As a meka pilot, equip your robot with various weapons and use your strategies to determine how each turn will play out!

Each player takes the Mekamon bodies of one color. Players can arm their Mekamons with powerful weapons and battle it out on the battlefield. Mekamons have four slots for weapon tiles, so crafting the perfect strategy is essential to achieving victory.

A player who has lost all his Mekamons is out of the game. When only one player remains in the game, he wins his remaining tiles and bodies from the board. Then, the player with the most tiles and bodies wins the game.

boardgame: Heroscape: The Snow Fields of Valhalla Terrain Expansion
description:
Heroscape: The Snow Fields of Valhalla contains over 120 hexes and 2 Evergreen Trees. It is fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions. Build new environments over which to battle for Valhalla in Heroscape: The Snow Fields of Valhalla!

New 24 Hex Snow and 3 Hex Ice Pieces

From daring cliffs to rolling planes, Valhalla’s landscapes are as vibrant as they are deadly. Each new arrangement of the land brings its own boons and challenges to the brave souls who would dare to cross it!

Heavy Snow and Slippery Ice (both optional): Movement over Heavy Snow/Slippery Ice is counted as 2 spaces. Figures may Fly or Leap over Heavy Snow/Slippery Ice, counting each he. As 1 space.

Double-Spaced Figures may move on Slippery Ice/Heavy Snow using the lead end of the base to count spaces.

Snow-covered trees may block Line of Sight.

boardgame: Islebound: Emerald Edition – Underground Buildings Promo Cards
description: 10 Underground Buildings cards contained in Arzium Archive Promo Box.

boardgame: Cat Packs: Whiskers in the Night
description: Expansion for Cat Packs that introduces 30 new horror themed cat cards and new card effects!

This is a new take on the classic game Battleship which includes powerful actions such as cruise missiles, torpedoes and more. It is faster and more tactical than classic Battleship. What is boring with the classic game? To us, missing your shot is boring and the fact that the game can drag out. In this version, you get a Maneuver Point (MP) every time you miss. This makes it a bit more fun to miss, since once you have a few MPs these can be used to trigger powerful actions such as releasing depth charges (efficient to find those elusive submarines), activate radar systems or firing of a cruise missile that travels over a full column or row until it hits a ship.

—description from the designer

The radioactive dust of the Last War has left the northern countries frozen.

In the ice desert, the few survivors live in a nice little hell: the “old world” resources are now exhausted, and traveling to the south is too long and dangerous.
 But a radio message is rekindling hope: the last icebreaker ship, the Aurora, is cruising along the coast, looking for survivors. In only a few days, those who cannot get on board the ship will be doomed by the ice.

It will be a race against time: to make it to the ship or surrender to despair. There’s still the light of hope on the horizon—a light to grab before it’s too late! Last Aurora is a post-apocalyptic game for 2-4 players set in a frozen, desolate land.

Each player has to manage their crew to gather resources, recruit survivors, improve their vehicle, and fight their enemies as they race to reach the ship before it’s too late!

Frostlands, the revisited edition of Last Aurora, includes new features like:

     modular maps, 
     modular bonus actions, 
     new combat system and loot mechanism.

In Last Aurora: Firelands, a standalone expansion of Last Aurora, players are again thrust into the harsh, frozen world of the original game. This time, however, the action moves to a new, scorching region: a desolate desert where an atmospheric processor holds back the clouds, allowing the sun to bake the surface into a post-apocalyptic wasteland.

At the heart of this wasteland lies the Citadel, a massive, fortified city. The atmospheric processor within the Citadel demands immense energy to operate. Unfortunately, various survivor factions and marauding raiders frequently sabotage the massive power cables running through the desert, disrupting the flow of electricity. With the threat of a catastrophic blackout, the players must guide their group of survivors through the wasteland to reach the Citadel before the processor fails and an enormous electrostatic storm engulfs the land.

In this new chapter, players will embody asymmetric teams, each with unique abilities, along with a brand-new resource system. Strategic decisions and tactical combat are key, as players must gather resources, fend off enemies, and navigate the dangerous desert terrain while racing against the impending storm. Last Aurora: Firelands introduces exciting new mechanics and modular features that add depth to the gameplay, ensuring that every journey through the wasteland is different.

Will you and your crew make it to the Citadel before the lights go out for good?

The race for survival has never been more intense.

Travail is a 1–2 player cooperative roguelike adventure, blending choose-your-own-path storytelling with fast-paced, tactical card-driven combat.

Across three acts, you’ll journey through desecrated hamlets, haunted ruins, and corrupted sanctums, confronting the grotesque fallen angels who plague this land with their blasphemy.

Every choice forges the path ahead, uncovering new discoveries with each run. Every battle is a test of positioning, synergy, and survival. And even in death, there is strength, as each attempt offers fresh encounters, branching paths, and hard-won victories, unlocking new cards and relics for your next run.

Heaven’s banished wander the earth. It falls to you to end their heresy.

-description from designer

Welcome to the Speculaas Café, a place where you can indulge in your favourite hot drinks while enjoying delicious speculaas cookies!

The Tea, Coffee and Hot Chocolate cards introduce goals that the players try to achieve during the game.

—description from the publisher

The Souls offers an immersive experience with the option to play solo, cooperatively, or competitively. Players assume the role of spirits navigating unique fates, unraveling mysteries, crafting items, gathering resources, and interacting with mythical beings. The game emphasizes growth through reincarnation, where each death opens the door to new possibilities.  How many incarnations will it take to free your soul once more?

Four modes of play are available:

    Full Cooperative Mode: All players unite as a team.
    Full Competitive Mode: Each player competes individually.
    Team Competitive Mode: Players form teams and compete.
    Solitaire Mode


The objective of the game varies for each player, team, or group based on the scenario they choose at the beginning of the game. Each participant works toward achieving their unique scenario objective, which may differ from others. In Full Competitive Mode, even after a player successfully completes their scenario, the remaining players can continue playing if they wish, pursuing their own objectives to complete the game. This allows for a more dynamic and extended gameplay experience.

When a player's vitality reaches zero, they die and are reborn with a new backstory card and set of stats. The cycle of life and death is key to the gameplay, offering players new opportunities with each incarnation.

-description from publisher

The Mythrohir slept as an unforeseen threat emerged from the forsaken desert mountains of Nul-Gar in the Third Act of the Age of the Living. Massive armies wearing the mark of Nul-Gador assembled at the shores of the Westerlore. The great kings of the Riverwide Alliance rose up to meet them. So, too, did the Denizens.

The sky burned red at the first clash of armies, signaling to all this would be a bloody and hard-fought war. It would not be decided by the might of armies nor the cunning of generals.

This would be a war of Heroes.

In Denizens: The Hero-War, players take control of a faction: the disciplined and tyrannical Nul-Gador, and the gifted but disparate peoples of the Riverwide Alliance. Players compete over two active theaters at a time, claiming some for victory and others to build up their position. At the end of every conflict, cards will change hands, rotating to reveal their new allegiance as the other player recruits them.

Players must choose their strategy wisely, but be ready to adapt as opportunities arise. Some may want to focus on winning theaters that allow them to recruit powerful cards for future battles. Others may want to rush the end game, thinning their deck by directing the war towards locations in which they have an advantage, and focusing on claiming points early to see if their lead will hold as their enemy’s power grows.

Denizens: The Hero-War is played using just 18 cards. It is, however, quite complex to master, so be sure to go into it ready for a challenge! The game’s two factions play very differently, so it is recommended to choose a faction and play with it a few times before trying the other one.

—description from the publisher

Showboating is a must-not-follow trick-taking game combined with push-your-luck. Winners of the trick may take the opportunity to show off and push their luck for extra points but, push it too far and you will go bust. Do you play it safe? or do you risk it for the big points? The person with the most points after 3 rounds wins.

—description from the designer

A cooperative and immersive experience in which you must coordinate as best you can by taking advantage of every glimpse from the lines of sight and overhearing every bit of information.

The game is divided into 3 days, each of which consists of 4 rounds. At each turn, players use up to 3 action points and, clockwise, choose whether to perform 1 or 2 actions until they run out of tokens.

During your turn you can move within the Panopticon, interact with rooms to activate or inspect them, or talk to prisoners you encounter.

Different actions carry an associated risk, to be checked by flipping the cards of the Panopticon deck. The risk will be gradual and will increase day by day.
 It is necessary to survive all together until the final showdown, otherwise the game will be lost before any escape attempt is made.

The heart of the game is to discover and execute at least 4 out of 5 phases of the same escape plan by dropping resource cards to accomplish the demands of the prisoners who possess this information.

In the meanwhile you will create objects in the workshop to dig a tunnel or damage the electrical system. You will whisper a prayer in the chapel and maybe you will take on a temporary job...

Communication is prohibited, but you can take advantage of lines of sight and adjacencies to cooperate and coordinate in order to acquire as much knowledge as possible by lowering the risk. Should the plan be too fragmented, you will have to gather in one room to interact, at the risk of being spotted.

The perception of control will be defeated: run away from the Panopticon!

Will you be able to grasp your new freedom, or will you die at your last step?

-description from developer

boardgame: Twilight Struggle: 20th Anniversary Hall of Fame Edition
description:
In 1945, unlikely allies toppled Hitler's war machine, while humanity's most devastating weapons forced the Japanese Empire to its knees in a storm of fire. Where once there stood many great powers, there then stood only two. The world had scant months to sigh its collective relief before a new conflict threatened. Unlike the titanic struggles of the preceding decades, this conflict would be waged not primarily by soldiers and tanks, but by spies and politicians, scientists and intellectuals, artists and traitors.

Twilight Struggle is a two-player game simulating the forty-five year dance of intrigue, prestige, and occasional flares of warfare between the Soviet Union and the United States. The entire world is the stage on which these two titans fight to make the world safe for their own ideologies and ways of life. The game begins amidst the ruins of Europe as the two new "superpowers" scramble over the wreckage of the Second World War, and ends in 1989, when only the United States remained standing. Twilight Struggle inherits its fundamental systems from the card-driven classics We the People and Hannibal: Rome vs. Carthage. It is a quick-playing, low-complexity game in that tradition. The game map is a world map of the period, whereon players move units and exert influence in attempts to gain allies and control for their superpower. As with GMT's other card-driven games, decision-making is a challenge; how to best use one's cards and units given consistently limited resources?

Twilight Struggle's Event cards add detail and flavor to the game. They cover a vast array of historical happenings, from the Arab-Israeli conflicts of 1948 and 1967, to Vietnam and the U.S. peace movement, to the Cuban Missile Crisis and other such incidents that brought the world to the brink of nuclear annihilation. Subsystems capture the prestige-laden Space Race as well as nuclear tensions, with the possibility of game-ending nuclear war.

Twilight Struggle 20th Anniversary Hall of Fame Edition adds even more beauty and play options to the original game. Here are some of the additions you'll see in this new Edition:

     A double sided map with a new map on the front and the familiar Deluxe Edition  map on the back.
     The Turn Zero and Alternative Space Race variants included.
     A new alt-history scenario, "Red Sun Rising" with new cards.
     A collection of promotional cards including the Kickstarter Promo Packs #1 and #2 and promo cards from foreign editions of Twilight Struggle
     A background booklet including retrospective essays from the designers


—description from the designer

totoANIMO is a 2 plyaer mind game.
Players have five cards numbered 1 to 4 and four win, lose or draw cards.

The number card is first put out face down. Next, the card that predicts the winner of the game is then played face down.

When both players have played their cards, they first turn them face down in numerical order and score a point if their prediction of the winner of their numbered card is correct.

The next turn is done in the same way with the remaining cards, with the player with the most points eventually winning.

Chiron is a chess variant that accomplishes finitude by some very unique but also surprisingly traditional means, instead of strange and obscure mechanics, fairy pieces combine with shogi-style drops to create a finite and drawless experience. this is because all the pieces in Chiron fundamentally progress as the game continues despite having both forward and backward movement. The trick is that each piece in Chiron has all of its basic movement contained by a 180 degree radius, meaning no move or combination of moves nullifies another. Because of this Chiron has a somewhat intimidating feature: a few asymmetric pieces, but it deals with this in several ways that restore symmetry to these pieces and the game as a whole. For one thing these pieces come with lefthanded and righthanded counterparts which reflect each other's movement but the pieces themselves have capture only moves which reflect their basic movement. There is also a piece called the Phoenix which is continually dropped on the board and can capture any piece that is on a rank behind it which ensures that the opposing King will eventually have to pass it and thereby be captured. Chiron has the distinction of being the first true chess variant that mathematically cannot cycle and has no potential to draw. Corey L. Clark designed Chiron in 2018 and thoroughly modified it in 2024.

Materials: a standard chess set in which the rooks can be flipped and the knights and bishops can be rotated to face eachother

Gameplay: The pieces in Chiron are The King, The Phoenix and Ant which all have basic symmetrical forward movement, along with the asymmetrical movers: The Crab, The Moth and, The Hummingbird. They are listed below with their movement capabilities, with the lefthanded pieces being depicted for the asymmetrical movers. Note, any piece movement which is not ranged like a rook or bishop is able to jump over any pieces regardless of color. You may capture any piece of your own at any time.

King: The King moves to any space 2 spaces away in the forward direction. It also has capture-only moves backwards in any of the three adjacent spaces behind it.

Phoenix: The Phoenix may only move one square forward generally but may capture any piece on a rank behind it, even leaping over any number of pieces of either color in the process. The phoenix may only be captured by a regular move (ie not one marked by an X) and is the only piece that may be dropped. During the game, if you capture an opponent's Phoenix, you recruit it to your side and then either player may spend a turn dropping it on the first rank of the player who owns it. Note: use a pawn to represent a second phoenix, if you have one.

Ant: The Ant moves one square forward and captures likewise. An ant which reaches the 8th rank may promote to any of your pieces that have been captured.

Crab: The Crab has the most exotic movement of the pieces, it may move one square diagonally forward, like a wide knight forward, like a rook horizontally to the righthand side, or like a wide camel (1-3 movement) backward to righthand side. It may also move while capturing as a rook to the lefthand side or as a wide camel to the lefthand side.

Moth: The Moth has relatively simple movement, it may move to the righthand side like a wide knight, move on either side like a long knight and retreats as a bishop to the righthand side. It may also move with a bishop move to the lefthand side or as a wide knight forward and to the left in order to capture.

Hummingbird: The Hummingbird moves as a rook forward, or else retreats as a knight to the lefthand side. It may also capture to the lefthand side as a knight backwards.

Note: Each Crab, Moth, and Hummingbird has a lefthanded and righthanded version, which are perfect reflections of each-other, at the outset of the game.

Game End: You win a game of Chiron by capturing the opposing King. Note: there is no concept of "checkmate" much less "stalemate" in Chiron and the king must move into danger if that is the only move. Players may say "check" as a common courtesy though, to point out when the King is under immediate threat of capture.

—description from the designer

Element of Fun is an easy-to-learn card game that allows players to battle with superheroes based on elements from the periodic table. The different types of substances that make up everything from the things we eat to the air we breathe come alive as exciting new characters in this game.

The goal of the game is to be the last player with an active Player card. Each Player card has health points, and when a Player’s health reaches 0, that player is eliminated. During their turn, players can make either offensive or defensive moves. Players always maintain a hand of 7 cards and can use Character cards, Special Ability cards, or combos (outlined on the combo sheet included with the game). To attack, players use the power points on their cards to reduce an opponent's health points. To defend, they can stack health points on their own Player card to protect it from attacks. To add to the game, Team can pre-decide to add more combos to the game in addition to the combos provided in the deck.

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Levels – Party Edition
description: Achieve four different levels by finding words starting with a letter on a D28 letter dice.

This mini expansion adds 10 cards in total: 6 cards of survivors and 4 event cards to add a little bit more of replayability to your games.

It was a preorder bonus for Portal Games 2024 editions.

boardgame: KeyForge Event Pack: Martian Civil War
description:
Keyforge Event: Martian Civil War follows the schism between the ruling factions of house Mars. By participating in this exciting event, players will be able to dictate the outcome of the war and change the face of the Crucible (and KeyForge) forever!

In the Martian Civil War event, each player will represent either the Ironyx Rebel or Elder side of the conflict. Get one Event Pack per 6 players containing an even assortment of Elder or Rebel pods* (the 12 cards representing the faction). These pods will include never-seen-before cards, exclusive to each side.

Fight for your side to change the future of the Crucible!

—description from the publisher

Grab a cup of coffee and immerse yourself in a serene city building game. Observe as your city develops organically while you fulfill goals. Unwind by admiring the unique cityscape that forms at the end. Sunshine Cities is suited both as a solo and co-operative experience for up to 4 players.

In Sunshine Cities, you will draw city tiles from a bag to build on different landscapes. City tiles represent residential, commercial, industrial, and services districts. Design your city layout to meet emerging citizen goals over time.

Complete enough citizen goals and your city will reach the next tier, opening new landscapes and allowing your city to flourish. To help your city, you also have powers to relocate districts, build on mountainous terrain, or request for new citizen goals - but use them carefully as they are limited in supply.

The game ends when your city no longer can develop further or has fulfilled all three tiers of citizen goals. Calculate your city score, review your achievements, and enjoy the unique city you have developed over time. Every experience will be different with the randomized landscapes, city tiles, and citizen goals.

Sunshine Cities is designed as a meditative, calm experience with the theme of city building. It is inspired by games like Sim City, A Gentle Rain, and Dorfromantik. Made with love from SunnySideUp Games.

—description from the publisher

This expansion includes four scenarios between British and German forces around Arnhem during Operation MARKET GARDEN in September 1944. These scenarios are for the Arnhem 2024 Tournament (that took place at Arnhem, Netherlands, September 12-15, 2024)

The four scenarios are:
ASL Scenario AR1 - Broken Column
ASL Scenario AR2 - Sheep In Wolf's Clothing
ASL Scenario AR3 - Bricks In Flames
ASL Scenario AR4 - The Overlook
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Misc: Dice Tower Hall of Fame

description:
Redsky is a science-fantasy game where iron-age civilizations compete with each other for land and resources, unaware that they’re secretly inside the hull of a massive spaceship. In the end, the mysterious Overseers who run the spaceship unleash the apocalypse on the iron age civilizations below. Almost everyone will die. In Redsky: The Boardgame, you lead a civilization in the hull from its heroic founding, through its golden days of empire, and then try to survive the apocalypse. Whenever a player loses control of their last city, they are eliminated from play. The winner of the game is the most virtuous survivor!

During setup, players create a unique and modular world map. Each player then chooses a faction and places their starting city. Each game, you settle new cities and place villages to gather five resources: Food, Wood, Ore, Gold, and Culture. You spend these resources to upgrade your cities, raise armies, and more. You roll d8 dice in intense battles over land and resources, and modify die results with combat abilities. Gain new utility and combat abilities by developing your own unique culture with powerful Virtue and Vice cards.

The winner of the game is the survivor with the most Virtue Points (each Virtue card is worth +1 and each Vice card is worth -1). To unlock Virtues and Vices, players first spend Culture to align with their Elements of choice (Redsky’s six philosophies). Each point along an Element’s 4-point track unlocks a new card for them to buy. A new Vice is unlocked at +1 and +3, while a new Virtue is unlocked at +2 and +4. Once unlocked, Vices are free, and tempt players with immediate bonuses to help expand their empire. Virtues come with strong abilities of their own, but are very costly, so players have to balance buying Virtue cards and preparing for the devastation of their cities. You can try to defend yourself and collect the most Virtues, or collect Vices and go on the attack to become the only survivor! You can also adopt a hybrid strategy, where you only try to eliminate players with more Virtues than you.

Redsky: The Boardgame is played over 8 Rounds (standard length) or 5 Rounds (quick length). Each Round, beginning with the player with the first player token, players take Turns doing up to 2 Actions. Players continue taking Turns–proceeding clockwise–until everyone has taken 2 Turns (i.e. up to 4 Actions). There are a total of 9 Actions to choose from in any combination. At the start of each Round, a new Event card is drawn. The first 4 Event cards give players free resources and opportunities to grow. The last 4 Event cards unleash the apocalypse. Oceans will rise. Your armies will be consumed by horrifying plagues. Your cities will burn. Only the most strategic players will survive.

—description from the publisher

In Cut the Crop card game, you reap what you sow. You're a farmer working your fields to plant and harvest the most valuable crops. Plant three matching crops in a row and you can harvest them to score points. Be careful not to pass along crops your opponents need. Do you harvest easy points now or hold for higher points later? The player who manages their field best wins.

Players start with a 3x3 grid of face down cards representing their farm with 2 cards flipped face up at the start of the game.

On a turn, a player must draw 1 card, either from the top of the deck or the top of the discard pile.

The player may then either discard the drawn card and flip over any face down card in their farm, or they may play the drawn card anywhere on their farm and discard the card it replaced.

If, after playing, there are 3 face up crop cards of the same type in a row (vertical, horizontal, diagonal) the player adds them to their score pile and replaces them with new face down cards, choosing one to be flipped face up. This may trigger an additional harvest.

The game ends when the last card is drawn. All players flip any remaining face down cards and conduct a final harvest. Players then score points based on the cards they have harvested.

1,2,3 Glisse! is a tile matching and dexterity game in which children and parents are on an equal footing about their chances of winning.  Who will be the first to find all the animals??

Players setup 12 animal tiles upside down on the table in a square grid.  Then set up the ramp and lake.

With a flick, players propel the turtle down the ramp into the lake, then turn over the animal tiles (1,2, or 3 depending on where they landed in the lake) in order to match animals and advance on their personal board. The first player to find all the animals on their gameboard wins the game.

Add even more depth and variety to your games of Tameri with this expansion’s three modules!
The set contains:
-15 new Weather spheres in 6 new colors, each with a different effect.
-7 Characters with unique powers to add an asymmetric element to your games.
-27 Request of the Pharaoh cards to create shared objectives.

boardgame: Never Mind the Billhooks: Here's the Ruckus
description:
Never Mind the Billhooks: Here’s the Ruckus, usually referred to as Ruckus, is a narrative heavy Medieval skirmish table top wargame inspired and influenced by Never Mind the Billhooks, where each player runs a company of warriors under the control of their Captain to contest the field and win glory.
