Like Billhooks, the game uses a deck of cards to create Random Activation and Special Events, known as Divers Alarums, and the old d6 to resolve shooting attacks, melee, and Will-to-Fight rolls. Gameplay is interactive and features an innovative Movement Gambit Mechanism which, when successful, allows Characters to perform Heroically and an unusual, interactive melee system to reproduce the Thrust and Parry of swordplay. The game can be played as a “one-off” or as a campaign with character development where characters gain new skills and attributes. 
Retinues are formed of a Captain and his followers based loosely on the historical “spear” One knight, two Men at Arms, three bill men and six archers make a typical Retinue, twelve characters. Ruckus is a fun, story driven game that builds a narrative through play. The game is scenario driven and prioritises having fun over simulating warfare or seeking historical accuracy.

As with Billhooks, Ruckus is initially set in the Albion theatre, the Wars of the Roses conflict, with supplementary rules for the other theatres outlined in a similar way to Deluxe. It is a time of misrule, lawlessness, and divers alarums! A time for Rebels and Outlaws, a time of border conflicts and raids, a time when old family feuds escalate, a time to settle scores.

Extensions to the game are regularly published in Wargames Illustrated.The campaign rules were published in Wi 438 and the rules for Mounted Combat in Wi 441
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Tameri: The Gifts of the Gods Mini Expansion
description: This mini expansion includes 14 additional Priest cards with unique and very powerful effects. We recommend adding them to the game only after you have become familiar with Tameri, because they increase the random element and add unpredictability. In our opinion, they are a great way to make your games more memorable and fun.

Mixtopian Saga RPS  is a fast-paced, travel-friendly card game with a clever twist on the classic rock-paper-scissors mechanism. Players compete using 12 hand cards that represent rock, paper, and scissors, but the real excitement comes from the 16 action cards that shake up the gameplay with surprising effects. With just 28 cards, it's easy to learn and quick to play, making it the perfect choice for on-the-go fun or casual game nights. Ideal for all ages, this game brings an extra layer of strategy to a timeless favorite!

—description from the publisher

Tableau building Dominion-inspired game where one's board is comprised of only 5 slots. Throughout the game, players will attempt ritual summons of spirits to add to their board and either gain more resources or gain more points. Once a player has reached 10 points, they win the game.

Nutty Space Adventure is a 2-4 player draw-and-play card game that can be played within 30 minutes. Players either play as a Squirrel, Chipmunk, or one of the Aliens (Greenies). Player's goal is to attempt to be the first player to collect 5 acorns in a 2-3 player game, or 6 acorns in a 4 player game. Each card has a special ability, that forces the players to make drastic measures. Beware the blackhole card that wipes everyone's hands and starts the game over. Games are quick paced, and easy to learn.

Each player receives a locomotive to place on the game board and draws a ticket for which they pay 10 tokens into the toll. The player must travel between Berlin and Dresden in the direction indicated by his ticket. 
Players always travel on the right side of the track and take turns to roll and move.
If you roll 
S (conductor), your locomotive moves forward 20 spaces, 
Z (train engineer) 25 spaces, 
J (inspector) 30 spaces, 
R (revision) 40 spaces. 
U (accident) moves you back 5 spaces.
V (loses the ticket) moves you forward 5 spaces.

If you throw a V, you have to buy a new ticket for the full fare of 10 tokens and start again from the beginning. You can also take the risk of continuing your journey without a ticket; if you throw an R (revision), however, you have to show your ticket. If you no longer have it, you have to buy a new ticket and start the journey again from the beginning. The obligation to show your ticket applies to everyone who throws a R.
The above provisions only come into effect when the locomotive has reached space 20. The first person to reach their final destination wins the game.

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Risk Fleet
description:
Your college history books were wrong. There never was a “dreadnought race” between Britain and Germany before the First World War. There was a marketing campaign by the Vickers-Armstrong shipbuilding combine to create a threat that could only be answered by more spending on more dreadnoughts.

That’s not some alternative-history starting point. That’s what the facts make quite clear, as we show in Risk Fleet. There was no race, except for profit.

Grand Admiral Alfred von Tirpitz did his part to build the myth. Tirpitz’s enemy lay not across the North Sea but rather just on the other side of the Brandenburger Tor in the War Ministry offices. Army and Navy battled furiously for funding, inventing respectively the (possibly mythical) Schlieffen Plan and the Risk Theory (less mythical, but just as detached from reality) to justify ever-higher levels of spending. In the event, neither got what they wanted: the Navy did not build a new class of dreadnoughts every fiscal year, nor did the Army get to muster two dozen new divisions to “reinforce the right.

But what if Tirpitz had won his real battle? Germany had the financial and industrial resources to build a fleet to challenge the British. What might this fleet have looked like, and how would it have stood up to the Grand Fleet in battle?

Risk Fleet studies this question with background essays, 39 new scenarios, and 70 new silky-smooth die-cut playing pieces. It is not playable by itself; you’ll need our Jutland game (and only our Jutland game) to play the scenarios. You can, of course, just read the essays and fondle the pieces without owning Jutland. We won’t tell.

This is a thoroughly revised version of the former editions, with a new set of pieces with artwork that takes full advantage of the crisp resolution now available to us. And many new ships, like the 1905 fast armored cruiser proposed by Kaiser Wilhelm himself, the enlarged versions of the battle cruisers Lion and Derfflinger proposed by naval architects on either side of the North Sea, German battleships designed but never built like the 1904 semi-dreadnought, the 1905 dreadnought, the 1912 dreadnought with eight 13.8-inch main guns, and the 1913 dreadnought with a dozen 13.8-inch guns. Plus more cruisers and battleships to fill out the classes of the German program, and the full Blücher class of six armored cruisers.

This is a powerful fleet that can stand toe-to-toe with the British Grand Fleet – the fleet of which the propagandists boasted but Tirpitz feared to actually build. Now you can lead it into battle.

Note: This book was formerly to be titled High Seas Fleet Third Edition.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
The players form 2 teams, recognizable by their different clothing. Each team consists of 11 men, namely 
5 forwards (S), recognizable by the ball in front of their feet, 
3 runners (L), recognizable by their outstretched foot, 
2 defenders (V), recognizable by their bent-over position, 
1 goalkeeper (T), recognizable by the ball in their hands. 
The figures of one team are placed on the full points, those of the other on the points with a ring.

The team that ends up in the 9m circle starts with its center forward and pushes to point A. Then the opponent pushes with one of his forwards towards the enemy goal, etc. All figures can move straight, diagonally, sideways and backwards on the lines, as long as no figure is in their way. The forwards of each team can go back to their own penalty area, but not into it. The runners and defenders go forward like the forwards to the center line and back to the goal area. The goalkeeper stays in his goal area, but can go to the 5 points in front of the goal.
The forwards try to get into the opponent's goal. If a forward has reached a goal, the player puts him back to a free S-point. Then a new forward can try to score a goal again. 
The forward who comes into the penalty area on the arrow pushes into the goal with the next move. After two halves of 45 minutes the game ends and the player who has scores the most goals wins the game.

Dice game.
It is the same as in the sliding game. Only one die is rolled. The pieces go to the next number rolled. Anyone who cannot or does not want to go to the number they rolled goes to an empty spot.

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Kingdom of Hawaii
description:
In 1897, the Japanese Minister to Washington urged his government to send a strong naval squadron to Hawaii to block the pending American annexation of the island kingdom. Assistant Secretary of the Navy Theodore Roosevelt ordered planning to begin for a naval war with Japan. The Japanese decided that the annexation could not be stopped, while Roosevelt would get his war a year later, but against Spain.

Kingdom of Hawaii is a Campaign Study for Great War at Sea, based on this American-Japanese confrontation that never became a shooting conflict. It’s got 14 new scenarios, in which the American and Japanese squadrons fight to take control of Hawaiian waters, whether the Hawaiians agree or not. Both fleets are far from home, depending on their supply trains for coal and other necessities.

You’ll need Second World War at Sea: Midway (for the operational map), Russo-Japanese War (for the Japanese pieces) and Remember the Maine (for the American pieces) to play the scenarios.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Furnace: Ruhrgebiet Promo Card is an additional double-sided Company card.
It shows Zeche Zollverein, a mine in Essen. The card was distributed at Spiel 2023.

Compensation effect: Gain 1 money OR Discard 2 money to gain 1 upgrade token. The players may divide their compensation
transactions between these two options as they wish.

Basic effect: Whenever you gain upgrade token(s) gain the same amount of coal.

Additional effect: Immediately gain 5 money.

The card uses effects from the Furnace: Interbellum expansion rules.

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Russo-Japanese War – Shopping Spree
description:
In the months just before the Russo-Japanese War, agents for both empires scoured the globe for additional warships they might purchase or, more often, stop the other side from purchasing. But you can have them all (for free, at least at first): Argentine and Chilean armored cruisers; Turkish, Chilean and Brazilian coast defense ships. We have their stories, the pieces, and the scenarios.

Golden Journal No. 52: Russo-Japanese War Shopping Spree provides you with 20 new Great War at Sea pieces for the ships Russia and Japan tried but failed to purchase ahead of their 1904 showdown. You also get six new scenarios so you can play with them in Great War at Sea: Russo-Japanese War.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements
Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
1989: Worsening tension in East Asia brings old adversaries closer to war. Europe stands on the brink of Armageddon. Destabilization in the Persian Gulf causes regimes to teeter on the brink of collapse. Will East Asia be the spark to start the firestorm that is World War 3?

1989, East Asia Front expands the theaters involved in the Third World War, DSE with the Korea, Hokkaido, Manchuria, and Taiwan sub-theaters — along with a new theater-wide battle. Several additions have been made to the system to capture the nuances of war in this region, including Tension Cards, Industrial Area terrain, Mega Cities that fill entire hexes, Railroads & Rail Transport, Front Supply Heads, and Fortifications.

Product Information:
Complexity: 6 out of 10
Solitaire Suitability: 8 out of 10 (no hidden units)
Time Scale: 1 week per turn
Map Scale: 45 km per hex
Unit Scale: divisions, with some brigades and regiments, 100 aircraft per air unit
Players: one to two per individual game, one to four for full campaign
Playing Time: 6-10 hours per individual game, 36+ hours for full campaign

—description from the publisher

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
As a bonus for Wizards & Co. Kickstarter backers, this "Loot Drop" was added. It contains:


     4 new creatures (16 cards)
     3 new artefacts (6 tiles)
     2 new enchantments (2 tiles)

boardgame: Mystères à Paris: Le jeu qui libère les esprits
description:
With mentalist Viktor Vincent.

Mystery-solving game where one player acts as Game Master, guiding the other players through one of seven mysteries, each set at one of Paris’s iconic locations during the Belle Epoque era (1900).

Players examine seven different scenario-specific cards, a relevant portion of a map of Paris, and hints from the GM and Viktor himself to try to solve the mystery.

boardgame: La Casa de Papel: Het Spel
description: La Casa de Papel: Het Spel ("La Casa de Papel: The Game") is a game full of bluffing and hidden identities set in the phenomenal series La Casa de Papel. Two teams face off against each other. The robbers, with the help of the Professor, must print as many banknotes as possible within 5 turns. The hostages must do everything in their power to stop them, assisted by Inspector Raquel Murillo. But who can you trust?
An Unofficial expansion pack for Cards against Humanity from Fortinet, a Cybersecurity group.

Astrorum (from the Latin word for "from the stars") is a dice action point movement for 2 players, suitable for all ages. It’s a strategic abstract game where dice represent spaceships. Players manipulate dice movements to create numerical advantages, aiming to outmaneuver and damage opposing spaceships.

Goal of the game: To be the player who eliminates 4 opponent spaceships first.

The boards and the rulebook need to be printed in order to play, also the player needs to provide the following components or similar to play:

- 14 Dice (7 Black & 7 White)

Disclaimer: the game can receive updates\small adjustments in the future following feedback from the players.

This is the game #05 of the PAC Series.
The PAC Series has the goal to deliver simple, yet engaging games with straightforward mechanics, offering a fun and challenging experience for fans of abstract games.

—description from the designer

The world of Serendall is teetering on the edge of chaos, and it’s up to the players to restore balance. With more than 120 unique spells, 24 heroes, and a variety of powerful items, the game blends strategy, deck-building, and immersive combat. It’s designed as a player-versus-player (PvP) experience, where mastery over the game's deep mechanics and deck synergy will determine the victor.​ Good resource management, card synergy, clever hero combinations, and a bit of luck is all you need to win.

Players take turns to battle each other's heroes, using spells, abilities, weapons, and arcane player-based magic. Both players place their heroes on the table in a 2-row, 6-slot grid. In your turn, draw cards until you have 7 cards on your hand, collect mana each turn, and spend mana on hero actions, spells, enchantments, or abilities.

A hero can perform one action each turn by default, but this can get altered by abilities or spells. Heroes have hitpoints, which differ from hero to hero, based on class. Some heroes can heal allies, others can do massive AoE damage with spells. There are 6 different hero classes: Fighter, Assassin, Mage, Cleric, Bard, Ranger. Dice rolls are used to determine damage, based on the damage range of an attack, and the probability of certain spell effects. Dice from d4 to d10 are used for this game.

The player who manages to kill all the opponent's heroes wins the game.

Game can be played 1v1 or 2v2.

—description from the designer

Bringing word games out of antiquity, Rejig is the word game for the next generation.

Build your own crossword (or as a team) against family and friends as you race against a timer to score the most points through word count, round categories, bonus words, donuts, and more!

With 10 different ways to score, 3 of which are unique every round, the best strategy is always changing so everyone has a chance against even the most hardcore vocabulary geeks!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
New character: the Lark, a more customisable hero. Tokens for money and health, extra cards & sheets.

Use this customisable pack to hold your favourite hero along with any equipment, inventory, town buildings and anything else you need.

Then use the spare cards to replace the ones stored here to allow you to adventure with other characters.

Tokens for health and coins (in both 1 and 5 value currency) to help keep track of both your characters and any monsters health, as well as having a real pile of coins to show your riches!

Equip the new Lark character with a multi-purpose shillelagh and level them up, customising their character with unparalleled freedom!

—description from the publisher

A deck of unique items and rewards to customise your adventurer and become the legendary hero you were destined to be!

Every card is a reward you can gain from adventures. None are added to the item deck, and cannot be bought in the town, but instead granted from completing the various adventures that give unique items.

This pack of 18 cards includes:

A dozen unique items cards, including a scroll, amulets, ranged weapons, swords, armour and other miscellaneous items.

2 buildings that can be gained as rewards.

New companions that can be recruited in a variety of ways, including the Valkyrie who can rescue you at your most desperate moments, a raven who grants wisdom... And a magical goat.

—description from the publisher

This expansion has all the additional cards from the new level 1, level 2-3 and level 4-5 packs, making sure the additional content is available to people who bought the original box.

If you have the original Vanaheim box, this expansion will give you the same content as the 3 leveled boxes combined.

There’s about 10 new items, a new monster, a new building, companion and 2 new quests, 3 new map cards, 10 new decision cards and 5 new “domains”. There’s also a few new treasure cards, skills and some other cards.

Domains:
These are in the treasure deck and are added to the maps and affect groups of map cards in new ways, such as new obstacles and rules that make the map more dynamic.

—description from the publisher

Rupert’s Land is a competitive game for 2-4 players set in the early 1700’s.

Founded in 1670, the Hudson’s Bay Company and its Governor and Company of Adventurers of England trading into Hudson Bay, by royal charter from the British Crown, have been given an exclusive trading monopoly over the entire Hudson Bay drainage basin. The King names his “dear and entirely beloved cousin” Prince Rupert as the Company’s first Governor of the territory.

Will this vast wilderness and the future of its Free Nations people be left up to the will of a company governed by proxy from Europe?

You play as a trading company, influencing the Rupert's Land with alliances, trade, negotiations, and wealth, attempting to undermine the HBC monopoly on the map. Hire traders and First Nations people, purchase goods, and skills in a market of cards. Use these cards in your tableau to affect your influence or raid trade outposts. The game ends when players trigger enough futures points in a specific outcome area.

What will Rupert's Land look like? You decide.

-description from designer

Palmodyssey is a storytelling game that can be enjoyed solo. Through the game, players create a story where the protagonist faces off against an 'Enemy.' Will the protagonist be able to defeat this 'Enemy'?

Players progress through the game using an event card deck. Each event card contains a 'story' that asks what happens to the protagonist, and one or more 'choices.' Players answer the 'story' questions, select a 'choice,' and resolve its effects as they weave their own unique tale.

The 'Allies' and 'Fortune' obtained during the journey will prove useful in the final confrontation with the 'Enemy.' However, accumulating too many 'Wounds' will result in a game over.

This game is designed to be played anywhere, without the need for a table.

—description from the designer

Food is scarce, the environment is cruel, and the other Tribes are out to get you. Can your Tribe of Nomads survive?

Nomads is a harsh, fast-paced, card game, where players roll dice and strategically use their Nomads' various abilities, in the hope of securing enough Food to feed all the Nomads in their Tribe each turn. Any Nomads who can't eat, have to starve.

In the end, only one Tribe will survive, and win!

—description from the designer

Expellere (from the Latin word for "to banish") is an action point movement game for 1\2 players, suitable for all ages. Players strategically spend action points to make tactical moves, aiming to banish pieces from the board and claim victory through careful planning and execution.

Goal of the game: To be the player who banishes the required pieces first.

The boards and the rulebook need to be printed in order to play, also the player needs to provide the following components or similar to play:

- 23 Discs (9 Black & 9 White & 5 Red)

Disclaimer: the game can receive updates\small adjustments in the future following feedback from the players.

This is the game #06 of the PAC Series.
The PAC Series has the goal to deliver simple, yet engaging games with straightforward mechanics, offering a fun and challenging experience for fans of abstract games.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Datos No Opiniones: Demo Jugable – Microexpansión
description:
Datos No Opiniones is that crazy and bizarre trivia game that you deserve as an adult.

This is a demo deck with 5 additional cards for Drogas Legales (Y de las Otras), El Reviente del Rock and La Historia de la Selección.

The deck has 15 cards with questions about an specific theme that are answered with some number. To start, they grab a random card, read it aloud and reveal the answer, that is going to be the starting point in the data row.

On your turn, you are going to grab the first question from the deck and, without seeing the answer, you are going to place it to the left or right of the card you used as your starting point. If you think the answer is a greater number than the one in the data row, you put your card on the left and, if you think it is smaller, on the right. When you have chosen where to put the card, you turn it over to see if you were right. If the card is placed correctly, it's the next player's turn, if it's wrong, you take it out and keep it. If you place two cards wrong, you are immediately eliminated.

Alert! As the game progresses there will be more cards in the data row and you will have to take them all into account when placing the question that comes your turn.

boardgame: 西遊記之花果山 (Mount Huaguo Journey to the West)
description:
Monkey King wants to escort Master to the west for Buddhist scriptures and leave Huaguo Mountain. He knew that there would be no leader any more in Huaguo Mountain, so Wukong decided to choose the next generation of Monkey King! As long as you pick the most peaches, the Monkey King's position is yours!

—description from the back of the box (translated)

孫悟空要護送師父西天取經啦!他想到自己走後花果山沒有了猴王,於是決定要選出花果山下代猴王!只要摘到最多桃子,猴王的位置就是你的啦!

—description from the back of the box

Metropolis is an engaging strategic board game where players navigate a dynamic world of trade, competition, and city-building.

Each game cycle begins with players receiving resource and army cards, followed by a trading phase that encourages negotiation and collaboration. If tensions rise over resource distribution, players can deploy armies to seize what they need. The cycle culminates in a construction phase, allowing players to enhance their cities with new structures. As the game progresses, the quest for power and prosperity intensifies, making every decision crucial in the race to dominate Metropolis!

—description from the designer

A unique, modern take on the classic game.  The counterstrike variant adds a level system and special powers to all of the pieces, introduces a simple resource management of counters, and adds additional tactical options in the form of counters.

In chess counterstrike, each piece has a maximum of 3 levels.  The level of the piece is increased by placing a counter under it in loeu of moving a piece on your turn.  Generally, the first level is a special attack, the second level improves movement, and the third level is a special ability.  Counters can also be placed on the board as landmines to restrict movements, plan for better board positions, or setup a future attack.

While the classic mechanics of chess are maintained, counters cannot be ignored. The special attacks create new ways for checks and checkmates and present new end game conditions.  You must have at least one counter in your supply at the end of turn or you lose the game and if you control three times as many counters as the beginning of your turn you win the game.

—description from the designer

Provocative, unusual and crazy themes for a guaranteed fun moment. For each unusual question, 10 twisted answers.

Everyone answers the same question and everyone has a chance to find an answer. The ultimate game for a spicy evening!

The unbridled quiz for fun between adults. Its portable and compact format makes it easy to take with you.

boardgame: Inventors of the South Tigris: North Sea Devices
description:
Originally available during Man vs Meeple 2024 (Season V) crowdfunding campaign.

Three new Device Cards for Inventors.

Trade Cart: Score for sets of Charcoal Influence and Charcoal Dice.
Spindle Whorl: Score for sets of Orange Influence and Orange Dice.
Horn: Score for sets of Blue Influence and Blue Dice.

—description from designer
family: Series: The South Tigris Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Water War: La Guerra per l'Acqua
description:
The World is adrift, in dire conditions. Climate change has upset every corner of the Planet, causing average temperatures to rise everywhere. The Desert is spreading everywhere creating new threats and new paths.
The inhabitants of the Globe are fighting for control of the few remaining sources of drinkable water.

Lead your Faction in this fight for survival.
Because without water, there is no life.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Dingo Duel is a 2 player, head to head, dice battle designed by Lime on the Loose Games. Players take turns rolling three dice and placing them on the corresponding play spaces. Players will continue alternating rolls with the ultimate goal being the first player to control three Dingos (dice bingos) at one time on the Dingo playmat.

Each player will have 15 dice.

Sixes are wild with special “Rules of the six”. A completed Dingo line can only be broken by rolling a 6.

The game ends when one player has three complete dingo lines simultaneously.

—description from the publisher

Dicker & Dice is a game of tension, cunning, and betrayal, where each player becomes a shrewd merchant in a bustling fantasy marketplace. Players must use their wits to negotiate, trade, and purchase valuable resources like potions, weapons, armor, and magical artifacts. However, in this world filled with magic and opportunity, every deal could hide a betrayal, and only the sharpest will survive the high-stakes tension of commerce.

Throughout the game, dice rolls will determine not only your fate but also that of your opponents. Will you form temporary alliances to get what you want, or betray your partners at the last moment? Every round offers a new chance to shift the balance of power—whether by manipulating prices, stealing resources, or forcing your rivals into unwanted deals. But beware, in this marketplace, there are no fixed rules: anything can happen when dice and magic are in play.

In Dicker & Dice, it’s not just about who has the most wealth at the end, but who knows how to play their cards (and roll their dice) best. Do you have what it takes to dominate this fantasy market, where words can be as dangerous as a sword?

—description from the designer

Dados y Tratos es un juego de mesa lleno de tensión, astucia, y traiciones, donde cada jugador se convierte en un astuto comerciante dentro de un vibrante mercado de fantasía. Los jugadores deben utilizar su ingenio para negociar, intercambiar y comprar valiosos recursos como pociones, armas, armaduras, y artefactos mágicos. Sin embargo, en este mundo lleno de magia y oportunidades, cada trato puede ocultar una traición, y solo los más hábiles sobrevivirán a las tensiones del comercio.

A lo largo de la partida, lanzarás dados que decidirán no solo tu destino, sino también el de tus oponentes. ¿Harás alianzas temporales para obtener lo que quieres o traicionarás a tus socios en el último momento? Cada ronda es una nueva oportunidad para cambiar el equilibrio del poder, ya sea manipulando precios, robando recursos o forzando a tus rivales a hacer tratos que no desean. Pero cuidado, en este mercado no hay reglas fijas: cualquier cosa puede suceder cuando los dados y la magia están de tu lado.

En Dados y Tratos, no se trata solo de quién tenga más riquezas al final, sino de quién sabe jugar mejor sus cartas (y lanzar mejor sus dados). ¿Tendrás lo necesario para dominar este mercado de fantasía donde las palabras pueden ser tan peligrosas como una espada?

—description from the designer (Spanish)

This expansion pack includes 12 Quests that were originally, and exclusively, released on the Ye Olde Inn's companion Facebook group.

Sunday Quest: Adventures continues where the popular Hero Quest expansion Wizards of Morcar left off. It is assumed that players will have completed and be familiar with Kellar's Keep, Return of the Witch Lord, Against the Ogre Horde and Wizards of Morcar expansion packs. On occasion the Quests herein will also dabble with elements taken from the U.S. version of the game system's two additional expansion packs: The Mage in the Mirror and The Frozen Horror.

SundayQuest: Adventures; Volume 10 has been designed to fit in with the original UK release of Hero Quest in the late 1980s. It should be compatible with either the original European or U.S. releases of HeroQuest (1989-1991). It can also be played with the more recent re-release of Hero Quest (2021).

Help Little Bluebird—and her flock—find a match! Once you do, let the new friends cuddle up in their nest. The player with the most birds in their nest wins! For additional play value, sort the 24 birds by color and accessories, count the birds in each category, and engage in open-ended imaginative play with these sweet characters and their cozy homes. With die-cut game pieces and a shaped box, kids will fall in love with matching these lovebirds!

The magnet fishing Game is a fun and educational way to learn and play.

Each fish displays a different number, after fishing all the wooden toys block pieces, add the scores together and count to see who wins!

It includes 26 wooden fish puzzles, which are patterned with the alphabet, numbers, and cute ocean creatures.

A few excerpts from the manual booklet:

"It's 1995 and we are still enduring the effect of the recession of 1990.  There is a technological revolution going on with an emphasis in discovering new energy resources."

"The object of this game is to educate people in the production of energy, while having fun."

The basic game objective is a simple one: "It will be the player who utilizes his/her energy and rolls the die in the most efficient manner that will ultimately have the most money and become the winner."

Yeah... the rule book doesn't actually SAY what the end game condition is.  As an edutainment game it's presumably meant to be timed to the length of class or whatever.  But this is not explained in the rules.

This is unusual for a game involving trivia as it encourages the owner of the game (presumed to be a teacher using the game to teach students) to customize the decks used for the trivia part of the game.

Ask up to 10 questions to guess major events in American history on the game card! Did this event occur before the 20th century? Were other countries affected as well? Think hard, ask up to 10 'yes' or 'no' questions, and be the first player to collect 7 game cards to win!

Packed with hundreds of new questions and top survey answers based on everything Disney and Pixar, Family Feud Disney Edition combines the popular TV game show with everyone’s favorite Disney characters, movies, and memories. Split into 2 teams and let the fun begin! Can you quickly name a Disney character that starts with an “M”? What Disney Princess first comes to mind when you hear the word “clever”? Try to match the top answers from a nationwide survey in order to score the most points. Steal points from the other team if you can come up with correct answers when they can’t. The competition heats up in the Fast Money Bonus Round, a head-to-head question challenge where top answers score triple points! Challenge your family and friends with Family Feud Disney Edition and score the most points to win!

THE SURVEY SAYS . . . DISNEY! Based on the popular TV game show, Family Feud Disney Edition challenges you to come up with the top answers to nation-wide survey questions about everything Disney and Pixar.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Innsmouth Conspiracy: Campaign Expansion
description:
In the shadowy, decrepit seaport of Innsmouth, a tangled scheme begins to unravel. A government agent goes missing. Strange creatures are sighted by the reef along the coastline. Celestial bodies align. And, caught in the center of it all, several outsiders awaken in a deadly pit with no memories of how they got there or how to escape.

In The Innsmouth Conspiracy Campaign Expansion, one to four investigators take search for their missing memories and the truth about the rumor-haunted town of Innsmouth. Flashbacks offer the investigators insight into past events, and may even influence the future… but do they really want to know the truth?

This expansion combines all of the campaign and scenario content from the original The Innsmouth Conspiracy cycle, which was the sixth cycle of expansions for Arkham Horror: The Card Game. Players only need a copy of the Arkham Horror: The Card Game Revised Core Set to dive into the story.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – The Innsmouth Conspiracy
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
In Göbeklitepe: Dawn of Human, you start off as the leader of a hunter-gatherer tribe who discovers and makes use of tools while racing to become the biggest contributor to the first temple on Earth.

Throughout the game, you take actions via your six action totems. Each tool you acquire enhances your action totems, giving you better or more actions. You may hunt, gather, craft, learn, build, and worship to find your own unique way to victory.

—description from the designer

Supply Drop is the first expansion for Snails - a game of strategy and combat featuring warlike mollusks.

It contains 14 brand new arsenal action cards to surprise your foes. Add them to the deck and then mercilessly Nuke the area, slice and dice your rivals with a Sword or Teleport them into a really tight spot.

—description from the publisher

On Christmas Eve, the snow is gently falling over the Krone family's elegant villa. With over 50,000 tons of gingerbread and cookies produced every year, the Krone company is one of the leading German manufacturers of Christmas baked goods. Dorothea Krone, head of the family and landlady, was already the third generation to run the traditional company. Since her hip operation five weeks ago, her family had been anxiously waiting to see when she would pass the torch.

Like every year, Dorothea had invited her family to her home to celebrate Christmas together. Everyone attending did their best to ignore the small changes in the way things are usually run for this event. But the carefully cultivated harmony of the evening was suddenly disturbed when the landlady was found dead on the floor in her bedroom. How could this Christmas party end so tragically?
family: Series: Murder Mystery Games (Culinario Mortale Inc.)

description:
Space Marines are genetically augmented warriors, created as Humanity’s ultimate shock troops. Formally known as the Adeptus Astartes, the Space Marines have defended the Imperium for over ten thousand years. They go to war armed and armoured with the best wargear the Imperium can provide, complementing their enhanced strength, agility, and resilience. They are steeled in mind, body and soul to endure ceaseless war, facing the most monstrous threats throughout the galaxy and knowing no fear.

These steadfast warrior knights each belong to a Chapter – a close-knit brotherhood that dispatches strike forces of Space Marines across the galaxy. Supported by transport vehicles, battle tanks, combat walkers, attack craft, and powerful artillery, they execute lightning assaults of blistering speed and power. The Space Marines are the Emperor’s fury made manifest, and all his enemies fear them as the Angels of Death.

Codex: Space Marines is an essential guide for anyone who collects the Adeptus Astartes and wants to claim glory in tabletop battles. In this book you'll find all the rules you need to play a Space Marines army in games of Warhammer 40,000 – including narrative Crusade campaigns and Combat Patrol skirmishes – as well as pages of background material and artwork detailing how Space Marines are made, wage war, mark their ranks and decorate their armour, and the dizzying variety of different Chapters.

Inside this 216-page hardback book, you'll find:
– Extensive background information regarding the Imperium's Space Marines, ranging from their genetic enhancements to the deeds of famous Chapters
– Exhilarating artwork depicting the Adeptus Astartes in epic battles against innumerable enemies
– 93 datasheets detailing the profiles, wargear, and unique abilities of every Space Marines unit, from newly-recruited Scouts to mighty Dreadnoughts
– Seven themed Detachments, such as the Anvil Siege Force and Vanguard Spearhead, each with their own set of special rules
– Crusade rules that will see your heroes fulfil mighty oaths as they defeat Humanity’s enemies, earning glory and honour
– Self-contained Combat Patrol rules and a painting guide, allowing you to play fast-paced games with Strike Force Octavius
– An 'Eavy Metal showcase of superbly-painted Citadel miniatures to inspire you, featuring a variety of Space Marines Chapters

This book also contains a one-use code to unlock Codex: Space Marines content in Warhammer 40,000: The App.

The Tyranids are an all-consuming alien swarm from beyond the known galaxy. Driven by the vast and ineffable intellect of the Hive Mind, their tendrils drive into fresh systems with every passing hour. These single-minded metapredators cannot be reasoned with, bought off, or put to flight. They seek only to strip each new world they encounter, devouring every shred of biomass to feed the relentless advance of the hive fleets and leaving dead, airless husks in their wake.

Endlessly adaptable, the Tyranids possess no mechanical technology. Instead, their weapons and wargear are symbiotic or parasitic organisms, bonded with their wielders and capable of annihilating prey with hails of bio-acid, living projectiles, and ravaging psionic energies. From seething masses of warrior organisms to malevolent leader-beasts and walking battle-tank-sized monsters, the Tyranid swarms are as nightmarish as they are unstoppable.

This book is an essential guide for anyone who wants to collect Tyranids and unleash the power of the Hive Mind on the tabletop. Inside these pages, you’ll find all the rules you need to play a Tyranids army in games of Warhammer 40,000 – including Crusade and Combat Patrol rules – along with detailed accounts of their rapacious behaviour, divergent hive fleets, horrifying biotechnology, and invasions past and present.

Inside this 120-page hardback book, you'll find:
– In-depth background information exploring the alien nature and murky origins of the Tyranids, from their nightmarish biology to the history of the Tyrannic Wars
– Stunning artwork highlighting the horrifying variety and vast scope of these ever-evolving xenos swarms
– 47 datasheets detailing the profiles, wargear, and unique abilities of every Tyranids unit, from swarming Termagants to the mighty Norn Emissary
– Six themed Detachments for Tyranids armies, such as the Invasion Fleet and Crusher Stampede, each with their own set of special rules
– Crusade rules for infesting and consuming entire planets in your narrative campaigns, as your army feasts on its victims and develops new adaptations
– Self-contained Combat Patrol rules and a painting guide, allowing you to play fast-paced games with the Vardenghast Swarm
– An 'Eavy Metal showcase of superbly-painted Citadel miniatures to inspire you, featuring a variety of Tyranid hive fleet colour schemes

This book also contains a one-use code to unlock Codex: Tyranids content in Warhammer 40,000: The App.

The Necrons are a deathless species of alien androids who dominated the galaxy in ages past. After millions of years in self-imposed slumber, they are rising from their hidden tomb complexes to march in conquest once more. Clad in self-repairing bodies of living metal, armed with weapons that harness the devastating energies of the cosmos, the Necrons are a terrifying enemy. They are without fear or mercy, soulless mechanoids who advance in eerie lockstep to lay down an annihilating storm.

These phalanxes are joined by techno-sorcerous war engines, scuttling constructs, and shards of shattered star gods, led to war by undying nobles with wills and bodies of unyielding metal, driven by the absolute conviction that they are the rightful rulers of the galaxy. Ravaged by their long sleep, many Necrons have been reduced to murderous monsters or delusional demigods. Yet their madness only makes them an even greater and more inescapable threat.

Codex: Necrons is an essential guide for anyone who wants to collect Necrons and display the might of their phaeron on the tabletop. In this book you'll find the rules you need to play a Necrons army in games of Warhammer 40,000 – including narrative Crusade campaigns and Combat Patrol skirmishes – as well as detailed background material and stunning art showcasing their ancient origins, impossibly advanced technology, and dynastic courts.

Inside this 136-page hardback book, you’ll find:
– Terrifying background information exploring the awakened Necrons, from their unfathomably ancient history to the structure of the tyrannical dynasties
– Stunning artwork that brings these undying legions and ancient tomb worlds to life (of a sort)
– 47 datasheets detailing the profiles, wargear, and unique abilities of every Necrons unit, from mindless Necron Warriors to Imotekh the Stormlord
– Five themed Detachments for Necrons armies, such as the Annihilation Legion and Canoptek Court, each with their own set of special rules
– Crusade rules for steadily awakening the systems of your slumbering tomb world, earning ever-greater mechanical powers
– Self-contained Combat Patrol rules and a painting guide, allowing you to play fast-paced games with Amonhotekh's Guard
– An 'Eavy Metal showcase of superbly-painted Citadel miniatures to inspire you, featuring a variety of Necron dynasty colour schemes

This book also contains a one-use code to unlock Codex: Necrons content in Warhammer 40,000: The App.

The Adeptus Mechanicus are the fanatical worshippers of the Machine God of Mars. For thousands of years the robed priests of the Machine Cult have presided over the fabrication, maintenance, and preservation of Humanity’s surviving technological wonders with reverence and ritual. Led by Tech-Priests whose weak organic flesh has been replaced with metallic augmentations, their armies fight to defend forge worlds and strike out across the galaxy in search of ancient archeotech.

Among the Machine Cult's ranks are cohorts of obedient cybernetic soldiers, lumbering warrior robots, and battle servitors. They bear an array of esoteric weapons, capable of annihilating foes in volleys of irradiated ammunition or incinerating them with torrents of searing energy. Those who seek to sabotage the divine works of the Omnissiah must face the might of this merciless mechanical war machine, devoid of both pity and remorse.

Codex: Adeptus Mechanicus is an invaluable guide to collecting and gaming with the Adeptus Mechanicus. Within its pages you’ll find the rules you need to play an Adeptus Mechanicus army in games of Warhammer 40,000 – including narrative Crusade campaigns and Combat Patrol skirmishes – plus a treasure trove of information on their mysterious techno-dogma, bizarre bionics, prominent forge worlds, and their role within the Imperium.

Inside this 120-page hardback book, you’ll find:
– Background information detailing the Adeptus Mechanicus, from the ancient birth of the Machine Cult to the specialties and traditions of forge worlds across the galaxy
– Evocative artwork that brings to life the mechanical mystique, strange cyborg forms, and massive scale of the Adeptus Mechanicus
– 30 datasheets detailing the profiles, wargear, and unique abilities of every Adeptus Mechanicus unit, from the sinister Sydonian Skatros to Belisarius Cawl himself
– Five themed Detachments, such as the Explorator Maniple and Cohort Cybernetica, each with their own set of special rules
– Crusade rules for seeking lost archeotech and rebuilding ancient machine-relics to augment your leaders
– Self-contained Combat Patrol rules and a painting guide, allowing you to play fast-paced games with Purge Corps Deltic-9
– An 'Eavy Metal showcase of superbly-painted Citadel miniatures to inspire you, featuring a variety of forge worlds

This book also contains a one-use code to unlock Codex: Adeptus Mechanicus content in Warhammer 40,000: The App.

Insania is a highly-immersive cooperative horror adventure game for 1-4 players. Step into the sandals of Roman legionaries defending the empire from nightmarish monsters. Set in a terrorized town, players must explore, scavenge for items, destroy dimensional portals and fight to keep their sanities. As you progress, develop your character’s stats and create unique item combinations to receive powerful blessings.

Choose from some of the bravest sons of Rome: Triarius, a centurion whose commands may turn the tide of battle, Laverna, a diviner who reads the future in the entrails of her dead enemies; Atticus; a fierce gladiator with melee mastery; Quintus, a physician with a sharp mind and sharp tools; among others.

The game comprises several missions that can be played independently. For groups looking for longer thrills, an expansion (sold separately) brings a thrilling legacy-lite campaign. For those who find it hard to hold a steady group for many sessions, INSANIA also introduces short, one-night campaigns.

Insania’s unique blend of dungeon crawling and strategy elements crafts an immersive experience where your every victory feels hard-earned. The system rewards cooperation, making each session a collective catharsis.

Insania draws inspiration from H.P. Lovecraft’s writings but has its own mythos, built around Ose, a demon from Ars Goetia. The narrative, driven by player decisions and full of “can’t unsee” moments, wouldn’t be out of place in a Tarantino film or Garth Ennis comic book. Players face sensitive content and immoral choices, such as whether or not to kill babies or recapture runaway slaves, making INSANIA a game for mature audiences.

How to Play:
In your turn, you must first replenish your hand of effort cards, drawing up to three - your limit is five cards at any moment. Then you take three actions, such as: movement, combat, breaking mystical barriers, item scavenging, destroying dimensional portals, inventory, revealing districts etc. Some points of interest in the map, like the circus, the forum and public baths have their own action you can take.

For most of your actions, you will need to use effort cards to meet a certain value. You can play only one card per action, unless you have cards that connect to each other, in which case you can add their values.

When your character is attacked, you may choose where to take damage: on your armor or shield (if any), health points, or effort cards (representing a dodge, block or parry). You can even human shield your partners with your effort cards.

Your character may sometimes stumble upon an encounter: narrative scenes in which you must make a choice that may lead to upgrades and bonuses, but will ultimately show you what kind of person you really are.

Turn order is defined by an initiative queue comprising all characters and enemies on the board. Beware: some enemies have the ability to cut in line, frustrating your plans. Every time you reach the end of the queue, an event happens, usually adding to the difficulty of the game.

Some enemies and conditions damage your sanity. If it drops to zero, your character gets a mental disorder: a permanent handicap that affects the way you play for the remainder of the game. Does it make the endgame easier? Well, no.

You win the game by meeting the mission’s goal. You lose if: a) all characters die or b) the deck of effort cards ends. Some missions may have additional conditions of failure.

Key Features:
Unique setting: Inspired by H.P. Lovecraft, but with a distinctive mythos, INSANIA’s universe is gory, sexy, unsettling and full of WTF moments, offering adult content board games seldom (if not never) display.

Handmade Art Style: Watercolor artwork by a master of “demonic erotica” adds an original and haunting visual appeal.

Over 40 highly-detailed, NSFW Miniatures: Immediately immerses players in INSANIA’s twisted world.

Strategic Gameplay: INSANIA replaces dice rolls with card-driven mechanics reminiscent of games like Pandemic, making strategy and cooperation paramount.

Unapologetic insanity mechanics: Losing your mind isn’t the end for your character. Just makes the challenge even more impossible.
Character Development: Enhance your hero’s abilities and stats to better face the horrors ahead.

Solo mode: Cause sometimes you want glory to be yours and yours alone.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Abbey Road Studios: Music Trivia Game
description:
Abbey Road Studios Music Trivia game is the result of a truly collaborative approach between Imagination Games, Abbey Road Studios, EMI Music Publishing, DCD Publishing and Tesco. The game was just gone on sale exclusively in selected Tesco stores in the UK and online through Tesco Direct.
The game is a mix of music trivia, missing song lyrics and guessing the song or artist.  The board features wonderful photographs from the famous studios, with special limited edition graphics and game pieces. Players start the game on the iconic Abbey Road Studios zebra crossing, taking it in turns to roll the die and enter the studios. Upon entering the studios players make their way through the iconic Studios One, Two and Three and chillout rooms, answering questions and collecting pieces of an 80th anniversary album token as they go.  The winner is the first person to complete their album and make it back to the zebra crossing.
The launch of the game includes extra special golden tickets hidden in five packs which will enable five lucky winners the opportunity for a once-in-a-lifetime guided visit through Abbey Road Studios. - description from the article of Licensing International

Step into the iconic Abbey Road Studios and put your musical knowledge to the test as you act, guess and draw your way to completing your album and winning the game.
But watch out, putting an album together is never that straight forward. Forget to book the sitar player or spill your coffee over the mixing desk and you could find yourself back on the Zebra Crossing. The first player to complete their album and make it back to the Zebra Crossing wins the game. - description from the box.

Sygil, The Mirrored Reality is a card playing game suitable for 2-5 players in which you play the part of dark sorcerers engaged on an arcane war set in the 80´s heavy metal biker universe of Hell Night.

The standard Sygil, The Mirrored Reality deck includes 66 poker sized cards which is all you need to engage in exhilarating arcane battles!

Featuring Psychopomps, an original mechanic that serves as an additional casting cost that must be performed theatrically by the player itself. Psychopomps are informed below the title of each card. Some of them are easy to do, others demand a heavy toll and may require a considerable effort from the Player! This provides lots funs and bizarre moments as each Sorcerer acts out the Psychopomp of their spells to cause havoc on the table.

Each card in your hand can be transformed into a Sygil which will produce the necessary Focus to summon otherworldly beings, cast curses and gain control of powerful ancient relics!

Each Player starts with 7 cards, 21 Life Points.
The single Grimoire (deck) sits in the center of the table and must be shared.
Limbo (discard area) is to the side of the Grimoire and must be shared as well.

boardgame: モントゴメリーの憂鬱：孤高のアルンヘム1944 (Solitaire Monty 1944)
description:
近年最大のヒットとなった「孤高の信長１５７０」に続く、完全ソロプレーゲーム第二弾。
プレーヤーは連合軍を指揮し、街道の両側から襲撃するドイツ軍部隊の妨害を排除し、空挺部隊と地上部隊の連携でアルンヘムへの突破をめざす。
プレーヤーは、ドイツ軍の増援がいつ、どこから侵攻してくるか、事前に全く予測できないため、連合軍が予測した戦線で上手くドイツ軍の反撃が食い止められる場合もあるが、逆に連合軍の予想していない場所から不意にドイツ軍の急襲を受ける場合もあり、従来のゲームとは全く異なる不確実性と戦場の霧によるスリルに満ちたプレーを体験することが可能になる。
あなたは第３０軍団を長躯突破させ、英第一空挺師団が待ち受けるアルンヘムに入城できるか！？

This is the second Total Solo Play game using the Solitaire Nobunaga 1570 system.

Players take command of Allied forces and aim to break through to Arnhem by coordinating airborne and ground forces to overcome German troops attacking from both sides of the road.

Players have no way of predicting when and where German reinforcements will invade, so there are times when the Allied forces are able to successfully hold off a German counterattack on their predicted front, but there are also times when the Germans unexpectedly attack from a location the Allied forces do not anticipate. This is a completely different experience from traditional games, filled with the thrill of uncertainty and the fog of war.

Can you break through with 30th Corps and enter Arnhem, where the British 1st Airborne Division is waiting for you?

boardgame: 孤高の曹操：建安元年 (Solitaire Cao Cao)
description:
This is the third installment of the Total Solo Play series (Solitaire Nobunaga, Solitaire Monty).

The fierce battles between Cao Cao and the anti-Cao forces, from the coronation of the Emperor in the first year of Jian'an, through the “Battle of Guandu” to the “Battle of Red Cliffs,” are recreated using the draw-driven system successfully used in “Solitaire Nobunaga” and “Solitaire Monty".

As in “Solitaire Nobunaga”, players can enjoy the thrill and suspense of a series of crises that tread on thin ice, all in a simple game that can be played completely solo and in just one hour!



完全ソロプレーシリーズ第三弾。
建安元年の献帝推戴から、「官渡の戦い」を経て「赤壁の戦い」に至る曹操と反曹操勢力の激烈な戦いを、「孤高の信長」「孤高のアルンヘム」で成功を収めたドロー・ドリブンシステムで再現。
「孤高の信長」同様、薄氷を踏むような危機の連続を、完全ソロプレーかつ僅か１時間でプレー可能なシンプルな内容ながら、スリルとサスペンスに満ちた展開を存分にご堪能ください！

The main computer has a virus and urgently needs maintenance! Help the 4 small robots, Chip, Ram, Pixel, and Bit activate the RESET button. But be careful, the virus will do everything to hinder you, disabling some control console keys. After the various tests, manually write the required numbers and complete the restart of the computer. Who will be the fastest and most brilliant robot? Funb3rs is a fantastic game about numbers: mental calculation and tables have never been so fun!

Melding Snow is a captivating trick-taking game centered around the fun of building snowmen. In this climbing-style game, players aim to "roll up" melds of cards over four rounds, striving to win tricks that earn points while quickly shedding their cards.

When a player goes out, they can choose a bonus card to enhance their strategy, which may be applied immediately, in the next round, or at game end.

At the end of each hand, players score their points by writing them in one of four spots on their snowman: the head, upper body, lower body, or the snow-covered ground. Points are tallied at the end of the game, and players can earn extra points by predicting their scores in sequential order, just like building a snowman, smallest on top to largest on the snow-covered ground.

After four rounds, the player with the highest total points emerges victorious!

—description from the publisher

The election is over, and it is time to compose a new federal cabinet by drafting members of the 42nd Canadian parliament. While the best appointments should account for experience, diversity, gender parity, and regional representation, you will need to prioritize. Take note of the political climate, sort out available ministers from all sides, and counter opposing strategies.

At the beginning of the game, players randomly draw voter cards to determine the political issues of the match. From there, players take turns drafting minister cards from a public supply, judging each on the kinds of diversity and experience they will bring. At the end of the game, whoever’s decisions matched up best with the voter’s wants wins the game.

Unleash the Star Power Within!
In the exciting world of Pup Idols, adorable doggies compete for fame and glory! Strategically manage your pack, collect resources, and put on amazing performances to become the Top Dogs! The game offers a variety of play modes, catering to both casual players and strategic masterminds. Choose from quick "Paw-ties" to epic showdowns, with options for 2-6 players, including thrilling team play!

On Your Turn: Lights, Camera, Action!
On your turn, the spotlight is on you! Assess your pack's resources and talents, then choose your stage wisely. Will you send your pups to one of the four vibrant venues to earn valuable tokens through performances, or will you head straight to the Canine Pawpularity Panel to earn fresh resources and Apaws? Each venue has its own unique rewards and challenges, with a primary focus on a specific resource type. As the game progresses, you'll unlock additional performance spots at each venue, offering even more exciting rewards and opportunities to collect various resources. Remember, each venue board is double-sided, offering a whole new set of options and strategies to explore!

Take the Stage & Wow the Judges! Once you've gathered enough resources, it's time to shine at the Woof-tastic Amphitheatre! Trade in your tokens and the all-important Apaws (the main currency earned from the Canine Pawpularity Panel) for prized Pup Point Award cards. These cards not only bring you closer to victory but also unlock bonus points, special abilities, and a chance to climb the Judge Scorecards for even more rewards.

Winning the Game
The game ends when a player collects a certain number of Pup Point Award cards, triggering a final round where every decision counts. The number of Pup Point Award cards needed to trigger the final round varies depending on the number of players and the chosen play mode. The player (or team) with the most Pup Points at the end of the game wins the title of the next Pup Idols!

Multiple Paths to Victory
While collecting Pup Point Award cards is the most direct route to victory, Pup Idols offers multiple paths to success. Strategic players can also triumph by maximizing Pup Points through clever resource management, high-scoring performances, and the clever use of Tasty Talented Treat cards. The stage is set for a thrilling contest of strategy and skill – will your pack reign supreme?

You live a cozy life with your fellow dogs and loving human. Each day brings new opportunities to receive attention from the human. Use your cute behavior cards to get belly rubs, treat, a trip to the park...there are so many things you want to accomplish! There are only so many days during the week and the other dogs will do everything they can to get more attention from the humans then you!

On your turn, you can play two behavior cards from your hand to gain attention from the human. You can also learn a new behavior that is added to your hand by collecting and spending dog bones. At the end of your turn, your played behavior cards are discarded, until you choose to take a nap on a future turn to recover all of your behavior cards. Objectives like "Get Belly Rubs" or "Be Fed an Early Dinner" can be completed by spending the attention you've gained from the human. Each objective is worth affection points, and the most affection points at the end of the week wins!

Introduction: Storm Clouds is a simple game with asymmetric piece movement and a slightly odd opening setup evocative of the game Dodgem. However unlike Dodgem, the goal is to wipe out your opponent. This is easier said than done because you can only capture backwards and to the side. A game of Storm Clouds cannot end in a draw. Corey L. Clark designed Storm Clouds in September of 2024.

Setup: Take a standard 8x8 checkerboard, arrange the 12 checkers in 2 rows of 6 starting in the northwest corner for black and the southeast corner for white.

Gameplay: The gameplay of Storm Clouds is simple. Black has a set of non-capturing chess king moves to the north, northeast, east and south east. Black also has a set of capturing moves like a chess queen in the remaining 4 directions. White has a set of non-capturing chess king moves to the east northeast north and northwest. White also has a set of capturing moves like a chess queen in the remaining 4 directions.

Objective: The objective of Storm Clouds is to eliminate all enemy pieces.

Forced passing: if on your turn you have no legal move, you may pass your turn, passing is otherwise strictly prohibited.

—description from the designer

(つ▀¯▀ )つ ☾

Find yourself in a spur-of-the-moment scramble, racing friends for a lark. Somehow turn your Dreams, Experiences and Items into rocket-reality, so you’re first to touch the moon!

In this single-player card game with game mechanisms of grid coverage and pattern building, you race to make the rocket shape by getting a card face-down sideways in each of your 7 Positions. You need to solve a Solitaire-like puzzle, strategically using 35 cards with diverse effects, turning your cards this way and that. You can't lose!

Sub in powerful new cards, win in new ways and become Magister Ludi (Master of the Game) in the expansion, The Four Masteries.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Uno Slam is one of many variations on the popular Mattel card game Uno. It includes 108 cards as well as an electronic Slam unit, with a big red button and two card holders. The main difference from the original Uno is that every time you play a card, you have to slam the big red button.

—user summary

The UNO button is SLAMMED to start a race against the clock. Each player in turn attempts to play one card from their hand by matching either colour, number or symbol. After their turn they must quickly SLAM the UNO button before the 'BOING' sounds. 
If they fail, they must add another four cards to their hand. Players must remember to shout "UNO" when they only have one card left. 
The UNO SLAM unit has adjustable times for different levels of play. This new and exciting way to play UNO will give hours of tantalising hun for all the family.

—description from the box (1996 First Edition)

In a time when plant-eating titans still roamed our planet, survival was a constant struggle. Food and water were scarce, and bloodthirsty carnivores were always on the prowl. Take on the role of a herbivore and make sure your group survives as long as possible. Endure the trials of the Jurassic period by migrating and expanding your group with new generations. Manage your food and water supply and try to increase the numbers in your group to such an extent that carnivores are no match for you. Will your herbivore be the greatest survivor of its time?

Herbivores is a strategic 2 to 4 player dinosaur-themed game, where players control a dinosaur group in the Jurassic period. Players need to eat, drink, growth and reproduce to make sure their group survives and is also successful enough to grow in numbers. The board game contains miniatures for each dinosaur which all have their unique stats and game play. The game offers many tiles to explore, carnivores to encounter and events to experience. The first player to have placed 4 groups of generations on the board wins the game.

—description from the designer

Empire is a Trading Card Game in which two players each play a different deck of Army and Civic cards, fight over control of three territories, and attempt to deal damage to their opponent.

The game is set in a fantasy world containing 8 different factions, each with their own visual identity and gameplay mechanics, which interact in different combos and have different strengths and weaknesses.

During the game, the players fight over and settle territories on the board, which gives them access to the stronger cards in their decks. Thus, the complexity and power-level of cards ramps up as the game progresses.

In the end, the player who manages to reduce their opponents Morale total to 0 stands victorious. To win, players must carefully manage when to fight for more territory, when to build up their resources from their Civic deck, and when to simply go for the kill.

—description from the designer

In the '80s, revenge movies continued to proliferate—those films about an ordinary guy bent on delivering justice, further motivated by the alarming threat of delinquents and junkies from that era. Nothing like the gangsters of the '20s, but cinema continued to use them, along with the noir films featuring a detective in a fedora hat. Of course, where there’s a detective, there’s a mystery to solve, and the suspense genre has always captivated the public, especially in Italy, where they gave it a unique personality and style, calling it Giallo. All these genres could easily fall under that catch-all genre known as Thriller, which no one really understands what it entails. To round it all out, let’s add a few films about “perfect” heists and robberies to the video store.

Genres included:
Gangsters and Detectives ("I'll make him an offer he can't refuse...")
Giallo ("Beast!")
Heists and Robberies ("If only everything were as long as that...")
Suspense ("I ate his liver with some fava beans and a nice Chianti.")
Thriller ("What's in the box?")
Revenge ("Bet your mom wasn't as bad as mine.")

En los 80 siguieron proliferando las películas de venganza, esas pelis de un tipo cualquiera dispuesto a imponer justicia muy alentado, además, por la alarmante amenaza de kinkis y yonkis de aquellos años. Nada que ver con los gánsteres de los 20 pero el cine siguió haciendo uso de ellos y del cine noir con detective de sombrero de fedora. Claro que donde hay un detective hay un misterio que resolver y el género de suspense siempre ha sido atractivo para el gran público y más en Italia que le dotaron de una personalidad y sello propio; lo denominaron Giallo. Todos estos géneros perfectamente podrían entrar en ese género comodín llamado Thriller que nadie sabe muy bien en que consiste. Para terminar, completemos el videoclub con unas pocas películas de robos y atracos “perfectos”.

Generos incluidos:
Gansters y Detectives (Le hare una oferta que no podra rechazar...)
Giallo (Bestiaaaaa!)
Robos y Atracos (Ojala tuviera todo igual de largo...)
Suspense (Me comí su hígado acompañado de un buen Cianti.)
Thriller (¿Que hay en la caja?)
Venganza (Apuesto a que tu mamá no era tan mala como la mía.)

Stained glass has always brought light to great buildings, and since its invention in medieval times we have been able to contemplate the spectral beauty of colors when light passes through glass.
Explore the ancient art of stained glass and immerse yourself in a world of colors and light! Master the skill of transforming simple pieces of glass into masterpieces and become a renowned master stained glass artist.
In Lux Nova, players compete for spaces in the construction of a large stained glass window. Look for the best fittings to stand out from the rest, choosing the right moment to place your part of the stained glass.
The beauty of your contribution will be well rewarded with victory points, but the more valuable an area of ​​the stained glass becomes, the more points it can also earn your opponents.
(description from publisher)

Each player will use cards that show different color patterns. Each turn, players must play a card from their hand, completing as many color patterns as possible with cards already played on the table to score the most points.
When the cards run out, the player with the most points wins the game.

Trials of MAYA is a expandable card‑driven combat game where you command champions across an arena using customizable decks.

Each Champion has their own personality and several potential playstyles, breaking the game in exciting and unique ways. Playing around your enemies’ strengths is as vital as exploiting your own.

Pick from several game modes, each with its own arena and rules. Each game mode requires varying game plans, and rewards different kinds of tactical skill.

Each Champion comes with a starter deck, but you may also freely construct your deck instead before each game begins! Customize your deck based on the playstyle you feel comfortable with.

Pilot your hero, manage your energy well, chain together mind-boggling combos, and strike at the most opportune of moments to win!

About MAYA:
MAYA is an epic high-fantasy universe unfolding over films, games, graphic novels, books, toys, immersive and physical experiences. MAYA chronicles the paradoxes of a civilization where technological marvels coexist with primitive suffering - across the chasm of haves and have-nots.

MAYA is a new mythology for the new age. Enter MAYA with Trials of MAYA.

-description from publisher

This is a chess-like two-player game in which the player who completes their company's route first (i.e., the player who has placed all of their tiles) wins. Every time you place a tile you move closer to victory and closer to checkmate for both you and your opponent.

However, tiles can also move around the board by following the tracks of the tile they are moving across and any of their opponent's tiles they pass through are removed from the board.

If your opponent takes your base, you will lose. So make sure you lay your tracks well!

Enjoy this new style of abstract shedding game.

Step into the captivating universe of Mindhell! An exciting cooperative Print & Play game designed exclusively for two players, where collaboration is key to overcoming challenges and confronting your deepest fears.

In the world of Mindhell, you will immerse yourself in a succession of terrifying nightmares, carefully structured into various campaigns. Your mission will be to fulfill the unique objectives of each challenge, allowing you to progress through different scenarios and ultimately triumph over all the chilling creatures that lurk. Get ready for an immersive and emotion-filled gaming experience as you delve into the depths of your own fears in Mindhell!

—description from the designer

A medium-weight strategy board game inspired by the cola wars of the 1980s. Can you beat the real thing?

It’s the 1980s, and you’re in charge of marketing for a startup brand of cola. The market is completely dominated by the evil CorpoCola. Over the next few years, you’ll run advertising, snag shelf space, and play a few sneaky tricks to try to control the market. Will you come out on top — or will CorpoCola reign supreme?

Every turn, you play an Executive.

Executives can perform actions like running advertising campaigns, buying sponsorships, running sampling programs, or negotiating for shelf space in stores and restaurants. You can also send them on retreats so they come first in the turn order next year.

Alongside your Executives, you may also play Secret Formula cards that give you powerful abilities to gain an edge over your fellow players and CorpoCola.

Once all players have finished playing their Executives and Secret Formula cards, the Consumer dice are rolled in the Vending Machine dice tower to allocate market share points. Consumers are hard to predict — that’s why it’s important to own as many shelves and memories as you can.

Cola Wars is played over six years, from 1980 to 1985. Each player is trying to win the most market share points by the end of the game. But if CorpoCola wins the most market share points, everyone else loses!

—description from the publisher

Butter Crime is a crime to solve. Analyze all the information and solve the puzzles to get as close to the truth as possible. Grandma Rosa Melo has died and you must find out if there is something or someone behind her death.

In Butter Crime, players will face different puzzles to solve. The game starts with a letter from your grandmother's lawyer notifying you of her death. From there, with the initial information and the elements we have, we must unlock the four houses in Villa Verde.

Similar to an escape room but in a box, we must solve the more than 8 puzzles in the game through the different letters, audios, websites, newspapers and other elements. The objective of the game will be to try to solve the crime and try to get as close as possible to the final resolution.

-description from designer

The Pi Game is a family card game for several players. It takes only a few minutes to learn to play and offers many hours of fun.

A game for 1-10 players from 6 years of age and about 20-30 minutes in length.

It is a game based on the number Pi (π), a frequently used mathematical constant known to have infinite decimal places.

The base cards of the game represent these decimals. During the turn, each player must place the corresponding number until they run out of cards. The player who runs out of cards wins. The game includes special and skill cards.

—description from the designer

W101 Solomons Campaign is a solitaire game covering the campaign in the Solomons in 1942-43, during and after Guadalcanal.

The player controls US forces, while the game system controls the Japanese via random actions. Ground units on both sides are regiments and battalions, air units are wings or squadrons, and naval units are individual ships in the case of fleet carriers and battleships, and divisions or squadrons for all other types.

World at War Magazine #101, March 2025

Invasion Australia covers a hypothetical Japanese invasion of Australia in 1942, based on the premise of a Japanese victory in the Coral Sea in May 1942 and subsequent capture of Port Moresby. Ground units are regiments and brigades, air units are several squadrons grouped by aircraft type, and naval ops are abstractions.

World at War Magazine, May-June 2025
