description:
You have passed over the Serra da Estrela mountain range in Portugal and turn south in Salamanca. You are told that there is heavy traffic over the Spanish capital. You descend slowly, turning northeast over Toledo and, at 5000 feet, you are given a runway for landing. You'll have to hurry!

This is the promo airport distributed with the purchase of the Spanish edition of Sky Team.

Contain one postcard with two additional missions (Yellow and Black)

Note: Available in Sky Team Turbulence

This game consists in reading complicated sentences while respecting the Twist set by the opponents (with a hot potato in the mouth, tight lips, without breathing, etc....).

Each player in turn is invited to turn over a card and read it aloud!
All the cards turned over must be read without forking or pausing. When you draw a special card that imposes a reading constraint, you place it under the phrase card.

The player who forks, forgets the constraint or laughs (which often happens) gets one or more penalty points!

In Tokyo Big Sight Card Game, players are dealt a hand of cards from a deck numbered 1 through 17. The frequency of each card is in a double-pyramid structure, that resembles the shape of the Tokyo Big Sight conference center.

The person who leads plays a single card from their hand and declares higher or lower. The next person must then play according to the previous declaration. If a player cannot or chooses not to play, they may pass.

Play continues until only one player has cards in their hand; that player loses.

Many types of cards have special abilities which let you play multiple cards from your hand at once, ignore higher or lower calls, etc.

Auction nights are a tradition among you and your friends, but tonight’s art pieces are exquisite. You grab your trust fund debit card and take a seat, eager to walk home with a Mashterwork. Or several. Point being, you really want to be able to show off your collection of art to your friends to kindly remind them of what they didn’t win.

Potato Party: Mashterworks is a lightweight auctioneering party game for 3-8 players. Players begin the game with a single Painting, assigned a random value from the Value Points (VP) deck. During the game, paintings will be auctioned off, change value, and traded (stolen..) around the table. When all available Paintings have been auctioned off, all players total up their VP. The player with the highest VP wins and thus may gloat about their superior taste in the fine arts.

—description from the publisher

AOITRT is a 3 player only cooperative trick-taking game about "the blue bird that flew away on July 24, 2023" - referring to Twitter. The rules are written as if it is the Twitter feed of an account with the handle Aoi_Trite@Instructions.

The game only uses cards with the ranks 2, 4, and 7, and players must follow the lead player's number; there are no suits. If the sum of the players' cards is 10 or greater, they lose a life. (An exception is made for one of each number being played.)

Players win if they can play all of the cards in their hands and the deck without losing all of their lives.
This expansion adds a 5th player to the RUSE Instincts of the den game. It is added to the base game and contains all the components to enjoy the experience with the 2 mini-expansions.

It's the UNO matching card game everyone loves, but in a special edition featuring characters and settings from the incredibly popular TV series The Office. Players won't have to survive small office boredom using sarcasm and hilarious pranks, but they will try to get rid of all the cards in their hand. And if you're not quick, you may end up drawing card with the 'Kevin's Famous Chili' rule - Whoever drops all their cards LAST has to draw two cards.

Gameplay is the same as classic UNO with players matching colors and numbers as well as throwing down wild cards and penalty cards to trip up their opponents. When they're down to one card, they have to shout like they just got a bonus, 'UNO!'

Did you know that a group of sharks is called a Shiver? Shiver of Sharks is a simple deckbuilding game. You play cards to collect bite which will allow you to entice sharks into your deck, or chomp them for an immediate reward. When you chomp sharks they get replaced with chum. And of course sharks love chum, so they're distracted, which might change the cost of the sharks depending on where it lands on a 3x3 grid of sharks.

You are trying to collect the most shark teeth before all of the chum is ate, which ends the game. It plays 2-4 players and lasts anywhere from 15 minutes to an hour, depending on how fast you choose to chomp the chum, or how long you love enticing the sharks.

The first in TANSANFABRIK's Death Game Card series, the game consists of a number of cards showing chairs and cards that say if blue or red is "safe".

A number of cards are set out equal to the number of players, and each player is dealt a random blue or red card, and holds it against their forehead - showing the other players but not looking at it themselves.

On your turn, either pass or attempt to cut the wire on your card. If you correctly state the color of wire on your card, you take one of the chairs and will proceed to the next round. If you stated the wrong color, you are eliminated.

If a player passes, they reveal one undealt wire card and place it face up on the table; then, the next player takes their turn.

Multiple rounds are likely played, and the last player remaining wins.

boardgame: Unmatched Adventures: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles – Shredder and Krang
description:
Now, you're in control. Shredder uses waves of Foot soldiers (and his loyal sidekicks, Bebop and Rocksteady) to control the battlefield. The inscrutable Krang uses the Die of Ultimate Destruction for unpredictable results -- something only an IQ of 968 could comprehend.

Includes 1 character card and 30 action cards for each of the villains for competitive play.

description:
The third in TANSANFABRIK's Death Game Card series, the game consists of a number of candle cards. Each player is dealt a candle and a flame. The length of a player's candle shows how much life they have left.

A number of cards showing additional lengths of candle are revealed from the deck, and the players simultaneously point to the one they want. If only one person points to a candle, they take it and extend the candle they have. If more than one person points to a candle, no one takes it.

Then, depending upon what candles are leftover, each player's candle burns down a certain amount.  Players are eliminated if their candle burns down completely. Rounds are played until only one player's candle is left lit.

boardgame: Touch the Moon: The Four Masteries
description:
(つ▀¯▀ )つ ☾

Tackle increasingly difficult challenges in The Four Masteries, a expansion for Touch the Moon that adds game mechanisms of deck construction and multi-use cards.  Venture beyond rocketeering and learn to touch the moon metaphorically by winning in off-beat ways, without making the rocket shape.  Maybe that's what your friend meant?

Become Magister Ludi (Master of the Game) by completing Masteries I-IV.

I. Aesthetics.
Figure out how to make fun new shapes with your cards, like Arc de Triomphe and Shoulder Pads.

II.  Logic.  
Advance to the Glass Bead Variant, which constrains where you can play cards. This makes the game harder in a very cool way, like you are rocketeering through wind and rain! This is the best way to play, once you're up for the challenge.

III. Mathematics. 
Start deck-building by subbing in powerful new cards. Explore new card combinations to improve your scores.

IV.  Meditation. 
Play the Magister's deck in its entirety. Score better than ever as you slow down.  Opt out of the space race and touch the moon metaphorically by finding fulfilment in different Dreams and Experiences, winning in off-beat ways, like by Eating Over a Sink!

—description from the designer

The fourth in TANSANFABRIK's Death Game Card series, the game consists of a series of rock paper scissors matches, with a chance to switch your throw.

A series of point cards are laid on the table, and will be resolved sequentially. If a player wins 4 matches, they win the game; otherwise, the player with the most points wins.

Each player has a limited hand of cards showing Rock, Paper, and Scissors. The first player goes first and places a card face up next to the first point card. Then, the second player places a card face down. Then, the first player can either stick with the card they have played or swap to a new card. After this choice, both cards are revealed and the winner of the first point card is resolved.

Manhattan Project: Energy Empire includes all the gameplay of the original edition, as well as the 'Cold War' expansion, the new 'UN Envoys expansion', and more!

From the ashes of war, nations rise to power in the atomic age! In Manhattan Project: Energy Empire, each player takes control of a nation struggling for power in the latter part of the 20th century. They build up their nation’s industry, commerce, and government by acquiring resources, building structures, and tapping sources of energy.

The major threat in Energy Empire is not war, but uncertain global impacts, that result from side effects of industrialization and pollution. Many actions come with a cost. So, as nations become more industrious, they also increase the amount of pollution in the environment. Careful use of science can mitigate the harmful effects of industry, and can also help avert global crises.

Energy Empire uses worker placement, tableau-building, and resource management mechanics. On each turn, a player can choose to either work or generate. On a work turn, a player plays a single worker on the main board, then uses workers and energy to activate cards in their tableau. Players may spend energy to use an occupied space on the main board, so no spaces are ever completely blocked. On a generate turn, players get to renew their supply of energy by rolling "energy dice" that represent nuclear, coal, oil, solar, and other forms of energy.

The back of the game board presents a way to play the base game and expansion seamlessly together as one big game. Note the new expansion content has not been released before and won't be available by itself.

—description from the publisher

Cute kitties are here! Use food, toys, cat wands, maybe your water cup, or their favorite cardboard box to attract kitties.
Each round, on the count of "1, 2, 3!", players raise their hands simultaneously to claim the item. But if too many people go for it, no one gets anything! Collect more cat-attracting goodies than the other players to earn the cat’s adoring gaze and cozy purr-filled cuddles!

Lonja is a tactical card game for 2-3 traders in which the players try to reorganise the display of coin cards in their favour by playing cards from their hand so that their own type of coin ends up with the majority in the rows and columns.

The coin cards in your hand are used to alternately cover one card from each of the 16 cards laid out in 4x4 format, which changes the majority distribution of the rows and columns.

At the end, the player who has achieved a simple majority for each row and column is awarded points in the form of the sum of all the coin cards in the row or column.

The sum of two game rounds decides the winner. If there are three players, there is one game round.

—description from designer

In magical Arunea, a world full of mages and mysticism, a decisive battle is about to erupt... Four mage factions - Elementalists, Illusionists, Necromancers and Spiritualists - have gathered their best mages to take possession of the secret relics. These are powerful items that serve as sources of various forms of magic. Take command of one of the four mage factions and compete against each other in this magical battle for the secret relics. Strategically move your mages around the battlefield, cast spells, capture your opponent's relic and bring it to your fortress to give your faction victory!

The Secret Relics of Wizards is a 2 to 4 players strategic game where players select a group of six mages to fight for them in an arena against the opponents mages while aiming to steal their relic. The game combines game mechanics like chess, basic war-games and capture the flag with less strategic concepts like random events and skills. Each group of wizards has their own unique spells en play styles and the first player to steal the relic and bring it to their base wins the game.

-description from publisher

What begins as a quiet school day at Nevermore Academy quickly spirals into a morbid murder mystery as Wednesday witnesses a future murder in a vision. As it turns out, you and several other Outcast students are suspects in Wednesday’s vision. Because of this, you are summoned to the secret Nightshade Library to solve this mystery. Collect information around Nevermore to find the perpetrator and when and where they will strike. But what if you find out that you are the future murderer?! Compete against your fellow Nevermore students and be the first to solve the mystery of the Raven’s Truth!

Wednesday: Raven's Truth is a deduction board game. Each player receives information at the start of the game and plays a role of an outcast at Nevermore. One role among the players is the perpetrator who needs to be found by the other players, but even the player being the perpetrator doesn't know this from the start. Players try to deduce information to find out who committed the murder before a deck of cards run out and the perpetrator wins.

—description from the publisher

description:
Operation Ironclad is a wargame covering the British invasion of the island of Madagascar in 1942. At this time, Vichy French forces controlled Madagascar with the potential of turning the island into a base for Axis attacks against Allied sea lanes in the western Indian Ocean. Ironclad was the first phase of the invasion which began in May and concluded in November.

The game has two players: British and Vichy French. Each player is provided with cardboard pieces called “units,” representing land, air and naval formations. Players move their units on the map to engage enemy forces and take critical objectives. Each player has a set of Operations markers which enhance unit actions and take special actions.

Overall, the game system models operations which were conducted in sometimes inhospitable terrain. Most actions were along major lines of communications for control of critical towns, ports, and airfields. Many of the combat losses were due to disease (British) and desertion (Vichy French), and this is reflected in the combat system.

description:
Explore scenes of a crime that is impossible to solve...or is it? Prove that you are the only detective capable of getting to the truth and that these crimes are imperfect.

In Imperfect Crimes: Pop-Up Escape Book, you investigate each pop-up as in a miniature escape room. You will be able to interact with the objects and elements as if you were really there.

—description from the publisher

The game is based on a Hungarian TV-show, where famous actors improvise in strange situations. The players can put themselves in the shoes of the actors and try themselves on the improvisation stage, while their try they best to remain in role.

—user summary

Improvizációs társasjáték mindenkorra, mindenkinek!
A nagy népszerűségnek örvendő Beugró Társasjáték család legújabb tagja; mely öt új játékfajtával, és az elmaradhatatlan zsebszöveg játékkal kínál mindenkinek önfeledt és sok nevetéssel fűszerezett kikapcsolódást! A kibővített játékszabály a rögtönzések mellett egy kis taktikázási lehetőséggel teszi még izgalmasabbá a játékot.

—description from the box

Your astronauts need to visit the most out-of-this-world places for their space centre to become the best! Can you use your mathematical skills to add the most visits as possible to win.

A game developed to help children with their mental arithmetic skills, using both addition & subtraction. The game grows as the player’s ability increases.

Each player starts with 4 cards and on their turn draws to 6. Using just addition or addition and subtraction the player tries to use an astronaut AND space card to reach the number 10. If they can they place this pair in their 'space station' (a area next to them). If they can't they discard two cards and play turns to the player on the left. The game ends when a player has five pairs in their space station.

For advanced explorers the 'Cosmic Cards' can be used to +1 or -1 to a number and allowing the player to use three cards. These Cosmic card can be activated when a player has two of the same card in their hand. This adds more options of math sums to the player. 'Achievement Cards' can also be added to the game to give players target pairings to make to earn an extra point at the end of the game.

For scoring, each astronaut is worth 1 point and any achievement cards are 1 point. The player with the most points wins, if it’s a tie, most space cards win, in the event it’s a tie again the players add up the numbers on their astronaut cards and the highest wins.
This expansion the the base game Rebenta a Bolha adds a whole new deck, Frases, that contain phrases to improvise.

Spain Rails takes us back to the tumultuous time of the creation of railways in Spain in the 1800s. Unlike other countries, there was no real standardization or controls, it was reminiscent of the old west, with a plethora of rail gauges and no real government controls. The game is for 3-5 players for about 90 minutes, the player with most money wins the game.

In addition to a game board comprising two 11x17 sheets, easy to read color railroad stocks are provided, in addition to the rules. Beside that, Spain Rails uses the components found in Preußische Ostbahn (Winsome), then German Railways (Queen) and now Prussian Rails (Rio Grande). The game is designed to be conveniently playable using the components found in any one of the three aforementioned games.

Nevertheless, you do not have to own any of the above mentioned games to play Spain Rails, it is a stand alone game with a complete rulebook. You will simply need a bunch of wood cubes and other bits, namely:

    - 30 Player cubes in 5 different colors, 6 per player 
     12 orange cubes 
     13 purple cubes 
     14 green cubes 
     15 yellow cubes 
     16 red cubes 
     17 blue cubes 
     18 brown track cubes 
     21 black track cubes 
     Money: 52 $1, 52 $5, 24 $25, 24 $100 
     A draw bag or cup for the Player cubes

description:
DARK MULTIVERSE INVASION!
The Dark Multiverse has sent more envoys to terrorize Earth. Team-up with your friends to take down The Batman Who Laughs’s forces and rescue Characters along the way to join your team. Watch out for Weakness cards as they can cause your Characters to be Captured!

KEY FEATURES:
30 New Crisis main deck cards now fill their own Crisis Line-Up!
11 New Characters face off against 12 New Nemesis Cards and 13 New Crisis Cards
Includes 2 Storylines (Dark Nights: Metal and Dark Nights: Death Metal) to progress through, with each Storyline building unique challenges to overcome
Compatible with any DC Deck-Building Game base game, but optimized for Dark Nights: Metal.

CONTENTS:
14 Oversized Cards
56 Standard Cards
1 Multiverse Divider
1 Rulebook

—description from the publisher

Year 3813. In the farthest reaches of the galaxy, brave explorers are dedicated to the arduous search for the remnants of the ancient language of their ancestors. Amidst distant stars and unexplored planets, their mission is to discover and catalog as many words as possible, paving the way for access to ancient knowledge on a vast range of subjects: animals, vehicles, professions, technology, history, culture and much more. These explorers, known as Dictionauts, set out on a journey where each word discovered is not only a linguistic treasure, but also a key to unlocking ancient secrets and illuminating the path of future knowledge.

In this game, players roll 2 dice and choose: one of them will be used to navigate between the available planets, and the other will tell how many letters can be bought from the bag or exchanged with those of the planet where the Dictionaut landed. Then, he must create the best possible word that is related to the theme of the planet — which can be simple like "animals", "countries" or "vehicles", or more complex like "rooms" or "languages". The game ends once a certain number of words have been created, and the player who creates the words that scored the most, wins!

description:
The pandemic is not going to get out of control!

Five new characters join the fight against diseases and in favor of progress in the Iberian Peninsula of the 19th century: A civil engineer, a palliative caregiver, a university professor and his student, plus a visitor from the future. Science and solidarity for the benefit of humanity!

This is an exclusive promotional expansion for the XIX International Games Festival Córdoba 2024 organized by Jugamos Tod@s.


                    
                
            
        
    


¡La pandemia no se va a descontrolar!
Cinco nuevos personajes se unen a la lucha contra las enfermedades y en favor de progreso en la Península Ibérica del siglo XIX: Una ingeniera de caminos, una cuidadora de paliativos, un profesor de universidad y su estudiante, más un visitante venido del futuro. ¡Ciencia y solidaridad en beneficio de la humanidad!
Esto es una expansión promocional exclusiva para el XIX Festival Internacional de Juegos Córdoba 2024 organizado por Jugamos Tod@s.

description: This provides an optional npc to play, essentially a new goal for the game: defeat President Trump and his t-rex followers. It contains a thirty-one trump deck with a trump and four maga-rex tokens.

Talavera is an 18-card game inspired by Azul. Originally developed in Talavera de la Reina, colorful and elaborate tile designs became a hallmark of Mexican architectural forish. You are tasked with acquiring and placing tiles to complete a grand staircase.

Players take turns drafting cards representing a collection of tiles. When all the cards in the market have been drafted, each player must place tiles to complete their specific customer order. After 4 rounds of drafting and placing, the game ends and each staircase is evaluated. Points are gained when you place enough tiles to complete an order and lost for excess tiles.

The solo mode requires careful planning (and a bit of luck) to place exactly four rows of 1, 2, 3, and 4 tiles.

—description from designer

La Légende du Colibri ("The Legend of the Hummingbird") is a cooperative dexterity game in which the players represent animals that try to extinguish a massive forest fire.

Players form a two-way bucket brigade trying to get water as well as seeds from the lake at one end of the table to the trees on the opposite end while passing animal cards in the opposite way, from the fire towards the lake.

Seeds and water are represented by beads that the players must pass to their neighbour by carefully holding them on a cardboard paddle representing animal paws. The beads are lost when dropped anywhere but on the trees. Each tree needs a speciic amount of water beads to be extinguished, then a seed might be placed on them to help them grow back.

The player next to the fire must skim through a deck of cards (while also having to place beads on the trees with their paddle) in search of animal cards, which they pass to other players toward the lake. When the card arrives at the lake, it allows the player next to it to take more beads and pass them toward the trees.

The game is timed and uses an app which plays rainforest noises, and some animal cries can be heard, prompting the players to make an action. For example, if the jaguar is heard, the players must freeze into place until the jaguar stops roaring, or they must stand up and sit back down when the hornets buzz.

After two rounds, the players must count the number of seeds they have managed to get to the trees. The more seeds they got, the better their score.

boardgame: Poetry for Neanderthals: Pop Culture Edition
description:
Poetry for Neanderthals is a competitive, team based, pop culture word-guessing game where you can only give clues by speaking in single syllables. So, instead of saying "Titanic," you'd say something like "this big boat hit big ice. I King of World!" If you mess up and use a big word, such as "iceberg," you get bopped on the head with a NO! Stick and you lose points.

The goal of the game is to score the most points by correctly interpreting words and phrases. The chosen Poet starts off the game with a Poetry Card and tries to get their teammates to say the listed word, using only words with one syllable within a 90-second time limit.

The team with the most points is the winner.
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Echo of the Predator is an electronic board game (not a video game) set aboard the cargo starship Distono. Three crew members awaken from hypersleep to find themselves alerted by ADAM, the Automated Data Analysis Machine and AI autopilot of the Distono. An alien presence has somehow infiltrated the ship and is hunting the crew. Players must work together to evade the alien while searching for items needed to escape in the pod. The game board represents the control panel in the ADAM control room, featuring 121 LEDs to indicate the status of the alien and crew members.

You play as Commander Foxton Read, Science Officer Maxx Mitchell, and Doctor Lana Dawn.  If one person is playing, they will control the moves and actions of all three of these characters. If three people are playing, then each of them will have control of an individual character.  if two people are playing, you make the rules about how to control the third character.

The main goal of the game is to exit the ship in the escape pod, but escaping the ship isn't the only concern of the crew.  Food and Water provisions must be found. A supply of coolant must be found to keep the escape pod in working order.  A flight plan must also be obtained for the flight path of the escape pod.  Finding a way to transmit an SOS would also be helpful.  If you find these items, you will almost certainly be rescued once escaping the ship.   The more items you find, the more optimistic ADAM will be about your rescue. However, there are also secret ways to end the game. Exploration will reward the players.

Several game options are available on power up.  You can have unlimited time to make decisions in the game (Default), or you can choose to have a 6-second or 3-second time-frame to make movements/actions. Or choose "Original" or "Randomized" item and weapon placement. Choose between "Lights-out" or  "Lights-on" mode. In "Lights-out" mode, you can only see the alien when it moves, which means that you have to really keep tabs on where the alien is. This adds suspense and a huge element of difficulty when playing the game.  In "Lights-on" mode, you can see where the alien is located at all times.

This game contains blinking LED lights that may trigger seizures in individuals with photosensitive epilepsy.

—description from the designer

Hexemony is a 3D/Paper Print & Play tabletop game that pits you against up to 3 players, featuring:


     ASYMMETRIC OBJECTIVES: Complete your own objective before your enemies complete theirs!
     3D/PAPER PRINT & PLAY: The game comes in FDM, SLA and inkjet printable versions!
     FACING-BASED COMBAT: The 6 sides of each unit have different ATK/DEF values, making facing and flanking crucial! 
     MODULAR HEX-GRID: Map changes every game for great replayability! 
     LEGION BUILDING: Forge your own legion with unique units, objectives, orders and terrains to suit your play style!

Name Game is a playful and thoughtful card game designed to help couples agree on the perfect baby name. With 100 boys' names and 100 girls' names, the game adds an element of fun to the often stressful task of narrowing down name options. Players work together to make decisions across three distinct rounds, each with its own set of rules, aimed at fostering collaboration and compromise.

In each round, five name cards are dealt face-up in the middle, and players take turns placing "Yes" or "No" cards based on their preferences. The goal is to agree on a name by the end of the game, using strategic choices and communication throughout the rounds. Blank cards are also included for adding custom names, allowing for personalization.

The game's structured rounds create a steady, thoughtful pace, making it an enjoyable experience for couples as they work through name options together. By the end, players will have found a few names they both like, bringing them closer to deciding on the perfect baby name.

This game is created in co-operation with 'wiretoys 3ringsha(san-ring-sha)', who manufacture wire-frame rubber band guns.

BANG!!
You'll get the satisfying sound hitting the target.
Play this game anywhere.
Put the target on the floor, on a table, or hang it off a curtain rail.

The winner is the highest player.
The game is the first and the second round.

The first round,
The players try to hide the own player maker in one of the three targets. Because it's a bonus point for others.
The players shoot the gun three times. The hit targets are scored.

The second round,
the player makes the tower made by the targets. The turn player try to hit it, and scored as the same.

—description from the designer

Entice is a climbing game which plays out over three rounds. Each player begins with identical decks of 15 cards, and choose 5 of them to use the first round; 5 others for the second; and the last 5 for the third round.

The decks are numbered 1 through 15, with most cards colored red or blue, but a few are yellow.

Players play one card at a time, and the next player must play a higher card. However, some cards have icons on them to say that they can always (or never) be played on blue (or red).

Players earn penalty points in each round for how many cards they had left in their hands when they were unable to play; the person with the fewest points wins.

It is played in rounds, where each round consists of two stages: the bidding stage and the diners stage. After the second stage, the round ends, the Improvement Market is filled until the initial number of cards is available, and a new bidding stage begins until one of the Bolicheros triggers the end of the game.
In the bidding stage they will be able to acquire new cards to improve the Culinary Experiences of their restaurants. Each culinary experience is made up of 3 different types of services to be completed (each one has a food menu, a drinks menu and a staff menu). 
In the diner stage, they will pay for the culinary experiences of each of the restaurants, increasing the Prestige of the Bowlers.
To do this, reveal the first card of the Diners deck. Depending on the type of service the diners request, each Bowler will receive Coins and/or Prestige Stars as appropriate:

a.    Base payment for the service: each Bolichero will receive $2 + $1 for each culinary experience that has at least one improvement card, this is the payment that diners will make for going to eat at their restaurants.

b.    Payment for requested service: diners will pay $1 for each improvement letter that the restaurant has that is the same type of service that they have requested (food, drink or personal), whether it is incorporated into a culinary experience or if it is in the service external. This payment is the tip for serving the diners who went to the restaurants very well.

c.    Increase in Prestige: count the number of improvement letters that your restaurants have for the service that diners have requested, both for their culinary experiences and those they have for their external service. The restaurant that has the greatest number of cards from that service will receive 1 prestige star. If more than one restaurant is tied for first place, all tied Bowlers will receive 1 Prestige Star, except if all bowlers are tied. For example, in a game of 2 Bolicheros, stars will not be distributed if there is a tie. These are the diners' recommendations to other people, where they spoke highly of their culinary experiences, which will increase the prestige of the restaurants.

d.    Complete services: Only the first Bowler to collect all 3 cards of the same type of Improvement (food, drink or personal) will receive 1 Prestige Star. Prestige stars will be placed on the board to identify which Bolichero was the first person to complete it. Setting a trend is very important and arriving first will increase the prestige of any restaurant, but only whoever is first.

e.    Culinary Experience: When a Bowler completes a Culinary Experience with his 3 cards he will receive $1 + 1 prestige stars.

f.    Advertising: You can pay $10 to buy 1 prestige star from the supply, however, when the end of the game triggers, no one will be able to use their coins to buy advertising.

Every year, thousands of people use the Bom Jesus Ascensor to travel the route that separates the road from the sanctuary.  The Elevator, the oldest in the world powered by water, is itself a symbol of the city and tourists take the opportunity to take the best possible photos along the way.

In Ascensor, players are the controllers of the Ascensor (PICA).  Their goal is to get as many tourists onto the Lift as possible, but so that they don't get in the way when taking photos.

On a turn, players choose how many tourists do they want to carry in the Ascensor, with the goal of providing them a great experience by taking the best possible photos.

Then, dice are rolled and the game starts playing. The player has some limited options to mitigate dice results and must use the wisely. Plus, trying to get too many tourists in the Ascensor is tempting but can be too risky, because if the sum of the dice is lower than the number of tourists, it means there were too many people struggling to take the best pictures, thus ruining each other experience.

And be careful, because the choices you make in the beginning will impact your way back, and crown your performance with a statue or... the oblivion.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Mystery Experiences Company: The Lost City
description: Escape room in a box game based on an Aztec lost city.

In this fast-paced ladder-climbing game, players race to be the first to run out of cards. But there's a twist—when a higher set is played, you can retrieve your previous card to power up for a bigger play. Beware, though—holding onto it too long could cost you the game!

Ding Dong! is a trivia game adapted from a game played in the RougeFM radio show “Véronique et les fantastiques”. Answering a question correctly will give you a token with a letter on it. To win, you need to be the first to gather enough letters to form “Ding Dong” with them.

How to play the game?

     A player chosen randomly picks a card and read the first hint of the first question. All the other players try to anwser the question. If nobody answers, the reader gives the second hint. The third hint is read if still nobody got the anwser. The sames steps are applied for the two other questions.
     If a player gets a good anwser, they win a letter token. Some cards will give the person who answered correctly some bonuses (win two tokens, exchange a token with someone, or steal a token from another player). If no one gets the right anwser for the first question, the reader gives the first hint of the next question. The reader must read all questions, regardless of whether a player answered correctly or not.
     When the reader read everything on his card, they discard it and the person on its left becomes the new reader. Previous steps are repeated until someone wins.
     To win, you need to have enough letters to form "Ding Dong" with them, whether it's your turn of not.


Serez-vous le premier à amasser toutes les lettres nécessaires pour former le son Ding Dong? Soyez le plus rapide à sonner et répondre aux questions. Utilisez judicieusement les avantages que vous procureront les questions spéciales pour remporter la partie! Adaptation du populaire jeu de l'émission Véronique et les Fantastiques sur les ondes de RougeFM.

OBJECTIVE The objective in Crown Hearts is to take as few penalty points as possible.

The game ends when any player ends a hand by reaching or going over 100 points, at which point the player with the lowest score is the winner.

THE CARDS The 60-card Crown Hearts deck is made up of 5 suits (Hearts, Diamonds, Clubs, Spades, and Crowns).

Cards rank from 2 (low) through 9, then Jack, Queen, King, and Ace (high). There are no 10s in Crown Hearts.

Each Heart is worth 1 penalty point, as is the 9 of Crowns. The Queen of Spades is worth 13 penalty points. All other cards have no point value.

Cards which are worth penalty points feature ‘solid’ suit symbols and the point value of the card is printed below the rank and suit. All other (non-point) cards feature ‘hollow’ suit symbols.

—description from the publisher

A game of physics, wits, and cunning, Power Well gives you "Puzzle Quest meets Dice Throne," a strategic tabletop dueling game for 2 or 4 Players.

Drop the marbles into the Power Well and watch as it randomizes them and expels them into the field. Take turns removing matching marble combos to activate your character's unique abilities, all while predicting your opponent's next move to achieve victory.

—description from the publisher

Recruit the best ninjas and show the Hokage that you are up to the task of leadership in this collectible edition of Monopoly featuring the popular anime show Naruto
Become one of the invincible shinobi as you buy, sell, and trade your way to victory through training and leadership to be the last one standing in this collectible version of Monopoly
Featuring 6 custom sculpted tokens - Gaara's Sand Gourd, Akasuki's Cloud, Kakashi Anubu Mask, Shinobi Sandal, Kunai, and Shuriken

Get ready to embark on the adventure of Fly For Fight ! Take control of a squadron of 5 planes in thrilling dogfights of the Great War! In this exciting game, precise programming of each plane's movements is crucial to strategically position yourself and shoot down enemies without getting shot!
In Fly For Fight, all movements and shots are executed simultaneously, offering an immersive experience of piloting and anticipation to track and take down your opponents.

﻿﻿﻿It's a game designed for 2 players aged 8 and up, offering games lasting just 25 minutes. Are you ready for this challenge?

Fly For Fight takes place during the Great War!

Engage in tactical combat and survival environment with stunning artwork by award winner illustrator Oscar Martin, in a dystopian world of anthropomorphic animals.

Cannibal World is an expandable dueling card game where you fight as one of four factions in a post-apocaliptic world. You will be able to customize your faction's deck as you see fit.

The gameplay revolves around strategic deck management, resource allocation, and tactical combat. Players alternate turns, drawing cards from their Main or Reinforcement Decks, and use meat counters to perform various actions, such as summoning creatures, initiating combat, or activating abilities.

Combat is central to gameplay, where players use creatures to attack their opponent’s creatures or attempt to ransack their opponent's habitat. Combat is resolved through damage mechanics, with both melee and ranged attacks.

The gameplay's focus on tactical decisions, resource management, and faction-specific abilities creates a highly strategic and engaging experience.

Victory is achieved through two main conditions: eliminating all of the opponent’s creatures (from play, hand, and deck), or ransacking their habitat three times.

—description from the publisher

Celestia, the multi-award-winning reference in push-your-luck games, comes in a Big Box including: the base game, the 2 expansions, all playable goodies, exclusive Twinples, stickers for the cards, and even a fully customizable aircraft! Ready to embark on extraordinary journeys?

—description from publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
AUGUST 13, 1932, EGYPT

When a young Englishman dies in Cairo, a straightforward murder investigation becomes something much more mysterious.

You have been tasked with finding a young archaeologist, and you find him dead.​ You follow his journey backwards down the Nile, looking for clues.​ But why are your thoughts drawn to Anubis?

Can you solve the case and break the curse?

Faunian is a strategy and adventure board game that transports you to a fantastic medieval world, where decay and magic intertwine in every corner.

Take on the role of a brave Faunian commander, leading your anthropomorphic animal faction in a quest to restore the glory of your realm. Gather resources, forge alliances, and engage in tactical battles as you navigate through treacherous landscapes and face formidable foes.

With every decision, you shape the destiny of your faction, combining cunning strategies and hand management to outsmart your opponents.

Will you rise to power and reclaim your land, or will the forces of decay prevail? Dive into Faunian and embark on an unforgettable journey of strategy and adventure!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Put Erik Acorn on the tree stump and put the different colors of nuts in the basket. Then gently pull on Erik's tail until you hear a click.

The player on turn throws the dice, here you can see how many nuts you may take and of what color. You put those nuts -carefully- in Erik's mouth.

Then it's the next player's turn. The more Erik eats, the puffier his cheeks become.

And you know for sure, Erik is going to poop out all those tasty nuts sooner or later. All players must then collect nuts - with one hand - as quickly as possible.

You put the gathered nuts on your supply card. And if that card is completely full and you are the first, then you have won.

It gets extra exciting when you roll the dice with a color that is no longer in the supply basket. In that case, you may take a note from another player and feed it to Erik or.... You must remove one of your own.

If Erik Acorn is snacking on lots of nuts,

you can make sure he poops faster.....!

Get your ghouls together for a spooky game of Spirit Monopoly! This game adds a Halloween spin on buying, selling, and trading properties. It's the perfect way to end a long night of trick-or-treating and causing mischief!

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – We Don't Need Their Scum Unit Pack
description:
Embrace the thrill of the hunt with some of the galaxy’s most feared bounty hunters! Boba Fett, Dengar, Bossk, and IG-88, each with their unique set of skills, are ready to undertake the deadliest of missions. As expert and relentless trackers, these hunters often succeed where all others fail. Boba Fett, with his advanced Mandalorian armor and silent demeanor, instills dread in his quarry. Dengar, scarred yet determined, proves to be a tenacious predator. Bossk, the ruthless Trandoshan, never fails his hunt, while IG-88, the droid assassin, executes missions with chilling precision. Their mere names invoke terror—dive into the hunt with these infamous bounty hunters!

Contents:
    4 Miniatures
    4 40mm Bases
    4 Stat Cards
    4 Stance Cards
    4 Order Cards
    Insert Sheet

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Maximum Firepower Squad Pack
description:
Seeking refuge from the Empire, the Rebellion has gone into hiding on the remote and hostile planet of Hoth. Frozen worlds are no barrier for the elite forces of the Galactic Empire and it falls to specially trained and equipped soldiers to root them out. Leading the assault is General Veers, a highly regarded commander skilled in using combined arms tactics to overcome both entrenched foes and dangerous environments. His hand-picked Snowtrooper Lieutenant and soldiers are outfitted with sealed and heated armor and breathers, enabling them to endure weeks of exposure to the most frigid environments while carrying sufficient firepower to overwhelm any resistance…

Contents: 
    4 miniatures
    4 40mm bases
    3 Stat Cards
    3 Stance Cards
    3 Order Cards
    1 Insert

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – What Have We Here Squad Pack
description:
As an independent mining colony and cosmopolitan hub for merchants and smugglers from across the galaxy, Cloud City faces many potential complications. The bureaucratic dance and too often hostile negotiations required to keep things running smoothly are left to Baron- Administrator Lando Calrissian. Calrissian’s a storied past as a former gambler and smuggler has given him the suave, savy, and swagger needed to ably navigate the challenges of responsibility. At his right hand is Lobot, a man equipped with advanced brain-augmenting hardware that lets him link directly to the city’s central computers, providing instantaneous control. Both are supported by the stalwart Wing Guard, capable police personnel who operate with discretion and know better than to question their orders…

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – This Is The Way Squad Pack
description:
Mandalorian culture survives The Great Purge! The Armorer, a stoic warrior and an adherent to The Way of Mandalore plays a vital role in forging beskar armor in the ancient tradition of her people. Paz Vizsla defends his beliefs and allies with all the firepower and resolve befitting a Mandalorian of his stature. He is flanked by his fellow Covert Mandalorians—proud warriors skilled in hit and run maneuvers.

Contents: 
    4 minis
    4 40mm bases
    3 Stat cards
    3 stance cards
    3 order cards
    1 insert

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Imperial Riot Control Squad Unit Expansion
description:
Deployed in response to growing unrest on Imperial Planets, Riot Control Squads are soldiers who specialize in suppressing unruly populations and containing violent civilian disturbances. They wield heavy shields, stun batons, and are commonly reinforced by specialist troopers such as Marksmen, or intimidating KX-series Security Droids. Their tactics are brutal and direct – often leading to physical confrontations between Riot Trooper squads and the citizens they are trying to manage. This expansion for Star Wars: Legion contains 7 finely sculpted Imperial Riot Control Squad miniatures, enough for one Corps unit, along with new unit and upgrade cards to ensure peace and prosperity across the galaxy.

Contents:
    7 minis
    7 27mm bases
    1 Unit card
    2 upgrade cards
    1 punch sheet
    1 insert

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Rebel Sleeper Cell Unit Expansion
description:
Rebel Sleeper Cells formed in secret across the galaxy to undermine the growing influence of Imperial rule. The effects of these cells are felt in countless ways, ranging from administrative headaches to large scale direct assaults on military targets. Regarded as experts in guerilla warfare, Sleeper Cells exploits various seams in established defenses to slip behind enemy lines and create the most chaos possible. Rebel Sleeper Cells are also creative and adaptable, armed with a variety of weapons, tools, and explosives to amplify their effects once revealed. This expansion for Star Wars: Legion contains 8 finely sculpted Sleeper Cell trooper miniatures, enough for one Special Forces unit, along with new unit and upgrade cards to surprise any adversary.

Contents:
    8 minis
    8 bases
    1 Unit card
    2 upgrade cards
    1 punch sheet
    1 insert sheet

boardgame: Star Wars: Unlimited – Twilight of the Republic
description:
Twilight of the Republic is the third set of Star Wars: Unlimited. This set puts the Clone Wars on center stage, with all the classic characters, ships, and weapons that hailed from the conflict between the Galactic Republic and the Separatist Confederacy. Battle droids and clone troopers take to the field en masse in this set, so much so that the set introduces a brand-new type of token: token units! There’s a lot of exciting content to cover, so without further ado, let’s dive right into Twilight of the Republic!

—description from the publisher

description:
Guess What (你猜怎么着) is a family party game designed for children aged 5 and above. In this game, players each wear a headband with a card that only others can see. The objective is to guess the content of your card by asking yes-or-no questions before the timer runs out. Each correct guess rewards players with coins, and the player with the most coins at the end of the game wins.

Gameplay is enhanced by multiple variations and a spinning wheel that adds additional layers of interaction and complexity.

boardgame: Middara: Back To School Resin Kit
description:
Making the Grade

Education serves an important role in the world of Middara. One foot through the portal, and your body's different, you might have crazy new powers, and all the animals want to kill you. The Kingdom of Elenia, where Unintentional Malum takes place, has an Institute system in place to educate new arrivals, so they can become productive members of society instead of greasy red stains on the wall.

Everyone has to make it through the Institute, including the heroes of our story. We see only a brief snapshot of this moment at the beginning of UM:1. Now we've created some awesome minis and items to explore the Institute with a little more depth.

This limited resin kit expansion includes 5 highly detailed resin miniatures of Nightingale, Remi, Rook, Zeke and Nyx. These miniatures may be used as alternate sculpts for their corresponding figures in Middara: Unintentional Malum - Act I. All characters include full color initiative cards, adventurer cards, art cards, and 9 new items for use in your games of Middara.

—description from the publisher

This combines the Vestalia A and B resin kits into one package with plastic miniatures.

Vestalia A:
Kill monsters, get loot, and level up - in a bikini.

This product contains 5 highly detailed resin miniatures, featuring Damocles, Rook, Justice, Shayliss, and Remi. These miniatures may be used as alternate sculpts for their corresponding figures in Middara: Unintentional Malum - Act I.

All characters include full color initiative cards, adventurer cards, art cards, and 9 new items for use in your games of Middara.

Vestalia B:
The sexier the armor, the better the stats.

This product contains 5 highly detailed resin miniatures, featuring Padric, Nightingale, Zeke, Effie, and Zafir. These miniatures may be used as alternate sculpts for their corresponding figures in Middara: Unintentional Malum - Act I?.

All characters include full color initiative cards, adventurer cards, art cards, 1 familiar card and 8 new items for use in your games of Middara.

—description from the publisher

This combines the previously available resin kits into one pack with plastic miniatures instead of resins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: We Have Goats!: Not For Kids Deck
description:
The Not for Kids Deck features extra cards that can be added on to the family-friendly version.   These cards have mildly suggestive and hillarious PG-13 humor.

Add these cards into the regular decks: The 2 red cards go with the red Goat Lab deck, and the 14 white cards go with the white cards.

Most cards have instructions and should be played just like their standard version.

Was an optional expansion in the Kickstarter.

This uniquely updated alternative to the classic crossword offers players the change to customize every game they play! Easy or difficult, simple or challenging, slow or swift, it's a fantastic festival of word fun for the whole family!
-from the back of the container

Set in the same world as TrollFest, players will put together teams of fantasy denizens to compete in the TROLLympics! Events are revealed and then athletes and action cards are drafted. Then for each event, players will put together a team to compete, wager on who the winner will be, play action cards, and then add their athletes values to corresponding dice rolls to determine the winner.

Score points by doing well AND by correctly guessing the winners of events. The player with the most points after a winter season and a summer season of events will be the winner!

—description from the publisher

Trick 100 is a game where you have to outbid - or you cheat! To do this, you play a card face down and simply claim that it is higher than the card currently on display and shares a digit/color with it. Whoever‘s turn it is can believe it or not. But be careful, not only cheating is possible in this game, but also bluffing. If you are wrong you have to draw 3 cards. And nobody wants that, because whoever plays all the cards first wins.

-description from designer
Very similar (this goes from 01 to 99) to 1000? (this goes from 01 to 66)

Failure mode is a racing game. As such, your 
objective is simply to move the farthest past the goal space at the end of the track.
But it has two twists:
The first is how you select the actions:
You play a card from your hand onto an actions row – then match either side of the card with a card on the row, and then a second, and match it with either end of the selection.
After you select all the actions (and extras), you use each of them, and with them you activate modules on your ship – those let you move, or attack other players.
The second twist is that every turn, after you select your actions, from players' attacks, or when you crash into obstacles, you must draw a number of failure tokens from your bag. These cubes will cause damage, and potentially destroy some or all the modules of your ship.
Can you manage the risk to keep your ship together, or will you push it to the limit and drag the pieces across the finish line?

Gameplay:
Every turn:

     Select your actions playing a catd from your hand.
     Draw new damage from your bag.
     Use selected actions to move and attack.
     Discard 1 card, and draw back to 5.
     Apply all damage, potentially destroying some or all your modules.
     Optionally, build a new module, for a risk cost.
     Refresh / refill modules market.


—description from the designer

Unbearable Bears is a comedically styled card game featuring over 54 cute bears, cards, and unique storylines.

Players must be the first to obtain 12 cards or outlast their opponents, all while avoiding the destructive Unbearable Bear.

Cards are strategically used to attack other players, change the game's setting, or play whimsical moves like shielding friends and accusing opponents of cheating!

Unbearable Bears is unexpected, loud, and pawsibly the greatest game ever made!

—description from the publisher

In Merchants of Haniwa, players draw tiles from a bag and arrange them in two lots on their player board. These lots are available for themselves or other players to buy, based upon the contents of the lot; if another player buys the lot, you collect the money, but if you buy your own lot, you pay the bank.

Tiles that are bought go into a player's exhibition, with various set collection incentives.  When a player's exhibition is full, it is sold for a profit.

The player with the most money at the end of the game wins.

boardgame: サンサンダイス (San San Dice)
description:
San San Dice is a dice drafting game for 2 players where you are placing the dice into a 3 x 3 grid in front of you. Each of the possible rows, columns, and diagonals of the grid are assigned a number, and the players are competing to have the best 3 dice poker hand among the 8 sets.

Players are also dealt two cards at the start of the hand which are kept secret from the other player. One of these cards specifies a position, and the other specifies a color or number; the die that player places in the specified position has the adjustment shown on the other card made to it.

The players compare row by row, column by column, etc. The person who wins the majority of these wins the game.
designers: Tsukii Yosuke (つきい　ようすけ)

description:
In Dropping Drops, players are simultaneously revealing cards and adding them, in numerical order, to the bottom of a grid of cards.

If a card ends up being placed orthogonally next to a card of the same color, the player who played that card takes that group of cards as a penalty.  Then, the columns of the grid collapse, and additional orthogonal matches may be created; the player would take those as penalties too.

The player with the fewest penalty points wins!

Das kuriose Österreich Quiz is a trivia game about Austria containing 202 question cards with three multiple choice answers each. In contrast to "Das neue Österreich Quiz" the questions are curious and funny.

The game is played in turns. One player is reading while the other players try to answer. For a correct answer the player earns a point.

Depending on number of players, the game ends after a defined number of rounds. The player with the most points is the winner

Sheng Xiao is a trick-taking game suitable for 3 to 5 players, where players compete for the positions of the 12 zodiac animals. Each turn, players can choose to play a combination of 1 to 4 cards, while the other players must respond by either countering with their own cards or choosing to pass. As the game progresses, each player must cleverly utilize their cards to capture the final trick and earn the highest score.

But beware, in this game, having the strongest mouse doesn't guarantee an easy victory! The seemingly weaker cat can turn the tide at just the right moment and claim victory. Who will be the ultimate champion in this animal showdown? Join the challenge and find out!

—description from the designer

A board game that allows you to travel the world while learning about countries and experiencing exciting real-life adventures.
The game's ultimate goal is to succeed in being the first traveller to globe-trot around the world.
Players will emerge victorious while building knowledge and familiarising themselves with traveling information!
As the Travellers make wise financial plans to travel at the lowest cost, they will experience various surprises that come with travelling!
So... let's begin the journey around the world!

—description from the designer

As a craft beer lover, nothing excites you more than a local brewfest. You have the opportunity to sample various beer styles from several breweries all in one place. The drawback: you only have a few hours and need to pace yourself so that you don’t drink too much and feel awful later. You will need to be smart to maximize your experience.

The objective of Brewfest Buzz is to score the most points by tasting different beer styles from as many breweries as possible. You will need to manage your buzz level and dehydration so you don't suffer from a post-brewfest hangover.

You will score points for sampling beers from different breweries and trying different styles of beer. You will also earn points by maintaining the desired buzz level, but don't get too dehydrated!

The player with the most points after everyone has finished their trips around the board will be the champion of the brewfest and the winner of the game.

—description from the designer

Happy Garden is a family game with a feel-good atmosphere, evoking the 70s and the Flower Power era.

In Happy Garden, each player must create the most beautiful garden. To do this, they choose one tile per turn from the Garden Center (flowers, garden gnomes, or windmills) and place it in their garden, adjacent to another tile.

The goal is to validate each tile to score points. To validate a flower, it must be surrounded by a flower of the same color (there are 6 colors), but of a different shape, as well as a flower of the same shape (there are also 6), but of a different color.

Garden gnomes are color jokers (each gnome contains 3 colors) and can be validated if they are surrounded by the 3 corresponding colors. As for the windmills, they are validated when aligned in a row or column containing 3 flowers of the same shape.

Of course, all of this would be simple if you could take more than one tile per turn, which is of course not the case! And to make matters worse, the Garden Center is not always fully available…
Things get even more complicated when you have to play with butterflies, wheelbarrows, earthworms, and moles to improve your score or reduce others’ ones!

Oh yes, it’s also important to mention that you need to be quick to complete your garden, as the number of turns is limited... ?

Behind its feel-good appearance and easy setup, Happy Garden hides a true gem of strategy and deep thinking. The one tile per turn rule forces players to plan the best combos to achieve the highest score possible.

Accessible enough for a mainstream audience and yet deep enough to satisfy the most demanding game enthusiasts, Happy Garden is one of those games you want to play again as soon as the first game ends!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: We Have Goats!: Earth Sprite Promo Card
description:
Backers who ordered We have Goats and Cast & Captire received one promo card for each game.

The Earth Sprite card is thr Kickstarter exclusive card for those who We Have Goats! It features the Earth Sprite (from Cast & Capture) as a movement card for We Have Goats!

Text says:
Move goat 5 spaces - the Earth Sprite allows you to pass through boulders and grass

Promo card celebrating the 25th Anniversary of the game. Shuffle the card in the deck.
Play the card at the end of your turn. It cancels your frog movement. Place the frog back on its previous position and flip any tile that was revealed back down. You can now chose to play the same frog, or to play a different one.

The French edition of the card was available in Plato Magazine 168.

West Front '44 is a two-player wargame of the campaigns along the Rhine in the winter of 1944-45 (December to March). The Allies are attempting to establish bridgeheads across the Rhine River while the Germans are planning for their Ardennes counteroffensive. The game uses a variant of the Boots/Manstein's War system to model command, control, and logistics on an operational scale.

Both players have a wide range of operational plans which they can execute to attempt to seize certain strategic objectives The general structure of the game is that each player uses command markers to activate their forces, which are divided into sub-commands (army groups and similar echelons). Each player has special markers which provide additional operational capabilities. Can you turn the tide of history in that decisive winter of 1944-45?

Each game turn (GT) represents two weeks. Each hex on the map represents 27 kilometers (17 miles) from side to opposite side. Units are primarily corps. Air units (Allied numbered air forces and German Flieger Korps). represent air cover for a sector of the front.

The 1942 Fall Blau offensive to seize the Caucasus oilfields. Game assumes that the Germans did not shift forces from Army Group A to Stalingrad so the Axis has an enhanced force. But they must make it to Baku on the Caspuan to win a decisive victory.

Game Scale:
Map: Rostov to Astrakhan, Batum to Baku
One hex = 27 kilometers
Turns = two weeks (summer, fall), one month (winter)
Axis units = divisions; Soviet units = mostly corps with naval infantry and Guards divisions
Air units = Luftwaffe geschwader, Soviet air armies

Scenarios:
What if Army Group A had full support (instead of elements being switched to the Stalingrad battle): Axis gains several mech class units, Italian Alpine corps.
Historical Operation Edelweiss: random events cause Axis units to be switched to Stalingrad front.

Game System
Double impulse, with second impulse requiring supply expenditure.
Assault and Mobile CRTs.
Demolition and repair of oilfields.
Panzer divisions can break down into kampfgruppe
Special units: commandos, naval infantry, optional German airborne.

Stalingrad campaign off-map determined by random events with possibility of a Red Army winter offensive.

boardgame: Hail Caesar Epic Battles: The Punic Wars – Hannibal
description:
The Hannibal Battle Set is the perfect way to dive into the Punic Wars with Hail Caesar Epic Battles! Designed specifically to allow you to easily represent the huge armies of Rome and Carthage on the tabletop, and presenting amazing modelling and painting opportunities, from the iconic massed ranks of the Roman Legions to the mighty and terrifying Carthaginian war elephants, this enormous box nets you complete Roman and Carthaginian armies, alongside a Hail Caesar Epic Battles rulebook, and a copy of the Scipio battlegame, a quick-to-play system that introduces players to the key concepts of Roman military tactics – the perfect primer for the Punic Wars!

The Punic Wars, fought between the great powers of Rome and Carthage, were the greatest series of conflicts of the ancient world. Vast armies fought across the Mediterranean and beyond to decide the fate of empires. Among those who would carve their name into legend was the Carthaginian Hannibal Barca, one of the finest generals of his age, who would cross the Alps to invade the Roman heartlands and come very near to destroying Rome herself. The Romans, meanwhile, would seek to establish and consolidate their domination of the Mediterranean, paving the way for future conquests.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Once upon a time, all animals on earth were wild. Even the cute pink-cheeked pigs were wild boars. One day, people realized that it was better and more convenient to domesticate them than to run through the forest and hunt. That's just for the first boar, you still have to rush into the nearest forest to lure and catch it!

Метнись кабанчиком! ("Run like a hog!") is a gambling family game in which you have to play cards with different baits so that the boar goes to you. A ripe acorn, a fragrant truffle, juicy carrots, or maybe a portrait of a cute wild boar — what will a wild animal like so far? Choose wisely and tame the boar first!

At the beginning of the game, a round token is laid out in the center, on which a wild boar is placed. Players choose a color and lay out the corresponding track cards, receive bait cards of the selected color and take 5 of them in their hand.

The game consists of a series of quick rounds in which players select 1 card from each hand, and then simultaneously open them and determine which way the boar will run. The boar will run towards the bait with the highest value, but if several players have played the same bait, then such cards are canceled.

The winner will be the player who managed to lure the boar to himself faster than the others.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In Dragon Cantina, 1 to 4 players step into the world of competitive restaurant management. Each player controls their own magical eatery, racing to serve enchanted customers before they get tired of waiting.

A delicious blend of drafting, engine building, and dice placement, where players will gather resources and hire top-notch staff to optimize their restaurants.

Each round, a number of dice are rolled, and the available cards for the round are revealed. Players will take turns to snag the best resources as they draft their way to a dream kitchen!

Activate your staff with dice to serve hungry clients, while hiring new team members and purchasing upgrades from the available cards.

Watch out for Felisberto, the dragon and former chef turned food critic, who occasionally visits to award bonuses to the restaurants that impress him.

The excitement builds until 10 rounds are complete. Optimize your setup with every turn and cook up a winning strategy!

—description from the publisher

Have you ever looked into a badger hole? They say it's very cozy there. However, after winter, things are not in their places at all: cans from the pantry got into the nursery, shoes from the hallway — into the kitchen, and books from the library can not be found at all. Let's help the badgers put everything in order in the burrow!

Полный порядок ("Very Orderly") is a fun point to point movement game with cute characters in which you will help the badgers put everything in its place, and at the same time collect 5 mini-puzzles. The playing field is a burrow of badgers with 6 different rooms, and each puzzle consisting of 6 large wooden tiles is an image of one of the rooms of the burrow. At the beginning of the game, all the tiles are shuffled and laid out face down in the rooms.

During the game, you will move your badger figures from room to room, open the tiles and move them to the right places. Your goal is to completely put together the puzzle of the room that is set on your goal card! As soon as all 6 tiles with fragments of this room are collected in one of the rooms and there are no extra tiles left in it, check your goal cards. If one of the players has a goal card that matches this room, he becomes the winner!

--description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Story of Many: The Sunken Empire
description: A brand new story arc where you will try to tame the Arkhosian Seas and become one of the Pirate Lords. Choose your allies between three different pirate factions, navigate perilous seas, dive into uncharted depths where the light does not dire to venture, and customize your own ship as you exchange crossfire with deadly pirates.
As a follower, gain for free the Reaverwitch Class and all its components with any pledge level. Also available as an add-on.

In this may-or-may-not-follow-the-suit trick-taking game, success depends on how players win rounds and allocate their workers. Demeter determines whether the highest or lowest value prevails, challenging players to manage their hands carefully.

Manage resources by gathering, trading, and selling goods, while the delayed return of workers until the end of the next turn emphasizes the importance of blocking locations and precise planning. The game demands constant decision-making and adaptation to changing conditions.

Game Turn:
On their turn, a player begins by playing a card from their hand. All players participate by following the suit or choosing not to, depending on the suit’s availability for allocating workers to specific locations. Players play one card each in a round. After all cards are played, the winner of the round is determined based on the current state of Demeter:

If Demeter is in Blessing, the highest card wins.
If Demeter is in Trial, the lowest card wins.
In case of a tie, the last card played wins.
The winner of the round collects the played cards, places them face down in their area, and then places their worker in a location corresponding to the suit of the winning card. Each location allows players to gather, trade, or sell resources. The available locations are:

Market (accessible with any suit): Sell goods for Miliarenses.
Forest (only with Forager): Collect wild fruits or mushrooms.
Field (only with Farmer): Collect cabbage or barley.
Pasture (only with Shepherd): Collect sheep or Angora goats.
Shearing Hut (only with Shearer): Exchange sheep or Angora goats for wool and meat, or collect baskets.
Players can also choose to perform a special action, such as offering their Garlands to trigger Demeter’s Blessing, which can help them manipulate the flow of the game.

Game End:

The game ends when all the cards have been played, meaning no cards are left in the draw pile or in players’ hands. Players then proceed to the final scoring.

Winning the Game:

The player with the most Miliarenses at the end of the game is the winner. However, in the case of a tie, the player with the most unsold goods wins. If the tie persists, the player with the most round victories is declared the winner. If the tie continues, the players share the victory.

There are 12 dice in the Fireside Story Dice tin. The two red dice are actions, the two yellow dice are moods, the five orange dice are objects, the two green dice are activities, and the single blue die is settings. Players roll some or all of the dice and make up stories based on the images. Perfect for inspiring story-telling around the campfire, on a car ride or anywhere you want to tell a tale.

Herring reAction is an interactive party and family game for kids aged 5 and up, but adults also have a lot to laugh about during the rounds.
With action, reactions, lots of fun and of course happy herrings, this game for 3 to 8 players is based on a memory-reaction game.

Which player literally turns themselves into a monkey herring?
Herring reAction is a crazy memory-reaction game where clown herrings stick their tongues out at each other and laugh as they bag the prize. You don't need the brains and the "Ahhh" effect of the inventor herring, but rather the nimble fingers of the rockstar herring to be the first to catch the matching pair of herrings with completely exaggerated moves and sounds.
A bird-herring-wild children's, family and party game that lets all inhibitions fall away.
Quick, easy and almost no rules!

—description from the publisher

Crete, 20 - 22nd May 1941

The game portraits struggle between invading German forces and Allied defenders of Greek island Crete in May 1941. The gameplay shows first 3 days, which decided German success. 
Gamers can play small scenarios using boards showing four landing zones. Moreover, there are four variants of landing German forces. Scenarios can be played simultaneously as campaign. This way German player must decide where commit reinforcements. He has elite units in his disposal, but his goals are ambitious.
Main challenge for Allied player is proper action coordination of possessed forces and mounting strong counterattack during first stage of operation. As we know, Allied commanders were unable to do this. Maybe you will perform better?

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great battles of Small Units (Strategemata)

boardgame: Das kleine böse Kartenspiel: Monster Mogeln
description:
Once again, all the monsters have escaped from the little bad book. Can you help banish them back into the book? 
To win this game, you MUST cheat! This is the only way to get the all the monsters and, above all, the  unicorns back into the book before the great sorcerer Magnus Myst realizes that something is wrong. A fun bluffing card game.

Um in diesem Spiel zu gewinnen, MUSS geschummelt werden!
Für alle, die »Das kleine Bösen Buch« lieben, gibt es ein neues lustig-böses Kartenspiel: Monster-Mogeln!
Denn um das kleine Böse Buch zu überlisten, müssen alle Spielerinnen und Spieler schummeln, bis sich die Balken biegen. Nur so bekommt man auch die weniger bösen Monster und vor allem Liebmaries Einhornkarten zurück ins Buch gebannt, bevor Magnus Myst merkt, dass etwas nicht stimmt ...

Even more quiz fun for the Smart 10 game family!

The new theme pack Rock & Pop can really get you thinking: What number should be added to the song title ‘When I'm ...’? And from which country are Ed Sheeran and Shakira from?

Who can score with knowledge or simply guess the right answer?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Guess My Animal!: Endangered Species Charades
description:
Learn about 40 endangered animals and their wild and wacky behaviors with this interactive card game featuring a pull-out poster showing every animal, and a colorful booklet with more fascinating facts about their habitat, diet, and behavior.

The charade activities in the card game Guess My Animal! introduce kids to the amazing and weird behaviors of 40 different animals: Roll into a ball like a frightened pangolin. Take a break and lie face down on the floor like a sunflower starfish. Hang around, slowly chewing, like a pygmy three-toed sloth. Crack shells on your belly like a sea otter. Smile and wave your frilled gills like an axolotl. Lie in wait, then lunge for your pray like a crocodile. Hunch up your shoulders, look down, and pretend to vomit on yourself like a nervous California condor!

From the aye-aye to the colorful puffleg hummingbird and the golden poison frog, every animal is featured on its own colorfully illustrated card along with simple instructions on how to act out one aspect of that animal’s distinctive behavior and challenge the other players to identify the animal. The deck is accompanied by instructions for beginner, middle level, and advanced players, a poster showing all of the 40 featured animals, and a booklet with more interesting information about each animal. This creative play card game makes learning fun and engaging for kids ages 6 and up, along with their adults.

Improve your Beyond the Horizon experience with this exclusive promo set.

The set includes:

     3 Leader Cards
     3 Territory Tiles
     3 Goal Cards

A Monopoly clone game based on the Canadian province of Prince Edward Island. 
You buy common tourism areas. 
You have to bankrupt your opponents to win.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: The Publish or Perish Game: Expansion 1 – The Revenge of Reviewer 2
description:
This expansion includes 38 Action Cards that make it easier to play the base game with more than 6 players. Some of the cards can also be used to achieve different effects compared to the Action Cards in the base game. If plagiarism and IRB audits in the base game are not chaotic enough for you, Reviewer 2 in this expansion will take it to another level.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Publish or Perish Game: Expansion 2 – Academic Sh*tpost
description:
This expansion introduces 12 new Manuscript Cards that set out to elevate the absurdist narrative of the original game. These additions include four “sh*tpost” articles in predatory journals that accept any Action Cards to get papers published. Additionally, this expansion includes eight Manuscripts that are (marginally) significantly inappropriate. The content ranges from mild to wild. Use this expansion at your own risk.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Publish or Perish Game: Expansion 3 – Midterm Grading Extravaganza
description:
This expansion includes 160 Trivia Cards that expand the trivia component of the original game. These cards can also be used in other trivia games.

—description from the publisher
Pick a theme and try to guess what your teammate would think, while they try to guess yours. If your answers are the same, you win.

Player, whose turn it is, gets one of the 12 clues and chooses a song fitting to the clue. Other players listen to that song and then try to guess, witch one of the 12 clues is the player referring to with his song. Correct answers will give players points. If other players like the song, player who played the song, gets points.

The Dumpling Defenders is a cooperative and dynamic game created by students of the Bezmezh school.

In Dumpling Defenders, players play as domestic cats who defend their delicious dumplings from the invasion of stray cats. Your goal is to team up and act like a real team to drive away the intruders.
