Use traps and other items to protect your dumplings. Will you be able to drive the cat away with a vacuum cleaner, or distract it with a ball? And what about the Cat Boss, who has his own cunning plans? Find out by playing Dumpling Defenders!

Game features:

Uniqueness: The game was invented by school students and has no analogues in the world
Cooperative game: Players work together to defeat a common enemy.
Interactivity: Each round brings new challenges and opportunities.
Flexible number of players: You can defend the dumplings with 1 to 4 players!
Creative approach: Your strategy will determine the fate of your favorite dumplings!
Play solo or with friends and enjoy a unique experience that will fill your evenings with laughter and unforgettable moments. Protect the dumplings and become a hero in your own kitchen!

—description from the publisher

The game is based on the Russian folk tale Little Hut (Teremok).
The teremok is located on the edge of the forest. It is neither tall nor short… And you have to decide who lives in it. Well-known fairy-tale animals will visit the teremok. Invite as many characters as you can to your teremok, but make sure there is enough space for everyone - otherwise, the teremok might collapse!

Терем-теремок ("Terem-Teremok") is a fun children's game where players take character cards from a shared stack and place them in their teremok-board. On the one hand, leaving a lot of empty space in the teremok makes it less likely for you to win, but on the other hand, if you get greedy and don't plan well, the house might collapse. "Terem-Teremok" teaches players how to plan ahead and be patient, as well as how to use their spatial thinking and design skills.

Depending on the number of players, the game can last 3 or 4 rounds.

In each round, players will place fairy-tale characters in their board. On their turn, they will open a character card, choose the appropriate piece, and place it in their board. They can then choose to open another card or end their turn. The fewer empty cells remain in a board, the more victory points (raspberries) a player will receive. However, if a character cannot be placed in the board due to a lack of space, the house will collapse and the player will not receive any raspberries.

In subsequent rounds, teremoks will grow and more characters will visit the house. The player who accumulates the most raspberries at the end of the game will be the winner.

--description from the publisher (translated)

With up to 5 teammates and 1 game master, you have up to 60 minutes to rob the biggest and the most secure casino in Las Vegas and escape!

If you don't solve all of the puzzles before 60 minutes, everyone loses together.

The game includes 3 different stories, 3 difficulty levels, 131 cards, 6 role badges, an hour-long soundtrack and a poster.

—user summary

Saurez-vous vous échapper ? . Vous avez 60 minutes pour vous enfuir ! . 3 scénarios différents avec des niveaux de difficulté facile, moyen et expert. . Le Blackjack est le casino le plus sécurisé de tout Las Vegas et c'est bien pour cela que vous l'avez choisi comme cible de votre braquage. Pour mener à bien un tel projet, il vous faudra recruter les meilleurs complices, dénicher les informations les mieux cachées, réussir à entrer dans la chambre forte et surtout sortir de là avec le butin, sans vous faire arrêter !. Vous avez 60 minutes, pas une de plus, si vous voulez cambrioler le Blackjack et repartir avec le pactole !

—description from the publisher

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Mountains of Godai – Living Card Deck
description:
Continue your adventure in the lush Kapikiawal Jungle and rugged Nobukazari Highlands. Add 60 new cards to supplement the Mountains of Godai Core Box.


     12 Caprakan Commission Cards: A 12 card arc to experience unique story-based play in the Xumucane Empire.
     12 Ishidan Commission Cards: A 12 card arc to experience unique story-based play in the Ishidan Empire.
     12 Harvest Cards: 12 new cards to add to any of your MoG Games.
     12 Power Up Cards: 12 MoG-Themed Power Ups for you to use in any of your HEXplore It games.
     12 Villain (Jaethi) Cards.


The content in this deck box requires the use of the Mountains of Godai Core box.

—description from the publisher (edited)

A General Election has just been called and a perfectly good suit has been soaked through. Pick the CON, LAB, REF, LD or GRN Party and do whatever it takes to win in just five short weeks.

You will need to carefully balance constructing the perfect manifesto against going out to canvass in constituencies and changing the voters' minds in your favour. Every manifesto has to balance the books (or at least give the impression of it) and you will find yourself desperately trying to outwit your competitors.

Players take turns to draw a policy, and either a second or  canvas in constituencies. They then lay out their manifesto. After all players have been, a poll is taken each week (round) to determine who's manifesto is most influential. After 5 weeks, the General Election occurs and the total impact of players policies on the constituents is calculated. The one with the most votes, points accrued along the way, wins.

Each set contains:

     Party specific policies based on the real manifestos
     Common policies that any Party can play
     Blank policies for you to extend beyond the basic game
     Issues that the voters care about
     Weekly events
     Theatrics representing the scandals and ridiculous sideshows that dominate the news

Experience the Upside Down in Risk: Stranger Things, collaboratively designed with the Duffer Brothers and using never-before-seen artwork from Kyle Lambert. Play as either good or evil forces, both sharing a common objective: stopping Vecna from reaching the Creel House. The game features custom-designed territories, iconic locations from the series, and includes bespoke tokens including Vecna himself.

Starfire is a game of battles of between fleets of space warships. Each player commands a fleet of starships, moves their starships, and fires their weapons.  Starfire also has campaign rules that allow players to create their own races with which to explore, expand, research, and conquer.

Admiral’s Challenge is a simplified (15-20 page) campaign rule set for people who don’t want to use the full campaign rules for Third Edition. It removes the bulk of the campaign related paperwork and also moves to a grid based campaign map to allow for more open movement (and fewer choke points) than the standard rules so that the focus of the campaign can be on combat rather than management.

Starfire is a game of battles of between fleets of space warships. Each player commands a fleet of starships, moves their starships, and fires their weapons.  Starfire also has campaign rules that allow players to create their own races with which to explore, expand, research, and conquer.

Admiral’s Challenge is a simplified (15-20 page) campaign rule set for people who don’t want to use the full campaign rules for Fourth Edition (Galactic Starfire). It removes the bulk of the campaign related paperwork and also moves to a grid based campaign map to allow for more open movement (and fewer choke points) than the standard rules so that the focus of the campaign can be on combat rather than management.

In Monopoly: Sonic the Hedgehog, released to coincide with the 2024 Sonic the Hedgehog 3 movie, players will race to purchase labs and high-tech facilities, while snatching up fan-favorites, such as Dr. Eggman, Knuckles, and Sonic himself. In addition to featuring these characters and the all-new mysterious Shadow, this game features six exclusive tokens inspired by Sonic's universe, including Sonic's shoe, Tail's tail, and one of the infamous gold rings.

Strawman Struggle is a fast-playing 2-player card game inspired by Zheng Shangyou and Sean Ross's Strawman Variations.

Players compete to shed out all of their cards first to win the game. Card play follows standard climbing rules found in many traditional climbing games. One player leads with a chosen combo, then the other player must either play a higher version of the same combination or pass.

Unlike other climbing games, in this game part of your hand is on the table, with face-up cards that your opponent can see covering face-down cards that are only revealed as the cards above them are played.

—description from the designer
Monopoly: Hello Kitty celebrates the fiftieth anniversary of the Hello Kitty brand and lets you play the traditional Monopoly game with tokens such as Hello Kitty's bow and her iconic face.

boardgame: Earth: Bighorn Sheep/Rocky Mountain Elk Promo Card
description:
Offered to all attendees of WSBG Vegas 2024

Bighorn Sheep (Ovis canadensis)
2+ bush, 2+ mushroom, 3+ wet, 3+ cold
(island/climate)

Rocky Mountain Elk (Cervus canadensis)
2+ tree, 2+ herb, 3+ sunny, 3+ rocky
(island/climate)

Heredity: Memories is an expansion for The Book of Swan campaign that features a two-chapter prequel set ten years before the events of the main story featuring the same characters: Maeve, Djamal, Selena, and Swan. Memories features slightly shorter scenarios than the base game.

Meet the 19 Legends of the Arkandes, a fictional world where horses are wobblier than ever!

Play your cards strategically to create synergies or sabotage your (soon to be) former friends and gain the most points in this short and competitive card game!

Each of the 19 cards does something different. Combine their effect and discover new combos every game.

—description from the designer

In Magic Filler the kingdom of Quivira is increasingly overshadowed by dark forces. Are you that adventurous mage who can turn the tide? Go quickly but circumspectly in search of 7 mythical magic potions. Summon them with the right spells to collect them. Strengthen their effects by multiplying them in the best magic cauldrons. Whoever can unleash the most powerful magic will become Quivira’s saviour.

Each turn you collect as many potions of the same kind & background that are orthogonal or diagional adjacent. If they have a bright background, you cast light magic which enhances their strength. If it's dimmed, you will fight evil with a dark spell to lower its power. In this way you change the position of the relics relative to the kettles and thus your reward at the end of the game. But be aware. Those who used more dark spells will score the dimmed side of the cauldrons!

Magic Filler contains a 'Party mode' for families and a 'Power mode' for seasoned players.

—description from the designer

Bomb Alley is a card-driven game (CDG) for two players that depicts the violent fighting that took place on May 21, 1982 in San Carlos Bay, Falklands Islands, where British ground troops landed. Throughout the game, players experience the constant roar of jet engines and the roar of explosions. The game includes a deck of 65 cards and 72 markers both single or double-sided and requires at least two 6-sided dice (D6).

Cards encompass Mission (Raid cards), Terrains, Objectives (ships, ground objectives), Aircrafts and Events. Markers include Hit/Critical hits markers, ammunition, Incident chits. Included are also Player Aid's cards summerizing the important informations from the 16 pages rule booklet.

After agreeing to a mission (one of the 7 different Raids), the players start "building" an approach to objectives using Terrain and Objective cards. Once the approach have been prepare, the attack per se starts. As the Argentinian tries to drop and armed bombs on Objectives, the British player tries to shot the aircraft down. Both players can interfere with the actions of their opponent by use of Event cards and Incident chits. At the end of a Raid, Victory Points (VPs) are given to player according fullfillment of mission goals indicated on the Raid card.

The game can be one single Raid, or a Campaign of 3 successive Raids. The player with the highest VP win a Raid, the player winning 2 Raids out of 3 wins the campaign.

Game duration varies according to players preference, from 15 minutes for a single Raid to over an hour for a three Raids campaign game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Game of Life: Hello Kitty and Friends
description: In The Game of Life: Hello Kitty and Friends, players will journey through life's adventures, making decisions and experiencing milestones within supercute world of Hello Kitty, while picking up colorful hairbows along the way.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring – Trick-Taking Game: Gifts of Galadriel
description:
Galadriel Grants Gifts to those who Support Hobby Retail
When you pick up your copy of The Fellowship of the Ring: Trick-Taking Game from your favorite local game store (affiliated to the "Hobby Next" program), you’ll also receive an exclusive additional chapter (chapter 16.5)! "Gifts of Galadriel" is added content only and is not at all needed to enjoy the game, but for those die-hard fans of The Lord of the Rings or completionist collectors… we grant you this side quest to visit a hobby retailer near you.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: The Lord of the Rings – Trick-Taking Game

description:
Slappy, the mischievous ventriloquist dummy, has unearthed a cursed secret, unleashing spine-chilling monsters from Horrorland upon the unsuspecting town.

Your mission in Clue: Goosebumps: thwart Slappy's sinister plan by uncovering which monster is aiding him, where they are concealing him, and the treacherous traps they've laid. Will you save the town from the clutches of these nightmarish creatures, or succumb to their terror?

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

boardgame: バクチはアクマでツいてから (Gambling King)
description:
"Bakuchi wa Akuma de Tsuite kara" (or "Gambling King" is a confirmed English title by the author himself) is a simple trick-taking game where you bet with power of demon. The objective of this game is to become the king of Bakurinpic which is held every 10 years. But in order to win in this game, you need to use demon powers, but since everyone will use it, the one who uses it the best will probably win.

Short summary:
The game is played throughout seasons (matches) and weeks (rounds). Players are dealt casino cards, they draw a demon card from demon deck and play each week to win prize. You can use demon powers to earn more cards, but the more you use demon cards the greater the punishment will be. With demon powers you can steal other players' prize cards or whole discard pile, you decide. At the end of each week, the winner gets green token and starts next week. It is continued till all seasons are ended and final points are calculated.
publishers: やすものゲームズ (Yasumono Games)

description:
The Deluxe edition comes in a special box and contains the base game along with the Escalation Expansion (which allows for solo play and expands the game to 5 players) and Exotic Matter Promo Kit.


Behold, Andromeda's Edge: A dazzling, uncharted region of space on the edge of the Andromeda Galaxy. Littered with the modular debris of the precursor civilization, patrolled by malicious extragalactic raiders, and bordered by dense nebulae, The Edge is a last resort for the brave and foolhardy who seek a new life beyond the oppressive reach of the Lords of Unity.

In this game, you lead a desperate faction seeking to build a new civilization on Andromeda's Edge. You begin with only a space station, a few ships, and a handful of resources. By carefully placing your ships, you will gather resources, claim moons, acquire modules to add to your station, populate planets and build developments on them. You will battle opponents and compete with others to ascend the progress tracks: Science, Industry, Commerce, Civilization and Supremacy.

On your turn, you either launch a starship or return your ships to your station. Launching sends one of your starships to a region of Andromeda, either collecting resources from planetary systems or taking actions at Alliance Bases. If the region is occupied by your opponents or fearsome raiders, face off in a dice battle, with Supremacy on the line but where strategic manipulation can turn a loss into a reward. Returning to your station allows you to activate your engine, using the modules you’ve acquired to generate energy, gain resources and carry out actions.

Throughout the game you will build up your unique faction, building developments (Observatories, Factories, Spaceports, Cities and Obelisks) and gaining station modules which move you up the progress tracks. Advancement on the tracks is rewarded both during mid-game events and at the conclusion, and is the key to victory.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Blood Recall is a battle deck building card game in which players fight to the death while shedding blood.

Based on the Cthulhu mythos and the idea of blood inheritance, players become a "Blood Recaller". Fight using the schools of your ancestors to prevent the end of the world.

Blood Recall is a deck building games where you purchase various cards placed in the market and strengthen your deck. The biggest feature of this game is that the closer you get to death, the stronger you become. The blood you receive and shed becomes a resource, and when your stamina is halved, you awaken and become even stronger.

The year is 1840, the Victorian era and the beginning of the industrial revolution. You’ll need to beat out your other Chess Rivals by either becoming the most famous chess prodigy, develop chess projects or become the ultimate chess master to win!

You’ll start the game by drafting your chess deck, which will be what you will play in the Chess Game to try to beat your opponent and raise your ELO score. You’ll want to balance your deck between opening, middlegame and endgame cards to ensure that you have what you need to crush your competition.

Each player will match up against their opponent to battle it out in the chess game. In each of the 5 rounds, you’ll only be able to play opening, middle or end game cards, depending on the round. When you play a card, it will move the token toward your side of the EVAL board a certain number of spaces, based on the strength of the card. The cards can also increase other factors that will affect the game, such as Checkmate, Queen Side Synergy, Center Control and more. These will give you bonuses or special powers during the game.
At the end of this tug of war, you’ll look at your EVAL score to see if you’ve managed to exceed the required number to win the game. If not, the game ends in a draw.

After each player faces off against each other in the Chess Game, you’ll move onto the Map Phase, where you will be moving around the map picking up bonuses, like adding cards to your deck, earning money, or developing projects. Each space that you move costs you $1. At the end of four rounds, you’ll need to move back to the starting space so that you can make it back to the Chess Game! Make sure you keep enough money in reserve to move yourself back to the starting space, because if you don’t, you’ll be late to the game and will not be able to play in the first round!

Play continues until someone wins by either achieving the highest fame, becoming the ultimate chess champion by raising their ELO score to the necessary level, or by completing the required amount of projects!

—description from the publisher

The poodles are romping around in the park, a perfect chance for the cheeky fleas to hop on and use them as a handy means of transport. Of course, the clever poodles know this and try to avoid giving them any opportunity to ride along, easier said than done! Who in the end will be fortunate enough to have collected the fewest fleas?

During Poodle Taxi, you place cards (containing a color and an arrow pointing left OR right) in a row in the middle of the table. On your turn, choose one of three cards from your hand and place it face up in the row, according to these rules:

• If there is NO card of the same color in the row, you place the card to the left or right in the row. 
• If there is another card of the same color in the row, you must place the card on top of it. 
• In both cases: If the arrow on the card points towards other cards in the row, you must take these as negative points (fleas).
 The game ends as soon as the draw pile is used up. Whoever has the fewest fleas wins!

In the game of Schools of Thought players take on the role of envoys from various vassal states, cultivating scholars of different schools within the Academy of Learning. These scholars travel between states to persuade and lobby, or utilize their knowledge to enhance their reputation. As time passes, the scholars continue to grow, evolving from apprentices to masters of their school. The player who can assess the situation wisely, cultivate scholars most suited to the development of the times, and achieve the highest reputation, will emerge victorious in the game.

—description from the publisher

在游戏《百家》中，玩家作为诸侯国的使者，在学宫当中培养不同流派的游士。他们在不同国家间进行游说，或者发挥自己的学识提高声望。随着时代流逝，游士会不断成长，从学徒走向一代宗师。谁能审时度势，培养出最贴合时代发展的游士，获得最高声望，就可以获得游戏的胜利。

—description from the publisher (Chinese)

Volatile Vintage is an engine builder that will have you building up vineyards in competition with others to produce the most wine barrels, all the while fighting off mob attacks, investing in your workforce, and signing accords with local villages. Print off a vineyard page for each player, shuffle a standard deck of playing cards, and grab a writing utensil, then you're ready to go! You will go through quick rounds of drawing two cards from the deck to reveal your options for developing your engine or try to withstand a mob attack and defend your vineyard. Make deals with nearby villages to unlock special abilities, and carefully choose your timing to invest in each aspect of your vineyard to maximize your production and your score. The cards have a unique arc for the game in store for you each time, so be ready to adjust your tactics and try different strategies in this quick-playing and highly replayable print-and-play game!

—description from the designer

Once upon a time, in the dawn of time, when the Earth was full of magic, four main elements appeared: fire, earth, water, and nature. Dragons were the first creatures, and humans came later. People, eager to prove their superiority, started a war with the magical world and emerged victorious. Dragons remained only in legends and fairy tales, or so everyone thought. But one day, a huge fire dragon emerged from the mouth of a volcano. You become one of four brave heroes who will fight dragons and save your land from the fire-breathing monsters!

Драконоборцы ("Dragon-Slayers") is a competitive roll-and-move game where each player has to upgrade their character, tame creatures of their element, and fight goblins and creatures of other types. Finally, they must be the first to defeat the dragon. Along the way, heroes encounter traps, treasures, creatures that can be defeated or tamed, and characters can be upgraded. Each of the four heroes has its own characteristics and strengths, requiring different tactics. Dragon cards also add variety. Until the last moment, players do not know what abilities the main villain of the game will have.

At the beginning of the game, each dragon-slayer receives a board with his character's equipment and resources, as well as development of skills and number of lives.

The player's turn consists of four phases:

     Roll dice and find what fate has in store for you this turn. Fortune, luck, or perhaps you will fall prey to a terrible curse.
     Perform actions on objects and strengthen your character. Magic potions, sharp blades and magical amulets can help you with that. The stronger your hero, the greater your chances of winning the battle.
     Continue your journey by moving your hero. There are many possibilities ahead of you: fight a monster or tame it, buy useful items at a shop, search for a chest or visit a witch.
     Execute the effect of the cell in which your character is located.


The hero who slays the dragon first is declared the greatest dragon-slayer and winner of the game!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Limpopo is a simple dexterity-based game for kids. During the game, players collect different snack cards and then try to eat them later.

Each turn players spin the circular board and use their crocodile to catch the type of snack they want to eat. Additionally, they can try to take additional snacks from other players. But beware! Healthy teeth are very important, so try to avoid hard logs.

The game ends when there are no more snacks in the river or at least one crocodile will lose all his teeth. The winner will be the crocodile with the fullest belly.

—description from the publisher

"Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to Who's Next?
From now on, you'll be playing the role of a famous musician who's about to give a wonderful concert with his band.
I'll be your conductor this evening. You'll have to follow the movements of my baton to make sure you don't get lost Whoot Whoot.
I'm offering you a multi-level apprenticeship so that all players learn the rules gradually."



- A fast-paced game where you have to keep track of who will be the next player.
- 12 hilarious illustrations of famous musicians such as Lady Gaga, Elvis Presley or Daft Punk playing classical instruments.
- Super easy to understand but hard to master under pressure.
- Maintain your concentration to avoid blue notes through 7 difficulty levels



HOW TO PLAY ?
The first player indicates the direction of the turn by pointing to his neighbour on the left or right. The players must start a countdown as detailed below on the Level 1 card (there are different levels of difficulty from Level 1 to Level 7).

The designated person then plays a Musician card from their hand. The card played will designate the next player whose turn it will be to play a card as well.

For example, if a triple Whoot is played, it is the third player a round the table that must play the next card.

Each time a player places a Musician card, they must read it aloud and restart the oral countdown (14,13,12...). Each player plays his cards in front of him in a staircase, so that all previously played cards a re visible. And so on, until:


     One of the players plays their last card: In this case, the round went off without a false note. Everyone takes their cards and recomposes a hand of 8 cards. Then take the Level 2 card if you wish, or redo a round of the current level.



     One of the players makes a mistake: If a player plays when it is not their turn or does not play when it is their turn, the other players can report him. The offending player rotates his False Note card to the next number. Everyone then takes their cards from the start of the round and the player who made the mistake starts a new round of play.

The first king of the 4th Dynasty, Sneferu (c. 2613–2589 BC), chose Meidum as the site of his royal tomb. What we see today bears little resemblance to the original monument, which was a step pyramid that was then transformed into a true pyramid with smooth sides.

Meidum is Abstract Strategy, has a 4×4 board and 32 pieces. The object is to place the fourth piece in a row or pyramid line in which all four pieces have at least one same or different color. The twist is depending on your opponent's laying, choosing by following your opponent color (any number) or opponent number (any color).

—description from the designer

The Christmas crime game from the series "Tatort Meer"

The bird island is snowed in. Snow falls quietly on the harbor and the sailing ship “Jan van Gent”. Santa Claus trudges across the snow-covered beach, Christmas music playing softly in the distance. Suddenly a loud shot rings out!


     Investigate different clues
     Check alibis and motives
     Analyze sound recordings and videos
     Research online
     Contact involved people by phone


You receive a multitude of documents with further information about different websites and other resources that are relevant to solving the case. You play either alone or together as a team and have to check alibis and deduce logically. Use all available accessories - even cellphones are allowed.

boardgame: みんなでトリックビーノ (Minna de Torikku Bīno)
description:
Minna de Trick Bīno (Trick Beans For Everyone) is a paper-and-pencil trick-taking game that can be played by any number of people.

Each player fills in a suit and number in the 16 spaces on their sheet. For each randomly drawn lead card, players mark the combination they filled in and write either a circle (o) or a cross (x).

If you create a bingo vertically, horizontally, or diagonally, you score points. Additionally, you can score more points by predicting common goals or the number of wins.

Wish Upon a Star is a game that combines trick-taking and deck-building mechanics.

Players compete based on the size of the numbers they play, with the winner earning happiness points equal to the number they played. The loser earns stars equal to the number they played, gaining the right to purchase. The first player to reach 50 points wins!

—description from the publisher

Vamos Dia is a light trick-taking game set in a demonic banquet (drinking party) where you try to avoid tricks making others drink without getting completely drunk yourself.

The cards 5, 15, and 25 are VAMOS cards. When someone plays a VAMOS card, they must shout, "Vamos! Vamos!" Then, everyone else who hears it also shouts, "Vamos! Vamos!" to get the party going. Celebrate by giving a congratulatory drink (a penalty card) to the person who wins the trick and win if you're the sober player at the end (with fewer penalty cards)

Noble Escape challenges you to save the denizens of the chateau fleeing the rebels by hiding them in carriages about to flee the estate; carefully use the limited abilities you have and balance their weights, as the heaviest carriage will be pulled over by the rebels and any nobles inside will be taken away for "questioning." You are a steward charged with getting all the denizens of a French chateau out and away from the mob into four carriages, the heaviest of which will get caught. Conveniently, the residents of the estate come out to the courtyard four at a time, forcing you to assign one to each carriage in each group of four (represented by four cards from a standard playing card deck).  Use a few special abilities for placing cards of the right suit in the matching carriage or varying number abilities that change every game to manipulate the weights and protect as many nobles as possible!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Todos contra a Dengue Roll and Write !
description:
In some location in a city, there are several breeding grounds for the Aedes aegypti mosquito, which causes Dengue Type 1, Type 2, Type 3, Type 4, Chikungunya and Zika, which are causing several health problems to the local population. It is up to the players to eliminate these breeding grounds for the mosquito that transmits Dengue. In their search to find the breeding grounds for the mosquito, they will be able to observe several trees in the area. Some of these locations will give bonuses to the players, which can help them find the breeding grounds for the mosquito. The player who contributes the most to eliminating the breeding grounds for the mosquito, as well as observing the largest number of trees, will be declared the winner. To achieve the objective, they must create paths on the map and thus eliminate the breeding grounds for the dengue fever. We decided to include the theme “Dengue” in the game, because here in Brazil, due to poor sanitary conditions, especially in the outskirts of large cities, there are many problems related to diseases caused by the Aedes aegypti mosquito, mainly related to health, since health centers, emergency care units and hospitals end up overwhelmed with patients who need adequate treatment.
One of the main ways to combat the mosquito that transmits the disease is to eliminate its breeding grounds, which must involve the entire population and public administrations.
The game symbolically brings the topic to the table and by playing, especially with children/students, the subject can be discussed at home or in classrooms, creating greater awareness of the problems caused by the Aedes aegypti mosquito and its consequences for society as a whole.
Fighting breeding grounds for the mosquito that transmits the disease is everyone’s duty!

—description from the designer

A collector's edition of Railroad Tiles that supports up to 5 players! A single "big box" including all components from the core game, the World Expansion, and all other expansions available as add-ons, as well as additional components to allow for 5-player gameplay for both core game and expansions.

Contains base game + 9 expansions:

     Canal Expansion (Medium Complexity)
     Countryside Expansion (Hard Complexity)
     Desert Expansion (Medium Complexity)
     Energy Expansion (Hard Complexity)
     Forest Expansion (Easy Complexity)
     Lake Expansion (Easy Complexity)
     Monuments Expansion (Medium Complexity)
     World Expansion
     5th Player Expansion (Collector’s Edition Exclusive)
     Hospital & Local Market Promo Tiles (if the game was bought through the 2024 Kickstarter campaign)


—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games
Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

description: Each kid takes a turn turning over a Center Deck card, if they have a match in their hand, they discard the card in their hand and picks up another card from their individual Player card pile.  Be the first to get rid of all your Player cards to win.

Your goal is to build Lakes and place Ferries in them to score points. Lakes also have another purpose: you can send a route into the lake side of a tile to create a Harbor (a bit like Canal End tokens work in the Canals Expansion), and all routes that are connected to the same Lake via Harbors are also connected to each other... basically, Lakes act as big Stations!

Another special feature of the Lake Expansion also lies in the way Ferries are placed. At the end of each round, when you look at the active Placement tokens and place pawns accordingly, you must also look at each one of your Lakes: if you have more Harbors than Ferries in a certain Lake, you may place 1 Ferry pawn for free on any empty Ferry pin point until you have an equal number of Ferries and Harbors in that Lake.

You score 1 point +1 point for each Ferry in the same Lake, up to a maximum of 3 Prestige points (they have a lower point limit than other pawns, which can be worth up to 5 points each, but you also place them for free on top of those coming from the 2 Placement tokens, instead of in place of 1 of them).

Like all expansions, the Lake Expansion comes with a few Objectives as well: for example, the Fishermen's Island must be reachable by at least 3 Traveler pawns, while the Hydroelectric Plant must be part of a closed Lake with no open sides.

—description from the publisher

Forest is one of the Expansion marked with "Easy" complexity: a good candidate to be among the first ones you try, or to play with inexperienced people.

Your goal is to build Forests all around your network, while keeping them separate from each other (a bit like Cities work in the core game; at the end of the game, you make 3 points for each group of at least 3 Forest tiles that are orthogonally adjacent).

Additionally, some Forest tiles also have pinpoints where you can place Lookout Tower pawns (which, like gondolas in the Canals Expansion, can be placed using any Placement token as a wild card, instead of placing the corresponding standard pawn). Each Lookout Tower is worth 1 point +1 for each orthogonal direction in which there is at least 1 Forest tile.

Like all expansions, the Forest Expansion comes with a few Objectives as well: for example, the Witch's House does not want to be in the same row or column as a Lookout Tower, while the Camping Ground must have at least 4 Forest tiles around it.

—description from the publisher

The World Expansion, the one all backers of this campaign are receiving for free with their pledge, is a special kind of expansion: it is the only one that can be mixed both with the core game and any other expansion (whereas you can only play with 1 thematic expansion at a time). It includes 2 kinds of components: additional Objective tiles and Special pawns.

New Objectives
The new Objectives included in the World Expansion work just like those included with the core game and are simply added to the roster of Objective you can choose from when selecting your 3 Objectives at the start of the game, which you must then place when a Placement token with a flag is revealed on the Station board and adds a new scoring condition for the end of the game. For example, the Observatory wants to be placed in a column or row that contains at least 6 tiles.

Special Pawns
Each Special pawn is associated to one of the main types (Cars, Trains, or Travelers; the Bus pawn is a special Car pawn, for example) and comes with specific rules that affect where you can place them and/or how many points they are worth when you place them. For example, the Mechanic is a special Traveler pawn that can be placed on a Train pinpoint, in addition to a regular Traveler pinpoint; additionally, if you do so, you gain 1 additional point.

To place a Special pawn, you need to wait for the Placement token of the corresponding type to come up, just like a regular pawn of the same kind, but with an additional limitation: you can place a Special pawn only if you have at least 2 pawns of the same kind already placed on your network (they don't need to be connected to each other or the Special pawn that you want to place, even though that is usually better for you in terms of score).

—description from the publisher

Each thematic expansion comes with its own bag of tiles, in addition to the other components (like pawns and objectives, as well as other special components that are specific to each expansion, like the Canal End tokens, more on those later).

When revealing Route tiles at the beginning of each round, players must follow a pre-determined mix of tiles from the Core Box bag and the Expansion bag. Each column will still contain as many tiles as the number of square icons at the top of that column, just like in a regular game with the core rules, so that the total amount of tiles available to players during a game will stay the same (which keeps the duration of the game similar to the core gameplay).

Even if the total number of tiles available during the game will be the same, it will be a mix of core and expansion tiles, though, which significantly alters the flow of the game and the availability of specific tiles during the game and creates new, interesting challenges. In the Canals Expansion, unsurprisingly, you will have to deal with the construction of Venice-inspired canals, bridges, and gondolas.

Your goal is to build Canals that are as long as possible (each Canal scores 1 Prestige point for each Canal tile it contains after the second tile, so a Canal with 5 tiles would be worth 3 points) and close as many of its open ends as possible (each is worth -2 points at the end of the game; to close a Canal, you just need to connect it to the Empty side of a tile, which allows you to place a Canal End token on top of it).

Additionally, some Canal tiles also have routes with open gaps over the canal itself where Bridges can be built when both ends of the "open" route are connected to other tiles. Bridges are very important to make points with gondolas (you want those romantic moments during your sightseeing tours), as each gondola is worth 1 point +1 for each bridge in the same canal.

—description from the publisher

Your goal is to create a power grid by building Wind Turbines and using Energy Cables to connect them to Town tiles and bring them energy. Wind Turbines have their own pin points, and can be placed by using any Placement token as a wild card, instead of placing the corresponding standard pawn, like gondolas in the Canals Expansion and Lookout Towers in the Forest Expansion.

You will need to use Energy Cables to connect the Wind Turbines you have built to the Town tiles you placed. Energy Cables can be thought of as an additional kind of "route"... but they have a peculiarity: they don't run across the middle of the sides of tiles, like railways and highways, but rather across their corners.

This adds a whole new layer to your strategies, because while managing the network of cables can complicate things for you, bringing one to the right corner can allow you to connect three tiles to the power grid in one go!

Wind Turbines don't score any point when you build them, but at the end of the game, each of your Town tiles scores 1 point for each 2 Wind Turbines it is connected to. Placing Town tiles smartly and sending Energy Cables to the right corners in order to maximize the Town tiles powered by the least amount of cables is the key to score a lot of points.

Additionally, the Energy Expansion comes with a few Objectives as well: for example, the Solar Farm want to be connected to Energy Cables on all 4 corners, while the Casino must have at least 2 electrified Town tiles around it.

—description from the publisher

Your goal is to create big Pastures with lots of Animals of the same type. In contrast to all other expansions, the Animal pawns you get are tied to a specific column of tiles you get from the central board during the draft phase at the beginning of each round.

Countryside tiles may come with two new features: barns and fences. Animal pawns must be placed not on routes, like cars, trains, and travelers, but on the barns in the fields that surround them. Fields connected to each other are called “Pastures” and are separated from each other by fences, but also by highways, railways, and towns.

Some Countryside tiles also feature blue buildings similar to Town tiles (in addition to a Barn); these are Village tiles: they count as Town tiles when building Cities, but unlike Town tiles they do not interrupt Pastures.

When you place an Animal pawn, you immediately score 1 point + 1 point for each other Animal pawn of the same type in the same Pasture, up to a maximum of 5 Prestige points.

Just like all other expansions, the Countryside Expansion comes with Objective tiles: for example, the Vineyard wants to be part of an area of at least 5 connected Field tiles with no Animal pawns, while the Hills must have at least 3 Animals around them.

—description from the publisher

Your goal is to create a Desert surrounding your regular tiles, placing Desert Attractions of the different types (Oasis, Pyramid, Sphynx, and Obelisk), connecting them to your regular routes through Desert Stations, and placing Camels to bring people seeing the attractions and gain a few extra points as well.

There's a special rule to follow when placing tiles in this expansion: When you place the first Desert tile of the game, it can be placed freely. All following tiles (either regular or Desert) must be placed so that at least 1 side is connected to a tile of the same kind.

Desert tiles feature regular Car and Traveler pin points, but they lack Train pin points. In their place, they feature Camel pin points. You can use a Train Placement token to place a Camel on such a pin point, instead of placing a Train pawn. Desert tiles also feature Desert Trails: these are special routes that can be connected to both Highways and Railways: when you do, you place a Desert Station on the joining point token to mark the connection. Desert Stations are important for the scoring of both Desert Attractions and Camels.

When you place a Camel, you score 1 point for each Desert Station it connects to. Additionally, at the end of the game you gain points for your sets of different Desert Attractions that are connected to at least 1 Desert Station: 1 point for a singl Attraction, 3 points for 2 different Attractions, 6 points for 3, and 10 points for full sets of 4 different Attractions (the same Attraction can't be part of different sets, of course, but the Attractions of a set don't need to be connected to the same Desert Station nor to each other).

Unsurprisingly, the Desert Expansion also comes with a few Objectives: for example, the Desert Market must be part of a route with at least 5 connected Desert Trail segments, while the Great Oasis must be surrounded by at least 4 Desert tiles... but it also counts as an Oasis Attraction!

—description from the publisher

This expansion is pretty unique in that each player has an additional pawn, the Archaeologist, that can be moved around the landscape you are creating by using the routes you have placed by spending Action points. Your goal is to place sets of different kinds of Monuments (Arch, Temple, Tower) when you find archaeological sites.

You have 3 "Action points" to spend each round, and you can gain additional ones by foregoing the use of a Placement tile in the Active zone that round. You can spend 1 Action Point to either move the Archaeologist to an adjacent tile connected by Highway or, if the tile where the Archaeologist is contains a Station, more them to another tile containing a Station connected to the starting one by Railway. When the Archaeologist is on a tile containing an empty Monument pin point, you can also spend 1 Action Point to place a Monument pawn of the indicated type on that pin point.

Monument pawns don't score you points when you place them, but at the end of the game, you gain 1 point for each Monument pawn you have + 3 points for each complete set of 3 different Monument pawns (Arch, Temple, Tower) on your tiles.

Additionally, the Monuments Expansion comes with a few Objectives as well: for example, the Museum wants the Archaeologist to be on or around it at the end of the game, while the Castle must have at least 2 Towers around it.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dead by Daylight: The Board Game – Gatekeeper Expansion
description:
Enhance your Dead by Daylight: The Board Game experience with this expansion. Play as one of the new Survivors and utilize their unique perks to escape from a new cabal of terrifying Killers with horrifying abilities you've never seen before! Explore a sinister realm and customize your experience with all new set-up options. Features 10 hauntingly detailed miniature figurines.

Each turn, players will plan their moves by secretly selecting paths on the board to move along. Each time a player moves, they will reveal new features of the board, called props, which they can interact with to further their objectives or to hinder their opponents.

Survivors are able to utilize many different props to aid in their survival and navigation of the trial. Killers can make use of fewer props—instead, they interact with the Survivors using their attack or their unique power. Each Killer has a different power that will completely change the way the game plays. Both Killers and Survivors have perks which grant them special abilities. Experienced players can customize their characters with various perk configurations, offering even more replayability.

Contains a selection of 5 new Killers and Survivors. Test your skill as The Clown, The Huntress, The Trickster, The Legion, or The Plague, each with new and cunning ways to corner the Survivors. Or you can choose from one of five new Survivors, Kate, David, Yun-Jin, Jeff, or Jane, each of whom have their own unique skills for thwarting the Killers and ensuring their survival.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dead by Daylight: The Board Game – Unbroken Expansion
description:
Enhance your Dead by Daylight: The Board Game experience with this expansion.  Play as one of the new Survivors and utilize their unique perks to escape from a new cabal of terrifying Killers with horrifying abilities you've never seen before.

The Unbroken Expansion introduces an additional 5 Killers and 5 Survivors:
Five new Killers: The Spirit, The Blight, The Twins, The Deathslinger, and The Oni
Five new Survivors: Adam, Felix, Élodie, Zarina, and Yui
New Perks for these characters.

Each turn, players will plan their moves by secretly selecting paths on the board to move along. Each time a player moves, they will reveal new features of the board, called props, which they can interact with to further their objectives or to hinder their opponents.

Survivors are able to utilize many different props to aid in their survival and navigation of the trial. Killers can make use of fewer props—instead, they interact with the Survivors using their attack or their unique power. Each Killer has a different power that will completely change the way the game plays.  Both Killers and Survivors have perks which grant them special abilities. Experienced players can customize their characters with various perk configurations, offering even more replayability.

—description from the publisher

At first glance, the world seems peaceful. But beware! It is not as peaceful as you would think.
War? No, it is not something human. It is neither environmental destruction nor human conflict that will cast mankind into the utter depths of horror in the future.
It is something most people are still unaware of – the abominable creatures called “monsters”. They come from man-made pollutants, from other dimensions, and even from outer space.
Hey, look behind you! Didn’t you see something black moving just now? Yes, it’s a sign that their shadows are creeping up on you. There’s only one way to save yourself. Play this game and discover their weaknesses!

This card game depicts the exploits of characters who seek out and seal away abominable monsters that lurk in the dark night, thus saving the world.
Players defeat monsters, and the player who has defeated the most of them by the end of the game will be the winner.
However, if the number of escaping monsters reaches a majority, all players lose.

boardgame: Palec Boży: Kinko and Ginko Promo
description:
Kinko and Ginko can use Prophets and Temples of any other god currently not in the game.
At the start of each generation, place 3 1VP tokens on this card. Whenever your prophet lands on an island, you may place one of your VP tokens on his disk. If a token falls off your prophet at the end of the Quest Phase, take it back to your Victory Points pool.

This promo was created to celebrate Polish Championship in Flick of Faith 2024.

In the early 1990s, despite the government's enactment of a law prohibiting the transfer of "things overflowing in the world" from one person to another, the elite and eccentric gathered in luxurious villas along the coast, seeking curiosity and thrill, to engage in a certain game. The winner learns to know when to pull back, and the loser learns to appreciate what is enough. This is the game called "Not for Me."

Players challenge themselves to quickly get rid of the "things" (cards) overflowing in their hands. There are only two ways to get rid of things: "Play" or "For You." In "Play," players place cards on the table according to the rules. In "For You," players can give away unwanted things to someone else. However, be careful—whether or not the recipient accepts the things depends entirely on them.

Game is sold at Tokyo Game Market 2024 autumn, limited by 120.

—description from the designer

In Entrenched: Gallia Guards, you take on the role of a French commander during the fierce trench warfare of World War I. As the leader of your forces, it's a 1 vs 1 battle where strategy and foresight are key. Before the fight begins, you must carefully select your units, each with unique strengths and abilities, to engage in the coming combat.

This deckbuilding game challenges you to use your cards as essential resources for giving orders, deploying reinforcements, and launching attacks. Your hand will determine how effectively you can move your units and dominate the battlefield. In addition to managing your troops, you can also call upon the special powers of your officers to gain a tactical edge when you need it most.

Victory is achieved by outsmarting your opponent and capturing their trenches to accumulate 9 victory points. With the right combination of units, orders, and clever timing, you can turn the tide of war and lead your forces to triumph in the unforgiving world of trench warfare.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Gems of Iridescia: Starlight of Iridescia
description: Starlight of Iridescia is a mini expansion which introduces one new gem, four relic cards, one item card, and five new hexagonal tiles. The expansion takes advantage of the reverse night time side of the board.

Arschlochmensh ("Asshole Man") is a casual card game for 3-6 players. Your goal is to nurture and (mis-)educate your own character as badly as possible, while helping other players Characters to become good man.

Every player tries to bring his human up as badly as possible by laying transparent upbringing cards with negative values on his human. Only the visible numbers count in the end. Other players can interfere with defense cards or by laying positive upbringing cards.

A painting themed version of the famous game Mistakos (Chairs), for 2 players. In this version of the game, the players can choose from the two colors (purple or yellow) and face each other in this  fun version of Mistakos.
Both player has 8 ladder of their color (2 of these are single ladders) and they have to get rid of all of their ladders before the other player does. If you knock down ladders from the structure during your turn, you must pick up all the knocked down ladders and your turn ends. The player who manage to get rid of all their ladders, receives a victory point. Whoever collects 3 victory points first wins the game.
The game is twisted with 4 playing field with purple and yellow paint puddles. The players have to place their ladders in these playing fields so, that their ladders can only touch their own color of puddles.

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Robinson Crusoe Investigator
description:
Robinson Crusoe investigator was created to celebrate the 25th anniversary of Portal Games.

Special trait : Shortcuts 
Once per turn instead of one action you can gain one damage  and move on any safe space (Enemies follow you).

     instead: move but enemies don't follow
     instead: move to any space and do not take damage
     instead: Move to any space and heal 1 point of stress


Common traits : Brawling  and  Stealth 

No mini included only board.

Robinson Crusoe  was added to orders  in the Portal Games store during the 25th anniversary celebration. On September 6-8, 2024.

A crazy arcade game in which you get rid of your chairs and build a common structure.

A special electronic platform plays music and spins, making the task more difficult. That's not all! As soon as the alarm goes off, the platform will start spinning and dropping chairs.

Use your agility and reflexes in this tense, crazy game and try to add all your chairs to the structure before everyone else does.

-description from the publisher's website

The race is on... but is it better to answer fast or last?

Get ready for a fast-paced, exhilarating quiz game where quick thinking could serve you well… or be your undoing!

In The Fast and The Curious, players race to answer quiz questions first and score big points, but beware… with these twisty questions not everything is as it seems, and sometimes it’s wiser to wait!

With 300+ questions, full of surprising facts and the occasional twist, The Fast and the Curious will keep you on the edge of your seat.

Will you score big as the fastest thinker – or will patience be your secret weapon? Remember: the first answer could also be the worst!

—description from the publisher

In Clues in Twos everybody has 90 seconds to write up to 10 two-word clues to describe 10 things. Swap your clues with another player and guess what they are describing. You both win points for correct guesses.

Much like the ideal clue, the game is short, sweet and perfectly formed! It takes minutes to learn and will have you laughing at how you and your friends see the world differently!

Posh crab!? Our inspiration for making this game came when our friend Elliot perfectly described a lobster as a posh crab. He set an epic benchmark for the perfect clue and showed us that less is always more!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Boribon és Annipanni: A nagy szamóca-vadászat
description:
A hungarian children's game with Veronika Marék's lovely, familiar characters, from a hungarian tale.

"Boribon and Annipanni are looking for strawberries. Help them get to the strawberry bushes! However, the path meanders back and forth across the meadow, I wonder which of them will get to the delicious strawberry sooner?
This game develops logical skills, you don't need to count, you don't need a dice. It's fast, fun, and it has a short playing time: it just matches the concentration ability of preschoolers." - translated description from the back of the box

Content:
- 5 strawberry cards
- 58 playing cards
- table
- game description

Gameplay:
Lay out the game board among the players and place the five strawberry cards on the middle of the meadow. Distributing the 56 route cards equally, the players place them face down next to the picture of Bourbon and Anni Panni.
The player in turn draws one card from their side and, starting from their character, places it on the first field (which is the leftmost field on the first row, following and connecting the little yellow path). However, the winding roads have to be laid down skillfully. If at some point the road runs off the board or turns back on itself, you have to take down your cards and start over your path. 
After this action, the other player in turn, who does the same.
As the roads are built, players get closer and closer to the delicious strawberries. The player who reaches it first, gets to take one of the five strawberrie tiles in the middle of the board. You can place this tile next to you and then remove all the road tiles from the board. Now the players have to make their way again from their remaining cards to collect their next strawberry.
Whoever collect three pieces of the delicious fruit, wins the game.

The Kippkopp stacking board game is a card game designed so that even very young children can play a matching memory game, or a picture supplement game, and the older ones can also find their joy in it, as an exciting card game in which the missing elements of the game boards must collect.

—description from the publisher (translated)

"Kippkopp looks at the colorful boards in amazement. Does each represent the meadow? but each of them has five strange, missing parts. Help Kippkopp find the missing pictures and put them in their place!

During an exciting card game, you have to collect the five cards that you can use to complete your game board. You can pull and throw, but be careful, use good tactics! A twisty board game with the popular character, Kippkopp.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Gameplay:
Each player gets a board and draw 6 cards. The game is started by the youngest, who draws an action card from the deck. If you have a card in your hand that can be placed on your board to complete a missing part of the picture, you can do so. After that you have to play a card face up from your hand. (The next player can pick up this card instead of drawing from the deck.) If a player plays an action card, the instructions on it must be followed.

There are six types of action cards in the game: draw two cards, draw three cards, reverse the playing order and miss a round, everyone give their hand to the person sitting to their right, or collect everyone's cards and shuffle them, then deal the cards again.

The player, who managed to place all the missing parts on their board first, wins.

Another playable variant of the game is the memory game, where there are 30 pairs of cards in the game in addition to the action cards.

boardgame: Circle the Wagons: Lone Cowboy (for Second Shot)
description:
Put your skills to the test against a wily opponent in the solo expansion for Circle the Wagons.

Lone Cowboy is a series of solo expansions for Circle the Wagons and Second Shot. Each set introduces new scenario cards. These tell the player which scoring conditions to use in the game as well as adding specific rules to determine the behavior of the non-human opponent. The solo game is played over two rounds but otherwise follows standard gameplay.

To setup a game using any of the Lone Cowboy sets, choose 1 scenario card and place it in the center. Find the two scoring conditions listed on the scenario and add them to the center.

Deal out 8 cards in a circle. The player drafts and places cards on their turn as normal, with any skipped cards going to the opponent’s pile. The opponent follows specific drafting criteria to determine which card they take. Skipped cards go to the player as normal.

Once all 8 cards have been taken, deal the remaining 8 cards in a circle and repeat the process. After all 16 cards are gone, the opponent scores based on the scenario card as well as for the three most common territories in their pile. The player scores their town as normal. If the player’s score is higher, they win!

boardgame: Circle the Wagons: Lone Cowboy Collection
description:
Wallet compilation of the three solo expansions for Circle the Wagons and Circle the Wagons: Second Shot:


     Circle the Wagons: Lone Cowboy
     Circle the Wagons: Lone Cowboy Rides Again
     Circle the Wagons: Lone Cowboy (for Second Shot)



Lone Cowboy is a series of solo expansions for Circle the Wagons and Second Shot. Each set introduces new scenario cards. These tell the player which scoring conditions to use in the game as well as adding specific rules to determine the behavior of the non-human opponent. The solo game is played over two rounds but otherwise follows standard gameplay.

boardgame: The Bad Karmas and the Curse of Cthulhu
description:
The Bad Karmas and The Curse of Cthulhu is the new installment in The Bad Karmas series. Once again the fate of humanity is at stake but this time the fight is against Cthulhu and many other Great Old Ones. In this new chapter our beloved heroes find themselves in the 1920s, a world where obscure cults and cosmic horrors are spreading, and ancient, otherworldly powers are awakening.

The Bad Karmas and The Curse of Cthulhu is a cooperative tactical boss-battle board game with a digital dimension. Work together to defeat the 12 Zodiacs who evolve and respond in real-time to player actions. The game comes with smart miniatures, board, dice, a free companion app, and the new Teburu game platform, which keeps tracks of everything on the smart board and enhances gameplay with artificial intelligence.

—description from the publisher

On June 25, 1986, a roll of 35mm film, the front page of a newspaper and a dried thistle were discovered in a bathroom at Edinburgh Castle with a desperate note that simply stated, "Please help me! Call the constable, I've been kidnapped."

When the film was developed, there were nine photographs that led constable to different sites throughout Scotland. At each location, they received reports of a Jane Doe who, under the watchful eye of her captor, left behind an encrypted clue and thistle flower. Ultimately, detectives were unable to decode her desperate messages, and the trail ran cold. Now, it's your mission to review the evidence and solve the case.

Get ready for battle! Take control of your tank and lead your crew to victory in "Tank Commander," a tactical dice-activation board game where strategy and wit are your best allies to claim victory and eliminate your opponents.

Tank Commander is an exciting board game that combines strategy and the unpredictability of dice rolls to simulate intense tank battles. Each player takes control of a powerful tank, with the objective of being the last one standing by eliminating rival tanks on a dynamic battlefield. With simple yet deep mechanics, Tank Commander offers fast-paced games full of tension, where tactical skill and luck combine in epic battles.

Tank Commander is a board game for 2 to 4 players who will face off in thrilling battles on a square board divided into grid spaces, simulating a battlefield.

Each player controls a unique tank, represented by an individual command board that details its capabilities and unique characteristics. The core of the game lies in dice-based activation; each turn, players roll a set of dice, and the results determine which features of their tank can be activated, from tactical movements to the use of advanced weaponry and crew coordination.

The different game modes ensure that each match is a new challenge, as it can be played in free-for-all (every player for themselves) or team-based formats.

The objective of the game is simple yet challenging: be the last surviving tank. Players must eliminate their rivals by strategically moving across the board and making the most of the actions allowed by their dice rolls. However, since activations are partially dependent on the luck of the dice, players must also quickly adapt to the unpredictable circumstances that arise during the game.

You'll need to turn luck in your favor by choosing the best strategy to lead you to victory.

The combination of strategy and luck is what makes Tank Commander so exciting. While dice rolls introduce an element of unpredictability, the true key to success lies in how players manage their resources and decisions. Knowing when to advance, when to attack, or when to retreat is crucial to gaining an advantage over opponents.

Tank Commander is designed to be easy to print. Additionally, it doesn’t have too many components, making it ideal for quick setup and play once printed and cut out.

—description from the designer

You face a seemingly unstoppable force known only as "The Miasma", a gigantic rampaging storm that seems to have a mind of its own. The key to stopping this storm lies somewhere on the streets of this city, and it's going to take everything you've got to figure it out.

Assemble a team of skilled vigilantes, each with their unique characteristics, personalized gear, and reason for taking on The Miasma. Take turns exploring the city of Bartle Haven, a post-dystopian version of Kansas City, by making your way through The Miasma revealing one location at a time and finding either gear and allies or monsters and peril. Once everyone has coordinated their movements and takes a moment to catch their breath, The Miasma and the Abominations creep around and destroy a part of the city making your search perimeter smaller and smaller.

It's a race against the clock as you try and get equipped while also searching through the entire city. Find the four generators that are powering the storm before The Miasma claims Bartle Haven and leaves it a desolate wasteland.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Tale of the Disappearing Beauty
description:
Journey back to the gaslit streets of Victorian London in our interactive mystery game. You will be presented with a perplexing crime by Dr. Watson, and assist the legendary Sherlock Holmes in solving the case.

Open sealed evidence envelopes containing meticulous notes, photographs, maps, and more. Gather your friends and family, debate clues, examine evidence, and piece together the puzzle. Can you match Holmes's brilliance and identify the culprit?

Key Features:

     The Most Authentic Sherlock Experience: Immerse yourself in the era with captivating narratives and period-accurate materials.
     Non-Competitive Deduction: Gather up to 8 players and combine your intellects to crack the case.
     Challenging Puzzles & Hidden Clues: Test your powers of observation and deduction.
     Flexible Gameplay: Enjoy an unforgettable 3-4 hour literary adventure you can share! 
     Ages 13+: Perfect for families, friends, and aspiring detectives.


The game is afoot! Gather your allies, sharpen your wits, and prepare for an unforgettable journey into the heart of mystery.

—description from the publisher

kaleiDOS is a ladder-climbing game for 3-5 players using many of the rules from the public domain game Big Two.

You can play singles, pairs, trips, and 5 different 5-card combinations into a trick. When all players pass, the highest combination in a trick wins and leads the next trick.

Hierarchy of suits:  There are 4 suits in the game, triangles, squares, pentagons, and hexagons. Triangles are the weakest suit while hexagons are the strongest (easiest way to tell is the number of lines/sides in the shape). Suits are used to determine highest strength when the numbers match. For example, a pair of 8s whose strongest suit is a pentagon, can be beaten not only by a higher pair but by another pair of 8s containing the hexagon.

Hierarchy of 5-card combos: Straights, flushes, full houses, 4 of a kind plus 1, and straight flushes can be played against any 5-card combo played into a trick. A 5-9 straight can be beaten by a higher straight (ending in a 9 with a stronger suit or a higher number), or a flush, or any of the other aforementioned 5-card combos.

1s and 2s are the strongest: These numbers are stronger than the 15s! 1s can be used to wrap at the end of a straight and 2s are just very strong. But don’t get caught with 2s in your hand as they will multiply your opponents’ scores!

If you like trick-taking and ladder-climbing games, this one is for you!

—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach space 40 wins the game. If a sheep gets on an occupied space, the sheep standing three has to move one space back. Whenever a sheep encounters a wolf or a thief it runs back to the shepherd on space 2.

—user summary

On the outskirts of a town, there stands a mansion, where ghosts and goblins are said to dwell. The mansion, known as the “Horror House”, has already fallen into ruin, and is no longer suitable for people to live in.
Therefore, Taizou, the owner of the mansion, decided to demolish it. However, before doing so, he enters the mansion to retrieve a music-box that holds dear memories of his youth.
When Taizou’s wife, Ume, finds out that her husband wants to demolish it, she enters the mansion to save a handful of valuable items.
Their grandson, Takeshi, is constantly called a weakling by his friends. He enters the mansion to show them that he is not a coward.
Yota, the exorcist, heard rumours of demons dwelling within its walls, and sneaks into the mansion to exterminate them.
Mai, the burglar, assuming that if it is going to be demolished anyway, she may as well look around inside, and thus she enters the mansion.
Rio, the policewoman, having received some complaints from the surrounding residents, enters the mansion to investigate.
Who among them will be able to escape from the “Horror House”?

In this cardgame, people with various agendas enter a haunted house, which is said to be difficult to escape from, and compete to see who shall be the fastest to escape while struggling against the odds.

In the aftermath of Atlantis's fall, ancient machines awaken to rebuild the empire from the floating ruins that remain. In Rex Atlantis, players take control of noble houses, guiding their automata to restore lost grandeur and seize power in a world reclaimed by nature.

Each turn, players move their automaton across eight distinct paths, representing different aspects of Atlantis's reconstruction. The main goal is to gather resources, and rebuild structures, while strategically positioning to gain influence over the empire's rebirth.

Each of the eight paths presents a range of crucial opportunities for players to shape the future of Atlantis. Along these paths, players can reprogram their automata to tackle complex objectives, adjusting their strategy as the game evolves. They can also activate advanced technologies, unlocking powerful abilities that can shift the balance of power in their favor.

Energy crystals, a vital resource, can be collected along the way, providing the fuel needed to carry out key actions. As players progress, they will also reconnect the floating islands, restoring the fractured landscape of Atlantis and opening new avenues for expansion. At critical points, they can contribute to rebuilding grand Atlantean structures, earning influence and cementing their house’s role in the empire’s resurgence.

Throughout the game, houses must balance their need for progress with limited resources. Only those who can outmaneuver their rivals and rebuild the most will secure control of the newborn Atlantean Empire.

The ending phase of the game begins when one player has used up all their markers. At the end of the following round, the house with the most influence (victory points) is declared the winner, cementing their legacy in the rebirth of Atlantis.

Crazy Shapes is a tabletop card game I designed and produced for my preschool son whom I was told suffered autism and ADHD.

It is a card discarding game wherein players drop matching sets of shapes or colors; victor being whomever empties their hand first.

With quick game cycles typically ~5 to 15 minutes max players have a similar level of agency as one has in Poker and incidentally, Crazy Shapes includes a ruleset for bluffing and calling bluffs.

There are no direct offensives, no attacks on one another's cards or actions to stymie other players, thus avoiding grief competition.

The game is a hit with adult crowds in adult environments despite the theme, as it is a lightly engaging activity and when playing with children, adults don't have to let the child win or take it easy meaning children appreciate an unironic victory or loss.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Ring Master: Godlanta no Hasha (リング・マスター： ～ゴドランタの覇者～)
description:
How many days have passed since you left the Ring Palace? Since then, you have been recognised as Ring Master, and have passed through many ordeals.
However, the further you travel, the graver the situation appears to be. Yes, all clues lead to the land of Tonad Raley.
What the heck might be lurking over there? Unfortunately, you are not yet powerful enough to step in at once.
The cooperation of neighbouring countries is of the essence. Once again, you set out on a journey to unite the nations of Godlanta. Just like before, when you travelled from Filian to Maulsonna…

This cardgame is set in the world of the videogame Ring Master.
Players assume the roles of Ring Masters and travel across the continent of Godlanta.
Your quest is to identify the source of the wicked and mighty “thing” that has descended upon Tonad Raley, thwart its agenda, and restore peace to the lands of Godlanta.
However, you are still inexperienced and could not yet stand against such power on your own.
Thus, first you must bring other countries under your control and build up your strength.
Shall you be able to gain enough allies, and accomplish your quest with success?

boardgame: Aeon Trespass: Odyssey – Beached Scylla and Promo Cards
description:
The Deluxe miniature set contains:

1 x Beached Scylla miniature
1 x special 100x60 mm oblong Scenic miniature base
1 x Ember of Guilt Gear card
1 x Baleen Cloak Gear card
1 x Nemesis Harpoon Gear card
1 x Certificate/Art card
1 x Postcard-sized Art card 
1 x craft box

The Beached Scylla is compatible with both Aeon Trespass: Odyssey and Twelve Sins of Herakles.

Everyone has music in their lives. SongFest! is a journey of fun challenge questions through five decades of music. Song QR codes provide hints and bring back memories instantly. It’s a “party-in—a-box” to enjoy with friends and family.
The Ness is More expansion introduces 2 new playable characters, Nessie and Chupacabra.

The Kickstarter Exclusive Edition includes the Base Game inside the box as well as the first Twisted Cryptids Expansion, Ness is More, the Unicorn Frenzy Mini-Expansion, the Field Trip Mini-Expansion, and the Alien Invasion Mini-Expansion. The Base Game includes four playable characters (Bigfoot, Loveland Frog, Mothman, and Jackalope), and the Ness is More expansion includes two playable characters (Nessie and Chupacabra).

Every Kickstarter Exclusive Edition of Twisted Cryptids features a Kickstarter Exclusive box. It also boasts an expanded vac tray that accommodates sleeved cards.

This Expansion introduces 2 new playable characters, Jersey Devil and Lizard Person, and a new mechanic that allows you to lead a Cult in The Wilderness.

-Cryptids-
Like all the other Cryptids in the game, the characters in this Expansion come equipped with their own unique Cryptid Powers. You can activate these abilities once per round by discarding a card from your hand.

-Cults-
The new feature of Cryptid Culture is in the name of the game is CULTS! When you play with this Expansion, you'll choose from one of 6 Cults. As your Cult's Influence grows by recruiting humans, you'll get access to powerful Rituals.

ROLL, MOVE, DRINK. THAT'S IT!
No money, no bankruptcy, no board flipping*.
Just risky dates, revealing truths, and ridiculous  challenges.


    Absence of board-flipping not guaranteed. We won't tell you to, but hey, it's your game.


Choose Your Token
Pick a token that fits your personality. Or if you’re feeling freaky, assign your friends one based on theirs.

Go on the Ultimate Board Crawl
Hydrate your way around the board, collecting Title Deeds like Pregame, Cornhole, and Dive Bar.

Drink or Dare?
Pay rent in sips — or steal the deed by completing an outrageous challenge. (Before playing, choose from 2 levels of spiciness!)

Spill Secrets and Get Awkward
With Chance cards, reveal a hilarious truth, make up a house rule, accept a dare, or play a mini drinking game.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

boardgame: Party Joy Series 84 Saint Seiya Gold Saint Daikessen
description:
Based on the 1986 anime Saint Seiya, compete with the other bronze saints to explore Sanctuary, battle the Gold Saints and discover where the evil pope is hiding.

Secretly choose one of five chips that indicates the Pope's location, and set it aside until the end of the game.

Each player places two game tiles to create the map of Sanctuary to reach the Pope's chambers and search of the gold saints.

Players must battle the gold saints to reveal their chips, which show locations where the Pope is Not. Gather enough information to deduce the Pope's actual location, then defeat him to win the game, but beware if you guess wrong and defeat an illusion is game over.

boardgame: Il Gioco è Tratto: Pocket Parole
description:
Il Gioco è Tratto is a project created to show how fun can still be had with simple “pen and paper” games—not only with timeless classics like Hangman or Tic-Tac-Toe, but also with modern games designed by international game designers, exclusively for Studio Supernova.
