Originally published as a book featuring 32 games across five major categories (strategy, calculation, storytelling, dexterity, and children's games), it is now available in a Pocket edition.

The second release of the Pocket series focuses on 10 Word Games, printed on erasable and reusable boards. These ten challenges test mental, lexical, and social agility, offering a variety of difficulty levels and suitable for all tastes. Play them alone or with friends! With their modern and original designs, these games are perfect to carry in your backpack for endless fun on the go!

Once upon a time, in the mystical land of Mithciros.

The kingdom of Mithciros was ruled by King Ruzar and Queen Nowsida, who were both known for their stern and often oppressive rule. One fateful day, the beloved Princess Netko was kidnapped and taken to a distant, foreboding castle, high atop a mountain. The culprit was none other than the fearsome Dragon Lord Meefca, a creature of immense power and dark magic.

In a desperate attempt to rescue his daughter, King Ruzar called upon the villagers, who had long suffered under his heavy hand. He offered a grand reward and the promise of freedom from his tyranny to anyone who could bring Princess Netko back safely.

Among the ordinary peasants, a young man named Jack was chosen by the royal guards for this perilous task. Jack was just an unknown villager, not a noble knight, but he accepted the mission with a determined heart, knowing the fate of his fellow villagers rested on his shoulders.

Equipped with nothing more than an old, tarnished suit of armor and a holy charm from the village monastery, Jack set out on his journey. The path ahead was fraught with danger, with treacherous dungeons and monstrous foes at every turn. But Jack had faith in the gods and in his own courage.

Let the light guide his way through the darkness...

—description from the designer

This 1 vs all horror survival games pits 1 of you as the killer who has trapped 2-5 victims in the house who are trying to escape.
Utilising hidden movement via a movement dial, you will have to try and out manoeuvre the killer and potential the other victims to achieve your personal goal, find objects in the house to assist you and plan your escape route.
This semi-coop game will have you second guessing everything you do and everything everyone else does.
Who can you really trust?

Are you the killer? Kill everyone! Do not let them escape.
Are you a victim? do you best to escape the house.

Are you playing solo? Play as the victim trying to outwit the killer.

—description from the publisher

Pyrrhus is the general of the expression “Pyrrhic Victory”

Pyrrhus took part in the wars between the Diadochi, the heirs to the empire of Alexander the Great, of whom he was a relative. He then spent the rest of his life fighting in Macedonia, Greece, Italy, Sicily against the Romans, Carthaginians and Spartans. He will finally die in battle and, through his exploits, he will be the model of Hannibal.

This book combines historical study of the epic of Pyrrhus and game. It brings together 20 battle scenarios (encounters, sieges, ambushes, etc.) led by Pyrrhus and his sons, playable individually or in the form of a campaign. It allows you to reconstruct these clashes with different rules (Saga, DBA, ADG, DBM, Hail Caesar...).

—description from the designer

Play the sport of ice hockey in a board game designed for players to out coach their opponents by creating line-ups at the start of the game using a list of skill levels for their individual figures.

Team names and individual player names can be created and used to keep statistics as leagues and competitions are developed between game players. After the game starting face-off is completed, game players move figures around the ice pad, followed by action stages in the form of poke-checking, body checking, passing and shooting.

The game continues with this rotation between offence and defence players being moved and the action stages being done when chosen by a game player.

Fighting, penalties, turnovers and saves by netminders are also actioned throughout the game, bringing all of the elements from the sport of ice hockey to the table top with Ice Hockey with Dice.

—description from the publisher

Craft My Cost is a cooperative game in which players try to deal with incoming flooding from climate change affecting a coastal urban region. With each turn, the menacing flooding will increase the level of danger. Players can use nature-based and engineering solutions to protect the affected populations.

Craft My Cost is part of the Craft My Street project, a research initiative held by University College Dublin to foster children's participation in climate change issues. Minecraft mechanics inspire the game, transposed to an analogue format.

Craft My Coast is a free print-and-play version that requires printing the board and using two D6 blue, red, green, and yellow cubes.

—description from the designer

Play the sport of baseball in a board game designed for players to out coach their opponents by creating batting orders at the start of the game using a list of hitting skill levels for their individual figures.

Team names and individual player names can be created and used to keep statistics as leagues and competitions are developed between game players. After the game begins with the away team game player rolling dice to emulate a pitch, the opposing game player can opt to hit the baseball.

Runs are scored throughout the game and after 9 innings has been completed, the team with the most runs scored are the winning team.

Catches, run-outs, double plays, triple plays and grand slams are also actioned throughout the game, bringing all of the elements from the sport of baseball to the table top with Baseball with Dice.

—description from the publisher

Happy Village is a real-time competitive card game about protecting a village from flooding using nature-based solutions. Players must negotiate and trade cards to play in their village. The first player to trade and play all their cards is the winner.

Happy Village is the first game from the Craft My Street research project at University College Dublin. It aims to create an engaging ice-breaking experience to prepare players for the other games, introducing some fundamental concepts of nature-based solutions. However, it can be played independently.

The game is a free print-and-play game that requires only a set of cards, one deck per player.

—description from the designer

27 AD. As the sun glides across the sky, a breeze slowly pushes your boat away from the bustling town. You dream of becoming the best fisherman on the Sea of Galilee.

Every turn, you'll play a single card from your hand, sometimes utilizing helpful actions. But beware, the tides of fortunes can change with a single card from the Galilee Deck.

The fisherman with the most valuable haul at the end of the game wins!

Little witch Lily wants to keep the forest safe, but she needs your help to do it. Use your magic to give her the items she needs or summon the right allies and complete missions.

Ring Ring is a cooperative game in which each turn you will play cards on the image of Lily. The cards have different objects or characters that modify the general picture, and the objective is to fulfill the requirements of the various missions. Players can't talk to each other, so they can't tell each other what cards they have, but if someone gets the bell card and says "Ring ring!" everyone can speak and change the order of the missions. Are you ready to use your magic to help Lily and keep the forest safe?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the Dungeon!

The dungeon battles have begun, and creatures of the underworld are ready for action. Step into your character and prepare to fight in a manner befitting to who you’ve become. Will you have what it takes to rule the dungeon, or will you be just another corpse on the dusty floor?

Choose Your Champion
From Mimics and Gelatinous Cubes to Wizards and Bards, choose from an assortment of champions, each with their own unique deck of cards and distinct mechanics! Familiarize yourself which each champion’s strengths and become a Force to be reckoned with!

Asymmetrical 18 Card Decks
Each asymmetrical character has a custom 18 card deck. Inside each you will find unique mechanics that thematically match the character and present unique play styles. 100% of each characters custom rules fit on the back of a single card so they are fast to learn. Even better, the ranges of complexity ensure there is a character for players of all ages.

New Strategies Every Game!
Champions will be given 2 artifacts to add to their deck at the beginning of each game to aid them in battle. Combine their powerful effects with your character's cards to create devastating combos that your opponent will never see coming!

Winning Battle Plan!
Each turn, spend your action points to draw and play cards from your deck. Utilize powerful action cards or string together devastating combos, but sometimes the best offense is a good defense. Every card can be deployed as a shield, and knowing when to defend may be the key to surviving. Plan your turns well and you will be crowned victor!

Solo Play
Choose a selection of AI decks with varying difficulty like a Rat Swarm, Murder Hobo or a raging Yeti. Just like the main character decks, each AI deck is packed with unique strategies and mechanics that bring the game to life. Each AI has unique mechanics that require different strategies and challenges.

—description from the designer

6 BLUEY CHARACTER MOVERS: Choose from 6 adorable cardboard movers. Kids can play as Dad, Mum, Bluey, Bingo, Muffin, or Socks

BLUEY-THEMED GAMEBOARD: Your little one can imagine adventuring with Bluey and family through her world, as they race to be the first to reach Bluey’s home to win! The board depicts fan-favorite locations, such as Shadowlands and Rug Island

DANCE, FREEZE, AND GIGGLE: Who will get Dad dancing or make Grandma freeze? When a player draws the Dance Mode card, it’s time for everyone to show off their sweet moves! Play the Magic Xylophone to make someone freeze.

EASY TO LEARN, TEACH, AND PLAY: No reading required! Just like in the original Candy Land board game for kids, players draw cards with colors and sweet treats to travel the colorful road

- description from publisher

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Parhelion Claws Promo Card
description:
The Parhelion Claws are a beta gear promo card that were included with the Sun Claws - Esther miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

The Claws have 2 speed, 7+ accuracy, and 3 strength. They read: If you wounded with this during your act, you may spend 3 survival at the end of your act to place a Mirage in an adjacent unoccupied space. Limit once per round.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – The Challenger Promo Card
description:
The Challenger is a character promo card that was included with the miniature Guinevere. It reads:

When you depart for a showdown against a nemesis monster, gain +1 luck token.

In Carbon Neutrality, players try to balance carbon emissions and activities that absorb carbon.

Carbon Neutrality is a simple game with several modules. The rules change to deliver different experiences, from competitive to cooperative play, and it allows solo play. The game is simple and tends to some abstraction.

Players use decks of cards, exchanging them to play on their territory, adding and overlaying cards. The purpose is to balance carbon emissions to score the most points.

This is a print-and-play game developed during the Craft My Street research project at University College Dublin.

Stabbin' in the Cabin is a hidden-role deduction game in the style of a cabin in the woods style slasher film. You play as a group of American teenagers who have gone to a cabin in the woods for a nice weekend, but unfortunately one of the teens is a secret stabber who just wants to murder everyone with knives.

The game is played over a series of night and day phases where the teens are each placed in an empty room of the cabin, because the first rule in horror films is everyone has to split up, and then stab each other. The secret stabber is trying to kill at least half of the teens and the innocent teens are trying to find and kill the secret stabber first.

Because the teens can only stab people in adjacent rooms the players need to use logic to figure out who could have done all the secret stabbings, and to make matters worse, the secret stabber can activate their booby trap once per game to stab anyone on the board.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Think of a word, then think a little longer! That is what you will be tasked with in Syllabuster!

Players will hear a prompt, think "Name a food that comes in a can". The first player to name an answer that matches the prompt will receive the point, unless another player steals it by answering with a word with more syllables than the first player!

The player with the longest syllabled word will score the point!

Syllabuster is a card game that is self published right in Manitoba, Canada! We hand make each of our games in our own home! From printing to cutting and assembly, we make each of our games with love and remain as ecofriendly as we can!

boardgame: The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: The Last Supper
description:
A supernatural spin on the beloved mystery game genre! Step into the world of the Magnus Protocol podcast and investigate the details of a unique, supernatural incident. Players must work together to read through the provided evidence, listen to the audio files (requires a paired website for the audio), and inspect pictures to piece together the truth of the case. Each case comes with one or two puzzles that must be solved, in order, to unlock all the necessary evidence. With their immersive, atmospheric gameplay, The Magnus Protocol Mystery games are perfect for anyone who loves deduction and horror, whether they've listened to the podcast or not!

In The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: The Last Supper, a famous young chef who specializes in cooking organs is found dead in her apartment, with an organ missing from her own body.

boardgame: The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: Six Feet Under
description:
A supernatural spin on the beloved mystery game genre! Step into the world of the Magnus Protocol podcast and investigate the details of a unique, supernatural incident. Players must work together to read through the provided evidence, listen to the audio files (requires a paired website for the audio), and inspect pictures to piece together the truth of the case. Each case comes with one or two puzzles that must be solved, in order, to unlock all the necessary evidence. With their immersive, atmospheric gameplay, The Magnus Protocol Mystery games are perfect for anyone who loves deduction and horror, whether they've listened to the podcast or not!

In The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: Six Feet Under, a small-town therapist goes to the doctor for chest pain and discovers that her lungs are filled entirely with dirt.

boardgame: The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: The Grinning Corpse
description:
A supernatural spin on the beloved mystery game genre! Step into the world of the Magnus Protocol podcast and investigate the details of a unique, supernatural incident. Players must work together to read through the provided evidence, listen to the audio files (requires a paired website for the audio), and inspect pictures to piece together the truth of the case. Each case comes with one or two puzzles that must be solved, in order, to unlock all the necessary evidence. With their immersive, atmospheric gameplay, The Magnus Protocol Mystery games are perfect for anyone who loves deduction and horror, whether they've listened to the podcast or not!

After a car accident, a man is hospitalized and in a coma — but even through his vegetative state his face is locked in a rictus smile in The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: The Grinning Corpse.

boardgame: The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: The Doppelganger
description:
A supernatural spin on the beloved mystery game genre! Step into the world of the Magnus Protocol podcast and investigate the details of a unique, supernatural incident. Players must work together to read through the provided evidence, listen to the audio files (requires a paired website for the audio), and inspect pictures to piece together the truth of the case. Each case comes with one or two puzzles that must be solved, in order, to unlock all the necessary evidence. With their immersive, atmospheric gameplay, The Magnus Protocol Mystery games are perfect for anyone who loves deduction and horror, whether they've listened to the podcast or not!

In The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: The Doppelganger, a man is terrified to see himself dancing in a club with his ex-husband, while a rash of bad luck tears its way through his friend group.

boardgame: The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: The Woman on Fire
description:
A supernatural spin on the beloved mystery game genre! Step into the world of the Magnus Protocol podcast and investigate the details of a unique, supernatural incident. Players must work together to read through the provided evidence, listen to the audio files (requires a paired website for the audio), and inspect pictures to piece together the truth of the case. Each case comes with one or two puzzles that must be solved, in order, to unlock all the necessary evidence. With their immersive, atmospheric gameplay, The Magnus Protocol Mystery games are perfect for anyone who loves deduction and horror, whether they've listened to the podcast or not!

In The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: The Woman on Fire, a block of flats burns down in the night, after which the residents claim they saw a woman made of fire wandering the hallways.

A supernatural spin on the beloved mystery game genre! Step into the world of the Magnus Protocol podcast and investigate the details of a unique, supernatural incident. Players must work together to read through the provided evidence, listen to the audio files (requires a paired website for the audio), and inspect pictures to piece together the truth of the case. Each case comes with one or two puzzles that must be solved, in order, to unlock all the necessary evidence. With their immersive, atmospheric gameplay, The Magnus Protocol Mystery games are perfect for anyone who loves deduction and horror, whether they've listened to the podcast or not!

In 1940, a boy is trapped alone in the dark with a broken camera. In 2022, with energy prices rising out of control, a strict blackout curfew is imposed upon the residents of an apartment building, and a student goes missing from his flat in The Magnus Protocol Mysteries: Blackout.

In a bold age of neo-humanistic advancement and technological breakthrough, life evolved, and the lines between biology and engineering blurred. The limits of possibility were shattered. A great civilization rose toward the stars, toward an enlightenment increasingly within reach. That civilization collapsed. How and why are long since forgotten. Chaos reigned. Technological understanding was lost to generations of strife and survivalism as time rendered everything (humanity included) into discarded scraps of what once was. In this fallen world, the last wretched vestiges of humanity vie for the only hope for significance that remains: leverage.

Vestige is a highly-strategic, competitive modern euro that you can play in an hour. It is built around a resource alchemy system modeled on the color wheel that offers great depth but can be understood almost instantly; for example, mix might (red) and cunning (yellow) to attain guile (orange). A modular game board; variable end-game conditions; and a card-tableau, engine-building heart makes every game crunchy, and combo-driven. Derived from Vindication, Vestige reimagines its systems in a new setting and with a new depth-to-approachability ratio.

Each turn you move around a randomly generated map of locations, each offering a unique action or effect. Visiting these locations you unlock your potential, spread your influence, develop your attributes, build your network of contacts across three factions (smugglers, mercenaries, lawmakers), and then work with that network to complete jobs your contacts are suited for (unearthing artifacts, claiming bounties, and developing augmentations). Through action selection, card-tableau combo building, area control, and careful resource management you seek to gain more leverage than your opponents, all while the powers-that-be use any and all insidious tricks at their disposal to keep you down where they want you.

-description from publisher

Merrify your Big Dill experience with sixty expansion cards all about your favorite holidays! Describe your love of eggnog, desire to build a Christmas village, and ability to stay on the Nice List. Plus, two festive new Dill Meters add up to two more players to your game. The holidays are kind of a Big Dill!

-description from designer

Get your dork on with sixty expansion cards featuring nerdery like math, Tolkien, and the Prime Directive. Describe your trust in artificial intelligence, love of LARPing, and ability to cleverly name a Wi-Fi network. Plus, two geektastic new Dill Meters add up to two more players to your game. Nerd stuff is kind of a Big Dill!

-description from designer

Make your Big Dill stand tall with these sixty NSFW expansion cards about the things adults do at night. Describe your willingness to send nudes, agility with a sex swing, and desire to drive an unmarked van. Plus, two lewd new Dill Meters add up to two more players to your game. Adult funtime is kind of a Big Dill!

-description from designer

Miniwars is a simple ‘pocket’ game of naval combat in the 18th and 19th centuries, ideal for getting into the world of wargames without having to learn many rules (complicated or not).
With a very simple and easy to assimilate system, in quick games it promises many hours of fun.

—description from the designer

Miniwars es un sencillo juego "de bolsillo", de combates navales en los siglos XVIII y XIX, ideal para introducirse en el mundo de los wargames sin tener que aprender muchas reglas (sean complicadas o no).
Con un sistema muy simple y fácil de asimilar, en partidas rápidas te promete muchas horas de diversión.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Each player in Circle the Wagons is fixin' to build up their own boomtown, but only one'll build the best in the West!

Circle the Wagons is a map-building game. Blaze a trail by draftin' cards 'round the circle and placin' 'em in yer town, tryin' to connect matchin' territories to score prosperity points! But don't forget about them three bonus cards in the center of the circle that can score ya even more points — that is, if ya play yer cards right.

With over 800 unique ways to score and millions of draftin' and placin' combos, you'll never build the same town twice!
family: Contests: Button Shy Game Design Contests and Challenges
Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
High-Rise is a print and play, dice roller microgame for two players about a Runner (thief, assassin or some other photogenic character) attempting to penetrate to the top level of a high-rise building, versus a Gatekeeper who is in charge of the passive and active security measures of the building the Runner is passing through.

The Gatekeeper sets initial Passive security ratings of each level, and places 3 Patrols that can move around in the High-Rise. The Runner uses dice rolls and Special Actions to disable the passive security and get to the top floor of the High-Rise.

The Runner wins if they enter the Safe Room of the top level (the penthouse suite of the target). The Gatekeeper wins if they capture the Runner.

-description from designer

In Slip Runners, players take on the role of a mercenary pilot in an agile spaceship racing against time (and each other) to reach the Slip Point, an invisible gap between spheres. Using Burn and Turn commands, players navigate using Newtonian physics, balancing speed and maneuvering to collect the encryption signal with secret information their employers are desperately trying to have them retrieve. Be the first to collect them and escape through the Slip Point before your competitors can.

—description from the publisher

Arsenal is an ultramodern drop-skirmish miniature game set in a not-so-distant future where distant corporations have to make use of mercenary security forces to protect their assets from each other. Games of Arsenal involve a dynamic board state where drop infantry and massive armored suits cause the battle to unfurl across the entire board. Infantry will wither under heavy fire from an MCV, and then more will drop in behind them, ready to counter-attack.

—description from the publisher

You are the only one who can help a devastated family locate and save their missing child. Bad news: it's an arid, post-apocalyptic world full of zombies, raiders, and beasts. Good news: you are one of the Trackers, legendary rangers from the past, able to withstand all the dangers awaiting you in the wasteland.

This is a one-page solo print and play roll and write game. The main goal is locating a missing child and saving her from captivity. There are 30 areas on the map and to find the exact location you must conduct investigation as you move across them. The problem is that most of the areas are too dangerous and you must first clear them from zombies, dogs and raiders.

You will need ammo, water and medkits to fight. You will loot the locations you cleared, you will pick dropped items from defeated enemies, complete side quests and trade in survivors' settlements.

The game is focused on combat and survival and involves both luck and strategy.

—description from the designer

In Oikos, you'll lead the population of a Brazilian native species. In this highly interactive game, each species has its own goals do reach victory, interacting with the other species and habitats in many different ways.

The game has five rounds in which each species will play their turn, starting from the species at the base of the food chain. A turn consists, basically, of: adding a new card to the forest (which enlarges the play area), move and collect the resource from the habitat you've landed on, and lastly obtain victory points. At the game's end, each player may score extra points spending pine cones, at a rate of one point per two pine cones. All the other resources (meat, fruit and eggs) may score an extra point for each species that have the most of each resource. After the final scoring, the species that scored the most point wins. In case of a tie, the species with the lowest priority wins (the one at the top of the food chain).

boardgame: Dear Holmes: Sherlockian Mysteries By Mail
description:
Solve Sherlock-Holmes-style mysteries using clue letters mailed to your door.

Each mystery includes 3-4 clue letters and one solution letter from Sherlock Holmes.

Mysteries vary, but include murders, robberies, forgeries, espionage, and more.

Clue types include secret codes and ciphers, maps, diagrams, and witness statements.

There are several ways to play. Play by yourself, with friends, or compete in the global Featured Detective Competition.

In this game of global proportions, compete to see who will be crowned the ultimate globetrotter!

Roll the die to see which continent you will explore, answering questions along he way.  You may have to identify a flag, or answer a question about a city, country, capital.  Could be about landmarks, geography, food culture or wildlife.  Collect a passport stamp for each correct answer.

Be the first to get all six stamps to win the game.

Qwixx: X-Change is a scoring block of player sheets that you can use in place of those in the original Qwixx game.

On a turn, after the active player has called out the sum of the two white dice, you can choose to mark out the leftmost white diamond space that includes this number in order to use the other number in that white diamond instead, e.g., instead of a 5, you can mark an 8, or instead of an 11, you mark a 3.

As with the other rows on this scoresheet, once you mark out a white diamond, you can't use any white diamond to the left of this marked-out one.

Scoring in the game takes place as normal.

Avec Infini Regret IV - French Wars of Religion, 1562 - 1598

JARNAC (March 13, 1569): the Protestant rearguard led by Admiral de Coligny is threatened by the entire Catholic army crossing the Charente near Jarnac. The heavily outnumbered Huguenots put up a desperate fight. Threatened with being turned on their right by the reîtres, the Protestants retreated in good order.
Condé, at the head of 300 lances, threw himself into the battle to delay the enemy's advance. The gendarmes only managed to push the Catholics back for a short time.  At the height of the charge, Condé was unhorsed; immobilized, he handed his sword to a Catholic gentleman as a sign of surrender.  Then Montesquiou, captain of the King's guard, intervened, shouting “Tuez...Mordiou...Tuez!” and fired a pistol at point-blank range, killing the Prince instantly. 
In control of the battlefield, the Royal Army was unable to capitalize on this victory.

ARQUES (September 21, 1589): Henri IV is waiting near Dieppe for reinforcements and money from England. The Ligueurs, led by the Duke of Mayenne, wish to prevent this landing and attack the royal troops entrenched at the junction of two valleys. The Ligueurs' attack, led by cavalry on the front and lansquenets on the left wing, broke through the Royalists' solid defenses. The latter counter-attacked and drove the enemy back to their original positions.
With no way of breaking through the defenses, the Ligueurs withdrew, leaving the victory to the King. The King expressed his joy to his comrade-in-arms who was absent that day: “Brave Crillon, pendés-vous de n'avoir été icy près de moy ...”.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
You are a corsair commanding a vessel navigating the oceans. The die tells you where to move around the island and when to land on it. The die can also activate or deactivate the Letter of Marque allowing you to double the coins you can win. You can attack the other players and fight for more coins.

The game continues until all the Treasure Island coins have been distributed to the players. The winner is whoever possesses the most coins at the end of the game.

There are no limits on the number of players or playing time, both can be increased or decreased as desired, as long as additional playsets and coins are available.

Expansions
The game sets are distinguished by the cardinal points, (N, E, S, W) and (NE, SE, SW, NW). Each set can be played by 2 to 7 players. By combining 2 game sets, the number of players can be increased to 14 vessels.

You are a corsair commanding a vessel navigating the oceans. The die tells you where to move around the island and when to land on it. The die can also activate or deactivate the Letter of Marque allowing you to double the coins you can win. You can attack the other players and fight for more coins.

The game continues until all the Treasure Island coins have been distributed to the players. The winner is whoever possesses the most coins at the end of the game.

There are no limits on the number of players or playing time, both can be increased or decreased as desired, as long as additional playsets and coins are available.

Expansions
The game sets are distinguished by the cardinal points, (N, E, S, W) and (NE, SE, SW, NW). Each set can be played by 2 to 7 players. By combining 2 game sets, the number of players can be increased to 14 vessels.

Vampires has lost their war against humans and now has to live and feed in the shadows under the rules of the humans in the streets of the ancient city of Drachenburg.
Are you going to be the one who returns the power to the Blood Dynasty?

Players in their hand can have numeric cards or special cards. Numeric cards on their hand represents hunger and in the streets pile represents preys. The players on their turns has two options, play a special card with various effects (gain life, attack other players in various ways, etc) or they can choose to satisfy their hunger looking for preys across the streets of the city. The streets pile is made of special and numerical cards.

Play your powers wisely and search for preys in the right moment in order to be the first without cards or the last one alive and win the game.

—description from the publisher

As a team of gnomes, you went to the mine to search for precious diamonds. But beware! You have to watch out for rival gangs, flying bats, explosions and lots of theft in the terrifying darkness.

Gameplay:
The players' task is to collect as many diamonds as possible before Snow White invites the dwarves to dinner.
Divide the cards into three decks according to the markings on the back (I, II, III), then shuffle them and place them on the designated space on the game board. Depending on the number of players: for 2 players, take out 10 cards, for 3 players, take out 5 cards marked with I. For  4 players, you don't have to remove a single card from the game. Deal 5 cards from the deck marked with I to each player. Everyone look at these cards (making sure no one else sees them) and try to remember the number of diamonds on the cards, then put these cards face down in front of them.
On the player's turn, the following steps must be taken: 1. Draw and discard cards. 2. Perform the step. The player on their turn draws from the cards on the first level and decides whether to keep it. If so, they replace one of their existing cards, if not, they place it on the discard pile designated on the field. The player have to perform the action on the card placed on the discard pile. 
When the deck on the first level of the mine runs out of cards, players descend further down the mine. To do this, they have to send a minecart to the surface, with some of the collected diamonds in it. They choose one of their face-down cards, which they turn face up and keep like that until the end of the game. The diamonds on the revealed card are collected and secured, cannot be taken away from the player. 
In the 2nd and 3rd levels, the game takes place in the way we have learned so far. The only difference is that special cards will also appear, which add an extra twist to the game. One of these special cards has Show White on it. This card marks the end of the game. Each player reveals and counts all of the diamonds they have collected. The player with the most collected diamonds wins the game.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Snow White

description:
The Skull together with the Skull Trophy Card is a micro expansion for the LEGO® board game Monkey Palace. It works with any other micro expansion.

Place the Skull on the Skull Trophy Card next to the other Trophy Cards in the game. On your turn, you may take the Skull and place it on an open end knob of an arch in the staircase that you just built, if you believe that none of the other players can build on top of the Skull. Take the Skull Trophy Card worth 3 Banana Points b and place it next to your Player Board. BUT... if one of the other players succeeds in building on top of the skull, they can take the Skull Trophy Card and will get the 3 Banana Points b. Hereafter the Skull Trophy Card cannot change hands.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Operation Konrad: Budapest 1945 – The Bitter End
description:
Budapest 1945: The Bitter End

This is a solitaire wargame that recreates the German Operation Konrad, which began on January 1, 1945. The player controls the German army and aims to rescue Budapest, which is besieged by the Soviet army.

Soviet infantry units are printed directly onto the map while their armored elements can move and counterattack if activated.

boardgame: CTHULHU: RPG Audio Box – Le Pacte d'Innsmouth
description:
Cette boite contient une aventure pour 1 à 5 joueurs. À l’intérieur, des dés, des fiches de personnages, une carte et un livret. Sortez votre téléphone portable, téléchargez l’application Elder Craft et flashez le QR code du livret.

Slimane-Baptiste Berhoun (Le Visiteur du futur, J’ai jamais su dire non, Les Opérateurs, La Théorie des balls, Le Golden Show, etc) sera votre meneur de jeu et vous plongera dans l’univers étrange de la ville d’Innsmouth. Le reste ne dépend que de vous...

Hommage créatif et respectueux rendu au maître de l’indicible par son traducteur contemporain Maxime Le Dain, Le Pacte d’ Innsmouth est une œuvre que tout fan de Lovecraft se doit d’expérimenter, et cette RPG Audio BOX est certainement la manière la plus immersive et originale de le faire.

In 1943, the Italians surrendered and switched to the Allied side.  The Eritrea was a small warship that had been operating with the Japanese Navy ever since the fall of Italian East Africa.

This is a two player postcard wargame, with the Italian player attempting to evade the Japanese forces, while the Japanese player attempts to out-guess and destroy or capture the Eritrea before it can surrender to the British at Sri Lanka.

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The Evacuation of Zhangjiakou

This is a solitaire wargame on the Battle of Maruichi Camp, in Manchuria, from August 19 to 21, 1945. This battle took place after the surrender of Imperial Japan.

To allow Japanese nationals in Zhangjiakou to escape safely, the Independent Mixed Second Brigade refused to comply with the demands of Soviet and Outer Mongolian armies to disarm, and instead defended Maruichi Camp which was on the road leading to Zhangjiakou.

The player controls the Japanese army. The Soviet/Outer Mongolian army (hereafter referred to as the Soviet army) is controlled by the game system.

This is a two-player wargame covering the Battle of Mukden. This was the final battle of the Russo-Japanese War, and took place from February 21 to March 10, 1905. One player commands the Japanese army, and the other takes the Russian army.

The Japanese must advance and push the Russians out of their fortifications and out of Mukden city to win.  Units have hidden combat values.

This is a two-player wargame that recreates the Shimada Tank Corps' night attack on January 6, 1942, in Burma.

One player takes the Japanese army and the other takes the British army.

The players take turns to move their pieces. The goal ist to capture the enemy flag. The center space is an obstacle that blocks all movement. The players have four different playing pieces:
1 flag (moves and captures like a king in Chess)
2 adjutants (moves and captures like a queen)
2 cannons (moves and captures like a rock)
10 soldiers (moves and captures like a pawn)

—user summary

boardgame: Advanced Fighting Fantasy: Dark Dungeons – The Boardgame
description:
Advanced Fighting Fantasy - Dark Dungeons: The Boardgame  is a dungeon delving boardgame based on the Fighting Fantasy gamebooks and the Advanced Fighting Fantasy RPG.

It uses random tiles to generate the dungeon, and Heroes have a limited time to either defeat the Boss or accumulate treasure, depending on whether the game is played Solo, Cooperatively or Competitively.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fantasy Commander: Underdark, Union of Spider Faction Set
description:
Dive into the shadows with the Underdark, Union of Spider, a new faction for Fantasy Commander! For centuries, the denizens of the underworld paid little attention to the world above, waging their own battles for control over dark caves and labyrinthine tunnels. However, the increasing incursions of surface armies seeking hidden passageways have compelled them to unite.

Under the formidable leadership of the Dark Elf Queen, the inhabitants of the Underdark have rallied together to confront these surface dwellers and exploit their weaknesses. Now with a multitude of new foes, the stage is set for epic confrontations.

This expansion introduces intricate new units and powerful abilities, enhancing the strategic depth of your gameplay. Forge alliances in the depths and master the art of subterranean warfare as you lead the Underdark against the encroaching light!

boardgame: Fantasy Commander: The Great Wolf Horde
description:
Unleash the fury of the Horde of the Great Wolf, a formidable new faction in the epic world of Fantasy Commander! Comprising ruthless orcs and cunning goblins, this expansion invites players to command a relentless army that sweeps across the battlefield, pillaging and plundering everything in their path. 
The Horde unites orcs and goblins from all over the world. Barbarians from Blackmarsh fight shoulder to shoulder with samurai from the Redrock clan.

This expansion introduces a wealth of new units, each crafted with the same high-quality, detailed resin miniatures that fans have come to expect. Players will find themselves navigating the chaos of battle as they harness the brutal strength and cunning tactics of their new forces.

Whether you choose to dominate your foes in strategic duels or expand your campaigns, the Horde of the Great Wolf brings fresh challenges and opportunities for glory. Forge your path and let the whispers of dark spirits guide you to victory!

Because a severe earthquake has devastated our home, we seek shelter in the nearby caves. There we face darkness, wrong paths and strange noises.

The caves force you to rethink your approaches to solving the other modules. In doing so, they increase the level of difficulty a little.

—description from the publisher

Argad ! is a mix of role-playing game and miniature skirmish game. The first edition was published in 1996.

The rules are devised for 28mm miniatures and work better when a game master (GM) or organiser imagines a scenario and story. They may also be used for 1/72 or 20mm figures.

Our "friendly simultaneous" (no IGOUGO, no initiative) system may seem strange and unfamiliar at first, but is simple and encourages very fast moves and daring actions.

Quick reference sheets include movement, shooting, and melee tables, which can be used easily to play adventures, skirmishes and fights.

Each player is represented by a character: adventurer, warband chief, military officer... accompanied by a small bunch of followers or small troop of warriors... or pirates... or soldiers... (typically 10-30 strong) or by only a handful of companions.

Players are encouraged to imagine that everyone running on the gaming table is really alive (not game pawns) and do not have to face boardgame-minded tactical choices that their characters could not understand. The most efficient tactic, as in true life, is to outnumber the enemy where it's really important; once in contact the dice do the rest ...but the best games happen when players try to delay or to avoid battle as long as possible. The system is so fast-flowing that 5-6 players can do 30-40 game turns and often more in a few hours, this allows many moves, terrain exploration, diplomacy...

The rules may also be used for scenarios where players should collaborate instead of fighting each other (as in more traditional RPGs) in which case their opponents are non-player characters (NPCs) handled by the GM / referee. Such games are often easier to prepare and to run, especially with new players or at a convention.

—description from the publisher

Pax Porfiriana – Latin for "The Porfirian Peace" – refers to the 33-year reign of dictator Porfirio Díaz, who ruled Mexico with an iron hand until toppled by the 1910 Revolution.

As a rich businessman (Hacendado) in the turbulent pre-revolutionary borderlands of the U.S. and Mexico, players compete to build business empires of ranches, mines, rails, troops, and banks while subverting opponents with bandidos, Native Americans, and lawsuits. Each turn goes as follows:

1. Action Phase: Perform three actions, such as play new cards, get new cards from the market, speculate on cards in the market, buy land, or redeploy troops.
2. Discard Headlines: Remove any Headlines (i.e. cards with the Bull-Bear icon) that have reached the leftmost position in the Market.
3. Restore Market: Restore the Market to twelve cards.
4. Income Phase: Collect one gold per Income, Extortion, and Connection Cube in play. If Depression, pay one gold for each card in play (includes Partners and Enterprises in your Row, and all of your Troops).

Four "scoring" cards (Toppling) are in the game and their effect depends on the current form of government. The government can change if troops are played and as a result of other cards. The form of government also influences different production values of the game, such as how much mines produce. Players win by toppling Díaz, either by coup, succession, revolution, or annexation of Mexico by the U.S. If Díaz remains firmly seated at the end of the game, then the player with the most gold wins.

The Ultimate edition features a brand-new card Era II set that extends the gameplay into the aftermath of the original events.

In Frankezen, players must compete against each other and against time to obtain parts from the black market, keeping them fresh until they can assemble and bring a creature to life before the superstorm arrives! Whoever manages to do this first will be the grand winner and will present to the world a completely docile and peaceful creature, a true Frankezen!

The game features mechanics such as ‘take that’, market, negotiation, hand management, events, and a race against time.

A fun and dynamic game with an unusual theme!

Irmã Nuna brings a comedic horror atmosphere in a fictional colonial Brazil where players take on the roles of aristocrats who are secretly vampires!

The nun, who gives the game its name, discovers this fact and, after being excommunicated by the Bishop, one of the vampires, gains superpowers to fight against the threat and defend the villagers.

Each round, village cards must be placed around a rondel on which a dice tower/platform, representing Irmã Nuna, will be positioned.

Players must invade the villages, placing their meeples (minions) in the villages according to their strategy. After the invasion phase, all meeples will, in a clockwise direction and alternately, execute the actions of the villages. These actions will interfere with the dice value (moving the Nun) and with the positioning of the meeples among the villages.

At the end of each round, all meeples in the village where Sister Nuna is pointing will be exorcised and will not gain the vitality reward provided by the village.

The surviving meeples will collect their vitality and a new round will begin.

At the end of the rounds, the Aristocrat whose minions have gained the most vitality will be the winner!

The game uses mechanics of dice rolling, worker placement, take that, asymmetric players, and progressive turn order.

Compete with other players in this field-to-table game of vineyard growth, in order to establish your winery.  Buy estates, build chateaus and reposition your harvest team in order to most efficiently deliver your grapes to the chateau for vinting.

Each turn  starts with the roll of our two varietal dice (one white and one red) which randomize the grape growing culture in the game, meaning you need to hope for mother nature to bless your vines to see your crops flourish.  In Vineyard Run, as in wine making, you can situate yourself perfectly and do everything right to prepare yourself for success but if your grapes don't grow...

Start the game in multiple vineyards across Ontario growing any of Ontario's 11 signature varietals.  The more different varietals you can grow the higher your chance of earning the cards you neeed to help you get into your wine production phase and therefore win the game.

Once the harvest process has begun you can use your truck to transport grapes from vineyard to your chateau where your vintner turns them into wine.  Whoever reaches the festival to deliver 100 bottles (or more) of their wine first wins the game.  It always helps to have a few extra bottles in case one of 3 tariff cards (20 bottle/coin penalty) is played against you.  All of this leads to a climactic and thrilling race to the finish!

-description from designer

Team up with some edgerunners hungry for game, eager to carve their names into the legends of Night city - and hoping for a fat payout from the grateful rich.

The Presidential Election is just months away. You’ve won your party’s primaries and you are their best bet to become President of the United States of America!

One player will be the DEM Presidential candidate, the other will be standing for the REP party. Over the seven months you will go campaigning in different battleground states. Our castle is under siege by an army of demons. You must save as many peasants as possible before the demons demolish the walls and finish them off. 
Activate the towers to attack from the corners of the board. 
Use the precision of the best archers. Plan strategies to make profit later on. Use the evacuation plans to evacuate peasants and fight extra actions. And whatever happens, don't let them burn your castle.

This is a flip & write game in which every turn players draw 3 cards that will be connected to our player sheet. 
When connected these cards will indicate: which side of the castle we add new groups of attacking demons, which groups of demons appear, which group of demons attack us this turn and with which soldiers we can defend ourselves.

The game ends after 12 turns. Players will have several ways to accumulate points while resisting the assault. The player with the most points is the winner.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Portwory w Tokio: Specjalna Mapa (Katowice / Gliwice)
description:
New promo  board created on 25 years anniversary of Portal Games

Use the new board instead of the standard one (regardless of which version of Tokyo Monsters you play).
For all game effects, KATOWICE replaces the City Center and GLIWICE replaces the Bay. When a Player leaves KATOWICE or GLIWICE, instead of placing their Monster pawn in front of them, they place it on any of the Suburb spaces on that board.

When a Player resolves the results of dice rolls and their Monster is in a Suburb, that Player also resolves the symbol from that Suburb when using the symbols on the dice. Monsters in the Suburb can only attack Monsters in Katowice and Gliwice, and are only attacked by them.

The board was added to orders in the Portal Games store during the 25th anniversary celebration. On September 6-8, 2024.

Everyone is gathered around the table to taste the King cake. It is time to distribute the cake slices. Among the cards representing 16 slices of cake, only one slice contains the lucky charm. During the game, with action cards, players will discreetly observe the cards, take cards in front of them, steal the opponents’ card and eat them. If a player manages to eat the slice containing the lucky charm, he immediately wins the game. To win, you will have to carefully observe the cards, memorize them and bluff your opponents.

Tout le monde est réuni autour de la table pour déguster la galette des Rois. Il est temps de distribuer les parts. Parmi les cartes représentant les parts, une seule contient la fève. Durant la partie, grâce à des cartes action, les joueurs et joueuses pourront discrètement observer les cartes, les récupérer dans leurs assiettes devant eux, les voler aux autres et les manger. Si un joueur ou une joueuse arrive à manger la part contenant la fève, il ou elle remporte la partie immédiatement. Pour gagner, vous devrez observer les cartes, les mémoriser et bluffer vos adversaires.

boardgame: Munchkin Panic: Friendship Potion Promo Card
description:
This card was created to promote Munchkin Panic.

Official Rules
Once per game, draw 1 Monster token. If it is a Monster, add it to your Trophy pile without adding Treasure to your hand. If it is a Monster Effect, discard it without suffering the effects.

The party game “Kopfkino à la carte” is about the wildest claims into the mouths of your fellow players.

These can be funny, appropriate or extremely charming. Always with a wink, the “anger factor” is not neglected either. Whoever who has written down the best statement wins.

Sentences like “When I see myself in the mirror at home after a night out, I think ...” and ”The craziest thing I've ever done was ... because ...” must be completed in a witty way for a player who has been drawn.

This person then presents the sentence as if she/he had experienced it herself/himself. If the sentence contains a “because ...”, a conclusive reason for this must be added ad hoc.

Laughter guaranteed!

Gameplay:
1) Read a Card
2) complete the sentence for an other Player
3) give the answer to this player
4) every player presents the answer as if he/she had experienced it herself/himself
5) The player, who has read the Card judges on who wrote the best Statement. She/he receives the the card (or gets a Point on scoreboard (sheet of paper)).

The game ends when every player has read a card. The person with the most cards after everyone has read a card, wins.

-description from publisher

This mini expansion adds 12 zodiac cards. During setup, each player can exchange one of their constellation cards with a zodiac card of the same value. Zodiac cards add longer lines connecting stars and each have two stars to score points instead of one.

Legendary characters are born with a story to tell. Included character cards foretell a journey on a grand scale. Use only one legendary character card per campaign. This character's story unfolds throughout their lifetime.

The mounting evidence of close scrapes and inexplicable escapes invites sideward glances wherever she stops long enough to be known. In her hands, anything can be the ultimate tool for survival.

—description from the publisher

"Gratte le Ciel" is a captivating card game where players take on the role of ambitious architects, collaborating to build skyscrapers while competing to claim the most prestigious structures. Each player contributes to the construction of shared skyscrapers by adding classic or special floors to shape their strategy. The game blends competition and cooperation, as although skyscrapers are built collectively, each player aims to complete the construction by placing a roof and claim the most impressive buildings to earn prestige points.

The core of the game play lies in card management and anticipating the actions of other players. Players draw cards, play floors, and use special cards such as Insurance or Earthquake cards to protect themselves or sabotage their opponents. They can steal cards, secure buildings with Insurance, or even trigger Tornadoes to swap hands between players. Balancing construction, protection, and attack is crucial for success, especially with constraints like the requirement to include Toilets in the skyscrapers or the rule that demands each new skyscraper to surpass the height of the previous one.

The ultimate goal of "Gratte le Ciel" is to earn the most prestige points by completing skyscrapers and winning specific Awards, such as building the tallest structure or constructing a homogeneous building made of only one type of floor. Every decision matters, as mistakes can lead to the collapse of skyscrapers, the loss of valuable rewards, or even gaining penalties like the "Fake or Smelly" if a building fails to meet certain requirements. In the end, the player who accumulates the most prestige points is crowned the best architect in the world.

•••

"Gratte le Ciel" est un jeu de cartes captivant où les joueurs incarnent des architectes ambitieux, collaborant pour construire des gratte-ciel tout en rivalisant pour s’approprier les bâtiments les plus prestigieux. Chaque joueur contribue à la construction de gratte-ciel communs en ajoutant des étages classiques ou spéciaux pour façonner leur stratégie. Le jeu mêle compétitivité et coopération, car bien que les gratte-ciel soient construits ensemble, chaque joueur tente de terminer la construction en posant un toit et de s'approprier les édifices les plus impressionnants pour gagner des points de prestige.
Le cœur de la mécanique repose sur la gestion des cartes et l’anticipation des actions des autres joueurs. Les joueurs piochent des cartes, jouent des étages, et utilisent des cartes spéciales comme des Assurances ou des Séismes pour se protéger ou saboter leurs adversaires. Il est possible de voler des cartes, de sécuriser des bâtiments avec des Assurances, ou même de déclencher des Tornades qui échangent les mains des joueurs. L'équilibre entre construire, protéger, et attaquer est crucial pour réussir, surtout avec des contraintes comme la nécessité d’inclure des Toilettes dans les gratte-ciel ou la règle qui impose de surpasser ses précédentes constructions en hauteur.
L’objectif ultime de "Gratte le Ciel" est de gagner le plus de points de prestige possible en terminant des gratte-ciel et en remportant des Prix spécifiques, tels que construire le plus haut bâtiment ou ériger une structure homogène composée d’une seule catégorie d’étages. Chaque décision compte, car les erreurs peuvent mener à la destruction de gratte-ciel, la perte de précieuses récompenses, ou encore l’obtention de malus comme le "faux ou puant" si un bâtiment ne respecte pas certaines exigences. Au final, le joueur qui accumule le plus de points de prestige est couronné le meilleur architecte du monde.
