description:
A cooperative card game about making childhoods possible. Sold by the youth sobriety movement for the children of the world charity.

Rounds are broken down, playing until all children are saved or there are no more threats to draw, in a communications phase, where players reveal 1 card, and the play round, where the players play resources from their hand to avert threats.

If all children are saved, the game is won.

Do you think you know everything about your friends and family? Put them to the test with Truth Detector! Select a question from the card and pick someone to interrogate. Watch them sweat as they put their fingers on the detector! With over 500 questions to choose from there will always be a question to catch them with! If they’re lying the machine will let you know, but if it’s not sure and flashes amber you need to ask them the question again. If the machine catches someone lying a siren sounds and the red bulb flashes, you can't miss it! If you tell a lie and get caught by the Truth Detector it means you lose a life. The winner is the last person left with lifecards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tatort Meer Junior: Robben in Gefahr
description:
Open the envelope and read the letter seagull "Manni" wrote. He needs help solving a crime: someone is sabotaging the seal sanctuary, where orphaned seals are fed, until they are ready to return to the sea.


     investigate different clues
     check alibis and motives
     find out who sabotaged the seal sanctuary and why she or he did it


You receive a multitude of documents in an envelope. You play either alone or together as a team and have to check alibis and deduce logically.

---

No internet required. No game material is destroyed.

Expand your re-building opportunities with the Life in Reterra: Moonrise Expansion.

First, this expansion features ten double-sided building cards and 54 building tiles, giving you more options for building powers and combinations of building cards without adding any new rules to Life in Reterra.

Second, the expansion includes thirty moon tiles that are shuffled with the land tiles in the base game. Choose one of the lunar event boards (or draw one at random), and place it next to the building display.

Whenever someone draws a moon tile to refill their hand at the end of the turn, they place it on the lunar event board, then draw another tile. When the lunar event board holds as many moon tiles as the number of players, the board event triggers, giving these tiles to whichever player has the most inhabitants, relics, or buildings depending on the board. These tiles are worth points at game's end.

The pet detectives need your help! Race around the board, flipping tiles to find matching pairs of pets faster than your opponents! This brand new memory boardgame has a tactical twist, perfect for kids and families to play together time and time again.

-description from Publisher's Website

In this family game you are a pet detective looking for lost pets in the park. You will move around the board flipping up tiles that have the picture of a lost pet and then move to the corresponding field on the board where you can find the pet. The first player to find 6 pets (or 4 in a three-player game) is the winner. Beware of the cheeky Chihuahua character that can mess up your plans!

The game has a modular board and can be rearranged time to time. 
Good for playing adults and kids as kids can benefit from their memory skills while adults can benefit from well-planed tactical moves.

-description from designer

boardgame: Clash Of Steel: Operation Unthinkable – German vs British Complete Starter Set
description:
Welcome to Clash of Steel. It’s 1948 and the war has started again.

Based on an Alternative History where the war in Europe finishes in 1944 with the surrender of Germany, but restarts just four years later in the face of Soviet aggression.

Players take to the tabletop with one of four initial nations in Epic Tank-On-Tank Combat using Highly Detailed Multi-part Plastic Miniatures, battling over objectives in an attempt to control the battlefield and defeat their enemies.

Featuring Dynamic Mission Based Game Play to ensure that every battle is unique and cinematic.

How will you re-write the history books?

This Starter Set contains everything you need to get started, including rules, miniatures, dice, tokens and much more. This box set contains...17x Highly Detailed Plastic Model Tanks, 22x Tank Commanders, Decals, Complete Rulebook, Quick Start Rules, 20x Dice, 2x Force Booklets, 19x Unit Cards, 34x Tokens, 3x Objective Markers, 2x Victory Point Dials, 26x Tactics Cards, 6x Mission Setup Cards, 9x Mission Rule Cards, 22x Objective Cards

—description from the publisher's website

In Catopomp, you are the new supervisor of a team of cat psychopomps. To ensure that no lost souls are left behind, you must lead your furry subordinates to guide these souls into the afterlife.

The goal of the game is to play all your cards and not be the last player with unplayed cards. Whenever you play cards from your hand, you must also reveal one additional card and place it in front of yourself. Over time, this will form a set of revealed cards that you will need to play as well. To help you with your task, once per round, you can use one or two dice from a shared pool to enhance your playing set. Time the dice well, and use them wisely!

If you choose to pass, the player who played the most recent strongest set of cards can pass one of their revealed cards to you. This means that you will have more cards to clear. Yikes! What will you reveal? How will you manage your hidden and revealed cards? Finding the balance is key!

The game also offers a competitive mode for players who prefer to have only one winner.

NOTE:
the Kickstarter exclusive expansions include 5 executive cards(Celeste, Ego, Jigokun, Taro, and Thoth)! Each executive cat provides their supervisors with a unique power to help guide souls along. You may add them to any Catopomp game variants. At the beginning of the game, randomly distribute one executive card to each player. Each player will possess the unique power of their executive cat for the rest of the game. Alternatively, players may take turns drafting one executive card before the game starts.

Build That Wall is a highly strategic game where your goal is to construct the most miles of walls while outsmarting your opponents. The deck is filled with cards that can help you sabotage others, steal their progress, or protect your own walls.

Each round heightens the tension as the stakes grow higher, forcing you to secure your defenses or risk losing everything.

Start by giving 5 cards to each player. The first turn is left of the dealer, and draw tree cards, If there are any play immediately cards (they will tell you in the top right-hand corner) They must be played as soon as you pick them up.

Your end goal is to build walls, or steal walls, it's up to you. 
You have 3 actions per turn whether that is building a wall, or laying down cards to keep the game interesting, you can only do 3 actions unless you lay a card that says otherwise.

-description from publisher

boardgame: SETI: Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence – Pluto Promo Card
description:
A promo card representing Pluto. It reads:

If you have a probe in the outermost ring, you may use a satellite or lander action to place that probe on Pluto.

Medieval houses vie for the king's attention.

The game consists of a board, several pawns, & a deck of cards. Shuffle the cards, & put them in the center of the board. Give each player four pawns.

On your turn, either draw a card, or trade cards with another player. After drawing or trading, play the cards to the board. If the card matches your house, you may move the appropriate pawn towards the center of the board. You may use cards that do not match your house to hinder other players.

First to the center of the board wins.

Not really a roll & move; the pawns are there to keep score.

-user description

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Heroes For Hire – Brewtus
description:
Smash the dungeon (and your thirst) with Brewtus Pintsmasher! Add everyone’s favorite frothy brawler to your Heroes of Barcadia: Heroes for Hire lineup with this single-character expansion. Brewtus comes equipped with a full suite of cards, a d20, and his own plastic Health Bar Cup—perfect for tracking damage or just staying hydrated mid-raid.

Brewtus allows you to heal an opponent of your choice by 10HP if you roll an even number. But as payment, they must give you a Loot Card.

Product Specifications:
- Replace 1 Heroes of Barcadia Base Game Character with a Hero for Hire!
- Recommended hand-wash only
- Includes Heroes of Barcadia: Heroes for Hire Brewtus the Barkeep Character Cup ONLY. Full game NOT included

Includes:
- 1 Plastic Health Bar Character Cup (Cup features character art on the front, and the measurements for the Liquid Life System™ on the back.)
- 1x 22mm acrylic d20
- 1 Plastic Signature Move Cards
- 5 Plastic Power-Up Cards
- 5 Plastic Loot Cards
- 1 Box

boardgame: Minos: Dawn of the Bronze Age – New Building Powers Promo
description:
A promo that includes:

- Architect tiles that add new powers to the Building, 
- Authority tiles that allow to manipulate the dice value once per era.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: Duel for Middle-earth – Grond Promo Tile
description:
Promo given for the early buy in store.

During setup, after revealing 3 Landmark tiles, shuffle the Grond tile with the other remaining Landmark tiles and form a stack, facedown.

Grond - Remove 1 enemy Fortress from any region.
If enemy Units are present in the region, they must retreat to a single adjacent region.
Note: If this retreat triggers a conflict, resolve it as normal.
Finally, place 1 Fortress and 1 Unit in the region.

Cat Packs: Gamefound Promo Pack adds five additional cats to your cat pack, the Jazz Orchstra, Slippery Bob, Schroompuss, the Poet and Slacker.

This is an add on from the Gamefound campaign.

boardgame: 文明源记：上古传说 (The Roots of Civilization)
description:
Lead a prehistoric clan in ancient China through the dawn of civilization. Players guide their tribes as they face the challenges of survival, develop new technologies, and progress through four distinct eras: Paleolithic, Mesolithic, Neolithic, and Mythic. Your choices will shape the path of your clan and influence the growth of civilization.

Gameplay
Players manage their tribes by collecting resources, advancing technology, and overcoming daily challenges. The game is played in rounds where players recall workers, gather resources, and take actions. Era cards bring unique events, and the game ends after the fourth era. The player with the highest civilization points wins.

In the depths of the Dreamlands, where reality mixes with the impossible, a group of investigators meets. Each of them was drawn by a vivid and disturbing dream, a call they could not ignore. They gather in an ancient city forgotten by time, where the stars shine differently and the air is impregnated with mystery.

The investigators, coming from different parts of the waking world, share their experiences and discover that they all dreamed of the same vision: a silver key, shining with an ethereal light, capable of opening portals between worlds. They know that this key is their only hope of returning to Earth, but they also realize that they are not alone in their search.

Among them may be cultists in disguise, servants of ancient entities who wish to keep the portals closed forever. These cultists will do anything to sabotage the mission, ensuring that the silver key is never found. Distrust begins to grow among investigators, as anyone can be a traitor.

With the map of the dream realms in hand, the investigators set out on their journey. They must traverse seven realms, each stranger and more dangerous than the last, facing unimaginable creatures and challenges that will test their sanity and courage. With each step, the line between dream and nightmare becomes thinner, and the true nature of the Dreamlands is revealed.

Objective of the Game
- If you are an Investigator: Complete seven story cards to find the Silver Key and win the game.
- If you are a Cultist: Prevent the Investigators from completing the seven story cards.

Gameplay
The game is played by rounds, where each round the active player draws a story card, distributes two dream cards to the other players (each player receives 2 cards, chooses 1 and discards the other) and promotes a regrouping to discuss which cards will be committed to the current story card.

Each player must commit at least one dream card to each story card, playing cards with the symbols present on the story card.
Attention, for each symbol played that is not on the current story card the active player loses a dream card, if he loses all dream cards, he wakes up and the game ends.

If they do not complete the symbols of the current history card, they suffer the penalties described in the card itself.
At any time, the investigators can try to find out who the cultist is, if they guess which person is the cultist, the game is over, the cultist loses, and the investigators win! If the person accused of being a cultist is an investigator, the game is over and the Cultist wins.

-description from designer

This expansion adds all of the ships scrapped at the 1922 Washington Naval Treaty. Along with the rules for integrating the ships into the game, the expansion includes:

    Minor navies such as Denmark and Poland.
    Mothballed ships
    Ship classes that were not large enough to warrant a piece in Seas of Thunder
    Ships that were almost completed but ended up rusting on the docks.
    Axis aircraft carriers
    Experimental designs that only existed on the drawing board.

KATZ: The Card Game is a fast-paced, strategic card game for 2 to 4 players, with each round lasting about 15 minutes. In KATZ, players hold a hand of seven cards and aim to craft the highest-scoring hand by the end of the game. Each turn, you’ll either draw from the deck or reclaim a previous discard, then discard a card to the discard pool. Cards with red base values can be permanently removed by placing them in a dead pile, extending the rounds of play and adding a layer of strategy.

At the heart of KATZ is the thrill of creating powerful combinations. Each card interacts with others in your hand, offering bonuses or penalties depending on which cards you hold. The challenge comes from balancing which cards to keep and which to let go, as every decision has consequences.

The game ends when ten cards enter the discard pool, but players can extend the game by discarding to the dead pile. With 80 unique cards and interwoven bonuses and penalties, every playthrough presents a fresh puzzle, testing your ability to adapt and strategize.

Fast-paced and engaging, KATZ offers a deeply satisfying blend of risk, reward, and evolving strategy. Can you outwit your opponents and craft the ultimate hand before the game ends?

—description from the designer

Players take turns rolling five six-sided dice, numbered 2-6 with a crab icon replacing the 1.

Each turn a player attempts to roll, in order, 3-4-crab-2-5. If a player rolls all five correct numbers on their first roll, they score five points and may take another turn. Otherwise, the player may set aside a 3. If they do, they may then set aside a 4, and then a crab, and then a 2, stopping once they can no longer continue the sequence. After setting aside as many dice as possible, the player may reroll the remaining dice up to four times, continuing to set aside dice each reroll (maintaining 3-4-crab-2-5 order with their previously-set-aside dice). If they successfully set aside all of 3-4-crab-2-5, they score two points. The next player then takes their turn.

Play lasts for three turns per player. Whoever has the highest score at the end of the game wins.

Welcome to the world of LavaRun, where strategy, sabotage, and clever play are the keys to victory! Are you ready to brave the heat of the lava field and come out as the winner? LavaRun offers a unique gaming experience full of surprises and challenges, perfect for families and friends.

How does LavaRun work?
In LavaRun, you share the same game board with your opponents. Each game is different, thanks to the tiles that are placed anew every time. Roll the dice and decide your next move: move your pawn, place a new tile, or remove one to completely change the game. Every turn can shift the tide!

What makes LavaRun so fun?
Strategy & Tactics: Carefully choose where to place the tiles. Everyone shares the same path, so each tile can be the difference between victory and defeat.
Surprising Twists: Special tiles like tunnels ensure that the game is never predictable.
Excitement Until the End: Collect gems from your opponents and try to be the first to return to your home tile. But be warned—your opponents will do everything they can to stop you!

Why LavaRun?
LavaRun is the perfect game for anyone who loves a challenge. Whether you’re playing with friends or hosting a cozy family night, this game brings everyone together in a race full of strategy, luck, and unexpected turns.

Do you dare to brave the lava field and become the glorious winner of LavaRun? Order now and experience the adventure of a lifetime!

Have fun playing and let us know how your LavaRun adventure goes!

—description from the designer

As a castaway, you're determined to return to civilization, but first, you need to build a raft. Construct bridges in the right places, sabotage your opponents' plans, and make sure to stay one step ahead of them. Along the way, you collect points. The player with the most points wins.

Island Escape is an exciting family game full of tactical possibilities. Since the island kingdom is different every time, the gameplay is always unique, and you'll need to adjust your strategy.

Why choose Island Escape?

Strategy and excitement: Plan ahead, but beware of your opponents. They will try to figure out your plans and sabotage them whenever possible.
Interactive and competitive: You share the same island kingdom with your opponents. Make use of what they do, or forge your own path.
Every round is different: You decide how the island kingdom is laid out. Each setup requires a different approach to win, so analyze and anticipate carefully!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Chaos Dwarf Team – Double-Sided Pitch and Dugouts
description:
Chaos Dwarfs are well-known for their love of arid heat, being underground, and Blood Bowl. All of that makes volcanoes excellent locations for the Chaos Dwarfs to build their stadiums within – carving the stands from the stone walls, redirecting bubbling lava flows towards the outskirts of the field, and constructing large iron monuments to honour both Hashut and Nuffle. The downside of choosing a volcano as a stadium is ensuring that there is a strict evacuation procedure in place to protect the fans, should the molten pinnacle decide to erupt mid-match!

One side of this pitch depicts a stone gridiron built inside of an active volcano. The reverse shows lava beginning to flow across the pitch ahead of the volcano's imminent eruption. Opposing players will be quaking in their boots.

You'll also find a pair of matching double-sided dugouts to complete the theme. Each dugout features score, turn, and re-roll trackers, plus Reserves, Knocked-out, and Casualty boxes. Rules for using each side of the pitch in your games of Blood Bowl are included.

The Chaos Dwarf Team Pitch and Dugouts set includes a double-sided pitch, complete with rules for playing on it, and a pair of team dugouts.

Contents:
1 Double-sided Pitch
2 Double-sided Dugouts
Rules

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cyberpunk 2077: The Board Game – Johnny Silverhand
description: Travel in time to 2020’s and experience thrilling edgerunner adventures as Johnny Silverhand, Kerry Eurodyne, Rogue Amendiares, and Spider Murphy! This Gameplay Expansion brings 4 new playable characters and 2 unique missions.

1 promo card when you buy the game or Matcha expansion at Spiel 2024 in Essen.
Also available as part of the Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter campaign (Pack A).

Adds a new bonus space by covering the Daimyo on the 3rd floor.
Effect: Get 1 Daimyo seal and 3 steps on the Passage of Time track

boardgame: Wings over the Sea: WWII Naval Air Campaigns
description:
Wings Over the Sea is a solitaire game that plunges you into the daring torpedo and dive bomber missions of World War II. Take command of legendary aircraft as you embark on a series of missions across various theatres of operation.

The game includes three modes of play:


     “Fast” print-and-play with printer friendly components: just print a few sheets of paper and you’re ready to go.
     Full color components, including counters, for a more immersive game experience.
     Vassal module


—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Over 20 years, the United States deployed over 750,000 service members to Afghanistan to oversee a transformation of the country that would cost 2.3 trillion US dollars and over 175,000 lives. The US military thwarted insurgents, while the creation of infrastructure, economic development, training and additional security was entrusted to the largest deployment of private contractors in an active war zone.

Each player will represent an American firm working to amass as much revenue as possible in the form of obligations for defense contracts during the US War in Afghanistan. Players will gain obligations by obtaining government contracts in their specialty.

The game can accommodate 2-5 players and will end after as few as 6 or as many as 8 rounds. The firm with the most obligations (VP) will win. If there is a tie, the firm with the most projects (completed and ongoing) among tied players wins.

Each firm begins with three pawns available and one pawn, two knights and a queen that can become available by use of the Lobby action. All such pieces are referred to as contractors and will be placed in unoccupied spaces on the board to select an action or on a contract card in that firm’s tableau. Unlike pawns, knights may displace an opponent’s pawn in any board spaces other than the Lobby. Displaced pawns in market contract spaces are pushed to the next available space in the queue (if able). Displaced pawns on other board spaces are returned to available. Knights are also better protected than other contractors during the Hazard Phase (see pg. 6).

Actions taken by a firm’s queen are executed immediately (unlike all other contractors). Queens may also displace an opponent’s knight or pawn in any board space with the same restrictions as a knight.

-description from publisher

Promo card for Essen Spiel fair 2024: Steamforger

Steamforger has Strength 9 and reads
"Attack: If you control at least 3 more creatures than an opponent, you win the game."

P4/2024

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Power Girl
description:
An additional hero.

Contents:
Power Girl Miniature
12 Cards

Crowdfunding exclusive figure from the Gamefound campaign available only with either the Titans Pledge or the Conquerer Pledge.

-description from gamefound campaign
Cat Packs: Promo Pack 2 adds five more additional cats to your cat pack: Cherry Cats, The Wizard, Mercat, Lumbercat and Historically Accurate Viking.

boardgame: Cat Packs: SPIEL Cat Essen 2024 Promo Card
description:
Cat Packs: Promo Card SPIEL Cat Essen 2024 adds one additional cat to your cat pack, the SPIEL cat.

This was given out at Essen Spiel fair 2024.

In Mystic Manor, players take turns spending courage points to move to a new location inside the house or perform actions outside of the house that allow the players to build up their character's maximum courage and storage, sell items they have collected, gain new items, enlist the help of a pet dog, or reset their courage points to re-enter the manor.

Inside the house, you spend courage to move to a new room to collect items, attack ghosts, capture imps, bargain with apparitions, or gain the companionship of a house cat. Players will attack ghosts by rolling a die that will be augmented by the pets and tools that they have collected in their inventory. Imps may be captured by using a net. Apparitions, which appear only in the attic, will require the sacrifice of one item in inventory.

Each player has a limited amount of inventory and courage and a unique skill. They must manage their inventory and courage to defeat the ghosts and to venture further into the house.

Once a certain number of baddies have been removed from the haunted manor, players calculate their scores by gaining a dollar value for every ghost defeated, apparition dispelled, imp released, painting sold, item remaining in their inventory, and personal bonus collected. The player earning the most money wins.

-description from designer

In the summer of 1942, after the capture of Tobruk, the German-Italian forces under the command of Field Marshal Erwin Rommel were in a strategic position to continue their advance in North Africa. The fall of Tobruk, a major Libyan port defended by British forces, was a severe blow to the British Eighth Army, which was forced to retreat rapidly eastwards towards Alexandria and the Suez Canal.

Rommel, having accumulated a series of successes, intended to use the opportunity to pursue the retreating British army, aiming to defeat it definitively and take control of the Suez Canal, vital to Allied supplies. His plan was to maintain constant pressure on the British forces, who were exhausted and ill-equipped, with the aim of forcing them to retreat further.

However, the situation of the Axis forces was not without its difficulties: Rommel suffered from supply problems, especially fuel, and logistics posed a significant challenge in the advance into the desert. Nevertheless, his determination to continue the pursuit and exploit the strategic momentum did not waver, and he prepared for what would become one of the decisive battles of the North African campaign: the Battle of El Alamein.

The game uses a new supply system based on the use and management of various types of resources: supplies, motor vehicles, ammo, manpower, afv and more.

The game offers five scenarios: two tutorial for learning the SLIT and three to relive the three battles of El Alamein, from July 1 till November 5.

One player is the Axis, the other is the Commonwealth.

In each Impulse a player may play a Pass, a Tactical Impulse or a Strategic Impulse.

Scale: 
Monthly Rounds, divided in alternated Turns. Rounds ends after two consecutive passes.
Units are Regiments (Axis) or Brigades (CW)
Map is 86x64cm, 1:10000, with one hex side corresponding to 6 km

Each scenario has its own victory conditions, with victory points for each eliminated enemy units and captured Logistics Hub.

Includes convoy wars in the Mediterranean Sea and the Atlantic Ocean.

-description from the publisher

Originally available at Essen Spiel 2024.

Meeps: 
take one card from the clearing
scores 1 point per card with woodland edge symbol
counts as a pawed animal and as a woodland edge card.

Frischling (squeaker): 
scores 1 point on it's own
counts as a cloven-hoofed animal and as a woodland edge card.

boardgame: Space Hulk (Fourth Edition): Disable the Shields
description: "Disable the Shields" scenario was published in White Dwarf issue 33 in September 2014.

boardgame: Light Speed: Arena – Promo Sponsored Asteroids
description:
Originally available at Essen Spiel 2024.

Light Speed: Arena – Promo Sponsored Asteroids brings two new Asteroids with special abilities to the game:

1. Tablescope - Play your Space
"Spaceships in sight of Tablescope's eye and positioned at least 3 asteroids away gain +1 damage to all their lasers. Other spaceships and asteroids can break the line of sight."

2. Tantrum House - Flipping Fun
"When hit, it flips an asteroid-sized table in the laser's firing direction, damaging all spaceships it lands on. The flip distance and damage are determined by the laser type.
Green Laser flips 1-2 asteroids in distance and does 1 Damage.
Yellow Laser flips 3-4 asteroids in distance and does 2 Damage.
Red Laser flips 5-6 asteroids in distance and does 3 Damage."

This Anomaly was distributed at the Phase Shift Games booth at SPIEL ESSEN 2024.

It contains an Anomaly randomizer card, an Anomaly rules card and 5 Swag Tokens. You also have to use the 3 comet tokens from the Drop Drive Box. 
The comets count as stations where you can deliver passengers and collect special ones.

boardgame: Railroad Ink: Brussel and Antwerpen Blueprint Promo Card
description: Promo Blueprint card for any of the Railroad Ink or Railroad Ink Challenge base games. The card is double sided with the cities Brussel and Antwerpen. The card was sold during Essen Spiel 2024.

boardgame: Railroad Ink: Utrecht and Amsterdam Blueprint Promo Card
description: Promo Blueprint card for any of the Railroad Ink or Railroad Ink Challenge base games. The card is double sided with the cities Utrecht and Amsterdam. The card was sold during Essen Spiel 2024.

boardgame: I'm Stuck in the lift: Floor 45 and 50 Promo Cards
description:
This promo cards were distributed as set at Spiel Essen 2024.
It adds two floors (Nr. 45 and 50) and so also 2 rounds to the game. 
45 triple points for delivering a package there.
50 multiply your points by the number of player delivering there in the same round

With area majority gameplay Metus is both a 2-player game as well as a solo COIN-style game.  Both representing a nation on the brink of civil war and the factions working within it to become the dominate social force.

Every map has shared Advisor Actions unique to the map, in 2-player Metus the players compete to sway these Advisors, then bid for turn order before the next round begins.

What makes Metus unique is that you not only are competing for territory, but winning Dominance in Areas actually creates backlash losing Adoration.  With 3 unique scoring tracks in 2-player Metus (Initiative, Adoration and Dominance) and 4 unique scoring tracks in solo Metus (Fear, Morale, Adoration and Dominance) each game is a careful balancing act on your path to victory.

—description from the designer

In Elemental Clash, players take on the role of generals in the mystical realm of Elementalia, where the kingdoms of Fire, Water, Earth, and Air are locked in an epic struggle for supremacy. The ancient balance of power has been shattered, and the armies of the elements, along with the mysterious Spirits, are vying for control in a fast-paced, strategic card game.

The objective of the game is to collect two sets of elemental cards by commanding your Clasher and utilizing powerful Fusion combinations. Each round, players reveal their Clashers and Fusion Pairs, using elemental superiority—where Water douses Fire, Fire scorches Earth, Earth withstands Air, and Air disperses Water—to gain the upper hand. The elusive Spirit cards, stronger than any single element, can provide a game-changing advantage, but their power diminishes when too many Spirits are in play.

Through careful strategy and tactical decisions, players must outwit their opponents and claim victory by forming either an Elementalist Set—one card from each element—or a Purity Set, made up of four cards from a single element. With each round presenting new opportunities for clever plays and powerful combos, Elemental Clash delivers a thrilling battle of wits and elemental forces.

-description from designer

A 10-card Inferno mini expansion, Papal Bulls, granting each player a superpower or the ability to perform a powerful action once per game.

Comes inside the deluxe edition but also available separately for those who pick up the retail edition preorder bundle.

The Deluxe Edition as well as the Collector's Edition expands the game to up to 6 players.

The Deluxe Edition includes everything from the standard edition plus:
1 mountain side, 1 3 and 4 sided mountain holder, 2 mountaineer miniatures, 2 guide cards, 2 wood score trackers,  2 upgrade boards, 4 character cards, 4 competition markers, 19 climbing route cards, 20 carabiners, 30 event cards, 30 supply tokens, 64 plastic pitons/

The Collector's Edition includes everything from the deluxe edition except the 192 pitons are wood instead of plastic, 60 wood carabiners, and 6 card & component game trays.

Mountaineers is a strategic board game in which 1-4 players (up to 6 with the Deluxe or Collector's edition) compete against each other and AI climbers to earn climbing points (victory points) as they maneuver around a 18 in./45 cm tall rotating 3D modular game board. The theme of Mountaineers is lighthearted and fun, primarily focused on competitive route finding, resource management, character building and upgrades, while challenging players to react to various exciting (and sometimes comical) events that occur on the mountain.

Strategy during both set-up and game play is important. At the beginning of a game, players need to choose four climbing routes, one character, and a starting position that work well together, in order to earn the maximum amount of points possible. However, as the game progresses, the mountain terrains fill up, making it more difficult to complete certain climbing routes. Additionally, various mountain conditions change (once per player), and event cards (drawn on each turn) require players to adapt their strategy, change their climbing routes, and purchase various combinations of gear upgrades to continue climbing.

Resource management is important as players start with a limited number of thirty route markers and will on average draw only sixty supply tokens during a game. Depending on the game and the various difficulty of event cards used, players have to balance how often they spend supplies and route markers in order to climb efficiently and complete routes. Climbing strategy is also important. Players must be careful in what order they complete routes, how they maneuver on the mountain, and whether or not they pay extra to "lock in" certain route markers to avoid sabotage by other players.

A typical turn involves drawing an event card, resolving the conditions of the event card (which may apply to other players), saving the event card for later use with your character, performing 0 to 2 regular actions, 0 to 1 special actions (if available), and special character actions (as allowed). Games with 1 to 4 players last ten rounds, each player drawing a total of ten event cards, and 5 to 6 player games last for eight rounds (with eight event cards/player). For 1 to 2 player games (and more challenging 3 to 5 player games), an "AI" mechanism can cause "automated climbers" to move around the mountain, placing route markers and blocking/sabotaging players. Players can also substitute more difficult event cards into the game, creating more flavor and challenge. The goal of a 2-6 player game is to have the most victory points at game end, and the goal of a one-player game is to have 55 victory points and four climbing routes complete at game's end.

"Bubble tea is overrated"

Agree or disagree? And who else thinks the same as you? Can you Read the Room as well as the other players, and correctly guess how many other players agree or disagree with these 300 controversial statements?! Get ready for some questionable opinions to be revealed!

During a round, a player reads a controversial statement out loud. Everyone then decides in secret if they either agree or disagree with that statement as well as guessing how many players will have the same opinion. If you can correctly guess how many players have the same opinion as you, you get 1 point. The first player to collect 8 points is declared the winner.

boardgame: Disney Lorcana: Kristoff – Reindeer Keeper Promo
description:
Kristoff - Reindeer Keeper Promo

Abilities:

     Song of the herd
     Bodyguard

A real challenge for all who like fast, dynamic entertainment. Fun for everyone regardless of age.

The game mechanism is designed in such a way that children, parents and seniors have equal chances. It doesn't matter how old you are, it doesn't matter what knowledge you have.

In this crazy race, the one with the sharpest eye and the fastest hands will win. Play and see how fast and exciting a board game can be.

Ahead of us is a real emotional rollercoaster and lots of laughter!

The rules of the game are very simple. Each player gets a dice with 6 different emoticons. Players are presented with a card showing a "combination" of emoticons to roll onto the dice, for example: 6 smiling faces and 2 sad faces. We all roll the dice at the same time, set aside the matching emoticons, and roll the remaining dice until we get the result. The first player who succeeds takes the highest value token (e.g. 4 players make it 4), and so on until everyone has rolled their tokens. The value of the marker is the number of squares you have to move your pawn across the board. The first one to finish wins.

The game mechanism is designed in such a way that children, parents and seniors have equal chances. It doesn't matter how old you are, it doesn't matter what knowledge you have.

In this crazy race, the one with the sharpest eye and the fastest hands will win. Play and see how fast and exciting a board game can be.

The first player who succeeds takes the highest value token (e.g. 4 players make it 4), and so on until everyone has rolled their tokens. The value of the marker is the number of squares you have to move your pawn across the board. The first one to finish wins.

Beasty Bar: The Limousine is a single card expansion for all Beasty Bar games (Beasty Bar, New Beasts in Town, Born to be Wild, Down Under).

It adds a little twist to the game - if an animal occupies the Limousine once you have carried out all animal actions, perform the “four-animal check.” Are there precisely four animals in the line? In this case, reveal the animal that occupies the Limousine and place it at the end of the line.

boardgame: The Wandering Towers: Change of Movement
description:
Promo Card for Wandering Towers. Also included in Wandering Towers: Mini Spell Expansion 1.

Effect:
If you use this magic spell, you may move any tower when playing a movement card for wizards, or you may move a wizard when playing a movement card for towers. If a movement card for wizards or towers is played, the movement value is switched.

This promo has printed German and English rules, but the tile itself has no text.

Setup:
• Take the 4 cards that match the flower on your personal player board.
• Place the 2 vertical cards along the left side of your board, and the 2 horizontal boards along the top of your board.

2. HOW TO PLAY
There are no changes to the base game rules.
Build your garden normally. The promo cards do not introduce special placement rules.

3. FINAL SCORING
• During final scoring, for each of the 8 rows and 8 columns, gain 1 victory point (VP) if the element shown is present (and visible) at least 1 time in the row/column.
• If the element is present multiple times, you still only gain 1 VP.
• The level on which the element is built does not matter

However, carefully placing decorative elements in your garden will help you gain extra points

"Brut" (Offspring) is a promo green alien role card. When it get turned face up you may turn face up the role cards from other player until you hit a human rule. Draw one card for each non human rule revealed this way.

This promo card was distributed at Spiel Essen at the Godot Games booth.

boardgame: King of Tokyo: Duel – Promo Pack
description:
This is a set of three promo cards for King of Tokyo: Duel
They are all marked with a light bulb in the bottom left corner.

ASSISTANCE
Cost: 4 Energy
Keep 
At the beginning of your turn you may choose to use only a maximum of one reroll. If you do, you gain a Dice Token

TURNOVER
Cost: 4 Energy
Discard
Choose two of the unused Monsters and let your opponent choose one of it. Change your Monster for the chosen one (you keep your HP, Energy, Power Cards and Dice Token)

DAZZLING
Cost: 7 Energy
Keep
Pulling one Fame if your opponent uses all his rerolls.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Blood Bowl (Second Season Edition): Spike! Journal #18
description:
Special guest editor Thuzzmer Stonecrusher gives you the lowdown on Chaos Dwarf Blood Bowl teams, including the Zharr-Naggrund Ziggurats, a highly decorated team that stands as a monument to their achievement. There's also a bit about Hobgoblins, if you care.

This installment of your favourite sports magazine delves into the background, rules, and lineup of Chaos Dwarf teams (including Hobgoblins). This issue taps a rich vein of brutal star players, bitter records of the great teams, and wry commentary on how best to coach this most violent and sturdy team to glorious victory in the name of Hashut.

It includes:

- Maniacal Masters of Metal: Background on the origins of Chaos Dwarf teams in Blood Bowl, and how they play on the pitch. Includes information on their team positions, Star Players, and a team roster.

- Famous Chaos Dwarf Teams: descriptions and background on some of the most notorious Chaos Dwarf teams, including Hashut's Harbingers, The Hobgoblin Team, the Ash Ridge Infernos, and Uzkulak Undertakers.

- Zharr-Naggrund Ziggurats Team Spotlight: an in-depth article focusing on the Zharr-Naggrund Ziggurats, one of the most feared and successful Chaos Dwarf teams to grace the Blood Bowl pitch. Includes details of their history, team profile and colours, honours, Spike! team rating, as well as the roster of their infamous 2502 -2503 squad, and loads more.

- Star Player – Zzharg Madeye: a profile on the deranged, cantankerous, blunderbuss-wielding Zzharg Madeye. Includes Zzharg's rules, in-game cost, stats, skills, awards, and career highlights from 2463 to 2502.

- Touchline Trends: Tatzina Barbarini is the resident fashionista at Spike!. This article investigates the Dark Lands' hottest garments, from fire-resistant overcoats to hats taller than Halflings.

- Star Player – H'Thark the Unstoppable: a profile on the violent and brutal H'Thark the Unstoppable, including rules, cost, stats, skills, awards, and career highlights from 2477 to 2503.

- Wasteland Leagues: brand new rules and advice for running Wasteland Leagues – a variant on the standard Blood Bowl league season, allowing players to compete in the Dark Lands Derby – open to all teams with the usual budget of 1,000,000 gold pieces – for the possibly cursed, but coveted, Obsidian Ziggurat Trophy. Also includes a unique Ash Wasteland Weather Table and a Dark Lands Kick-off Table.

- Volcanic Lair Blood Bowl Pitch: rules for using a new Blood Bowl Pitch in your games, including special rules for Oppressive Heat and Eruption.

- Chaos Dwarf Balls: background and rules for 2 unique Chaos Dwarf Balls – Curse of Stone Ball, and Hot Rock Ball.

- Chat with the Rat: ace Skaven reporter, Hackspit 'the Scribbler' Quillchewer, talks with the ever-resourceful Barik Farblast and the clinically insane Zzharg Madeye.

- Coffin Corner: Spike! Journal's obituaries column commemorating those players who are no longer with us, whether trampled into the pitch, stabbed in the back, or possibly even sacrificed to the gods.

- Master the Malicious!: a detailed introduction to coaching the Chaos Dwarf teams. Includes an overview of the strengths of the team, tactics, positions, and much more.

- Star Player – Rashnak Backstabber: A profile on the fearsome Hobgoblin assassin, Rashnak Backstabber, including rules, cost, stats, skills, awards and career highlights from 2468 - 2501.

- Dungeon Bowl Update: Chaos Dwarf Teams: provides a narrative and rules update for players to use their Chaos Dwarf Team miniatures in games of Dungeon Bowl.

- A Burning Need for Revenge: A comic strip featuring the Zharr-Naggrund Ziggurats.

boardgame: A New Order of Samurai: Clash of Sengoku Daimyos, 1570-1580
description:
September 7, 1568, Oda Nobunaga sent troops from Gifu of Mino province. In just over one month, Oda Clan conquered Kyo, suppressed Kinai, and helped Ashikaga Yoshiaki, the last shogun, to restore formal authority. Sengoku Japan was shaken dramatically.

Beneath the brief peace, however, a new storm was gathering. Powerful daimyos were waiting for their opportunities. It was rumored that the shogun, unwilling to become Nobunaga's puppet, was secretly sending letters, pulling up an "Anti-Nobunaga Alliance." The next decade witnessed the most spectacular and turbulent years of Sengoku Period. Finally, in 1578, with the sudden death of Uesugi Kenshin and the overwhelming victory of the Oda navy in the Second Battle of Kizukawaguchi, the unification of Japan could never again be stopped.

The lineage of A New Order of Samurai could be traced back to Here I Stand and Virgin Queen. From that basis, the game adds sub-systems and components unique to the 16th century Japan, including different clan traits, legendary generals and skills, provinces and vassalization, merchants and intelligence, and the dreadful Ikko-ikki (large-scale rebellion by the lower class).

A card-driven, panoramic multiplayer war game set in mid-late Sengoku Japan, A New Order of Samurai covers this period's major political, military, diplomatic, and economic events. 3 to 6 players will control major Sengoku powers: Oda Clan, Takeda Clan, Uesugi Clan, Tokugawa Clan, Mori Clan, and the Hongan-ji. Each power has its own unique way of winning. You must form diplomatic alliances, fight against each other, recruit troops, deploy your army, and overwhelm opponents with strength and strategy. The daimyo who wins these clashes will hold sway over Japan's future.

—description from the designer

Updated by the designer:

https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=3113630206  Here is the Tabletop Simulator (TTS) module for this game. Anybody is warmly welcomed to download and play.

boardgame: Die Schule der magischen Tiere: Zutritt verboten!
description:
Ida, Benni, Anna-Lena, and Jo are in the museum at night with their magical animals, trying to help Silas rebuild a fallen dinosaur skeleton before anyone notices. They must navigate stealthily, rolling dice to reach Silas while discovering the path on tiles. If the museum guard is rolled, he moves closer to them. If all the kids reach Silas, they successfully reconstruct the skeleton and win the game.

Cooperative dice placement game set in the world of Die Schule der magischen Tiere. A thrilling race in a board game format, players work together to achieve their goals.

How to Play
On your turn, roll the six blue dice. Decide together which dice to use and which to reroll, then carry out the actions.

Actions
Where to go? Use the flashlight action to discover path tiles and the shoe action to move forward. Want to speed things up? Use the Magical Animals cards for extra steps, causing the night guard to move back.

More Excitement
If the night guard lands on the same space or skips over a figure, he catches you, and that figure is removed from the board. Ida can only help when the night guard finds the footprints, allowing her to distract him.

Play cards in front of you and gather the necessary musicians to form your own band before the rest of the players, but be careful, because if you have among your ranks any musician who plays playback, it will subtract you.

Object of the game: We have to complete the points that appear on our objective card before the rest of the players.

Points: They are obtained by completing rows of 4 musicians of the same suit, which will be placed in front of each player. Until we don't get all four cards, we don't get the points and we can't start a new row.

Cards: We find more than 30 characters distributed in three different types of cards:

49 point cards (Wood, Brass, Percussion and Joker).
26 action cards (with them you can improve your tableau and take points away from the other players).
6 objective cards (they will tell us how many points we have to get from each suit to win the game).

—description from the publisher

Super Bowling is a fun-mini-dexterity game for 2 players and ages from 6 to 99.

In this game, players compete simultaneously, aiming to knock down their opponent’s 5 red pawns before going for the black pawn in the center to win. However, be careful! If you knock down the black pawn before the 5 red ones, you’ll have to set it upright again before you can continue playing.

How to play:

Connect the 2 game board pieces and attach the 2 catapults in place. Position 5 red pawns in the circles in front of each player’s catapult, and the black pawn in the circle in the middle of the board. Give each player 3 yellow balls and you are ready to begin.


     Players, playing simultaneously, place a ball on their catapult and launch it towards the red pawns of their opponent.
     If you knock down one or more of your opponent’s pawns, remove those pawns from the game. If you knock down one or more of your own pawns, you must set them upright in their original position before you continue to play.
     If all pawns of the opponent are out of the game and you knock down the black pawn in the middle, you win the game! If you knock down the black pawn before having all pawns of the opponent out of the game, you must set it upright in its original position before you continue to play.


—description from the publisher

Chopstick Barrel Battle is a delightful, mini dexterity game designed for 2 players or 2 teams, suitable for ages 6 to 99.

In this game, players compete simultaneously, using chopsticks to move barrels from their side of the board to their opponent’s side through an arc. The goal is to be the first to clear all barrels from your side of the board to win.

For team play, each player uses one chopstick, working together to maneuver the barrels cooperatively.

The game is easy to assemble and disassemble, and it conveniently stores in its custom box.

———————————————
How to play:

     Play 1 vs 1 or in teams of 2 vs 2.
     At the start of the game place 4 barrels on each side of the board in a random formation, standing up (not lying on their side).
     Each player must hold one stick in each hand. If you play in teams, each player holds one stick in one hand. By using the sticks, players should grab the barrels one at a time and toss them through the center arc, across to the other player’s side, as fast as possible. If a barrel falls out of the board it gets retrieved back to the side it was standing before.
     Battle continues back and forth; The player or team that runs out of barrels on their side of the board at any time, wins!

Set your sights on the horizon! Following an ancient map, you'll sail the beautiful yet unpredictable Azurite Sea.

Joining you on board in the 200+ cards in Disney Lorcana: Azurite Sea are friends from Big Hero 6 and Chip 'n' Dale: Rescue Rangers! Whether you face kakamora pirates or the whirlpool of a new mystery, your shipmates are ready to head bow-first into adventure.

—description from the publisher

The classic word game created specially for kids. Players roll a die (showing numbers 3-5) and flip up the corresponding number of letter tokens. These are then utilized to make a word, and collected for scoring. Unused tokens are flipped face down again. Remembering these face-down letters gives a player an advantage in making words in subsequent turns.

The memory element and limited word length (5 letters maximum) allows a balance of skills between kids and adults.

50 sheets 5 categories 10 rounds per sheet 300 terms

Play in teams at Blocktivity in 10 rounds against each other. The categories used are Explaining, Drawing, Pantomime, Word Chain and Most Words. The team that collects the most points after 10 rounds wins the game.

A set of 11 cards: cover card with rules on the back, 5 witches and 5 potions.

These witches may use one or more action points during the collect phase to draw a potion or a witch from the top of the draw deck instead from the location.

A box containing the exclusive content for backers.

1 Pack of 9 ticket office cards (game play)
1 Set of 4 enamel pins
10 golden ticket workers
10 Otter tokens
10 Wizard tokens
10 Statue of liberty tokens
4 Visitor tokens
4 hapiness tokens
4 victory points tokens
4 player tokens
4 5-Value coins
4 3-value coins
4 1-value coins
1 metal starting player token
1 metal bumper car (turn token)
1 metal ferris wheel (round token)

Each player receives 24 sticks of one color, which they place in the middle of the table (cash register). Each player takes turns to roll the die. The player may take one stick of the color rolled. If he rolls his own color, he receives two sticks of that color. If there are no sticks of his color left in the cash register, he may take two of his color from his left neighbor; if the neighbor has no such sticks, then from the next. The game ends when there are no more sticks in the cash register. The sticks are then exchanged: each player tries to exchange all of the other player's sticks in his possession for his own. If, for example, blue has two yellow sticks, he exchanges them for two blue ones with yellow, and so on. If yellow has no blue sticks, however, an exchange cannot take place. The two yellow sticks are then worth nothing. Only the sticks of your own color count. Whoever has the most sticks of their color at the end of the game is the winner.
There are two variant rules to the game included.

OKI 24 is a game in which one initial stone and 24 stones are placed on the board.
The game is very simple and easy to play. The player who places the desired number of stones in a straight line vertically, horizontally, vertically, and diagonally loses. It is a two-dimensional version of the stick-erasing puzzle games that were popular in the past. The game is a two-dimensional version of the popular stick-erasing puzzle games of the past, so it is rather similar to a puzzle game. The key to the game is that the first move starts with the initial stone, and the stones are placed starting from that initial stone. Mathematical thinking is required to win.
The game can be finished in a very short time because the game always ends.

Stones
Two players use one initial stone and 24 placement stones.

How to play
Players place their initial stones on the board in order.
The first player places his/her initial stone on any square on the board. The second and third players place their initial stones in the same order.
Any number of stones may be placed in a straight line, vertically, horizontally, vertically, diagonally, etc. However, at least one stone may be placed in the same square. However, at least one stone must be placed in such a way that it connects to a stone already placed on the board, either vertically, horizontally, vertically or diagonally.

Forbidden move
When stones are placed on the vertical, horizontal, or diagonal center line, they cannot be placed in a symmetrical layout (mirror or rotation) across the line. In this case, the player loses the game.

Victory or defeat
The player who places the last stone loses.

—description from the publisher


2012年春の新作ゲームです。 イニシャルストーン1個と24個の石をボード上に置くゲームです。お察しの通り名前はAKB48をパクリました。（笑）
縦横斜め一直線上に好きな数の石を置いて行き最後の石を置いた人が負けという、とても単純簡単なゲームです。 昔流行った棒消しパズルゲームを2次元的にしたゲームです。なのでどちらかと言うとパズルゲームに感覚は近いです。 先手はイニシャルストーンの配置から始まり、石の配置はそのイニシャルストーンを起点に始まるようにしたのがミソです。 勝とうと思うと数学的思考が必要です。
必ず終局になるので非常に短時間でゲームが終わります。

プレー人数:2人
プレー時間:1分～5分
戦略度:★★★★★=5

【持ち石】
2人のプレーヤーで1個のイニシャルストーンと24個の配置石を使います。

【プレーの仕方】
先手後手の順番を決め、盤上に石を置いていきます。
先手は最初にイニシャルストーンを盤上の任意のマスに置きます。 以降は後手、先手の順番で石を置いて行きます。
石は縦横斜めの一直線上なら何個置いても構いません。 ただし配置した最低1個の石は盤上にすでに配置された石に縦横斜めのどこかでつながるように置かなくてはなりません。

【禁じ手】
縦横斜めの中心線上に石が配置されている時に、そのラインを挟んで対称な盤面配置（鏡面、回転とも）に 石を置くことは出来ません。その場合は負けとなります。

【勝敗】
最後の石を置いた人が負けです。

In this game, each player has 30 stones and competes for points by capturing the 12 high-scoring (2 points per stone) gems and enemy stones (1 point per stone) initially placed in the center of the board.

Players take turns playing one move at a time. Each player can place any number of stones on the board, from 1 to 6 per move. The player can capture gems and enemy stones by jumping over them with the stones placed on the board.
The game is based on the system of “Amen,” a 4-player board game invented and released by Tsumura Creation in 2011, and is a fully abstract game for 2 players, but the rules have been modified to make it abstract.
The tactical aspect of the game is the timing of when to remove the initial stones that are initially placed in the corners of the player's own team. In order to make the removal of initial stones more effective, the rules have been modified so that stones placed in corners other than your own and the enemy's can be removed and won at any time during the enemy's turn.
It is a simple game of placing stones, but it is a game that keeps you on your toes until the very end because of the possibility of a big turnaround at the end.


お互いに30個の置き石を持ち、ボード中央に初期配置された12個の高得点（1個2点）の宝石と敵石（1個1点）を捕獲して得点を争うゲームです。

1手づつ交互に手番を行います。手番に出来ることは持ち石をボードに配置すること。1手で1個～6個まで任意の数の 石を置くことができます。置いた石で宝石や敵石を飛び越えると捕獲することができます。
ツムラクリエーションの津村さんが2011年に考案発売した 4人制置き石ボードゲーム「Amen」 のシステムをモチーフに、2人対戦の完全アブストラクトゲームに仕上げていますが、 アブストラクトにするためにかなりルールに手を入れて調整しています。
自陣のコーナーに初期配置するイニシャル石をどのタイミングで取り除くのかが大きな戦術となっています。 またイニシャル石の取り除きをより有効にするたために、自陣と敵陣以外のコーナーに置かれた石は敵の手番の時にいつでも取り除いて獲得できるようにルールを工夫しています。
石を置くだけのシンプルなゲームですが、最後に大逆転が起こり得る可能性があるので最後まで気の抜けないゲームになっています。

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Similo: Jurassic World – Mosquito in Amber Promo Card
description:
Description:
Dinosaur blood found in mosquitoes trapped in fossil amber allowed InGen to bring dinosaurs back to life.

The game is played by controlling a dog and a cat on a 4x4 board to obtain high-scoring canned food pieces. The game incorporates elements of Scissors Shogi, Sanme-jabira, and a chain reaction system.
The game can be played by 2 to 4 players.

Pieces
Each piece is printed with 3 illustrations of a dog and a cat, and 6 illustrations of a canned food.
The 12 dog and cat pieces are shared pieces.
The 12 canned food pieces are owned by each player.
(In 2-player game, each player has 3 large and 3 small pieces; in 3-player game, each player has 2 pieces; in 4-player game, players 1 and 2 have 2 large pieces and 1 small piece; players 3 and 4 have 1 large piece and 2 small pieces).

Initial Arrangement
There are no pieces on the board.

How to play
The first move is decided by an appropriate method, and the players take turns to move in clockwise order.
The first turn is to select a piece (any size, any illustration, any size of the pieces you own or share) from the off-the-board pieces and place it on the board.
The piece can be placed anywhere on an empty square. The piece can be placed anywhere on an empty square, or a smaller piece can be placed on top of a larger piece that has already been placed.

Scoring Pieces
When three pieces of the same illustration are placed in a straight line, regardless of size, or when three pieces of the same size are placed in a straight line, regardless of illustration, the topmost three pieces in a straight line are removed from the board and scored.
If a canned piece is placed between two canned pieces of the same type on the board, either horizontally, vertically or diagonally, the canned piece will be removed from the board and a point will be scored.
If two canned pieces are placed side by side, they are removed from the board and scored as well.
If a piece is removed from the board, and the pieces below it are arranged in a way that allows the player to gain another piece, a chain of pieces can be removed from the board to score points.
The chain continues until it ends. The move ends when the chain ends.
If two or more effects occur at the same time, the pieces are removed in any order.
Depending on the order in which the pieces are removed, the chain may be broken. If the chain is broken, the player's turn ends at that point.

Prohibitions
Players may not remove the upper pawns from the stack to check the illustration of the lower pawns. Passing is not allowed.

Winning
The player wins if he or she has 3 canned pieces at the end of his or her turn.
The game is over when all 12 pieces are placed on the board. If the game is over and the winner has not yet been determined, the winner is the player with the highest number of points. In case of a tie, the game is a draw.
Each piece is worth 1 point. Canned pieces that cannot be placed during the game are worth a minus 5 points.

LOSS
The player loses the game if he or she has more than 4 canned pieces at the end of his or her turn.
If 4 pieces of the same size and the same illustration are lined up vertically, horizontally, vertically, or diagonally, the player loses the game (if there are 3 or more players, the player is disqualified).
However, if three canned pieces are lined up in a row, the player loses the game (if there are more than three players, the player is disqualified).
If more than one effect is placed at the same time, the losing (disqualified) placement has the highest priority.
If there are three or more players and a player is disqualified, the game continues with the pieces remaining in their original positions.


4×4の空き地（ボード）の上で犬と猫を操って高得点の缶詰駒を獲得するゲームで す。 はさみ将棋、三目並べの要素と連鎖反応のシステムを組み込んでいます。
2人～4人でプレー出来て、駒には敵味方の区別が無いゲームです。

【持ち駒】
大小各々の大きさの駒には、犬と猫のイラストが3個づつ、缶詰のイラストが6個づつ プリントされています。
犬と猫駒の12個は共有駒です。
缶詰駒の12個は各プレーヤーの持ち駒となります。
（2人プレーの時は各プレーヤー大小駒各3個。3人は各２個、4人は1,2番が大2個小1 個、3,4番が大１個小２個が持ち駒です。）

【初期配置】
ボード上に駒はありません。

【プレーの仕方】
適当な方法で先手（1番）を決めて、手番は右回り順番に回ります。
手番に出来ることはボード外の駒（持ち駒と共有駒のどのサイズ、どのイラスト駒で もOK）から任意に1個を選択し ボードに配置することです。
配置する場所は空いているマスの上ならどこにでも置くことができます。また既に配置 された大きな駒の上に、小さい駒を置くこともできます。

【駒（得点）の獲得】
サイズに関係なく犬猫の同じイラストの駒で縦横斜めに直線状に3つ並んだ場合と、 イラストの種類に関係なく同じサイズの駒で縦横斜めに3つ並んだ場合は、 直線状に並んだ一番上面のそれら3個の駒をボード上から取り除いて得点として獲得できます。
犬または猫の同種類の駒をボードに配置したことにより縦横斜めいづれかの方向に缶詰駒を挟むと、 缶詰駒をボード上から取り除いて得点として獲得できます。
缶詰駒を2個並んで挟んだ場合も同様に取り除いて得点として獲得できます。
ボード上から駒を取り除いた結果、下にある駒の配置が再度駒を獲得出来る並びの場 合は、連鎖して駒を取り除いて得点にすることができます。
連鎖は終了するまで連続します。連鎖が終了して手番終了とします。
同時に駒を取り除く2つ以上の効果が同時に発生した場合は、プレーヤーの任意の順番 で駒を取り除きます。
取り除く順番によっては連鎖が途切れてしまうこともあります。 途切れた場合はその時点で手番は終わります。

【禁止事項】
積み上がっている上駒を外して下駒のイラストを確認してはいけません。パスはでき ません。

【勝ち】
手番終了時に缶詰め駒を3個獲得すると勝ちです。
12個全ての共有駒をボードに置き終わればゲームオーバーとなります。 ゲームオーバー時点で勝敗が確定していない時は獲得駒の得点が多い順に勝ちとなり ます。同点の場合は引き分けです。
犬猫駒は1個1点。缶詰駒は1個2点、ゲーム中に配置出来なかった持ち駒の缶詰駒はマ イナス5点です。

【負け】
手番終了時に缶詰め駒を4個以上獲得すると負けとなります。
同じイラスト駒、同じ大きさの駒が縦横斜めに4個並ぶと負け（3人以上の場合は失格） となります。
ただし缶詰駒は3個並んでも負け（3人以上の場合は失格）となります。
同時に複数の効果配置が出来た場合は、負け（失格）の配置が最優先となります。
3人以上のプレーで失格者が出た場合、駒はそのままの配置で残しゲームは継続します。

—description from the publisher

This is a balance game with dice rolling. The player will throw a dice and load a log with the same total of the dice's pips on the raft. The player who accumulates the highest score at the end of match wins.

Component in the box

     A raft board.
     A wood die.
     Two kind of 12 logs numbered 1 to 6.
     12 penalty stones
     12 score tiles
     A binding log holder


Players: 2 or group

Playing

Decide the first player and then the players will take their turns alternately. The active player throws a die and selects one log with the same value as the die pips and loads it onto the raft. There are no limitations regarding placement of the logs.

If there isn't a log with the same value as the die, then the player must put a penalty stone on the raft. It isn't a score for the player but the player can disrupt the opponent's next placement of a log by the placement of the stone.

When all logs are loaded safely on the raft, play is over. Players obtain the score they safely loaded on the raft. If a player causes any logs or stones to fall from the raft, the play is over, and the game ends. The payer loses any points scored during the game, and any other players will obtain the score they could safely load.

The active player has the responsibility for the safety of the raft during their turn. After 5 seconds have passed from placement the responsibility moves to next player.

Winner

Once all players have been the first player, the match is over. The player who accumulated the highest score wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Each player has 8 pieces of each of the red and yellow reversible pieces. The player can place his/her pieces on the board or move his/her own pieces on the board.
You win the game if your pieces line up in a straight line with the color of your piece, and if you can maintain the four-position line until your next turn. The feature of this game is that you can change the color of your pieces by turning over the pieces on the board that you have moved over.

Board
Hexagon board with 19 intersections and paths.

Pieces
16 reversible red and yellow pieces. Each player has 8 pieces. In the initial configuration, there are no pieces on the board.

Winning and losing
The winner is the player who can maintain the 4-piece line in a straight line in the color of his or her piece until his or her turn to play. The game is won if the 4-piece line is maintained until the player's next turn, or if the 4-piece line is already completed at the player's turn.
However, if the 4-piece lineup is completed in more than one pair, the player loses the game.
Passing is not allowed, so you lose even if you do not have a legal hand.
If a player wins and loses at the same time, the loser takes precedence.

How to Play
The players decide who will play first and who will play second in an appropriate manner. The game is played one move at a time, taking turns.

Move placement and movement
On your turn, you can do the following
1: Place pawns on the board. You may place up to two pieces at a time. However, only one piece can be placed in the first move.
2: Instead of placing a piece, you may move one of your pieces on the board. Whenever a piece is moved, it must jump over the piece (both its own and enemy pieces) in a straight line in the direction it is to be moved to the next available space. It is not possible to move without jumping over a piece.
If there is no space available at the destination, the player cannot move.
All pieces (both own and enemy pieces) that are moved over are turned over and their colors are reversed.

Thousand-day move
When the same piece placement is repeated twice, the third time the preceding player must change the move.


赤黄色リバーシブルの駒各々8個が持ち駒です。手版にできることは、手持ちの駒をボードに配置す る、またはボード上の自駒を移動させることです。
自駒の色で直線状に４目並び、次の自分の手番まで４目の配置を維持できれば勝ちとなります。 駒の移動で飛び越したボード上の駒を裏返して駒の色を変えられるのが特徴です。

【ボード】
19個の交点と経路からなるヘキサゴンボード。

【持ち駒】
赤黄色リバーシブルの駒16個。各々8個が持ち駒です。 初期配置では盤面上に駒はありません。

【勝敗】
自駒の色で直線状に４目並び、次の自分の手番まで４目の配置を維持できれば勝 ち。または自分の手番の時に既に4目並びが完成していても勝ちとなります。
ただし、5目並んだ場合と4目並びが2組以上出来た場合は、その時点で負けとなります。
パスは出来ませんので合法手が無くても負けとなります。
勝ちと負けが同時に発生した場合は負けが優先します。

【プレーの仕方】
適当な方法で先手、後手を決めます。ゲームは交互に１手づつ行います。

【手番の配置と移動】
手番にできることは
1：ボード上に持ち駒を配置すること。最大一度に2個まで配置することができます。 ただし先手初手のみは配置する駒は1個のみです。
2：配置の代わりにボード上の任意の自駒を1個移動させることができます。 移動する場合は必ず移動する方向直線状に並んだ駒（自駒敵駒共）を飛び越して次の空きスペースに移動します。 駒を飛び越さずに移動することは出来ません。
移動先に空きスペースが無い場合は移動できません。
移動によって飛び越した駒（自駒敵駒共）は全て裏返して駒の色を反転させます。

【千日手】
同じ駒配置が2度繰り返した時は3度目の先行プレーヤーは手順を変えなくてはなりません。

—description from the publisher

This single-tile mini-expansion can be added to any Carcassonne game. The design, however, matches with the New Edition of Carcassonne (2014).

This promo was given out (for free) when making a purchase (of at least 15€) at the Hans im Glück booth at Spiel 2024, Essen.

This tile presents a city segment with two opponent fields. No specific rules are included with the tile. The tile is simply mixed with the other landscape tiles. When a player draws the tile, he or she should place the tile according to the usual rules.
This special short Chronicle is distributed for free as promo goodies, accompanying the release of Oltréé: Montures & Destriers.

boardgame: Boss Up Everything: Play Tester Promo Card
description:
Its a promo card for Boss Up Everything for people who helped with playtesting the game. In gameplay this card replaces the Human location card.

When you enter this location nothing happens. But when you're challenged by an opponent while standing here and you lose the battle, you may act like the battle haven't started yet.

When you own the card, challenge an opponent and win the battle, the opponent may act like the battle have not started yet.

boardgame: What the Rule?!: Community Regeln Erweiterung
description:
Additional rule cards in cooperation with German boardgame influencers.

Released during Spiel Essen in 2024 and given for free as a promo when buying the main game.
A compilation of all 3 Vegas Nite games by International Games.

A puzzle-based game of Tic Tac Toe. The game includes 33 puzzle pieces. On 32 pieces one side has an X, the other side an O. One piece is blank and is placed on the table first. In scoring, it will count as both an X and an O.

Players choose to play X or 0 and randomly decide who goes first, attaching their piece to the blank with either X or O face up.
Players score 1 point for each three-in-a-row they make. They will also score 1 point for each member of their longest chain. Again, the blank piece is used in scoring for both X and O.

When a player has created a 3-in- a-row, they do not get additional points for simply extending the line with a fourth piece. However, they can use any member of their 3-in- a-row to complete a 3-in-a-row in a different direction.

-description from designer

Imagine any situation broken down into 100 steps. Debate, discuss, and talk about the situation to recreate a timeline for it by placing all the cards in your hand on the table in chronological order to win. A cooperative and simple game to enjoy with friends!

T'as l'choix entre is the Quebec version of the trashy and disgusting dilemmas Would you rather. Win points by making funny comments while everyone discuss one of the numerous dilemmas. Make your guests die of laughter or make them cringe. But beware, this is a game for very mature audiences!

Duke is a game of successive confrontations in which the winner is determined over several rounds. 
In a round, players take turns by either playing a card on their defense line (at the top) then playing a card on their attack line (bottom line) or by simply passing. The defending card must be the same color and strength as the last attacking card played by an opponent (with some exceptions).
To win a round, simply play your eighth and final card into your tableau. The card value of this last card is scored as victory points.
The first player or team to reach 15 points wins the game.

At 4 players, the game is played in teams.

Duke is loosely based on Goïta Shogi, a card game derived from Shogi. Shogi, literally "generals' game", is a traditional Japanese board game similar to chess.

Set of 5 extra advanced (blue ornament) caravan cards that were given to everyone who purchased the game at Essen Spiel 2024.  It was also included in the German edition of "Spielbox" (Issue #5, 2024).

Bonus tile that you can ask & get for free at Spiel '24 in Essen.

If you are the first person who has filled their 6-space enclosure, you may take the Sloth from the middle of the table and place it next to your zoo. The Sloth stays with you until the end of the game.

You can decide once whether you want to skip your turn instead of performing an action. If you want to skip, place 1 coin from your supply on the Sloth and your turn is finished. The coin remains on the Sloth until the end of the game.

Cardboard tile that was originally given as a promo at Essen Spiel 2024 if you bought the game at the ABACUSSPIELE booth.

It contains a wheel that you can punch out. On the cardboard tile an arrow is present and by randomly putting in the wheel in the hole on the cardboard where you have punched the wheel out, one of the 6 colors is selected.

Every card of the color you have at the end of the game will score you 1 VP.

In Scope, players try to guess the excact location of an answer to a geography oriented trivia-quiz question, by placing a Scope-marker on that position on the game board. The answer is then checked with the Scope-ruler, that indicates the correct location with coordinates.

Closer you guess, more points you get. The other players may score too, as they can guess if the answer is north, south, east or west from the guessers point.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: SMASK! is a fast-paced game where players compete on who grabs the correct apple slice the fastest. Players throw the Dice Worm to indicate which slice they must catch. The player who grabs the correct slice gets a point. If the slice indicated by the Dice Worm is already taken by a player, the fastest to shout the name of the player, gets the slice for themselves! Smask! is a fast-paced game requires quick reflexes, pattern recognition, and attentiveness.

Toilet Divers is a memory game of treasure seeking in the sewers. The players dive into toilets and try to navigate the layered pipe maze to find lost treasures. The route can be blocked by various obstacles, which might require help from other players to get through. Learn the structure of the maze as the game goes forward. Try to remember where you saw the hidden treasure and try not to get lost! You get points by finding the hidden treasure, and your helpful co-players get rewarded, too. The player with the most points wins the game.

Toilet Divers vertical maze is assembled into 4 toilet "notebooks" with 8 layers/pages each, and the content of each layer is randomized by shuffling toilet-cards and attaching them into the toilets binding coils.

boardgame: Minos: Dawn of the Bronze Age – New Decree Cards promo
description:
3 extra Age I and 3 extra Age II decree cards that were given for free if you pre-ordered the game, but can be purchased from Board&Dice.

The Age I cards let you build a boat for free or raise a previous build boat 2 steps for free.

The Age II cards let you build a tree for free or gives you 1 card per tree you have already built.

This promo contains 4 barrier standees. Each player gets one at the start of the game.

Once during the game you can place your barrier across any road. The two sites it links are considered to be no longer adjacent.

Ever wanted to know how the most popular killer became such scary individuals? Play as one of the most iconic horror movie killers ever and study at the academy how to become one of them.

Train on victims, find the right gear for you but be aware: don't fail on your training and become the hunted one.

Play as an iconic killer and evolve through out the rounds. Collect cash and souls and get abilities and equipment in exchange for it. Level up and increase your stats but be aware, your victims will become stronger throughout the rounds and won’t make it easy to reach the top position.

At first you team up with other killers but as always there can only be one. In a final battle your will find out who of you will be victorious.

—description from the designer

Enhance your base game by adding the Edmond Halley character. Utilize his unique comet ability to make the advanced game mode even more dynamic and challenging!

The new hero ability is:

Halley has three personal comet spaces and one special comet tile. The comet spaces are:

- give a lecturers gain one quadrant
- increase two of your dice by 3
- gain 7 points

Halley’s Comet as two attributes.
It has value 2 on the starting side and value 3 on the flipped side. You use it the same way as other comets while observing.
When you use it for the second time you may place it on your personal comet spaces or on the home board.

When you trigger a "spot a comet" action, you may use this comet instead of one on your player board. This allows you to use this comet multiple times. Once you take it from a comet space it was placed on, the reward on it becomes available again. This applies even for your personal comet pages.

boardgame: Deep Rock Galactic: Grunt Brew Promo
description:
Originally available at Essen Spiel 2024.

When you kill a Grunt with a pickaxe hit, all Grunts adjacent to it panic - move them up to three spaces in any direction

“An honorable peace?” is a straightforward recreation of the Battle of Tarqui, which took place on the plains of Tarqui near Cuenca, in present-day Ecuador, on 27 February 1829. This battle was fought between the armies of Peru and Gran Colombia, which at the time included the modern-day countries of Colombia, Ecuador, and Venezuela. Although little known outside these countries, it was a key battle in defining the spheres of influence of the countries.

During each turn, players will alternate activating their units one at a time until all units are either exhausted or the players choose to stop activating them. Each activation allows a unit or stack to perform one action: movement, combat, or fire. After each activation, units must always check their readiness status. Victory conditions involve the elimination of enemy units and control of key geographical objectives.

-description from designer
