description:
Free promo for bying the base game at Essen SPIEL 2024.

Rules:
Equipment.
Circle, if it isn't yet, the active monster on the space that you've finished your movement. This doesn't prevent damage.

Free promo card for Imperium (Classic/ Legens/ Horizons), given out at Essen SPIEL 2024.

Rules:
UNDAUNTED
Fame card
Free play. You MAY acquire a UNCIVILISED/ REGION.
You MAY take a UNREST to steal 1 PROGRESS from each other player.
Worth 2 VP per UNREST.

The German Border, 1944
The German Army is full retreat after heavy defeat in Normandy. The Allied forces are sweeping across France and Belgium towards the German border, soon Germany itself will be invaded. To halt the Allied advance and buy time to rebuild their shattered forces, the German high command begin to rapidly reactivate and man the defences of the Westwall.

Throughout the autumn, the advancing US armies, delayed by supply shortages, will find themselves engaged in bitter fighting to breakthrough the lines of concrete bunkers, minefields and anti-tank obstacles. Whilst behind the Westwall, theremaining panzers forces re-arm, ready to fight again.


     BACKGROUND


A general overview of the war along Germanyä western border in the autumn of 1944, as the US armies push their first troops onto German soil. From the US 1st Army's battles at the Aachen Gap and through the dank Huertgen Forest, to the US 3rd Army's tank battles in the Lorraine and capture of fortress-city of Metz.


     ARMY LIST


Designed to be used with the US and German army lists already published in Battlegroup: Overlord Beyond Beaches, this books contains a new army list, for the fresh German panzer brigades deployed from September 1944, as well alterations to the German and US army lists for 1944, to better recreate the battles at Germany's border in the autumn of 1944.


     SPECIAL RULES


New special rules to adapt the core Battlegroup game for battles against the Westwall in 1944. The special rules detail German bunker construction and defences along the Westwall as well as the hard fighting under the trees of the Huertgen Forest and the tank battles in the Lorraine between US armour and the newly formed panzer brigades.

This book requires Battlegroup: Overlord Beyond the Beaches to use full, including its Battle Rating counters.

* CAMPAIGN
Six new historical scenarios in a detailed narrative campaign for Operation Clipper, he battle to breakthrough the Westwall at Geilenkirchen.

—description from the publisher

LOGOS DIVINÆ is a card game system that also serves as a historical guide to the mythology and deities of antiquity. Designed to function both as a conventional deck for classic card games and as an esoteric tool for tarot readings, it incorporates new mechanics inspired by Roman religion, alchemy, and astrology—reimagining your favorite classic games with historical depths.

The OCCULT CLASSIC edition includes 91 cards: a standard 40-card deck featuring pips from Ace to Ten, each depicting symbolic and mythic figures; the 22 Major Arcana; 16 Alchemy Cards in place of traditional court cards; and 7 "Ludogems"—cards that add versatility for stringing together classic games like War, Go-Fish, and Blackjack. Players can use these cards for competitive play, solo sessions, family game nights, or as part of their meditative or spiritual practice.

Every card in LOGOS DIVINÆ is fully illustrated, drawing inspiration from real-world archaeological discoveries of ancient Mediterranean and Mesopotamian cultures. Each card has an epithet, describing qualities that enhance its use in tarot readings. Each card contains a "Numen"—an additional symbol that can be used in a variety of ways. These symbols, based on major deities of antiquity, bring unique elements not found in traditional card formats, enabling you to play inventive versions of classic games like Rummy, Scopa, Klondike Solitaire, and Crazy 8s.

A group of hearty heroes are the only thing that stand in the way of Genemshyr and the destructive power of the Red Dragon, Ardor. Work together to save your town and live to tell the tale!

Objective: 
The heroes win if they are able to defeat all the hit points of the Lair Boss before the deck runs out. The Lair Boss wins if a slot on the board that requires a card cannot be filled because the deck is empty.

Gameplay:
1. HERO Phase:
Use (1) MAJOR Hero Ability: The heroes get to decide to use (1) major ability before MOVEMENT phase begins.

2. MOVEMENT Phase:
Turn order is up to the heroes to determine as they deem most effective and efficient. Each hero has the ability to do 1 or all of the following things:

- Roll for Actions: All heroes roll their dice and are then able to move up to the number that they rolled on their dice.
- MINOR Hero Ability: If a hero rolls a 1 on their Roll for Actions, they can use their MINOR Hero Ability.
- JUMP: If a hero rolls a 1 on their Roll for Actions, they can choose to JUMP to the opposite side of the Lair Boss with their 1 movement action.
- Collect WEAK POINT cards: Heroes are trying to collect cards from the board that add up to 5 of a particular target weak point to do damage. When a hero ends their turn, they get to claim the card that is at their final movement location. If it is a WEAK POINT card, they claim it to their inventory. If it is an ATTACK card, the heroes take a DAMAGE. 
- Avoid ATTACK zones: The Lair Boss will clock around the board trying to collect ATTACK cards. By knowing how many times the Lair Boss will move, the heroes can predict the dangerous zones on the board and determine if it is worth the risk and reward. 
- ATTACK the Boss: Use WEAK POINT cards to deal damage to their respective target by having 5 or more points of collective WEAK POINT cards and then moving to the target location. Remove a hit point cube from the target location when damage is dealt.

3. BOSS Phase
The Lair Boss moves a number of times equal to the number of heroes in the game, a minimum of 2 times. The Lair Boss clocks toward the closest ATTACK card, prioritizing clockwise if there is a tie. When the Lair Boss reaches an ATTACK card, it performs the ATTACK on the card. Heroes in the ATTACK zone of the card take 1 damage and the ATTACK is consumed/discarded.

4. RESET Phase
When the Lair Boss has finished its turn and all ATTACKS have been resolved, the round is over and the RESET phase is reached. Using the deck, deal out cards from the deck, starting from the LOWEST EMPTY number until all of the empty slots on the board are filled. If a slot on the board cannot be filled because there are no more cards in the deck, the Lair Boss has defeated the Heroes and the game is over.

A. RAMPAGE Event
There is a Level Indicator on a number of the WEAK POINT cards. Depending on the Level that the heroes have chosen to play, every time a card is flipped over, in the RESET Phase or from DAMAGE taken by the Heroes, that has that respective level, an instantaneous event occurs. The Lair Boss performs a special attack. Draw a card from the RAMPAGE deck. Spin the Lair Boss spinner and resolve the special attack where the Lair Boss Head lands. After resolving, continue play where it left off.

-description from designer

boardgame: Gloomhaven: Buttons & Bugs – The Beast Tamer Scenario
description:
A new promo scenario originally available at Essen 2024.

This scenario is meant to be played after scenario 11 or 12. After completion you the Everchill Sunflake item and then you proceed to Scenario 13.

"The Network Expansion" for Algae, Inc. was a Gamefound-exclusive mini-expansion which introduces two exciting modules, "NETWORKS" and "CAPITAL CITIES," designed to enrich your gameplay experience and offer new strategic avenues for success.

With the Networks module, players will now randomly select a Network card and place it at the top of the game board. During the end of the month phase, players will receive victory points based on the active Network card.

In the capital cities module, players combine four reward cards with four randomly chose Capital City cards. At the end of the game, during the end of the month phase, players score VP according to the reward cards if exported to the cities on the Capital City cards. For those seeking an extra challenge, there's an option to play with a more demanding variant where the VP are rewarded immediately after exporting to the city on the Capital City card.

It was later made available by itself at Essen Spiel 2024.

-description from the publisher's website

boardgame: The Dick Sits: Pride Dicks Expansion
description: 10 unique dicks decorated with pride flags.

boardgame: The Dick Sits: Bodily Functions Expansion
description: 10 cards with bodily functions.

boardgame: The Dick Sits: Kitchen Utensils Expansion
description: 10 more cards featuring kitchen utensils.

"The goal is to collapse adversary’s system into confusion and disorder causing him to over and under react to activity that appears simultaneously menacing as well as ambiguous, chaotic, or misleading."

John R. Boyd, “Patterns of Conflict”


At 0400 Zulu Time, July 24 1985, Warsaw Pact forces cross the Inner German Border and assault NATO positions in West Germany. The offensive against the US VII Corps sector has a first echelon of eight Soviet and Czechoslovakian divisions, and three main objectives: Pin the NATO forces defending the area, inflict as much casualties as possible, and force CENTAG to commit its reserves to the defense.

Taking its roots from SPI’s Central Front and NATO: Division Commander, In a Dark Wood is the fourth module of the C3 series, focused on Command, Control and Communication and pioneered by Less Than 60 Miles - one of the five nominees for the 2019 Charles Roberts Awards as Best Post-WW2, Cold War, & Hypothetical Era Board Wargame.

Several typical wargame mechanics have been reinterpreted, and both sides must fight three equally dangerous foes: the enemy, their own plan and time. Even a simple action can quickly turn into a disaster when facing an opponent using more efficiently the real key to victory: the OODA Loop theorized by John Boyd in the early ‘80s and used today as the basis for several military doctrines.

Military doctrine, tactics and peculiarities for each side are represented, allowing a skilled commander to use his own strength points and exploit the enemy's weak ones. Some examples are:

    US VII Corps command problems due to its multinational structure.
    West German Auftragstaktik (Mission-Type Orders), giving Bundeswehr a flexibility hardly obtained by other armies.
    French incomplete integration into the wartime NATO structure.
    Soviet Sappers, first-line assault units used to crack the toughest nuts.
    Soviet Assault From March.
    The abundant but partially outdated Czechoslovakian artillery.
    Warsaw Pact Axis of Advance.
    Warsaw Pact vulnerable supply network.


				
				
					Game Highlights
    5 km per hex, 3 hours per game turn
    Regiment / Battalion sized ground units
    Detailed Order of Battle set in 1985
    Close Air Support and Attack Helicopters
    Command, Control & Communication related problems
    Electronic and Intelligence Warfare
    Artillery interdiction and Counter-Battery fire
    Chemical and Nuclear warfare
    Specific events for Warsaw Pact and NATO
    Random events to further increase battlefield chaos
    Two Campaign Games:
        "The Main Problem", the Warsaw Pact offensive against US VII Corps.
        "One Minute to Midnight", Unrestricted nuclear warfare after days of build-up.
    
    Four Smaller Scenarios:
        "Just a Second Away", US 2ACR and 1/3I Brigade meet the Soviet 28th Tank Division.
        "Shortcut to Schweinfurt", Soviet 28th Army turns south and clashes with West German 12th PanzerDivision.
        "Where the Forest Whispers", US 3rd Infantry Division faces one Soviet and one Czechoslovakian Army.
        "Operation Marsh Harrier", the NATO counterattack after the arrival of the French forces.
    


				
				
					Game Components
    98×85 cm matte plasticized map, covering the US VII Corps Area of Responsibility
    7 countersheets, 1200+ 5/8″ matte plasticized counters
    40 Action and Event Cards
    24 pages full colour Rules Booklet
    28 pages full colour Scenarios & Designer’s Notes Booklet
    8 pages full colour Examples of Play Booklet
    2x Tables booklets
    5x Players' Aid Charts
    2x 10-sided dice
    10x zipbags


-description from designer

description:
Thief Rush is a frenetic card and dice game that allows games from 1 to 4 players, in which you will have to get into the skin of a seasoned thief to get as many prestige points as possible and win the game.

To do this you must act quickly, get the necessary equipment and get the combination that opens the safe, all while avoiding the police!

On your turn you can perform one of 3 actions: discard and draw up to 2 equipment cards, attempt an objective or use your Police card.

The game ends at the end of the 10th round or when a player reaches 10 prestige points (whichever comes first). At that point the number of prestige points each player has earned will be checked and the player with the most points will be the winner of this Thief Rush.

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

-description from the designer

boardgame: El Castillo: The Temple of Kukulcán
description:
El Castillo: The Temple of Kukulcán is an original game of strategy and cunning for 2-4 players. Players compete with each other to be the first to place four of their game pieces on to the stepped pyramid in order to form an unbroken line of 4 pieces. Piece placement is key as all pieces played must trace an orthogonal line back to a piece on the bottom tier of the board.

Each set comprises:-
- An MDF game board that breaks down into two halves for easy storage
- Game rules
- 4 Sets of 32 counters (colours may vary from those shown)
- First player marker

When assembled the board measures 200mm x 200mm.

-description from publisher's website
Includes 4 new project cards. Each of them grants 0 step and, when triggered, 1 point for every instance of the shown pattern: the indicated Building must be on two Plots of the same Sector.

The Prosperity Box is an exclusive add-on for Spark Riders 3000.

Contains two new playable characters and new equipment for your missions!

This exclusive add-on is produced in extremely limited quantities and will not be available in retail.

Content of the Prosperity box:

- 2 miniature Droid

- 2 Crew Member cards

- 6 Thruster miniatures

- 7 Poker chip character tokens

- 25 Premium equipment Prosperity cards

Sure, it’s fun to build things, but it’s even more fun to burn it all down! Hire an arsonist to set your opponents’ city blocks ablaze. Put down a fire hydrant to keep them from doing the same to you! Set off the alarm and call in the fire brigade to put out the flames before all your hard work goes up in smoke!

Urban Insanity just lit a match and got hot in a hurry!

We’re takin’ it to the streets! Go double or nothing for points on your road with a gas station tile. Use the gas station to fill up your vehicles and get some extra movement. Use the helicopter to air-drop your Urbanite into unfriendly territory and score big!

The Gas & Go Expansion Set has two full sets of playing pieces so that you can now play up to six players at the same time!

Just when you thought it couldn’t possibly get any more insane!

For the engineer in you that wants to build huge city blocks and major roads, we have a bulldozer for you! Special “bridge” tiles let you take over you opponents’ city blocks and create a huge metropolis. But don’t get too comfortable – here comes a demolition crew with a wrecking ball! But don’t give up! You can use the Wild Tile to complete your construction project and dominate the board!

boardgame: The Vale of Eternity: Meeps the Mascot Promo Card
description:
Originally available at Essen Spiel 2024.

A promo card that gives you one red coin for each board game in your bag.

This upgrade adds 6 Unicorn Chaos Magic cards and an oversized Unicorn meeple to the Kickstarter Exclusive Edition of Twisted Cryptids, and it opens up a modified set of rules for the game.

It is available as a print and play for those who purchase the retail version of the game.

boardgame: Flock Together: Birds of a Feather Eggspansion
description:
Your favorite co-op coop game is back with Flock Together: Birds of a Feather Eggspansion!

With 6 dauntless chicken heroes, 6 nefarious predators (new loot!), 3 extra weather conditions, and an additional player board allowing up to 6 players, the expansion adds opportunities for even more complex decision making as you battle your way to the end of the final season!

-description from designer

Stability Island is a fast moving strategic game with a unique theme centered around atomic monsters and navigating instability! Players must balance their collection of nucleons and Atomster tokens while considering the risk of chain reactions and the strategies involved in trading and gathering equipment. Here’s a quick summary of the key elements of the game:

Key Features of Stability Island:
Exploration and Navigation: 
Players use nautical dice to decide their movement options across the game board.
Compass headings and pips guide the navigation and movement range.

Gathering Nucleons: 
Players collect Red Proton and Blue Neutron crystals when they land on specific tiles.
Balance is crucial; collecting too many nucleons can trigger a chain reaction that affects other players.

Chain Reactions: 
Extra nucleons must be passed to the next player, leading to potential losses and rewards.
Players can absorb nucleons, gaining points while mitigating chain reactions.

Equipment for Atomster Tokens: 
Players start with equipment cards to gather Atomster tokens.
Exploration Cards provide purchase power or equipment requirements for gathering tokens.
Negotiation is essential for trade; players can rent or purchase equipment.

Building Atomsters: 
Players gather Atomster tiles to build their atom monsters on a player mat grid.
Discarded tiles during chain reactions go to a trade zone for others to select.

Endgame and Scoring: 
The game ends when a player fills their Stability chart with balanced nucleons.

The winner is determined by calculating the aggregate score based on Atomster tokens and balanced nucleons.

Advanced Play: 
Players can delve into real-world science by calculating their atom monsters’ elements using the periodic table.

This game combines elements of strategy, negotiation, and a fun thematic twist on atomic theory. Players will need to think critically about their moves, manage resources efficiently, and engage in social interactions to succeed.

-description from designer

This pack contains 8 Legendary cards including the Siege Troll, Hammersmith, Crypt Titan and Forest Elemental! Plus 6 full art Gem resource cards and 2 custom Attack token cards. This promo pack will not be printed again!

—description from the back of the pack

Adds all new cards to the Forged Realms Core Set. There are no random cards, you get 2 of every card in the Fields of Flame add-on including the powerful Burst Knight, Goblin Sorcerer, Wolf Druid and many more.

—description from the back of the pack

Originally released as part of Alhambra: The Falconers but is was sold separately at Spiel 2024 as a new Queenie.
The tiles have a different shape (octagon) than in the expansion (circles) but the rules remain the same.

“Building sites” allow players to purchase buildings for half the cost, but the building must be finished in order to count towards majorities.

Establishing a building site:
The player pays at least half (rounded down) of a building tile’s cost and takes the tile. Such a purchase is never regarded as paying the exact amount. The player takes 2 building site tiles, keeping the backs hidden from other players, and chooses one of them to place on top of the building tile just purchased, with the construction side face up. The other tile is shuffled back into the stack.

The player can add this building tile to their Alhambra or place it in their reserve. If added to the Alhambra, the walls on the tile count during scoring, but not the unfinished building itself.

Completing an unfinished building:
On their turn, a player may choose to complete the building as an action, by paying the full amount for the building in the currency shown on the back of the building site tile. After completing the building, the building site tile is removed from the game.

Remember: The player can’t just pay the difference, but must pay the full amount!

If the exact amount is paid, the player takes one more turn immediately.

At the end of the game:
Before distributing the remaining building tiles, each player must reveal each building site tile in their Alhambra and discard all money cards of the currency shown on this tile.
Ignore all building site tiles in a player’s reserve.

Notes
• The Vizier (from the module “The Vizier’s favour”) cannot be used to set up a building site.
• Camp tiles (from the module “The Camps”) and Bazaar tiles (from the module “The Bazaars”) cannot be set up as a building site.

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.
description:
Heroes come in all shapes and sizes. Some can fly, some can shoot energy from their hands, and some are just really, really strong. But there are some heroes who don’t wear capes, heroes who stay out of the spotlight, working tirelessly behind the scenes to keep the world safe from covert threats. These are the agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.

It’s the beginning of a brand-new wave of Marvel Champions: The Card Game! As the game’s eighth campaign expansion, Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D. brings a number of classic S.H.I.E.L.D. characters to the table, including two new playable heroes, Maria Hill and Nick Fury, each of whom comes with a pre-built deck ready to play from the get-go. Take on covert threats from the forces of A.I.M. while also working to expose the traitor within your own agency. This expansion includes five brand-new scenarios, each of which can be played individually or as part of a larger campaign.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
Tavern Ball is a strategy sports game played between two players. Each player is the coach of a team represented by a deck of cards. During a match, coaches position their teammates on the field, activate their unique actions and issue them commands in order to gain possession of the ball, push it into the opposing team’s half and score points.

With each play-through, choose a play-style from 12 teams each with a diverse range of strategies adopted by the street urchins, firefighters, sea cults, thespians (and more!) of Red Harbor - then mesh it with a second choice to form unique combinations.

Your team’s success relies on your ability to deploy and command your player cards before fatigue or injury removes them from the play. As the match unfolds, unleash the unique actions of your players, activating their special abilities to gain the upper hand. Player cards enter the fray face down, building the tension as coaches attempt to read the tactics of their opponents while attempting to disguise their own.

Start a league! Tavern Ball can be played simultaneously between 6 players. Optional rules allow coaches to control which teams are available each draft, removing feared strategies while allowing new combinations to shine.

—description from the publisher

This is a promotional mini-expansion, given out at SPIEL '24.

It includes a bag, 7 Unfinished Business poker chips and a German rulebook.

During the game setup, every player receives randomly one of the poker chips and keeps it facedown in front of his or her safe.

In phase 2, after performing a succesful attack action, the active player may have a look at the poker chip's bottom in front of him or her without revealing the value to the other players. The attacker may instead swap his or her poker chip with the one of the defender without looking at the values.

At the end of the game, every player reveals his or her poker chip's value and scores its value additionally.

In Yowie Kapowie you are an Aussie Monster Hunter, exploring the Australian Outback looking for legendary creatures like the Bunyip, Drop Bear, and Burrunjor. You'll throw, drop, and flip your monster cards onto the table and capture any other monsters they touch. Cards you capture join your collection and earn you points: 1-3 points if they're face-up, but only 1 point if they're face-down. Some cards will let you manipulate that collection: flipping over cards to gain points, or adding stickers to cards and gaining bonuses for collecting particular sets of creatures. Over time those stickers change the value of different cards in your game, and make your copy of Yowie Kapowie completely unique! The game ends when the deck of monsters is exhausted and the winner is the player with the most points amongst their collection.

-description from designer

boardgame: Corduba 27 a.C.: Instigator & Historian Promo Cards
description:
3 promo cards for game. 1 card is double because it is in Spanish and English language. Other 2 cards are language independent.

Historian: when building a monument, apply the construction bonus from each district that provides Prestige.

The Instigator: you are not required to spend Dinarii or food during phase 4. Check happiness.
Each time a riot occurs, you decide where to place it.

Promo card for basegame Celtae handed out in Essen @ Spiel'24

Its an alternative leader card with special art (stylized pic of a content creator, fan of the game) and different end goals for the game.

boardgame: Age of Rome: Emperor's Tower Promo Card
description:
Promo for the backers of the game and available in Essen 2024.
Also available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's biome pack.

All players earn 2 coins. 
Do not rotate the provinces board at the end of this round.

A Giant Panda promo card for base game handed out at Spiel'24 Essen, with small logo on it.

Action: For each creature that eats leaves add 1 coin.

Salento is a board game for anyone who lives in or has the Salento peninsula in their heart.
Travel between the territories of Lecce, Brindisi and Taranto with the dice and invest to make it even more beautiful and richer.
In the Lampu and Toccu cards, you will find all Salento dynamics: dialect expressions, traditions, typical situations, to face a 0 km challenge.
Test your business and bargaining skills and accumulate as many nags as possible.

In オーバーツーリズム京都 ("OVER TOURISM Kyoto"), players aim to enjoy sightseeing, dining, and shopping while using Kyoto’s public transportation. However, if tourist congestion occurs during your travels or at shops, over-tourism strikes. Can you avoid over-tourism and still enjoy your Kyoto sightseeing adventure?

In each round, players will choose 1 card from their hand and simultaneously play it face up in front of them.

Whether to use the front or back side of the card is crucial. If two players play cards with the same color, over-tourism occurs.

After 8 rounds, players will have a row of 8 cards, and scoring will begin. The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Adh Mor means "Good Luck" in Irish. The game begins with just four of each player's pieces on the board. A roll of the unique dice dictates the choices available to place or move a peg. The goal is to be the last player to move. Sounds simple, but the more pegs there are on the board the less you are able to accommodate the next die roll.

The game is a small peg game, very portable and convenient for travel. Rated for ages 8+, 2-players, 20 minutes.

-description from designer

There are only 18 cards in Apiary but you can only acquire them (and their points) by rolling dice in specific sets. Each set of three cards represents a beehive, and the dice are the bees and nectar needed to have a successful hive. Some cards are worth more than others, but the best score is gained when a hive is complete and all three of the set have been won. Be aware that a crafty beekeeper can chose to acquire a card that you need but fear not — you might get the chance try to steal that card back later in the game. It's push your luck die-rolling fun for the whole family.

-description from designer

boardgame: Avalon: The Riven Veil – Excalibur Promo Card
description:
This single promo card was given out (for free) at the booth of Shadowborne Games at Spiel 2024, Essen.
The promo provides two swords and has an ability saying: 
[cross symbol]: copy the [cross symbol] effect of any one of your faction's other artifacts.

boardgame: Mini Rogue: The Creeping Death & The Long Stretch Promo Cards
description:
The Creeping Death & The Long Stretch are two promo cards for Mini Rogue, packaged together.


     The Creeping Death is a new boss that slithers in the dark and can most likely be encountered in ...
     ... The Long Stretch, a new Lore card that leads you into a dark corridor with unknown perils


Because The Long Stretch is a Lore card, the Depths of Damnation expansion (or potentially other expansions that let you draw Lore cards) is required to play these. The promo cards were originally released at the SPIEL '24 in Essen.

Changes in Setup:
Place all bicolor dabba tiles on the table as a supply.

1st PICK-UP PHASE
Every time you completely fill a level on your player board with dabba tiles, you receive a reward:

Take 1 bicolor dabba tile of your choice from the supply and place it immediately.
You may not put it aside for later use, but you can choose to not gain the reward.

Note: Completely filling a level with an empty dabba tile or a bicolor dabba tile is fine: You will gain the reward of (another) bicolor dabba tille.

2nd DELIVERY PHASE
In 3) Deliver dabba tiles and receive tips you score every bicolor dabba tile twice: Once for each of the two colors depicted on the bicolor dabba tile.

Components: 24 bicolor dabba tiles, 4× per color combination.

Only one of you can become the Trombone Champ! Based on the award winning video game, this is the definitive card game about trombones! But there are also lots of hot dogs and baboons.

This is a dedicated deck game where you are playing note cards to extend trombone slides and complete the song to score toots! The first player to score 15 toots is the winner.

You thought you were the only pirates on the high seas?

You were wrong!
In this expansion, 4 more pirates will fight with you for treasure and glory. With them come events that make the sea even more dangerous.
Luckily, the parrots will help you! Loyal companions that every self-respecting pirate captain needs!

Ihr dachtet, ihr seid die einzigen Piraten auf Hoher See?

Da lagt ihr wohl falsch!
In dieser Erweiterung kämpfen 4 weitere Piraten mit euch um Schätze und Ruhm. Mit ihnen kommen Ereignisse, welche das Meer nur noch gefahrenvoller machen.
Zum Glück unterstützen euch dabei die Papageien! Treue Gefährten, die jeder Piratenkapitän braucht, der was auf sich hält!

Up Dog is a fun and easy-to-learn card game where everyone plays fetch cards at the same time, to complete the goal cards in the center. The trick is, you want to be the ONLY doggie to complete a goal, otherwise no one gets to move! The race is on, will you be the dog to have its day? Or will you be stuck in the doghouse?

IT’S A RACE TO WIN!: In this simple card game, race your furry friends through town and get to the food bowl first to win!

SIMPLE TO LEARN CARD GAME: In this simple to learn card game, play fetch cards to complete certain goals. If you do complete a goal and your the ONLY dog to do so, you move along the path!

MOVE UP DOG: Complete a goal that has YOUR pup on it, and you get to go UP DOG! No only moving along the path, but also skipping UP the path.

PLAY AGAIN AND AGAIN: No game of Up Dog is the same! Each time you play it will be a brand-new experience.

Contents: 
- 1 Neighborhood Board
- 5 Reference Cards
- 54 Fetch Cards
- 30 Goal Cards
- 15 Paw Tokens
- 5 Dog Pawns

The I C E: Unlimited Box contains the Collector version of the base game as well as the Collector version of the two expansions, Edifices & Guilds and Relics, all within the same Big Box and featuring deluxified components like Collector meeples and metal coins.

Note: the box has the title I C E: Collector
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Available at SPIEL Essen ’24. Add more variety to your Spotlight games with this exclusive Promo Cards Pack!

The deck adds 5 new search cards.

Includes two new sets of ducks to play up to nine players, a new special card for the Draw deck, and reminder cards for the discard area limit for each player count.

You will find many amazing ducks in this pack drawn by many differents illustrators!

@ Adrien Journel, @ Fred Navez, @ Cyrille Bertin, @ Dominique Mertens, @ Antoine Lanau, @ Aoulad, @ Cyril Bouquet, @ Alex Bonvalot, @ Debbie Ridpath Ohi, @ Antoine Petit, @ Hervé Marly, @ Eva Madenoglu, @ Cédrick Caumont, @ Julien Delval, @ Ismaël, @ Philine Wollast, @ Koda, @ Maud Chalmel, @ Quentin Greban

Component upgrades for the original Above and Below!

Shipped in a tuckbox. Components will fit in the original Above and Below game box.
Includes:

     50 metal coins
     22 Solo Mode cards
     10 wooden cider barrels
     20 wooden potions
     4 wooden torches
     1 wooden moon

Features 3 hero upgrade chips: air defense, auto-retaliate, steal Source, as well as landmark and market chips, 2 large isle hexes, and five new solo scenarios that conclude the story of the Joining War.
family: Theme: Floating islands in the sky

description:
Kawarimi is a game where 4 ninja clans fight to defeat their opponents.
In kawarimi, you build a base of 3 to 5 facedown cards to hide your ninja among traps and decoys. The interesting part is that you can choose the stealthy way and leave your ninja in your hand.

The objective is to find and eliminate the opposing ninjas using deception, prediction, and memory.
Each player in his turn can perform 1 of 3 actions, 
Aim to discover: Ask an opponent to show you a card from their base. 
Attack: Use an attack card to remove a card from the opponent's base.
Accuse: Ask your opponent to show you his hand, if the ninja is there he loses the game.

If a player aims and discovers your ninja, you will be exposed to losing the game with an attack card, it is time to defend yourself with the KAWARIMI.
With this card you can collect all the cards of your base, mix them with the cards of your hand, and make a new base, therefore eliminating all the information that your opponents might have about the location of your ninja.

The game ends when there is only one ninja left, who takes the honor to his clan.

-description from publisher

Welcome back to Furroughfield! And you’re here in time for our favorite time of year. The leaves are changing, the days are getting shorter, and the smell of blue-ribbon pies is in the air. It can mean only one thing: The Fall Festival is in town, and it’s the commonwealth’s largest and most beloved event as we come together to admire the craft and creativity of our neighbors.

This expansion brings all-new locations to our little town, where festival-goers can earn a few extra coins, harvest their fields, and get Fall Festival favorites for new actions, benefits, and ways to score. Plus you’ll bring home the finest pies – blueberry, pecan, pumpkin, strawberry-rhubarb, and Furroughfield’s famous sugar cream!

This fall, fall in love with Harvest all over again.

—description from the publisher

In another world, a land of fantasy…
On a continent of sword and sorcery, there were five royal families. Five kings competed and fought for hegemony over the continent. According to the ancient doctrine, each king formed three armies, bearing their own crests, and threw themselves into fierce warfare. Eventually, one of the royal families won the battle and ruled the continent. Time passed, though, and the king’s days were numbered.
„Oh, descendants of the four old families! Gather your three sacred crests, and bring them to the castle. He who bears his crests first, shall ascend the throne.” – with these last words, he passed away.

As a descendant of a royal family, you must gather your crests before others assuming the royal name. The first player to have three crests of the same type shall be the victor, inheriting the throne. As soon as this occurs, the game ends.

A cardgame about struggling for the throne set in a world of fantasy. Your objective is to keep fighting and gather your crests for victory.

Cubattle is a tactile game played with cubes that stick together [with velcro]. The game can evolve and grow into fantastic structures. There are two colours; one for each player and the strategy of the game is to expose your colour and hide your opponent's. In the process a player must score a run in one's colour. A "run" is two or more of the same colour in a row without the interruption of your opponent's colour. A run can run along one face and go up and over the cube and onto the board or next cube. Scoring more points than your opponent wins the game.

Pumpkin Rally is a 1v1 strategy game where two rivals—Harlie the dog and Booboo the ghost—embark on an intense quest to collect pumpkins in a spooky, mystical realm.

As a player, you will pick a character and navigate your way through this haunted landscape. You'll shape the road map to reach pumpkins scattered across different carnival sites, set clever traps to hinder your opponent, and plan the safest and fastest route to carry the pumpkins home. Facing haunting creatures and thrilling obstacles, only the most strategic and cunning will succeed in the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Disney Lorcana: Mickey Mouse Musketeer Gen Con Promo Card
description:
Promo card from Gen Con 2023

Bodyguard

All for One: Your other Musketeer characters get +1 strength.

Rock & Roll Brouhaha is an asymmetric, customizable, expandable card game, in which players assume the role of band managers who are tasked with assembling musicians, buying musical gear, looking for gigs and scheduling venues for their bands.

Managers each play their own deck of cards that represent musicians and supporting personnel, venues, gear, and actions.  Resouces are produced by venue cards, or by sacrificing a card in hand to become a salary card.  Resources are used to play all other cards, to activate certain card abilities, or to pay for expenses when musicians attempt to perform a gig.  The effects of action cards are resolved immediately.  Other cards remain in play until another effect removes them from play (discarded cards go into the retirement pile).

There is a separate card that tracks the volume level.  The manager of the "chill" deck has cards that benefit from a lower volume level, while the "forte" manager benefits from higher volume level.

Play progresses through various phases:

     The draw phase, where managers each draw 1 card from their deck
     The gig phase, where managers combine venue cards with cards and then assign musicians to those sets of cards in an attempt to score points from the gig.
     The main phase, where managers can play musicians, venues, salary and gear from their hands.
     The payout phase, where venues and salary cards produce resources.
     The end phase, where certain card effects are triggered and cards that were used during this round are reset.


Additionally, there are 5 activities to which a musician or personnel card can be assigned during the gig phase.  These activities have an immediate effect, such as card drawing, additional resource production, or adjusting the volume level.      These activities add a worker placement flavor to the game, as only one card can be assigned to an activity in a particular round.

Victory points are scored when a group of musicians achieves the requirements on a gig card to which they are assigned.  Whichever manager can score 20 victory points first is declared the winner.

boardgame: The Naval SITREP: The Journal of Naval Miniatures Wargaming #67
description:
The Naval SITREP is published twice a year by the Admiralty Trilogy Group. It supports the Admiralty Trilogy game system with scenarios, articles on history and technology, and rules expansions & corrections. 

The Naval SITREP Issue #67 was published in October of 2024. 

Features

Mighty Ike’s Battle Axe Guarding the Red Sea

The US Navy’s New EW Pod
