family: Magazine: The Naval SITREP – Journal of Naval Miniatures Wargaming

description:
In the land of Storia, a Traveler is in search of a happy ending – and you get to imagine how they get there! The players choose a card to be the Traveler, and the first player begins the story. As they tell the story, the first player flips over three cards and use the three magic coins to mark which card they think should be in the story next – so only they can see. The other players guess which card they think the storyteller chose. A wrong guess means the inkwell marker goes down a notch. As a team, try to tell the tale before the inkwell runs dry! As each player imagines their part of the story, they roll the booster dice to add exciting elements that they must work into the tale. Have fun, use your imagination and work together to reach a happy ending before the inkwell runs out of ink. It’s a cooperative game that fans of fantasy, creative or role-playing games will love playing with friends and family.

     Use three Magic Coins to guide your traveler through an adventurous tale that you tell together!
     Cooperative games empower young learners to learn teamwork and basic game play
     Kids practice communication, group strategy, creativity and storytelling
     Play again and again…and again! It’s a different story every time!
     Fun for groups of mixed ages
     Use in the classroom for lessons on cooperation and story structure


—description from the publisher

Chicken Fight is a card game inspired by the classic pool game, reimagined with an actual chicken and eighteen other super-powered birds. Each bird is assigned a number that determines its pecking order (strength in a fight), allowing higher-numbered birds the ability to knock off lower-numbered ones.

Every bird also possesses a unique ability, from knocking off a rival’s top bird to swapping places with it. These abilities can expose weaknesses in stronger birds, shake up the game, or alter its course. Master when to play, discard, and engage your birds in combat to transform your flock into a formidable fleet.

The goal in Chicken Fight remains true to its namesake: be the player with the last stack standing to claim victory. Whether you win or lose, you'll enjoy this game that blends nostalgic charm with strategic depth.

Don’t be a chicken, fight!

-description from designer

family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

description:
Puzzle book with many puzzles awaiting to be solved.

CASE STORY

The forgotten diary of some Romanian explorers hides the greatest secrets of an isolated cave in the Carpathians. Inexplicable phenomena, mythical creatures and amazing discoveries crown the expedition recorded in the pages of this journal. Follow in the footsteps of the three explorers and experience the adventure first hand! Nothing will be the same again!

POVESTEA CAZULUI

Jurnalul uitat al unor exploratori români ascunde cele mai mari secrete ale unei peșteri izolate din Carpați. Fenomenele inexplicabile, creaturile mitice și descoperirile uimitoare încoronează expediția consemnată în paginile acestui jurnal. Calcă pe pașii celor trei exploratori și trăiește aventura pe pielea ta! Nimic nu va mai fi la fel!

boardgame: Dylan Dog: L'alba dei morti viventi
description:
Collaborate and complete a mission inspired by the first historical Dylan Dog album.
Choose a character and, by moving your pawn on the board, try to discover the mystery behind the death of Dr. Browning, eliminate all the zombies and foil Xabaras' diabolical plan.

After successfully completing this adventure, new missions of increasing difficulty await you!

Will you be true Nightmare Investigators?

Players will play one of the characters between Dylan Dog, Groucho, Bloch, Sybil and Morgana, and will have to work together by combing the city of London and the village of Undead to find the clues that lead to Xabaras, kill the zombies that protect him and finally defeat him!

Collaborate e portate a termine una missione ispirata al primo storico albo di Dylan Dog.
Scegliete un personaggio e, muovendo la vostra pedina sul tabellone, cercate di scoprire il mistero che si cela dietro la morte del dottor Browning, di eliminare tutti gli zombie e di sventare il diabolico piano di Xabaras.

Dopo aver portato a termine con successo questa avventura, nuove missioni di difficoltà crescente vi attendono!

Sarete dei veri Indagatori dell'Incubo?

I giocatori interpreteranno uno dei personaggi tra Dylan Dog, Groucho, Bloch, Sybil e Morgana, e dovranno collaborare setacciando la città di Londra e il villaggio di Undead per trovare gli indizi che portano a Xabaras, uccidere gli zombie che lo proteggono ed infine sconfiggerlo!

Embark on an adventure to rescue the unicorns.

Players take turns spinning the dial. If it lands on a color move to the next instance of that color. If it lands on a rainbow, draw a rainbow card. Rainbow cards will either have you move 1-2 spaces of a specific color and prompt you to make different animal sounds while you move.

If players are lucky enough they may find secret paths to take acting as a short-cut but be careful they can also bring you backwards.

Step into the untamed Wild West with Armas Salvajes, a fast-paced, action-packed skirmish game designed for quick and deadly shootouts. In this game, players control gunslingers, bandits, and lawmen, facing off in a battle to be the last one standing.

Armas Salvajes is a simple yet strategic wargame that uses a standard deck of playing cards for its core mechanics. The rules are concise enough to fit on just two playing cards, making it easy to learn and quick to play.

boardgame: Бестиарий Сигиллума. Коллекционное издание (Bestiary of Sigillum: Collector's Edition)
description:
Bestiary of Sigillum: Collector's Edition contains the base game and two expansions as well as some rule changes.

The game has neither random events, nor hidden information, which makes it a great battle of wits, which couples greatly with impressive replayability, strategic depth and room for all kinds of tactical decisions.

In Bestiary of Sigillum you take on the role of summoners in the Kingdom of Two Castles. To play the game, you choose and summon your force of otherworldly heroes. Each character has a distinct combat role with a unique set of abilities which, when combined well, will compliment and reinforce other heroes.

The main goal of the game is to destroy the opponent's castle. In order to do so, you should control siege towers placed on the game board, and destroy enemy characters. You should carefully think through every move, keeping an eye on the opponent’s actions and pick characters paying attention to their strengths and weaknesses.
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
All children want to eat cookies... but eating too many can lead to problems.

Battle of the Cookies is a trick-taking game that can be played by children aged 7 and up, in which your aim is to collect pieces of cookies by winning tricks, which can bring you points at the end of the game.

The cookie pieces come in 4 different types: 3 yummy and 1 spoiled, and you need 4 pieces of the same type to make a complete cookie. The cookie pieces are taken from a hidden pile and kept secret by the players.

The game is based on the usual trick-taking mechanism, but the lower-value cards, although they are unlikely to ensure that the trick is won, bring spells for the players.

Spells are single-use effects that can be played at any time for various purposes, such as stealing cookies pieces of other players.
At the end of the game, the players reveal the cookie pieces they have taken. Each yummy piece brings points and complete cookies (4 pieces of the same type) bring bonus points. Spoiled pieces bring negative points.

The player with the second highest score wins the game, as the one with the most points got a stomach pain from eating too many cookies.

These rules came out of a vague sense of dissatisfaction with historical wargame rules in general. While researching historical battle reports, I continuously came across the situation that the side that kept its reserve uncommitted the longest usually held a great advantage. Unfortunately, this is the opposite of how wargames work. Usually, anyone who keeps a reserve is a fool, because your opponent, with all his troops in line, beats you piecemeal

So I set about trying to create a set of rules to allow for this problem, and reward those who kept a reserve. The rules grew and became more and more grand tactical in concept as I added new ideas. While perhaps not unique, these rules should generate a game slightly different from most you've played. The different modules, Volume of Fire, Zone of Observation, Officer Ability and others, were created independent of each other for some of my other rule sets, but when I started putting them together in this one, they blended far better than I ever expected. For instance, things like the Grand Tactical Movement plus Zone of Observation went together to create a fog of war effect that seems to mimic hidden movement. In fact, on the tabletop, the location of enemy figures out of sight of any friendly troops should be considered merely their last reported position.

To sum up, these rules are meant to be more than just a collection of game mechanics to play a game, and are actually an attempt to imitate in a simple form what might have happened on a battlefield. They can only succeed in a limited fashion, but keeping this in mind might help when trying to figure out how a rule is meant to work. Ground scale is 1 inch = approx 50 yards and the players are meant to be divisional and/or corp commanders, instead of brigadiers. If you wish to use centimeters instead of inches just double all measurements, unless you plan to use these rules for micro 1/300 scale, in which case just use centimeters.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Usolli: The Strategic Game About Salah
description:
Pronounced “oo-sol-lee”, Usolli thematically captures the experience of trying to do Salah (Islamic daily prayer) on time throughout the day. the game is built on the idea that passing on Islamic heritage and tradition can be a fun and engaging family experience. Usolli features beautiful hand-drawn art with minimal text to create an immersive playing experience. Created using modern board game design principles, Usolli creates pleasant memories associated with Salah and provides an opportunity for parents and educators to talk about the importance of this pillar of faith.

In Usolli, players take turns to one of the following:
- Either move to collect a card,
- Or discard cards to perform a Salah to get points. Praying on time and with Khusyu’ provides bonus points.
Watch out for Event cards that can help or hinder, and be mindful that the clock is always ticking! Just like in real life, you can't pray in advance, so aim to pray on time or make up for it if it's past the time.

To win, you strategically collect cards to perform the 5 daily Salah and obtain Hasanat (or points). The game ends when the Adhan tracker goes past Isha. Once the game ends, players add up their collected Hasanat. The player with the most Hasanat wins!

The default setup is simple with no downtime, perfect for beginner and younger players. An advanced mode offers more in-depth choices during your turn, suitable for older players or those more strategically-inclined. For example, Musafir cards that allow you to combine prayers, Sunnah prayer cards for bonus scoring, and special movement rules.

-description from designer

ZODIAC is a game of formation with own pieces and neutral pieces. The winner is the player who has more pieces (more than 3) when all 6 intersections around the center are filled with pieces.

Board
A star-shaped board with 12 intersections and paths.

Pawns and initial placement
The player places 3 pieces of each of the black-and-white and reverse bull pieces, with his/her color facing up, at the 3 apexes of the large triangle.
Two neutral pieces (red pieces on both sides) are placed at the center of the intersection of the two large triangles.

How to play
The players decide who will play first and who will play second, in an appropriate manner. The game is played one move at a time, alternating.
1: Move any one of your own pieces or a neutral piece.
2: Movement of a piece must jump the piece immediately in front of it (either its own piece, an enemy piece, or a neutral piece) and move it to an empty space beyond it.
3: All pieces jumped by a move are turned over.
4: Neutral pieces move one space on each of the six intersections around the center and cannot move to any other intersection.
5: Neutral pieces that have just been moved cannot be moved back to their original positions on the next turn.
6: Neutral pieces cannot be moved in three consecutive moves.
7: If you move your piece to an intersection other than the six points surrounding the center, you must jump either your piece or an enemy piece. Only neutral pieces may be moved by jumping.
8: Passing is not allowed.
9: If a player's turn loops and a piece is placed in the same position twice, the player who placed the piece loses.

Victory or defeat
The winner is the player with more pieces than his or her opponent when all six intersections around the center are filled with pieces. In case of a tie, the game continues.
The player cannot pass the game, so if there is no legal move, the player loses the game.


自駒と中立駒を使った陣取りゲームです。中央を囲む６か所の交点全てが駒で埋まった時に、 自駒がより多く（3個以上）配置しているプ レーヤーが勝ちとなります。

【ボード】
12個の交点と経路からなる星型ボードです。

【持ち駒と初期配置】
白黒リバースブルの駒を各々3個を自分の色を表にして大きな三角形の頂点３か所に配置します。
中立駒（両面赤駒）２個は大きな三角形の交点中央の２か所に配置します。

【プレーの仕方】
適当な方法で先手、後手を決めます。ゲームは交互に１手づつ行います。
1：自駒または中立駒の任意の１個を動かします。
2：自駒の移動は必ず直前にある駒（自駒、敵駒、中立駒のいづれか）をジャンプしてその先の空きスペースに移動させなくてはなりません。
3：移動によってジャンプされた駒は全て裏返ります。
4：中立駒は中央を囲む６か所の交点上を１スペース移動します。６か所以外の交点には移動できません。
5：直前に移動した中立駒を次の手番で元の場所に戻すことは出来ません。
6：中立駒を３手番連続して動かすことはできません。
7：自駒を中央を囲む６か所以外の交点に移動させる場合は、必ず自駒または敵駒のいづれかをジャンプしなくてはなりません。 中立駒のみをジャンプして移動することは 出来ません。
8：パスは出来ません。
9：手番がループして２度同じ配置になった場合は配置プレーヤーの負けとなります。

【勝敗】
中央を囲む６か所の交点全てが駒で埋まった時に自駒がより多く配置しているプ レーヤーが勝ちとなります。同数の場合はゲームは継続します。
パスは出来ませんので合法手がなければ負け となります。

—description from the publisher

The fast-paced, quick-thinking party game of stealth and speed. Players race to collect five animals by being the first to grab the cheeky tile that the caller shouts out. But as you collect more animals, you gain a target on your back as players start trying to steal your cards from under your nose! The winner of the tile calls out the next animal for everyone else to grab.

If you claim your fifth animal, don’t forget to shout “HANDS OFF MY..” and the animal you collect, and win!

Pawns
Each player has 4 white and 4 black soldier pieces and 1 king piece (total of 9 pieces). Enemies and allies are separated by the shape of squares and circles.

How to play
First, the player decides on the first move and the second move, and makes the initial placement.
Kings are placed in the base square (the square with the sun). Troop pieces can be placed in any of the two squares in the front row. However, you may not place six of your pieces in a straight line, including the initial placement. If you do, you lose the game. In the official rules, the first and second players place their pieces one by one, but in the local rules, if both players agree, they may place their pieces at once.

The first player and the second player take turns moving their pieces. No passing is allowed.
Each piece can move one square diagonally.
If you put your red pieces between your opponent's pieces (red and black king pieces), you can take all enemy pieces between them. However, if there is a piece of your own between the red and black pieces, it is not considered to be sandwiched.
If you put an enemy black or king piece between your red piece and the enemy's red piece, you can take one of the trapped pieces.
The same applies to black pieces.
A king piece can take any number of enemy pieces (black and white commander pieces), regardless of which color they are placed between the king piece and the red or black piece if the king piece is one of your soldiers. However, if there is a piece of your own in between the two, the king piece is not considered to be in between the two.
Kings cannot be taken with enemy soldiers' pieces or kings' pieces between them.

Victory or defeat
When a king piece is taken, the game is lost. If the king piece reaches the enemy's base square, the player wins.


久しぶりに本格的対戦ボードゲームを思い付きました。ここ1年はパズル的なゲームが多かったので、ガッつり系の２人対戦ゲームは久しぶりの考案です。
きっかけは1ヶ月程前にギガミックス社のクアルトという４目並べゲームを見た事。このゲームには駒に敵味方の概念が無く駒の持っている要素で４つ並べることが面白いゲームです。
そして先日のデザインフェスタに出展していた大橋新さんが考案した挟み将棋みたいなゲームです。身方の駒が敵対する２つの要素からなり、同じ要素駒だけが敵を挟むことが出来るとても面白いアイデアのゲームでした。

さて、この２つのゲームのアイデアがミックスされて、ちょっと新しいゲームが頭に浮かびました。
概要はこんな感じです。
赤い部族と黒い部族が２人の王様の家臣に別れて支配され、挟み将棋のように戦うゲーム。同じ色の部族同士は敵味方関係なく強力して挟めるのがポイントです。
自分の中に敵がいて、敵の中に身方がいるというちょっとややこしいゲームです。赤い部族にとって敵の赤い部族は敵でもあって味方でもある。黒い部族もしかり。なんてパラドクシカルなルールなんだ。
後にこのシステムを「Dual」と名付けました。
一応ゲーム名は『R＆B』（アールアンドビー）。リズムアンドブルースの音楽みたいだけどRed&Blackの略だよ。なんて単純！

『基本ルール』

【持ち駒】
お互いに白4個と黒4個の兵隊駒と王様駒1個（合計9個）が持ち駒です。敵味方は四角と丸の形状で別れています。

【プレーの仕方】
最初に先手後手を決めて、初期配置をします。
王様駒は基地マス（太陽が描かれたマス）に配置します。兵隊駒は手前２列のマスに自由に配置出来ます。ただし、初期配置も含めて横一直線状に自分の駒を6個並べてはいけません。並べた場合は負けになります。 正式ルールでは先手後手の順に１個づつ配置して行きますが、ローカルルールではお互いに了解すれば一度に並べても構いません。

先手後手交互に駒を動かします。パスは無しです。
どの駒も前後左右斜に１マス動けます。
自分の赤の駒同士で相手の駒（赤黒王様駒）を挟むと、挟んだ全ての敵駒を取れます。ただし挟んだ間に自分の駒が居る場合は挟んだことになりません。
自分の赤の駒と敵の赤の駒で敵の黒または王様駒を1個挟むと、挟んだ駒を1個とることだできます。２個以上挟んだ場合は取れません。
黒の駒の場合も同様です。
王様駒は自分の兵隊駒なら赤黒どちらの色の駒と挟んでも敵の駒（白黒指揮官駒）を何個でも取る事ができます。ただし挟んだ間に自分の駒が居る場合は挟んだことになりません。
王様駒は敵の兵隊駒、王様駒を使って挟んで取ることは出来ません。

【勝敗】
王様駒が取られた時は負けです。王様駒が敵の基地マスまで到達すれば勝ちです。

—description from the publisher

Help us pay more non-voters to give a shit by getting our all-new 2024 Election Pack, featuring 30 fresh cards about botched assassinations and weirdos who want to force women to become broodmares for the state. 100% of profits fund apologies.

-description from publisher's website

In this cooperative game, players have a limited time to find, by touch and feel in a large shared cloth bag, some of the letters needed to write the name of the two animals on their card, a simple one with few letters and a difficult one with more letters.
The players then together try to find the name of the other players' animals.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Heaven's Will: The Attack on Ulithi Atoll, November 1944
description:
Heaven’s Will: is a solitaire game on the Japanese suicide Kaiten (Japanese for “turning heaven’s will”) mini-submarine attack on the American fleet’s anchorage at Ulithi Atoll in November, 1944. The Japanese planned the raid to disrupt the American’s upcoming invasion of the Japanese home islands. The original plan was for three submarines to ferry and launch a half dozen of the Kaiten mini-submarines at the atoll. Given the successes of the aerial Kamikaze attacks against American ships, much was expected of this underwater “special attack”.

The active player takes on the role of the Japanese Kaiten midget submarines. You must endeavor to overcome the American defenses, night-time visibility and poor pre-raid intelligence gathering to inflict as much damage as possible before being sunk or escaping.

A variant for the game, that includes 4 additional counters and 1.5 pages of rules, assumes that the March 11, 1945 Operation Tan 2, the longest range Kamikaze attack of the war, occurred at the same time as another Kaiten attack on Ulithi Atoll.

Each copy of Heaven’s Will is composed of the following:

•    One 8.5 by 11 inch map with game record keeping tracks.
•    29 double-sided units
•    3 pages of rules

Players will also need a standard deck of playing cards and a six-sided die (D6) to play the game.

-description from designer

From the saga of the lost shaman, Talisman in the mist is a solo RPG where you need to fight against monster to find the key and the way out from the jungle maze. Using the maze brain system, it places monsters, traps and treasures within the maze to create ever-changing environments.
In this game you play the role of a young shaman who must find a way out of a jungle/labyrinth.

For each game turn you move your pawn through a room and face adversities (monsters, traps).
In addition to this you can arm yourself with 6 types of weapons (from dagger to powerful magic).
You can take advantage of some special action types that upgrade your weapons or allow you to heal yourself.
At the beginning roll the dice to see what your level of strength, dexterity and will is. These are necessary skills for handling weapons and if they reach zero you will have lost.

How to win the game: Try to kill the monster that is hiding the key and at that point try to find your way out by going through the various rooms.
The important thing is to know that the game takes place between paper and device on which the situation is plotted and there are skill counters, coin counts and duels with monsters.

For each duel, you must use physical dice and match them with the dice thrown by the monster on the device. compare the values and see how much damage you have inflicted on the monster (greater or equal value wins).

—description from the designer

Print & Play Boardgame about the 1941 Insurgent siege of the German garrison of Kraljevo in occupied Serbia. Download and print out the Map file in A2 format and use an ordinary deck of Cards, with the 4 Card symbols denoting Insurgents (Hearts), Germans (Clubs), Time (Spades) and Chance (Diamonds).

Play as the INSURGENTS (Yugoslav Army/Chetniks with Communist Partisans) and try to liberate Kraljevo and take its garrison as POW, or as the GERMANS, and hold out until relief, or Chetnik-Partisan war, which will surely erupt.

The gameplay takes place on the 6 Map Spaces - 3 outside Spaces are controlled by the Insurgents, while the 3 inner Spaces (Agricultual School, Airport and the City of Kraljevo itself) are initially controlled by the Germans. The goal of the game is to control as many of inner Spaces, with the City of Kraljevo being the most important - Insurgents immediately win if they control it.
 Combat takes place between linked Spaces, with Players placing Cards face down during the first phase of the Turn, followed by drawing the Time Cards and consulting the Effects (German or Insurgent Reinforcements, Insurgent disunity, etc.). Finally, each Player has 2 rounds to initiate Combat or Move Cards. The Cards are revealed only when Combat is initiated. Chance Cards are used as a Combat Bonus. The goal is to have a total of 8 or more higher than the opponent, who then has to Discard a Card.

Good luck!

RUN FROM THE DINO TONGUE: Help hungry cavepeople hunt for food while dodging the legendarily hungry Snack-O-Saurus Rex. Snag a snack or get knocked back in this game that’s tongues of fun!

HUNT AND GATHER FUN: For players who love snacks, jungle adventures, and dodging dino tongues to collect the yummy snacks, like tasty taco-dactyl and prehistoric pizza, along their path.

SNACK PACK: Move your hungry caveperson around the board to land on spaces and collect snacks. Use Snack-O-Saurus' tail to launch his dino tongue to score more snacks or at other cavepeople to make them drop theirs. Collect two pairs of snacks to win! Be careful: the food-loving dino will grab extra snacks when you launch his super-long tongue. Aim the dino at the snack to snatch a treat or aim at an opponent to send them back!

Includes:
- 1 Snack-O-Saurus Rex (dino & feet)
- 1 Base
- 1 Base Connector
- 1 Gameboard
- 20 Snacks (4 of each type)
- 4 Cavepeople
- 1 Die
- Instruction Sheet

In Lazarus Rising Games' modern revival of the classic card game, OverPower sees players assembling a team of 4 characters and building a custom deck containing associated Special cards for their team, numerical Power cards, and other tactical actions.

Each character possesses a "power grid" which defines their proficiency in the areas of Energy, Combat, Brute Force, and Intelligence -- represented by a numerical value from 1 - 8. Characters also have a Threat value which determines how many points they count towards for assembling a legal team less than or equal to the maximum point threshold. Some characters may also have Inherent Ability text which affords the character an additional, ongoing effect.

The game is played across shared rounds, where players draw 8 cards, encounter various Events which change the landscape of the impending battle, and can strategize by "placing" a number of cards to each of their characters as a way to save them for future battles and build card advantage. Battles consist of alternating actions between players, where the active player makes an attack from one of their characters to an opponent's character, using either cards from hand or placed cards. Attacks then open a response window for the opponent who can choose whether or not to defend the attack. Then, priority is passed to the next player who can make an attack or even concede the battle. A character can be knocked out of the game should they receive 20 or more points of damage, or hits with 3 or more of the 4 power types (Energy, Combat, Brute Force, Intelligence). At the end of the battle, the total value of successful attacks is calculated to determine the victor of the match.

A unique aspect of OverPower is the Venture system. Each round prior to the battle, players must wager a number of their 7 Mission cards, which function similar to poker chips, based on how confident they are that they'll win the upcoming battle. The winner of each battle, determined by how many successful points were scored by their team, then wins the number of Mission cards they wagered at the start of the round. Players can win the game either by knocking out all of their opponent's characters, or by successfully advancing all 7 of their Mission cards.

—description from the publisher

Put down your cards and be ready to wave at the missionaries, shake the bishop's hand, or do another fun action.  Make sure you're not the last one!

Deal out all the cards, as evenly as possible.

Players play the top card on the center of the table.  Players around the table play their top card as well until an action card is played.  Every player much preform the action that is associated with the card.

Missionaries - Wave
Mom - Give yourself a Hug
Bishop - Pretend to shake hands
Church Teachers - Cup hand to ear to show you are listening.

Last player to begin the action is the loser and takes all cards on the table.

Get rid of your cards first and win.

A one-card mini-expansion, the Depot, adding an extra threat that must be defeated to win the game. The card is now included with new copies of the game. The card can also be purchase separately to be added to one’s first edition copy of the game in a bundle with Chemistry Set, Coincidence’s new game.

CONNECTOMANIA is a simple, yet strategic, tile-placement game where two to four players connect tiles such that five-in-a-row wins.

COMPONENTS:
•80 colored tiles in four colored sets of 20, some with symbols
•8 secret symbols.

SETUP:
1.Shuffle the tiles and secret symbols.
2.Give each player their complete set of 20 tiles, face up.
3.Give each player two secret symbols.
4.A random person places one of their tiles on the table.
 
GAME PLAY:
Players take turns placing one tile at a time. Tiles must be placed adjacent to any existing tile, edge to edge or corner to corner. When a secret symbol is used, a tile matching the symbol can be placed on top of that tile.

Secret Symbols: 
Secret symbols are used for stacking tiles. You place a tile matching the symbol on top of an opponent’s tile with the same symbol. The secret symbol is then placed on top of the tiles. If available, grab another secret symbol. 
With more than two players, it is possible to stack more than two tiles.

Winning a Round: 
One point goes to the first player to get five of their tiles in a straight line. Start another round.
 Winning the Game: 
The first player to win two rounds is the winner.

STRATEGY: 
•Avoid playing tiles with symbols at the end of the game. Playing them early minimizes surprise wins. 
•Stack a tile to break up a possible win by an opponent.

-description from designer

This is a variation of Dama (a.k.a. Checkers or Draughts) that is unique to the Philippines. The board appears quite different from the usual checkerboards, but the arrangement of spaces is the same as the squares used in the more common game. Though most of the rules are familiar, there are some exceptional powers for the Kings - long moves and long jumps - that make the latter half of the game very exciting.

Ahoy mateys! Command your dragon pirate crew to plunder the Islands of Numbers in this thrilling strategy math game.

Multiply your dragon pirates' numbers to match or get closest to the island's number without going over. Claim treasure chests and raid opponents for their gems to amass the greatest hoard. Victory awaits the savviest captain who masters this multiplication game and strategic plundering!

—description from the publisher

This is a small peg-and-dice game that challenges two players to a race. Players roll 2d6 then choose one of them for their movement. Should you pace yourself and hold back? Burst ahead to take the lead? Stay with the pack? The trick is that the first player in every round is the player in last place. The race runs from the "city gates" in one corner, follows a parallel course of two rows of spaces to the "tower" at the opposite corner, then back again - a total of 24 spaces each way by the shortest course. The first player whose peg returns through the city gates wins the race. (Zavod is a simple race game for kids, a fun game for travel or family outings.)

-description from designer

Mall cop is a two-player game in which one player takes on the role of a mall cop while the other player takes on the role of a wily shoplifter.

As the mall cop, you will use your investigation tools to track down the shoplifter, but be careful, they may be trying to fool you!

As the shoplifter you must carefully watch the mall cop to determine if it is safe to leave the mall with your pilfered items or perhaps, try to bait them into falsely accusing you.

As the Mall Cop your player pieces will always be visible on the main board. 
 You will investigate decks to deduce if items have been taken or is the Shoplifter just browsing.
 You will use drone and check zone actions to narrow down where the Shoplifter is hiding. 
 You will use your Mall Cop and Dog pawns to search for the Shoplifter. 
 You will make agonizing choices, choosing when to use your limited STOP door guard cards.

As the Shoplifter your player piece will mostly be hidden, played on a mini map of the mall behind a screen. You will only reveal your player piece if you are found by the Mall Cop or leave the mall. You will however give clues to your location through play, by stealing or swapping since you will have to be in a store space (two spaces with a deck of cards between them) and pick the deck of cards up to steal or swap from it. 
 You will Take items from stores (or bluff and take just browsing cards), Swap items previously taken to throw off the Mall Cop. 
 You will try to stay hidden from the Mall Cop player long enough to sneak past the door guard, but did they play a Stop card this turn, do you have the guts to find out?

War-themed card game in which the objective is to eliminate the other players by exchanging cards. The last player standing at the table wins the game. Several games are played until the agreed score is reached.

The game contains cards of different types: To create an army, weapons to attack other players, cards detrimental to your own game and cards that affect player turns.

Once the cards have been dealt, the player will take one more from the deck and discard one against any other player on the table or in the discard deck. These cards can be used to weaken your opponents.

When a player is ready and has the "Bellicus" card in his possession, he can attack any other player. Cards are exchanged and the best player wins. The defeated player leaves the game until the next round.

—description from the designer

family: Series: Battles from the Age of Reason (Clash of Arms Games)

boardgame: Masterpiece: The Third Battle of Kharkov
description:
Masterpiece was the last in a series of battles in early 1943 between Germany's Army Group South and the Soviet Red Army centered in the Ukraine.

Masterpiece - Feb.-Mar. 1943: The Third Battle of Kharkov was the last in a series of battles on the Eastern Front of WW2 between Germany’s weakened Army Group South against the exhausted but still menacing Soviet Red Army. In a virtuoso performance, FM von Manstein skillfully maneuvered his forces across the Ukraine, shattering Soviet forces and leading to the German recapture of both Kharkov and Belgorod.

Postcard map
20 counters
Complexity - Low
Die rolling to resolve combat

A drafting game with positional benefits, you start with a hand of six cards, playing them one at a time, to the right, the left, and the right again. You build up bonuses on the side of the cards that face you, and then add a scoring goal. At the end of the draft, count up all the point categories and scoring goals, most points wins.
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

description:
The British and their allies launched Operation Brevity to both make a spoiling attack against the Axis forces that were besieging Tobruk, and to get into better jump off positions for a future major offensive.

The game factors in the gaps between tank and anti-tank abilities and the organizational approach to war between the two sides, plus the different AFV types. The game models the British Art of War at that time.

Units: Section, Company, Battalion.
Hex size: 3 miles
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
An expansion for the game sixto, with 2 new variants for the game

SIXTO Cross comes with 2 variants. The basic game remains unchanged in both variants. However, there are 3 areas that will be scored as additional columns at the end of the game. You note the points for the extra scores in the respective spaces at the top of the game sheet.

Variant A

Three diagonals are drawn on each of the game sheets. These diagonals also intersect each other, and the intersections count towards the score for both diagonals.

Variant B

There are 3 areas of 6 contiguous boxes marked on the game sheets which will each score as additional columns at the end of the game.

Sixto Hits comes with 2 variants. The basic game remains largely unchanged in both variants. However, there are now 12 boxes that can be crossed off under certain conditions - but these boxes also score bonus points if they are crossed off following "regular" SIXTO rules. It's therefore important to decide whether to cross off boxes quickly or try going for bonus points.

Variant A

There are 12 boxes that show a S. These boxes may be crossed off even if the corresponding die shows a lower number.

However, if the box is crossed off using the exact number shown, you can also cross off a $3 bonus symbol (at the top of the game sheet). Each crossed off bonus symbol will score 5 points at the end of the game.

Variant B

There are 12 boxes that show a These boxes may also be crossed off, when the box directly after it is crossed off.

However, if one of these special boxes is directly crossed off through a die roll, you may also cross off a bonus symbol (at the top of the game sheet). Each crossed off bonus symbol is worth 5 points at the end of the game.

In a charmingly cute dinosaur world, players need to carefully observe and react quickly during dinosaur duels by comparing the size of numbers and making the correct action responses. It's a joyful game designed to enhance focus and train react time.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Q萌的恐龍世界裡, 玩家需要通過仔細的觀察與敏銳的反應在恐龍對決中, 快速進行數字大小比較, 並做出正確的動作反應, 是一款提升專注力與訓練反應力的歡樂遊戲.

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Heroes of the Sanctum: The Strategy Card Game – Epic Expansion
description:
"As the Incursion ravages our lands, we summon more champions to step bravely into the fray.  Let the memory of their sacrifice be eternal." -The Sanctum of the Scribes.

Heroes of the Sanctum: The Strategy Card Game - Epic Expansion introduces 6 new powerful heroes, allowing players to quest with all new strategies!  Also included are new Quests, Regions, Proximities, Loot, the all-new ability to recruit Companions and Multiplayer support with Skill Sheets!

Game Contents:
-6 new Hero cards & Skill Sheets
-2 new Quests and Region cards
-8 new Proximities
-33 new Loot cards
-Heroes can now recruit Companions

boardgame: Heroes of the Sanctum: The Strategy Card Game – Multiplayer Expansion
description:
"Become united in life, undivided in death.  There is none among us who stand alone against the darkness." -The Sanctum of the Scribes.

Heroes of the Sanctum: The Strategy Card Game - Multiplayer Expansion empowers you to undertake quests cooperatively with up to 4 players!  This expansion introduces new mechanisms and features for the Hero Phase to maximise multiplayer experiences as well as 2 new quests!

Game Contents:
-12 Hero Skill Sheets
-12 Multiplayer Stratagems
-4 Action Trackers
-2 Quests
-1 Multiplayer Rulebook

boardgame: Heroes of the Sanctum: The Strategy Card Game – Comic Campaign
description: This Comic Campaign allows players to experience a series of core game Quests inside a seamless narrative.  This campaign is fully compatible with all expansions and game modes.

boardgame: SundayQuest Adventures: Echoes of the Witch Lord
description:
This expansion pack includes thirteen Quests, a selection of Fimir Magic Spells and a number of treasures.

Echoes of the Witch Lord continues where Wizards of Morcar left off. It is assumed that players will have completed and be familiar with Kellar's Keep, Return of the Witch Lord, Against the Ogre Horde and Wizards of Morcar expansion packs to the original game.

Echoes of the Witch Lord is a sequel to the Return of the Witch Lord expansion.

—description from the designer

BECOME A HUNTER!

Cursed Hunter is a game set in a dark fantasy universe inspired by European and Asian legends and folklore. You play a pariah, rejected by society for a past offense, who has become a hunter as a result of troubled circumstances. To earn a meager living and survive on the outskirts of the city of Lys, you hunt down, on behalf of the officials, all humans and creatures that disturb the prosperity of the city and its merchant guilds.

As you explore the lake, you'll discover mysterious and dangerous places, meet unexpected people, and face terrifying enemies.  Your condition as a hunter gives you unusual powers and the ability to confront these dangers, but it comes with a price that must be paid...

Gameplay: Once you've created your character and filled in the character sheet, you'll move on to the Lake map. Each night you'll have 3 action points to spend on travelling, exploring or trying to solve moon hunts. At the end of each night, you can camp to heal yourself, collect food or learn new disciplines.
Each successful hunt will bring you trophies, which you can exchange for banners and copper coins.

The game is extremely replayable and every session is different, thanks to the random events and hunts drawn according to the moon. Challenges and hunts are resolved by rolling dice following an original D6 system.

Victory condition: you'll have 24 nights to collect 10 banners to restore your rank or heal your curses and finally find peace. If fate spares you, you have a slim chance of escaping corruption, ravage, void and inhumanity...

Defeat condition: the games ends after 24 nights or if you get 4 Curses.

—description from the designer

Crown: War of Succession is a competitive board game that combines action point allocation, area movement, and hand management mechanics. Players will take on the role of animal heroes participating in the battle for the sacred forest relic — the Crown.

The game consists of multiple chapters, where each player can take actions during their turn, such as traveling, resting, playing action cards, or using action points to enhance their attributes for more powerful skills. Players will accumulate combat experience, gather resources, and acquire items to strengthen their characters through battles or by conquering settlements.

There are three different ways to win: accumulate 15 victory points, control 6 settlements, or raise your combat skill to the final level.

A promo card that's simply shuffled into the Black Market deck when playing the Advanced Mode of the game.

Ceasefire: Return all agents in both play areas to the box. Players may look at them at any time.

It is a number-matching puzzle game.
It is a little difficult to add up the numbers. Spatial awareness is also required.

The object of the game is to place 12 number tiles on the board in such a way that the numbers do not overlap.
The sum of the numbers on each of the 12 tiles is different.
Odd and even numbers are equal, 6 tiles each. (White is odd, pink is even).
The total number of odd and even tiles is equal to 120.

The players decide who goes first and who goes last by dice roll or rock-paper-scissors.
Next, the players roll a die to determine the game field (if the die is odd, the field is odd).
(An odd number means an odd field, and an even number means an even field. (An even number of even numbers means an even field, which later determines whether the player wins or loses the game.)

The 12 tiles are lined up so that their numbers are visible.

The players play one move at a time, taking turns to play first and second.
Players choose one tile at their discretion and place it on the board.
Up to the fourth tile is placed in any available space on the board.
Tiles must be placed in such a way that the same number of tiles do not overlap each other vertically, horizontally, and vertically.
(Tiles can be rotated 360 degrees).

After the fifth tile, a tile is removed from the board and a new tile is placed in the place where it was removed.
The removed tile becomes the player's winning tile.
The game is over when all 12 tiles have been placed.

The player loses the game if he/she cannot find a legal move and cannot place a tile during the game.
The winner is the player who has 4 winning tiles lined up on the board at the end of the game, with no overlapping numbers vertically or horizontally.
If neither of the four tiles can be placed on the board, or if neither of the four tiles can be placed on the board, the winner is the player with the highest total number of tiles won.
If the total is the same, the player with the most tiles on the board (odd or even) from the dice roll before the start of the game wins.
If the total and the number of tiles on the field are the same, the player with the least number of tiles on the field wins.
2014.1.18 The order of victory conditions has been partially changed.


数字合わせのパズル系ゲームです。
ちょっと算数足し算がやっかいです。空間認識力もひつようです。

12枚の数字タイルを縦横の数字が重ならないようにボードに配置するゲームです。
12枚のタイルの数字の合計は全て違います。
奇数と偶数は6枚づつ同数です。（白が奇数、ピンクが偶数）
奇数タイルと偶数タイルの数字の総合計は120で同数です。

ダイスまたはジャンケンなどで先手後手を決めます。
次にダイスを振りゲームの場を決めます。
（奇数がでれば場は奇数、偶数がでれば場は偶数となります。後に勝敗の判定に影響します）

12枚のタイルを数字が見えるように並べます。

先手後手順番に1手づつプレーします。
プレーヤーは任意にタイルを1枚選び、ボード上に配置します。
4枚目まではボード上の空いているスペースに配置します。
この時、タイルの数字が同じ数字が縦横重ならないように配置しなくてはなりません。
（タイルは360度回転させて配置できます）

5枚目以降はボード上のタイルを1枚取り除き、新しいタイルを取り除いた場所に配置します。
取り除いたタイルがプレーヤーの獲得タイルとなります。
12枚のタイルを全て配置した時点でゲームオーバーとなります。

勝敗は途中で合法手が見つけられずタイルが置けなければ負けとなります。
ゲーム終了後に4枚の獲得タイルを、縦横に数字が重ならずにボード上に並べられた方が勝ちです。
どちらも並べられる、または並べられない場合は獲得したタイル数字の合計が多い方が勝ちです。
合計も同じ場合はスタート前に振ったサイコロの場（奇数か偶数）のタイルを多く持っている方がかちとなります。
合計も場のタイル枚数も同じ場合は後手の勝ちです。
2014.1.18 勝利条件順番を一部変更

—description from the publisher

Try to guess the combination you have on your glasses!
All players will discover their characters at the same time.

It already starts with a general laughter! There are hundreds of wacky combinations.

It’s perfect for playing with friends, at home or outside.
Put on your glasses and have a good laugh!

The player who finds their character and action first wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Each player places eight pieces in the two positions in front of him.
The first player and the second player take turns moving one piece at a time.
Pieces can move forward and diagonally one square. They cannot move sideways or backward.
If there is a piece (friend or foe) in the direction of advance, the player can jump that piece to advance.
You can jump any number of pieces in a row, but you cannot jump over a piece if there is no square in front of it.
You may not move a piece once it has entered the opponent's position.
Passing is not allowed. If a player runs out of legal moves to move his/her pieces, he/she loses the game.
The winner is the first player to move all of his or her pieces into the opponent's position.

The interesting part of the game is this,
If you don't think about where to move your pieces into your opponent's position, you will end up with pieces that cannot move into your opponent's position. (You lose).
If you move up faster than your opponent, you may lose the game because you have no legal moves at the end of the game.


各々８枚の駒を手前２段の陣地に配置します。
先手後手交互に1駒づつ駒を動かします。
駒は前と斜め前に1マス進むことができます。横と後ろに動かすことはできません。
進む方向に駒（敵味方とも）があれば、その駒をジャンプして進むことができます。
連続している駒は何個でもジャンプできますが、飛び越えた先にマスが無い場合はジャンプできません。
いったん入った相手の陣地内で駒を動かすことはできません。
パスはできません。途中で駒を動かす合法手がなくなった場合は負けとなります。
全ての駒を先に相手の陣地に入れた人が勝ちです。

面白いところは、
相手の陣地に上がる場所を考えないと、上がれない駒が出来てしまいます。（負け）
相手より早いペースで上がってしまうと、終盤に合法手が無くなってしまい負けてしまうことがあります。

—description from the publisher

Shiver and have fun !

Receive an invitation to a costume party in a haunted castle, but at midnight, mysterious murders occur.

Guests search for equipment to defend themselves in three game modes: ESPRIT ES-TU LÀ ?: uncover hidden identities, GOOD AGAINST EVIL: fight as a team, and SLAUGHTERHOUSE: survive alone.

Dive into this captivating and fun card game, but be prepared to feel terror at every corner of the castle. Unmask the 12 Humans and the 12 Demonic Creatures before becoming a victim. Will you survive this terrifying night ?

—description from the publisher

Pawns
Each player has 3 pieces with dots 1, 2 and 3.

How to move pieces
The first piece is moved to an empty space.
The 2 piece is moved to an empty space.
3 pieces are moved to the empty space.
The pieces cannot move into the spaces occupied by their own or opponent's pieces, so the spaces occupied by their own or opponent's pieces are jumped.
You cannot move a piece onto a space with the same number of dots as the piece.

How to play
The player decides who moves first and who moves second, and takes turns moving pieces. You may move any piece.
A piece is moved from the outside of the board, and is finished when it goes out of the board on the opposite side. The pieces must be perfectly matched to each other in order to advance. No backs are allowed.

Winning and losing
The first player to move all three pieces out of the board wins. If a piece cannot be moved, the player loses the game.


【持ち駒】
各々のプレーヤーはドットが１、２、３の駒3個が持ち駒です。

【駒の動かし方】
１の駒は空きスペースに１つ動かします。
２の駒は空きスペースに２つ動かします。
３の駒は空きスペースに３つ動かします。
自分や相手の駒が入っているスペースには入ることができませんので、自分や相手の駒のスペースはジャンプします。
駒と同じドットの数のスペースの上に駒を動かすことはできません。

【プレーの仕方】
先手後手を決めて交互に駒を動かします。どの駒を動かしてもOKです。
駒はボードの外から動かし、反対側のボードの外に出て上がりです。駒はぴったりでなくては上がれません。バックも出来ません。

【勝敗】
３つの駒全てをを早く上がらせた方が勝ちです。駒を動かせなくなった時はその時点で負けとなります。

—description from the publisher

What you can do on your turn
The first player to place a bridge tile on the board is the second player to play it, alternating on each turn.
The first player to place a tile on the board may either place or remove a bridge tile, place or remove an over-bridge tile, or move a relief piece.
Tiles cannot be placed in the middle of the board. Relief pieces may not return to the center once they have left it.
Once a piece has reached the center, it may move to any place on the center (except for enemy relief pieces) on its turn.
Rescue pieces can move only on polynomino tiles of their own color in straight lines (not diagonally). (They cannot move diagonally).
When a rescue piece reaches a rescue piece, the rescue is successful and the piece is removed from the board and a point is scored.
The monomino tile with the rescued piece removed becomes a normal bridge tile.
You can also rescue an enemy rescue piece. Only in this case, it can enter the enemy's polynomino.
Overbridge tiles can be placed on top of enemy polynominoes to cross them.
It can also be hung diagonally across two polynomino tiles of its own color.
Overbridged tiles (1 enemy tile or 2 of your own tiles) cannot be removed on your turn.

Scoring
Rescued pieces are scored (3 points for the player and 8 points for the enemy from the center). Enemy rescued pieces can also be rescued (1 point).
If you are the first to move up to the enemy's position, you score 5 points. After the enemy player moves up, the second player can continue to play for the number of pieces he rescued.
If he is the second player to go up, he gets 1 point. If not, the player loses the game.

Expansion
When a polynomino tile is placed in all five surrounding squares, the position is considered to have been reclaimed and expanded, and the six squares in the corner become the player's position.

Victory Condition
If a player cannot move up to the enemy's position, he or she loses the game.
If both players move up, the player with the higher total points wins. In the case of a tie, the first player to move up wins.


【手番に出来ること】
配置先手がプレー後手となります。1手番ごと交互にプレーをします。
手番に出来ることは、橋用ポリノミノタイルをボード上に配置する、取り除く、 オーバーブリッジタイルを配置する、取り除く、救援駒を動かすのいづれかです。
中州にはタイルを配置することはできません。一度出た陣地に救援駒が戻ることはできません。
中州に到達した駒は手番で中州上の自由な場所（敵の救援駒がいる場所を除く）に移動できます。
救助駒は自分の色のポリノミノタイルの上のみ縦横直線状に動くことができます。（斜めには動けません）
救援駒が救助駒まで到達した場合は救助成功で救助駒はボードから取り除き得点になります。
救助駒を取り除いたモノミノタイルは通常の橋タイルとなります。
敵の救助駒を救助することもできます。その場合のみ、敵のポリノミノ内に入ることができます。
オーバーブリッジタイルは敵のポリノミノの上に置いて渡ることができます。
自分の色のポリノミノタイルの2つにまたがって斜めに掛けることもできます。
オーバーブリッジを置かれたポリノミノタイル（敵タイル1個、または自分タイル2個）は手番で取り除くことができません。

【得点】
救助した救助駒が得点となります（中州より自分側3点、敵側8点）。敵の救助駒も救助できます（1点）。
敵の陣地に先に上がれば5点を獲得します。 敵プレーヤーが上がった後、2番手プレーヤーは自分が救助した駒の数だけプレーを継続して行えます。
2番で上がれば1点獲得します。上がれなければ負けとなります。

【陣地の拡大】
対角のコーナー1マスが陣地ですが、周辺の5マス全てにポリノミノタイル（敵味方関係なく）が置かれた場合は、 陣地が埋め立てたれ広くなったとみなし、コーナーの6マスが上がりの陣地となります。

【勝利条件】
敵陣地に上がれなければ負けです。
両方上がった場合は獲得した合計点の多い方が勝ち。同点の場合は先に上がった方が勝ちとなります。

—description from the publisher

Pieces to be held
The first and second players hold 10 reversible pieces of the same color, 5 pieces each.

How to play
The first player and the second player take turns to roll the dice. On your turn, you can either place your pieces on the squares with the same numbers as the dice you rolled, or move your pieces on the board. However, you can only move one piece diagonally. If you move a piece and it intercepts an opponent's piece, you can turn it over and use it as your piece, just like in Othello. The center square is a free space. Both players consider that their pieces are placed in this square, but they cannot use the Free Space to insert an opponent's piece and turn it over.

Winning and losing
The first player to line up four pieces in any direction (vertical, horizontal, diagonal) wins.
If a player cannot place or move a piece in a square with the same number as the dice, he or she loses the game. For example, if all the pieces are placed in the square marked “1” and the dice rolls “1”, the player loses.
The player loses the game even if five pieces are placed in a row.


【持ち駒】
裏表リバーシブルの駒10個を先手後手が５個づつ色を決めて駒を持ちます。

【プレーの仕方】
先手、後手順番にサイコロを振りプレーします。手番に出来ることは、振ったサイコロの目と同じ数字が書かれたマスの上に持ち駒を置くか、 盤上に置いてある自分の駒を動かします。ただし駒は前後左右斜に１マスしか動けません。 駒を動かして相手の駒を挟むとオセロのように裏返して自分の駒にできます。 中央のマスはFreeスペースです。お互いに自分の駒が置いてあるとみなしますが、Freeスペースを使って相手の駒を挟んで裏返すことはできません。

【勝敗】
縦横斜めどの方向でも早く4目並べた人が勝ちです。
サイコロの目と同じ数字にマスに駒を置いたり移動できない場合は負けになります。 たとえば「１」のマスに駒が全て置かれたいる時にダイスの目が「1」が出れば負けになります。
5目並んでも負けになります。

—description from the publisher

Pieces to be held
The total number of pieces is 10 pieces, 5 for each of the two players.

How to play
Tiles 1 through 9 are placed face down on the board.
The first player chooses a piece from his or her pieces and places it in the center, then turns over one tile from any board. If it has the same number as the piece, the piece goes up. If the piece hits, play continues. If not, the board tile is turned over and it is the opponent's turn to play. The first and second moves are repeated alternately.
If the turn changes, the same tile on the board can be turned over and over again.
Multiple pieces can be played as a single piece. (For example, 2 and 3 can be used as 5 pieces.)

Win/Lose
The winner is the first player to move up all five pieces as quickly as possible.


【持ち駒】
1～9と先手を決める5の駒で全10個の駒を２人で5個づつ分けます。

【プレーの仕方】
ボードには1～9のタイルを裏返して置きます。
先手は自分の駒から駒を選び中央に置き、任意のボードのタイルを１枚表に返します。駒と同じ数字であれば、駒は上がりです。当たった場合は続けてプレーをします。外れた場合はボードのタイルを裏返して相手のプレーヤーの手番になります。先手と後手を交互にくり返します。
手番が変ればボードの同じタイルをくり返し表に返してもOKです。
複数の駒を１つの駒として出すことができます。（たとえば2と3で5の駒として使えます）

【勝敗】
5つ全ての持ち駒を早く上がらせた方が勝ちです。

—description from the publisher

Promo that provides additional rules for the base game.

Each type of tower gets its own power.

Board
A 7x7 checkerboard is used.

Pawns and Rings
Each player has 7 knight pieces.
The silver piece has 28 rings for a score of 1 point, the blue piece has 6 rings for a score of 3 points, and the big blue piece has 1 ring for a score of 5 points. The total score is 51 points.

Initial Placement
Each player places 7 knight pieces on the two blue squares in the front row alternately.
A large blue ring is placed in the center square, and six blue rings are placed on the center line. Silver rings are placed in the other empty squares. There are no empty squares.

What you can do on your turn.
The first move is decided in an appropriate way. Each turn is played one move at a time.
The player can move one of his or her knight pieces on the board or place an off-board piece on the board. You can only place knights on squares with rings. The knight is awarded the ring and becomes the holder of the knighthood.
A knight piece can move one square in each of the eight directions except for the square where the knight piece is located. If there is a ring on the square where the knight is moving, the knight gains the ring. The ring is placed over the knight's piece.

Acquisition of rings 1
A knight piece that gains at least one ring is promoted to the rank of holder knight.
The holder knight can jump forward, backward, diagonally, left, right, and in eight directions, except for the square in which his piece is located, for a number of squares equal to the number of rings he has earned.
However, the knight cannot move to squares where there are no rings or enemy pieces.

Acquisition of rings 2
If you move a knight piece into a square where an enemy knight piece is, you can take away all the rings that the enemy knight piece has won and keep them for yourself. The ring is placed on the knight piece. A knight piece whose ring is taken is removed from the board and becomes a possession of the opponent. A knight can only be placed on a square with a ring on the board on his turn, and he becomes the holder of the ring. If a piece that does not hold a ring is removed from the board, the piece is out of play.

Game Over.
The game is over when all rings placed on the board have been won by knight pieces. However, if the last ring was won by the first player, the game is over at the next turn of the second player. If there is a legal move, no pass is allowed. The game is over when two players pass in succession.

Victory Condition
The player with more rings at the end of the game wins.
It is not simply the number of rings. The total score is 51 points.
In case of a tie, the game will be a tie. (A tie is possible only when the game is over due to a pass.)


【ボード】
7×7マスのチェッカーボードを使います。

【持ち駒とリング】
各プレーヤーは各々7個の騎士駒が持ち駒です。
その他に35個のリングがあり、シルバーは得点1点で28個、ブルーは得点3点で6個、大きなブルーは得点5点で1個です。 合計得点は51点となります。

【初期配置】
各プレーヤーは各々7個の騎士駒を手前2段のブルーマスの上に交互に配置します。
中心のマスに大きなブルーリングを置き、中央ラインに6個のブルーリングを配置します。 その他の空きマスにはシルバーリングを配置します。マスに空きスペースはありません。

【手番に出来ること】
適当な方法で先手後手を決めます。手番は順番に1手づつ行います。
手番に出来ることはボード上の自分の騎士駒1つを動かす、またはボード外の持ち駒をボード上に配置することです。 騎士駒を配置する場合はリングが有るマスの上にのみ配置出来ます。リングを獲得してホルダー騎士となります。
騎士駒は自駒の居るマスを除き、前後斜左右8方向に1マス動くことができます。 移動先のマスにリングがあれば、それを獲得できます。リングは駒の上から被せます。

【リングの獲得1】
1個でもリングを獲得した騎士駒は昇格してホルダー騎士になります。
ホルダー騎士は自駒の居るマスを除き、前後斜左右8方向に獲得したリングの数と 同じ数のマス分ジャンプして動くことができます。
ただしリングや敵の駒が居ないマスには移動する事ができません。

【リングの獲得2】
騎士駒を移動させて敵の騎士駒が居るマスに入れば、敵の騎士駒が獲得しているリングを全て奪って 自分のものににできます。リングは騎士駒の上から被せます。リングを奪われた騎士駒はボードから取り除き 相手の持ち駒となります。持ち駒は手番の時にボード上のリングが有るマスの上にのみ配置ができ、リングを獲得してホルダー騎士となります。 リングを保有していない駒がボードから取り除かれた場合は、その駒はプレーオーバーとなります。

【ゲームオーバー】
ボード上に配置された全てのリングが騎士駒によって獲得された時点でゲームオーバーとなります。 ただし最後のリングを獲得したのが先手プレーヤーの場合は、次の後手プレーヤーの手番でゲームオーバーとなります。 合法手があればパスはできません。合法手がなければパスとなります。2人のプレヤーが続けてパスをした時もゲームオーバーとなります。

【勝利条件】
ゲームオーバー時点でより多くのリング得点を獲得しているプレーヤーが勝ちとなります。
単純にリング数ではありません。合計得点51点の取り合いとなります。
同点の場合は引き分けとなります。（パスによるゲームオーバーの時にだけ引き分けの可能性があります）

—description from the publisher

Fuer is about expanding the playing space from a central land mass set up.

In Fuer, players add space tiles and add gems and move gems to mark their territory. Players win if you have 4 of your own pieces or 5 of your enemy's pieces in a straight line at the end of your turn.  In some variations, it can be any 5 gems.

If 6 pieces are lined up in a row, the game is lost. You also lose if there is no legal move in your turn.

—description from the publisher

Barrage: The Far Companies expansion includes rules for two new companies to be used in addition to the four base ones and the fifth one already included in The Leeghwater Project expansion. These two companies have unique characteristics that significantly distinguish them from the standard companies both in terms of game mechanics and components.


     The Japanese company uses Bases and Elevations inspired by the “Earth-fill dams”: on one hand, it is possible to use the Elevations as if they were Bases, greatly increasing the number of Dams that can be built; on the other hand, these dams will be limited in height and thus in capacity. Finally, the machinery used for the Elevations are Excavators instead of Concrete Mixers.



     The Brazilian company specializes in versatility: Bases, Elevations, and Conduits can be built in any order from its board, with a greater choice of strength and type of bonuses; however, the elements related to stronger bonuses will result in the permanent loss of one machinery or more.


Both companies unlock a special company ability when the third powerhouse is built.

You are an adventure racer who has entered the most exciting race of your life. It's not just a race; it's a test of skill and endurance that will push the limits of your abilities! While the game is easy to play, it demands all your wit and endurance to overcome your opponents, battle fatigue, and navigate the unforgiving terrain. Are you prepared for the challenge, or will the course spit you out?

Each game unfolds on a different course, presenting unique challenges that require wise decisions to conquer. Will you rise to the occasion, or will you be left behind? This game is perfect for anyone aged 14 and up, ideally suited for 2-5 players. If you're passionate about adventure and looking for excitement on days when the outdoors isn't an option, this board game is for you! Embrace the adventure, test your limits, and discover if you have what it takes to emerge victorious in the most thrilling race of your life!

"Who could have imagined that the entire world once teetered so close to the brink of irreversible catastrophe... Under the shadow of the impending war, a secret project, unrecorded in any history book, was quietly unfolding. Under the direct command of the Leader of the Third Reich, a high-level research department was established. This department's mission was to develop a practical and epoch-transcending flying saucer technology on the basis of current technological advancements, aiming to ensure Germany's future global dominance. They believed that the nation possessing this advanced technology could easily alter the outcomes of future wars... This flying saucer was later named Haunebu."

Haunebu is a tabletop simulation game set in an alternate historical context, featuring both solo and two-player modes. In the two-player mode, one player takes on the role of the main person in charge of the Third Reich's Haunebu secret research project, while the other plays the role of an Allied intelligence agency, with the goal of thwarting the opponent's plans. As the German player, strategic considerations such as concealment and efficiency must be weighed to select a suitable location for establishing a Haunebu flying saucer production and research base, organize internal logistics, and allocate resources wisely for the development and production of these advanced aircraft. However, getting Haunebu operational is no easy task. Players need to advance technology and may even need to form expeditions to collect mysterious energy and special materials from various global mystical sites to enhance Haunebu's performance, from the ruins in the poles to the relics in the dense forests. Once the war breaks out, players must also ensure that secret resource transportation routes across the war-torn continent remain unobstructed. This requires maintaining a semi-competitive, semi-cooperative relationship with other German secret weapons research teams, where exchanging resources can bring strategic advantages such as improving sea transport routes, opening air corridors, or enhancing defenses against Allied attacks to protect the base's operations and efficiency. Players can also develop specialized Haunebu saucers for transport or even control combat-ready Haunebu saucers for testing and combat, gaining favor from the leadership through their military achievements to secure more funding and manpower. As a player of the Allied intelligence organization, you must relentlessly collect intelligence on flying saucer technology and secret materials, using various means to steal or destroy these secret plans.

As time progresses, this covert contest intensifies. Every expedition and every secret battle could decide the future face of the world. In this unseen war, everyone must choose to stand on the side of justice or evil.

Access the Matrix. Dodge the Corps. Stab Your Buddy. For the first time ever, Munchkin enters the world of Shadowrun! In this exciting (and hilarious) version of the modern classic, created in collaboration with Catalyst Game Labs, players will encounter Trolls, Orks, Elves, and Dwarves in a cyberpunk/fantasy-themed future. The new Matrix track gives players a whole new way to hack their way to victory. Are you ready to 'Run, chummer?

Munchkin Shadowrun is packed with 193 cards full of cyberpunk and magic.

In addition to all the great Door and Treasure cards you expect in Munchkin, this set introduces the Matrix, a series of cards with additional gameplay goals that can be used to win the game in a whole new way!

Welcome Paranormal Investigators to Hauntsville, the most haunted town in the world! You have all come here in order to prove yourselves worthy of hosting the new ghost hunting reality show, Eerie Explorers!

Each of you will be investigating paranormal activity in a haunted house at the same time and trying to film a show with the highest VP (viewer points)! Be careful because you only have so much time to investigate before the sun comes up and whichever investigator has the highest VP will then be declared the winner!

Hauntsville is a "Bingo Caller" type flip and draw game, similiar to "Welcome To," where cards are flipped up and players then draw and score on their own individual sheets using the same cards. Players will use polyomino shapes to strategically fill in rooms and create satisfying combos.

-description from designer

Immerse yourself in the warm atmosphere of a Ukrainian village, where every object has its own story and everything preserves folk wisdom. The Rich House  board game offers you a chance to learn about ancestral traditions through exciting game mechanics. Collect family heirlooms, restore order in the owners' home, and become part of a magical world where every detail is important.

In addition to the fun of the game, you will learn something more. The Rich House  develops attention and concentration, allowing children and adults not only to have fun but also to learn Ukrainian cultural values.

The goal of the game is simple: collect as many collections of four items as possible by placing them in front of you and accumulating points. Each move requires not only luck, but also the ability to find a common language with other players. The game ends when all the collections are collected.

Whoever collects their collection the fastest will be the winner and earn the title of the main owner of the house.

By collecting cards, you not only restore order to the house, but also discover the rich world of Ukrainian life, where each collection of objects is a whole story. The game mechanics stimulate communication and involvement of all participants, and the ease of the rules allows everyone to quickly get involved in the process. Who knows, maybe you will be the one to collect the most valuable collections and become the main winner?

-description by the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Earn as much money as possible and as quickly as possible. This is done through assignments, buying shops and/or investing in the bank. This game has various possibilities from easy money making to full area management and bribing police so that one can engage to stop the other player's goal.

You can choose between a fixed board game layout with shops already placed on boards. Or you can create a flexible layout, which allows you to choose how everything is placed.

You can win the game:
By bringing an agreed amount to your own boss.
The certain amount that is determined at the beginning of the game, you have to bring that amount to the Player Chief.
At the beginning of the game, all players decide how much to save for.
Example: If you agreed for $10,000 ($10000 = +-1h of playing time).
Then if the first player collects $10,000 and brings it to his own boss, wins the game.
This can be done by carrying out assignments, buying shops and companies, investing in the bank.
Or by eliminating enemy player boss.
Find enemy boss and try to take it out and you're the last player standing.
But first you have to find the enemy boss, which is only visible to your own player.

—description from the publisher

Dive into the thrilling race of Überhol mich doch! where every turn counts and strategy meets speed! Complete missions, outsmart your friends, and be the first to claim the treasure! With a clever twist that transforms the game into something new, it's a delightful experience for families and friends alike. Who will reign supreme on the path to Treasure Island?

Gameplay:

This competitive game is played over several rounds. Each player starts with five cards, which come in three colors and have values ranging from 1 to 7.

Missions: On your turn, you aim to complete one of three possible missions by playing a single card:


     Value above 3
     Orange card
     Even value


Successfully completing a mission grants you a bonus along with an additional card.

Moving Your Raft: After completing a mission, you can play a set of cards from your hand. These must either be the same color or the same value. Playing these cards allows you to move your raft forward.

Strategic Bonuses: Some bonuses allow you to move your competitors backwards, adding strategic depth to the game.

The first raft to reach the treasure wins the game.

The players take tuns to move a figure. The area covered by the guns (the light green spaces) may not be entered or jumped over by friend or foe. The two flag spaces can be occupied and jumped over by the figures in the same way as the other spaces on the game board.
The figures jump one, two or three spaces straight forward, left or right sideways, left or right diagonally forward but not backward. The jump carries over both free spaces and over friend and foe. The jumped over figures are not removed. You can only jump onto free spaces or spaces occupied by the enemy. In the latter case, the enemy figure is removed.
The figures of the fortress army, after crossing the boldly drawn brown line, also jump backwards on the three rows of spaces behind this line - according to the same rules as forwards. They can also jump back over the boldly drawn brown line once with their full jump distance and knock away an enemy figure standing in the target space while being eliminated too.
The seige army wins if a figure reaches a flag space and stays there till it's next turn. If a figure of the fortress army captures this figure, the fortress army wins.

FlickForce! A competitive dexterity game system. Use your finger-flicking skills to send your fighter ships bouncing off asteroids and planets to attack your opponent's mothership.  But be careful or else your ship will become trapped and sent to the brig until it can be rescued by a skillful shot to the corner.  This fast-paced game includes 12 colorful ship disks in 2 team colors of your choice, 2 motherships, 6 planets, 8 asteroids, 2 home bases with trap doors, 4 corner brig with rescue launch, walls and connectors to turn any table into a tabletop space adventure!  Play the included Space Battle or Strike Force Soccer rules, or make up your own excited game with the colorful, durable components.  3D print-n-play is available to make your own game set.

—description from the designer

"Allmoge paintings" are traditional Swedish folk art characterized by intricate, hand-painted designs typically found on items such as bridal chests and other furniture.

In a game of Allmoge players take turns drafting pattern cards from a central supply to build their own pattern. The central supply is organized in alternating layers of open and hidden cards where the lower layers partially cover the higher layers. Drafting starts from the bottom and then moves up as the cards become uncovered.

Most of the symbols on the cards are placed over the edge, requiring 2 cards with similar symbols at the edges to create a complete symbol.

A round is over when all cards have been drafted, after each round the starting player changes to the left and when all players have been starting player the game is over.

At the end of the game each complete symbol in a player's pattern are worth 1 point and special icons like the Dala horse earns extra points per Dala horse in the player's pattern (1 -> 1 point 2 -> 4, 3 -> 9 and so on), birds (1 point per adjacent symbol) and blue/yellow flowers (1 point per blue flower in the pattern).

The player with the most points win.

boardgame: Viscounts of the West Kingdom: Principi Promo Card
description:
Promo card for the base game originally given out at Modena Play in May 2022.

Ability: Gain 4 Silver when this card drops off your player board
family: Series: The West Kingdom Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Paladins of the West Kingdom: Principi Promo Card
description:
Promo card for the base game originally given out at Modena Play in May 2022.

Ability: Gain 1 Labourer when dismissing Townsfolk Cards
family: Series: The West Kingdom Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Škoda kouzla, které padne vedle: velké rozšíření
description: The big expansion to Škoda kouzla, které padne vedle, called "The Big Expansion", was released during October 2024 and brought 112 new cards to the original game. In addition to the standard 2 player game, these will allow you to play in 3 and 4 player games or in Solo and Co-op modes. The expansion adds new cards and a large number of game modifications to the standard 2 game, including a small expansion to the base deck. Last but not least, it also includes the Sorceress characters that many players have come to look forward to.

A new Action Card, originally available in the Man vs. Meeple S5 Kickstarter campaign.

Action :

     You may make up to 2 exchanges
     Gain 1 clone

Step into a fierce battle of nature! In this card game, each player controls a set of cards numbered 1 through 9, representing different levels in the food chain. Players face off by placing cards face down, concealing their strength until revealed. Higher numbers dominate lower ones, allowing you to capture the previous card and build powerful chains. The game ends when all cards are played.
The player with the highest scoring sets would win the game. With all nine cards played each round, careful planning and keen guessing are key to outsmarting your opponent. Will your cards reign supreme at the top of the food chain?

—description from the designer

Six animals compete in twelve track & field events: 100m, 200m, 400m, relay, hurdles, high jump, long jump, triple jump, pole vault, discus, shot put, and javelin.

Despite the fact that each animal uses a different dice, behaves differently and runs on a specialised lane, they all have the same probability of winning.

During play, the players learn fundamental concepts of statistics (mean, variation, etc).

Moreover, the game also teaches the difference between equality and justice (related to psychology). While all the animals are given the same velocity (translated by the mean), we also need to ensure that they all have the same winning chance by applying some important adjustments.

The simple version includes pure luck. To add some strategy, there is also a complex version by using extra cards (lasso, dust, trap, and dino).

boardgame: Tidal Blades 2: Rise of the Unfolders – Exclusive Scenario Book
description:
The Exclusive Scenarios booklet (and the additional components for those scenarios) were packaged in a plain white box and shipped to backers of the Tidal Blades 2: Rise of the Unfolders Kickstarter campaign.

The book feature five extra scenarios not found in the retail edition. Each one is a stand alone sidequest players can attempt after the main story.

5 Scenarios:
1. Caiman's Revenge - two level map, new creature (Guaracuda), 7 new cards)
2. Panic at Lamara - extra NPC (Miskie), 7 new cards
3. Shifting Sands - new creature (Sandaconda), 7 new cards
4. The Fire Mountain - 3 piece map, extra NPC (Angler), 7 new cards
5. Backer created scenario

5 extra cards that were given as an extra if you bought the game from Delicious Games at Essen Spiel 2024.

It consists of: 2 bonus cards, 1 sack card and 2 special cards.

Electrify invites 2-4 players into the role of skilled electricians tasked with wiring an expansive hotel under construction. In this vibrant and strategic board game, each player competes to lay down the longest and most efficient cable networks while installing essential electrical components in various rooms.

Throughout the game, players draw cable cards and room cards, allowing them to connect rooms to the main power source. To succeed, players must strategically manage their cable supplies, align with specific room requirements, and carefully navigate the board’s connector boxes. Points are earned based on cable length and the completion of room installations.

The game combines a mix of planning, resource management, and a touch of tactical competition, as players work to control the hotel's electrical network. Do you have the skill to illuminate the entire hotel and outscore your fellow electricians?

-description from designer

Sci-Me! logic is a quick card game about logic and scientific publishing. In the game, you are a scientist creating logical expressions that you try to publish. Test your logical thinking and be the first to successfully present your findings!

In the card game Sci-Me!, you are a researcher trying to publish your scientific findings. Since publications are the scientific currency, your goal is to publish and being the first to accumulate three publications. During the game, you need to use your limited time strategically and balance the risk of failure and time spent on each publication.

The main principle of the game is to order cards in a meaningful manner creating true logical statements. During the course of the round a player does two actions from the set of five available actions. To obtain successful publication one needs to form true statement out of the cards in front of them. After the statement is formed, it needs to pass a random check which is harder the shorter the statement is.

—description from the designer

Begurk – The word game where no words are necessarily wrong

Begurk is a competitive word-building game for 2 to 6 players that challenges your vocabulary and strategic skills as you race to complete a lap around the game board. Each turn, players draw random letters and create words to earn points, with special board spaces adding unique conditions to test your creativity.

Game Overview: In Begurk, players take turns drawing letters from a bag and using them to form words on the board. The goal? To craft words that meet board-specific criteria or bonus spaces, earning you points and moving you forward. Certain board spaces present extra challenges – you might need to use specific letters, reach a minimum word length, or meet vowel/consonant requirements. But be careful: if another player doubts your word’s validity, they can challenge it, and you’ll need to consult the chosen reference (like Wikipedia) to confirm!

Mechanics:

Word Building: Players create words with randomly drawn letters.
Race Game: The winner is the first to complete a full lap around the board.
Set Collection: Certain spaces reward using specific letter sets or word patterns.

Whether you’re a wordsmith or simply love a good challenge, Begurk brings a blend of strategy, and linguistic fun to every game night!

-description from designer

Players take turns placing tiles, called mana, starting with a main tile called Mana Gran. The object of the game is to surround or eat your opponent's Mana Gran.

The game is played without a board, but mana can't move or be placed beyond an invisible 5x5 grid.

Each mana can enter Burst Mode for one action, which allows them to move more freely, and in some cases eat an opponent's mana which is returned to their hand.
