description:
The gentle cozy vibes of Park Life make way for more chaos and control!

Park Life: Storm expansion features 5 new mini-expansions:

5 Disaster Warning Cards - If this card wins the sale, everyone who won a trick this hand discards the rest of their cards, including the player who won with this. Other players continue to play.
Lightning Card - When played, whoever wins the trick discards the card they won with instead of keeping it and draws a new card. Whoever played this goes first next round.
Fireworks Card - Everyone wins the card they played this trick, discarding as normal. Whoever played this goes first next round.
Trump Map - Whatever suit wins the trick becomes the trump suit next round. However, the leading player cannot play this suit.
Hedgehog Bus - Alternate play direction each hand.

This expands Park Life: Hedgehog Edition, Park Life: People Edition, or both when merged together, simply by shuffling the cards into the deck.

—description from the publisher

The royal court in Fontainebleau reeks of conspiracy, and it's up to you, René Bianchi, perfumer to Catherine de Medici, to get to the bottom of things. You must use your flair, medical expertise, and appetite for intrigue to find out what is preventing the crown princess from conceiving an heir — and fast.

To start Guilty: Fontainebleau 1543, you'll read the introduction, then place the specified cards on the table. Choose a card, then turn it over and read it. What will you find there? And behind reading, you might have to put your skilled nose to the test with scratch-and-sniff cards that bring history to the present.

Time will keep passing, possibly leading to surprises as you become immersed in the French royal conspiracies of the 16th century, which are based on historical facts. Conduct your investigation, card by card, but time is short and your choices have consequences...

boardgame: 五等分の花嫁 Card Game (5hanayome Card Game/Quintessential Quintuplets Card Game)
description:
What is "The Quintessential Quintuplets"?

"The Quintessential Quintuplets" is a hugely popular romantic comedy written by Haruba Negi.
It was serialized in "Weekly Shonen Magazine" until February 2020, and the total number of copies of the series has exceeded 20 million.
It is also being developed as an anime series.

What is "The Quintessential Quintuplets Card Game"?

A completely new card game released by Bushiroad, based on the original concept and supervision of "The Quintessential Quintuplets" author Haruba Negi.

[About the game elements]
"The Quintessential Quintuplets Card Game" is a one-on-one card game in which players compete with collected cards.
Compete against opposing players alongside the quintuplets!
Enjoy a battle in which the quintuplets approach "Futaro" and become his bride!
You win if you get three brides before the opposing player!

How do I get started?

To play "The Quintessential Quintuplets Card Game", you will need a deck filled with cards.
For first-time players, we recommend the Starter Deck, which comes with a pre-constructed deck.
Once you're more familiar with the game, you can strengthen your deck with booster packs!

—description from the publisher (translated)

「五等分の花嫁」とは？

「五等分の花嫁」は、原作・春場ねぎによる大人気ラブコメ作品。
2020年2月まで「週刊少年マガジン」にて連載し、シリーズ累計発行部数は2,000万部を突破。
アニメシリーズも展開されている。

「五等分の花嫁 カードゲーム」とは？

「五等分の花嫁」原作者・春場ねぎ 原案／監修、ブシロード発売の完全新作カードゲーム。
 [ゲーム要素について]
「五等分の花嫁 カードゲーム」は集めたカードで競い合う1対1の対戦カードゲーム。
五つ子たちとともに対戦相手のプレイヤーと勝負！
五つ子たちが「風太郎」にアプローチして花嫁になっていくアプローチ合戦を楽しもう！
相手のプレイヤーより先に3人の花嫁を獲得すれば勝利！

はじめるには？

「五等分の花嫁 カードゲーム」で遊ぶにはカードを構築したデッキが必要です。
はじめての方には、あらかじめ構築されたデッキが手に入るスタートデッキがおすすめです。
慣れてきた方は、ブースターパックでデッキを強化しましょう！

—description from the publisher

Pigeon Punch puts you in the shoes of a legendary pigeon fighter, so get ready to be the last one standing!

At the start of the game choose one of the fighters, each one has a special ability that determines their play style. Then each player draws 5 cards from the deck.

Each turn you must use one type of card: 

     SUPERCHARGE: add damage to your next COMBO.
     COMBO: main source of damage, each card does one damage.
     BLOCK: your salvation against your opponents' COMBOs
     SPECIAL ATTACK: unique cards that have different effects and can turn the tide of combat.


All cards except the SPECIAL ATTACK cards have kick or punch icons, this is important when it comes to boosting COMBOS and BLOCKING attacks. Easy to learn but hard to master, that's fighting in Pigeon Punch!

—description from the designer

Catharsis Sagas are true solo game boxes that include one character & one boss. If you own the base game, the boss plays up to five players. The game requires strategy, cooperation, and a touch of luck to succeed as the party fights through traps, monsters, and more. You will hunt down the boss while going through an epic narrative. Roll your dice to activate your powers, but be warned, your powers are also your health. You must carefully decide when it's wise to activate a power and how to resolve damage. Spend your morale wisely, or else you and your party will fall to the corruption of Verity.

Contains the hero Kai Stormbringer, Aetherial Swordmaster, and the boss deck Empress Sofiya.

In Lava House your goal is to reach the exit first. If you've ever played Floor is Lava you'll get it in no time. 
Each round you roll your dice which will indicate the amount of balance, strength and momentum you can use. Analyse the different routes on the board and spend these points to advance towards the goal.

At the start of the game and at certain points along the way you will receive special cards to boost your rolls and make your journey easier.

But be careful, because each time you roll, you are gambling on getting the lava icon. If you accumulate 3, you will fall into the lava and you will have to pass the jump test to avoid losing a life. If you lose all your lives, you will be back to square one.

Manage your cards and rolls well and win by being the fastest to reach the finish line!

Park Life is a may follow trick taking game about timing and hand management.

People Edition added set collecting and tableau building, profiting from opportunity, and adapting to other player's strategies. (Note that People Edition was designed for 'gamers' while Hedgehog edition was streamlined for 'families' and 'hardcore trick taking fans'.)

People Edition Deluxe adds 5 more set collecting cards and 7 more wild cards, creating more risk and rewards. Not designed for first time players. 
Deluxe also adds a berry token mini-expansion that lets players give up their token to discard one less card.
The Deluxe rulebook include modes for solo against an automaton, 8 player games, and more!

Park Life: People may be combined with Park Life: Hedgehog to play with up to 8 players, competitively or cooperatively.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Maretak / Bostulp Promo Card
description:
A promo received with the Dutch version of the expansion Woodland Edge.

Maretak: 0 points for this tree, play a bird. 1 point for every plant. 
Bostulp: 3 points.

Dr. Paulina Green, a famous botanist, has been found dead in a heavily wooded area within Pine Barrens State Park, and her killer remains at large. Using your evidence cards, be the first detective to figure out the suspect, weapon, motive, and location of the crime. But watch out! False evidence is floating around, so make sure you don’t get sent in the wrong direction. You get to decide if you want to work alone or share intel with your fellow detectives.

It’s a different crime every time in this infinitely replayable true crime inspired game.

boardgame: Summer Camp: A Party Hack Game
description:
Adventure awaits in this ultimate party hack! Get ready to feel like a kid returning to their favorite Summer Camp in this unique, immersive party game experience! Become a camper and complete hilariously fun challenges throughout the evening. Your host and Camp Director will have everything they need to lead you all through memorable activities and mini games. In the box, you'll even find party prep ideas, so you and your fellow campers are fully prepared to have the best midsummer madness!

—description from the publisher

Park Life is a may follow trick taking game about timing and hand management. Hedgehog Edition streamlines the deck and only includes number cards (as opposed to the more complex People Edition). Hedgehog edition was streamlined for 'families' but also 'hardcore trick taking fans'.

Hedgehog Deluxe adds 12 higher value JQK cards that increase hand size and player count (up to 8).  Hedgehog Deluxe also adds 8 cute caterpillar tokens and a first player bus token to replace the cards in pocket edition. The Deluxe rulebook include modes for solo against an automaton, 8 player games, and more!

Gameplay is simple, everyone plays one card per trick. The winner of the trick wins points equal to the value of the card they played, but the lowest card wins and the winner must discard cards from their hand. Winning with small cards early is dangerous because the round only ends when everyone is cardless.

As a may-follow trick taking game, you have agency to choose what strategy you want based on the cards dealt to you and the choices of others. This well-balanced hand management and action economy game avoids perfect information and fills Park Life with rich choices.

For solo and co-op, play against the automata with rules in the deluxe rule book (you can download the deluxe rules for free below).

Park Life: Hedgehog may be combined with Park Life: People to play with more cards in the 5-8 player games, competitively or cooperatively.

—description from the publisher

Calling all elves! Santa has a job opening for a spot on the Elf Crew. It's up to you to compete to earn your way onto Santa's team. Players work to succeed in 3 key areas. The first to complete all of the challenges becomes Santa's Helper!

The game is played in 3 sections and players must complete the objective of each section before moving to the next: Santa's Village - Players answer questions correctly to collect 3 challenge cards; Santa's Workshop - Elves enter Santa's workshop to "build" 5 toys; Santa's Helper - Elves deliver 5 gifts to homes around the world. The first elf to complete elf training, build 5 presents, deliver their presents all around the world, and move to the Elf Crew space wins the game!

boardgame: SAS: Rogue Regiment – Black Ops Special Edition
description:
Raise merry hell with the legendary operators of the Special Air Service!

SAS: Rogue Regiment is a WW2 stealth action game for 1-4 players. Taking on the role of SAS soldiers in the pivotal weeks following D-Day, players must carry out acts of sabotage deep behind enemy lines.

Ambush convoys, assassinate high ranking officers, destroy ammo dumps and much, much more in your four man campaign against the Axis war machine!

Strike from the shadows!
Take down enemy patrols and sentries with the vast array of tools at your disposal, from rifles, explosives and grenades to vehicles, traps and your trusty fighting knife.

Leave no trace!
Move quickly and quietly to complete your objectives. Kill silently and hide the bodies to avoid detection. Get spotted, make too much noise or take too long to carry out your objective and the enemy will raise the alarm. Your heavily outnumbered team won’t last long in a firefight so when the attack comes, make sure you’re in position to make four men feel like a hundred!

Gameplay
Each turn, every SAS operator has 4 action points which they can use to move, shoot and carry out tasks. During the "stealth phase" of the game, enemy movement is dictated by patrol lines, sentry points and an event card which is drawn each turn. Each mission has a stealth meter which will slowly fill each time the players arouse suspicion (firing weapons, leaving bodies out in the open etc.) If the stealth meter completely fills the players are detected & the alarm will sound, triggering the "battle phase". The aim of the game is to carry out the mission objective and escape the area unscathed, the easiest way to do this is to avoid detection for as long as possible.

—description from the designer

The Kickstarter ‘Black Ops’ Special Edition box includes all the expansions* as well as a set of 6 red beret pearl logo dice (d6) and a watch-tower look-a-like dice tower (cardboard) in one big (black) Box.

    Expansions included are:


SAS: Rogue Regiment – They Who Dare (2024)
SAS: Rogue Regiment – KS Set (2024)
SAS: Rogue Regiment – Jaeger (2024)
SAS: Rogue Regiment – Heavy Metal (2024)
SAS: Rogue Regiment – Hot Dogs (2024)

boardgame: Marrakesh: Queenie 1 – Boats & Queenie 2 – Chess Sets
description:
2 Queenies for Marrakesh that are sold together and have a combined rules sheet.

Queenie 1: Boats
Boat are a luxury good

When a player acquires one of the boats depicted below, they draw 3 oasis tiles that are not in play and choose 1 tile. That oasis tile is placed on the boat. The other 2 oasis tiles are mixed in with the rest of the unused oasis tiles. The choosen oasis tile will be scored in addition to the players' 3 claimed oases of the highest value.
There is also a one time bonus for acquiring these boats:

Queenie 2: Chess Sets
Chess Sets are a luxury good.

When a player acquires a chess set they lose 4 points in addition to spending goods keshis.
The player gains one of the keshis depicted on the bottom of the tile and places it on their player board, gaining the deployment bonus if applicable.
In addition they get 2 dinar.

Broken Fate is a two player area-control board game where 2 players will command troops and compete for resources in an epic battle between Order and Chaos!

At the start of every turn, a player can take up to 3 energy tokens without going over 10 and play as many times as they want.

Manage those resources and play up to 5 times in a turn in this strategy based board game.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Discover "Athos Bulcão | Master Pieces", a game created by the duo Renato Keiteris [Tutan Tumba] and newcomer Fabiano Costa, which combines art and strategy into a unique experience.

In this game, you’ll be challenged to use your intelligence and creativity to master the mechanics of Cube Placement, Area Control, and Collective Pattern Construction inspired by the Tile Master | Athos Bulcão.

Complete contracts, make strategic decisions in open buying, and use the power of take that to turn the game in your favor. Every move is an opportunity to create, plan, and compete ingeniously while building breathtaking patterns.

In the game *Athos Bulcão | Master Pieces*, 2 to 4 players compete to recreate the famous tile patterns of Brazilian artist Athos Bulcão. The goal is to place tiles on the main board, trying to connect patterns and control areas to score points. Each turn, players can perform up to two actions, such as picking or placing tiles, trading tiles with other players, or adjusting the orientation of the tiles.

The patterns formed immediately score points, and patterns that match the Contract Cards provide extra points at the end of the game. The game ends when all tiles have been used, and the player with the most points wins.

"Athos Bulcão | Master Pieces" is deeply enriching, allowing you to engage with the Master Works of this celebrated Brazilian artist. This is the first game in the Master Pieces series, which will feature other Great Brazilian Figures...

Ideal for those who love art and intelligent tactics, "Athos Bulcão | Master Pieces" is the perfect fusion of style, culture, and learning. Are you ready to master this moving work of art?

"Athos Bulcão | Master Pieces" is the Official Game of the artist and is endorsed by the "Athos Bulcão Foundation" – Brasília – Brazil.

Who was Athos Bulcão?

Athos Bulcão (Rio de Janeiro, July 2, 1918 – Brasília, July 31, 2008) was a Brazilian painter, sculptor, draftsman, and artist.

Born in the Catete neighborhood of Rio de Janeiro, he abandoned his medical studies in 1939 to dedicate himself to visual arts. His first solo exhibition was in 1944, at the inauguration of the headquarters of the *Institute of Brazilian Architects* in his hometown.

For his body of work, he received numerous awards and honors, including the "Order of Cultural Merit", awarded by the Ministry of Culture of Brazil in 1995.

He passed away at the age of 90 in the Sarah Kubitschek Hospital in Brasília, due to complications from Parkinson's disease.
The prison staff joins the escape adding 3 new playable characters! This includes 3 custom meeples, 3 decision decks, holy water tokens, a rules card, and character cards.
The women's ward in the South Wing has been unbolted! Add 4 new prisoners with 4 meeples, Prisoner cards, decision decks, rules, and medicine/poison tokens.

Unlock the dark secrets of the East Wing as its notorious gates swing open! Immerse yourself in the ominous atmosphere with three new prisoners, each with their own meeples, Prisoner cards, decision decks, rules, and special tokens.

In the 5th century, we're in the kingdom of Ahza.
It has a clever king, a strong army and fertile lands.
But it is surrounded by enemies, betrayal and disaster. 
Will the kingdom survive or will it perish?
And who will be the player who can build their own kingdom as the Kingdom of Ahza collapses?

You should try to get the highest score in each round by making the best strategy. At the end of the round, the person with the highest score can add the character from the last card (7th card) placed on the table to their Kingdom. The first person to build their Kingdom by collecting all the characters required for a Kingdom will win the game.

—description from the designer

A game of some historical importance, this an "Anti-Monopoly" game published in the Communist Party of the United States Youth Magazine -- The New Pioneer.  In the game, up to four players move their pawns around a Monopoly like board.  They start with one pawn in each of the cardinal directions. They must make a circuit of the U.S., visiting its many social injustices, before entering the “home stretch" toward Soviet America.

Along the way, players can land on squares that are conducive to the  proletarian revolution (United Farmers League or Militant Auto Workers — advance three spaces), or to bourgeois or revisionist detractions (Farm Misleaders or Boss Thugs — go back three spaces). If you’re unlucky, you’ll land on a “blockade” (Child Labor, Deportations, Ku Klux Klan), where players have to wait for a comrade to arrive to set you free.

When a player has gone around the board once, they head for the center, and communist utopia. The center space is illustrated with Vladimir Lenin, Karl Marx, and Joseph Stalin  as well as William Foster, communist candidate for President of the United States.

A card game, easily transportable and fast, to practice mental arithmetic while having fun. This game is made to reconcile anyone with math, created by an arithmophobia (fear of math), it is proof that everything is possible. Don't worry, you're just asked to count to eleven.

Tales of Skyland | Adventurer’s Dawn is a cooperative card-based adventure game where trust is earned, choices matter, and the land itself remembers. As adventurers from distant lands unite to push back a rising evil, they must forge alliances, face unpredictable challenges, and confront not only external foes but the growing corruption within.

The game unfolds through modular Path Cards, which replace the traditional board and create a dynamic journey through Skyland. Each path is shaped by player decisions—some routes offer opportunity and growth, others may split the party or lead into dangerous territory. With every stop, players draw Adventure Cards that reveal new locations, enemies, events, quests, and treasures, resolved in sequence to guide the story like a living dungeon master.

Combat is fast and tactical, blending real-time cooperation with careful planning. Players roll D6s to manage strikes and defense, and D20s to trigger powerful Sigils and skill-based actions. Face bosses with multi-phase behavior, parry or evade enemy blows, and shield your teammates when the odds stack high.

Choose from 12 species and 10 professions, each freely combinable with your choice of equipment and customizable sigils. Build your own unique playstyle—whether you fight with a bow, dual blades, or ethereal energy, no class locks you in. Earn experience, evolve your stats, and equip legendary gear as you grow stronger with each battle.

But beware: the more reckless or selfish your choices, the more Corruption seeps in. Once your corruption reaches the threshold, you must draw a hidden Alignment card, changing your win condition and possibly turning you against the party. Will you remain loyal, or betray everything for power?

With endless replayability, evolving strategies, and a rich world that unfolds through cards, Skyland offers a fresh, immersive experience for fantasy lovers and fans of cooperative RPGs alike.

—description from the designer

The Devil's Antiquarian is a competitive board game for 2-4 players that immerses you in a dark and mysterious world where you become a rival antiquarian. Your objective is to outwit your opponents and become the most renowned collector by acquiring powerful relics, casting spells, and navigating the unpredictable influence of the Devil himself.

Gameplay Overview: At the start of the game, each player opens their own antique shop, complete with slots to display collected relics. The game features a shared marketplace where relics, spells, and deals are available for acquisition.

On your turn, you can:
Generate Resources: Collect resources from your relics and shop abilities.
Acquire Relics: Spend resources to purchase relics from the marketplace. Each relic has a point value and may offer special abilities or bonuses.
Cast Spells: Use resources to cast spells that can aid you or hinder your rivals. Spells can manipulate resources, affect the Devil's movements, or alter game conditions.
Make Deals with the Devil: Risk making a deal to gain powerful advantages. Deals offer significant benefits but come with potential costs or future consequences.

The game ends when a player accumulates relics totaling 10 points.

Strategic Depth: Balance resource management with risk-taking as you decide when to invest in relics, cast spells, or make deals with the Devil.
High Player Interaction: Engage with opponents through spells and deals, creating a dynamic and competitive environment.
Easy to Learn, Hard to Master: With straightforward rules that take less than 10 minutes to learn, players can quickly start their adventure while discovering deeper strategies over time.

—description from the designer

You've inherited a large estate from Madame Medora, a truly odd character. However, in order to claim your inheritance, you must solve a series of puzzles and riddles while spending one night in her House of Curiosities. If you do, treasure awaits!

In order to find the hidden treasures and move on to the next area of the house, you and your partner will need to work together to solve mysteries and puzzles.

Engaging Narrative: Get lost in the intriguing, haunted, spirit-themed storyline of this mystery-solving puzzle game.

Diverse Puzzles: Test your skills with six unique, connected, and challenging puzzles.

Interactive Components: Comes with puzzle cards, quill pen, colored pencils, and digital game elements.

Family Friendly: Family-friendly, so no need to hunt a killer and no murder involved.

Expert Level: This challenge is best suited for expert puzzle solvers. However, if you need help, this game comes with a step-by-step hint booklet to help nudge you in the right direction.

—description from the publisher

This epic new Here to Slay Expansion introduces new effects and mechanics, and a new way to win the game! Claim Banners by strategically building your Party… but remain ever vigilant, because another player might seize a Banner from your grasp at any moment!

The first player to end their turn with 3+ Banners in their Party (or SLAY 3 Monsters)wins the game ?

Throughout the game, you’ll claim Banner cards that will give you an edge over your opponents. Each Banner card lists an effect that you can use on each of your turns as long as that Banner card is in your Party.

Your Banner cards cannot be stolen, destroyed, attacked, or returned to your hand, but they can be seized by another player.

There are two types of Banner cards you can collect:

Class Banner Cards: You’ll find 10 Class Banner cards in this expansion, one corresponding to each of the 10 classes in the Here to Slay base game and expansion Packs. To claim a Class Banner card, you must be the first player to reach 3 of that class in your Party (including your Party Leader). Note: If you play Banner Quest with the Base Game but aren't using the other Expansions, you can easily remove the Warrior, Druid, Berserker, and Necromancer Banners, cards, and tokens.
Hunter’s Trophy Banner Card: There is only 1 Hunter’s Trophy Banner card in this expansion. To claim the Hunter’s Trophy Banner card, you must be the first player to SLAY a Monster card.

After that, each time a player SLAYS a Monster card, they seize the Hunter’s Trophy Banner card.

Certain cards in the Banner Quest Expansion allow you to change the class of a Hero card, giving you the upper hand to Slay Monsters or collect Class Banners!

(Don’t worry, you don’t have to keep track of it all in your head! We included double-sided class tokens in the Expansion that you can use to easily denote the current class of a Hero card.)

—description from the publisher

A snappy card game for lovers of all narrative media. Easy to teach and endlessly replayable, Videography is the perfect party game for your next boardgames night. There's no turn order in Videography! Jump in and play a card at any time. To play a card, simply say a Movie, TV Show, Anime, Book, Theatre Play or Videogame that contains what's on the centre card and what's on the card you're trying to play. First person to play all their cards wins!

—description from the designer

Grotesque Reflection

The majority of The Graft’s minions are “grafted” versions of familiar minions from other factions that The Graft has absorbed into its ever-growing mass of tissue. You’ll recognize familiar talents that can be used in deadly new combinations against their former compatriots.

Since The Graft is one giant organism, it has a unique promotion mechanic that involves absorbing defeated grafted minions into nearby spires. When those minions redeploy in a later wave, they come out on their promoted side, in which they have been grafted yet again with another previously-existing minion type. It’s gross, yes, but it’s also a riotously fun take on past factions that will have you thinking about your skills in a new way.

Nucleus of Power

But it’s not all about grafted minions. The heart of The Graft is Nucleus, the faction’s starting hero that also operates as a mobile fortress gate. The Graft’s fortress upgrades often impart new abilities to Nucleus itself, leading to a powerhouse that you can deploy to ravage your enemy (and, if you’re not careful, a tempting target for your opponent to take down).

Though Nucleus is the brains of the operation, The Graft also is home to a variety of parasitic worms that live in its many nooks and crannies. These worms have a variety of nasty abilities, but one of the worst is Infest, which allows the worms to embed themselves in opposing heroes and villains and drain their HP away, step by deadly step.

There are two types of minions in the Graft’s arsenal: worms and grafted units. Worms are semi-autonomous creatures who thrive in the folds of the Graft and who can be called upon for nasty parasitic purposes. Some of them spit acid, but all of them have in common the Infest ability, which allows them to burrow into opposing units and cause them to take damage whenever they attack. If you see one of your worms about to take the fall, try to use its Infest ability, denying your opponent a Source reward and hastening their demise in the process. Grafted units are melds of familiar base game faction units. When they upgrade, they will gain additional abilities from a different base game faction unit, symbolizing the Graft’s further processing and reshaping of the matter it absorbs. They also look pretty gross, and we love that for them.

Besides Nucleus, the two other Graft heroes fall into one of the two unit types. Fury Worm represents a particularly powerful worm unit, while Graft King Nestor is the former heir monarch (brother to Jaelana) who has fallen prey to the Graft’s onslaught.

It’s All About the Spires

Spire placement is always important in Cloudspire, but never more so than for the keel. The keel spires represent the darkness of the keel’s void-based technology casting a shadow across the land. As such, spire placement on a tile is the only way a keel unit can transform into its much more powerful promoted version. Some spires even impart new abilities to the promoted minions, creating an even more potent situation for the faction’s enemies.

But beware! If your minion leaves an island hex with a keel spire on it, it will flip back over to its non-promoted version. To stay mighty, you’ll have to maintain a firm hand over the Source wells along your path.

Heroes of Ankar

The keel also boast some of the game’s most dynamic heroes. The faction’s starting hero, Chira, has the ability to increase the keel’s CP (pending proper spire placement, of course), while the keel leader General Eko counts as a spire in his own right.

And don’t sleep on Chira, whose Poise ability is a personal favorite in the Chip Theory offices. Poise allows minions to increase or decrease their movement stat, a huge factor in strategic play that can keep your forces more powerful for an extra turn or help them push ahead to a quick victory.

—description from the publisher

The Shada is a powerful new elemental-based faction that takes advantage of terrain differences the way no other faction ever has. Each Shada minion has a specific type of terrain it can move on in addition to path, forcing you and your opponents to view the map differently and take advantage of each unique layout.

In addition to the unique terrain movement abilities, each minion has a special ability it can take advantage of while on that terrain. The faction’s spires also benefit from specific terrain placement, boosting their abilities and promoting strategic tile control and variety. Terrain bonuses become even more lethal with fortress upgrades that tie boosted CP and multi-terrain types into spire placement.

Combine Your Forces

The Shada boast a unique promotion system in which their four basic minion types (stone, wind, fire, and water) can combine with each other and form unique and powerful new minions. Fire and water become steam, and wind and stone become dust. These new minions boast new terrain allowances and abilities, and their high combination variety allows you to adapt your strategy to every new opponent.

Your fortress upgrades will also boost your combination abilities, allowing you new opportunities and Source rewards for combining units. The ambitious Shada player can even grab the Bioweapon die, allowing them to combine already promoted units for explosive results!

—description from the publisher

Cloudspire’s epic battle system has been distilled into a deck of cards! Experience the tactical crunch of Chip Theory’s acclaimed tower-defense battler with the strategic resource management twist of the 20 Strong system in the latest expansion to the beloved solo card game series. Featuring all 10 Cloudspire factions and a variety of spire, hero, and minion options, the Cloudspire 20 Strong deck puts the world of Ankar in the palm of your hand.

Grafted enemies march toward you each turn, threatening to deal damage to your hero, but you can throw up spires to fire dice at enemies before they reach you. 20 Strong: Cloudspire even allows you to use misses to offset enemy damage... just make sure the dice you roll match your spire types!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Celles: the Battle Before the Meuse
description:
Published in 1978 as part of the SPI quadrigame Battles for the Ardennes and as a standalone softpack.

Celles simulates the final desperate German attempt to reach and cross the River Meuse in the Battle of the Bulge. One of only two standalone games on this section of Hitler’s Ardennes Offensive.
family: History: World War II (Battle of the Bulge)

boardgame: 一統中原 (Yi Tong Zhong Yuan)
description:
In a charmingly cute dinosaur world, players need to carefully observe and react quickly during dinosaur duels by comparing the size of numbers and making the correct action responses. It's a joyful game designed to enhance focus and train reaction time.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Q萌的恐龍世界裡, 玩家需要通過仔細的觀察與敏銳的反應在恐龍對決中, 快速進行數字大小比較, 並做出正確的動作反應, 是一款提升專注力與訓練反應力的歡樂遊戲.

—description from the back of the box

The title of this game translates to "Unite the Central Plain" in English.

A Japanese-style push battle puzzle game where heroes from Japanese history proudly engage in combat.

Push Off is a tile game for two players. Each tile has a Japanese character from Kamakura-era to Meiji Bakumatsu-era. Score points by pushing the tile with lower power off the playing field.  Push tiles, flip tiles, move pieces - enjoy deep strategy with simple rules.

Shark alarm off the coast of Hawaii! The infamous shark twins Bob and Ruth are doing their rounds and they’ve worked up an appetite! So look after your fish and get them to safety fast by creating shoals. With a bit of luck, they will slip by the hungry maws of Bob and Ruth. Whoever is the best at schooling their fish will be declared the finner!

Goal:
The goal is to get the most points. To win, place cards with as many of your own fish as possible in the group shoal, taking care they don’t get caught by the two sharks.

Gameplay:
Each player takes 1 set of 23 cards in one colour, shuffle them and place them in front of you in a face-down pile.
On your turn, play 1 card from your hand, placing it face up in the middle of the table. 
The first card is the start of the shoal, which must be exactly 4x4 cards at the end of the round. Place 1 shark next to as well as above the first card played.
Check after each turn whether you need to change the position of the sharks. Whenever there are more fish (regardless of their colour) in a row or column than in those being shadowed by the sharks, move the sharks accordingly to that row or column.
Once there are 16 cards in the shoal, the round ends. Now remove all cards from the row and column that the two sharks are currently shadowing.
Set aside any remaining cards from the shoal as a points pile.

Game end:
The game ends after the third round. 
Count up all the fish in each colour. The winner is the player with the most fish in the points stack in their own colour.

boardgame: King of Tokyo: Gift Promo Cards – Worldwide Season
description:
Three new gift cards in 2024:

     Tour Guide: Keep - When you Yield Tokyo, you may choose another Monster to take your place, instead of the attacker.
     Souvenir Collector: Keep - Gain 1 (star) when you buy a DISCARD card.
     Cultural Exchange: Keep - After a Monster Smaches you, you may lose 1 extra (heart) to make them lose 1 (heart).


These cards are parts of the King of Tokyo: Tournament Kit which contains 18 promo cards (specifically six copies each of three new cards)

While establishing humankind's first permanent moon base, an astronaut is murdered. One of their fellow crew-members MUST be the killer, but it's up to you to uncover who is responsible for committing the Murder On The Moon.

This is an investigation / mystery game with puzzles inspired by escape rooms. In addition to the puzzles in the game box, you will also need to have an internet enabled device on-hand, such as a smartphone or laptop, to research and sleuth your way through the case. You can interact with the various games characters via SMS text, email and even phone calls!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
When the cards snap, race against your opponent to say something from their category before they something from yours. For example, can you think of a way to ruin an ice cream sundae before your opponent names something that smells terrible?

Thinking fast under pressure produces answers ranging from the brilliant to the bizarre (and sometimes just bad!) and gets players of all ages laughing. The cards are all family friendly, but what comes out your mouth is up to you!

The game is straightforward and quick to learn and there are special cards that hide, move and add new categories in to add extra fun and variety.

—description from the publisher

A fun set collecting game of wheeling, dealing and concealing. Players all play at the same time, trading cards and playing sets of 3, 4 or 5 animals to try and earn maximum points. Watch out for Armadillo of Doom cards moving around the table too - these punish you with a 6 point penalty if remaining in your hand at the end.

The game moves fast, helped by the fact that the first player to go out earns a jackpot of points for being the first to lose all their cards. The game moves fast and can get very hectic as players become increasingly desperate to build sets, get cards on the table and get rid of their armadillos!

Simple to learn and quick to get started, rounds of the game last around 10 minutes ensuring lots of fast moving fun for players of all ages.

—description from the publisher

In Heroes you compete with another player to hire the best group of heroes ever. You draw cards from a shared deck and take turns playing cards from your hand, trying to form a group of five heroes in different ways.

This game was chosen to celebrate the 10th anniversary of Ludus Magnus Studio.
A simple game, but one that can become very strategic depending on the skill and luck of the players. Heroes, in this celebratory version, features a series of protagonists chosen by our community from the various LMS games.

"Saltpeter War" is a variant of "Red Sun Blue Cross" that recreates the Pacific War (or "Saltpeter War") fought between Chile and Peru from 1879 to 1884. Covers the battles up to 1881. One player leads the Chilean army and navy and the other will be in charge of the Peruvian army and navy.

Banzai Magazine #15

boardgame: Бестиарий Сигиллума: Новые Земли (Bestiary of Sigillum: Distant Lands)
description: An expansion which provides players with new creatures with different abilities, unique castles, new battlefields and a campaign mode about Victrum's story, which contains logic puzzles and mission with different rules

This promotional Ruins card was released at the SPIEL '24 in Essen.

The Celestial Raider forces each of your opponents to discard one resource of their choice.

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000 (Tenth Edition): Codex Supplement – Dark Angels
description:
The Dark Angels are a dour brotherhood, characterised by their deep commitment to millennia-old rituals, loyalty, discipline, and self-sufficiency. They are a fighting force whose allegiance to the Emperor and Imperium is nigh impossible to dispute, yet they hide deep secrets.

Codex Supplement: Dark Angels is your essential guide to using the Dark Angels and their successor Chapters in your games of Warhammer 40,000, as well as collecting, building, and painting them. When combined with the contents of Codex: Space Marines, you’ll be able to build an army with their own fighting style, specialised units, and unique formations. You'll also find everything you need to use them in narrative Crusade games and games of Combat Patrol. The book is loaded with artwork, stunning examples of painted miniatures, and fascinating background material to inspire you.

Inside this 96-page hardback book, you'll find:

     History and background of the Sons of the Lion, from their origins in the Age of Darkness to the return of their Primarch.
     Atmospheric artwork, shedding light on the darkest corners of this most enigmatic of Chapters
     16 datasheets to add to those found in Codex: Space Marines, including the Dark Talon, Land Speeder Vengeance, and more
     Three themed Detachments, the Unforgiven Task Force, Inner Circle Task Force, and Company of Hunters, each with their own special rules
     Crusade rules to show how your characters plunge deeper into the Chapter’s mysteries as they hunt the Fallen
     Combat Patrol rules to take command of the Vengeful Brethren in fast-paced games
     An 'Eavy Metal showcase of expertly-painted Citadel miniatures to inspire you


You'll need a copy of Codex: Space Marines, available separately, to use the contents of this book fully.

This book also contains a one-use code to unlock Codex Supplement: Dark Angels content in Warhammer 40,000: The App.

This promotional Ruins card was released at the SPIEL '24 in Essen.

The Stasis Hourglass allows you not to turn your ship's wheel this turn. You can therefore carry out the same action as the previous turn.

Orks are brutal and violent aliens who love nothing more than to fight. They are tough, with hulking physiques, thick skulls, and toothy maws. When gathered in an excitable crush of noise and death, Orks become infused with a strange energy that drives them to even greater acts of unrestrained ferocity. They gleefully pile into the fight, bellowing their deafening war cry – ‘Waaagh!’.

This 152-page book contains a trove of background information covering prominent Ork conflicts from the past and present, accompanied by spectacular artwork and galleries of painted miniatures. Codex: Orks grants you a detailed look into the organisation of Ork armies, be they small planetary raiding forces or monstrous Waaagh!s, as well as the troops, leaders, and war machines that fight among them. The book also contains the rules for leading a brutally effective Orks army in Combat Patrol, Crusade, and Matched Play games of Warhammer 40,000.

Inside this hardback codex, you'll find:

     Thrilling tales of legendary Ork escapades, from war on a galactic scale to smaller, more 'covert' operations
     All of the army rules you need to play games of Warhammer 40,000 with your Ork miniatures
     Lore on the Ork kulturs and how they structure their armies
     Detailed matched play rules, including dedicated secondary objectives
     A full suite of rules for playing narrative Crusade campaigns and intense Combat Patrol games


This book also contains a one-use code to unlock Codex: Orks content in Warhammer 40,000: The App.

The Adeptus Custodes are towering champions clad in gleaming auramite who will protect the Emperor at any cost. With bodies and minds honed through ancient secrets of gene-alchemy and wielding individually crafted weapons of incredible potency, even a small band of these terrifying exemplars can carve a bloody path through hordes of foes. They fight side by side upon the battlefield with the cold-eyed witch seekers of the Sisters of Silence. Theirs is an eternal war, for the threats to the Master of Mankind are manifold and spread across the stars, but they wage it with breathtaking skill and ferocity, for to falter is to doom all of Humanity.

Codex: Adeptus Custodes is your essential guide to using the Adeptus Custodes and Sisters of Silence in your games of Warhammer 40,000, as well as collecting, building, and painting them. You'll find everything you need to use them in narrative Crusade games and games of Combat Patrol. The book is loaded with artwork, stunning examples of painted miniatures, and immersive background material to inspire you.

Inside this 112-page hardback book, you'll find:

     Background material exploring the role of the Adeptus Custodes and Sisters of Silence, their sub-factions, and how they're recruited and trained
     Majestic artwork and photography showcasing the awe-inspiring beauty of the Adeptus Custodes, each a work of art in its own right
     18 datasheets with the profiles, weapons, and potent abilities of every Adeptus Custodes and Sisters of Silence unit
     Four themed Detachments, like the Auric Champions and Null Maiden Vigil, each with their rules and specialisations
     Crusade rules for sending your force on vital missions all across the galaxy, in the name of the Emperor
     Combat Patrol rules and painting guide for Tristraen’s Gilded Blades
     An 'Eavy Metal showcase of superbly-painted Citadel miniatures to inspire you, featuring plenty of gleaming gold armour and deep, rich reds, along with variant colour schemes


This book also contains a one-use code to unlock Codex: Adeptus Custodes content in Warhammer 40,000: The App.

New Word cards (8):
This promo pack adds 8 new Word cards to the game. Each Word card is based on real world language and is one of a kind. All command symbols are represented. You recognize the new cards by the flag representing the language in the top right corner. Adding these cards will make your game a little bit harder. Are you up to the challenge?

The T'au Empire is amongst the most dynamic and determined powers in the 41st Millenium. Their technology is advanced and ever-improving, from grav-skimming gunships and artificial intelligence, to potent energy weapons and formidable battlesuits. Their territories expand with every victory, be it military or diplomatic. Yet the T'au Empire's greatest strength is its unifying philosophy – they believe it is their duty to spread the doctrine of the Greater Good to every corner and culture of this dark galaxy, and will let none stop them from bringing enlightenment to the stars.

Codex: T'au Empire is a vital guide for collecting and playing games with the T'au Empire and its alien allies in Warhammer 40,000. You'll find an absolute treasure trove of information to help you muster, paint, and command your armies – including rules for narrative Crusade campaigns, Combat Patrol skirmishes, and matched play games. There's also a stunning spread of painted miniatures, evocative artwork, and immersive background material to inspire you for the Greater Good.

Inside this 136-page hardback book, you'll find:

     Background material detailing the T'au Empire, including its rigid caste system, varied sept worlds, and meteoric expansion, as well its long-time allies amongst the Kroot
     Inspiring artwork that sheds light on the sleek aesthetic, complex technology, and alien auxiliaries of the T'au Empire
     38 datasheets detailing the profiles, wargear, and unique abilities of every T'au Empire unit, from Stealth Battlesuits to savage Krootox Rampagers
     Four themed Detachments, including the Kauyon and Mont'ka metastrategies, as well as dedicated Kroot Hunting Packs, each with their own set of special rules
     Crusade rules to launch a Sphere Expansion of your own, as you assimilate new systems through diplomacy and conquest
     Combat Patrol rules and a painting guide for playing fast-paced games with the Sudden Dawn Cadre
     An 'Eavy Metal showcase of superbly-painted Citadel miniatures to inspire you, featuring different T'au septs and Kroot kindreds

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000 (Tenth Edition): Codex – Chaos Space Marines
description:
Chaos Space Marines are genetically augmented transhumans who seek only to conquer, slaughter, and despoil. Clad in baroque power armour bedecked in macabre trophies and infernal iconography, Chaos Space Marines do battle alongside growling battle tanks, monstrous Daemon Engines, and thronging masses of cultist thralls. Some are possessed by daemonic allies, others bear Chaos-tainted artefacts or wield warp sorcery. No matter what form they take, they pursue their Long War with pitiless brutality, slaughtering any who stand in their path in an ever-escalating campaign of vengeance, conquest and apocalyptic violence.

Codex: Chaos Space Marines is an essential tome for building and painting a collection of Heretic Astartes, and using them in games of Warhammer 40,000. Inside this book you'll find an infernal hoard of inspirational background material and art to inspire you, along with rules for waging your own galactic war with narrative Crusade campaigns, skirmish-sized Combat Patrol games, and plenty of rules for matched play games. This grimoire is also packed with photography to evoke the horror of facing down this terrible foe, as well as more detailed examples of single miniatures.

Inside this 160-page hardback book, you'll find:

     Background material about the many hosts of Chaos Space Marines, including some of the most notorious warlords of the Black Legion
     Macabre artwork revealing the horrible truth of these infernal soldiers at war, including their daemonic allies and insane, gibbering cultists
     48 datasheets detailing each unit, including their wargear, profiles, and unique abilities, from nightmare Spawn to immortal Daemon Princes
     A massive eight themed Detachments, each with their own detachments rules, Enhancements and Stratagems
     Crusade rules to lead your warband on a furious campaign to seek the favour of the Dark Gods
     Combat Patrol rules to use Zarkan’s Daemonkin in fast-paced skirmish games
     An ‘Easy Metal showcase of stunning Citadel miniatures from the incredible Chaos Space Marines range

Genestealer Cults are fanatical sects of alien hybrids that infest Human worlds beyond count. They lurk unseen for generations, hiding and preparing in the dark until their Day of Ascension arrives. Then they rise in a tide of mutated acolytes, turncoat soldiery, and predatory xenos monstrosities to tear down those they see as oppressors.

Codex: Genestealer Cults contains a trove of background information covering prominent Genestealer infestations from the past and present, accompanied by spectacular artwork and galleries of painted miniatures. Take a detailed look into the organisation of these xeno-tainted armies and the troops, leaders, and war machines that fight among them. This book also contains the rules for leading a Genestealer Cults army in Combat Patrol, Crusade, and Matched Play games of Warhammer 40,000.

Inside this 120-page hardback book, you'll find:

     In-depth background and lore on the Genestealer Cults, how they take over planets and prepare them for the Great Devourer – even going so far as to infiltrate the Imperial military structure
     Rousing artwork that will inspire your cults to acts of exemplary sedition and a showcase of expertly-painted Genestealer Cults miniatures
     All the rules you need to play your Genestealer Cults army, including 24 datasheets covering every unit and five Detachments
     A full suite of rules for playing narrative Crusade campaigns and intense Combat Patrol games

The Adepta Sororitas are the armed wing of the Imperial faith. They defend the Emperor's cathedrums, chapels, and holy shrines from the heretic, the mutant, the witch, and the alien. Encased within power armour, the Battle Sisters of the Orders Militant wield boltguns, flamers, and chainswords as they march relentlessly towards their enemies, bearing relics of fallen martyrs and singing prayers above the din of battle. Their tanks and wargear are bedecked with the iconography of the Adeptus Ministorum, a demonstration of their zeal to any who would opposed them. Where the Adepta Sororitas tread, so does the light of the Emperor shine.

This 129-page book is richly embellished with art and background information celebrating the courage, strength, and zeal of the Sisters of Battle. You'll find within these pages all the rules, inspiration, and reference material you'll need to collect, build, and play games with your mighty armies, including datasheets for every unit in the army, and a Combat Patrol for playing fast-paced games of Warhammer 40,000.

This hardback Codex contains:

     Background material illustrating the rich history, organization, and ethos of these holy warriors
     Stunning art capturing the majesty of the Adepta Sororitas, and the horrors into which they charge headlong, plus a showcase of beautifully painted miniatures to inspire you

     A full complement of rules for launching a cleansing crusade with your Adepta Sororitas, even resulting in one of them possibly becoming a living saint

Imperial Agents emerge from the shadows when heresy threatens to destabilise worlds, cultists seek to summon daemonic abominations, and xenos corruption endangers Humanity's realm. Some are Inquisitors of the Holy Ordos and their motley cadres, but this umbrella term also encompasses terrifying Assassins, swashbuckling Rogue Traders, fanatical priests of the Imperial faith, and other more specialised individuals. Some join more conventional Imperial armies to either further their own ends or lend their resources to an important battle. No matter the cause or nature, Imperial agents are amongst the most secretive and esoteric servants of the Golden Throne.

Codex: Imperial Agents delves deeply into the murky origins of these clandestine organisations, offering insight into their methods of war. This also includes the mysterious Deathwatch Chapter of Space Marines, seconded as they are to the Ordo Xenos, and other key units attached to various agencies, including the Sisters of Battle, Grey Knights, Imperial Navy, Adeptus Arbites, and more. You'll find loads of grim and sinister artwork to inspire you, along with beautiful galleries of painted miniatures. This hallowed tome gives you all the rules you'll need for engaging in the shadow war for the heart of the Imperium, whether it's in Combat Patrol, narrative Crusade campaigns, or matched play games of Warhammer 40,000.

Inside this 128-page hardback book, you'll find:

     Background material and lore for the various different types of Imperial Agents, from the Assassin temples, to the Ordos of the Inquisition, and the forces that commonly fight alongside them

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000 (Tenth Edition): Codex Supplement – Blood Angels
description:
The Blood Angels are one of the most revered of all the original Space Marine Legions, but are also the bearers of a terrible curse caused by the death of their beloved Primarch, Sanguinius. As one of the oldest and most successful Chapters, they have a proud heritage, culture, and aesthetic that diverges from what is now considered standard. They celebrate their beauty, but do not shy away from their savage nature in the heat of battle.

Codex Supplement: Blood Angels is your essential guide to using the Sons of Sanguinius and their successor Chapters in your games of Warhammer 40,000, as well as collecting, building, and painting them. When combined with the contents of Codex: Space Marines, you’ll be able to build an army with their own fighting style, specialised units, and unique formations. You'll also find everything you need to use them in narrative Crusade games and games of Combat Patrol. The book is loaded with artwork, stunning examples of painted miniatures, and fascinating background material to inspire you.

Inside this 104-page hardback book, you'll find:
– History and background of the Sons of Sanguinius, starting with their glorious origins in the Age of Darkness, to the death of their beloved Primarch, and its effects on the current Chapter
– Beautiful artwork, shedding light on both their glorious record of deeds, and the secret darkness at the heart of their bloodline
– 15 datasheets to add to those found in Codex: Space Marines, including the Death Company, Baal Predators, and Sanguinary Guard
– Three themed Detachments – the Lost Brethren, Liberator Assault Group, and the Angelic Host – each with their own special rules
– Crusade rules to show how your characters either ascend to the Sanguinary Guard, or succumb to the Black Rage
– Combat Patrol rules to take command of the Sanguinary Spearhead in fast-paced games
– An 'Eavy Metal showcase of expertly-painted Citadel miniatures to inspire you

You'll need a copy of Codex: Space Marines, available separately, to use the contents of this book fully.

This book also contains a one-use code to unlock Codex Supplement: Blood Angels content in Warhammer 40,000: The App.

Loud fanfares ring out through the quiet town of Schalottenburg! Vegetable athletes from all different backgrounds parade through the streets with their flags. That can only mean one thing: The annual vegetable competition is upon us! All of Schalottenburg's citizens quickly gather to cheer on their teams as they compete against each other in the earthy raised vegetable bed arena. Of course, there will be a lot of pushing and shoving again, with the aim of creating the largest possible formation of the same types of vegetables. Whoever succeeds best will lift the coveted trophy into the night sky of Schalottenburg!

Gameplay:
Take the tile in front of you and push it into the raised vegetable bed from the outside, with the vegetable side facing upwards. Then place one of your wooden markers on this tile. Take the piece you pushed out and keep it for your next turn. Continue playing until everyone has placed their last wooden marker (2 or 3 pieces). 
At the end of each round mark as many fields on your scoring boards as the number of tiles with the same type of vegetable that are directly connected to each other vertically or horizontally.

Game end:
The game ends as soon as there are only 3 or fewer face-down vegetable tiles left in the raised vegetable bed. Finish the current round and then add up the points of all the vegetable teams on your scoreboards. The winner is the player with the highest score.
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Duel – Void Preorder Promo
description:

boardgame: Las Ligas de la Ñ: El juego de los muertos vivientes
description:
Las Ligas de la Ñ: El juego de los muertos vivientes (“The Ñ Leagues: The Game of the Living Deads”), is an asymmetrical fighting board game for 2 to 12 players that includes classic characters of Spanish literature in card form, with their name, work, author, representative quote and unique powers (there are no equals). The time has come for Don Quijote, Sancho Panza, Celestina, Lazarillo, Fortunata and Jacinta, Segismundo, Bernarda Alba and others!

In this new Ñ game (the eighth in the series), players have to control one of the playable characters in order to score more points than their opponents (after summing up the points for final position and for eliminated elements). Each turn, the players use their cards or powers to maneuver, attack or defend, displace enemies and make other actions. There are several types of playable characters: some have allies, some are a pair of heroes, some undergo evolutions and transformations, and some go on their own.

Each board contains 24 different living dead forces (each corresponding to a different square and having its own gameplay characteristics). Throughout 2025, new boards will be added (special "Edgar Allan Poe", "World Mythology"…).

The game's main source of inspiration is the Super Smash Bros. video game series and it maintains important similarities with both the Unmatched board game series and battle royale style games.

—description from the designer

Sonic Strike is a single-player expansion for Car Wars Sixth Edition that comes with 40 ALL-NEW cards to add to your collection. Use sonic weapons to bring your opponents to a screeching halt. Say hello to six new Crew members and take advantage of their unique abilities. Need to grab a bite? The new Grilled Cheese Dispenser will help keep you going until the battle is won. Make the arena your own with Sonic Strike!

This is an expansion for Car Wars Sixth Edition. It is not a stand-alone game.

From the same family of Party & Co, Party & Co: Ultimate is a Party & Co game that requires a bit of strategy. It is made out of 4 different categories:


     PartyMimo: Mime and sounds.
     PartyTrazos: Drawing.
     PartySaco: Guessing. The player must answer to their team using the format specified in the tile, like "Say YES for NO" and "Say NO for YES".
     PartyDado: Guessing. The player has to read the card description to the team as specified in the dice, for example, opening the mouth as much as the player can.


People make two teams and compete with the other team until a player doesn't have more cards. The team with more points win.

Penitent is a solitaire game where players take the role of Quaker reformers, tasked with designing a visionary American penitentiary in the early 19th century.

Players will draw a floorplan for their model penitentiary and determine how it is governed by resolving a sequence of event cards. Your model will be evaluated at the end of each era based on its influence among reforms and architecture, funding, and the impact it has on the moral fiber of its inmates. Players will expand and renovate their blueprint at the conclusion of each era. Your penitentiary must endure three eras of events to win.

Will your penitentiary adhere to the Auburn reform's silent system or Pennsylvania's bold solitary confinement system? Will your inmates achieve rehabilitation through ceaseless labor or intense religious instruction? Will your penitentiary serve as a model of reform or be instructive only for its failure.

—description from the designer

Cat Saga is a cooperative, story-driven adventure game. Explore the world together and make decisions that impact your unique saga.

Story
Looking back, adventure has called you since you were just a kitten. Now, setting off on a journey that's beyond your wildest dreams, you are faced with an important question: Are you prepared to embrace an epic saga and forge your very own heroic tale?

Overview
Cat Saga is a cooperative, story-driven game, where you explore the world together over the course of five Sagas. As a team you will make decisions that impact the story, engage in combat and use your abilities to overcome danger. You will create a wonderful Saga together, try epic actions, get in fights, find valueble items, and level up!

Objective
You win together if you manage to reach the end of the final Saga. You lose together if one Catventurer reaches zero health.

Gameplay
Start reading the Saga together, out loud. Your decisions and accomplishments will lead you to certain Saga Numbers. All players will follow the Saga together and a decision or accomplishment counts for the entire group.

—description from the designer
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach the finish wins the game.

Meet Savage Skies, the expansion to the Aldarra board game that elevates your strategic warfare to new heights. This expansion introduces 72 additional finely crafted miniatures and two new factions, adding layers of complexity and variety to your gameplay experience.

With new factions come new abilities and play styles, demanding a more dynamic strategy to counteract fresh challenges. Adapt your tactics, master new faction abilities, and command a larger fleet to dominate the skies like never before. Savage Skies is your ticket to a more intricate and engaging war over the treasures of Aldarra.

—description from the publisher
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The players start at the white space and the first to reach the finish wins the game.

Munchkin: Dead & Deader is a 56-card Munchkin expansion that adds more Undead to your deck.

The spirits are restless!

You would be, too, if you constantly had the living bothering you . . . With a Medium, a Spirit Caller, and a Reality TV Crew banging around the house at all hours, who has time to rattle chains?

And a very spooky house it is: From the Paltry Geist all the way up to the terrifying Ghostzebo, Munchkin Dead & Deader provides lots of new Undead for your Munchkin deck.

Strap on your Talismantle, equip your Hex-Ray Specs, and charge your Ectoplasma Rifle – it’s ghost-hunting time!

boardgame: Ayacucho 1824: End of the Spanish Empire
description:
The battle of Ayacucho was the last big and important clash or arms between the Royalist (defenders of Spanish Monarchy) and the Patriots ( American pro-independence troops) in December 9th of 1824 in Peru.

The result of this decisive battle was the victory of the patriots and the independence of Spanish America and the consequent end of the Spanish Empire.

Uses a game system rarely seen in wargames, suitable for the battles of Spanish America, which are like Napoleonic pocket battles.

On their turns, players must activate Division generals who then can move the battalions along the marked positions for each one. Then combat takes places while players may use the event cards to surprise the enemy and modify the results.

The battle ends when the morale of one of the armies reaches cero, and the other army will be the winner.

—description from the designer

description:
Emblem Five is a fast, fun, head-to-head two-player card game about forming and battling teams of colorful "Emblems". Emblems are mysterious, sentient representations of inanimate objects, ideas, and concepts from our world. Each player will form their own deck of these Emblems, then use them to engage in fun battles that utilize teamwork, strategy, timing, and hand-management. The winner of each battle gets to choose which Emblems progress, and which are doomed to be forgotten. Creating a strong deck of Emblems that work well with each other is the key to victory!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Unsolved Case Files: Mini Case – Luna Knight
description:
CASE No: I5-081021 Luna Knight

On June 21st, 2014 rising pop star, Luna Knight, died during a brief intermission at her own concert. Police arrived on the scene and investigated her death. With so many possible suspects the case eventually went cold.

Luna's best friend, Justine Delgado, wants to reopen the case and uncover the truth. Justine is certain she knows who her pool of suspects are, she only needs help nailing the killer. Can you help Justine catch her best friend's killer after all these years?

The Suspects:

Esmerelda Garcia - Luna’s one-hit-wonder aunt who mentored her until Luna’s fame outgrew her own.

Suzanne Hurley - Luna’s overly controlling manager who wanted Luna to make life decisions based around her “image” and not her “feelings.”

Tyler Hall - Luna’s inconsistent boyfriend who was caught in a questionable position with another woman only days before Luna’s death.

Are you up to the challenge of solving this first-person detective game?

SOLVE IN AN HOUR - Immerse yourself in this cold case murder mystery game and solve the case in about an hour. The Luna Knight case lets you showcase your investigative skills and put the killer behind bars in record time, which is perfect for entry-level detectives who want an engaging experience that won't take all night long to solve.

29 INVESTIGATION DOCUMENTS AND PHOTOS - This unsolved case file game includes just under 30 evidence photos and documents for you to investigate, including a newspaper article, crime scene photographs, a police report, witness statements, and more. Our quick play case gives you the same great quality with fewer items to sift through, so you can fit your detective work into your day with ease.

ONLINE ANSWER KEYS - To get past each step of the case you’ll need to visit an online answer page that requires you to prove you cracked it. If you don’t have the answer right you can keep investigating without being spoiled by the right answers.

1943. Between September 9 and 13, Rimini becomes a Platzkommandantur occupied by the 65th Infantry Division of the Wehrmacht. They occupy the central square, the barracks and the airport. Captain Ugo Ughi, a former officer of the royal army who had waited for the arrival of the Germans, is appointed Commissario Prefettizio. A curfew is imposed from 9:00 p.m. to 5:00 a.m. and a ban is placed on meetings, rallies, parades and public demonstrations. Ex-soldiers, daring young people and responsible citizens form patriotic groups to organize the Resistance to an increasingly ferocious war, between two foreign armies that distrust Italy, as they do not recognize it as an allied nation, but only a defeated enemy that is now a co-belligerent.

At the beginning of the game, each player is associated with one or more of these 6 patriotic formations. They will set-up their tokens on to the map, built by square tiles each containing one or more of the six available resources, and each player will play the rounds in the order assigned by the group, moving the tokens with their movement points assigned. Each group has its own peculiarities in terms of pawns, total movements, priority for the round order and special effects.

During the longest thirteen months, until 21st September, 1944, each participant leads one or more patriotic formations to overcome, by collaborating with others, the repressions and deprivations that the game exercises representing both the Nazi-Fascist occupation and the Allied offensive of liberation.

Each month is represented by a round (13 total), where a card with the active repressions and potential additional effect such as the curfew, that limits the movements of the players, or winter months that limits the available resources. Each round an event that activates the repressions and moves the occupants' pawns is revealed, starting with the bombing that during the game will first reduce the resources available in the bombed tiles, then removes the tile from the map, reducing the available space. Players will then take their turns by moving and collecting resources to fulfill action cards and play them from their hand, to activate effects and/or score points.

At the end of the game, after 13 rounds, points are calculated to determine the winner.

Rimini Libera is a recreational, informative and cultural work promoted by the Istituto per la Storia della Resistenza e dell'Età Contemporanea della Provincia di Rimini (ISREC) and created with the contribution and collaboration of the Comune di Rimini and the Biblioteca Civica Gambalunga on the occasion of the 80th anniversary of the Liberation of Rimini.

Rimini Libera is a game about war, not a war game. It is inspired by real historical events, sourced from authoritative books, with the aim of spreading the news and recreating the contexts to provide a highly immersive experience. A challenging and at times frustrating reenactment, to achieve the goal of passing on and making people understand the value of historical memory.

—description from the designer

These rocks are on a roll!

Munchkin: Taken for Granite is a 56-card Munchkin expansion that combines a ton of earth and a pebble of magic into a legion of the weirdest, wackiest constructs you’ve ever seen!

Run from the Sock Golem, have a drink with the Calcium Golem, and chill out with the Frost Golem. Watch out, though, because even non-Golem monsters can become Hard as a Rock, so you’ll need your Hard Hat and an Automatic Chisel to really Break the Mold.

OVERVIEWSWIRLS is a simple but very competitive 2-6 player tile game. It is intended for children, families and party players who is looking for a quick, fun and highly energetic gameplay.

In essence, the gameplay is based on rolling 3 dice with Colors and Swirl Patterns. Then, at the same moment, all players try to find and grab the matching tile as fast as possible. Everyone tries to be quicker than others, to take the tile to the own tile pool. After taking, the tile has to be resolved and some decisions have to be made. In the end, the one who collects most tiles or gains highest score wins the game.

Despite the simple basic concept, there are meaningful features included that heat up the gameplay. SWIRLS uses classic dexterity mechanism of roll and be the first to find/grab, but the gameplay is elevated hugely by introducing solutions like:

Intensive rivalry - gameplay keeps players engaged constantly, forcing them to compete and fight for each tile. Matching tiles may be also found in other player's pool, so players never feel safe with already collected ones. They may keep losing them next turns if they are not fast enough with finding.

Combined patterns and colors for recognizing - two varied but complementary conditions on rolled dice that activates different senses of vision and perception for tiles recognizing. It makes searching for the right tile even more demanding.

Decisions - based on the number of colors on the found tiles players choose additional tiles. So, will you choose the tile that provides additional tiles when found or the one that is less probable to be rolled, to keep it safer?

Tactical variant - additional variant with the alternative scoring methods enhances the gameplay with a tactical aspect which makes selecting tiles by a player very crucial. This allows players to compose and shape their own tile collection for higher score. So, will you choose the tile that provides additional tiles when found or the one that gains more points? Or maybe you choose the tiles that will let you build sets of the same color combination to gain more points?

Memory aspect - players who will memorize more collected tiles in their own and other players’ pools will have the advantage of finding tiles easier and faster.

All these features make the game great for 5 year old younglings as a children or family game. Also as an educational game for practicing quick recognition, observation, dexterity and making decisions. It is also very suitable for adults as a typical party game that provides a lot of tension and laughter.

So, if you are a fan of the intensity and energy of the Jungle Speed but need a bit more depth, then SWIRLS is the perfect game for you, your family and friends. The game offers an endless replayability. This, combined with a very quick setup and pleasantly short gameplay time, encourages players to play several games in a row. With small, handy components, the game fits perfectly for family occasions, parties, trips or any occasions when there's only a short time available but there's a need of having fun.


				
				
					Check the forum thread for game presentation and all official resources/files/links
[OFFICIAL] - Game presentation + PnP files + Links + WIP Thread + Digital versions

Each player is dealt a 2x2 grid of 4 face down cards per round. At the start, you can peek at and memorise the two cards closest to you. On your turn, you 1) draw a card from the face down deck or take the top face up card from the discard pile (if available), and either discard that card or exchange it with a card in your grid. Some cards once discarded have special optional abilities, which include peeking at one of your cards, peeking at an opponent's card, exchanging one of your cards with an opponent's without looking at them, and looking at an opponent's card and exchanging it with one of your own if you want to. Once a player believes their grid has a value of 5 or less, they say 'Spooky Boo!' If correct, they score 0 while the other players add up the value of their cards. If incorrect the person receives a penalty of 10 points and the other players score 0. At the end of 5 rounds, the player with the lowest score wins.

Bidder Battle is an exciting auction-based board game where players compete to win valuable items hidden inside storage units. With every turn, players have the chance to become the Auctioneer, setting the stage for fast-paced bidding wars. The goal? Make the most money by acquiring items at a bargain and outsmarting your opponents in a high-stakes auction environment. Only those with sharp instincts, quick decision-making, and a knack for strategic bidding will come out on top.

Each round, the Auctioneer presents a storage unit filled with face-down items, which could range from valuable treasures to tricky "Havoc" cards that could render all contents worthless. Players must decide whether to pay the entry fee to buy into the auction, gaining them the right to bid. Peeking and flipping cards give savvy players glimpses of what’s inside, but too much information might raise the stakes for everyone. It’s a delicate balance of risk and reward as players try to outbid their rivals without breaking the bank.

As the bidding heats up, players shout their offers, hoping to secure a unit at the best price. If no one else matches their final bid, the storage unit—and its contents—are theirs! But be careful: soiled mattresses, busted treadmills, and black mold may be waiting for them. The game is full of surprises, keeping every player on their toes until the very end.

Once all players have taken turns as the Auctioneer twice, the game concludes. Players tally up the value of their remaining cash and collected items, and the player with the highest total claims victory as the ultimate bidder in this battle of wit, strategy, and nerve!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 暁のナイル (Nile in the dawn)
description:
Set 5000 years ago in Egypt, players managed agriculture and livestock while predicting the Nile River's floods to develop their civilization.

Players act as nobles, growing wheat, raising cattle, and constructing buildings to earn points. After 12 rounds, the player with the most points wins.

To achieve the highest score, overcome the effects of the Nile River's floods, manage resources, and build structures.

Manacaster is a tabletop card-battle game for two players.  It was designed by experts with an emphasis on balanced gameplay and encouraging skillful play.

Units are the driving force at the heart of Manacaster.  Without your army of monsters, mercenaries, and machines, you are nothing.

Actions allow you to manipulate Units, refill your hand with cards, or gain back life points.  The right Action, deployed at the right time, can win you a game outright.

Manacaster was designed to put players ( not randomness ) in command of their destiny.  Here are a few ways we achieved that goal:


     Both players act each turn using a simple back-and-forth priority system.



     Each turn, one player is the Attacker and the other is the Defender.  



     Both players get a Mana card each turn, selected from their mana deck.



     Players are shown 14 cards to start the game, of which they select 7.



     Strict, mathematical framework to maintain balance among cards & abilities.



     Rigid structure to determine which Keywords correspond to which Elements.


—description from the publisher

A set-collection board game where players force Alien G into opponents' rooms, helping them build the most potent Alien G kingdom.

Points are awarded based on how large and powerful the Alien G kingdom grows in the opponent's room.

Protect your room while pushing Alien G into opponents' rooms to make it settle there and create a kingdom, aiming for victory.
