You are part of the 788th Bomb Squadron, under the 467th USAAF Bombardment Group based in Rackheath, England. Your missions will begin in April 1944, during which you will manage a squadron of six B-24 bombers through various primary and diversionary combat missions.

Load your ordnance, assemble your pilot and crew, and head out on perilous missions across Europe. Minimal components and footprint makes playing the game easy and enjoyable.

After performing your take off you will move across mission zones using 2D6 rolls to check your pilot skills and navigation. Arriving over your target you will use 2D6 rolls to align bombs on the targets using a roll and write style of gameplay matching your rolls with numbers on target clusters. Then continue through the mission zones back to your base.

Arriving back at base you must perform your landing, and tend to the damage to your crew and bomber.

—description from the designer

By the People is a game for 2-4 Candidates running for President of the United States of America. Similar to real life, in order to win you must win the most Electoral Votes, which you earn by winning States to vote for you. Each state is won on Election night by having the best score among the Candidates based on the voter interests in that State.

The game is played out over 2 stages: The Campaign trail, and the Election night. During the Campaign trail, candidates must maneuver the challenges of rallying support by visiting States with their Candidate pawns each turn to different States as well as alternating rounds proposing policies to appeal to different voter interests by drafting policy cards and handling debates within the game in a simultaneous take-that debate card selection. These policy cards and debate cards increase or decrease the Candidate's voter interest among the 12 categories used to score the State cards. After the 4 rounds of the Campaign Trail are completed, the Candidates head towards Election night.

Candidates will also try and earn momentum on the campaign trail BY earning Endorsements in order to gain an advantage on Election night. Endorsement cards are goal cards which are achieved by meeting certain Voter interest levels. This momentum is spent to increase a Candidate's final score trying to win a State.

While the game is based in the political realm, it avoids connection to specific real world politics or organizations. On Election night, each of the 50 States and the District of Columbia are scored and their respect Electoral votes are scored for the Candidate which best meets their voter interests.

—description from the designer

Profiler is a cooperative game where players must guess which character one player, called the Profiler, is describing using “Character” and “Situation” cards. The Profiler places cards on a scale to guide the other players' thoughts. Together, they discuss and try to identify the correct character among those presented. The goal is to complete all 4 rounds as a team to achieve the highest score.

This party game combines thinking and humor, with each round sparking lively debates about the characters. With situations ranging from absurd to everyday life, Profiler fosters a fun group dynamic where interpretation and discussion are key to winning.

—description from the publisher

Sblocca la Porta: Freetime is an escape room for children.
Lots of riddles to solve with wit and logic and clues to discover in order to complete the adventure. An original and modern game that will involve young and old in a whirlwind of fun! A puzzle adventure and a journey to be enjoyed by recovering all the clues and solving the puzzles while training the mind and lateral thinking.
Included in the box are 3 adventures set in places of entertainment and leisure: the library, the toy shop and the house of horrors.

Krach 29 is a fast-paced, real-time trading game set during the tumultuous days of the 1929 stock market crash. Players take on the role of ambitious traders who must offload as many cards as possible to avoid losing money. Each player starts with a hand of cards, each containing two actions, and the goal is to make the best combinations to secure bonuses and avoid penalties. But watch out—if you're the last to place your cards, penalties await!

In this chaotic, party-style game, players must negotiate and trade cards with each other to find matching actions, while trying to sabotage their competitors. The player who loses the least money wins the game. Set in the roaring 1920s, this game immerses you in a world of fast deals, high risks, and fortunes won and lost in an instant.

—description from the publisher

De Influence is a card game, designed for 3 or 4 people, in which you will try to become the most important fashion influencer of all. Become a famous influencer, receive the best products from the best brands and completely change your life.

Play your cards to have the most "mediogramers" and attack the other players.

A game base on the song of the same name by Spanish rapper Er Christian.

boardgame: Survive the Tombs of the Cryptids
description:
A dungeon crawler tile based game that can be played solo or co-op with fellow wannabe survivors!


     Embark on a spine-chilling journey through ancient tombs, where every turn could be your last.



     Face off against 35 different terrifying cryptids in this survival horror board game. Whether you brave it solo or team up in co-op mode, the challenge is relentless!



     With 30 event cards, where YOU make the choice, every decision shapes your escape route, ensuring that each playthrough offers a unique and unpredictable adventure. Shuffled tiles and cards guarantee no two games are ever the same, keeping the experience fresh every time.



     Level up as you confront cryptids, boosting your skills and unlocking powerful items like the Time Reversal Artefact, Ancient Armor, Charmed Daggers, a Crystal Staff, and 40 more!



     Master this eerie labyrinth with strategic dice rolls, facing monstrous cryptids while unlocking ancient mechanisms to escape. With endless combinations, can you survive the Tombs of the Cryptids?


—description from the publisher

Lobotomy II: manhunt - 2024 Essen Promo

1 new character card and associated mini.

Brian Nicepond

Stats :

9 Health
2 Strength
3 Imagination
3 Dexterity
1 Wisdom

Abilities :

No regeneration
When a character in range (0-1) receive wounds, unless you pay 1 insanity you receive the wounds instead of them (reducing the damage by 1 to a minimum of 1).

90% G Spot
After defeating a monster gain 1 extra insanity

Odyssey
You may move up to 4 spaces as long as none of them contain monsters. Any other characters on the space you start on may move with you to the final space.

Golden eagles
Attack twice with 2 red dice (special effect = Pierce)

Jatra is a fun board game to help boost your kid's creativity. Jatra, was inspired by the Nepali festival, Gai Jatra (Procession of cows).

The objective of the game is to make the queen laugh making up silly stories, funny noises, and pretentious acting during the game. 
The illustrations itself is adorable with cute Nepali monsters and weird flying pigs. Best for kids Age 3-10.

Game Set up
1. Place all the cards face down as shown.
2. 3. 4. 5. 6. Start with the youngest player.
Pick an action card and get on stage and perform! The used card is collected in a discard pile.
After you complete the action on the action card you will gold coins as stated in the card. If you pick and action card with the queen you skip your turn.

If you pick a group activity card, all players needs to perform the action and everyone who completes the action earns a coin.

Note: Pick the object card only when it says on the action card. Used card goes back into the deck.

Winning the Game
3+ : When playing with the 3+ cards the game ends once any player gets the third queen card.
6+ : When playing with the 6+ cards the game ends
once any player gets the fourth queen card.

Winner
The player with the biggest stack of coins wins!

Waterloo: Napoleon's Last Battle is a simulation that recreates the battle between the French army led by Napoleon and the British, Dutch, and Prussian armies that occurred near Waterloo, south of Brussels, the capital of Belgium, on June 18, 1815. It's a game.

This game is a reprint of the Hiroki Ueda game published in 1989 by Show Planning. The components have been completely revamped, and the rules have been clarified by the designers themselves.

Banzai Magazine #15

boardgame: Unsolved Case Files: Photo Case – Angela Justice
description:
Karmen Justice witnessed her mother's murder at 4 years old and hunts down the killer years later.

Local lawyer and mayoral candidate, Angela Justice was stabbed in her home on July 15th, 2007. Her daughter Karmen, who was only four at the time, witnessed the attack while hiding under the bed. Despite the local sheriff's best attempts to solve the case, the killer was never found.

Karmen is now in college and on a mission to hunt down the killer to bring her mother justice. Surprising discoveries stand between her and the truth.

Can you help Karmen find the truth behind her mother's murder?

Suspects:

Jesse Winston - Sheriff who arrived first on the scene and closed the case without a suspect.

Jason Driver - Weapons expert who helped collect evidence and stayed back to help wrap-up the crime scene.

Denise Sanderson - Forensics Specialist who examined evidence in the lab, but found nothing helpful in identifying a suspect.

Ray Barlow - Team photographer who documented the crime scene and the evidence collection process.

Diego Alvarez - Team lead who organized the investigation and instructed techs on what to cover during the investigation.

This unsolved case file game is the UCF’s first photo centered case. Explore the story through more images, through suspect interviews, and other documents including newspaper articles, crime scene photographs, witness statements, and much more. Everything about this mystery game is fiction, but will feel just like you are a real detective.

—description from the publisher

In Grunling: Fantastic Fruits, players take turns building the most valuable set of Grunlings, each with unique scoring methods. Many Grunlings prefer to be collected in sets, and some sets are modular, varying from game to game to keep things fresh. Each turn presents tactical choices, from playing Action cards and managing your Fruit Basket to drawing new cards, with a memory element adding strategic depth. Watch out for the ever-shifting Nature Effects, which change in order and type every game and often wreak havoc, and the mysterious Darklings, who limit your discard options.

On your turn, you can play Action cards, manage your Basket by storing or swapping cards, and draw new cards from Draw and Discard piles. If your hand and Basket exceed the card limit, you’ll need to discard tactically. Leaf tokens can be acquired and spent to discard extra cards, giving you more control over your strategy.

The game ends when the final Effect card is triggered. Players then reveal their cards and tally points. The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the designer

The bigger they are, the harder they fall. Add a Builder’s Bounty set of bricks for the the ultimate in creative castle building.

42 Castle pieces (21 for each army)
12 gray 2×1 bricks
2 gray foundations
1 gray turrets
1 gray towers
1 gray spire
1 gray balcony
1 gray stairs
1 gray rocky steps
1 gray prison with gate
12 tan 2×1 bricks
2 tan foundations
1 tan turrets
1 tan towers
1 tan spire
1 tan balcony
1 tan stairs
1 tan rocky steps
1 tan prison with gate

In Silk's Trace you need to find matching patterns in a real-time environment before your opponents.

Roll the die. Then all players will simultaneously and individually try to locate the starting pattern shown on the die.  Once identified, trace the path of intertwined lines through a set of 3-6 cards in order to find the final pattern.   All players are doing this individually at the same time. Then quickly identify which of the 8 set aside maid cards has that symbol on it and grab it before your opponents can.  No matching maid cards, no problem, you can still gain a point by grabbing the 'no trace' marker before you opponents

Continue grabbing the maid cards with the matching patterns until you have 6 and can win the game with the title of Silk's Trace Expert.

If you want to add some variety to the game, you can play with optional rules to require having maid cards of 6 different postures to end the game or play that wrong guesses will actually cost your previously earned precious maid cards.

—user summary

The Ultraman Card Game is a trading card game designed based on the Ultraman Universe and combines collectability with playability that appeals to both adults and children.

The game will be released globally on Friday, October 25th 2024 in 15 countries and regions, with cards printed in Japanese, English, Simplified Chinese and Traditional Chinese.

In this game, each player use a 50-card deck to fight.Players will set their cards face down and then flip together.In the judgement phase each turn, compare BP in all battle areas to determine the outcome of each battle.After all battles in the judgment phase are completed, the player who wins three or more battles in a turn wins the game.

Friko Bestiale pits the populations of Friuli Venezia Giulia against each other in 5 epic battles fueled by humor, culture, and traditions.

Triestini, Friulani, Isontini, Bisiachi, Carnici, and Naoniani will face off in battles across the Gulf, Collio, Plains, Magredi, and Alps, using 110 thematic cards that feature everything from the bora wind to the Krampus, from osmize to purcit, from Gigi Pirola to the Plevan of Malborghet, from "no se pol" to "cumbinin." Each card is illustrated with an animal typical of the region.

In addition to winning battles, players will aim to collect 16 valuable artifacts, from the piria to the tractor, from the tai glass to the Olympic clanfe dive.

Soldiers, Generals, and Strolighi, each with a unique power depending on their population, will be victorious at the end of the fifth battle if they score the most points.

—description from the designer

Friko Bestiale mette in competizione le popolazione del Friuli Venezia Giulia in 5 epiche battaglie giocate a colpi di ironia, cultura e tradizioni. 
Triestini, friulani, isontini, bisiachi, carnici e naoniani si sfideranno così nelle battaglie del Golfo, del Collio, della Pianura, dei Magredi e delle Alpi, utilizzando 110 carte tematiche, dalla bora ai Krampus, dalle osmize al purcit, da Gigi Pirola al Plevan di Malborghet, dal no se pol al cumbinin. Ad ogni carta è associata una illustrazione che riguarda un animale tipico della Regione.
Oltre alla vittoria delle battaglie, le popolazioni punteranno anche a conquistare i 16 preziosi artefatti, dalla piria al trattore, dal tai alle clanfe olimpiche.
Soldati, Generali e Strolighi, ciascuno con un potere diverso a seconda della popolazione, risulteranno vincitori alla fine della quinta Battaglia se avranno fatto più punti degli altri.

—description from the designer (Italian)

coloWArs is a fast-paced card game for 2 or more players, ages 8 and up. Players take on the roles of the greatest spacetime painters, abducted by a mysterious entity and forced to compete in a magical tournament to the last brushstroke.

In coloWArs, players collect and play color cards to complete their canvas, strategically mixing primary and secondary colors. White magic aids their artistry, while black magic disrupts opponents' progress.

Gameplay is divided into rounds with four phases per player: exchange (optional), draw, action (optional), and discard. Players draft cards to build their hand, optionally exchanging unwanted cards, and then play color or magic cards to build their canvas. Tactically, some cards can be kept in hand to counter opponents' moves.

The first player to complete a perfect canvas with all primary and secondary colors wins instantly. Otherwise, when the shared deck is empty, the player with the highest total of points (indicated in the upper right corner of the cards) on their canvas at the end wins.

Sharpen your swords and ready your spells because the people of the Darkmoon Vale are in danger and they need your help! Gather up to three friends and explore the mysteries of the vale and uncover the villain behind the sinister forces that threaten to reduce the entire region to a pile of smoldering ash.

Pathfinder Quest is a cooperative adventure board game for 1–4 players. Build your character(s) from the ground up by choosing their ancestry, class, and ability cards—or dive right in and play a prebuilt iconic hero! From character creation to combat challenges and exploration, each player’s actions matter, with choices that ripple throughout the entire multisession campaign.

Designed to bridge the gap between quick, randomized dungeon crawlers and sprawling, prep-heavy delves, Pathfinder Quest offers a full campaign of 12 possible adventures, each lasting approximately 2 hours. With no GM required, an introduction that swiftly teaches the rules as you play, and a rich narrative that rewards teamwork, this game is sure to be a hit whether your group is new to tabletop gaming or seasoned veterans of the adventuring life!

Pathfinder Quest includes an Adventure Book (detailing each one of the possible dozen adventures you might experience), a Challenge Book (containing the hidden outcomes for many of your choices), map tiles, custom dice, hundreds of cards, monster pawns, and select miniatures as well!

—description from the publisher

Pathfinder Quest: Endless Adventure Mode uses the creatures, map tiles, and components within Pathfinder Quest's box to generate random encounters and challenges. Don't have time for a full session? Roll up some encounters and dive right in!

boardgame: Ode to Mare Nostrum: Conflicts of Western Europe 1212-1250
description:
On July 16th, 1212, the Coalition of Iberian Monarchs gained a decisive victory against the Almohad Caliphate in the Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa. This marked a shift of tide in the Spanish Reconquista and the Iberian Crusades. These were changing times, and the Iberian Christians finally saw a chance to make full use of the Peninsula's vast potential.
During this era, Western Europe saw no lack of ability in rulers. Aragon's Peter would soon be succeeded by his son, James I of Aragon, the Conqueror; North of the Pyrenees the Capetians of France was ruled by Philipe Augustus, first King of France, with his sons Louis VIII "the Lion" and Louis IX "the Saint" close in the line of inheritance; over the Alps the Holy See was governed by Pope Innocentius III, arguably one of the most powerful and ambitious Popes whom only Julius II could potentially match his perspectives; Towards the South of Rome, young Frederick Hohenstaufen of Sicily was seen by the Pope as a tame apprentice and a competent puppet, yet little did he know this pup would soon become the powerful wolf that would reshape Medieval Europe known as Frederick II of the Holy Roman Empire, “greatest of the princes of the earth”. The conflicts and cooperations between these magnificent men would shape order in the region for many years to come, until the next age of Sages almost 300 years later.
With nothing but respect and homage paid to the masterpiece history simulation games by Ed Beach, Here I Stand, Virgin Queen and Tanto Monta, Ode to Mare Nostrum seeks to build upon those sturdy foundations to formulate another distinct experience. A card-driven, panoramic multiplayer wargame set in 13th-century Western Europe, Ode to Mare Nostrum seeks for players to enact this period's major political, military, diplomatic, and economic events. 5 players will control major powers in Western Europe at the time: the Almohad Caliphate, the Iberian Nations, the Kingdom of France, the Hohenstaufen Family(or Frederick the Great himself) and the Holy See. Each power has its own path towards winning paved by unique mechanisms and interactions. In order to achieve victory, one must form diplomatic alliances, fight against each other, recruit troops, deploy your army, and overwhelm opponents with strength and strategy.

The second expansion of Gnomistic adds two new modules, a new main action, new resources, new cards, and many new ways to award points. This expansion also adds more complexity to the game with new layers of strategy and tricky decisions.

During the game, when you take all the cards from a path using the "Contribute to a Path" action, you will also gain resources: Conventina Shell tokens if you take the cards from the riverside path, Ninfa tokens if you take them from the flower's way, and mandrakes if you take the cards from the scary trail. You can use these tokens instead of coins to perform your paid actions, and by doing so, you will improve your nature track on your new player board to award aquamarine stones.

Also, during the game, a "Start of the Rainbow" card will be revealed, and after that, a new main action will become available: buying surprise cards from a new deck of cards. All the surprise cards are amazingly powerful and will give you a significant advantage, so all players will also need to buy these cards to stay in the competition.

—description from the designer

Travel back in time to the radical 1980s where you'll put together a crew of criminals for the heist of the decade. Play a Crew member to your tableau, move about the city to avoid the cops, gather intel, and then grab the most cash when the window of opportunity opens.  After you've stolen your big score, you'll need to cash out and launder that money to win the game.

In Disco Heist Laundry, you control a Kingpin and their gang of thugs aiming to launder the most money and get away with it so that they can retire in infamy. On your turn, you play a Crew card to your tableau, move about the city, and activate any abilities in your tableau to recruit, heist, extort, and escape from the cops. The perfect heist isn't the one with the most money, but instead the one you get away with. This game is your chance to compete with other criminals to recruit the perfect crew for the biggest score, and launder that money so you can retire in the lap of luxury.

—description from the designer

This card game was developed by 3dtotal Publishing and based on Cassandra’s comics. It is a clever game where you can score points by persuading your opposing humans you are good at this “life thing” – or by catching them lying about their own lives.

—description from the publisher

西安繁华录 (Splendor of Xi'an) is a Monopoly-like board game that invites players to explore the rich history and culture of Xi'an. Players move around a beautifully illustrated 2.5D map, collecting historical landmarks and treasures throughout the ancient city. The game incorporates Xi’an’s iconic cultural sites, such as the Terracotta Army, Bell Tower, and Xi’an Museum.

Additionally, players can unlock unique abilities by claiming historical figures like Qin Shi Huang at the Terracotta Army. They may also travel through time by drawing Dynasty Cards at the Ghost Market, which alters gameplay by adding buffs or debuffs until the card is removed at the Bell Tower.

Victory Conditions

     Be the wealthiest player by the end of the game.
     Collect 10 stars from Treasure Cards to win instantly.

Bastard is a fun children's game where the imagination gets free play and two well-known animals turn into a fabulous animal. Flip the card and be the fastest player to place your pieces on the two animals that you think the mix card consists of.

When you are done placing the pieces, flip the card in the middle of the table before the other players. If you're correct you get 1 point. first player with 10 points winner.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Discover an exciting card board game that is easy to learn and perfect for any occasion. Its quick setup allows you to start playing in minutes, and its replayability ensures that each game is unique. Plus, you can adjust the level of difficulty and game time so that both beginners and experts can fully enjoy the experience.

Survivors is a competitive game for 2 to 4 players where, on your turn, you must play a survivor from your hand or move them around the board, defeating zombies or defending yourself from other survivors. Each player has 4 survivors with different abilities that can be used throughout the game.

When you defeat a zombie, you’ll earn supplies; however, in the process, your survivor might get bitten by one of them, leaving them infected. Think carefully about your next move to avoid infection!

The game ends when a player can no longer move any of their survivors or has no survivors left in play. The player with the most supplies at the end wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: RONE: Exclusive Heroes – Morda and Lena Fragella
description:
RONE: Exclusive Heroes – Morda and Lena Fragella is a promotional card set for the game RONE, featuring two unique heroes: Morda and Lena Fragella. This set includes three cards for each hero, providing players with additional strategic options during gameplay.

Morda: When Morda levels up, the top card from your graveyard is returned to the top of your deck. The first damage dealt to Morda this turn is reduced by 1. This hero is a tribute to Pavel Mořický, the first playtester for RONE, who greatly contributed to the development of the game’s core concepts. In honor of his memory following a tragic car accident, this card features his portrait and serves as a lasting homage.

Lena Fragella: Lena Fragella allows you to increase your maximum number of technologies in play by 1, offering a tactical advantage in managing your battlefield resources.

These Kickstarter-exclusive heroes were only available as part of the campaign and will not be released to retail.

boardgame: Battle for the Deep: Powered by Axis & Allies
description:
In Battle for the Deep, four aquatic factions vie for control of the vast oceans: the Undersea Kingdom led by Coraline Oceanus, the Leviathans commanded by Scyphozoa Regina, the Protectors and their Turtle Mother, and the Denizens of the Deep, who are controlled by the necromancer Azazel Dreadborne.

After centuries of battle, it has become obvious that no one kingdom is powerful enough to bring peace to their world. The factions must choose an ally to win the day.

Battle for the Deep is a 2v2 battle experience where the players choose the teams and starting locations. Using gameplay mechanics from Axis & Allies, players battle for control of the underwater world.

—description from publisher

The 19th Century was a dynamic, yet turbulent time when huge areas of the Ottoman Empire, the Indian sub-continent, China, Indochina, and the East Indies were fought over by competing empires.

In Diplomacy: Era of Empire, seven great empires — Britain, France, Russia, Turkey, China, Japan, and the Netherlands — vie for control as alliances are formed and trust is betrayed. Players negotiate and outwit one another in a delicate balance of co-operation and competition to gain dominance of the region. Players must rely on their own cunning and cleverness, not dice, to determine the outcome of this game of conspiracies and conquest.

Diplomacy: Era of Empire is a standalone game for new or experienced Diplomacy players that's based on 1994's Colonial Diplomacy, but with an updated map for balance purposes, the removal of certain mechanisms that required three pages of errata, and other rule tweaks.

—description from the publisher

At the start of the game, a 5x5 grid of treasure tiles is placed face up on the table. Each player secretly draws a tile indicating their treasure color, then moves across the board during their turn, flipping tiles as they go. A player wins by being the first to flip all of their assigned treasure tiles chest side up, successfully hiding their treasures without revealing their color to opponents. While players aim to flip their own treasure tiles to the chest side, they can bluff by flipping other colors to confuse opponents. The game ends when a player flips all their treasure tiles, or when another player accidentally flips their final tile.

boardgame: My Little Pony: Festival of Lanterns
description:
Join Twilight Sparkle and her friends as they visit Mistmane's Eastern Village, where they will celebrate all of their accomplishments in restoring Equestria to its peaceful ways by releasing Cutie Mark lanterns. As the glowing lanterns rise up to form patterns in the sky, they all can feel the magic of friendship abound!

In My Little Pony: Festival of Lanterns, players have a hand of tiles depicting various color arrangements of floating lanterns, as well as an inventory of individual lantern cards of specific colors. When you place a tile, all players — both you and your opponents — receive a lantern card corresponding to the color on the side of the tile facing them. Trade mooncake tokens for new lantern cards, and return lantern cards to claim a dedication tile worth points. After all tiles have been placed, all players get one final turn, then whoever has the most points wins.

My Little Pony: Festival of Lanterns features gameplay similar to Christopher Chung's earlier game Lanterns: The Harvest Festival with some modifications, including a solo mode and a "Gala" expansion in which you add up to four friend cards to the game from the sixteen included; on a turn, you can now spend mooncakes to use the power of a friend to fain a bonus lantern, make a second dedication on a turn, force someone else to trade lanterns with you, and so on.

Discover an enhanced Grand Prix racing experience with the 1962 Expansion for LACORSA. This exciting addition introduces new cards that modify car performance, introduce pit stop strategy, and add the potential for accidents, bringing new thrills to every race.

The Expansion must be combined with the original LACORSA game, so you can now enjoy racing with up to 12 drivers with one car each or form six teams of two racecars each.

FIVE GREAT NEW FEATURES

Lead Driver Advantage: More talented drivers can push the pace, but not without risk.

Incidents: Two incident cards provide the possibility for crashes and DNFs.

Track Cards. Seven unique house rules for each race of the championship.

Performance Cards. 18 new Marker Cards with differentiated car performance.

Pit Stops: One pit stop card that allows drivers to pick up new cards during the race.

—description from the publisher

West Story: Pioneers is a collection of cards introducing more variety and complexity to the game.

There are new buildings like Sheriff, a new animal Racoon and cards introducing the Gold Symbol, which all get marked and executed immediately on purchase. For players looking for more asymmetry, there are Starting Cards to be put on player boards before the game even kicks off.

The expansion includes Automa Cards to play solo or with an additional automated opponent Tom!

—description from the publisher

ARDEVUR: The Game of Resources is a modern-mining themed medium-heavy economic simulation where every move counts.

With asymmetric player powers, tight strategy and high interaction, ARDEVUR promises an ever-changing gaming experience where the best strategy dominates all.

In the 2020s, a global Mining Fever roars louder than ever. At the peak of the Metal Age, mining critical materials is no longer about profit: it’s a chase for supremacy. The decade from 2014 to 2024 witnessed unprecedented technological breakthroughs across multiple sectors, such as AI and robotics, once again proving metals to be the backbone of human civilisation.

In the asymmetric and ever-changing gameplay of ARDEVUR: The Game of Resources, powerful mining corporations with unique strengths and strategies go head-to-head in a volatile, demand-driven economic landscape.

With Africa and Europe at the heart of the action, this 2 - 4 player competitive strategy game focuses on mining and supply chain operations, resource management and economic expansion, turning the board into a high-stakes corporate showdown, a race to meet global demand for critical minerals and dominate a turbulent market where every move counts.

Your growth and success depend entirely on your strategy: Explore new territories, extract and trade valuable resources, grow your capital and invest in tactical assets, all in pursuit of Total Control.




Inspired by the designer’s real-world experience as a CEO in the mining and metals industry, ARDEVUR delivers a tense and engaging gameplay experience grounded in realistic market mechanics, challenging players to balance short-term gains with long-term investments and outmaneuver rivals to stay ahead.

ARDEVUR transforms complex industry dynamics into an educational tabletop experience, spotlighting the vital role of metal commodities in our tech-driven future, while exposing the deceptive agenda of those who play the “Game of Resources”.




GAMEPLAY




Take control of a powerful Corporation and compete to build your own Mining Empire!




* ARDEVUR Commodities
 * G.R.E.A.T. Mining
 * Africa Mining Union
 * Machine Corp




Dive into an asymmetric and everchanging gameplay experience, exploring the unique strategy and playstyle of each Character, which allow you to negotiate, disrupt or gain tactical advantage over your competitors!

Acquire Territories and develop your Mining operations; Build and grow your infrastructural presence; Extract, process and sell your Minerals to the global market!

Manage your resources effectively to keep growing in an unforgiving environment, where every move counts. Fight your Competitors, who can damage your infrastructure, hack into your sensitive data, hijack your valuable cargo, and much more.

Dive into the deep theme of ARDEVUR: The Game of Resources - experience realistic dynamics of the mining industry like never before! Choose your strategy wisely, to dominate in the Metal Age and achieve Total Control!




TURN STRUCTURE




ARDEVUR: The Game of Resources is a game with a complex strategic framework, but it’s not hard to play!
Begin your journey by awarding Mining Territories through the Government Auction, and get your starting capital rolling with your first investment!




Your Turn consists of 4 Phases:




* Extraction: Collect Resources from your active mines
 * Strategy: Pick Development Cards or Action Cards and plan your next move
 * Development: Build and Upgrade Assets or Sell Resources to keep expanding
 * Reward: Collect Strategic Targets or Government Incentives based on your achievements




Run your operations with the goal of building the most efficient mining supply chain:




Begin Operations

* Source equipment, services, workforce and licenses
 * Explore a territory
 * Set up infrastructure in your region
 * Establish your first mining operation
 * Collect resources




Capitalise and reinvest

* Sell resources to grow your capital




Keep expanding

* Build assets to grow your operational efficiency
 * Upgrade assets to power up your supply chain




Fight to gain supremacy

* Execute tactical strikes against your competitors
 * Reach strategic targets
 * Collect rewards and government incentives
 * Grow your presence and establish regional dominance




WIN THE GAME




Race to reach your corporate goals before your rivals!

Earn the most Supremacy Points to defeat the competition, with either righteous or unethical business practices!

The winning score is determined by a combination of reached targets, owned assets and available wealth.

Will you dominate the industry, or get left in the dust?




—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
During the Sengoku Era, fierce cat warriors come together to battle in a  psychological game of Rock Scissors Paper!

This is a rock-paper-scissors game for two players with no cards the same.
By changing the way the cards are dealt according to the age and level of the players, you can play in a way that suits each player.

Players are dealt 3 cards each time and draft until they have 6 each.  Both players then reveal 1 card from their hand.  The card with the stronger symbol wins.  If there is a draw, the card with the higher number wins.  But watch out for the Shinobi Ninja, that card can win against stronger cards, but is weak against lower numbered cards.  The first to win 3 times is the winner!

Quadro is a tower-building game played with 45 plastic blocks in three different colors and dice with colored circles that match the colors of the blocks. The blocks are stacked in a tower formation. Each story consists of three blocks of different colors placed adjacent to each other along its long side, and each story is placed perpendicular to the previous (so, for example, if the blocks in the first story point north-south, the second story blocks will point east-west).

The game is intended for 2 or more players. The first player rolls the dice and takes one block at a time from any place of the tower(except the top) in the color determined by the dice. The player who drops a block while removing loses the game.

The fate of the Earth once again depends on five girls. Help them fight the difficulties. Remember that WITCH are only truly powerful when they act together. Therefore, you must combine the corresponding elements to win the battle against the cruel Nerissa.

You begin the game with a six-sided JOKER card and 4 adventure cards. Shuffle the six-sided card deck and put them facedown. Place the two starting six-sided cards in any way so that they touch on one side(that they match).

You will draw a six-sided card and try to put it down to the starting cards so they touch on two sides minimum(that they match) and you have a matching adventure card too otherwise other players can steal your card.

The game ends when all the adventure cards are fulfilled or when all the six-sided cards are already on the table and no more can be added from the hand. At this moment, the players add up the points obtained for the completed adventure (however, they must remember the minus points for completing with the help of the joker) and bonus points for multiple combinations of cards, which they must write sideways on a piece of paper. For each remaining adventure card in the hand, the players deduct two points, and for the remaining unlaid six-sided cards, even a full five points. The one who scored the most points in total becomes the winner.

-Introduction
A deadly plague has struck the Kingdom of Trokv, reaping many victims in a short period of time. Word has spread that the Royal City of Dair is offering a cure for those willing to pay a large monetary sum, attracting crowds from all over the Kingdom.
You are one of those pilgrims, on their way to the Royal City.
The journey will be hard, with many dangers on the horizon. Do you have what it takes to make it out alive?

-Gameplay Overview
In Plaguefall, you'll have to traverse 4 cards, called Regions. Regions have 4 spaces each, and each space may propose you with quests or combat encounters.

Quests are small side missions which may earn you gold or equipment. Combat instead will wear down you and your weapons, and encounters will only get tougher as you get closer to your goal.
Of course, Infection is always looming in the shadows, threatening to erode your physical capabilities.

If you manage to survive all challenges on your travels, one final trial will decide your fate: Gold. End the game with enough gold in your pockets and you'll have obtained the elusive cure, fail to do so and suffer a rotten fate.

Plaguefall offers:


     8 Map Cards to form a different Kingdom each time;



     14 Weapons, with 6 different classes and abilities;



     16 Quest Cards;



     Dangerous & Infectious encounter types.


All of this within 10-15 minutes!

—description from designer

Adding the Alien Invasion Mini-Expansion to Twisted Cryptids introduces a new adversary for the Cryptids to compete against, allowing you to play a semi-cooperative or solo variation of the game.

This Mini-Expansion includes 1 tarot-sized UFO Control Panel, an Alien Activity Deck (7 Cards), and a UFO Meeple. Each round, the UFO will move around The Wilderness and Abduct Humans before they have a chance to encounter any Cryptids.


     The UFO meeple denotes the UFO’s current Site, which is the Site from which the UFO may Abduct Humans.



     The UFO Control Panel holds all Humans abducted throughout the game. The UFO Control Panel is divided into 4 Pods, and each Pod can hold up to 5 Humans. Three of the Pods correspond to a specific Flavor of Human (and can only hold Humans of that Flavor), and the fourth Pod can hold any Flavor of Human.


Note: When playing solo or with 2 players, Humans cannot be placed in the fourth Pod; it must remain empty.

-Alien Activities operate the UFO to move it around The Wilderness and Abduct Humans! If an Alien Activity Abducts a Human, move that Human from the UFO’s current Site to a corresponding Pod on the UFO Control Panel. Some Alien Activities refer to the Energy of the UFO; this equals the number of players plus the number of filled Pods.

This expansion features two new modules. The Crop Circles Module introduces another element of long-term strategy. The Permanent Skill Tiles Module features diverging asymmetry. You may decide to include one or both modules in your plays of SILOS, but we only recommend the Permanent Skill Tiles Module for experienced players

Note: This mini-expansion increases the complexity and playing time of the game.

—description from the draft rulebook preview

Our journey through the galaxy is long and arduous, yet we find ourselves making the most of our downtime (for better or worse). Each ambassador has many private meetings with their planetary government — briefings and progress reports via space transmission. We also have regular joint transmissions with the Council of the Solar System, the interplanetary authority that formed and funded EGO, where the ambassadors seize every opportunity to outshine each other in front of their superiors.

If only those transmissions were the most eventful part of our flights. Cabin fever has resulted in more than one quarrel within, and Space Pirates threaten our journey without. Our quest for galactic power is not without its costs.

The ID (Interstellar Disruptions) Expansion features two new modules. The Ships Module extends the journey map with thrilling ship boards featuring new competitive transmission events. The Special Alliance Tokens Module increases the variety of alliance benefits.

— Description from the publisher

This expansion includes three modules that can be added individually or in any combination.  The Navigation Tokens module makes navigating the board a little easier and shortens the game.  The Hyper Accelerator module adds an upgradeable engine that changes the strategic movement possibilities.  And the Artificial Nebulas let's you make permanent changes to the map that block spaceship movement and increase a planets orbital speed.

—description from the draft rulebook

Backstage Business is a murder mystery game for 18 to 22  guests and one host. It is set in 1986, backstage after a 1980s hair-metal concert.

Backstage Business is a musical evening of 1980s hair-metal bands, investigation, betrayal, and music! Players are in the middle of the action, and it is up to them to wheel and deal to get what they want.

Backstage Business lasts for about four hours and is suited to an evening.

—description from the publisher

三藩之亂：平西王 ("Three Feudatories: Pingxi") is a 1-2 player strategic level wargame to simulate the Revolt of Three Feudatories in Qing dynasty in China.

There are 8 rounds in one game. Player commands the Qing armies to conquer the rebels in southwestern China.  Qing armies may move into the revolt areas and roll the die to attack rebel armies. Be careful, the rebels may ambush you randomly. In the end round, you need to score at least 7 vps by capturing the rebel feudatories and sweep the rebel armies.

Three Feudatories: Pingxi is the first game of the Revolt & Conquer series. You can combine Pingxi and Yanping maps together for playing a campaign game with 3-4 players.

—description from the designer

三藩之亂：延平王 ("Three Feudatories: Yanping") is a 1-2 player strategic level wargame to simulate the Revolt of Three Feudatories in Qing dynasty in China.

There are 10 rounds in one game. Player commands the Qing armies to conquer the rebels in southwestern China.  Qing armies may move into the revolt areas and roll the die to attack rebel armies. Be careful, the rebels may ambush you randomly. In the end round, you need to score at least 7 vps by capturing the rebel feudatories and sweep the rebel armies.

Three Feudatories: Yanping is the second game of the Revolt & Conquer series. You can combine Pingxi and Yanping maps together for playing a campaign game with 3-4 players.

—description from the designer

Tritium is an abstract strategy game for two players, who take turns creating and growing regions, and claiming them. Once the board is full, the player who controls most tiles wins.

				
				
					Setup
     Board: a hexagon of hexagons without its center cell. Recommended sizes are 5 to 7.
     Hexagonal tiles in three colors, each in an amount equal to the number of cells of the board divided by three. These pieces are shared by both players.
     Player flags in two colors, in sufficient quantities.


Prepare one flag for each player next to the board, keep the rest in a supply.

				
				
					DefinitionsA region is a complete group of connected cells containing tiles of a single color. A region is free if it contains no player flags. A player controls a region if they have a strict majority of flags in it.

				
				
					GameplayPlayers take turns. On their turn, a player must take one of the two possible actions:


     Place a hexagonal tile of any remaining color on any empty cell. OR
     If they have a flag of their own color prepared next to the board, place it on top of any tile in a free region. Once both prepared flags have been played, prepare again one flag for each player.


If they cannot perform any of these two actions, they must pass.

				
				
					EndThe game ends when both players pass in succession, that is: all the tiles have been placed, and there are no more free regions.

Each player's score is the total number of tiles in all the regions they control. The player with the highest score wins.

In the rare case of a draw, the player who played a flag first loses.

—description from the designer

You and your friends are camping out in the wilderness. You decide to throw a friendly competition to see who can make the yummiest smores. Everyone knows the key to a delicious s'more is a perfectly toasted mallow. You will try to master the art of roasting mallows over the campfire. Will you be able to tame the flames and bend them to your will or will they burst your mallows into ashes. You have to use the supplies you brought in order to manage the flames with kindling, logs, water buckets, and a fan to tame the dreaded and unruly smoke.

Players will take turns drafting useful items to stoke or douse the flames in various ways and choosing where to sit around the fire pit. Moving closer gives you a +1 heat bonus while moving away gives you -1 heat. Space is limited, you will have to manage the flames while keeping an eye on your fellow campers.

After all turns have been taken, a log is placed on the fire. This happens by placing heat cubes in a dice tower to determine which sections get more heat cubes. Players will immediately track their heat status. They can choose to score their mallows at any time for points but to get the most points you'll need to go for "Toasty". But be careful, if you overheat your mallows, they will burn and lose you points.

The game ends after four logs (4 Rounds) and players reveal and count up their total mallow points. The player with the most is the winner.

—description from the designer

MechCommander's Handbook is intended to be one part small-scale campaign book (rooted in Chaos Campaign), one part overview of warfare and tactics (i.e. both in-universe and pure gameplay), and one part a guide to the product line and world of force building.

Sometimes, it takes two to survive. Strong bonds have been formed between two adventurers, having faced many challenges on the Island, and they have become inseparable! They have sworn they will get through this together!

Mini expansion of 2 extra meeples in each color, with a "heart shaped" symbol on the bottom.

Replace your Adventurers with a value of 1 by the two BFF meeples of your color. The game is played the same way as usual. At the end of the game, check your meeples on the Rescue Islands. If you have saved both BFFs in the same color, score 12 points! If, however, you have saved only one (or none), score 0 points. The rest of your saved Adventurers score normally.

Mind Me! includes 100 cards with 300 questions, 7 pens and 7 erasable slates

Everyone takes turns drawing a card and reading the statement out loud. For example, “Me, on a scale of 1 to 20, I am a handyman….”

Everyone writes a response on their slate, including the one who read the card aloud.

Everyone shows their slate: the one who has the answer closest to that of the person who read the card wins the point!

We then change reader, question, and continue the game until a player obtains 7 points.

Do you think you know your loved ones better than they know you? We'll see that!

Grim Reaper has several cards up his sleeve, and the only way to save yourself is by playing your cards right... by condemning someone else.

The goal of the game is to be the player with the fewest deaths at the end of the last round. All players take the same number of turns.

On your turn: you declare an attack on another player. Other players may play tokens and/or a curse card. The attacked player takes a scenario card, chooses one of the two scenarios, and announces the time of death. The countdown begins. You have to tell your story before time runs out.

Make the best story in the shortest time possible and you'll be safe. Fail... and you'll be history.

君到姑苏见 (Scenes of Suzhou) is a Monopoly-like board game that immerses players in the historical and architectural charm of Suzhou. Players move around a 2.5D illustrated map, collecting properties and landmarks throughout the city. The game incorporates Suzhou’s iconic gardens, gates, and ancient town features.

Victory Condition

     Be the wealthiest player at the end of the game.
     Alternatively, achieve instant victory by collecting all four city gates.

In Cats vs Dragons, players summon armies of cats and dragons to control the four dragon gods of Ekratoria while harnessing feline-themed spells to create chaos for opponents and fend off hostile spells.

Each round, all players receive the same number of resource points, which are used to play cards from their hand on their turn. Cards include creatures (which are worth points), spells (to interfere with other players) and artefacts (for points and to block certain spells), and players draw them randomly from a shared deck. When played, creatures and artefacts must be assigned to one of the dragon gods, whereas spells disappear after one use.

Each turn, a player can draw one card from the communal deck, and the game ends after the last card is drawn and everyone has had a final turn. The player who has the most points on a dragon god controls that god, and the player who controls the most gods wins.

Lightweight and quick to play this game is very dynamic, with a balance of strategy and randomness.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Invasion: The Conquest of Kings – Heroes of Kaldora
description:
The proud leaders of their armies will lead their followers to victory or defeat, as they clash with sword, axe, bow, and spell. Kings and Queens will rise and fall, and their heirs will follow in their footsteps, to enact vengeance on their sovereign’s killer. Some will collect a pile of crowns, proof of their enemy’s demise, others only death and defeat. To decide who will reign supreme over the lands of Kaldora, every ruler will be tested, and only the strongest will survive.

Invasion Heroes of Kaldora is a competitive strategy game where players will control powerful heroes in a dance of death and triumph. Gain resources through building or taking castles, and grow your empire, through land or growing armies. Take on the role of a mighty hero with a specialized class that is purely unique. Use your wits, politics, and brute force to fight your way to victory, for when the battles are over, only one hero will collect the crowns of the others, and rule over Kaldora.

—description from the publisher

It contains questions in Cres, geography, biology, history, literature, art and mixed topics. A rod suitable for the topic can be placed in the cylinder, and the spikes are turned based on the answers. If all answers are correct, the rod can be pulled out.

Kresz, földrajz, biológia, történelem, irodalom, művészeti, és vegyes témakörökben tartalmaz kérdéseket. A hengerbe a témakörnek megfelelő rúd helyezhető, válaszok alapján elfordítjuk a tüskéket. Ha minden válasz megfelelő a rúd ki húzható.

Horrified: Krampus is a special single monster addition for either Horrified: Greek Monsters or Horrified: World of Monsters.

The monsters of Horrified have begun to roam! Do you dare to face off against Krampus, the demon of the season?

St. Nicholas and the Children are scattered around the board. They function as citizens or legends but are unique in that they are ignored by all Monsters except Krampus who can see them for what they are... St Nicholas and a bunch of naughty naughty children. Krampus is a level 5 frenzy monster out to capture them!

As Heroes your job is to gather the Children safely around St. Nicholas. If Krampus defeats St. Nicholas or any Children the Terror Level will rise, and they will be held hostage in Krampus’s bag until freed. Once all Children are safely under St. Nicholas’ protection you may attempt to defeat Krampus.

Follow instructions in Horrified: World of Monsters to learn about combining some Horrified sets.

Prepare to be Ho… Ho… Horrified!

As subject of many a tale, there were many who doubted that the magical realm of the mountain Berg exists - until its recent discovery. Step into the shoes of a chronicler, who buckles up with his trusty crew of dwarves and goats to explore this wonderland. The Chronicles of Berg are yet unwritten and it is up to you to capture the mystical mountain's many tales: There are tales of gems as big as houses, grand feasts, feisty beasts, the fearsome Krampus and many more on this alpine fairy-tale mountainscape. Will your story become history?

The Chronicles of BERG is a friendly, yet competitive 2-5 player strategy game. It combines elements of deck-building and route planning and gives you small narrative decisions on the tales your chronicler writes down. It is your goal to gather the most renown by the time the game ends, so that your chronicles are the ones to go down in history. Each turn represents the start of a new expedition and a new chance to grow your renown by helping the new settlers of Berg and writing down tales in your chronicle. After a set number of turns, the game ends as the Mountain has been fully settled and its many stories have already been told.

Each player starts with a deck of five action cards. The players take turns playing one card at a time, which determines the actions they take. These actions allow you to move your pieces on the board, gather resources, help the settlers on Berg, use the finest of dwarven tools and capture the various stories of the mountain. During gameplay, you will add more action cards to your deck and you will write down the stories you gathered in your chronicles, which gives you unique bonuses and possibilities for the rest of the game.

Each playthrough offers a new experience as the layout of the mountain, the tools available, the stories you experience and your historian vary from game to game.

—description from the designer

The adult party game for vegans. Based on the classic Cards Against Humanity, Plants Against Veganity is a humorous take on plant-based living in a meat-eating world, making fun of vegans just as much as everyone else. Designed and written by vegans, and published through This Is Not A Game Ltd., the world's first vegan tabletop games company.

The goal of the game is to make the funniest possible combinations of cards.

Each turn, one player (dubbed 'the Vegan Overlord') takes the topmost green card (or "prompt" card) in the face-down deck in the centre of the table. This prompt card is read out with a blank space included.

The other players have a hand of white cards, or "response" cards, which are used to fill in the blank on the green card. They pick whichever card in their hand they deem to fill in the prompt card in the funniest, most accurate, or best fitting way. They place this face down near the prompt card.

The Vegan Overlord picks up the response cards, shuffles them, and reads them out in conjunction with the prompt card.

They select whichever response card they deem the best. The player who played this response (white) card wins the prompt (green) card.

The Vegan Overlord position then moves on to the next player.

Play continues until all prompt cards have been used.

The player with the most Prompt cards at the end wins the game.

—description from the designer
publishers: This Is Not A Game Ltd.

description:
MicroMacro: Jigsaw – Funfair combines the charm of jigsaw puzzles with the investigative fun of the MicroMacro series. Based on the concept of the original Game: MicroMacro, this edition introduces jigsaw-style pieces—yet, like other titles in the series, you can play directly on the large map without needing to complete the puzzle first.

The funfair setting is a playful excerpt from the world of the award-winning cooperative detective game MicroMacro: Crime City. While this version contains no criminal cases, it features over 50 kid-friendly "search and find" tasks to solve once the puzzle is assembled.

Perfect for families and younger detectives looking for a lighter, puzzle-packed MicroMacro experience!
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description: It is said that a few sips of wine will solve everyone's tongue. Are you also in the most philosophical mood when the good wine is bubbling? Then you will really enjoy this game! Do you have that feeling when you've done so many rounds that you already speak EVERY language with confidence and have a wise thought for EVERYTHING? Then you can really excel in Pialógus! Grab a card, pick one of the topics, and say the first thing you can think of about it, and let the others continue this wine-fueled conversation. But be careful, because if you sit down at the party, you can drink the penalty rounds!

TripLets is a simple word building game where players compete to make three-letter words within a 3x3 grid. For two or more players ages 6+, plus a solo mode.

COMPONENTS:

     5-inch game wooden gameboard with 3x3 grid
     80 magnetic letter tiles
     4 dry erase scoreboards
     three-letter word list
     dry erase marker.


GOAL:
Score the highest points by making the three-letter words
    
SETUP:

     Shuffle and place the letter tiles face down between the players.
     Give each player a scoreboard.
     Grab a pen and paper to keep track of words made.
     Take nine random tiles and place within the game board, one tile per cell.
     Give each player a random letter tile.


GAME PLAY:
Playing in turns, a player can perform one of two actions

Place their tile onto an existing tile in the grid
 or
Move a tile within the grid.

Making Words
Upon placing a tile, check to see if one or more three-letter words have been made in any of eight directions.

Scoring a Word
If a new word is made, total the value of the letters and add to your score on your scoreboard. You only can score on words connected to the letter moved or revealed.

Stealing a Word
Once a player says their turn is over, any player can “steal” a word missed by their opponent. This happens when the player fails to spot a word. The player finding the new word scores the value.

Tracking New Words
Each word made must not have been previously formed. You cannot repeat words. You can track words made on a separate piece of paper. Optionally, use our free tracking app at www.brainy.games/triplets.

Validity of Words
Any word that is not an acronym or abbreviation is allowed. Optionally, allow first names (GUS,ABE).

WINNING:
After all tiles have been played, the person with the highest score will be the winner. In case of a tie, play another round.

—description from the designer

boardgame: El Monstruo Debajo de la Cama: One Card
description:
The player must fill the courage track before the monster track or fear track is filled.

Roll the die and decide whether to confront the monster or cross out spaces on the fear track. If you challenge, make a roll for the monster. If you win, you add courage. If you challenge and the monster rolls a higher value on the die, the monster adds courage.

Can you face your fear and defeat the monster?

GloBUM! promotional card - Year 2025.

Preparation: At the start of the game, shuffle this card into the animal deck.

Rules: Kay, due to his size, needs two small value balloons to fly.
On your turn, you will have to play one of the two requested colors, and then wait for all the people to do the same to play the next color. 
Whoever adds the lowest value between both required balloons will win the Kay's card.


Tarjeta promocional de GloBUM! - Año 2025.

Preparación: Al comenzar el juego, baraja esta carta en el mazo de animales.

Reglas: Kay, por su tamaño, necesita dos globos de valor pequeño para volar.
En tu turno, tendrás que jugar uno de los dos colores solicitados, y luego espera a que todas las personas hagan lo mismo para jugar el color siguiente. 
Quien sume el menor valor entre ambos globos requeridos, ganará la carta de Kay.

—description from the designer

The Cursed Menagerie: Endless Nights adds 4 new ghosts and 12 Charms—these new components help your investigator to thwart the spirits. But you'll have to pay close attention to the clues of which ghost you face or you might just find yourself in a dire situation!

This first expansion for Dinner Party contains 45 new Guest cards as well as Coffee, a new Drink.

Coffee can be claimed from the Bar for one Drink icon, like Water. It doesn't go on Guests, but instead can be discarded to allow re-rolling any number of dice.

Guests in this expansion include Honoré de Balzac, Jane Austen, Niccolò Machiavelli, Malcolm X, the Elephant Man, Nefertiti and René Descartes.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Path of Civilization: Gwenesday Promo Card
description:
Available at Spiel 2024.

Level III Leader

Worth 5 VP

Card number GL-50

When you acquire Gwen, ou go directly to graveyard. Until the end of the game, every of your cube army that died in conflict go on Gwen card.
At the end, 5VP + 1 per cube on card.

boardgame: Dinner Party: Time to Get Served
description:
This second expansion for Dinner Party includes 45 additional Guests, as well as a new mechanic, Point Tokens.

Point Tokens look like little plates, and can be claimed by many Guests from this set if their specific conditions are fulfilled. At scoring, each Point Token is worth 1 Point. Depending on the Guest, this can open new ways to victory, new risks, and new strategies.

Guests from this expansion include Sun Tsu, Queen Victoria, Hernán Cortés, Agatha Christie, Montezuma, Benito Mussolini and H.P. Lovecraft.

—description from the designer

Heritage Promo Cards 2024 was available at Spiel 2024.

Three new monuments and one new artifact. All four of them are shuffled into the standard deck.

Expansion Icon: Mask

6 Exclusive cards: Downtown, Market Street, Training Area, Main Temple, Panorama, Terraces.

Available at Spiel 2024 and included as part of The Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign 'Pack C.'

Car Wars: Tailgate Trouble is a single-player expansion for Car Wars Sixth Edition. Inside are 32 cards and one sheet of tokens originally included in the Dropped Weapons Pack, as well as the Kinetic Diffuser from Road Tiles and Off-Road Suspension from Playmat #1. Add new weapons, structures, accessories, and terrain to your game, including Paint Grenades, Spikedropper, Spikes, and Wreckage. With Tailgate Trouble, you’ll be ready to rule the road!

—description from publisher

Aggressive Arsenal is a single-player expansion for Car Wars Sixth Edition that comes with 40 cards to add to your collection. Customize your game with enhanced options for weapons, structure, accessories, and terrain. From the stopping power of the Dreadnaught to the precision of the Advanced Firing Interface, Aggressive Arsenal has what you need to secure the victory! This set reprints all cards originally found in Miniatures Set 1. It’s time to get aggro!

—description from the publisher

Risk 2210 A.D.: Frontline contains four expansions previously released only as tournament prizes in the mid-2000s.


     Mars: Shake up combat by fighting in two new locales: Mars, and the Martian moons Phobos and Deimos. Unlike the Earth map, these maps add neutral territories that don't belong to any continent, so no continent bonus can be gained from having control of them.



     Tech Commander: Introduce a sixth commander to the game, with a new command card set that adds advanced weapons and tactics such as mind control and technical espionage.



     Factions: Before the game begins, each player chooses one of six factions, which determines the amount of energy, cards, and commanders you start with, as well as which special abilities you have.



     Invasion of the Giant Amoebas: At the beginning of a turn, the active player draws from the amoeba event deck, with amoebas being an extraterrestrial threat with advanced technology that will threaten everyone.

Car Wars Offensive Ordnance, a single-player expansion, features all the cards from Miniatures Set 2 as well as the hazard cards from Road Tiles. Stay powered up with the Thundercat Battery and duel with your opponents using the Lightning Scythe. Upgrade your vehicle with the Roll Bar and Reinforced Weapon Brackets, but watch out for that Icy Road. Twists and turns await on the freeways of the future, but Offensive Ordnance will help you come out on top!

—description from the publisher

This single-player expansion comes with 45 cards originally found in Uncle Al's Upgrade Pack (but not the checkpoint cards). Customize your vehicle, including unique weapons, accessories, crew, upgrades, and more! Heat things up with a Fire-Resistant Coating, then use the Turreted Heavy Rocket to stop your opponents in their tracks. Need help making a precise shot? Add the Steady-Aim Gimbal to your arsenal for some extra edge.

With Car Wars Albert's Advances, you’re ready to roll – offensively!

—description from the publisher

The Silver family maintains an iron grip on the three planets Ceres II, Virenos, and Mithras, but their reign may soon be over. You can play the wayward heir to the monarchy as Roux, or lead the Viren rebellion as Sima.

Unstoppable: Tyrant's End introduces two new playable characters to Unstoppable, new cards for the core decks and upgrade deck, and a new keyword. Take your card crafting to new heights!

—description from the publisher

Car Wars Gonzo Gear is a single-player expansion containing 40 all-new cards to add to your favorite autoduelling game. Take a swing at your opponent with a Wrecking Ball, or get up close and personal with a Katana. Use the Advanced Targeting Computer to get a read on other drivers, or fake them out with the help of your Fake Passenger. Gonzo Gear is a great way to add even more thrills to every turn!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Trek: Picard – Murder on the Titan-A
description:
A Murder Mystery for 1-6 Players

Dive into the world of Star Trek: Picard to solve a murder.

A murder mystery game for 1+ players. Enter the world of Star Trek: Picard as you try to solve a murder and exonerate Captain Liam Shaw.

Use augmented reality technology to submit evidence, get hints, and launch Jean-Luc Picard quotes.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Star Trek Mystery & Escape Room Games (Beadle & Grimm’s)

boardgame: Star Trek: Strange New Worlds – Mary Celeste
description:
An Escape Room Game for 1-6 Players

Join the Away Team from Star Trek: Strange New Worlds, explore a derelict ghost ship, and try to unlock its secrets.

Includes an Augmented Reality Cadet badge that you’ll use to solve puzzles and explore the ghost ship.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Star Trek Mystery & Escape Room Games (Beadle & Grimm’s)

description:
The game is about collecting "sets" of candy-shaped cards. A set consists of four candies that share the same color and pattern but differ in shape: triangle, square, circle, and star. There are 12 possible sets in the game.

Each player starts with seven cards, and on their turn, they ask another player for a specific card (e.g., "blue striped square"). If the opponent has it, they must hand it over. The player continues asking until they make an incorrect guess, then they draw from the deck, and the next player goes. Special "Amnyamchik" cards let players request any candy of the same color from another player.

The game ends when all cards are played, and the player with the most complete sets wins. A simplified version for children reduces the number of sets in play.

Excess Baggage is a mini expansion for the game MUAMBA, now allowing up to 5 players in the game. As a result, the customs officers had to call for reinforcements: the friendly and nosy police dog "Brazix K9lha."

Contains:

14 new merchandise cards
1 rule booklet
2 police tokens
1 police dog token

Six playmats that are as well an expansion as an accessory that are sold separately by Explor8.

They have a unique AI printed on them, and if you play cards on certain spots, you trigger an effect like change the color of the card just played, exchange 2 cards, change the number of the card or add and discard a card to your reserve.

boardgame: Trekking the National Parks: Postcards from the Parks
description:
The Postcards from the Parks Expansion adds 18 new cards to the game called Trekking the National Parks. At setup, you’ll select any two of them to add to the display. They introduce unique rules and strategies that can be combined in different ways to shake up your game of Trekking the National Parks. The set also includes coin tokens (worth 1 VP each) which are utilized on some of the Postcards.

This expansion is compatible with both the 2nd and 3rd Editions of Trekking the National Parks.

—description from the publisher

Customer Disservice is a game where you get a chance to play out your fantasy scenario in customer service by letting the customer know what you’re really thinking. Each player takes turns choosing a customer message. The other players then chooses a card from their hand that represents how you would really respond to the customer and another card that represents how you would want to respond to a customer (but never would in real life). Then the player with the message card reads off the responses and chooses the best one from each category. Players get a point for each card that is chosen as a winning response. The player with the most points, after each person takes a turn as the customer, wins the game.

The perfect game to play after work with your friends or coworkers after a day of dealing with horrible customers and clients. Choosing cards that say how you would really like to respond is the perfect therapy needed when having to deal with customers all day long. But beware, this game has explicit words and is NSFW, unless you have coworkers who are down with swearing and making fun of customers.

—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. The numbers on the die show the thrust with which the individual players (astronauts) fly through space. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. If a spaceship lands on an occupied space, the spaceship standing there is put 1 space back.

Each player receives a spaceship and a starting capital of 3,000 Solari. The players deposit an ore-marker on the planet "Alpha". This ore is later picked up and transported to the customs station. The value of this ore is 2,000 Solari. The players start at Uranus. 
The winner is the one who is the first to land on the target planet Earth. The earth must be rolled directly. If an astronaut succeeds in doing this, the next player can prevent his landing by paying 4,000 Solari to the bank. If the latter does not have 4,000 Solari left, he can ask the third player to pay the rest. The pilot whose landing was denied due to this payment must now return to orbit and roll a 1 or 2 to be able to land again. The landing can be prevented by other players as long as the players have enough Solari.

金陵风华 (Grandeur of Nanjing) is a Monopoly-like board game that invites players to explore Nanjing, one of China’s most culturally significant cities. Players move around a 2.5D illustrated map, collecting properties and landmarks throughout the city. The game incorporates Nanjing’s iconic historical and cultural landmarks, such as the Ming City Wall, Yuejiang Tower, Presidential Palace, Yangtze River Bridge, and Nanjing Museum.

Victory Conditions

     Be the richest remaining player at the end of the game.
     Collect four Spirit Beast Tokens at the 四方聚气 (Four Directions Gathering) point.

Три пирога ("Three pies") is a children's game where you help Grandma Bear harvest ripe raspberries and bake delicious pies.

At the beginning of the game, players receive 5 cards from a common deck and a basket card. There are raspberry tokens, pie tokens, and the remaining cards in the general stock.

During his turn, the leader must play a bear cub card (and perhaps an additional helper card) on the discard pile and get as many raspberries as indicated on those cards, or play a card that harms the other player (stealing raspberries, returning raspberries to reserve, skipping turn, etc.). Players get up to 5 cards, and the turn passes to the next person.

As soon as a player collects 6 raspberries in their basket, they must say "In the oven!" and trade 6 raspberies for a pie token. That token remains with that player until the game ends.

The first one to collect three pie tokens wins!

—user summary

•••
В детской карточной игре "Три пирога" вам предстоит помочь Бабушке Медведице собрать спелый урожай малины для выпечки вкусных пирогов.

В начале игры игроки получают по 5 карт из общей колоды и карту корзинки. В общем запасе находятся жетоны малинок, жетоны пирогов и оставшиеся карты.

В ходе своего хода ведущий игрок должен разыграть в сброс карту медвежонка (и возможно дополнительного помощника) и получить столько малинок на карту корзинки, сколько будет указанно суммарно на этих картах, либо сыграть в сброс карту как-то вредящую оппоненту (украсть малинки, вернуть малинки в запас, пропуск хода и т.п.) при этом указав на него. Игроки добирают карты до 5 штук и ход переходит следующему игроку.

Как только игрок набирает в своей корзинке 6 малинок, он должен сказать "В печку!" и обменять 6 малинок на жетон пирога. Жетон пирога останется с данным игроком до конца игры.

Первый кто наберёт три жетона пирога - победил!

—user summary (Russian)

boardgame: Trekking the World: The Bucket List Expansion
description:
The Bucket List Expansion contains two modules that can be mixed into the base game of Trekking the World: Second Edition. You may play with both modules together or with just one or the other.

The Bucket List- There are so many amazing places in this world you’ve wanted to visit. With your bucket list written—now is the time to journey out and experience the world in one big life-fulfilling trip!

Each player begins the game with a Bucket List card, which has eight locations around the world they can visit. At the end of your turn, if you are on a location that matches any location on your Bucket List, “cross off” that location — then gain the Itinerary token depicted on there, either a Sightseeing token or a Worldly token.

Travel Personas- At setup, each player is given 2 Travel Persona cards to choose from. One is discarded, and the one that is kept becomes your Travel Persona for the game. The Travel Personas grant unique powers to use throughout the game and also offer a starting boost with new sets of starting tokens.

Note: The Bucket List expansion came packaged inside the base game's box for Kickstarter backers, but will be sold by itself.

—description from the publisher

In Tyr, you play as a legendary duelist battling in the arena. You can engage in quick 5-10 minute 2-player matches or participate in a Grand Melee where 3 or more players fight to be the last one standing. Each duelist is represented by an 18-card deck, with unique strengths and weaknesses.

Each deck contains 4-6 double-sided Hero Cards that form the Hero's body. The remaining cards are single-sided Duel Cards. Hero Cards represent the areas of the Hero’s body that can be targeted by the opponent, including the head, body, legs, weapon, and hand. Duel Cards, numbered 1-9, represent the Hero's basic attacks (white numbers) and more powerful attacks (black numbers).

Gameplay involves playing Duel Cards, resolving attacks, and using special abilities. A Hero wins by: (1) killing the opponent by wounding their body after it's already wounded, (2) killing the opponent with a 5+ card Power Attack, or (3) forcing the opponent to concede by leaving them unable to draw a card.

Demonuki is an exciting, modern twist on the classic game Babanuki (Old Maid). In this fast-paced card game, each card has powerful abilities that will allow you to strategically position yourself in a way to win the game!

On your turn, you will either discard pairs of cards, or play individual cards in order to reverse the order of play, slide extra cards into an opponent’s hand, make them reveal some of their cards, or various other effects. The loser of the game is the player holding the last card in the game (typically a Demon, but possibly a Werewolf), after all other cards have been discarded.

—description from designer

7 additional cards for 3 games:

     Crazy Sherlock for 3–5 players
     Yellow Brick Road for 2 players
     Migo for 2–4 players


+  1 double-sided rules card

Crazy Sherlock: 4 Clue cards from the “Location” category (2 Buildings and 2 Bridges cards).
Rules: When setting up the game, mix these 3 cards with the other “Clue” cards before setting aside one card from each category. When a player makes an "Announcement", he must specify the floor or arch that houses the silhouette.

Brick Road: 2 Bridge cards.
