Rules: Unlike other cards, the Bridge card has 2 sections. They are not related to each other. When setting up the game, each player takes a Bridge card and places it face up next to them. On his turn, he can play it instead of playing a card from his hand. He does not draw a card the next round because the hand is limited to 2 cards. A Bridge card is worth 2 points if it is not linked to any other card. If it is linked to other maps, each of its sections must be counted individually, whether they belong to the same road (loop) or to two different roads.

Migo : 1 double-sided Expedidion card (2 prayer wheels on the front and unsherpa on the back)
Rules: Add this card to the deck and discard one more card when setting up each round. Prayer wheels are “wild card” items. They can be compared to any other equipment but not Wool Hats. When you take this card, add it to your card collection and immediately decide what type of equipment they are assimilated to. This cannot be changed later. If you don't yet have a card in your collection when you take the Prayer Wheels card, they will be assimilated to the type of the next equipment you obtain.

Introduction:Edaraz (from Proto-Germanic: Edaraz, meaning "edge" or "enclosure") is a territorial game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of a hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 7 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 9 are also valid. There is also a place outside the board called prison. Each player has a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A lifeline is a path of empty points in a straight line from a stone to an empty cell on the edge of the board or to a friendly stone with lifelines. Stones can share their lifelines between several friendly stones without requiring any empty points between them.
     A territory is one or more empty points connected to each other. You own a territory if all the stones connected to it are of your color.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point where it has at least one lifeline, then move all dead enemy groups to prison. After this action, all friendly stones must have lifelines, and the board position must be different from the positions of the board at the end of all your previous turns.
     Remove an enemy stone from prison. If the prison contains stones of both colors, remove pairs of stones of opposite colors until only stones of one color or none remain.


				
				
					End of the game:The player who performed the last action wins the game.

To make the game fair, before starting, the first player places several black stones in prison, and then the second player chooses the sides. This balancing method is called komi pie. For handicap games, the weaker player takes black and opens by placing a number of black stones on the board proportional to the difference in skill between the players.

—description from the designer

Lingarda (from Old English: līn "line" and ġeard "enclosure") is a territorial game for two players: black and white. It is played on the intersections (points) of a hexagonal board with triangles, recommended to be 7 points per side, but boards of 5 or 9 are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of stones of their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A lifeline is a maximal path of unbroken empty points in a straight line from a stone to the edge of the board or to a friendly stone that has a lifeline. Stones can share their lifelines across multiple friendly stones with at least one empty point between them.
     An enclosure is a set of connected empty points that is completely surrounded by stones of any color and/or the edge of the board. You own an enclosure if the majority of the stones surrounding it are of your color.
     To flip is to replace enemy stones with friendly stones.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point where it has at least one lifeline or pass your turn if you cannot do it.
     Flip enemy stones that do not have lifelines.


If at the end of your turn any of your stones do not have lifelines, your move is invalid.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when no more valid moves can be made. At that point, scoring is conducted by adding the empty points within each enclosure controlled by a player, plus the stones of their color on the board. To avoid ties, the last player to make a placement receives half a point.

To ensure fairness, after placing the first black stone, the second player chooses a side.

—description from the designer

Just like in a real basketball game, the time limit is ever-present, whether it's in the quarters that govern the flow of the game or in the rules to follow, such as the one that requires the attackers to make a shot within 24 seconds. The theoretical concepts of basketball are introduced in a fun and educational way through the design of the game.
A board present the basketball field with its different zones and each player has 7 cards to play either in offense or defense. A pair of dice is used to determine certain actions success or failure. A clock is measuring each quarter 15 min time limit.

A solo dungeon crawler inspired by the poker game Scoundrel.
The game begins with player turning over dungeon cards and resolve them(monsters, items, weapons). Once the dungeon deck is empty and the room is cleared the game is won.

After 1000 AC the great king Uzal united all the lost kingdoms of the great continent and it is when mankind finally achieved world peace.
To celebrate this great act Azreal, the archangel of the high heaven built the Under Tower for mankind to explore. It is said that those who reached the final floor of the tower will be granted a wish. A wish that makes dreams come true.

—description from the designer

The game series Long Story Short tests your ability to make decisions. Each situation gives you two options to choose from. You must pick one of them.
Progress through the story one card at a time and try to reach the ending. If you fail, try again with a different approach.

Carmen & Carlos
Today marks the Día de Muertos! The day the dead visit the living. The perfect opportunity for Carmen, a local waitress, and her departed lover Carlos.
Guide them through this eventful day so they can finally meet again.

boardgame: Long Story Short: Le Trésor du Capitaine Boyle
description:
The game series Long Story Short tests your ability to make decisions. Each situation gives you two options to choose from. You must pick one of them.
Progress through the story one card at a time and try to reach the ending. If you fail, try again with a different approach.

Le Trésor du Capitaine Boyle
Kingston, 1815. Eager for some action, you join the crew of a merchant ship. After several boring days at sea, you overhear words of an undiscovered treasure belonging to a famous pirate Captain Boyle, hidden on an island not far from your position.
According to the rumors, three map pieces must be assembled to find the lost riches.

最忆是杭州 (Memories of Hangzhou) is a Monopoly-like board game that transports players to the ancient city of Hangzhou, renowned for its historical landmarks and cultural treasures. The game board is a beautifully illustrated 2.5D map featuring iconic locations such as the West Lake Ten Scenic Spots, including Leifeng Pagoda and Su Causeway.

Players navigate through the city as poets, painters, scholars, and artisans. Each player receives a profession-based task and corresponding item card, such as a brush for painters, which must be used to complete their objectives. Along the way, players interact with historical landmarks, answer trivia questions related to Hangzhou's history, and unlock unique abilities by collecting special cards.

Victory Conditions
The game ends when one of the two remaining players passes the Gongchen Bridge. The wealthiest player wins.

重庆迷宫 (Labyrinth of Chongqing) is a Monopoly-like game set in the magical 8D urban maze of Chongqing, China. Players move through a beautifully illustrated 2.5D hand-drawn map featuring iconic Chongqing landmarks, such as the Li Ziba Light Rail Station and the Yangtze River Cableway.

In Chongqing Labyrinth, players take turns moving along two routes: the Ground Route, which follows a clockwise direction through the streets, and the Light Rail Route, where movement is counterclockwise. Along the way, players collect Event Cards, Property Deeds, and Ingredient Cards. Special Mahjong Cards provide powerful one-time abilities, while gathering ingredients enables players to open a Hotpot Restaurant.

Victory Conditions
The game ends when one of the two remaining players passes the Hongya Cave. The player with the most wealth at the end wins.

上海风云 (Turbulence of Shanghai) is a Monopoly-style board game set in Shanghai during the Republic era. Players navigate a 2.5D map featuring iconic locations, competing as various characters through time and space to achieve their goals.

Players utilize unique item cards to trigger event tasks that recreate historical scenarios. With a blend of property acquisition and social deduction, the game maintains the classic Monopoly mechanics while adding layers interaction.

Victory Conditions
Players aim to control three target properties from their secret missions. The game ends when a player achieves this, leading to victory.

boardgame: 我在成都养熊猫 (Raising Pandas in Chengdu)
description:
我在成都养熊猫 ("Raising Pandas in Chengdu") is a Monopoly-like board game that transports players to Chengdu, renowned for its cultural heritage and adorable giant pandas. Players traverse a beautifully illustrated 2.5D game board, collecting bamboo and iconic landmarks while aiming to raise pandas to achieve victory.

Victory Conditions

     The game ends when one of the two remaining players passes the People's Park. The wealthiest player wins.
     Alternatively, a player wins instantly if he/she manages to raise three pandas.

boardgame: Pirate Days: Super Cannon Promo Card
description:
This is an extra card for the Pirate Days game, available with their Kickstarter campaign.

The "Super Cannon" is similar to the regular "Cannon" card which is included in the base set. Like the Cannon, the player rolls to determine the day blasted, but this also additionally blasts the adjacent days, potentially allowing three days to be blasted at once.

boardgame: Showdown: Championship Drag Racing – Top Fuel & Funny Car 2023 Season
description:
Welcome race fans and gaming enthusiasts to the world’s fastest and exciting sport in history-Top Fuel-Funny Car drag racing, 2023 season. The game covers all the events in a full season of racing action, and all you do is choose a racing class and enjoy a fun filled season in this simple simulation in some exciting fast action racing. There are three difficulty modes to choose from depending on the detail of results you wish to have in each race through the season…tokens only, tokens and elapsed times, or tokens, speed trap, and elapsed times.
The system uses dice on gear tables, each class has unique gear tables allowing for a different racing experience for the fastest auto racing in the world...nothing compares to this excitement.
          Get Ready for some Exciting Drag Racing!

—description from the designer

“Oh, no, it’s Ady or Petőfi again! Why can’t I just draw Kosztolányi?!” It sounds like the graduation exam, but it’s not. It’s a card game where famous Hungarian poets and writers look back at you from the cards, and if you are vigilant and lucky enough, you can build the most valuable tableau of them! You can play four different types of games including set collection, tableau building, memory and quote recognition. A card game, which brings literature closer!

Gameplays:

Collecting poets:
During the game, each player receives 6 or 7 poet cards (depending on the number of players) and a secret tableau card. The tableau card determines how the poets should be arranged on the table. In each turn, players select a poet card from their hand and place it on the table, then pass the remaining cards to their neighbor. From the received cards, the following actions can be performed: placing another card adjacent to a previously played card, placing a card on top of an identical card already in play, moving a previously placed card to a new place, or passing. The remaining cards are then passed again. The round ends when a player runs out of cards; the players then play one more round before scoring.

Tableau building:
Players draw 4 poet cards each and form a draw pile from the remaining cards, then secretly draw one tableau card each. The goal is for each player to shape the jointly built tableau according to their secret objective. The first player to collect 12 crystals wins.
The gameplay proceeds as follows: the starting player places a poet card and then draws a new card, followed by the other player. There are strict rules for placing the poet cards: the cards can only be placed adjacent to each other, and a maximum of two identical poets can be present in the shared tableau.
Scoring can happen at any time when a player exchanges the current tableau state for crystals based on their secret tableau card, showing the matches. Rows and columns earn 1 crystal, while diagonals earn 3 crystals. After this, the player draws a new tableau card. The game continues until one player collects the 12th crystal.

Memory:
Shuffle the poet cards and place them face down on the table in seven rows of nine. Unlike traditional memory games, here you need to turn over different cards and not identical ones. The goal of the game is to collect the most poet cards.
The active player's turn can end in two ways:
1.    They decide to stop and take all the cards they have turned over so far (up to a maximum of 9).
2.    They turn over a poet card they have already revealed during that turn. In this case, all cards must be turned back over, and the next player takes their turn.
The game ends when all 63 cards have been drawn. The player with the most cards collected wins the game.

Quote recognition:
Shuffle the poet cards and place them face down. Choose someone among the players to read aloud. The others should take paper and pen. The reader reveals 10 cards and places them in front of the others, who can look at them for a few minutes. After that, the reader takes the cards, shuffles them, and discards five. The remaining cards are held in such a way that the others cannot see who is on the card, and the reader reads the quotes aloud. The other players write down on their paper which poet they think the quote is from. Once everyone has made their guesses, reveal the cards in the original reading order! Players earn 1 point for each correct answer. The player with the most points wins.

No colors, no numbers - no problem! ALL CARDS ARE WILD! Swap cards with other players with the special "Grogu's Help" rule!

—description from the back of the box

Grogu's Help allows the player that has played it to look at another player's hand and swap any equal number of cards with theirs.

After the Romans' withdrawal in the early 5th century, Celtic rule over Britannia is seriously threatened. The fleets of Germanic peoples arriving on the eastern and southern coasts of the island are increasingly numerous. These are tribes of rugged men who worship warrior gods and choose their leaders in assemblies of equals.

The Saxons, led by Cerdic, have settled in the south.

The Angles, under the command of Eomer, are expanding their territories in the east.

The Jutes, led by the legendary Hengist, have moved north in the game setting, although historically they were found in the southeast.

The Britons, whose king is Vortigern, are the legitimate Celtic rulers of Britannia, although they are in decline and have retreated to the west.

Each of them commands armies to expand their domains and fight for the resources produced by the lands of Britannia. These resources can be invested in recruiting new troops and building villages, ships, and fortresses. But there can only be one victor, and it will be the one who demonstrates the greatest power.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: League of Six:  Master of Horse & Land Judge Promo
description:
Limited edition of two tax – available only at Essen 2024 with the purchase of the game at the booth.

These additional tokens are added to the pool at the start of the game and are placed in the cities to determine players' income (using the arrows).  Besides the tax they collect, these also provide players with a new companion whose special powers they can use.  The Master of Horse provides points at the end of the game, while the Land Judge has no on-going action but gives players access to all the available taxes.

Introduction:Enfargar (from Gothic: ains "one", farwa "color" and gards "enclosure" or "garden") is a two-player annihilation game: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of a hexagonal board with triangles. The recommended board size is 9 points per side, but boards of 7 or 11 are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

This game is based on Dale Walton's Stargazers, which is in turn based on my game Leblin.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A lifeline is a maximal path of unbroken empty points in a straight line from a stone to the edge of the board or to a friendly stone that has a lifeline. Stones can share their lifelines across multiple friendly stones without needing empty points between them.
     To flip is to replace enemy stones with friendly stones.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point where it has at least one lifeline or pass your turn if you cannot do it.
     Flip enemy stones that do not have lifelines.


If at the end of your turn any of your stones do not have lifelines, your move is invalid.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when only stones of one color remain on the board. The player who possesses the remaining stones wins the game.

To ensure fairness, after placing the first black stone, the second player chooses a side.

—description from the designer

A bluffing, card management, and negotiation game where everyone secretly plays a different role: must have the most coins, or protect someone, or make someone die. When it's your turn to choose someone to fight, you play 1 card. The player you fight with must play a card that matches the color below. Both of them continue playing until one of them can't play. That player will lose a heart. The game ends when one players die. 
Whoever completes the goal wins.(There can be more than 1 winner)

Tenorimadori is a game in which players build rooms in a rented house. Players place the floor plan cards in the palm of their hands and build the rooms like a puzzle. The score is tallied when the cards are no longer in the palm of the hand. There are also customer cards, and bonus points are awarded for installing the customer's desired amenities in the room.Muscle strength is important because your hands get tired as you play. This is a physical card game.

SPARK The Magic of Storytelling is a one-of-a-kind, music-inspired storytelling game. With story prompt cards and a music app, SPARK gives players everything they need to tell fantastical, hilarious, and riveting stories.

The object of the game is simple: with a hand of random prompt cards, tell an improv story to music within a given time limit.

With each turn, storytellers decide out how incorporate random objects, plots and story twists against a backdrop of imaginative soundtracks.  It’s unbelievably fun and easy to play.

SPARK The Magic of Storytelling can be played cooperatively, individually, or competitively.

No table required - play in any room, in the car on road trips, or around a campfire.

Eylau-1807 Solitaire is a 1-player wargame where you can take command of the French Grand Armée of Napoleon or the army of the 4th Coalition of Count Bennigsen on the famous battlefield of Eylau.

There are 9 games with 3 difficulty levels for each side resulting in a total of 18 games.

All you need is a pencil, an eraser and two 6-sided dice to play.

The game features are:
Players: One (solitaire)
Playing time: 45 minutes per game/battle
Replay value: high
Complexity: low
Language: English
No map or counters to mount. Just print and play.
The book contains rules with examples (18 pages) and a detailed playthrough (15 pages).
The battles are fought on 18 maps including 18 BOT sheets (9 French and 9 Coalition).
A battle results table where your victories or defeats can be recorded can be found at the end of the book.

—description from the publisher

description:
Zodiac Stack is a game of delicate balancing, stacking and interlocking, using the 12 animals of the Chinese Zodiac.

You'll need patience, wisdom, and steady hands to be a Zodiac Master - do you have what it takes?

Three game rules are included: a puzzle mode (to construct a combination of animals in a given way); a competitive mode (construct the most puzzles in 6 minutes); and the flow mode where you start with a given animal and then build a stable structure on top if it any way you like.

An ancient garden attracts a crowd of enthusiasts. As these colorful ferns slowly populate every corner of the garden, dedicate visitors seek the best vantage points. Strike the perfect balance between placing ferns and visitors, to secure your victory!

You start the game with five different visitors and an hand of 4 random Fern tiles.
On your turn you make 2 actions, each of which can involve planting a Fern into the Garden, or placing a Visitor on a Bench; if the bench belongs to the other player, that visitor is called a Disturber. Then you draw Ferns to come back to a 4 tiles hand.

The winner is determined by calculating the Visitors’ happiness. Each Visitor seated on a Bench of their own color earns points equal to the number of their favorite ferns visible in their Side. If the Bench has a Disturber, subtract the number of ferns of the Disturber's type visible in that Side (the score for that Bench can drop to zero but never below).

—description from the designer

boardgame: Q-Ra Code: A QR Code Manipulation Pen & Paper Game
description:
Find out which cells need to be filled in to make the QR Code (Q-Ra code) scannable. You win the game, if you successfully scan the code.

When downloading the game sheet, you receive a unique random puzzle.
To play the game, you need a QR Code Scanner, e.g. your mobile phone, but there is no internet connection and no app installation required. All QR Codes contain plain text, no links.

There are sixteen hints, hidden in canopic jars (small QR Codes).
So it is a logic puzzle?
- Yes, it is, though you don't get clues for free! It's your choice which canopic jar you want to open each turn, but to do so, you must fill in one cell of the Q-Ra Code. This can potentially destroy the code a bit further!
On top of that, four of the canopic jars contain an event instead of a hint. Whenever you encounter an event, you must fill a number of cells based on the chosen difficulty level.
Fortunately, all events share a feature (e.g. same canopic jar head or same row), which helps you to avoid them in the future.

Can you decipher the hieroglyphics and recover the QR Code before too many cells are filled and the hidden information is irretrievably lost?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Heroes of Might and Magic III: The Board Game – Big Box
description:
Heroes of Might & Magic III: The Board Game: Big Box includes base game and first five expansions : Battlefield, Inferno, Rampart, Fortress and Tower.

Do you stand beside Queen Catherine Ironfist and vanquish the lurking chaos? Do you help the power-hungry Mutare fulfill her plans and turn her into a dragon? Or, maybe you despise all of this and want, together with Sandro, to cover the whole world with the shadow of death? Move through the beautiful land of Antagarich, plunge into adventures and strategic battles with endless possibilities.

Heroes of Might & Magic III: The Board Game is an adventure-driven strategy game for 1-3 players set in the cult fantasy universe. The game includes competitive, cooperative, and solo scenarios to battle and explore your way through. The adventure maps will be represented by tiles, with each tile divided into seven hexagonal fields.

Play different scenarios with different victory conditions, explore the adventure map to discover various locations, and play out epic battles using the unique miniature models that represent the iconic units from the original game. All battles will be performed with models on separate boards.

—description from the publisher

The box dimensions: 510x310x220 mm

boardgame: Heroes of Might and Magic III: The Board Game – Big Box Expansion
description:
Contains Stronghold, Conflux and Cove expansions.

Stronghold: The harsh lands of Krewlod are filled with barbarians craving for victorious tales to echo through time. Two of them, Crag Hack and Yog, join forces to vanquish necromancers from troubled Erathia.

Conflux: In the Conflux, Elementalists and Planeswalkers rule their realm amid oppressed spirits, elusive Phoenixes, and other elemental creatures. Join Tarnum as he battles Elemental Lords and confronts his magical biases to become a Wizard.

Cove: The waters and islands of Antagarich teem with pirates' clashing desires. Jeremy and his crew must navigate these treacherous waters, with only luck on their side and the promise of a mysterious artifact at the end of the journey.

The box dimensions: 310x510x180 mm

Video Game Theme: Heroes of Might & Magic

description:
Ouch! Frog's throat is hurting! Squirrel caught a cold, Mouse fell and hurt its leg. Hurry, help all the animals to get healed! All you need to do is roll the dice, and with a little luck and skill, everyone will soon be healthy again. In cases of emergency Doctor Brummbrumm will come and help.
An exciting game with simple rules based on Bartos Erika’s popular series. Contains two game versions: for younger and older children.

During the game, players must acquire disease cards, with a maximum of 2 per turn. A card can be obtained by placing one die of the corresponding color on each of its fields. The active player rolls all three colored dice and checks which card or cards they can acquire based on the rolled colors. They then place the dice on the chosen card(s). A card that requires 1 die is worth 1 point, 2 dice are worth 2 points, and 3 dice are worth 4 points.
If the player cannot acquire a card or is not satisfied with the available cards, they can roll any number of dice ones more and place the new colors on the cards. The player takes the card(s) and marks the points received on their thermometer. (The first player to reach the blue space on their thermometer wins the game.) If no card is acquired, the player receives a Brummbrumm doctor tile as a consolation price, which can be used as a joker starting from the next turn.
If a Brummbrumm doctor card is revealed among the disease cards, a role-playing part of the game takes place: each player "cures" their favorite stuffed animal following the card's instructions (e.g., pretending to measure its temperature or blowing its nose).

boardgame: Maladum: Dungeons of Enveron – The Forbidden Creed Expansion
description:
The Forbidden Creed expansion is a standalone storyline campaign that can be played at any point in your party’s career from Maladum: Dungeons of Enveron. It consists of six new quests detailing the rise of the cabal and their endeavours to access even greater power and command the spirit of the Ascendant. Like the Dungeons of Enveron campaign, the quests are linked and your actions will determine the outcome of the story.

The influence of forbidden magic is spreading throughout the region and it is your task to put a stop to it. You must fight the corrupted maladaar, overcoming their supreme magical abilities, while wrestling them for control of the Ascendant. It will be your death or your salvation – it’s down to you which one!

As with everything else we've done in this campaign, almost all components are cross compatible, so the Forbidden Creed Campaign can also feature Hidden Locations, Side Quests, crafted equipment, and all manner of NPCs and Events.
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

boardgame: Maladum: Dungeons of Enveron – Oblivion's Maw
description:
The Oblivion's Maw expansion campaign consists of six new quests, detailing the Malagaunt's search for ancient relics to tether the Corrupter to this plane. It's structured in a similar way to the core Dungeons of Enveron campaign, with your actions throughout the story determining what the Malagaunt will do next.

If you can wrest control of these powerful relics the Malagaunt is seeking, you can benefit from their abilities in future quests. All components are cross compatible, so the Oblivion's Maw Campaign can also feature Hidden Locations, Side Quests and all manner of NPCs and Events.

description:
This add-on includes 1 Depth Dragon meeple and 1 Depth Dragon eDNA card for DinoGenics.
It also includes DinoGenics themed events and tiles for use with the game Deep Shelf.

AIM of The Game:

The aim of the game is to start 
3 escape pods. 
Only when the 2 exopets escape pods are fully loaded and launched can the 
Crew escape pod be launched with the active CREWMATES!

You Lose:

If a player does not have a crew ID card
he must draw a new crew ID card in the next player phase. If he cannot do this because the HR stack is empty, everyone loses the game at the same time. 
Or if the VDL round counter reaches the end of the VDL Rounds on the VDL BOARD.

How it is played:


     Every Player unfreezes one of 20 crewmates in the HSC (HYPER SLEEP CHAMBER) 
     To keep your crewmates safe you must find the crew escape pod
     Before your crew gets permission to start, you must evacuate two fully loaded Exopets Escape Pods. Total 26 Exopets!
     How to get Exopets? With every new Room discoverd a dice decides how many Exopets (token) you have to draw. If some one in your friends (other player)  has a similar Exopet, one attacks the owner. A diceroll decides who will be attacked by the camoflaged Mimicry. Depending of the Ship-Level you are Standing you lose ALSs.
     To get permission to enter a Escape-Pod you have to Check your Exopets, if they are clean or dirty. Test them in the Laboratory!
     Untested and DIRTY (infected) Exopets can be flushed away in the LOO. Importent to get an ELMA with only clean Exopets inside.
     There is constant threat of Mimicry attacks and sabotage. Some can be prevented if the respective fuse Control is blown off. 
     Reach all goals before the shipAI Ursula blasts all hexagons into space after 16 or 19


VDL rounds.

Rounds: 
The game is played in rounds. There is a maximum number of rounds, depending on the number of players and indicated on the respective VDL  board side. The game ends before the final round if you reach your goals or you run out of crewmates.

Phases:
Each round consists of phases. It always begins with the player phase and ends with the VDL phase, which closes the round. In the Complex GAME, the MIMICRY phase is added as a secound phase if there is a Mini Mimicry or Boss Mimicry on the playing field.

Player phase:
Theoretically, anyone can start in the player phase, as there is no turn order. Players can exhaust ALC for actions in parallel whenever they want. Not all ALCs have to be exhausted at once or one after the other. This phase ends when no more players want to take an action, even if there are still ALCs left!
 The only exception is drawing and collecting EXOPETS from the Exopet draw pool. Never do this at the same time, otherwise triggered effects cannot be dealt with properly. To avoid this, the player should take the ACTIVE EXOPET COLLECTOR and signal to the other players that he is now 
drawing exopets and that everyone 
else must pay attention.

Mimicry Phase: 
This phase is only available in the COMPLEX GAME. It comes into play if there is a Mini-Mimicry or BOSS-Mimicry on the playing field. The nest only counts as part of the playing field after it is connected as last 
hexagon to the playing field on which the ELMA figures are 
moving. If there are Mimicrys on the playing field, they move 
and can attack when confronted on the same Hexagon field.

VDL Phase: 
The round counter is moved forward to the next round slot. If the round counter comes to a slot that is connected to the image of a FUSE-CONTROL hexagon, it must be determined whether this optically identical field has already been detected and removed. 
If both conditions are not met, a mimicry sabotage is rolled. 
The effect removes only hexagons named by the effect but not neighbouring ones, even if they are not connected annymore.
After this phase, the next round begins again with the player     
  phase.

ELMA (Exopet Loot Managing Automat): 
Each player controls an ELMA. Each player uses it to manage their ALC, exopets, loot and the crewmate escorting it. In addition, ELMA automatically awakens CREWMATES in the next round if a player has lost his escorting crewmate. 
Attention: Elma can only enter an escape pod if it has nothing but clean Exopets stored. ELMA does not fly with an escape pod!

ALC (Action Life Coin): 
Each player exhausts his ALC to be able to perform actions
in the player phase. In addition, ALC are also life points which, when lost, reduce the action possibilities more and more until the death of the escorting crewmate.

Next round: 
All players refresh exhausted ALC (turn over to green side). Lost ALC remain next to the ELMA-BOARD. Players whose crewmate was frozen, flew away or died in the previous round may draw a new crewmate from the HR-STACK and place 3 fresh ALC on their ELMA-BOARD.  If the HR-STACK is empty so that a player can no longer draw a crewmate , the game ends and everyone loses!!!

Contains 4 games:

     뉴클리어 카운트다운 (Nuclear Countdown)
     우주 정거장 (Space Station)
     치과 의사 (Dentist)
     비밀 요원 (Secret Agent)

description:
You’re getting ready for a game night with friends and you have to choose the right games from your shelf given that you will need several copies of the same game to satisfy your group’s needs. Will you be the first one to find the best games for your group?
On your turn you can choose a card to add it to your hand, play an action card or score a set. 
The first player who scores 3 sets of identical board games wins the game.

boardgame: Murder Mystery: Murder on the Express
description:
It's the 1920's and Vienna and Venice express has ground to a halt after setting off in Austria Lady Looselips has been found murdered before hosting the 5 million dollar raffle on board the trains VIP carriage!
You must play your roles in brining the murderer to justice!
set in the 1920's this hilarious murder mystery game will provide you with a night you will not forget.
Invite your friends, dress up, throw a party, set the scene and get into character

Get ready to work together to win together. Grab a partner and team up to play a series of mini-games that test how in sync you are. These fun mini-games come in four categories: Challenge, Fill in the Blanks, Word Match, and Number. Each mini-game victory moves you closer to the winning side of the board, while each loss sends you back towards the losing side. Your goal is to make it all the way to the winning end of the board and successfully complete the final challenge by being optimally in tune with each other.

Dice Dreams is a 2-4 player competitive game. Roll dice and push your luck to claim or steal dream cards in hopes of earning the highest score by the end of the game.

Cards are based around various dream types and are worth points depending on how difficult they are to acquire. Most cards have one-time or on-going abilities that give the player a buff or sometimes a debuff. One card, Night Terror, is worth negative points if you are unlucky enough to accidently acquire it.

The game ends when two of the three care piles are depleted, so be strategic when choosing what cards to try to acquire.

Players are allowed two re-roll maximum and can push towards trying to acquire higher value cards through re-rolling.

Dice Dreams is a small and lightweight game with minimal components, alluring imagery, re-play appeal, exciting moments and packaged in a convenient metal tin.

—description from the designer

Don’t miss your chance to own a piece of gaming history with Insecta: The Complete Facsimile Edition. This exclusive, limited-edition reprint of the original 1995 Insecta board game is a one-time offer—only 1,500 numbered copies will ever be produced. Expertly restored by the original developer Neal Sofge, this edition faithfully replicates the authentic Insecta experience, complete with the same paper quality, texture, and vibrant printing colors of the original. Each copy includes a certificate of authenticity, signed by the game’s designer, Phil Eklund.

Insecta is a unique strategy game of bug-to-bug conflict, where players design mutant insect teams and navigate "The Hive," a dangerous environment filled with real-world arthropods controlled by an adversarial Hivemaster. This complete edition also includes Insecta: Rainforest and Insecta: Trilobite, expanding the game's depth and replayability.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Stack and balance to win with T-Rex Topple! Balancing Game from Mudpuppy! A classic game that benefits development of motor skills, dexterity, and hand-eye coordination. Children ages 4 and up stack and balance wooden sticks on the back of the wooden dinosaur to win the game.

Instructions:


     Players take turns stacking sticks. Who can balance the most?
     If a player knocks down any sticks, they are out of the game.
     In a 3+ player game, the other players can continue stacking on top of any sticks that remain balanced.
     The last player to remain in the game wins. If more than one player remains when all the sticks are balanced, victory is shared!

Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 12 Kickstarter campaign. Card is printed on foil cardstock.

Frenzy

Play: Set aside all other creatures.
Defeated: Return them to play without activating their Play effects.

boardgame: Varázsösvény: Építs utat! Hátráltass másokat! Juss ki a mocsárból!
description:
Are you ready for a magical adventure? In the Magic Path board game, you have to find a way out of the dangerous swamp as a lost sorcerer's apprentice! Intrigue, cunning and magical spells await you. Build your way with ancient stones and use the power of spells to surprise your opponents! Who will be the first to escape and who will remain a prisoner of the swamp?

You use road tiles on the board to build your way to the goal, and in the process you also hinder your opponents. And with refined spells, you can surprise each other even at the last moment!

Where you need to go depends on the chosen game variation, because you can choose between 3 versions that are slightly different in terms of rules, but completely different in terms of gaming experience.

—description from the publisher

Készen állsz egy varázslatos kalandra? A Varázsösvény társasjátékban elveszett varázslótanoncokként kell megtalálnod a kiutat a veszélyes mocsárból! Cselszövés, ravaszság és mágikus varázslatok várnak rád. Építsd meg az utat az ősi kövek segítségével és használd ki a varázslatok erejét, hogy meglepd ellenfeleidet! Ki lesz, aki elsőként megmenekül és ki marad a mocsár rabja?

A táblára útlapkákkal építitek az utatokat a célig, és közben szó szerint keresztbe is tehettek egymásnak. Rafinált varázslatokkal pedig még az utolsó pillanatban is okozhattok meglepetéseket egymásnak!

Az, hogy hová kell eljutnotok, a választott játékvariációtól függ, ugyanis 3 szabalyait tekintve kissé eltérő, de játékélményben teljesen más verzió közül választhattok.

—description from the publisher (Hungarian)

boardgame: Wild Tiled West: The Matchmaker Promo Card
description:
Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 12 Kickstarter campaign.

This is an additional Partner objective card to add to your games of Wild Tiled West.

It provides the following scoring: Pairs of building tiles adjacent to each other but not to any other buildings.

1-2 Pairs: 6
3 Pairs: 14
4+ Pairs: 22

The theme of Книга чаклуна ("Wizard's Book") centers around a mystical battle between two wizards vying for control over the elemental forces of the universe: Fire, Water, Earth, Wind, and Lightning. Players discover a magical book while exploring an attic, and are immediately drawn into a duel where they must wield the powers granted by the elements. The book, the Book of the Wizard, contains powerful spells that allow players to control the elements and engage in magical combat. Throughout the game, players use these spells to both attack and defend, with the ultimate goal of mastering the elements and defeating their opponent.

Gameplay involves using a deck of elemental cards and mana tokens. Each card contains both attack and defense capabilities, and players take turns playing their cards to either strike their opponent with an elemental attack or defend themselves from one. Players must match the attacking element with the corresponding defensive element. Mana tokens are used to restore health and enable actions during the game, and the battle continues until one player's deck is depleted. The winner is determined by the player who has used the least amount of mana tokens while successfully managing their attacks and defenses.

boardgame: How Dare You?: Game Boy Geek Trivia Card
description:
Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 12 Kickstarter campaign.

description:
Categories / scattergories board game with 72 different and innovative categories for great fun, played according to the classical scattergories rules. The aim is to collect as many points per round as possible in order to win the game after 12 rounds.

Das Stadt, Land, Fluss Spiel mit 72 abwechslungsreichen, lustigen und innovativen Kategorien für großen Spielspaß. Verpackt in einem praktischen DIN-A4 Block mit 50 Spielbögen. Gespielt wird nach den klassischen Stadt, Land, Fluss Regeln. Ziel ist es, möglichst viele Punkte je Runde zu sammeln, um das Spiel nach 12 Runden zu gewinnen.

     프리즌 브레이크 (Prison Break)
     바이러스 (Virus)
     공포의놀이공원 (Welcome to Funland)

description:
"Adopt and love forever!" – this is the slogan of Vau, which highlights the importance of dog adoption and responsible pet ownership.

Shuffle the shelter cards and place them face down in a stack. Action cards are also shuffled, and each player is dealt 5 cards, with the remaining cards forming a draw pile. Each turn consists of two phases: first, the player performs an action, then draws a card from the action card pile.

Players can choose one of the following actions during their turn: play a treat or a dog trap action card, adopt a dog for 3 bone coins or 4 love cards (by drawing the top card from the shelter pile), place a love card under an adopted dog, or discard a card and draw a new one (without activating the discarded card's action). At the end of the turn, played cards are placed in the discard pile unless specified otherwise, and players always draw one card from the action card pile.

The shelter pile contains cards representing dogs available for adoption, and players need to adopt 5 dog cards to win. Simply adopting a dog isn't enough; players must also show love to their pets. Some dog cards have special (one time or permanent) abilities that activate upon adoption. Bone coins are used to adopt dogs from the shelter, while love cards can be used to adopt dogs (4 cards required) or to show affection to existing pets.
Treat cards provide one-time benefits to the player, while dog trap cards can hinder other players. The "Not Allowed!" card can be played in response to another player’s action, negating that action.

The game ends when a player has successfully cared for 5 adopted dogs in their yard, making them the winner.

Brace the elements in this sky-high adventure game where each player is fighting nature - and each other - in a race to climb the mountain first! Featuring a real 3D mountain with climbers that really climb, there's no end to the fun as your battle avalanches and unexpected encounters to climb even higher until someone finally plants their flag at the peak!
Will you summit the mountain with ease, or be desperately searching for supplies before you plummet?

—description from the designer

Flush is a shedding card game between 2-6 players where the aim of the game is to release all of your cards, resulting in the rest of the table accruing points. Once a player reaches a set limit of points (30 is recommended), they are removed from the game. The last remaining player wins.

Alternatively, the Arena format of Flush plays the game in the same way with no eliminations. Instead, 5 rounds are played with players accruing points as normal. The 5th and final round is double points. The player with the least amount of points at the end of the 5th round is the winner.

The game has a good blend of skill with elements of luck. One of the main decisions that players are required to make, is deciding on whether to release Flush cards before another player wins the round. This element of risk is one of the reasons that Flush is very fun to play. Risk it and go for the big win or suffer the consequences of making the wrong decision.

Another interesting element of Flush is that larger tournaments can be played similarly to Poker (where players are eliminated until a final table is reached and the last remaining player is declared the winner).

—description from the publisher

A group of explorers during an excursion in the jungle suddenly find themselves with their feet in quicksand! One by one the explorers begin to sink. Who will survive? 
This board game is very simple. It starts with the players taking turns rolling the colored die and extracting a stick from the jungle of the color corresponding to the color rolled on the die. Then all you have to do is avoid falling into quicksand. The last player to remain standing on the quicksand wins the game!

Un gruppo di esploratori durante un'escrusione nella giungla si ritrova improvvisamente con i piedi nelle sabbie mobili! Uno alla volta gli esploratori iniziano a sprofondare. Chi riuscirà a sopravvivere?
Questo gioco in scatola è molto semplice. Si inizia con I giocatori che a turno tirano il dado colorato ed estraggono dalla giungla un bastoncido del colore corrispondente al colore uscito sul dado. Allora tutto quello che dovete fare è evitare di cadere nelle sabbie mobili. L’ultimo giocatore a rimanere in piedi sulle sabbie mobili vince la partita!

Nurmijärvi is in turmoil! A storm of time and space has demolished the local history, shuffling dates, significant people and locations all over the place. Help the people of Nurmijärvi to find their lost history in a time-space continuum gone wrong!

Lost in Nurmijärvi is a co-operative board game for 1-6 players. The goal of the players is to build a map of Nurmijärvi, consisting of notable events, locations and people, and afterwards finish one of four challenges that will appear, depending on player choices during the map-building phase. All players either win or lose together.

This game was commissioned by the Library of Nurmijärvi as part of a project intended to improve the reading abilities and activity of young people. The art for the game was made by students of the local Finnish vocational school Keuda, and the game designed by two members of the Finnish Board Game Society.

—description from the designer

A new addition to the "La Bataille" series of Napoleonic battles, this game covers the dramatic 1813 Battle of Kulm. Following Napoleon's victory at Dresden, he ordered several columns to close in and try to capture the fleeing Allied armies. French General Vandamme commanded a corp full of conscripts and tried to "bag" the Allies around a series of villages, the largest being Kulm. But Vandamme ended up being swallowed by the retreating Prussian and Austrian troops and lost most of his corps, including being captured himself.

The defeat flipped the entire campaign and set up Napoleon's big loss later at Leipzig.

This intriguing battle is presented in the full "La Bataille" style, following the same production techniques as La Bataille de Vauchamps.

Time, map and unit scale, from the rules:

1.1.1 Each complete turn represents approximately 20 minutes of real time.
1.1.2 Each hex is approximately 100 meters across.
1.1.3 Each Increment point is equal to approximately 100 infantry men, 50 cavalry men (with mounts), or 4 artillery cannons.

IMPORTANT ERRATA (dated October 16, 2025):
13.7.2 (correction) When units advance into a vacated hex, they must maintain the same facing and formation (if multiple units in different facings and/or formations, only one must advance).
family: Series: La Bataille (Marshal Enterprises / Clash of Arms Games)

boardgame: Murder Mystery Cases: Death of an Influencer
description:
A pall of grief hangs over the paradise island of Bali today as news of the shocking death of renowned health and style influencer Josh Mendoza reverberates across the globe.

The 27-year-old sensation, celebrated for his contributions to the world of health and style, was pronounced dead on the morning of Monday May 27, 2024. The famous influencer was seen staggering alone from a privately rented villa, where a party is believed to have been held. He reportedly collapsed in the street. Medical assistance was called to the scene but paramedics were ultimately
unable to revive him.

Famous influencer, Josh Mendoza, was found dead a few blocks away from the exclusive Island Breeze resort on the island of Bali, where he had been staying with friends, family, and foes.

Use your detective skills to uncover who is responsible for his death ... and why

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 如鲠在喉：1940~1942年地中海战役 (A Stick in the Throat: Battle of the Mediterranean 1940-1942)
description:
如鲠在喉 (A Stick in the Throat) is a historical wargame that simulates naval campaigns in the Mediterranean theater of World War II. Players are to step into the position of commanding officers, such as Italian Navy commanders (e.g., Riccardi) or British Royal Navy front-line commanders (Cunningham and Somerville), leading their respective forces in a struggle for the control of the Mediterranean during the 28 months period of intense naval operations, starting from July 1940 (Operation Catapult) and ends in October 1942 (just before Operation Torch).

While the standard ruleset provides a streamlined experience of the operational level naval actions. There are also a range of optional rules that brings additional what-if units extra historical details and fog-of-war rules which can stir up the play and brings additional flavors.

The game includes a 2-turn long learning scenario (covering the 3 major resupply operation of Malta: Harpoon, Vigorous, and Pedestal),3 standard scenario (covering the naval operations during 1940, 41 and 42 respectively), 1 campaign scenario covering the entire 28 month period, and 1 alt-history scenario where Italy has finished its war preparations and enters the war in 1942 into a grand showdown with the reinforced and modernized British Mediterranean Fleet.

Components:
1 Rulebook
5 Player Aids
1 594x420mm Map
280 Counters
44 Cards
1 10-sided die

Get ready for the ultimate hip-thrusting showdown! Compete against your family and friends as you go head-to-head in 12 sausage-swinging minigames!

—description from the publisher

Get ready for the wildest game night with 21 Inches! Its the ultimate would you rather party game with infinite ridiculous possibilities. Would you like to eat 99 slugs? Or would you prefer to have hiccups for 99 hours a week? Flip 3 cards, roll the dice and The Judge secretly assigns each number to a card, creating hilarious combos. Everyone else is trying to guess how The Judge will vote. It's the game that takes ridiculous X-rated fun to a whole new level.

—description from the publisher

William Hanson and Jordan North present the sophisticated game for unsophisticated people.

Help I Sexted My Boss The Official Party Game is a must have for any G&Diva. Players take on hilarious minigames from six categories inspired by the antics of the podcast, and race to concoct the perfect gin and Dubonnet cocktail!

Expose your guests’ guilty pleasures, test out your acting skills, get creative and complete agony aunt dilemmas and put your etiquette knowledge to the test.

Compete in 5 different categories of etiquette challenges: Jordan’s Impressions, Listeners Dilemmas, Clinking is Common, Mother’s Shoebox, & Etiquettemology. When you win a challenge, you draw a Gin & Dubonnet Cocktail ingredient tile from the bag! You will need 1 lemon slice, 2 shots of Dubonnet, 1 shot of gin, and 3 ice cubes to make the perfect Gin & Dubonnet Cocktail. The first player to make a perfect Gin & Dubonnet Cocktail is the winner!

—description from the publisher

In this quick game, your guild of adventurers is ready for anything: battles, rescues, and the "liberation" of whatever pays the most. Choose a quest and roll the dice! You may win fame, and even recruit a new comrade for your party. Or you may fail, and slink back to the Tavern.

This Deluxe edition includes all the cards from the Hack & Slash 2 – Whack & Stash expansion, which first introduced Treasure cards!

Adventuring on your own? Try out the solitaire rules for the game!

Rapid Rhyming Party Game - Shout a word from one of the cards and go around the group taking turns to respond with a rhyme

No Hesitating - You only have 4 seconds to respond with a rhyme so make sure your vocabulary is on point!

250 Word Cards - This game includes 235 of the best rhyming words you can find... Just be careful with words like 'hunt'!

—description from the publisher

Engaging Group Game: Dive into the ultimate party game of deduction with Lucky Egg's Anti Draft. This fun and challenging game is perfect for adults and teens, making it a hit at any family game night or social gathering. Get ready for rounds of laughter and excitement as you try to outsmart your opponents!

Stimulating Brain Teaser: Anti Draft isn't just a game, it's a mental workout! This guessing game will have you thinking on your feet as you try to deduce the answers from the clues given by other players. It's a fantastic way to keep your mind sharp while having a blast with friends and family.

—description from the publisher

Kippkopp and Tipptopp build a snowman in January, pick strawberries in June, harvest in October, and decorate the Christmas tree in December. Each of the 12 months consists of four cards. Collect the quartets – the one who gathers the most wins! After the game, you can read a short tale about each month in the story booklet.

The box contains 3 versions of the game:

Quartet for older kids: A quartet consists of 4 cards from one month. These must be collected through questioning. At the beginning of the game, the cards are dealt among the players. Everyone checks if they have a quartet. If they do, they place it in front of them. Then the game can begin. The dealer starts by randomly asking someone for a card, specifying which month's card they would like. If they are lucky, they receive it and can ask again. If not, the turn goes to the player they asked. If someone completes a new quartet, they place it on the table and can continue asking until they get stuck. They can only ask for a month from which they already have at least one card! The game ends when the cards run out. The player who has collected the most quartets wins.

Quartet for younger kids: At the beginning of the game, everyone is dealt 5 cards, and the rest are placed in the center. If someone already has a quartet in hand, they can lay it down! The starting player asks someone for a card, specifying the month but not the number. They can only ask for a month they already have a card from! If they are lucky, they receive it and can keep asking until they are given a card. If the requested card is not available, they draw a card from the pile, and the next player takes their turn. If they gather a quartet during their questioning, they lay it down and cannot ask anymore. They draw a card, and the next player proceeds. (At the end of our turn, we always draw.) We keep playing until the pile is exhausted. We then finish the round. The player with the most quartets wins!

Slipper: We form a large circle with the cards facing down, spreading the deck loosely. This is called the slipper. A face-up card is placed in the center. Players must place cards on it that match either the month or the number. The starting player draws a card from the slipper. If they can play it, they do; if not, it stays in their hand. They keep drawing until they can play a card. Then, the next player takes their turn. If a player can play cards from their hand when it’s their turn, they can do so without having to draw from the slipper! The game ends when the cards in the slipper are all used up. The player with the fewest cards left in hand wins.

Duck Duck What is the perfect family-friendly game for every game night! Dive into a world of water-loving animals, from fully aquatic creatures to those who love to swim and soak. These playful animal friends are at the heart of the fun.

Gameplay revolves around players taking turns collecting cards from the "mushy pile" to make pairs, but watch out for the Goose cards! These sneaky cards can disrupt the flow and shake up the order of play. The game ends when a player breaks the ring around the mushy pile or plays their entire hand. The player with the most points at the end is crowned the winner!

For even more excitement, extended gameplay adds new layers of strategy and fun—keeping everyone engaged and eager for more. No one will want to stop playing Duck Duck What!

In Uncle Silence’s secret garden, the colorful and magical fruits are ripe for picking! The clever owner of the garden announces a competition: whoever collects the most fruit by dusk gets to ride the griffin! The value of the fruits can be multiplied by hardworking hedgehogs and flying pigs, and if you manage to attract the garden's guardians, Uncle Silence has additional rewards for you.

In this charming tactical collecting game, you’ll embark on adventures in Uncle Silence's magical garden. As you carefully plan your path, you must keep an eye on other adventurers to prevent them from snatching up the goodies!

Throughout the game, you will move your pieces in the rows and columns formed by stacked tiles. Each turn, you'll collect the tiles you've moved from, discovering hidden fruits, special creatures, and executing certain tile actions. Gather valuable fruits, acquire hedgehogs and flying pigs that increase their value, and lure the hungry guardians of the garden. Uncle Silence rewards you with gold coins, and the player who collects the most coins wins the game.

Once you're familiar with the game, you can also collect guinea pig cards. If you play skillfully and manage to complete the challenges on the cards by the end of the game, you can earn additional gold coins.

Use the scorebook to sum up the victory points!
A version of Monopoly themed around Gastonia, North Carolina.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
In Knitten we take on the roles of Grandma Hilda’s beloved cats. Grandma Hilda adores both her cats and knitting. Her house is filled with balls of yarn, and the cats love to play with them. As the cats play with the yarn, the shapes of the yarn balls catch Grandma Hilda’s eye and inspire her. She immediately starts knitting clothing or toys for her cats based on the yarn’s patterns. Since we want to be Grandma’s favorite cat, we strive to get her to knit for us the most. We collect Grandma’s knitted items in our basket. The yarn balls and clothing items in our basket earn us points. At the end of the game, the cat with the most points in their basket wins.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Cluckaneers, a strategy game where you and your pirate chicken crew embark on a daring adventure to collect treasure and outwit your opponents.

In Cluckaneers, you and your friends become captains of pirate chicken crews, each vying for the most loot. The game combines strategic betting with push-your-luck and set collection mechanics. You'll need to manage your crew wisely and outsmart your opponents to become the wealthiest Cluckaneer Captain.

Use your loyal crew members in open auction style fights for loot. Collect loot all while drafting your new crew for the next round! Be careful when drafting your new crew, as some crew members will cost you much more than others. Don’t stay on the island for too long or you might dig up a cursed relic and end up losing that loot you worked so hard to get.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Raccoon's Game: Seven Saves the Notch
description:
According to animal legend, a clever raccoon is born with seven rings on her tail on a night with a bright copper moon. This raccoon is destined to save a mythical forest called the Notch. Use Speed, Strength, Scouting, and Wisdom cards to move ahead quickly while navigating strategic decisions about die rolls and card draws to earn your tail rings.

To win a player must earn seven tail rings and be the first raccoon to reach the Notch. Players must decide when to race ahead and where to pause and earn tail rings.

Players take turns rolling the die or using Friend Cards (Speed, Wisdom, Scouting, Strength) to navigate the path to the Notch. Players must stop at locations like the Old Raccoon's House and Big Water to draw and resolve action cards. They can also choose to stop at other sites to battle wild animals or save forest animals.

Tail rings are earned by collecting Friend Cards, winning dice battles, and resolving cards drawn at key locations on the path.

—description from the designer

Termik is a paragliding race in the air.
It's a race that consists of connecting marked points (flying over them), in any order you like... and that's the important thing: everyone will have the same possibilities, represented by cards. The choice of course and the order of play will make the difference. It's up to you to choose the best route, watch your altitude so you don't fall, and be the first to reach the landing point.

Termik est un jeu de course dans les airs, en parapente
Il s’agit d’une course qui consiste à relier des points balisé (passer au-dessus), dans l’ordre que vous souhaitez… et c’est là le point important : tout le monde aura les mêmes possibilités représentées par des cartes. Le choix du parcours et l’ordre du jeu feront la différence.A vous de choisir le meilleur itineraire, bien surveiller votre altitude pour ne pas tomber, et être le premier a râler le point d'atterrissage.

Over Crest Rally is an 18-card rally game played entirely in hand. You can play alone or with an infinite number of players (each player will need a copy of the game). The objective is to complete a timed stage in the shortest time possible. The art features a camera on board a rally car to increase immersion and driving sensation. The game has 6 stages, but you can create as many as you want. Within each stage you will face different curves and control the car with the steering wheel, throttle and brake. With good driving you will achieve a good time, but if you push too hard your car may suffer damage or be out of the race.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dreams of Empire: The Wars of South India 1730s-1740s
description:
Dreams of Empire: The Wars of South India 1730s-1740s is a Two-player Operational-level board-wargame set in South India during Europe's Age of Reason. This is not a 'colonial' game. It focuses on the wars of the native states during the 1730s and 1740s AD, such as the Travancore-Dutch War (which Travancore won), the Canarese invasion of Northern Malabar, the Madurai Succession Wars, and kingdom-building by Muslim adventurers on the southern fringe of the Mughal Empire.

The game uses two map scales (4 leagues per hex for the sandbox grand campaign scenario, and 1 league per hex for the various minor scenarios, which use different maps). Unit scale is 'brigade' and manoeuvre-units are armies and 'columns' commanded by leaders. Specialists such as pioneers, elephants, or assassins, are allocated to the regular formations for specific mission requirements (bridging, siegecraft, killing an enemy leader, etc.). Turns represent 3 weeks and scenarios cover periods from one to ten years.

16th volume of Red Sash Games' Lace Wars series and rules-compatible with the other games in the series. Includes rules for adding RSG's Sea Lords module Carnatic War (sold separately in the Four Corners box) to create a 3 to 4-player game.

-description from designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
XSCAPE is an abstract strategy game where players move around a hexagon board that is becoming more and more restricted with every move.

In XSCAPE, players take turns moving one space at a time. After each move, place your Trail Chip on the space you just left to mark it as played. As you navigate the play area, the board evolves and blooms with color. Each space can be played on twice — after the first play, it remains open, but after the second play, it’s sealed with a Black Trail Chip, indicating it's no longer available.

Landing on an X to activate The Reaper. The player who activates The Reaper decides where it begins, with possible starting points marked by blue Xs. 
Once activated, The Reaper moves clockwise, destroying each space it lands on. After each player's move, The Reaper advances one space at a time. If you stand in its path, you're eliminated. The Reaper can also move onto already destroyed spaces.

Outlast all other players (and the Reaper!) to claim victory!

Labyrinth: The Rise of Al-Qaeda, 1993 - 2001 is the third expansion for Volko Ruhnke’s acclaimed Labyrinth: The War on Terror, 2001 - ?, a 1-2 player card-driven boardgame depicting the geopolitical struggle in the Middle East between revolutionary Islamists (mostly Salafist) on the one hand (represented by the “Jihadist” player), and the US-led West plus their supported regimes on the other (represented by the “Coalition” player).

This is the prequel to the original game, but it builds on the more sophisticated mechanics provided by its first sequel, Labyrinth: The Awakening. Rise of Al-Qaeda requires ownership of Labyrinth to play, but it is a full expansion in its own right – you don’t need a copy of Awakening. It can be played with the base game alone to provide a satisfying experience, but it's also possible to use it as part of a “campaign” spanning all of the expansions.

Rise of Al-Qaeda takes the series back in time to cover the period following the World Trade Center bombing, mere weeks into Bill Clinton’s first term, and invites players to compare the dynamics of this earlier period. Despite some obvious differences in how the US fought against Islamist terrorism in the 1990s, the Labyrinth series mechanics are a perfect fit for the era given their strategic, geopolitical, scope.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Five Parsecs From Home: Bug Hunt
description:
This new 63-page PDF edition of Five Parsecs From Home - Bug Hunt includes all the mechanics from the original, updated to fit into the core game rules. This includes features like alien spawn points, alien sub types like the spitter and screamer and alien leaders like the driver or lancer. With 6 sub-types, 6 leader types and 16 alien species to fight, there is a lot of scope for players who enjoy painting, building and converting cool miniatures.

Your troopers can be supported by any of 12 support units from the scientific survey team to a team of tough K’Erin assault troops.

Bug Hunt is a full campaign game with character progression as your troopers advance through their careers. You can even carry over characters from both games - take your crew members from Five Parsecs From Home on a Bug Hunt or vice versa, allowing your characters to experience a whole new level of adventures.

The only question that remains is whether you forgot to activate the security grid. They do say the vent crawlers are active in this sector…

Four Corners contains expansions for the four Sea Lords games set during the time of the War of the Austrian Succession:

1) King George’s War expands the map of Yellow Jack to include New England and Maritime Canada, with a focus on the taking of the French fortress of Louisbourg. This expansion is the most complex, involving expeditionary forces and the same land combat system as Yellow Jack. It also includes a large number of counters.

2) The Carnatic War covers the First Carnatic War of 1744 to 1748 from a purely naval perspective. This is the conflict in which the French captured Madras from the British. Although the maps are isolated from the regions covered by any of the games, the expansion is officially linked to the game Cold Waves. This expansion uses a standard ‘operational’ map for the region of conflict and a strategic map covering the whole of the Indian Ocean. Few counters are provided; most must be supplied by Cold Waves.

3) The Eastern Med expansion extends the map of Mistral to include the remainder of the Mediterranean basin. The expansion itself includes only a few counters — very little action took place in the Levant — but includes the entire 2.0 Upgrade package for Mistral, which was previously available only as a Print-n-Play, and all the errata counters for Mistral.

4) Osmanli Gölü adds a map for the Black Sea. This map can be linked to the Eastern Med and Mistral but the expansion is intended to be used with the Lace Wars games Türkenkrieg and Heirs of the Golden Horde, adding the Russian and Ottoman fleets, which were abstracted in the original games.

Note that these expansions will NOT be sold separately.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Join a Mafia family in this traditional, team based or head to head trick-taking game. A new Challenge arises with each round which must be dealt with, and could even turn the game upside down. The only thing that doesn’t change is the struggle to be the dominant family.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blueprints of Mad King Ludwig: Sax Serenade Room Promo Card
description:
Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 12 Kickstarter campaign.
Also included in Blueprints of Mad King Ludwig: Promo Card Pack 1.

1 new blueprint card. 
6 points.

Only available at Barnes & Noble, the Caffeine Boost promo pack consists of 3 new drug cards called Coffee!

The Coffee Card allows you to play one more card on your turn, meaning you get one more action in addition to your usual two actions per turn. This could give you a huge advantage! But you'll need to be careful: the side effects include Tremors and Anxiety.

Place the Coffee card in your Psyche (not on a Disorder) and you'll instantly get this new ability. You cannot discard it once it's on your Psyche. Sip responsibly!

You are a hunter defending your village. Your mission is to protect your village from the bear menace. It takes more than just you and other players to defeat a bear. You will have to call for help from other hunters living nearby. However, the budget for the rescue are limited. Thorough discussions are needed to defeat the bear on a limited budget!

The Bear Hunter is a cooperative game where you use your limited budget and skills to defeat the bear. You pay neighboring hunters to increase their dice rolls, and if the dice rolls meet the required conditions, you can defeat the bear. Try to defeat bears efficiently. The goal of all players is to defeat five bears without running out of budget.

—description from the designer

Gameplay
Turns: Players alternate turns, either moving/attacking with a Bee (a die) or adding a Reinforcement Bee.
Bees slide like chess pieces; Scouts (#3) can jump over pieces.
Attacking: If a Bee ends its move on an enemy Bee’s hex, the enemy Bee is removed.
Reinforcements: Use Red dice points to bring back removed Bees.
If the Queen is killed, it must be immediately replaced if points allow; otherwise, the game is lost.
Honey Gems: Block movement for all Bees except Workers (#1).
Game End: The game ends when a Queen is killed and the player cannot reinforce with another Queen due to insufficient points.

Bee Types and Movement
Queen (6): Moves 1 hex in any direction.
Warrior (4): Moves any number of hexes in a straight line.
Scout (3): Moves 2 hexes straight, then 1 hex in a different direction.
Drone (2): Moves 2 hexes in any direction.
Worker (1): Moves 1 hex in any direction, can carry Gems.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Showdown: Championship Drag Racing – Pro Stock & Pro Mod  2023 Season
description:
Welcome race fans and gaming enthusiasts to the world’s fastest and exciting sport in history-Pro Stock/Pro-Mod drag racing, 2023 season. The game covers all the events in a full season of racing action, and all you do is choose a racing class and enjoy a fun filled season in this simple simulation in some exciting fast action racing. There are three difficulty modes to choose from depending on the detail of results you wish to have in each race through the season…tokens only, tokens and elapsed times, or tokens, speed trap, and elapsed times.
The system uses dice on gear tables, each class has unique gear tables allowing for a different racing experience for the most exciting auto racing in the world...nothing compares to this excitement.

Get Ready for some Exciting Drag Racing!

—description from the designer

It’s time to literally shake things up with Cocktails, a big-box spirited expansion for Distilled.

Your distillery has now been established, and it’s time to bring your famous spirits to the world! Expand the production capacity of your distillery by adding a second washback, providing you with more acclaim and revenue for your brand. Craft unique cocktails for customers, requiring special mixers that are paired with the spirits from your distillery, spreading your international renown around the globe. Perhaps even consider seeking out an esteemed collector, attracting them with your most prestigious spirits to unlock new end game scoring opportunities.

—description from the publisher

Vietnam, the first “helicopter war.” For over a decade US Army helicopters flew and fought in the skies of Vietnam, Laos and Cambodia. Now Chopper Pilot Vietnam lets you take part in that conflict as a pilot of the ubiquitous Huey or the deadly Cobra.

Chopper Pilot Vietnam is a solitaire and cooperative game in which you fly as a US Army Warrant Officer helicopter pilot during the Vietnam War. You will start your career as a UH-1 “Huey” pilot. Missions will include transport infantry units to designated landing zones, resupply units already in the field, medevac wounded grunts from the battlefield and more. You may be afforded the opportunity to become an AH-1 Cobra pilot and fly assault missions in support of the infantry.

If you are good enough, you might be called upon to work for the infamous Military Assistance Command, Vietnam – Studies and Observation Group (MACV-SOG). Your missions there will be in support of the special forces Spike Teams which you will drop off deep into North Vietnam, Cambodia or Laos, then pick them up under dire circumstances if they are discovered by the North Vietnamese.

Regardless of your mission, there will be ground troops depending on you, either to get them to the battlefield or resupply them under fire or to evacuate them if they are wounded in combat. Are you up to the challenge?

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Take turns adding a single letter tile into a Connect Four style frame to make words and score points.

The Golden Journey takes you on a trip all around the world, and just like the journey of life, this journey is filled with various challenges that must be overcome, where knowledge must be demonstrated and learned every day, and where luck may help us out (Or not!).

The difficulty of the challenges could vary in order to fit the player. This means it can be played competitively, for fun, or for cognitive stimulation. So anyone, from children to senior citizens, can join in on the ride.

Cognitive Challenge for All Ages: Designed for kids, adults, and seniors, this adaptable board game helps improve memory, attention, and cognitive skills while exploring the wonders of the world.
    Educational Geography Adventure: Travel across countries, cities, monuments, and animals as you navigate the board, learning fun facts about each location and enhancing your global knowledge.
    Memory Game with a Twist: Engage your mind with exciting memory challenges that make learning geography interactive and enjoyable for players of all ages.
    Family-Friendly Fun: Perfect for family game nights, this game brings generations together for a shared learning experience that’s both fun and educational.
    Adaptable and Inclusive: Suitable for players of all skill levels, with adjustable difficulty settings to challenge everyone from kids to seniors, making it ideal for cognitive stimulation.
    Beautifully Illustrated Game Components: Vibrant and engaging illustrations of landmarks, animals, and countries immerse players in a world of discovery and adventure.
    Travel the World from Home: Explore famous monuments, fascinating animals, and diverse cultures without leaving your living room—ideal for curious minds and geography enthusiasts.
    Perfect Gift for Learners and Travelers: Whether for kids, parents, teachers, or seniors, this game makes an ideal gift for those who love to learn, explore, and challenge their minds.

To play, you roll the die, and move your pawn.  You will land on a space that can be either, Test your Luck, Expand Knowledge, or Cognitive challenge.  The Tour Guide(Another player) reads the question or sets up the cognitive challenge cards.  If you are correct, you follow the directions on the bottom of the card (move x spaces forward and wait for your next turn)  First player to reach the end space id the winner.

TrapBall is the ultimate ring sliding game!

It's a fast action game where 2 players complete to trap weighted balls inside the rings.
