Warm up your wrists and go! With a little practice you will get these rings sliding across the table, underneath the balls, to stop right where they need to be around the balls!

Slide them from further and further away for an even more challenging game!

Win the game by being the first to trap 3 balls!

—description from the back of the box

Opetellaan Liikennepeli is an educational board game for children aged 6–10 that teaches them traffic rules and regulations. Players must visit specific points on the board. Each turn begins with a traffic trivia question, and correct answers will give more movement points.

Gameplay
On their turn, a player takes 2 actions:

Moving a Bee
Bees jump like a knight in chess.
If a Bee lands on a hex with Pollen, the player collects it and performs any associated action.

Moving a Butterfly
After moving their Bee, the player moves a Butterfly.
Butterflies also move like a knight in chess.
If a Butterfly lands on a hex with a Bee, the Bee’s owner loses a Pollen, which is randomly selected and removed from the game.

Game End
The game ends when the last Pollen is drawn from the bag.
Different colors of Pollen have different scoring rules.
The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

While returning from your field trip from the planet of Sweets, you realize your rockets are running out of fuel to reach planet Ultia.  Eat the sweets you don't need, and generate fuel to hopefully make it home safely!

PAKU PACK is the next game set in ULTIA's world designed by the same team.

PAKU PACK is a 3-6 player deck building game set in space.  
Each turn you need to generate energy by playing your cards. However your rocket (card deck) is filled with candy which slows down your energy generation.

Players can "Start the Engine" only once per turn to convert any number of cards into energy, and use that energy to gain new cards from the display.

Player can also play any number of cards to perform the actions listed on the cards.

The first player to convert a certain amount of energy is the winner!

A simulation of the Soviet offensive against Finland in the summer of 1944. The Soviet player must defeat Finland as soon as possible within a limited timeframe so that the Red Army can return to the decisive direction of the war, the Bagration offensive in June-July 1944.

Historically, the Soviets achieved a marginal victory — the Finnish had to withdraw from Ladoga Karelia, but nevertheless prevented the Soviet forces from achieving their ultimate objective — the unconditional surrender of Finland and the Sovietization of the country.

Each game turn represents about seven days. Each hexagon is six miles (10 kilometers) from side to opposite side. Units are corps, divisions, brigades or equivalents and some specialized smaller units.

boardgame: The Path to Victory: Middle East 1941
description:
The Path to Victory: Middle East, 1941, is a simulation of the operations in Iraq, Syria and Iran (Persia) during the Spring and Summer of 1941. In this game, both players must carefully use their limited resources (military and political) to try to wrestle control of the Middle East away from their enemies.

The game begins with the Iraqi coup in early April 1941. The Axis player may attempt a number of diplomatic/military actions: supporting the Iraqi coup, trying to take control of Persia, unleash an Arab revolt in Palestine, send aid to Iraq, Vichy Syria and possibly Persia. The Allied player must use their limited resources to assert control in Iraq, Persia, Syria and Lebanon as soon as possible to protect the rearguard of the Suez Canal and the Western Desert Front.

Each game turn represents about 10 days. Each hexagon is 37 miles (60 kilometers) from side to the opposite side. Units are army, divisions, brigades, regiments, and battalions or equivalent, and some specialized smaller units.

A two player alternative history wargame that examines the possibilities inherent in the campaign that could have occurred in northwest Africa late in 1940 if the French had resolved not to give up. The assumption is they instead chose to evacuate as much of the French Army as possible to Tunisia, Algeria and French Morocco.

Both sides’ orders of battle are historically based. That is, the German force in Spain is what they had intended to use for the invasion of England around this same time. The assumption there is, if they believed they could lift that force across the English Channel, the Strait of Gibraltar would have presented less difficulty – eight miles wide vs. 21 at the Pas de Calais – once Spain had been dragooned into joining the Axis.

The Spanish divisions are the two corps of their Army of North Africa (the original supporters of Franco). The rest of their ground force is assumed to be invading Portugal, as was their plan had things gone this way. During the game the Allied player will have to cope with decisions made ‘above his head’ in London in regard to fleet commitment and the BEF’s initial landing site. That arbitrariness is reflective of the Allies’ historic inability to create smoothly operating joint service headquarters during the war’s first half.

Each game turn represents half a month. Each hex represents about 10 days. Each hex is 20 miles (32.4 km) from side-to-side. Units are divisions.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Theme: High and Low is a card game that combines elements of strategy and chance. The theme revolves around comparing the values of cards and using special cards to manipulate the game's flow.

Game-play: Players start with a hand of cards and aim to play cards with higher values than their opponents. Special cards, such as the 2, 3, 10, and 7, can be used to change the game's dynamics. For example, the 2 resets the count, the 3 skips a turn, the 10 removes all cards, and the 7 forces the next player to play a lower card. Players can also play pairs, triplets, and four of a kind to challenge their opponents.

Goal: The primary goal of High and Low is to be the last player remaining with cards in their hand. Players can achieve this by strategically playing their cards, using special cards effectively, and avoiding collecting cards from the center. The game is often won through a combination of skill, luck, and strategic thinking.

—description from the designer

A Purrfect Murder is a downloadable modern-day murder mystery game for 9 to 12 guests and one host. Set at the Purrfect Paws Pageant, a cat-fancy show, characters include contestants and judges.

It is the final of the Purrfect Paws Pageant, a cat-fancy competition boasting a $1,000,000 prize! The feline contestants, their owners and the judges arrived at the Catford Hotel and Exhibition Center yesterday evening.

Unfortunately, Lady Catherine “Kitty” Percival, the extremely wealthy owner of competition frontrunner Baba Ganoush III, was found dead in her hotel room earlier this morning. The judges and contestants are detained in the exhibition center pending a police investigation. Police have cordoned off the hotel, preventing the public from attending.

However, the show must go on! The contestants and judges are gathered in the exhibition hall to mingle, crown the pinnacle of feline grace and beauty and, perhaps, find the killer of Lady Percival.

High-stakes cat fancy is a dangerous world rife with bribery, theft, blackmail, and all kinds of dodgy dealings – even murder! Who killed Lady Percival – and why?

—description from the designer

The Roswell Incident is a 1940s murder mystery game with an alien theme for 10 to 13 guests and one host. Set in 1947 Roswell, New Mexico. Find out what really happened in Roswell. Did a flying saucer crash – or was it something else?

In June 1947, an Idaho pilot reported seeing a string of nine shiny unidentified flying objects flying past Mount Rainier at speeds of 1,200 miles an hour. One week later, something crashed near Roswell, New Mexico (population 25,000). An experimental aircraft? A weather balloon? A secret military project? Or something else?

Two days later and Roswell has visitors. It is noon, July 3, 1947.

Welcome to The Roswell Incident, where we discover what really happened in Roswell. Why did the US Army and FBI arrive in Roswell? What happened on the Turner ranch? What did crash outside Roswell?

—description from the publisher

A game of matching bus, route, and driver cards in order to collect the most passengers.

You and your fellow players are new trainees learning to co-ordinate the buses in Reading. You have a depot full of brightly painted buses, a large stack of routes, and your driver colleagues. Each bus card shows one of the seven different colours or a rainbow replacement bus (wild card). Each route card is one of the seven routes or the big map of everything (wild card). Each driver can drive three different routes — but there are no wild cards because all the drivers are very careful. In the middle of the table are four different bus stops, at which passengers are waiting. You collect passengers by matching a bus card, a route card, and a driver card to the colour on the passenger card. The player with the most passengers at the end of the game wins.

Busy Buses was co-designed by children at a primary school in Reading. At its heart, it is a simple game. Collect the cards, match the cards, pick up passengers. However, players can pick up multiple passengers, so sometimes it’s worth waiting around, especially in a two player game. Players can also pick up an additional card by discarding matching cards in their hand, which could be a risky strategy, especially if you can’t remember what has already been discarded. In short, this is primarily aimed at children aged between 6 and 12 years old, but with enough interest to capture the interest of adults too.

—description from the publisher

Compete in a series of perilous quests in the first card game officially supported by the family of legendary author Bram Stoker.

The game is played using 4 decks of cards - North, South, East, and West. The winner is the first person to complete 7 quest cards, or 5 if you get at least 1 from each location.

There are 3 types of quests:
VANQUISH MONSTERS - Requires 2 specific object cards.  
SHATTER CURSES - Requires a set number of cards from each specified location. 
DEFEAT HUNTERS - Requires a rival to draw from specified locations during their turn.

But beware... 
The sinister Count Dracula is poised to strike when least expected! Play powerful Dracula cards to help complete your quests, or hinder the competition.

Stokerverse is quick to learn, fun and strategic to play, and features a beautiful Gothic horror art style.

—description from the designer

Match what you see to what you hear.

Each of the 400 challenge cards features 8 colour-coded clues and 5 answers. The answers to 6 of the 8 clues will form pairs of homonyms (sounding the same, spelt the same way, but with different meanings). The remaining 2 answers are the 'red herrings'. They will be homophones (sounding the same, but spelt differently and with different meanings).

Your task is to identify the 2 homophones within 30 seconds to score points. Be the only player to find the red herrings and you will score a double win!

—description from the designer

In Loose In The Library, the players are apprentices in a cursed library. Literary terrors have escaped from the books and are roaming the shelves. The long-suffering Librarian has set you a number of different challenges. Who will be the first one to capture the Kraken, Fenrir, or Grendel's Mother? Or terrors with specific symbols? Or use a fictional object? Each challenge is worth points, as are the terrors themselves. You will have both public challenges for which everyone competes and private challenges which you score only for yourself at the end.

You capture the terrors by drawing fictional and historical heroines into your hand. These inspirational women provide you with different symbols, and so you are looking for the right combination of symbols to allow you to capture the terrors currently visible in the middle of the table. You're after Martian Robots? That's two red hearts and two green ovals, please. Luckily Anne of Green Gables and Marie Curie have you covered! Succeed in capturing a terror, and you get a reward. It may be a magical object, or a piece of helpful library equipment — such as the photocopier, which allows you to duplicate symbols on your book cards, or the library clock, which gives you an additional action.

At the end of the game, add up the points gained from captured terrors and completed challenges. The player with the highest score wins.

Loose In The Library is played in multiple different ways. There are three chapters, with the possibility of more to come. Each chapter uses the same cards, but has slightly different mechanics. Chapter 1 is the base game. Chapter 2 introduces negative points. Chapter 3 includes drafting. Play them all in order, then decide which one will become your standard version!

—description from the designer

World War II wargaming using block models that are fun for anyone to make. No more glue and paint, just fun straight out of the box. Accessible and fun for everyone regardless of age or experience. Super simple rules and are constantly developing the game to make it appeal to the history buffs as well as the young and enthusiastic.

In The CHD Game, you are a Contract Hardware Distributor.

Starting with a small team and a little money, your job is to create and execute a strategy to turn your business into a streamlined and efficient product delivery machine. You'll be challenged to use your resources carefully...which markets to serve, what upgrades to invest in, what actions to take (and at what time) for the greatest return.

Think ahead, react, adapt, and anticipate...and keep an eye on your competition.

(please note that this is an educational game targeted to distributor personnel or professionals who work in the architectural hardware industry)

-description from the publisher

D-Day Dice: Pacific is the newest entry into the D-Day Dice franchise. Following on from D-Day Dice - 2nd Edition, which featured the Normandy Landings, this stand-alone expansion centers on the Pacific/South Asian Theater, with new maps, new card types, and a new roster of Specialists, Items, Awards and Vehicles.

In D-Day Dice: Pacific, players not only fight against the enemy, they are also at war with the environment. The game reflects the realities of tropical warfare by offering battlefields that are both dangerous and unpredictable. Its Battle Maps include new features like rivers to cross, cave maps to explore, and infested sectors that can make your specialists sick. The game also includes “Strike” cards, a deck that simulates naval and aerial bombardments from both the Allies and the Axis, with all the dangers associated with friendly fire and collateral damage.

As with all other editions of D-Day Dice, all content here is backwards and forward compatible with all previous and upcoming D-Day Dice games. You do not need any previous DDD products to enjoy this game. The box contains everything you need to play.

—description from the publisher

Zona reveals an unfamiliar face to the toughest veterans. Scavengers Wish is an expansion to the board game Zona: Secret of Chernobyl, which will enrich your gameplay with new elements and allow you to delve even deeper into your adventure in the radiation-contaminated zone.

New event cards – complete quests, acquire valuable Rarities and have adventures while resting in bunkers.
Encounters – meet other players on the trail. Will you help each other or fight?
Travel companions – recruit companions and take advantage of their unique skills.
Persistence Dice and Fortune Cards lets you manage good and bad luck.

There is also more of the original goodness - add more event cards, items and artifacts, mutants, anomalies and a few lock tokens to Zona.

—description from the publisher

An expansion for D-Day Dice featuring sea maps, Battle Conditions, Servicemen, Officers and new War Stories.

—description from the publisher

Scoop Mania is a strategy game based in Ice Cream Land. You are an ice cream sundae maker; striving to make sundaes. Brace yourself for an unforgettable journey as you use energy to reach the ice cream carts and scoop up ice creams to make sundaes. Use your energy wisely as you will be left stranded without energy. As you savour each delectable sundae, you’ll earn YUM points, revealing your prowess as the true sundae maker. Earn maximum YUM points to emerge as the reigning champion Sundae Master!

Scoop Mania is designed to be played as a fun family game. Kids, parents and grandparents can easily play this game together for some wholesome family time.

—description from the publisher

Hungry Hoppers is a light strategy tile placement game that will leave you hungry for more!

You, as a farmer, choose and build fields of vibrant vegetables for the Hungry Hoppers to eat. You devise cunning strategies to attract as many hoppers to your fields as you can. The weather fairy may also visit you and bestow beautiful weather, resulting in bonus harvests. So come along, join the other farmers on their quest to satisfy the ravenous appetites of the lovable hungry hoppers as you compete to score the most points.

Hungry Hoppers is designed to be played across multiple age groups too. Kids, parents and grandparents can easily play this game together for some wholesome family time.

Game Play Highlights
Choose tiles to add to your farm
Place the tiles to create new or add to existing fields 
Place and Feed the hungry hoppers to score points

—description from the publisher

A story telling game where you Imagine, Express, Create.

Telling stories is an important activity in childhood. Story telling enables and fires up the imagination and creativity of the developing mind. It develops oral expression and fluency in spoken language. It creates a good conducive environment to try out new vocabulary. Story elements have to be logically connected, which boosts logical thinking. Story Time is a fun travel game which assists with all this!

Story Time requires no setup except a shuffle. Shuffle the cards and put them face down in the center. Draw a card and start creating a story using the character, object, scene or good manners. Draw another card and continue the story. Conclude the story when "The End" card is drawn.

Story Time is sized like a double deck of playing cards and so easy to carry around. It's great for indoors or outdoors or even when on the road!

—description from the publisher

A threat looms over Nevermore School and chaos ensues. The students must defend themselves! Playing as one of your favorite characters, you'll try to thwart a monstrous threat that is terrorizing the school's surroundings. Work together to prevent Hyde's attacks and protect the other students.

In this completely cooperative game, each player takes on the role of one of Nevermore's students, each with a unique power and a hand of 3 cards with values between 1 and 5 in five different suits.

Each turn, the player decides whether to stay where they are or move to an adjacent location. They then place a card from their hand to their current location, without revealing the card, and replenish their hand. Each location has modifiers for the cards, valid at the moment of the attack. Players are not allowed to say which cards they have and which they have place at each location, creating a communication barrier for the team.

After each player takes a turn, an attack token is revealed, showing where Hyde was spotted. But the attack only actually takes place on the last token revealed, which means that each round the players have more information about where the attack will actually take place. When the attack happens, the players reveal all the cards played at the location and apply the appropriate modifiers from the location. If the total points of the cards is greater than Hyde's current attack, according to a table showing the number of players and the difficulty chosen by the group, the players have prevented the attack. Otherwise, a student has been killed.

The cards from this location are discarded and Hyde's tokens, except where attacks have already taken place, are shuffled and the game continues until Hyde has carried out attacks on all locations. If the players manage to save a certain number of students, they win the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In the peaceful garden, little underground engineers are digging tunnels everywhere. There’s no time to waste—it’s time to uncover the adorable moles!

Gather sets of moles, but there’s a problem: as soon as you catch one, the others hide!

Form sets of three matching moles, trigger special effects, and explore the advanced mode where a friendly dog will help you in your search.

Who will become the best mole collector?

—description from the publisher

2-player abstract board game where you create higher and higher piles when you capture each other's pieces, and capture each other back. Object of the game is to escape to the outer ring with all your piles.

The piles are called "stacks", escaping to the outer ring "exit", hence the name "Staxxit".

It was inspired by classic games like chess, checkers and go, but with every aspect a little different. The way the game starts and ends, the way the pieces move, the way you capture each other: everything is uniquely different.

—description from the designer

Talvisota (Finnish for "Winter War") is a card-driven simulation of the Winter War of 1939-40. It uses the same card driven system as Normandy 1944 (Command Japan #141) and Manstein's Trump (Command Japan #132) by the same designer.

All players form one team. Passing and receiving colorful wooden pieces, the team works together to solve increasingly complex puzzles as the missions progress. During the game, no one can speak. No gestures, either. Yet somehow, through this silence, players find ways to communicate and complete the puzzles. Sometimes what you hope your teammate will understand gets across instantly, in the blink of an eye. Other times, a misunderstanding ends up becoming the key to solving the puzzle. One player might take charge and lead the team, while at other times, the entire group works together seamlessly, finishing the puzzle without even realizing it. And then, out of nowhere, the entire team might burst into uncontrollable laughter—loud, joyful, and full of energy, as everyone realizes the hilarity of the moment. It’s a game where unexpected moments of success and fun often surprise you. Enjoy everything from the satisfying feeling of completing the puzzle together to the amusing frustration when things don’t go as planned, all in Silentalk.

Capture a heavily-fortified hill, track down a wayward Marine officer, and engage in four wildly different missions inspired by some of the most renowned and popular Vietnam War Hollywood movies!

—description from the publisher

Lead a small yet heavily armed Marine Force Recon Team on extremely dangerous Keyhole patrols, report on the position of enemy units, snatch prisoners and search for hidden enemy bases, all the while trying to avoid getting caught in a firefight you can’t win.

Force Recon: Keyhole is a new 3-mission mini-campaign that you can play right after completing the core Purple Haze campaign. It offers a tense and challenging experience, with new movement mechanics and Marine specializations, and a variety of new weapons.

—description from the publisher

Lead a small yet heavily armed Marine Force Recon Team on daring Stingray patrols, search for enemy units and eliminate them with extreme prejudice, employing stealth, ambush tactics and multiple fire support assets in the air and on the ground.

Force Recon: Stingray is a new 3-mission mini-campaign that you can play right after completing the core Purple Haze campaign. It offers a tense and challenging experience, with new movement mechanics and Marine specializations, and a variety of new weapons.

—description from the publisher

Collect gems so that they don't overlap with other players, put up a barrier to prevent other players from stealing them, and steal the gems before other players get them.

Players point at the gem tables lined up in the center of the table at the same time. If you're not pointing at the same gem as someone else, all of the gems will be yours. If you choose the same gem as someone else (if you end up batting), everyone who chose the same gem will not be able to get the gem. There is also a bonus for matching three colors of gems, which increases your score, and you can read each other's moves, such as "I really want blue" or "Everyone is going for red, so let's aim for a place that doesn't seem to be popular." The gems you get are "temporarily" placed in your safe. However, from the next time onwards, the "safe" will also be a target for pointing, so there is a possibility that it will be stolen by someone. To prevent this, you can "put up a barrier" to prevent the gems from being stolen, but if you put up a barrier, you will have to pass on your next turn. Do you throw away the chance to get the gem and put up a barrier, or do you get through by bluffing and saying, "I'll put up a barrier, so don't point at me." It's a game that always creates tension and excitement.

Echo from the Dark is the fourth game in the Irregular Conflicts Series, depicting an interstellar struggle between divergent factions after humanity discovers a material that allows for faster-than-light travel and communication. Players will use this material to deploy unique technologies that shape their faction identity across the course of the game, meaning that every playthrough is a unique experience. As they expand across the galaxy, the human population will follow, providing new incentives and opportunities. Eventually, their visions for the future will prove to be irreconcilable: only one player will be able to determine the fate of humanity.

Highlights:

     A highly variable setup: Choose from a selection of different factions to arrange on a modular map. As the game goes on, players will discover valuable Salt and dangerous anomalies that further change the texture of the map.
     A shifting population: At this scale, population grows, moves, and declines over time, encouraging factions to consider the longer-term ramifications of their actions.
     Technology: Every round reveals a new technology that can be researched to shape each faction over the course of the game.
     Unique non-player opposition: Aliens, artificial intelligence, and disease form some of the possible opposition factions that each act through their own special system.
     Streamlined sequence of play: Every faction may act on every round, with smaller actions allowing a player better initiative in the next round. Player factions all have access to the same basic actions and can use researched Technology no matter what action they take.


—description from the publisher

Portal Block - Pandora's box blows open - a technology that provides the power of teleportation disrupts beyond imagination. Each character in the game and the cards hints at the various threads of fiction spawned from a technology capable of manipulating everything; bio-hacking, the fabric of space and time, other dimensions, other worlds...everything. This game is made to incite the imagination with its iconography and fictional smorgasbord.

Welcome to Portal Block! 
The unique unboard game. A Challenging and action packed tabletop experience.

Players assemble levels during play with blocks drawn randomly and strategically placed. Each player moves their special characters using currency, cards, and dice to navigate the Cubescape. Portal Block is a volatile race, full of twists, dips, and dupes. A mix of strategy and chance ratchet into an intense finale!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Root: The Squires and Disciples Deck
description: Try out new strategies with the Squires and Disciples deck! Just like the Exiles and Partisans deck, this deck can be swapped in to replace the deck from the base game. This deck includes cards inspired by the Marauder and Homeland factions and abilities that depend on hand composition, with a dash of goofs and shenanigans. Want to change when the game ends or how dominance cards work? You can!

Three new hirelings enter the fray: the Freshwater Refugees, Heartwood Peacemakers, and the neutral Prosperous Farmers. Each comes with a double-sided tarot card and new meeples, adding even more variety to your games of Root.

In Mining Colony: Master Plans, a mini-expansion pack to Mining Colony, players collect Master Plan cards during the excavation phase which provide them with new goals for their mining colonies.

The pack comes with 16 Master Plan cards. In each round, a card is added to each excavation zone. When a player takes the resources from a zone, they also take a Master Plan card and immediately place it around one of the 4 sides of their mining colony, pointing to 1 of the rows or columns. Players score bonus points at game end if the item on the Master Plan card matches items in the row or column.

The pack gives players even more to think about as they build their colony.  The pack is compatible with the Mining Colony: Secret Missions Pack.

—description from the designer

Inscryption IRL is based on the popular roguelike deck-building video game "Inscryption". It has been fully re-designed from the ground up, specifically for two player gameplay.

Players draft a deck of unique creature cards to battle each other. These creatures have different strengths and weaknesses symbolised by sigils.
But don't get too attached, in order to beat your opponent, you may need to sacrifice some of your own cards.

Each player has two lives. Upon death, players are reborn into a powerful death card, which can be played with in all future games.

Assign dice to the monsters lurking in the dark crypt corridors! Choose the dice that you will roll. Then try to get out with the most gold before any other adventurer! The Butt-load of dice dungeon is a strategic medieval adventure where you compete for gold. But be careful, the monsters get stronger each turn!
Game Mechanics: Resource management, memory, character advancement. 
Buttload of Dice Dungeon is a game where you roll a bunch of dice into the box lid and assign them to monsters as you navigate the dungeon. There are decks of room cards that contain helpful items and quests, if you can remember where they are located.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tiny Laser Heist: Double Cross Expansion
description:
Glitter Locks, Sabotage Cards, and Bribable Guards!

The base game of Tiny Laser Heist is accessible to anyone, with a focus on keeping heists fast and fun. But, if you're a serious Mastermind looking for more a mind-bending and cutthroat game, the Double Cross Expansion just for you.

—description from the designer
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

boardgame: Dwellings of Eldervale: Oracle Hex Tile
description:
The Oracle is a Ruin realm tile. Place a Unit on the Oracle to purchase one Adventure card or gain one Magic card.

Looking to add opportunity to do more Engine Building while playing Dwellings of Eldervale? The Oracle tile is your best option.

The Oracle Realm may extend game length since players will be able to gain Adventure cards and build their set of Tableau Actions without pulling a tile from the Realm Stack - one of the two "game clocks".

~~~ provided by the Publisher

On June 25, 1950, the North Korean People's Army advanced in force across the 38th parallel. Its mission: the forcible reunification of the Korean peninsula.

Trevor Bender's Korea: The Fight Across the 38th recreates the opening salvos of the Korean War. It is the third volume of the C3i Combined Arms Series, after Battle for Kursk: The Tigers are Burning, 1943 and Desert Victory: North Africa, 1940-1942. The series rules have been adapted to capture the ebb-and-flow of operations as well as the rugged terrain and the logistical challenges faced by each side.

Korea: The Fight Across the 38th is featured in C3i magazine nr. 38, which also includes: 

     The Siege of Cadiz 1625, a solitaire design by Daniel Hernández Iniesta
     Battle for Moscow, a re-issue of Frank Chadwick's classic


Game scale
Units: Division
Map: 1 hex = 25 miles
Turns: One month
Playing Time: 2 to 3 hours

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
War has started on the island of Hampshire in this 3-8 player survival party board game. Dive into Hampshire Battle Royale by competing in a series of dice, card and Beirut inspired mini games. Hampshire consists of 100 mini games, 25 hack cards, and 20 legendary loot cards.

Objective: Avoid reaching the center of the island, Mariki's Maelstrom, while trying to outplay your opponents in various mini-games.

Outcomes:
Poor Performance: Players who perform poorly move closer to the center.
Good Performance: Players who excel move further away from the center.

The game continues until only one player remains on the game board, declaring them the winner. Each victor gets to sign the back of the board forever etching their name in the history of Hampshire Battle Royale.

Will you be the next to outlast your opponents in Hampshire Battle Royale?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Epic Seven Arise: The Boardgame – Acolytes Set
description:
The  Acolytes Set includes two boss characters Kayron and Specter Tenebria with three Wyvern. This set completes the three acolytes that appeared in Episode 1.

This set includes 10 enemy cards with quests that are compatible and fit perfectly with the chapters from the Core Box.

boardgame: Epic Seven Arise: The Boardgame – Guardians Set
description:
Guardians set introduce two new mechanisms in the game; summon guardians and full coop game mode.

3 Guardians Arkasus, Kromcruz and Zeaon acrylic standees. Zeaon is standing approx 11 to 14cm tall!!

boardgame: Dwellings of Eldervale: Legendary Upgrade Kit
description:
Tales will be told of the day you acquired this legendary upgrade kit for Dwellings of Eldervale! Let's make some NOISE!!!

The Legendary Upgrade Kit includes 8 new Elemental Monsters and 9 Sound FX Bases. During game play players will now have a chose as to which Elemental Monster should appear when a Monster Lair tile is added to Eldervale.

Each Monster is 3 components: a 50mm Miniature, a standee (as an option to using the miniature), and a game card (details the Monster's unique abilities/game effects). The miniatures are made of colored plastic - the color of their Element - and have a black "wash" applied to highlight the sculpt details.

Elemental Monster Name and Element:
- Cloud Spirit (Air)
- Charging Earth (Earth)
- Volcanic Terror (Fire)
- The Kraken (Water)
- Golden Gryphon (Light)
- Haunting Phantom (Darkness)
- Bringer of Justice (Order)
- Arcane Thrasher (Chaos)

Each sound FX base is loaded with 2 Monster sounds so may be used with either the Deluxe or Legendary Monster miniature.

The 9th sound FX base is for the Frost Giant and Mother of Dragons optional play mini expansions included in the base game set of components.

ALL these components FIT INSIDE the Standard base game box.

~~~ Provided by Breaking Games

Murder on the Istanbul Express is a downloadable murder mystery game set aboard the luxurious Istanbul Express for 7 to 11 guests and one host. Set in 1914, at the outbreak of World War I, characters include the train crew, merchants, inventors – and spies!

The luxurious Istanbul Express takes three days to travel between Istanbul, Turkey and Paris, France. The train line is lavishly opulent and has a glowing reputation. Tonight, some of Europe's most notable citizens and travelers (and some who are not so notable) are journeying in style through the European countryside.

Two days ago (June 29, 1914), just before the train left Istanbul, newspapers announced that Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria, heir to the throne of Austria-Hungary, had been assassinated in neighboring Bosnia. Everyone is now on edge – this could be the start of a great war.

A further trouble is the discovery of a dead body in the kitchen car this evening. Selda Zaim, the maid of Turkish merchant Esra Hoca, appears to have been bludgeoned over the head with a cooking pot.

It is now midnight and only a few hours until the Istanbul Express arrives in Paris and the proper authorities can investigate the crime. But until then, there are many mysteries to unfold…

—description from the publisher

Vanished in Vegas is a downloadable present-day murder mystery game for 15 to 20 guests and a host. Set in Las Vegas’s Sapphire Hotel and Casino, guests include the staff, performers, guests, and visitors.

Welcome to Las Vegas, where, as the saying goes, "What happens in Vegas, stays in Vegas."

The Sapphire Hotel and Casino hosts Vegas' hottest ticket – Jaspar the Great's Magic Show. Jaspar never disappoints, and his show climaxes with his famous vanishing act, where he disappears into a case and reappears wearing an extravagant costume unique for each show!

Tonight, however, when his assistant unveiled the case, Jaspar did not reappear – the case was empty! His assistant shrieked and ran off the stage in tears – but was that all part of the act? What happened to Jaspar?

It is now an hour later, and Jaspar has yet to make an appearance. Undercover FBI agent, Declan Grant, is holding everyone in the casino for questioning. Everyone may mingle but cannot leave until the mystery of Jaspar's disappearance has been solved.

The pandemonium is just beginning… a magician, a bag of money, and a pet chihuahua have all vanished. Who is responsible? A ghost and a Bengal tiger aren't the only suspicious culprits. Con artists, mafia thugs, blackmailers, and thieves will all come into play.

Get ready for a one-of-a-kind, spectacular evening at The Sapphire on the Strip in Vanished in Vegas!

—description from the publisher

Go on your investigation and solve the riddles that will lead you to the culprit, using visuals to decode.

Who put the fish in the laundry basket? Who tore up the Chinese manuscript? Was it the suspect Sing-Song? Was the culprit wearing a hat?

Visit the witnesses and collect the clues using the decoder. Think you've spotted the culprit? Check that you've done your homework by using the Revealer.

Who will be the first to discover the culprit?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Pars mener ton enquête et résous les énigmes qui te mèneront au coupable à partir de visuels à décoder.

Qui a glissé un poisson dans le panier à linge ? Qui a déchiré le manuscrit chinois ? Est-ce le suspect Sing-Song ? Le coupable portait-il un chapeau ?

Rends visite aux témoins et collecte les indices grâce au décodeur. Tu penses avoir repéré le coupable ? Vérifie si tu as bien enquêté en utilisant le révélateur.

Qui sera le premier à découvrir le coupable ?

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
This expansion pack is made up of 6 NOT very popular genres in the domestic market. The romantic genre was marked by two unrivaled hits: “Pretty Woman” and “Dirty Dancing”. These romantic stories took a step further after the sexual revolution and erotic cinema lived in great splendor during these decades. Films based on real events always had their audience in the form of traditional iopics, trials and prison dramas or sporting exploits. And within sports, cars and frenetic races always stood out.

—description from the designer

Este pack final incluye estos géneros “descolgados” y los une para terminar de esta forma este gran compendio lúdico de las películas que marcaron toda una época y a toda una generación. El género romántico estuvo marcado por dos éxitos inigualables: “Pretty Woman” y “Dirty Dancing”. Estas historias románticas dieron un paso más tras la revolución sexual y el cine erótico vivió
con gran esplendor estas décadas. Las películas basadas en hechos reales siempre tuvieron su publico bien en forma de tradicional biopic, de juicios y dramas carcelarios o hazañas deportivas. Y dentro del deporte siempre destacaron los Autos y las carreras frenéticas, como la carrera que se enfrentan los jugadores para ganar la partida.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Gravity Three is a fun unique set of games based on the law of gravity.

Memory×Gravity -  Choose 3 weights to get the heaviest combination.  Players take turns testing and lifting the unknown weights and taking 1 of them.  With 3 weights you form a digit with 3 numbers.  The best 3-digit combination wins!

Comparison×Gravity - Outwit your opponents in a 5 round battle to see who can tip the seesaw scale!  
Chose a location to place one of your 5 weights.  But play strategically, as the closer you are to the center of the scale the heavier the weight you will need to tip the balance in your favor.

Doubt×Gravity - Outbluff your opponents to get rid of all your weights.  Roll the dice to determine the target number.  Players can play to add their 'target number' weights in the central tower or pass.  Will you be brave enough to call out each other's bluffs?

Three games for up to three players using weights with different masses.

Point Art is the result of a collaboration between Cranio Creations and Quercetti.
Make the most of the pins you draw to make your friend guess the right picture!
Will they be able to pick on your clues?
Play in cooperative mode or challenge each other in team mode!

This game has two variants: one is cooperative, the other is a competition between two teams.
In both versions, one of the players must try to make others guess an image among the 16 visible ones. However, they cannot draw it using paper and pen; instead, they must use randomly selected colored pins. The task will be quite challenging! Players need luck and must use the few available pins wisely to provide hints through the placement of certain colored points. When the Artist feels they have done a good job, they give the signal and the other players try to guess!
Try to guess as many images as possible! Beat the record!

—description from the publisher

Animal chess with beer mats on a playing field of 4x4 squares. Arrows indicate the possible moves. If you capture an opponent's animal, you can use it again as your own. With an expansion animal.

—description from the publisher

Step into the untamed frontier with Treacherous Trails, a thrilling Wild West adventure and follow up to Treacherous Tides.  Do you have what it takes to outsmart your enemies, stake your claim, and build your fortune in the Wild West? Use your wits, strategy, and the unique abilities of your chosen character, whether it's a sharp-shooting outlaw, a cunning shaman, or a savvy mechanic.

Treacherous Trails delivers a mix of tile exploration, treasure hunting, and intense duels. In this standalone game, you’ll craft your own map of the wild, flip journey tiles to explore unknown lands, and uncover hidden treasure. But beware — danger lurks at every turn, from mutinous allies to rival treasure hunters! With every flip of the tile, the West unfolds before you. Will you find fortune, face betrayal, or be lost to the sands of time? Each game changes, like the sands of time, and new frontiers will be forged.

Bring your Pirates to the Wild West Tile Mechanic, or take the Outlaws to search the high seas in Treacherous Tides by merging your pirates with cowboys.

—description from the publisher

Shuffle the cards well and place them in a pile face down. In turn, players draw one card from the pile. As soon as a player has cards in hand that form a set, those cards are placed in front of the player on the table.

If the player is left without cards, they wait for their turn to draw a new card. When the pile is depleted, the players count their points. One point is awarded for each card in their own pile, minus one point for each card left in hand. The player with the most points wins. The game can continue for several rounds, just keep track of the points and add them up.

Pöbbkviss is a popular and entertaining trivia game that everyone can join, whether in large groups or smaller circles of friends. The game is flexible, allowing for various ways of playing, and there is no limit to the number of participants.

With Pöbbkviss 4, a new and exciting edition is released, featuring 1,000 brand-new and diverse questions that challenge knowledge across various categories.

In a Mexican town suffering from a terrible wave of violence, a group of searchers try to discover what happened to their missing relatives.
This game invites reflection on the importance of knowing the truth and getting to know the actors behind all this violence.

In this Tabletop “Card Fighter” Battle Royale, you will play various unique cards with tons of different effects in order to fight to be the last player standing.

Before you get to walloping your opponents, you’ve got to set up your board, and plan your first move!

Choose your character!

There are 6 different ones, each with a unique color.

Draw your arsenal!

Five Action Cards should fill your hand, each containing a possible action.

Know your foes !

You should have special Target Cards as well, one for each other player.

Lock in!

Pick an Action card and a Target card from your hand, and put them face down in front of you.

The action you picked will affect the target you chose!

Reaveal!

All your planning comes to a head! all players flip to reveal their hidden Action and Target cards, and resolve their effects!

Resolve.

Action cards resolve in a special Phase order, denoted by their symbol.

Types of Action Cards

Attack to deal damage, Block to prevent it, or Heal to restore it !

Equip a Treasure and a Potion to bolster your strength, or weaken your enemies!

    Discard cards from your hand to build up a strong one!
 Use Magic spells that have powerful effects!

Or, perform a sneaky Reveal action, that goes before everything else!

Reset!

After everything is resolved, everyone discards their played Action Card, and takes back their played Target Card. Be sure to draw back up to 5 Action Cards, and If there is more than one person left standing, do it all over again until there is only one player with any Health remaining, and they are crowned the Notebook Knockout Champion!

Something happened with the Core Micro Device! Your most essential equipment is about to stop working, but everything depends on its proper functioning. You cannot let this happen and must interfere urgently to repair it!

The maintenance of the Micro Device will take place in 3 phases and for this you will need:

     The Device Card
     3 Six-Sided Die
     Pencil and Eraser or Erasable Pen


Phase A: Circuitry Reconnection: Draw lines to reestablish the circuitry connections.
Phase B: Component Repair: Roll and distribute dice, and use power tools to repair damaged components.
Phase C: Access Code Restoration: Recover the access code and get your core micro device working again! Or not...

—description from the designer

Bigotes en Caos (Whiskers in Chaos) is a card game designed by 10 year old Montserrat Reyes Ch. 
Each player starts with one cat card in front of them, plus four hand cards. On a turn, you draw a card from a face-down deck, then play a card from your hand in front of you and execute its effect. Card effects include stealing cards from other players, exchanging cards on the table, looking at other players' cards, etc. 
The game ends when the draw pile is gone and a player runs out of cards. Each cat in front of players counts the points printed on it, sets of three of the same card or all seven different cards count extra points. The player with most points is the winner.

The frog Calyptocephallela Gayi, native to Chile, feeds on many creatures, including other smaller members of its own species. In this game, each player will control one color of Calyptocephallela Gayi frog. Your goal will be to eat other frogs and bugs. The player who keeps the most of his frogs alive and who has eaten the most bugs and frogs from the other players will win.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Chaos Dwarf Team – Zharr-Naggrund Ziggurats
description:
The Chaos Dwarfs approach Blood Bowl in the same way they tackle a new project in the workshop – plan meticulously for far too long, practise until they have achieved one successful test, before throwing a good chunk of that out of the window in favour of hammering away with as much ferocity as they can muster. This approach has served them incredibly well when it comes to action on the gridiron, where their physical dominance is hard to overlook.

This multipart plastic kit builds the Zharr-Naggrund Ziggurats, a Chaos Dwarf team for use in games of Blood Bowl.

Contains: 
6 Hobgoblin Linemen
4 Chaos Dwarf Blockers
2 Hobgoblin Sneaky Stabbas
2 Chaos Dwarf Flamesmiths

You'll find two head options for every body, plus more options to customize your team. In addition, there are two double-sided Blood Bowl coins, two turn and re-roll markers, four themed balls, and a transfer sheet.

Rules for using Chaos Dwarf teams in your games of Blood Bowl can be found in Spike! Journal: Issue 18, which is available separately.

—description from the publisher

Each player in Roll a Gain has their own player board, five dice, and tokens, and three mini-game boards are shared among all the players.

On a turn, you roll your dice, place a pair of them on your player board, then either stop or roll the remaining dice to get a higher result. As long as you keep getting higher numbers, you can move up your player board, which will give you points and symbols when you stop before busting. With the symbols, you cover spaces on the mini-games with your tokens, scoring points as you move or stack tokens.

You can acquire cardboard chits that are placed in notches in your game board that give you bonuses when you move past them with your dice.

Our idea
As passionate card players, we have taken the best of the TCG genre and integrated it into a new system. The influences range from Pokemon, Yugioh, Magic, WoW TCg, Hearthstone, Rune Terra to established board games like Risk.
We pay special attention to a fair card balance, we avoid mana explosions and OTK combinations and bring you a game with constant fun and unpredictable situations, from the first to the last round.
CreatureCrush DCG offers an easy introduction for beginners and pros will be surprised by the progression this game has to offer. 
Support our vision and experience a new way of playing.

Introduction
Creature Crush DCG is the first dice card game of its kind. Experience the unique combination of dice skill, card combinations and classic strategy elements.
Two opponents face each other in a duel. Both try to crush their opponent's creatures as quickly as possible. Each player has their own playing field, which can accommodate up to 6 creatures and up to 3 crush cards.
The battle must be carefully planned, because a victory that seemed certain can quickly slip away. Losing important key cards can have serious consequences.
Bold manoeuvres and great skill are needed to achieve the goal. But even the best tactics can be undone by bad luck with the dice.

Product details
Combination of board, card and dice game. Suitable for beginners and advanced players of the TCG genre.
CreatureCrush DCG 1st edition, limited to 1,380 copies.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Chaos Dwarf Team Card Pack
description:
From the Publisher:
Chaos Dwarf teams are some of the most formidable in the game, blessed by Hashut with stout and strong bodies, and filled with a burning contempt for all others.

Ready to build the bone crunching dream team of your rivals' nightmares? This deck of 44 cards helps you focus on your game and keep track of your stats and special rules. Chaos Dwarf team coaches can hire players in six positions available for hire, plus three Star Players, and these cards will ensure you have everything you need to hand during your games.

CONTENTS
- 6x Chaos Dwarf players reference cards for Hobgoblin Linemen, Hobgoblin Sneaky Stabbas, Chaos Dwarf Blockers, Chaos Dwarf Flamesmiths, Bull Centaur Blitzers, and Minotaurs.

- 3x Star Player cards for Rashnak Backstabber, H'thark the Unstoppable, and Zzharg Madeye

- 8x Dirty Tricks cards

- 8x Miscellaneous Mayhem cards

- 18x Blank Positional cards, to fill in for each player on your team: 7x Hobgoblin Lineman, 2x Hobgoblin Sneaky Stabba, 4x Chaos Dwarf Blocker, 2x Chaos Dwarf Flamesmith, 2x Bull Centaur Blitzer, 1x Minotaur

- One all-important card that explains how to use all of the above cards, with a red card on the reverse

—description from the publisher

To win Fifty Fruity, you want to have the most bananas in your collection, and to get them you might have to lie.

On a turn, the active player draws two cards from the deck, then shows them to another player, who either truthfully says what's on both cards (but not which is which) or lies about one card while saying the truth about the other. The active player then keeps one of the cards and gives the other to the player who spoke.

Some cards have only bananas on them, while others have green bananas, mangoes that ripen the green bananas, coconuts with special powers, bombs that blow up coconuts, a banana split, and more.

Can you tell what's disappeared in Magicaboo?

Each turn, one player takes the role of magician, spreading a large cloth over all the wooden pieces in the center of the play area, then picking up one item under the cloth and carting it away. "Magicaboo!"

The other players try to be the first to identify which item is missing, winning a star if they do.

A light combination of worker placement and tile laying game where you explore the island of Sumatra and take on the roles of Darwin and Wallace. Take action cubes to research, explore, observe, and publish your findings to gain victory points. Exploring allows you more options to observe more animals and plants.Publishing findings with the most diverse amount of tokens, types of animals, plants, and research; gains more victory points. After four rounds, see who wins!

boardgame: St. Vith: The Sixth Panzer Army Attack
description:
Published in 1978 as part of the SPI quadrigame Battles for the Ardennes and as a standalone softpack

St. Vith simulates the attack of the northern wing of the opening German attempt to break through to the River Meuse in the Battle of the Bulge.
family: History: World War II (Battle of the Bulge)

description:
This is the logical puzzle game to guess the opponent's 6 numbers by digital numbers.

During the game, you ask the question "How many segments(the part of digital number) in the row or column" ,"Which of the two numbers is the larger?" or "Is that number odd or even?" to guess the opponent's number.

This game is recommended for people who usually enjoy logic puzzles such as Sudoku and Wordle etc.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Clervaux: Breakout of the 5th Panzer Army
description:
Published in 1978 as part of the SPI quadrigame Battles for the Ardennes and as a standalone softpack.

Clervaux simulates the breakout of the southern flank of the German drive to the Meuse in December, 1944 (Battle of the Bulge). Germans must capture the important crossroads town of Bastogne.
family: History: World War II (Battle of the Bulge)

description:
Published in 1978 as part of the SPI quadrigame Battles for the Ardennes and as a standalone softpack.

Sedan, 1940 simulates the  German breakout to and across the River Meuse in the Battle for France, against French and Belgian opposition.

An Adventure Around Sydney is a roll and move game devised by A. A. Fryor and published by Lonsdale and Bartholomew (Aust) Ltd. The playing board shows a map of the main streets of Sydney.

Players move counters through the streets of Sydney by the roll of a dice. Players visit all the main attractions of Sydney and return safely. Hazards along the way can delay their journey. First to return home is the winner.

The game was advertised briefly in Sydney Newspapers in 1929 for 2 Shillings.

—user summary

Krakkakviss is a fun and educational trivia game perfect for any occasion. The Krakkakviss series has gained incredible popularity, selling tens of thousands of copies in Iceland.

Krakkakviss 4 includes 100 cards with 300 brand-new questions covering a wide range of topics.


     Which members of IceGuys are brothers?
     How many types of candy can you choose in a Bragðarefur?
     On which island is Siam Park located?
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description: When playing, you can play as your favorite character from the Polish series "Lombard. Life under pledge". The game is about earning as much money as possible. To do this, you need to make thoughtful and accurate decisions when buying, selling and bidding on goods. Goods may have hidden defects that determine their correct valuation. The game forces interactions between players. Randomness is provided by the movement track with events and customer cards. Participants decide how long the game lasts.

In Ham Fisted, you're a butcher on your way to the renowned Porkshire market when disaster strikes - your van breaks down! Now, you must carry as much of your precious produce to market as possible. Choose wisely and make every cut count!

Gameplay highlights

Setup: Shuffle and deal six cards to each player
Rounds: Play rounds "in hand," arranging cards to maximise your score within a 5x5 grid.
Scoring: Connect produce orthogonally, arranging your bacon in pairs, ribs in threes, and isolating legs of ham. All while attempting to display the most sausages.
Dexterity Challenge: By the end of the round, you must hold all your cards between one finger and thumb. You must be able to flip your hand without dropping any cards.
Beware! If a card ever falls from your hand, you can't pick it up. There’s no five-second rule here! Dropped food is lost food!
Endgame: Tally scores to determine who becomes the Master Butcher of Porkshire.

—description from the designer

77 brand new cards. Each card is unique.
The first edition is limited to 500 copies!

Playing the expansion without the basic game
To play New Babylon without the basic game, we recommend at least 8 dice in 2 different colours. The square playing field of 3x3 squares can easily be imagined. There are no special rules for the expansion. On this page, we would like to familiarise you with the rules of the game. If you have any specific questions, please use the contact form.

boardgame: 乱戦空域 (Fighter in Gun Sight)
description:
Players shoot down enemy aircraft using famous aircraft such as the Yak, Messerschmitt, Thunderbolt, Spitfire, and Zero fighters.

At different altitudes, speed becomes attack power; at the same altitude, maneuverability becomes attack power.

The attacking aircraft moves to the altitude where the target is located, and the aircraft with the greater attack power shoots down the aircraft with the lesser attack power.

The player who shoots down the most targets wins.

Checkmate the king of the jungle. The arrows on the animals indicate the possible moves. Special features: There are also arrows that only allow movement without capturing. Some animals, such as the monkey or the parrot, are very agile, while others are stronger at capturing. If you capture an opponent's animal, you can re-deploy it as your own. Includes an expansion.

—description from the publisher

Having condemned their homeworld, the Memges made the planet Zimi their last hope. But this refuge has rebelled. Their efforts to cultivate, build, and preserve life proved poisonous to the Umbra Morfes, unleashing a generation of mutant horrors created by the twisted fusion of the native Umbra with the colonists themselves and their chimeras. These monsters emerge from the Umbral Rifts, forcing you into a war of survival where there is no moral victory: you must destroy the planet's ancestral inhabitants to secure the future of your race.

 Umbra Morfes is a combination of role-playing game and wargame, with solo, cooperative, and arena modes where players can compete against each other, either in teams or one-on-one. Its unique terrain creation system, based on hexagonal tiles, allows players to design dynamic, customizable maps. Additionally, the game includes cards that grant new abilities to heroes, ensuring that each adventure is completely different.

Create your terrain map, choose a character, and place it on the space truck tile. You’ll start with basic abilities, but as you defeat creatures and gain experience, you’ll unlock new skills and improve your character's stats with better modifiers. Choose from six unique heroes, each fitting different playstyles, whether you prefer dealing heavy damage, strategic ranged attacks, healing, or tanking.

The mission of the Heroes is to seal the umbral Rifts, cracks that connect to Zimi’s inner layers, where the Umbra Morfes reside. Only by doing so can the Memges create an optimal ecosystem for life, ensuring water, air, and vegetation — elements that are deadly to the Umbra Morfes.

The game ends without victory if your life points are below 0. The game ends with victory if you complete all the objectives.

boardgame: The Breach: LMS 10th Anniversary XAN4TH.RAR Guardian alternative Description card
description:
The system learns, the system progresses and adapts to the continuous hacking attempt. Similarly, the Guardians mutate to become more and more effective in preventing the leakage of information vital to Achab Corp. 
You can use this Description Card by replacing the Guardian Description Card included in the core box.
Effect: This Card changes the way Guardians Activation Card are added to the Activation Card Deck. It also makes it possible for Avatars to defeat XAN4TH.RAR. The Guardian can only be eliminated when it is assigned to the third Virus.

—description from the publisher

This promo card is part of the Ashes Magazine #10 celebrating Ludus Magnus Studios 10 years anniversary

boardgame: Trial of the Gods: LMS 10th Anniversary Three new Travel Cards Promo
description:
The Aegean Sea always holds new perils and surprises for the brave Argonauts who intend to face its waves. These three new Travel Cards can be added to the Travel Card deck in Step 9 of the Table Setup (see page 6 of the Core Box Rules)

—description from the publisher

This promo cards are part of the Ashes Magazine #10 celebrating Ludus Magnus Studios 10 years anniversary

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: LMS 10th Anniversary three Event cards Promo
description:
In appreciation of the immense love that players are showing for Black Rose Wars: Rebirth, and on the occasion of the announcement of the second edition of the core box of the first chapter of Black Rose Wars, we thought it would be a good idea to get you into the Lodge's atmosphere right away which 3 exclusive Events! These events will introduce you (or remind you for veterans) how ruthless and devious the Black Rose can be. 
Each Card belongs to a different Moon (first, second and third) and can be added to their respective decks during the preparation phase.

—description from the publisher

This promo cards are part of the Ashes Magazine #10 celebrating Ludus Magnus Studios 10 years anniversary

Discover the story that gave rise to the zombie apocalypse in the Zurvivors saga!

Prepare to embark on a journey to another world.

Zurvivors Origem ("Zurvivors Origin") is a card game in which players take on the role of brave astronauts on a challenging mission: to collect samples of rare specimens called Alieninjas and safely store them on the imposing S.O.L.A.R. ship. To make this happen, players must simultaneously play item cards and use them to perform actions on their turn: walk along the Trail, collect samples, and Heal Damage. You can also use Oxygen to double any of the actions. But be very careful! A deadly creature is lurking... protecting its offspring and pursuing all players throughout the game. The player who plays the card with the highest number will control the Creature and can deal damage to their opponents. Meanwhile, the player who plays the card with the lowest number will receive a special item card that will serve as extra help! Be careful not to take too much damage and face penalties. Additionally, each Astronaut has a unique ability that can be activated once during the game, making the mission even more exciting! Collect samples of Alieninjas shown on your Objective Card to earn more victory points at the end of the game. But remember to store all samples on the S.O.L.A.R. ship; otherwise, the mission will have been in vain. Collect. Survive. And escape in Zurvivors Origem.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Haunted Lands: LMS 10th Anniversary Promo Assignment Cards
description:
The city of Kalena is in vigorous expansion. Every day there's a new task awaiting perceivers; this will bring glory and fame to the perceiver who completes t, but more importantly, prosperity and well-being to the city and its inhabitants!
In Step 10 of the Setup (page 10 of the Rulebook), assign each player the Promo Assignment Card of their color in addition of those available.

Effect: Gain 4 Value for each Region in which there is at least one of your Windmills, one of your Lumberyards, one of your Mines, and one your Norias.

—description from the publisher

This promo cards are part of the Ashes Magazine #10 celebrating Ludus Magnus Studios 10 years anniversary

boardgame: D.E.I: Divide et Impera – LMS 10th Anniversary Black Market Action Cards Promo
description:
The Black Market is a place to constantly watch out for - you never know when a new resource will go on sale. Will it be an old boot or a drone activation code that will make your day?
These new Action cards can be added to the Black Market Action Cards, following Step 8 of the Setup (on page 9 of the core box rulebook)

—description from the publisher

This three promo cards are part of the Ashes Magazine #10 celebrating Ludus Magnus Studios 10 years anniversary

Get ready for a hopping good time with KinderBunnies, the zany card game where players race to collect the most Bunnies and Carrots!

Roll your way through the chaos with five quirky dice while juggling Radish and Milk to keep your bunnies fed and dodge those wacky Safety Hazards that could turn your fun upside down.

Packed with vibrant animal characters and uproarious activities, KinderBunnies guarantees a “hare-larious” adventure that will leave everyone giggling with delight!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chamber of Wonders: LMS 10th Anniversary Grandma's Ashes Promo Card
description:
When they say that help comes in many different forms, even from your dear grandmother. Grandma's Ashes is a "Wild" Wonder Card that, therefore, doesn't belong to any of the game's existing sets. The Wonder Card must be shuffled into the Wonder deck and follows its normal rules for being drawn and played
Effect: The player who displays Grandma's Ashes can use the power of one of the Contracts left in the box (thus not present in the game), other players can do the same by paying 1 coin to the player who displays Grandma's Ashes.

boardgame: Sherwood Bandits: LMS 10th Anniversary Red Cross Wagon Promo Card
description:
You can never be sure what kind of wagon is roaming around Sherwood Forest; some of them absolutely must not be ambushed, or you will make a fool of yourself in the eyes of Robin Hood and the other Merry men. The Red Cross Wagon is a new Wagon Card for Sherwood Bandit, which is to be added to the Wagon Deck and follows the normal rules, up to the Loot point of the Ambush Phase

Effect: If the Wagon has been defeated, players determine the Victory Points and Loot obtained from the Assault differently from the normal rules.

Victory Points: Each player who still has one of their Dice on the Assault Slot loses 1 Victory Point. If this Die is the rightmost one among the ones present, the player loses 1 additional Victory Point. In addition, if the Bandit paired with the rightmost Die is lying on the ground (due to Little John's effect) the player loses an additional Victory Point (for a total of 3).

Loot: Starting with the owner of the rightmost Die, all players who participated in the Assault open their Chest and can look at all the Loot Tokens in their possession. Next, they choose a Token and put it back into the common reserve. Finally, the Loot Token are placed back inside the Cest.

—description from the publisher

This promo card is part of the Ashes Magazine #10 celebrating Ludus Magnus Studios 10 years anniversary

boardgame: Nova Aetas Renaissance: LMS 10th Anniversary Nero Promo Card
description:
From today you can use the massive power or the Magister of Destruction by shelling our several Florins you will be satisfied but never refunded.
Nero is a new Companion for Nova Aetas Renaissance. He follows the normal Companion rules for being hired and will assist you during each Mission.
-Description from the publisher

This promo card is part of the Ashes Magazine #10 celebrating Ludus Magnus Studios 10 years anniversary

The game board is laid out so that the first row of squares shows a light square on the right. Each player has 16 pieces (towers) of the same shape and color, which they place on the first and second row of squares on the game board. A metal ring is placed on each tower.

The movement of the towers takes place diagonally, either by a simple move into a neighboring square or by a jump. In a jump move, you either jump over your own or your opponent's towers. If the jump can be continued according to the rules, a chain move is created.

By jumping over the enemy tower, it is considered to be captured and is removed from the game. The capturing tower takes over its rings.

If a tower reaches the opposite eighth row, it becomes a double tower. It is marked as such by a red ring. If a tower captures a double tower, it becomes a double tower itself by taking over the red ring. The double tower can be moved to any square in its diagonal row. However, if it jumps over one of its own pieces on this row, it must always move to the square next to it in this row.

The player's task is to conquer the rings of the opposing towers by attacking them appropriately. The game is over when a player only has one tower left that cannot capture on the next move. The winner is the player with the greatest number of metal rings.

—user summary

In an Old West teeming with monsters, only the fearless, the sharp-witted, and the clever will make it out alive. Recruit your band of outlaws, gunslingers, lawmakers and more to fend off terrifying creatures-and each other-in a gripping battle for survival.

Wrangle a winning poker hand with unique abilities and secret cards in your holster. Attack your enemies and defend your cards to come out on top when all hands are revealed.

boardgame: Spire's End: Hildegard – The Pumpkin Patch
description:
The Pumpkin Patch is a small pack of 21 cards that adds delightful variety to Chapter 1 of Hildegard. Encounter a host of exciting and comical new characters who confront Hildegard with a tempting proposition, possibly leading her into some unexpected trouble! With flying corn and jack-o'-lanterns, this expansion captures the festive spirit of Halloween. Featuring vibrant illustrations by Diego Frias, Charlie Potter, and Audrey Rouvin, get ready to embrace the charm and mischief of the season!

—description from the designer

In Elenco your goal is simple: to become the star of the theater by winning the audience's favor and receiving their ovation. 
However, it won't be so easy, because the competition in this world can be ruthless... so don't hesitate to use any means at your disposal to achieve it!

Elenco is a trick-taking game, agile and suitable for all audiences. It is relatively simple: you won't have to spend hours reading a manual to learn how to play. The rules are easy and you learn to play quickly, but it also has a strategy component that you will be able to master to have more chances to win, discovering which cards are better in combination with each other or according to the order of the turn.

In Elenco you will be part of a fictitious theater play, and you will have to try to get the highest score in each “show” to get the audience's ovation. To do this, the first thing to do is to try to choose the most appropriate role in the play.

There are many options, from being a simple extra to the main character! In the casting of roles everyone will see the options available for each one, but the final choice is secret, so you have to predict what the others will do and choose accordingly and carefully: choosing a good starting role has its advantages, but you can also trigger the envy of the others who will do everything possible to take it from you....

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Two Promo Cards from GameBoy Geek Season 12 Kickstarter

Musical Instrument

Whenever you draw an Entertaining, draw again.
