Colorful board game for you and the whole family! May the funniest one win!

BEAUTIFUL COLORFUL GAME FOR EVERYONE - Whether you're playing with friends or family - with this creative party fun it won't be boring!

IN 5 CREATIVE CATEGORIES FOR LAUGHING - Funny Mime, Name Three, Curious Explanations, Fun Duels and City Country Oink! - may the funnier win!​​

A LITTLE MORE SERIOUS OFFENSE IS GOOD FOR US! - Whoever laughs first loses! Simple basic rules, quickly explained and really fun! Well, do you feel like it?

CONTENTS IN THE FUNNIEST GAME OF THE YEAR - 300 playing cards, 1 pair of piggy glasses, a game board, 8 game pieces and much, much more.

—description from the publisher

Buntes Brettspiel für Dich und die ganze Familie! Möge der Lustigere gewinnen!

WUNDERSCHÖNES BUNTES SPIEL FÜR ALLE - Egal ob beim Spielen mit Freunden oder mit der Familie - Mit diesem kreativen Partyspaß wird es sicher nicht langweilig!

IN 5 KREATIVEN KATEGORIEN ZUM LACHKRAMPF - Lustige Pantomime, Nenne drei, Kurioses Erklären, Spaßige Duelle und Stadt Land Oink! - möge der Witzigere gewinnen!​​

EIN BISSCHEN MEHR ERNST TÄTERÄTÄTE UNS GUT! - Wer zuerst lacht, verliert! Einfach Grundregeln, schnell erklärt und saulustig! Na, hast du Lust?

INHALT IM LUSTIGSTEN SPIEL DES JAHRES - 300 Spielkarten, 1ne Schweinchenbrille, ein Spielbrett, 8 Spielfiguren und vieles vieles mehr.

—description from the publisher (German)

Get ready to step into the shoes of a wedding owner extraordinaire as you embark on a whimsical journey through the joyous world of tying the knot. In this enchanting board game, you'll table for delightful challenges that capture the magic of a wedding day: Seating, Ceremony, Reception, and finally Cake!

Hedged In is a two player pen-and-paper game where you'll be making hedge mazes and casting spells. Your goal is to ruin your shared garden for your rival, while still keeping it nice for yourself. The game itself is a small book of many different maps that you can play through as a campaign, or just pick what looks fun. As you progress, twists and turns get added to the game, and at the end of the book, you'll be able to see who ultimately comes out on top and who gets hedged in!

—description from the designer

Step up your game nights with Bet The Line!

Designed for ages 14 and up, this engaging card game is perfect for groups of 2 to 8 players. Test your knowledge and intuition with 200 trivia questions across multiple categories, ensuring a diverse and challenging experience for all players. Each card features a unique question, and the answer is cleverly hidden on the back. Uncover the answer with our Red Reveal Decoder magnifying glass, adding an extra layer of fun and intrigue to the game! Keep track of your score on the included erasable Scoreboard, making it easy to tally points and declare the ultimate trivia champion! For an added challenge and a chance to score extra points, players who guess the exact answer earn bonus points, adding a strategic twist to the game. Whether you're hosting a party, gathering with friends, or looking for a new family favorite, Bet The Line! promises endless entertainment and friendly competition. Get ready to debate, laugh, and learn something new with every turn!

Features: 

     Suitable for ages 14 and up
     Great for 2 to 8 players
     Includes 200 trivia Question Cards
     Covers multiple categories for a varied experience
     Comes with a Red Reveal Decoder magnifying glass
     Erasable Scoreboard and Dry Erase Marker included for easy scorekeeping
     Bonus points for exact answers
     Perfect for parties, family game nights, and gatherings with


—description from the publisher

Welcome to the world of BloodFeud, a tabletop miniatures game where you can lead your army to victory in competitive and solo play. Choose a mighty host from history, create your warlord avatar and you can personally engage in mortal combat. Vikings vs Samurai, Mongols vs Egyptians, Romans vs Crusaders - it’s all possible in the world of BloodFeud. With revolutionary rules, tools and training, you can recreate battles from history, or feud it out in incredible ‘what if’ match-ups.

1 Battle - 1000 Outcomes
BloodFeud makes use of innovative turn-based effects through random card draw. The tension and excitement builds as each warlord discovers how the battle space has changed and evolved as the battle progresses. From simple weather changes, to discovery of equipment or even being joined by fresh troops or being set upon by assassins, the outcome of each battle is never truly known until the last sword is drawn.

Historical but Exciting
BloodFeud has its roots in history and all troop types, equipment and heroes come from historical reference. BloodFeud seeks to be different however to the mainstay of historical wargames in that it allows the player to leave their mark and make the game their own. Roman Gladiators can battle it out with Saracen Marmluks. Samurai can lead a last charge against Hannibals war elefants. The strictures of historical wargaming are lifted, and BloodFeud allows the players imagination to drive the game forward.

Unforgiving Fast Paced Combat
Dice rolling is the bane of modern wargaming. Buckets of dice and counters reign as rolls to hit and wound are coupled with armor and invulnerability saves. What this means is that half the game is spent counting dice when it could be spent playing out a grand strategy or walking your opponent into a cunning trap. Combat in BloodFeud is by single die rolling for each attack. Rolling to hit, wound and save is built into one single roll on a D10. Beyond the metrics of mathematic battle, BloodFeud introduces the player to battle outcome effects. Battle lines can be pushed back, morale can waiver or formations can even be broken into disarray, to be ridden down as they flee. Death on the battlefield has never been closer than in BloodFeud. Models and heroes particularly, including the player as the warlord, do not have a buffer of wounds to make up for hazardous strategy. An arrow that pierces armor is just as deadly to a footman as it is to a king. If you lead from the front, be prepared to pay the iron price.

Artificial Intelligence for Solo and Coop Play
The Battle Buddy AI system is the ever ready gaming companion that the player has never had. Let’s be truthful, in a modern world where life can be all too busy, it can be difficult to connect with other players for an afternoon and play a tabletop game. Even with BloodFeuds advanced mechanics allowing for a game to be played in as little as 45 minutes, sometimes, time is the chief enemy. Battle Buddy AI brings a new level of solo or cooperative play to the table. While not truly ‘artificial intelligence’, Battle Buddy does bring a scalable opponent, of variable difficulty and aggression to fight against. Turn by turn the Battle Buddy’s army will manoeuvre and engage your forces in a coordinated strategy. Battle Buddy will seek to take objectives, launch spearhead attacks and defend against your thrusts. Not only is Battle Buddy a fantastic training tool and genuine gaming opponent, the Battle Buddy can also be your loyal ally in multiple army games with both Human and Battle Buddy armies or in cooperative play against a common foe.

Custom Miniatures
BloodFeud’s proprietary miniatures range is crafted to allow for maximum customisation by the player. Joints are designed around balls and sockets, so torso’s, arm’s and head’s can be posed in an almost unlimited number of ways. Stepping away from the industry wide mono-pose models, BloodFeud offers you the flexibility to use your imagination. Have you ever wanted to run battle axe wielding Ninja’s or bare-chested Viking Beserkir’s on horseback? With the BloodFeud WarChest App, you can order the parts you want for your miniatures and your army, and build them up the way you want.

Its all about YOU - In BloodFeud, you are not playing with someone elses models, armies and heroes. In BloodFeud, YOU lead YOUR army on the battlefield by creating an avatar of yourself to be your warlord and equipping yourself with weapons and armor as you choose.

Battle Buddy AI - BloodFeud offers the player a revolutionary system for solo and cooperative. While not truly AI, the Battle Buddy AI system allows players to play against an enemy, or with an ally, that takes actions based on objectives and opposing forces, in a coordinated army wide strategy. With scaleable difficulty, the Battle Buddy AI can be a fantastic sparring partner or a deadly foe - underestimate it at your peril.

Before the Battle Cards - This feature focusses on the factors that lead up to battle that can determine the outcome. The ‘Before the Battle’ card draw adds the first element of BloodFeuds ‘1 battle - 1000 outcomes’ principle of variation and replayability. Preparation is key to performance!

Battle Rages On Cards - This feature again focusses on the random and unforseen events that occur in the heat of battle and introduce the ‘instability’ of combat that can make a victory, or break it. This feature is the second  principle of ‘1 battle - 1000 outcomes’ and can provide the player an opportunity to strike back. No plan ever survives first contact with the enemy!

Unit Activation - In a step away from ‘I Go/You Go’ gameplay, the warlords, characters and units on both sides activate relative to their own statistics, meaning faster and lighter units can take actions before heavier and slower units. This gives both players an equal opportunity to enact their strategies and outmaneuver their enemies. Unit activation includes movement, ranged combat, close combat, issuing of orders in the case of characters and warlords, and other special actions relevant to specific units and characters. A warlord who knows his own army, and his enemies, will not fear the result of a hundred battles!

Adaptive Terrain - The concept of a ‘hill’ just being a ‘hill’ is replaced with a system of variability, so that the terrain within a battle can be explored and its true strategic value exploited. This feature focuses on a card draw that can enhance or diminish the strategic value of battlefield terrain and can even provide the player with additional units and equipment and adds an element of the unknown.

Innovative Combat - Close combat in BloodFeud is simultaneous, fast and seamless. Players can no longer rush in and attack knowing that the counter-attack will be weak. Combat is simultaneous (just like real life) and the force that has superior combat power will prevail. Ranged combat in BloodFeud also aims for realism by requiring players to guess the range to target, and then adjust their guess based on the skill of the firing models. This means that the first shots may go wide, but again, just like real life, once the player has their eye dialled in the firepower can be delivered with great accuracy, or an early lucky volley could turn the tide.

Combat Outcome Effects - The art of killing on the BloodFeud battlefield isn’t restricted to hit points, wounds and saves. Every time an attack is completed the full effect of the casualties inflicted can cause a battle line to waiver, get pushed back or even break, resulting in further disadvantage for the losing side. This emphasises the power of ‘breaking the line’ to achieve victory, as opposed to just killing enemies.

Battle Orders - The warlord and their lieutenants can have a powerful effect on combat performance. The right order at the right time can increase the combat power of a unit, or cause the enemy to waiver. This feature emphasises leading from the front, but remember, your warlord, just like you, is only human!

The Role of Religion - Divination deals with the concept of religion, conviction and belief, as a critical component of the psychology of battle. Omens, portents and hexes reign as the priests, monks and crones call for divine intervention to strengthen their allies and disrupt their enemies. While not specifically ‘magic’ this phase allows players to engage the cultural and religious practices of factions to provide support during desperate times. Many strategies have been developed and many battles have been won by the strength of ‘consulting the bones’ or through the incantation of prayer before battle - religious belief can cut as deeply as a blade or shield a bearer from one!

D10 Die Rolling - The 10 sided die or D10 offers the player more opportunity for variability of success and rolling. Further, a D10 system offers greater variation in the types and statistics of units, and this in turn provides a more realistic and rounded game mechanic.

Fast Play - The rolling mechanics in BloodFeud are designed to enhance speed of play. Attack rolls, armor saves and combat effects are all built into one roll. In a world where everyone is busy and time is precious, BloodFeud can provide a full and fulfilling battle experience in as little as 45 minutes for a 250 point battle, and a little over an hour for a major engagement of 500 points.

Custom Miniatures - The miniatures in BloodFeud are designed to be reposeable and be parts interchangeable. At this end of the day there’s not much fun playing with someone elses models. BloodFeud offers you to make your army your own, and play the way you want to play. The core game set contains injection moulded miniatures and follow on miniatures will be 3D printed.

Whats In The Box:

- 32 page Rulebook
- 40 page Samurai versus Vikings Battle Book 
- 63 miniatures (27 Samurai/36 Vikings - pictured) 
- Two ten sided dice
- Ruler
- 60 Before the Battle cards
- 63 Battle Rages On cards
- 50 Battle Buddy AI cards
- 150 Adaptive Terrain cards

boardgame: インサイドアウト (Inside Out)
description:
Inside Out is a quick and simple word game where parts of the words you have to read go missing depending on the dice result.

On a turn, the active player will draw a word card. Once all the players around the table have checked the word for the turn, the active player rolls the die.

Depending on the die result, the way player will have to read the word changes.

The faster among players to read the word correctly gains the card. Once a player has gained a certain number of word cards, that player wins the game.

Inside Out is the 5th game from the doujin group Lana & Papa

The land has fallen into chaos, overrun by savage monsters, dark magic, and the looming threat of six Demon Lords. Only you, a lone adventurer, can traverse the perilous Badlands, gather vital clues, and confront those ancient evils. Armed with just your wits, courage, and a blade, you’ll explore a vast hex map filled with deadly foes, mysterious ruins, and encounters with enigmatic NPCs. Collect Gold, Experience, Luck, and powerful items as you prepare for the ultimate showdown. Demonslayer is a retro-fueled, sword and sorcery hex-map game for a single player. All you need is the rulebook, the map, and two six-sided dice.

Will you be the one to slay the demons, or will you fall victim to the shadows?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rattenkrieg 2: Battle for the Barrikady Factory
description:
Solitaire postcard game of the German attack on the Red Barricades Barrikady Factory in Stalingrad

Postcard map
20 counters

boardgame: Age of Automaton: Promo Specialist Cards
description:
Set of 5 new specialists whom were given to everybody who pre-ordered or bought the game at Spiel Essen 2024.

Their names are: Tatariga, Umi, Vatcharis, Walker & Xenos.

Promo 4 for The White Castle.
To be played with the Matcha expansion.

3 dice tiles providing the new chasen resource.

A small expansion for Formosa Tea released as part of the 2nd Edition Kickstarter. Introduces tea tree cultivation and adds domestic market tiles.

Robert Swinhoe
Guo Chun Yang
Chen Tian Lai
1937: Nitta Black Tea Sent to The London Tea Auction

boardgame: Dragon's Gold: Goblins Party and Magic Item Cards – The Cake and All-Mighty Glove
description:
A new party and two magic item cards included with the fourth edition Kickstarter by Samaruc Games.

Goblins Party: Adds an alternate party for players to choose from.

The Cake: Allows a player to return an opposing character to its controller's play area.

The All-Mighty Glove: Allows a wizard to return an opposing character to its controller's play area and immediately defeat a dragon.

International Rescue Thunderbirds game see players race on a gameboard with graphics of all the Thunderbird characters from the 1960s TV program.  Movement is by the roll of a dice from 1 to 100 squares.  As the advance, certain squares aid or hamper their progress.

The most interesting part of this game is the playing pieces. Fantastic futuristic helmeted spaceman that look very similar to the 1950s Ajax/ Archer Spacemen toys, manufactured by DS Plastics. Players take off the helmet to begin play and acquire a helmet by landing on certain squares. They need the helmet by square 50 to continue advancing. The first to reach square 100 and the evil Hood is the winner.

The original box insert has a cut out model of the Thunderbird 1 rocket.

—user summary

Biodio is a card board game for 1 to 6 players, the goal of which is to build the most diverse ecosystem possible. Players explore a complex web of relationships between animals and the environment to create a harmonious whole. Despite the complexity of natural relationships, Biodio is a simple game with an intuitive progression. Each player assembles different environments to create their own ecosystem, which gradually populates with animals, from tiny ants to the majestic golden eagle. The player with the most diverse and valuable ecosystem at the end of the game wins. Players naturally learn about the relationships between different animals and their connection with the environment and how everything in nature is interconnected.

Copper Creek is a game about building an Old West town. Players will construct the town and invest in the businesses, earning money and scoring points. It’s a simple and unusual game with plenty of luck and strategy, and just a little politics.

The deck is 24 tile cards with one or two buildings on each. Buildings earn money based on their open doors, and they also create income for their neighbors based on the doorways they share.

On each turn, the active player adds one new card to the town, and then decides where to buy a share in any building. If the active player makes a buy, so can everyone else, and this repeats until the active player decides not to buy anything.

Once in a while there’s a scoring round, when buildings earn points for their owners, and after the third scoring round, the player with the most points wins!

What has been invented first - the tea bag or the zipper?
This is a fun game where every player guesses which of two events had happened first.
You can end the game at any point in time to secure your victory points,

The goal of the game is to build a cave consisting of 6 cards. All cards are shuffled and placed face down on the table. Then the children are playing in clockwise direction: Every child takes two cards and flips them. If both cards show the same symbol, the child takes them and starts to build his/her cave. The child who finishs the cave first wins.

The players place one card each turn and try to take part in the next animal musical quartet and score points. The quartet includes 4 of the same animals or 4 animals of one colour in a row. The most points however go to the well-known noise-maker quartet for Bremen. 
The winner is the one who cleverly combines the animals and scores the most points.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Town Musicians of Bremen

boardgame: Armata Strigoi: Killers with The Cross Special Deck
description: 10 special cards not included in the game Armata Strigoi.

The Bestiary is a unique ciphering and drawing game where players don’t just decode an ancient alphabet — they invent it. Don't be fooled by the look of it: this is the "final boss" of cipher-games.

				
				
					Overviewin each game 2–7 Assistants draw inspiration from a grid of 200 absurd Beasts. Within this Bestiary, each Assistant secretly selects 3 to 12 Beasts(depending on the chosen difficulty); their coordinates in the grid form the hidden message they must convey. By blending features of these creatures, players create a cryptic alphabet designed to guide another player, the Curator, toward the correct coordinates — all while concealing their meaning from rival Assistants.

Players score points when the Curator successfully deciphers their code without their rivals uncovering it first.

				
				
					What Makes It UniqueBecause every cipher is hand-drawn rather than composed of fixed symbols as in other games of decryption, The Bestiary captures the true essence of deciphering a lost writing system. Just as the letter “A” was once an abstract representation of an ox’s head, these puzzles invite players to uncover meaning hidden within seemingly random elements that tehy chose from the real world (no animals but Beasts in this case). Beyond deciphering, players also become creators, inventing a language accessible only to a select few, making every game feel like a discovery of a hidden, arcane system of communication.

All 55 cards are shuffled and put face down on the table. The youngest player starts and roles the dice. If it's a 6, he can choose a number.
Another player takes the first card and reads the question with the corresponding number. If the player knows the answer, he gets the card. Ohterwise, the card is paced below the stack.
The player who manages to get 5 cards first wins!

boardgame: Sandy Diggs in the Pyramids of Madness
description:
You and your opponents are archaeologists, hunting for lost treasures of the ancient world. On each turn you may visit either the market, where you can buy maps and sell artifacts, or the desert, where you can use your maps to find artifacts.

Map cards are simple puzzle patterns that you can find in the “shifting sands,” a 5x5 grid of black and white tokens. When you find an artifact it flips over the tokens in the pattern, and this can lead to more discoveries for you and the other players!

Note: This game is still in progress and is still being developed by the creator, so most images in the gallery have a preliminary and not final status.  More information about the status of this game can be found at the designer's page: https://crabfragmentlabs.com/tomb-of-the-ancients

Meet three little hedgehogs which instead of cleaning their spines before bed, keep running away from mum to play tag!
This is a jump and run game for little children with 2 different variants for children of 3 resp. 4 years. The wooden material is very cute and introduces the children to jump and run mechanics. Cooperative game, the players win when all three hedgehogs hop into the fort. They loose when the mum hedgehog gets inside before.

PlotWeaver: Cards of Creation is a storytelling game for individuals or groups at any skill level to create new stories with every play.

PlotWeaver is a multi-player solitaire game, where all the players are guided by the same Plot Pattern, such as the Monomyth with five Acts or Frame Tale with three Acts. You progressively go through the Plot Pattern and weave in new elements often not knowing how your story may end. For each Act, you randomly draw your own Story Element cards such as characters, settings, conflicts, themes, and twists, and weave them together to drive the story forward in creative and often surprising directions. At the end of each act, you share your stories, then move on to the next Act.

PlotWeaver can be played solo, collaboratively, or competitively, and its expansion packs, such as Genres, allow for even more narrative diversity, with settings ranging from epic Fantasy realms to thrilling Crime Noir worlds. Whether for seasoned storytellers or newcomers to narrative-driven games, PlotWeaver offers a fresh and rewarding experience every time.

When played with others, the game concludes with Moments of Reflection, allowing you to reflect on the overall journey and celebrate standout moments in each other’s stories. This focus on creativity and reflective feedback replaces traditional score-keeping with a shared appreciation for storytelling.

—description form the publisher

In Half Byte, players take on the roles of ambitious programmers competing to become the top coder in a tech-savvy world. The game draws players into a competitive environment where they must strategically manipulate binary code using specialized cards to complete unique programming tasks. Every player is given a mix of cards, including Task Cards and Command Cards, which represent the tools and challenges programmers encounter in their quest for coding glory.

The gameplay centers around each player’s ability to match binary sequences to complete objectives. Task Cards represent coding challenges, each with a unique binary “number” that players must recreate to earn points. Using Command Cards, players can manipulate Bit Cards to shift or flip binary values to reach their target code. Success requires a combination of strategy, foresight, and adaptability, as each decision impacts not only a player’s progress but also the chances of opponents achieving their goals.

To win, players must complete either their Secret Mission (a private task) or Common Objectives (shared goals visible to all). The race to accomplish these tasks creates an engaging mix of strategy and competition, as each coder works to stay one step ahead. With every move, Half Byte challenges players to think like a programmer, making tactical choices that bring them closer to the title of ultimate coder.

—description from the designer

L'Assedio! 1312 Firenze is a scenario in the strategy game Calendimaggio (the struggles between the Guelphs and Ghibellines around 1300) with its own separate rules.

The game map represents a reconstruction of Florence around 1300 and is divided into areas. Within the city, the division follows the "Sesti" (six Florentine districts), while outside the city are the approach areas to the walls.

The game tokens symbolize the forces historically present at the time and generally bear the emblem of the Lordship or free Commune to which they belong. The emblems can also represent noble families and military commanders. The game can be played by 2 players.
family: History: Wars of the Guelphs and Ghibellines

description:
Abyss is a strategy game for 2 to 4 players where players become leaders of powerful factions, each trying to bring glory and freedom to their people. Players gain resources to build advanced machines, which help them find even more valuable minerals. On the main board, players discover Random Fields, where they can get Random Cards that offer special benefits, allowing for creative strategies and surprising moves. These fields also give players the chance to buy a teleport to another Random Field, opening up new tactical choices.
When it’s time for combat, players first buy combat cards at the Barracks to strengthen their forces, and then move to a second board called the Combat Board, where exciting battles take place. Players fight monsters to gain rare resources, and besides monster battles, they can also challenge each other, testing their tactical skills.
The main goal of the game is to defeat all players or Abyss, a tyrant who rules a mythical realm. Only the bravest leaders can face him and free their people from his control.

—description from the publisher

The musical battle to play everywhere!

Sing more excerpts than your opponents, in French or English, with required words!

How to play?


     Form 2 teams and draw a card.
     Find and sing (even a little out of tune!) extracts of songs with an imposed word.
     Find more extracts than the opposing team to win the duel!

When the fires burn, it becomes dark ...

The orcs have always sought to overcome the borders of the realm and devastate the land. That's why the humans have erected a chain of watchtowers: the Dark Watch! Take on the role of the brave men who oppose the orcs' raids and embark on a dangerous journey to light the signal fires of the Dark Watch and earn a place at the table of Rondra, the goddess of war, as true heroes!

Adventures in a Box is an exciting new game series that lets players explore diverse fantasy worlds through easy-to-learn rules. Adventure in a Box: Finsterwacht, the first game in the series, takes place in the universe of Das Schwarze Auge. In this game, a Game Master (GM) guides players through a mix of storytelling and combat encounters. During narrative rounds, players make decisions that shape the storyline and affect future outcomes. Combat rounds are played on a board, where players face enemies represented by tokens and work together to achieve specific objectives, similar to classic dungeon crawlers. The entire game spans four sessions, each lasting 2–4 hours, for a total playtime of 10 to 20 hours. The game is won after succeeding the final fight, or may be lost anytime when all players are defeated.

Unnützes Quizzen: Fußball ("Unuseful Knowledge: Soccer") is a German trivia game with obscure facts about soccer.

—user summary

Test your unuseful soccer knowledge! Who has scored the most own goals in Bundesliga history? How many penalties did Gerd Müller miss? And how long does the winter break in the Ukrainian league actually last? The big questions in soccer - in this quiz from the popular section of the NEON magazine, they will all be answered. For at home, on the go, for in between: 99 quizzes to always have with you.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Teste dein unnützes Fußball-Wissen! Wer schoss die meisten Eigentore der Bundesligageschichte? Wie viele Elfmeter versemmelte Gerd Müller? Und wie lange dauert eigentlich die Winterpause in der ukrainischen Liga?Die großen Fragen des Fußballs – in diesem Quiz zur beliebten Rubrik aus dem NEON-Magazin werden sie alle beantwortet! Für zu Hause, für unterwegs, für zwischendurch: 99 Quiz-Fragen zum Immerdabeihaben.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rock Paper Scissors: Logic. Memory. Luck.
description:
This card game consists of 11 suits. Each suit has only three cards: a rock, paper, and scissors. What’s the twist? 
The suit is also visible on the back of the card, so your opponent always has insight into your hand.
You’ll take turns attacking and defending, gaining more information as the game goes on, as long as you can remember what you’ve seen. Once you lose 5 battles, you are eliminated. 
This card version pays tribute to its enduring legacy of the hand game, with suits designed to represent a wide breadth and depth of geographies, epochs, cultures, and movements.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secret Santa: A Family Token Bluffing Game
description:
Secret Santa is a family token bluffing game. The tokens are reminiscent of POGS. Each token has the same back: wrapping paper. The other side is one of the 78 gifts from the 12 days of Christmas: 12 drummers, 1 partridge, and everything in between.

On your turn you will either take an unopened present from the middle, or steal one of your opponents' three presents (think White Elephant meets 3 Card Monty). Your goal is to end up with the rarest token once all gifts have been opened. So if you happen to get your hands on the Partridge remember to keep your cool, or others will try to steal it from you.

—description from the publisher

This is an ice cream trick-taking game.
Players serve the trendiest ice cream in town and build up their store's reputation.
However, if you fail to serve trendy ice cream, your customers will think you are a lame store and your reputation will be ruined.
Be careful not to offer a lot of trendy ice cream and not to take the dassa point!

In Quack Heads, players are dealt 7 cards, and the objective is to be the first to discard all cards. Players take turns placing a card face down on the discard pile, claiming it beats the previous card based on Rock-Paper-Scissors rules. If a player doesn’t have the right card, they can bluff, but opponents can contest by saying “QUACK.” If the card is revealed as truthful, the contester draws 1 card; if it’s a bluff, the bluffer draws 2 cards.

Special cards add unique actions like challenges, drawing penalties, and wild advantages. Players must strategically decide when to bluff and when to play truthfully, using these elements to gain an edge over opponents.

How to Win
Be the first to discard all cards in your hand while outsmarting opponents through a mix of clever card play and well-timed bluffs.

boardgame: Sagrada: Promo 25 – Game Boy Geek 2024 Window Pattern Card

Math focused push-your-luck style dice rolling game. Familiarizes kids with coin recognition, values and basic addition. Be the first to tally $1.51 or more to win.

Each player uses two special numbered dice. Roll both dice and if they're different numbers add the dice up and place coins equal to what you rolled in the "risk it" box. Decide to push your luck or stop and "bank" your coins. You must keep rolling until you make at least $0.25 to bank it. Only quarters can be banked each turn. If you roll doubles you bust and lose any coins tallied in the "risk it" box for your turn.

This game, themed around fairness and equality, uses a Simultaneous Action Selection with Conflict mechanic. All players hold the same hand of cards (0, 1, 1, 3, 5), and the reverse side of each card always shows "2."

Each round, a round card placed on the table is revealed, and it depicts either "Fair" or "Equal."

When the round card shows "Fair," the value of the card played by each player becomes their score. When it shows "Equal," all players score 2 points. If more than half the players choose the same number, their cards are canceled and returned to their hands, making strategic bluffing crucial.

Additionally, before the game begins, players can peek at one round card, allowing for strategies based on information asymmetry.

—description from the designer

boardgame: アクオチクエスト(Fallen Quest)
description:
Fallen Quest is a game inspired by RPGs, incorporating elements of cooperation and hidden roles.

What makes it unique is that, despite the hidden role mechanic, there are no assigned roles, meaning it is possible for everyone to win together as a cooperative game without any betrayal.

Since there are no restrictions on in-game conversations, players can fully immerse themselves in their characters, enjoying the experience similarly to a tabletop RPG.

প্যাঁচফোড়ন (Pyachforon)  is a party game where players are divided in groups of two and together try to guess words.

In the first round, clue givers can say anything they want - and the guesser can guess as many times as they wish. For the second round, clue givers can only say one word and gestures - and the guesser can only guess once from now on. And in the final round, clue givers can’t say anything at all: they can only use gestures.

Children love nature. With this game you can put your knowledge to the test. They can train their skills in the ball maze. Lots of questions and fun activities make every round a new adventure.

Game idea
Who will be able to control the ball as cleverly as a mouse and as strong as a bear? Answer clever questions and demonstrate powerful actions.

At 20 miles per hex on a large-hex (3/4”) map covering all of France, the Benelux, and west Germany over to the Ruhr, and game turns each representing one week, its main scenario covers from the initial Normandy landing on 6 June 1944 through to the failure of Operation Market-Garden at the end of September.

The 1942 scenario covers “Operation Sledgehammer,” the invasion that was to be run that autumn, in place of the North Africa invasion, if it looked like the Soviets were in terminal trouble. The 1943 scenario covers an invasion launched late that summer instead of the Salerno landing. One sheet of 280 small-size half-inch NATO-style counters, with corps and armies as units of maneuver.

Alicia and Felipe hand out new equipment. They say that the next tournament is decisive, that thousands of people are going to see it and that we are going to win it. The truth is that they are a bit strange, maybe nervous. They say that we have to show strength, emotion and all the passion we feel for soccer in this great tournament. Tiki taka, a game with more tension than the classic. If you want to win, you will have to sweat it out by playing the pool, reliving the goals that took our breath away and remembering the most legendary players.

To beat your friends in this game, you have to try to answer like the majority. When someone is part of the least common answer it adds up to one dummy point. When you reach 10 points you will be proclaimed the Village Fool and lose your dignity in front of your friends.

Don't be the People's Fool, show everything you have learned in the university of life!

What is the limit of humor?

We don't know and neither do you.
We invite you to inhibit your limits and laugh at everything in the healthiest way possible. In the Banana Republic there are no taboos or politically correct people. Let's play!
Includes 150 cards and endless hours of laughter.

The Spanish Vaccine, the game that will give you the dose of humor you need!

After a very difficult year, finally comes the dose of humor we needed. With Banana Republic, The Spanish Vaccine, you will achieve immunity against all the imbeciles we live surrounded by. The effect of Politicians, naysayers and any other kind of filthy creatures will be drastically reduced and you will be able to keep your mental health.

This pack contains 150 cards with which you can play independently from the original game. However, we recommend adding it after a short time to increase the fun and get a permanent immunity.

The player with the most money - in cash and property - wins, so it's time to do whatever it takes to get the sale of the century!

Embezzlement is a board game of stealing, lying, and covering your tracks. Players take turns (over)paying invoices. Once paid, every payment stays on their board for the next 7 turns; giving opponents a chance to catch them in their lie.

You’re an underpaid, overworked, and ill-appreciated employee in the accounts payable department of Whispering Goat Games Incorporated. You and your colleagues have been “skimming a little off the top” to make ends meet and “get back at the corporate fat-goats” who run this company.

Recently, Jackson, from audits, got caught taking company funds. Now corporate are on the war path, setting traps, trying to weed out you and your embezzling colleagues. They’ve gone as far as to incentivise snitching; handing out rewards for information that lead to a successful Audit.

Things are getting tense, but you still don’t have enough money to run away to the desert. How much can you embezzle before your luck runs out?

In this escape room game, you have to show skill, creativity and teamwork to get to your destination on time. You have to solve puzzles, decipher codes and keep track of everything so that you can escape from the train madness. A fun escape adventure on rails with a familiar everyday opponent.

Gefangen im Bahnsinn
Da muss man mal eben schnell mit dem Zug von Stuttgart nach Leipzig. Nur ein Umstieg in Heidelberg, 30 Minuten Zeit dafür. Das klingt doch machbar, oder?
Nicht, wenn die Bahn da ein Wörtchen mit Zureden hat. Eine Baustelle hier, eine Gleisänderung an und am Ende hat nicht einmal mehr der Zugführer Lust, seine Arbeit zu verrichten. Da heißt es: Jeder Passagier für sich!

In diesem Escape-Room-Spiel musst du Geschickt, Kreativität und Teamwork beweisen, um noch pünktlich an dein Ziel zu kommen. Es gilt Rätsel zu lösen, Codes zu entziffern und dabei auch noch den Überblick zu bewahren, damit deine Flucht aus dem Bahnsinn gelingt. Ein lustiges Escape-Abenteuer auf Schienen mit dem un allen wohlbekannten Alltagsgegnerin.

boardgame: Three Days of Gettysburg: Deluxe Edition
description:
Three Days of Gettysburg traces its lineage back to Richard Berg's "Terrible Swift Sword" (1976). Take command of the Union Army of the Potomac or the Confederate Army of Northern Virginia in the 1863 Battle of Gettysburg, the largest battle of the American Civil War.

Players command their armies via the chain of command from corps down through divisions and brigades to the individual units - regiments of infantry and batteries of artillery. The series relies on interactive chit-pull mechanics to simulate the often-chaotic nature of the 19th Century battlefield at the regimental level.

Three Days of Gettysburg Deluxe Edition will include ten-plus scenarios. They range from small skirmisher contests on half-sized maps to the ultimate Gettysburg experience on four full full-sized maps depicting the entire battle, including the East Cavalry Battlefield! Other scenarios will depict the first day’s fight for the hills surrounding Gettysburg; the second day’s assault on Sickle’s salient; the third day’s cavalry fight on Cress Ridge; Buford’s defense against Heth as his division approaches Gettysburg using the new skirmisher rules; the July 2nd evening assault on Cemetery Hill; the fight north of town on July 1st, and several others. The game includes smaller scenario maps; experienced players will be able to play many of the scenarios in one sitting. The 3DoG Deluxe edition will include many exclusive rules to represent the special situations at Gettysburg. However, many rules are optional, allowing players to decide what level of complexity they want.

New components include cavalry counters of four strength points or less that allow the player’s tactics to resemble historical cavalry tactics at Gettysburg better. Confederate dismounted cavalry counters allow for the dismounted and mounted fight on the East Cavalry Battlefield. Many artillery units have corrected weapon types, and some batteries can be broken into sections for the smaller scenarios (think of Calef’s battery on the first day!). Additionally, artillery overshoot rules elegantly show the Confederate defective fuse issues.

There are two types of skirmisher units, detachments from infantry and dismounted cavalry and breakdown skirmish units of specialized units. Players will find skirmishers well suited for their historical missions and challenging to move (or remove) unless faced with formed units. The Bliss Farm can be the problem it was in the actual battle.

New rules allow artillery to take losses in lieu of a Disorder result. Now, Alonzo Cushing can make his heroic final stand at the Angle! New rules for when units Collapse allow elite units like the Iron Brigade and the 26th North Carolina to continue to fight as they did historically, even after severe losses. Revised rules better represent how units recover manpower losses; no longer are the better Cohesion units more likely to recover, and players will find it beneficial not to run regiments into the ground. With a new battle-book rule, refused units with plenty of manpower are no longer the weak links in your firing line.

New town rules help show the difficulties formed units faced moving through the streets and limit the firepower of units in town when firing. Shock in town hexes will be less deadly, and Gettysburg will not be the defensive bastion of previous editions. However, skirmishers can duplicate the problems they caused the enemy when sniping from the houses of Gettysburg.

The new maps continue to use Mark Simonitch’s beautiful artwork but include the following:

Stonewalls.
    The house-sized rocks specific to the Devil’s Den.
    Sloping hexes that better represent the uneven terrain and slopes at Gettysburg.

While woods have no Shock benefit, Rebels facing entrenched Yankees on Culp’s Hill will suffer like their historical counterparts. The many rock formations are presented differently from earlier editions, and artillery moving into a hex that takes more than four movement points will roll for Disorder. Players will find moving cannons up the slopes of Little Round Top as tricky in the game as it was in the battle.

We have added the eastern map that covers the cavalry battle between Hampton and Gregg on July 3rd. However, the addition allows for much more than just that. Both sides will find themselves using infantry to protect or threaten flanks on the new map, and Williams’ division of the Union 12th Corps can be in its historical position near the Daniel Lady farm on the afternoon of the first day.

-description from publisher's website

description:
ConspiraSea is an immersive social deduction game where players dive deep into a world of deception, set on a submarine infested with dangerous molluscs. Inspired by games like Mafia, Among Us, and Town of Salem, it combines these mechanics with original twists to deliver a unique, oceanic survival experience. Players navigate an ocean of deceits as they attempt to unmask hidden threats within the crew or, conversely, act in secret to undermine others.

Seoul Night is a wargame that allows you to experience the military struggle between high-level South Korean officials that briefly erupted in late 1979. The game takes only 6 turns and can be completed quickly by players familiar with the rules. Due to the simple rules it is very suitable as an introductionary wargame.

boardgame: The Naughty or Nice Christmas Lights Competition
description:
Roll dice, color the lights, and sabotage your neighbors in a festive print at home battle for Christmas glory!

Are you ready to compete in a Christmas Lights Competition in the City?! In this print and play roll, draw and color game, to win you must choose to play fair or bribe, steal lights or loosen bulbs to win big! But make sure to stay under wattage or blow a fuse and lose it all! If your house sparkles brighter than your neighbors, bask in the bragging rights of the season!

In the Great Pyramid you are group of Mayan constructors working together. The constructors that build with the best blocks and in the best locations will earn better rewards. The constructors that get most rewards at the end of the game win.

Each player will take turns buying material blocks and placing them, earning rewards.

The Pyramid game is an abstract game, block colocation, with simple mechanics but deep decision-making. It's a filler that takes between 20 and 40 minutes. For 2 to 4 players.

A casual game that can be played with children, family, and casually at a gathering, or it can even lead to a great and deep 1vs1 game. You can socialize while playing and still need to focus on your turn to score the most points. It's not a silly game, but one where you have to make the best decisions with the blocks you acquire. Luck is part of the game and is mitigated by a market, but luck is there to create fun moments when you draw a very good block or to laugh if you draw a bad one.

The Great Pyramid is a block placement game with an extra twist by adding a novel mechanic when building on top of other blocks.

—description from the designer

Osmosis is a unique solitaire game because you only need to match suits — the order of the cards doesn’t matter. Start with a 52-card deck, and lay down a row of four face-up piles of four cards each. Deal one card, face up, next to the top row. Try to match the suit of that card with any of the cards in your four piles. Add each card of the same suit to that foundation. Eventually you’ll have four foundation piles — one for each suit. But the trick is, you can only play the same numerical value of one of the cards in the foundation above it. So if the first foundation is hearts and has a five, jack, ace, and two, you can only use one of those cards in another suit to begin my second foundation row.

In this clever, butterfly-themed board game for two players, strategically (and simultaneously) move Caterpillars and Butterflies to capture your opponent's Monarch!

The game board is divided into three sections. Each player begins gameplay on their milkweed, or home row. The meadow fills the space between each player's milkweed; the fence is the dividing line at the halfway point of the meadow.

Each player begins gameplay with four Caterpillars and one Monarch, placed in their respective positions (1, 2, 3, 4, or M) on their home milkweed. Caterpillars can move one space orthogonally (up, down, left, or right). If Caterpillars successfully make it across the meadow into their opponent's milkweed, they automatically metamorphose into Butterflies and return to their starting position at their home milkweed. Butterflies can also move orthogonally but gain the added power of moving diagonally one space in any direction as well.

Monarchs are the most valuable piece in the game. Not only are they the piece that each opponent strives to capture but their movements are unique. They begin the game with the power to move one space orthogonally and diagonally, but cannot move beyond the fence. They also cannot be moved twice. As mentioned above, the goal of the game is to capture the Monarch. Any Caterpillar or Butterfly can capture the Monarch.

What makes Monarch different is simultaneous gameplay. Each player makes their move secretly behind a screen (the grove) on a compass, where their next move is decided by placing a die in the space corresponding to their next move. Illegal movements (those that cannot be made because they go against the rules of the game) will lead to the immediate end of the game. When each player has placed their die corresponding to their next move on their compass, both players groves are removed at the same time to reveal their moves simultaneously. Moves are made at the same time. If any Butterfly or Caterpillar lands in the same space as any other Butterfly or Caterpillar (whether that other Butterfly or Caterpillar is one's own or one's opponent), both pieces return back to their home milkweed. If any Butterfly or Caterpillar lands on the same space as a Monarch, the game is immediately over. Players can only move the Monarch or make no movement once (not twice in a row) before moving again; doing so twice in a row immediately ends the game. Lastly, if players choose "no movement" in the exact location of their opponent's movement, they have fortified their unit, which means it does not have to return to its home milkweed space (the opposing unit does, however!).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Imperial Miners: Aztecs vs Weirdlings – Pre-order promos
description: Seven promo event tiles, primarly given as a pre-order bonus for Imperial Miners first expension, Imperial Miners: Aztecs vs Weirdlings.

Devious is an anti-set collection card game. Build the strongest faerie alliance over five rounds and claim the crown. To do so you must play and hold in your tableau a single faerie from each of the faerie tribes, of the highest rank possible. But be wary, a second faerie of a given tribe negates your score - and more than two plunges your score into the negatives. Seek only the highest ranking faerie from each tribe for the strongest alliance possible. But each tribe has a unique power and the ability to manipulate faeries from around the table, including your own. Gaining the upper hand will be both chaotic and devious.

To set up before the game, players decide which six faerie tribes to use in the game from the 13 suits included in the box. They can be mixed and matched so game play varies from game to game.

In each round, all players are dealt three cards. But interestingly, they are not yours, they are your opponents. You look at them, decide  the order in which to stack them - and therefore in which order your opponent will later encounter them, then pass that stack in the direction of play. Likewise, you'll receive your stack in the same way.

Then look at the top card of the three-card stack and announce your intention to keep the card for your tableau or discard it, hoping for a better card your opponent may have buried deeper. If you choose to keep, discard the remaining stack of cards, without seeing them. If you choose to discard, look at the second card and choose to keep or discard. If the first two are discarded, the third card MUST be played. Did your opponent trick you into playing the worst card at the bottom of the stack. Was the best card the first card, which you discarded?

Once everyone has chosen their faerie card for the round, they are played in order of their Rank. The highest Rank plays their card into their tableau and executes the faerie's ability. Some Faerie Powers are only important for scoring purposes at the end of the game. Others have effects that target one or more faeries in play. A cunning player may choose to pass on a high ranking faerie for an ability that is more powerful when played later.

After the fifth round, players score each faerie suit in their alliances:

1 card of a Tribe: Score its Rank (from 1 to 5, depending on the card).
2 cards of the same Tribe: Score 0 points.
3-4 cards of a given Tribe: Lose points equal to the highest Ranking faerie in the Tribe.
5 or more cards of a Tribe: Score the summed value of all the fairies in the Tribe.
The player with the highest favor score wins.

Then, change the faerie bands in the deck, with their own unique powers for a new challenge and constant variety in play.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: The world has fallen into an era of chicken shortage. As a well-known successful man, in addition to countless properties at hand, the rich man Reas also owns the "Golden Hen" which is a symbol of supreme power. At this moment, he, who loves to travel around the world, keeps the hen in his friend's villa, and the news has also spread to the ears of nearby residents. So people with different scheming ideas gathered around the villa, coveting any wealth that the hen might give birth to.

Śledztwo w Białym Domu ("Investigation in the White House") is a detective card game where you step into the role of a witness, creating a criminal story of crime or solving political and criminal intrigues as a member of the investigative team!

Who blackmailed the president? Where did the CIA agent assault happen? Whose body was found at NASA headquarters?

Feel the atmosphere and excitement of criminal mysteries as you play with friends. Blackmail, human trafficking, the dark secrets of politicians – remember, everyone and everything is interconnected.

The game Investigation in the White House offers:

     political and criminal intrigue,
     sex, money, power,
     a variety of cases,
     simple rules, high replayability, and an immersive storyline.


—description from the publisher (translated)

Śledztwo w Białym Domu to detektywistyczna gra karciana, w której wcielając się w postać świadka tworzysz kryminalną historię zbrodni lub rozwiązujesz polityczne i kryminalne intrygi jako członek zespołu śledczych!

Kto szantażował prezydenta? Gdzie doszło do pobicia agenta CIA? Czyje ciało znaleziono w siedzibie NASA?

Poczuj klimat i emocje kryminalnych zagadek podczas rozgrywek ze znajomymi. Szantaż, handel ludźmi, mroczne sekrety polityków  - pamiętaj, wszyscy i wszystko jest ze sobą powiązane.

Gra Śledztwo w Białym Domu to:

     intrygi polityczne i kryminalne,
     seks, pieniądze, władza,
     różnorodne sprawy
     proste zasady, wysoka regrywalność i wciągająca fabuła


—description from the publisher

For its 25th anniversary, Crôa! is getting a makeover with a new double-sided board and cards!
Rediscover this family classic and become the Queen of the pond!

In Crôa!, take control of the pond that reveals itself as your frogs move around. Expand your colony with the help of males. Spread out quickly using mosquitoes and lily pads. But beware of pikes and rival frogs: they won’t show any mercy!
Play with optional cards for even more twists and turns, and be
the last Queen standing!

Play with the 55 cards to add more fun twists and turns to your games!

—description from the publisher

A fighter pack expansion to Fatal Knockout, Fatal Knockout Oddities Expansion adds four new fighters to the roster.

The aim of the game is to do five damage to your opponent before they deal it to you. The game is themed around old-school arcade fighters.

—description from the publisher

A fighter pack expansion to Fatal Knockout, High Rollers Expansion adds four new fighters to the roster.

The aim of the game is to do five damage to your opponent before they deal it to you. The game is themed around old-school arcade fighters.

—description from the publisher

Nestled deep in the undergrowth of Whitlock Grove, the sprightly Foraglings work together to grow their budding hamlet into a thriving community. Known for their benevolence, Foraglings contribute to this shared goal by scavenging the brush for resources and assisting with projects in the village. However, despite their compassion and kindness, they can also be a little devious, choosing instead to hoard some treasures for themselves rather than give them away.

In Foraglings, a 1-4 player competitive inventory management game, players will take on the role of a unique Foragling competing to collect the most coins throughout 6 rounds of play. Foraglings will bid for resource tiles and exchange them in the market as they work to build a hand of community projects to score at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher

El Dictador is a humorous and quirky board game that plunges you into the ruthless world of dictatorship. In this party game for 2 to 7 players, your goal is simple: seize power by any means necessary!

Game Mechanics
Each game lasts about 15 minutes and sits somewhere between 1000 Bornes and Exploding Kittens. The game consists of two types of cards:

     Vote Cards: To accumulate and build your electorate.
     Action Cards: To attack your opponents or defend yourself.


On each turn, you can either lay down Vote Cards in front of you or play Action Cards. The game ends when the "Counting" card is drawn. The player with the most votes wins the game and becomes El Dictador!

A Unique Visual Universe
The game's illustrations were created using artificial intelligence (Midjourney), resulting in a striking propaganda aesthetic that ties everything together.

El Dictador guarantees moments of laughter and betrayal among friends, where all bets are off in the race for supreme power! Suitable for ages 14 and up.

—description from the publisher

Off the coast of southern Australia sits Kangaroo Island, home to an abundance of diverse wildlife. Mobs of kangaroo inhabit the scrublands, while local little penguin colonies thrive in nearby rocky burrows. If you look up into the occasional eucalyptus tree, you're sure to see a pair of koalas, and if you peer into one of the many waterholes, you may spy the venomous platypus.

In Kangaroo Island, you'll control a growing herd of these animals as you explore and lay claim to the island's finite territory. Earn nature stars by taking a balanced approach to claiming territory, while unlocking new abilities for your animals and watching out for bushfires and human construction sites along the way.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Décorum: Our Family Plays Games Scenario
description: A Bonus scenario from the Our Family Plays Games 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

A game for real losers…

Get rid of all the losers and become the winner!

L00ser is a fun, mischievous and exciting card game with lots of fierce competition and teasing.

The game - just like the well-known party game - Meyer, Løgn and Røvhul, is about constantly playing over the previous players card. You should try to do that all the time - regardless of whether you have the cards for it or not! If you don't want to end up as a real loser, you must - with the help of bluff, lies and good cards - try to be the last player at the table with points, because then you have won!

On the journey to victory, it is important to avoid being caught lying and at the same time you must always be aware of the other players, so that you might catch them in a false report and thus cost them points!

The game also contains some so-called L00ser cards, which you must do your best to get rid of discreetly, because if you are caught using them, it will all end very abruptly for you...

L00ser is easy to learn and at the same time fun and highly addictive.

A game for real losers and the worst winners.

A fast game that's fun too, even if there are only two of you!

L00ser contains the cards from 0 to 9. You play two of your cards from your hand. One card is played invisibly with the back side up, so that you only reveal one of your cards that way. For example, if you place two 4s, you can either announce 44 or you can throw yourself into more imaginative announcements, since the others can only see one of the two 4s cards. For example, you can report 64 or even 94, if necessary or if you want to push the report higher, to put pressure on the next participant.

You can thus play - or at least claim to have played - from 01 to 100. The 100 is the highest play and means that you have placed two 0s. This play can only be matched by the next participant if he can ALSO play two 0s. If you cannot play, you can either lie, try to turn over the invisible card and see if the opponent is lying, or pay a point to clear the table and start with a fresh message.

L00ser is also really fun and mischievous for just two participants.

—description from the publisher

The Celtic Oppidum is a set of 3 complementary maps for the OPPIDUM game. Combined with the Oppidum 1 map provided in the game box, they allow you to reconstruct a large fortified city. Each map features different buildings from the Iron Age: Houses with their adjoining courtyard, a large villa with several rooms and interior courtyards, or a sanctuary with its sacrificial pits.

Supplied with an 8-page booklet with a description in English or French and 4 scenarios.

The maps meet the Cry Havoc standard, are printed in high definition (300 dpi) and on a strong varnished cardboard similar to that of the original maps.

—description from the publisher
