description:
Which are the most spoken languages in the world? The most commonly traded currencies? The countries with most Olympic medals?

In QUIZehn, each round a new Top 10 category is revealed and, one by one, you have to guess something that appears on the list. If you don't know any answers, you can pass - or keep a straight face and say something believable. Or challenge other players' answers. If you challenge a correct answer, or someone catches you bluffing, you lose a life. 
When someone is correctly challenged, the challenger gains the card. If everyone but one player passes, that player gains the card. 
First player to four points (or last player standing) wins the game.

Which countries have the largest desert areas in the world? Which animals most commonly appear as last names? Which are the most common chemical elements in the universe?

In QUIZehn, each round a new Top 10 category is revealed and, one by one, you have to guess something that appears on the list. If you don't know any answers, you can pass - or keep a straight face and say something believable. Or challenge other players' answers. If you challenge a correct answer, or someone catches you bluffing, you lose a life. 
When someone is correctly challenged, the challenger gains the card. If everyone but one player passes, that player gains the card. 
First player to four points (or last player standing) wins the game.

Bananas are obtained at the auction and given as gifts to the monkeys（”Monpi and his younger siblings). The player who pleases the monkeys the most with banana gifts wins. Keep an eye on the rapidly changing banana prices.

Which are the best selling comic book series? Directors with the most Golden Globe nominations? TV series with the highest number of episodes?

In QUIZehn, each round a new Top 10 category is revealed and, one by one, you have to guess something that appears on the list. If you don't know any answers, you can pass - or keep a straight face and say something believable. Or challenge other players' answers. If you challenge a correct answer, or someone catches you bluffing, you lose a life. 
When someone is correctly challenged, the challenger gains the card. If everyone but one player passes, that player gains the card. 
First player to four points (or last player standing) wins the game.

Abstract board game for 1 to 4 players, where each player is trying to get one of their pieces across to the opposite side of the playing area.

The distinguishing feature of this board game is the board itself. It can be configured different ways for differing game play.

boardgame: Monster Hunter International: Miniatures Game – CLASSIFIED
description:
CLASSIFIED is the expansion for the MHI Miniatures Game! 
It includes Two New Factions! --- The MCB (Monster Control Bureau and The Shackleford Vampire Coven) New characters and miniatures to make your games even more epic! Requires Core Game to play! 
 Game Description: 
Step into the world of Monster Hunter International, the most thematic and action-packed miniatures game inspired by Larry Correia's New York Times best-selling novels. Dive into epic, dice-chucking skirmishes where every roll could mean victory or doom in a tactical, competitive showdown of epic proportions!

Choose from a roster of truly unique Hunters and Monsters, each one pulled straight from the books and brought to life with stunningly detailed miniatures, making every game a tabletop spectacle. Will you assemble a team of ruthless Monsters, each with their own sinister skills and monstrous abilities, or lead a squad of Hunters wielding epic weaponry, ready to take on the supernatural? Each character stands ready, packed with dynamic abilities, powerful gear, and distinct personalities that make every game unforgettable.

Whether you’re playing grid-based or free-measurement style (rules for both are included!), the game’s highly immersive design will have you strategizing across a multitude of game modes and objectives. It’s not just a battle—it’s a war for survival, where every roll and every move matters. With a huge variety of objectives, no two games are the same. Gear up, roll those dice, and bring your best game face because Monster Hunter International isn’t just a game—it’s the ultimate, monster-stomping adventure for fans of epic dice-chucking fun!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeon Saga Origins: Glimmer of Greed Expansion
description:
Adventure calls again! Glimmer of Greed is an exciting expansion for the Dungeon Saga Origins game featuring a new story to play through that introduces a host of new enemies from the dangerous Goblin faction in the world of Pannithor.  With a range of new components, new cards, and a sprinkling of new rules for the Overlord and Hero players alike it’s time to muster your favoured companions and best equipment for another heroic tale!

A New Adventure of old friends

Glimmer of Greed is designed as a follow-up storyline to the Trial of Tyranny quest found in the Dungeon Saga Origins core game.  This makes it ideal to play through using the original four characters, Danor, Madriga, Orlaf, and Rordin as a continuation of their tale!

—description from the publisher

It's a game where you race rabbits with borrowed money. You are penniless, have already borrowed money twice, and start with 8 gold. You can also borrow more money, but the interest will increase and you will eventually have to pay it back in full, so be careful!

Ronny, in his steamroller, flattens anything in his way. The goal? Get your traffic cones safely across the street before they’re crushed! This simple yet strategic game combines luck with tactics, making it easy to pick up yet engaging for all ages.

Two to four brave players aged five and up try to guide their cones from the forest to the city by crossing four lanes while keeping a close eye on Ronny, who’s happily daydreaming in his steamroller. On each turn, players roll two dice. The first die moves one of their cones forward, with a star allowing a jump of up to four lanes towards the goal. The second die dictates Ronny’s action. If it shows one or two colors, Ronny's steamroller shifts to the corresponding lane or stays put. But if it shows the "move" symbol, Ronny rolls across the lanes, flattening everything in his path! Any cones caught on that lane are squashed, sent back to the start.

You can only move cones to the goal once none remain on the start space, and you must land there with an exact roll. Otherwise, you’ll have to wait and hope Ronny doesn’t roll through again. The first to get all four cones to safety wins this fast-paced game of chance and strategy!

—description from the publisher

In Memorias de Julio Verne, players work together to draw cards with Objects from the deck and try to remember which of the face-down Great Inventions they relate to.

Players progress through levels of increasing difficulty, opening envelopes and adding new cards. Additionally, the game dynamics can change, adding Great Inventions to the deck and other surprises.

A level is complete when all the Objects are paired with a Great Invention.


En Memorias de Julio Verne, los jugadores colaboran para robar cartas de Objetos del mazo e intentar recordar con cuál de los Grandes Inventos, que permanecen boca abajo, están relacionados.

A medida que avanzan por niveles de dificultad creciente, los jugadores abren sobres y agregan nuevas cartas. Las dinámicas del juego también pueden cambiar, incorporando al mazo nuevos Grandes Inventos y otras sorpresas.

El nivel se completa cuando todos los Objetos han sido correctamente emparejados con un Gran Invento.

Roll and (re)write the 2024 US presidential election in this tense electoral strategy game. Draft dice in this interactive roll & write to thwart the other candidate in a tense game that could go any way – right to the end.

One player rolls three dice, and chooses one die to keep for themselves. The other player then similarly picks from the remaining two, with the final one being available to both players as a shared die. Each die is a different colour which relates to an age demographic; odd and even values allow you to target blue-collar and white-collar voters respectively.

Each player uses the value and colour of the dice to claim voters in one of seven swing states, targeting them by demographic in order to be the first to claim the independent 'purple' voter, and win the electoral college victory points for that state. Or they can use the dice values to activate powers on their player sheet, to manipulate the dice in future.

The first to win the majority of the swing states' votes wins the game, and the presidency!

—description from the designer

Few stories have captured the imagination as strongly as the use of gladiatorial combat in ancient Rome. For centuries it served as entertainment for the masses, serving a similar purpose as modern sports today, albeit in a more barbaric fashion. For the gladiators themselves, it was a harsh way of life in a harsh world – but with the potential to rise above the status of being a slave, and possibly win freedom …

Gladiators: Blood & Glory is a 2-player or solitaire game that places you in charge of running a Ludus – a Roman gladiator training school. You will make all the training, equipping, and logistical decisions a Lanista would make, and make the tactical decisions for your gladiators as they attempt to survive the Arena.

You will start with a stable of two gladiators, but will be afforded the opportunity to bid on more at auction to increase your numbers (or to replace losses). Training and managing finances will be key in your ability to field gladiators who have a chance to win in fights to the death. As time progresses and gladiators survive, they may use the experience gained in the arena and in training to improve their odds of success by purchasing skills. Victories help to narrate the player’s eventual goal – to have gladiators survive the arena and earn their freedom.

The gameplay moves quickly, with matches in the arena involving back-and-forth combat with a deck of cards. However, the tactical fighting in the Arena is just one aspect of the game, as players will also train, purchase supplies, gladiators, and weapons, and plan for future games. Additionally, players may wager denarii (Roman money) on matches in an attempt to build up their available funds. Optional rules include fighting against wild beasts such as tigers and cheetahs.

The game includes six of the major types of gladiators who saw combat in the arena:

Murmillo (short sword, large shield)
Dimachaerus (two swords)
Retiarius (trident and net)
Hoplomachus (spear and small shield)
Thraex (hooked sword)
Secutor (variant of Murmillo)
Product Information:

Complexity: 4 out of 10 
Time Scale: Semi-Abstract (individual fights, up to 5 per month)
Map Scale: Abstract 
Unit Scale: Individual gladiators 
Number of Players: One or two 
Suitability for Solitaire: Solitaire rules included 
Average Time to Play: 10-15 minutes (single turn) 4-5 hours (full career)

—description from the publisher

This mini-expansion builds on the LACORSA Grand Prix Game: 1962 Expansion with a new set of racecar pieces in Walker Blue, and a full set of Car Performance cards and new track card specifically for the 1960 Grand Prix season.

—user summary

This mini-expansion builds on the LACORSA Grand Prix Game: 1962 Expansion with a new set of racecar pieces in Belgian Yellow, and a full set of Car Performance cards and new track card specifically for the 1961 Grand Prix season.

—user summary

Word Whiz is an exciting twist on traditional word games. Players earn points by being the first to use all of their tiles in the shared crossword and by making longer words.

Each player has their own set of tiles (25 letters with 2 wild), each a different color. There are no turns during play - everyone plays at the same time on the same crossword. Players will have 7 tiles available to play at any time, and they can play words of any length. However, only words of 4 letters or longer earn points, with longer words having increased value. The first player to play all of their tiles into the crossword earns the First Finish Bonus, increasing their odds of winning.

Players tally their points after each round on the provided scorecard, and the player with the most points after 4 rounds wins!

Stepping Into Hell is Volume 10 of GMT's Great Battles of the American Civil War, which traces its lineage back to Richard Berg's "Terrible Swift Sword" (1976). Players take on the role of Generals Grant and Lee to fight this regimental-level American Civil War game.

The game is structured to provide a comprehensive experience of the 1864 Battle of the Wilderness. It contains a small division level, hypothetical learning scenario playable in one sitting, introducing players to the series and battle-specific rules. The game also covers the full battle with two scenarios: one with an early start, which allows players to tinker with alternative strategies, and one with a later start, which restricts players to a more historical line of play. Two slightly smaller scenarios cover the action on May 5th on the Orange Turnpike and the May 6th battle on the Orange Plank Road. The other seven scenarios run up to five turns long and cover primarily historical battles, such as Warren’s initial attack, Longstreet and Gordon’s flank attacks on the second day, and Hancock’s massive attack on the morning of May 6th. The player will understand why Robert E. Lee accepted battle facing a Union army almost twice his size in the difficult terrain of the Wilderness. The player will also see the beginning of Grant’s transformation of the Army of the Potomac from McClellan’s timid army to the grinding force that would ultimately defeat Lee’s Army of Northern Virginia.

The game introduces several unique features that add depth and challenge to the gameplay. The second growth of the Wilderness battlefield profoundly affected command and control. Each hex spans 110 yards, which was well beyond the vision and knowledge of a regimental commander given the dense undergrowth, which was also challenging to transverse. A new system mechanic called Drift affects units moving in this terrain. They may move as intended, drift in any direction, or become disordered. Both sides will struggle with the terrain. Additional features include Brushfires, Ambushes, and the effect of expiring enlistments on many of the Union units. Both sides suffered from surprise attacks on unprepared or exhausted men. Three scenarios challenge the players to maintain the momentum of a surprise attack or build a firewall to stop it. Both sides historically suffered potentially decisive collapses.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great Battles of the American Civil War (Richard H. Berg)

description:
Castle of the Vampire is a FREE 1 Player Roll & Write Adventure with a 1 Page Hand drawn Map and Character Chart (see BGG Files).

Who will End this Evil? Three Heroes step forward to face the zombies, ghouls and werewolves with the goal of reaching the Castle of the Lord Vampire and to Defeat him once and for all.

YOU must choose their path using the map and items to unlock the way, each enemy encounter will involve rolling a d6 dice to determine types and strength but the use of items, terrain and abilities may yet give your team the upper hand.

All you need is a printer, pencil, eraser, a D6 dice, 20mins.

—description from the designer

MRBLS is a card game that requires cooperative teamwork and strategy, where players work together to deplete the deck. Unlike traditional competitive games, everyone works together, creating groups of colors as you plan each move. Communication is key as players discuss tactics, anticipate each other’s moves, and share the thrill of a last-minute victory, making every game an engaging social experience.

Players take turns playing cards from their hand anywhere in the game row, trying to match colors and make groups of threes, called triads. If a triad is created, players remove the triad from the game row and draw a new card in their hand. If a triad is not made, players place a new card from the top of the game deck at the end of the game row. Players win if the game row does not pass ten cards and no more cards are in the deck or the players' hands.

MRBLS is perfect for players who enjoy collaborative problem-solving and dynamic gameplay. This game offers endless replayability and strategic depth.

—description from the publisher

The Price of Coal is a card-based prompt-driven storytelling game about coal miners and their loved ones in West Virginia, 1921. It’s about the struggles faced by those who stand up for their rights, and about communities that come together through hard times. It’s about a history that should not be forgotten. It’s about the fights we still face today.

The game uses character and prompt cards to help you tell the story of a year leading up to a terrible battle. During this year, characters will face many trials together, both inside and outside the mines. The Price of Coal draws inspiration from a variety of storytelling games, like Red Carnations on a Black Grave, For the Queen, The Quiet Year, and Montsegur 1244.

Manatliq is an engaging board game where players step into the role of competitive taxi drivers, each striving to attract passengers, maximize earnings, and ultimately become the wealthiest driver in the city. Players race to various locations, competing to collect valuable rewards while managing resources like fuel levels and avoiding encounters with the police. Strategic planning and quick decisions will be essential to stay ahead of the competition and claim victory.

—description from the designer

Your building is under siege by a kabutar, who is wreaking havoc by pooping everywhere. The residents are on the verge of a breakdown. It's either the kabutar or you.

In Kabutar, you play as one of the building residents, uncovering the secrets of the building one action at a time. Along the way, you will hide from pigeon poop, clean the building, and search the apartments and the common spaces to find and confront this elusive kabutar. Be warned: If the kabutar walks into the apartment of a weak-willed neighbor, that person might shelter it and turn against you.

It's time to send a message — not with the kabutar, but to the kabutar.

—description from the publisher

Players score points when they eliminate all of their cards or defeat all of the players around them using the spirits of rock-paper-scissors island.
The player with the highest score after multiple rounds wins. Cards are marked with number strength and rock-paper-scissors symbols, which are used to play cards and battle. When a card is played, the Spirit's technique is triggered, and the effects vary from exchanging hands to battles occurring. In battles, cards are played one at a time to compare their strength, and a new system is employed in which the game is decided by a game of aiko, using rock-paper-scissors marks and numbers. The dramatic game development that is created even in the short game time is appealing.

—description from the publisher

Today is a good day to die… for the enemy crew!

Battlefront Valkyrie is a groundbreaking 3D printable wargame that delivers the thrill of epic fleet battles right to your tabletop, all at a cost that’s just a fraction of traditional games!

All models are optimized for printing on FDM printers and print without supports.

This comprehensive starter set includes:

10 Detailed Ships: Ready to print and assemble for immediate play.
Asteroids: For strategic gameplay and scenic enhancement.
Dial Counters: To manage hull and engine damage effectively.
Game Counters and Templates: Everything needed to start playing immediately.
Full-Color Rulebook: Complete rules to dive into the action.
Model Assembly Manual: Step-by-step guide for building your fleet.
Ship Cards: Detailed stats for each ship model.
Drones & Mines: Add tactical depth to your battles.

With Battlefront Valkyrie, immerse yourself in the universe of massive space combat without breaking the bank!

—description from the publisher

description:
Skip, jump, hop on one leg and spin around to gain tokens and win the game.

The first player flips one of the random pieces to see what color it has on the other side. If it match the player color, they can pick it up. Before the turn ends, the player must perform the aerobic exercises that the playing pieces describe.

The next player then tries to find a piece with their matching color. If any player flips a piece with a different color than their own, it stays face up, free for any player with a matching color to grab it on their turn.

With luck, you may get a "Mr Nilsson"-brick, which allows you to steal pieces from other players. Once you've won three pieces, you gain a Pippi-token and have to mimic the described gesture. First player to gain 3 Pippi-tokens wins the game.

This is a Party Game where you play against others. One has to roll a die and press the button in the app on which square the figure lands, which will then open up a minigame. Minigames can be played alone or in teams.
By succesfully earning 12 coins, the player wins the game.

Denkprofi ist das Familienspiel - aber auch auf Partys mit Kindern oder Erwachsenen der Hit. Lustige Minispiele, die Fingerfertigkeit & Geschicklichkeit fordern, sorgen für viel Action gegen Langeweile. Die Kombination aus Quiz-, Wissens-und Denkaufgaben mit Minispielen macht Denkprofi zu einem der interaktivsten Brettspiele. Jeder der 3-6 Spieler wird aktiv ins Spielgeschehen integriert und muss 12 Münzen sammeln. Mehr als 1000 Aufgaben, die ständig erweitert werden, liefern aufregende Vielfalt. In diesem Spiel kann jeder beweisen, dass er es mehr drauf hat als die anderen Spieler!

Einfache Regeln - nach 1 Minute versteht jeder das Spiel: Gewürfelt wird im Uhrzeigersinn. Wer zuerst 12 Münzen sammelt, gewinnt das Spiel. Jeder spielt für sich. Gelangt man auf ein Feld, drückt man das entsprechende Symbol in der App und das Spiel wird gestartet.

Test your love of ONE PIECE!

One Piece Vivre Rush is a game of knowledge, intuition, and speed, using cards with "ONE PIECE" character panels and data.
The cards feature a large collection of 100 characters. Quickly get the card whose first appearance or bounty matches the theme! A tabletop competitive board game to find the "one and only right answer

One Piece Vivre Rush is the first product of the board game brand by Shueisha and Shueisha Games, which makes reading manga fun. We will create a game that makes you want to reread and discuss the manga as you play. We hope you will enjoy it by placing it next to the original comics.

—description from the publisher

The game will be packed with the charm of BLEACH, including beautiful illustrations by Mr. Kubo Taito, and the powerful and delicate words of the opening song and quotes.

Additionally, the game comes in a package that is the same size of the manga, making it perfect to be displayed next to your BLEACH collection!

Bleach Songs of the Soul is the first product of the board game brand "Manga Bodoge" by Shueisha and Shueisha Games, which makes reading manga fun. We will create a game that makes you want to reread and discuss the manga as you play. We hope you will enjoy it by placing it next to the original.

—description from the publisher

A board game centered around the essence of cheering, an indispensable part of volleyball matches.

Observing the succession of 'Haikyu!!' players as they appear, swiftly announce their names when the player is complete, and call out the team names when teams are assembled! The quickest participant receives a card, and the victor is the one who accumulates the most cards.

Additionally, catering to passionate 'Haikyu!!' fans, the game includes expert rules that elevate the challenge of the calls. Competing by swiftly announcing the words on banners when the teams are assembled, and reciting the players' lines when the players are complete, allows for further engagement in the world of 'Haikyu!!'.

—description from the publisher

Players race to pack their bag first. Grab a tile from a face-down selection - if it's one of yours, place it on your bag board. But if the tile belongs to an opponent, you must reveal it to all other players before flipping it face down again. The first to complete their packing is the winner!

boardgame: Jujutsu Kaisen: The Cursed Spirits Escape – Shibuya Incident-
description:
Jujutsu Kaisen Cursed Spirits Escape -Shibuya Incident- is a 3v1 survival game, in which you become a cursed spirit and escape from the most powerful Jujutsu sorcerer, Satoru Gojo.
The attack of the cursed spirit does not affect Gojo in any way and the cursed spirits are exorcised just by being in the same area. The cursed spirits must use the human as a shield to escape and buy time until they can seal Gojo in the Prison Realm.
Satoru Gojo must pursue and exorcise the cursed spirits with his god-like powers while also saving humans throughout the game.
Who will emerge victorious in this ultimate 7-round curse battle?

—description from the publisher

description:
Reunion with Death is a downloadable present-day murder mystery game for 6 to 9 people where the host can play too. Mikolette Lukanis, former prom queen, has been killed just before the 15-year high-school reunion gala party. But who is the murderer?

A 15-year high-school reunion, in smallish-town America. Former students are gathering in the town's main hotel, ahead of the big party tonight. But one of them, former prom queen Mikolette Lukanis, has been found murdered!

Everyone is locked in together in the hotel ballroom where the gala was to have taken place, pending a full police investigation. High-school rivalries will re-emerge, old grudges and secrets will surface: along with a heady mix of present-day envy, betrayal, and lust.

Get out your glad rags, consult your old high-school yearbook, head for the complementary bar, and join the alumni of Holborrow High as they prepare for their Reunion with Death

Reunion with Death lasts for about four hours and is suited to an evening. It is often played over a meal – a finger buffet if possible, to allow guests to circulate and talk in private.

—description from the publisher

In Candy Necklace, the players take turns gathering candies from the board. Each turn, the active player gives candies to the Magpie, then takes some for him/herself. At the end of the game, the player that gathered most candies wins, but beware: the candies that the Magpie didn't like (the ones it picked up less) score no points!

—description from the designer

MadMatix is a dynamic card game where numbers meet strategy in a head-to-head competition of arithmetic mastery. Designed for 2-8 players, MadMatix combines simple math operations with clever tactics to keep everyone engaged and thinking on their feet. The game's unique blend of Number Cards, Operator tokens, and Action Cards allows players to build scoring Sequences — or disrupt their opponents' — making every turn an exciting challenge.

Players take turns adding Number Cards to their own or opponents’ Sequences, using operators like +, −, ×, and ÷ to maximize their score. Action Cards add an extra layer of strategy, enabling players to modify Sequences, or force others to draw cards. Every choice can swing the game, and with the right tactics, players can boost their scores or sabotage their competition.

The goal is straightforward: be the player with the highest score when any player runs out of cards. Scoring is calculated left to right, and is always affected by the order of play — so strategy is key. But be careful: one wrong move could result in a zero score, especially when dividing by zero!

MadMatix is easy to learn and offers engaging gameplay for all ages. It’s ideal for casual players, families, and math enthusiasts alike, blending simple math with strategic depth. Perfect for game nights, it’s quick to set up, thrilling to play, and sure to bring out the competitive spirit in everyone!

—description from the publisher

Get Ready to Set Sail with Coconut Cannon!

Dive into the fast-paced, pirate-themed adventure of Coconut Cannon, a quick matching game designed for family fun and friendly competition! In this action-packed card game, players race to discard their cards by matching them as fast as they can. Be the first to clear your hand and claim victory in just 10-15 minutes of exciting gameplay!

Featuring hilarious swashbucklers, mischievous mermaids, squiggly krakens, and glittery gold, Coconut Cannon offers endless entertainment for kids, teens, and adults. Perfect for 2-5 players and easy to learn, it's great for family game night, parties, or on-the-go fun!

Created with pride in St. Pete, Florida, Coconut Cannon is designed to bring families and friends together in a race to smash, match, and outsmart each other. Whether you're camping, traveling, or just looking for a quick and hilarious game, Coconut Cannon is the perfect choice.

Key Features:

     Fast Action, Quick Matches: Discard cards as fast as you can in this thrilling pirate-themed matching game.
     Family-Friendly Fun: Ideal for ages 6+ and great for 2-5 players.
     Compact & Portable: Perfect for travel, parties, and family gatherings.
     Unique Pirate Characters: Featuring hand-drawn, fun-loving characters, designed to make every round an adventure.
     Easy to Learn, Fun to Play: Set up in seconds and dive into the action!


Set sail for fun with Coconut Cannon—where quick thinking and faster reflexes will help you conquer the high seas!

—description from the designer

Uncheckered is an innovative strategy game that adds a new dimension to two-player classic board games. Maneuver your dice and capture your opponent’s to control the board and clear a path to victory. What’s the catch? Dice only jump lower valued dice, and you can merge two to increase one's value. It's simple to learn, but hard to master.

To win the game, be the first player with a 6 in the row closest to your opponent.

--description from the publisher

Roll 4 Ruinsis a puzzle dungeon-crawling game where players navigate through Ruins, engaging in combat with Enemies and strategizing with dice combinations. Travel choices affect gameplay, testing Resolve, overcoming Torments, and gathering Grace to enhance survival chances while managing the ever increasing Blight. Each of the game’s 3 Chapters culminates in a Boss encounter.

Eisenhower is a tabletop game of the great battles of the European and Mediterranean theatres of the Second World War. Command entire corps and armies as you struggle over the famous battlefields of the war, from North Africa to Russia to France.

Choose one of six major armies: Britain, France, Germany, Italy, the Soviet Union, or the United States. Create battles or fictional scenarios based upon famous historical campaigns. Make decisions that bring tens of thousands of soldiers into action.

Eisenhower is played with miniature figures. In addition to this rulebook, you will need models or counters in order to play.

The basic Eisenhower game can be played by two or more players in 2-3 hours.

The advanced rules introduce airpower, naval support, invasions by air and sea, and can accommodate as many players and be as large in scale as your imagination permits.

—description from the publisher

The Munster Gang heads out to the spooky haunted Manchac swamp of Louisiana for some drag racing fun. They have the mud tires on the Dra-Gu-la and Koach car and ready to dual it out. I had children in mind for this one but it is a fun game for adults also. A simple dice mechanic which includes some fun and scary add-ons, initially designed over the Halloween season. Drag Racing through the swamps can be tough at times but they are always having a blast.

boardgame: Rapid Fire Reloaded! The Road to Carentan
description:
The Road to Carentan is a 36 page, fully illustrated PDF providing four scenarios for actions involving elements of the American 101st Airborne Division (The Screaming Eagles). 
It covers the period from D-Day to the initial stages of the battles to capture the vital town of Carentan and thus link up the landings at Omaha and Utah Beaches. 
The four scenarios were inspired by four of Paul Woodadge's excellent YouTube WW2TV shows.  
The scenarios are Angoville-au-Plain (D-Day), Vierville (D-Day+1), Saint-Come-du-Mont (D-Day+1) and Purple Heart Lane Breakout (D-Day +4). 
It's a break from our normal scale for units, focusing on small scale actions that saw improvised US para forces taking on their German counterparts in the 6th Fallschirmjager Regiment (The Lions of Carentan) as well as infantry and Ost battalions.

Designed to be played on 6' x 4' or 5' x 4' tables using Rapid Fire Reloaded rules (or RF2 if players prefer) they include historical backgrounds, full graphic orbats, battle maps, special rules and a section on 'what troops do I need to play the scenarios'.

—description from the publisher

Bombs Away! is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine simulating the USAAF 8th Air Force precision daylight bombing campaign against Germany during 1942-1945.

As the pilot of a United States Army Air Force B-17 heavy bomber during 1942-1945, your ultimate goal is to complete either 25 or 35 bombing missions inside Germany, France, Belgium, and the Netherlands, while at the same time bringing your aircraft and crew home safely. 
It won’t be easy, but England, America, and all of occupied Europe are depending on you.

Don’t let them down!

—description from the publisher

description:
Two if by Sea is a set of scenarios to be used with the TwoFatLardies rules Kiss Me Hardy using the changes for ships under the rate from To Covet Glory. 
The book contains ship stats for a large number of vessels from large Ship Sloops to the smallest cutters and Bermuda Sloops used by the Royal Navy and the American Continental Navy and State Navies (Georgia, South Carolina, North Carolina and Virginia).

The scenarios include twelve scenario sets. Each scenario set is one or more unique historical or fictional scenario from the American Revolution. 
The first scenario details the British Schooner Halifax in 1769 in an incident before the war started. The scenarios continue through July of 1781. There is also a guide on setting up convoy escorts or raids on the Atlantic, Caribbean, or Far East trade routes.

There are fifty nine pages of scenarios and 121 pages of ship lists. There is also a section noting the types of merchant vessels that were likely to be encountered on the waters of the Americas during the war. Lastly, there are notes on the available models in both 1/300 scale and 1/700th including links to the manufactures.

The scenarios should be adaptable to any rules system that can handle individual ship battles with vessels that are frigates or smaller.

—description from the publisher

Numerica is a board game where you must alter the board to weaken your enemies and strengthen yourself, being always alert for the arrival of the end of the game.

Start the game by assembling the board with the cells (tile cards) in a grid. These cells must be randomly located and randomly rotated and flipped between front and back. This way they will form an unique starting board for each game.

With more than 50 different cells, boards can get as big as 7x7 squares that will make the game a long experience or just 4x4 or 5x5 that will turn it into a quick game. Depending, of course, on the number of people playing the game.

Numbers in the cells will show you which cells are connected and which are not. That will indicate how to move the pawns and how to expand your dominion over the board. Change cells with actions to make it easier for you to expand.

Use action cards to change the board and complicate things for your adversaries. You can eliminate one or more of its pawns or even steal one pawn and make it yours. You can do as many actions as you want in the same turn to make unexpected combos of actions to shift the course of the game.

Numerica will make you think how to expand your pawns in the board. It is easy to feel confident but in just a game lots of things could change. Pawns are easily made and lost, but victory is not as easily accomplished. The game only finishes when all the board is completed and the one with more points wins. But there is a trap here, choose well where to place your pawns because not all places will be good.

—description from the designer

Sveaborg Viapori 1855 is a solitaire war game depicting the bombardment of the Russian fortress of Sveaborg-Viapori (in front of actual Helsinki) in 1855 by the Allied Navies of Great Britain and France during the Crimean War. 
It was one of the most import events of the war in the Baltic Sea, and the objective of the British Admiral Dundas was to blow an hit to the Russian pride and gain some advantage at home, cheering the crowds and pleasing the Queen Victoria with an humbling victory. In three days the Allied fleet bombed the fortress, deployed on 6 islands, burning some of them but actually without leaving too much damage; the Russian barely answered to the fire, but a more aggressive Russian commandant would have the opportunity to score some hits. 
You are in command of the Allied fleet; 
will you be able to burn all the fortress and get knighted by the Queen? 
Or you will loose ships and honour?

—description from the publisher

description:
Soviet Guards vs German Panzers: Poland, August 1944
After the 3rd Guards Tank Army crossed the Vistula River near Annopol, it was met by strong German counter-attacks. By 16 August, the German attacks had petered out leaving the Soviets in control of Annopol and the surrounding area. 
Had the German counter-attacks succeeded, the Soviet attacks into Poland would have stalled.

Can the Germans change history by eradicating the Soviet bridgehead? Or will the Soviet Army secure its hold and drive deeper into Poland?

This scenario pack contains four ready-to-play missions for Combat HQ and Total War. 
Each mission includes a mission briefing, a map, plus Battle Group and Unit Statistics.

—description from the publisher

DiceKeepers: EndHaven invites players into a realm of high-stakes fantasy where strategy and luck collide with the roll of the dice. Take on the roles of heroic adventurers tasked with defending the town of EndHaven from encroaching dangers—dragons, witches, vampires, and other fearsome creatures beyond imagine. Take them head on with unique allies at your side and compete to become the most renowned hero of the land.

Players will take turns leading the day and completing Adventure Cards by covering all their Number Dice Slots with dice. Completed Adventure Cards net players Reptutaion Points or RP (the games winning point value). By placing even a single die on a card, players can reroll their remaining dice keeping them in control of their chances. Any Adventure Cards a player fails to complete will have it’s consequence effect that player at the end of their turn. Once all players have gone the game will proceed with the End of Day.

At the end of each day any remaining Adventure Cards with the Red Border will trigger their consequences for each player possibly Attacking the Town.

Players must also rely on the clever use of Ally Cards to shift the odds—these cards can adjust dice rolls, offer protection, or remove difficult Adventure Cards from the field. Luckily, players always have access to the local Tavern, where a completed 3 of a kind on the card’s Match Dice Slots will allow them to draw an Ally Card, giving them a constant chance to bolster their ranks.
Even if a player dies, they will carry on the fight with the use of a Resurrection Stone. Be warned the Resurrection Stone takes a toll on the town and is worth -5 RP at the end of the game.
The game will end once a player reaches the winning Reputation Point (RP) value or if the town is destroyed.

—description from the designer

Murder Mystery Getaway is a game where one player plays as a killer trying to get away with murder and others play as innocent suspects trying to find the killer. Throughout, the game players give alibis and check each other's clues. The game ends when someone is successfully accused. The killer wins if the accused is innocent, thereby framed, and the innocent suspects win if the killer is accused.

Catharsis: Second Edition is a revised and remastered version of the original game. The gameplay is still the same, but we have updated the game majorly with better design, new characters, bosses, roles, powers, artwork, narrative and much more. We took all the feedback our fanbase gave us and made the game vastly better.

Catharsis: Second Edition is a true solo & cooperative board game for players who are heroes trying to stop one of the many corruptions spreading through the lands. The game requires strategy, cooperation, and a touch of luck to succeed as the party fights through traps, monsters, and more. Each game is a different experience as each hero has a deck of powers they draw from, creating unique synergies every playthrough. Moreover, the players must hunt down a boss by progressing through its Boss deck, a unique deck of cards for each boss and randomized for each game. The players must carefully balance using their skills and creeping closer to death with burning through their morale and giving up in despair.

Religion: You can increase your religiosity on this board. In return, you receive various bonuses and can use special actions. However, your ability to research is deteriorating.

You immediately receive a prophet that you can use on the Game Board, where he can support you in various ways (cause unrest with other players, raise your religiosity, make your people more happy). It is very important to keep an eye on your vigilance and maybe boost it with some agents - or use these to force operations against the other players? Or use them to fight unrest in your provinces?

boardgame: The Silver Bayonet: Italy – The Shades of Calabria
description:
Venture into the undead-ravaged lands of southern Italy with this supplement for The Silver Bayonet, featuring a solo/cooperative campaign, new soldiers, and new foes.

Something sinister has risen in Calabria, the hauntingly beautiful yet dangerous province of southern Italy. Still recovering from the destructive earthquake that killed tens of thousands of people, the region's clergy preach a crusade against France and its allies. As the major nations vie for military, political, and ideological supremacy over the Mediterranean, the Bourbon royal family plots to reclaim its Neapolitan throne, sending agents from its Sicilian stronghold to support the briganti – Calabria's bandit gangs – in waging a bloody guerrilla war against the occupying French. Meanwhile, rumors spread that the dead no longer rest easy, but rise from their graves to wander isolated valleys and mountain paths...

You are sent into this powder keg to seek out the truth behind these tales. Amid the chaos, you will encounter enemies both mortal and monstrous, including a variety of undead… and those who command them. A solo/cooperative campaign and competitive scenarios will challenge your troops, so recruit new soldiers and steel your nerve to face the many perils of mysterious Calabria.

—description from the publisher

From veteran gamer James Morris comes a new tabletop miniature wargame rooted in the heroic leadership of Achilles, Boudica and Beowulf.  Players take control of a force of warriors and heroes as they build their reputation and crush their enemies through mighty feats of battle. Midgard Heroic Battles can be played in any historical, legendary or fantasy setting where warriors fight it out with swords, spears and shields. From the siege of Troy to the battle of Hastings, from the frost giants of Norse myth to the dwarfs of the mountain halls, from Arthur to El Cid, Midgard places you on the field of battle.

The game is miniatures – and scale –  agnostic, so you may play with any figures that you choose. This book contains all the rules that you need to play, including sorcery and flying sections, five scenarios, twelve sample force lists and an appendix to help you create your own forces and heroes.

Will the bards sing of your reputation…or will you be food for ravens?
