The War in Iraq expansion takes place in the desert of Iraq. The war has concluded, and lucrative oil fields have recently been discovered. Your goal is to ship the oil to the US and Europe to enrich your business. The US Army will, of course, aid your cause...

The Berlin Wall expansion (first released in 2008) features a new Engineer action allowing a player to build three pieces of track or remove one red track marker from the board. Goods may be delivered from east to west (and vice versa) only through holes in the wall by removing the red track markers. Red cubes represent the Soviet Army and Blue cubes the American, British and French forces; movement of these cubes is restricted until the round marker reaches the red player marker that was placed at the start of the game. At this point all restrictions are removed!

Soviet armies began their offensive to isolate the Hungarian capital of Budapest in October 1944. The city would be surrounded in December. During that time, as German and Hungarian troops fought to keep the Soviets from completing the encirclement, fighting stubborn defensive actions backed by repeated counter-attacks.

Fire & Sword: Battles for Hungary 1944 is a complete Panzer Grenadier game based on the Soviet effort to encircle Budapest and the German/Hungarian efforts to stop them.

Fire & Sword includes 913 die-cut playing pieces, most of which originally appeared in our long out-of-print Road to Berlin game. There are also five brand-new map boards, and 39 new scenarios organized in our popular story-arc format with historical background to put the scenarios in their context and battle games to tie them together.

Fire & Sword comes in our Playbook format: everything you need to play, except dice, inside a book.

—description from the publisher

Relocation is an easy and challenging game to play. It is a game for 2, 3, or 4 players. The play consists of throwing 3 dice and moving around the board, climbing from one level to the next as the respective goals are accomplished, as indicated on each player's "Goal Cards." Having met all 3 goals, player retires dice, and next attempts to solve the "Resolution" stage by use of cards, as directed. When player has achieved his goals and arranged the "Resolution" cards appropriately first, the game is won.

boardgame: Room 25 Double Rush: Cartes Bonus Yan Et François
description:
Promotional card offered with issue 166 of June 2024 of Plato magazine.
Yan (Moussu) is the artist of the game and François (Rouzé) is the designer.
Card that replaces one of the double-sided character cards in the game.
Yan has the 3rd eye power, he can secretly consult his next three rooms without changing their order and François has the power of the 6th sense, he can consult the 2 rooms discarded at the start of the round at any time.

An area control print and play game with a Greek mythology theme, published as part of Ludo Brasil Magazine number 36. Two to four players take their armies to action through dice combinations, and special cards can be used to interfere in the game. The 9 cards available represent a non-canonical version of the Dodekatheon gods from mythology, and two of them (Hades and Medusa) affect all players, not just the rivals. The board represents Mount Olympus, and not all of its terrain can be crossed. Conquering and keeping areas of the board grant players Victory Points. The first player to reach 20 VPs is the winner.

Splitters! is a card driven game about the left wing political movement of the 70s. The players take on the rolls of ideologists struggling to have the movement embrace their separate interpretations of socialism. The game either ends when a player successfully achieves three secret goals or when the last card is drawn from the deck, in which case the player who controls the most party members wins.

Order from Moscow adds several elements, such as character powers and the titular orders from Moscow to add more chaos to the game

Several times a year, when we feel like it, the Golden Journal brings Gold Club members some fun extras for their Avalanche Press games. Our new-model Golden Journal features a “real” booklet, just like a small magazine, and a small set of die-cut, silky-smooth playing pieces.

Like all other major powers, the Red Army studied and rejected many proposed tank designs during the course of World War II. Some of these were intriguing vehicles that likely would have made an impact on the battlefield, but the Soviet leadership did not make the German error of frittering away their productive capacity on an array of different vehicle types. Instead, they built just a few types of tanks, and built hem in huge quantities.

Stalin’s Tanks looks at four of these proposals: the T34/57, maximized to fight enemy tanks. The T34/95, a support tank made necessary by the T34/57’s relatively lesser performance against soft targets. The massive KV5 “Tiger Hunter,” armed with a high-velocity 107mm gun. And the KV220, an early attempt to fit an 85mm gun in the KV heavy tank in place of its standard 76.2mm weapon.

You get 24 new die-cut and silky smooth pieces and new scenarios for Panzer Grenadier: Kursk Burning Tigers so you can play with these tanks in history’s greatest tank battle.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements

description: Quiz game with tricky questions and answers. E.g. "Why is the Egyptian pharaoh Tut-ench-Amun so well-known? The guessing person has to decide if it is right or wrong and then you read out the interesting answer. The person who gets the most 'right' wins.

In Forest Floor, you and your friends hike into the woods to soak up the last few weeks of summer. You spot wildlife, hunt for bugs, pick berries, and marvel at the beauty of the ground.

Forest Floor is a roll and write game featuring 16 unique elements. Flies, worms, sprouts, leaves, frogs, bees and more all interact with one another in clever ways once drawn on your sheet.

Each round, players will resolve a round effect that boosts the development of their ecosystems. Perhaps worms will multiply; maybe a bee will start a new flower patch!

Next, each player selects a domino tile featuring two elements and adds it to their own personal forest floor. Placed elements seed further growth and must be carefully arranged to meet unique scoring conditions. Birds eat worms and like to be surrounded with enough sticks to make a nest. Water causes flowers to multiply, and logs to produce mushrooms. Many elements turn into soil when eaten or dampened. Sprouts take advantage of this decay, scoring a point for each adjacent soil!

The player who oversees the most diverse and vibrant section of forest wins!

—description from the designer

FREAKZ! is a rules-light, skirmish wargame set in a bombastic, post-apocalyptic world of mutants and cartoon violence.

The game pits savage mutant gangs against one another as they pillage the ruins of a devastated world in pursuit of hallowed Artefactz. These objects of great atomic power were forged in the flames of nuclear destruction and can bestow mighty mutations on their bearers in an instant. Gain leathery wings and fly above the battlefield, grow giant plates of bone armor, or develop chameleon-like skin and fade from sight — but beware the consequences of this power! Collect too many Artefactz or rip too many from the bodies of your fallen foes, and you may lose control. A mortal can bear only so many mutations before they are twisted beyond humanity, becoming one of the monstrous creatures that stalk the wastes.

FREAKZ! features streamlined gameplay to get you playing quickly. Generate an entire squad in seconds by drawing from a standard deck of playing cards. Move your fighters without measuring. Resolve attacks in a single matched dice roll. Lead your fledgling group of nobodies through a campaign as they evolve into mutant killers and pledge their allegiance to the Atomic Godz!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the very first expansion of Trimod: Trimod SciFi Expansion! In this set of cards you get two brand new adventures with a different theme, science fiction. Following exactly the same game system this is a proof that this game can be adapted and expanded to any other themes.

What Kimchi is set-colletion game with betting
you will win if you gain 8 kind of 9 Kimches but if you gain 4 of kind, you will lost the game
when one player lost the game by colleting 4 of kind, player who got most pointstone will win the game
each turn, there can be 'betting' phase. in betting phase players who didn't see the card can participate in betting

This game is an engaging entertainment in the genre of hidden roles, set in the years leading up to the Iranian Revolution. Inspired by the narratives and events of that era, this game introduces players to the occurrences, incidents, and conditions of the time. دایره شناسایی is a fast-paced and concise game based on the contemporary history of the Islamic Revolution, offering high replay ability.

—description from the designer
publishers: Institute for the Intellectual Development of Children and Young Adults

description:
원두원두(Beani Beani) is deck building game that goal is remove '원두원두' card as many as possible.
game contaians 27 kinds of cards that discribed dessert or drink
all cards (action, equipment, Beani) has number and category.

Death in Venice is a downloadable present-day murder mystery game set aboard a luxurious yacht for 5 to 9 people where the host can play too. Clay McFarland, hotshot movie director, has been killed just after receiving the prestigious Golden Lion award at the Venice Film Festival. But who is the murderer?

Last night at the glamorous Venice Film Festival, controversial director Clay McFarland picked up the prestigious Golden Lion award for this season's hit movie Never Look Back. But this morning, he was found lying in front of St Mark's Cathedral – hacked to death with a meat cleaver.

The stars of the movie and some of their guests supposedly spent the night sleeping off the effects of the post-awards party, on the Casanova, their luxury yacht moored in the marina – but it seems that someone was up to no good. Traces of McFarland's blood found on the Casanova's dinghy mean that police are confident that the culprit is one of those who are now confined aboard the yacht.

As cathedral bells toll out across the ancient city, on board the Casanova a frothy ferment of vineyards, memoirs, gossip, jealousy, and movie-set punch-ups will come to the boil.

Charge your glasses, put on your designer sunglasses, and join the cast and crew of Never Look Back as they try to solve the mystery of Death in Venice!

Death in Venice lasts for about four hours and is suited to an evening. It is often played over a meal – a finger buffet if possible, to allow guests to circulate and talk in private.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Food is to Die For
description:
The Food is to Die For is a downloadable murder mystery game for 12 to 15 guests and a host. Set in an up-market restaurant, guests include restaurateurs, chefs, waiters and celebrities.

At The Food is to Die For, Pierre Baudelaire's restaurant changes names faster than playboys change girlfriends. Nobody cares though, because the man's a genius and makes food that is absolutely to die for. Everyone just called the restaurant "That place at the end of Maple" for years, until Pierre decided the joke was hilarious and changed the restaurant's name, formally, to At the End of Maple.

Whatever the name, this is THE restaurant in town – where anyone who's anyone is seen dining. Celebrities, politicians, philanthropists, and foodies, all wait and wait for the chance to get a table. Tonight is a special night – C.J. Charles, the most discerning and respected restaurant critic in the business, is dining here and will write a review.

Most of the staff came in at 10 this morning to make sure all the preparations were in order and took a break between 2 and 6 pm, in time for the dinner service to start.

When they arrive though, Pierre is sprawled on the floor of his kitchen, in a puddle of beet soup and blood. Pierre was a gifted chef, but he made enemies. Who hated him enough to kill?

The Food is to Die For lasts for about four hours and is suited to an evening. It is often played over a meal – a finger buffet if possible, to allow guests to circulate and talk in private.

—description from the designer

Make your terrarium really sparkle by introducing a centerpiece plant. Be warned: these unusual plants bring equally unusual critters.

Take your terrarium to the next level by including a centerpiece plant, a new hungry critter, or both! Centerpieces sit above your terrarium and must be completed by forming a unique pattern using your other plants, while the new critters have powerful features that limit which plants can be moved or grown.

Centerpieces 6-card expansion introduces 3 new Centerpiece plants as well as 3 new Critter cards. You may use either a Centerpiece or a new Critter separately, or you can add one of each.

To include one of the new Centerpiece plants, simply add it above the center position of the top row. You must grow the Centerpiece before the game ends by arranging plants in the terrarium in a specific pattern. Once the pattern is formed, the Centerpiece is grown by flipping it over and resolving its effect, after which it is considered completed.

When using a new Critter card, it replaces any other Critter card and acts using the same core rules for movement. Each of the new Critters has its own method of dealing damage, as well as a constant effect that restricts your actions regarding certain plants.

Here is the 3rd expansion of the Saint Seiya Deckbuilding game

Adds new material and new mechanics:

     A new Playmat with a new river of cards (the specters)
     The new Specter cards can be sent to opponents to add penalties to them.
     A new board for each player with the arrival of the "Senses" to offer new effects that speed up the game.
     Lots of new cards (Heroes, Armor, Epics, etc.) as well as new Ability cards offering new effects.
     A new "Duel" game mode allowing 2 players to compete as the Golden Knights.


—description from the designer

The starter set for the Data Theft game series. Two exciting types of attack collide. Will your nimble botnets saturate the firewall and reduce the enemy's data? Or will the classic spam message - I LOVE YOU - ultimately help you to victory? More topical than ever, exciting at every turn, and incorrigibly human. A living card game experience for two - without any previous technical knowledge - to experience the complexity of cyber threats.

—description from the designer

Duel of Chaos is the decisive test that will allow Chaos to choose who will accompany him in the battle against his brother. Faced with your opponent, exploit the diversity of characters by orchestrating their different effects.
The objective of this deck building: empty your draw pile in a single turn before your opponent.

Cosmic Calamity and Tides of Triumph, expansions from the Kickstarter now in a standalone box. Each add 18 cards to the base game and allow players to dive deeper into the beautiful Abyssi Cluster and the events that have riddled its history.

Use your Nature Elements to beat your opponents in this easy, fast paced card game.

Nature Elements are related to each other and there is a balance on powers of Nature.

Water extinguishes Fire. Fire burns Wood. Tree roots gets deep into Earth cracking Rocks.

Every player gets Nature Elements cards. The first player to get rid of all the cards wins the game.

—description from the designer

Carrier is a social deduction game set in a post-apocalyptic world where players take on hidden roles as either Survivors or Carriers. Survivors aim to identify and protect the Curefinder, the last hope for humanity, while planning their escape. Meanwhile, the Carriers, infected but still intelligent, work to sabotage the Survivors’ efforts and eliminate the Curefinder.

Gameplay revolves around encounters that require teamwork, trust, and deception, as players try to gather resources, complete missions, and outwit opponents without revealing their true roles. With easy-to-learn rules and strategic depth, Carrier provides an intense and immersive experience for both new and seasoned players.

Will you work together to escape, or will the Carriers' hidden agenda spell the end of humanity?

—description from the designer

Road to Academia is a Euro-style game in which you’ll manage a research team in a laboratory, aiming to become the most renowned professor in the scientific community.

During the game, you will be able to:


     Secure grants
     Equip your lab
     Hire staff: researchers, PhD students, and postdocs
     Participate in congresses and conferences
     Conduct research
     Analyze your results
     Write papers
     …and publish your work before others do!


But be careful not to burn out your team members!

A spine-chilling scary expansion of Reckless Sloths (2020). Includes 15 new cards.

—description from the publisher

A sweet and sugary candy expansion of Reckless Sloths. Includes 15 new cards.

—description from the publisher

Standalone sequel to Reckless Sloths (2020).

How To Play: To start each player starts with 5 action cards in their hand.

Then on your turn take the following steps:

     Start your turn by drawing a card from the deck.
     Play as many power cards as you like.
     End your turn by saving a sloth.


Unless!!! Someone plays a Too Slow! card.

New Fake Sloths! Fake sloths don’t count as sloths, but give you special powers!

Plus Madness Unleashed! When you see this card, the danger zone gets shaken up and every player can steal a card from an opponent.

At The End: First player to have 5 SLOTHS in their sanctuary wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Reckless Sloths: Madness Unleashed – Pirate Sloths
description:
Set sail on a new adventure with the Pirate Expansion! 15 new cards filled with booty, treachery, mutinous crews, and cursed treasures await!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Reckless Sloths: Madness Unleashed – Adorable Sloths
description:
Get ready for cuteness overload with the Adorable Sloths expansion! This delightful expansion adds 15 new cards filled with rainbows and cute things to your game.

—description from the publisher

Flash God is a 300-card game that can be played with your smartphone. Combine humour and knowledge with this quiz game that does not take itself seriously.

To activate it, you need a smartphone, a connection, but above all friends!

300 cards questions about things you like:
series, sports, beautiful and useless people, ass, but mostly things that will make you laugh!

Quality indices, maps evolving in time original answers and sometimes questionable humor... 300 playing cards including 100 upgradeable!

Keep your eyes peeled in this fantastic game of fast reactions. Simply turn over the top card and look at the back of the next card to see what you’re looking for. The fastest to shout out the correct answer wins! With 32 objects each in 4 colors and thousands of card combos, this game will always keep you guessing!

Can you spot the biggest thing in the real world on the card below?

FAST-PACED GAMEPLAY: Engage in a thrilling game of quick reactions, where players flip cards and race to shout out the correct answer, making each round exciting and unpredictable.
FAMILY FRIENDLY: Designed for 2-6 players and suitable for children aged 5 and up, making it an ideal choice for family game nights and gatherings.
EDUCATIONAL BENEFITS: Enhances observation skills and fast thinking, providing a fun learning experience while promoting quick decision-making.
REPLAYABILITY: With 6 different rules that can change with each card played, Big Fish Little Fish offers endless variability, ensuring no two games are the same.
RICH VISUAL DESIGN: Features 52 picture cards with 130 vibrant illustrations of objects in various sizes and colors, ensuring a visually engaging experience for all ages.

—description from the publisher

Spot the match first! With 4 mini-games designed specifically for this Giant version, play Spot it! like you’ve never played it before!? Challenge your family and friends to play everywhere, who has the best reactivity and speed

—description from the publisher

In 2012, a provocative card game centered around prostitution emerged, shaking institutions to their core and triggering four separate parliamentary inquiries. The game sparked such an intense public reaction that it seemingly generated a wormhole (or perhaps a "butthole") linking Ancient Greece, the Middle Ages, and the Future, culminating in a time-traveling gathering of sex workers from various eras. This is the backdrop of Squillo Deluxe - Time Travel Edition, an extreme and daring card game. Players step into the roles of madams or pimps, managing a team of sex workers with diverse backgrounds, genders, and abilities, each with their unique attributes, fees, and sometimes... organ resale value.

This collection includes 110 of the game’s best cards, carefully selected from expansions such as Satyrs and Bacchantes, Hags and Harlots, and Deep Space 69. Players navigate through unexpected events, health power-ups, and the mighty Oracles to strategize their way to victory. The objective? Outsmarting opponents by skillfully maneuvering your lineup of sex workers to gain dominance over the competition.

Super Mega Ultra Dreidel is a fast-paced card game that puts a new spin on the traditional dreidel game, perfect for family gatherings during Hanukkah. Combining strategy and luck, players spin the dreidel and draw cards that correspond to the dreidel's result, offering a variety of actions to choose from.
 Will you collect gelt?
Buy powerful Miracle Cards?
Or steal from your opponents?

The first player to collect 18 gelt or the last player standing wins.

Each set includes 90 cards (divided into 5 decks — Gimel, Hey, Shin, Nun, Miracle), a 6-sided die, a wooden dreidel and a rule book. Players provide their own delicious gelt (chocolate coins, any small candy or even poker chips).

The game is designed for 2-6 players, ages 7 and up, and typically takes 15-30 minutes to play.

Super Mega Ultra Dreidel was created by 12-year-old Oz, who loves games and Hanukkah.
Players flick discs on a movement target board, then move their stock car according to the result.  First car to reach the finish wins the race.

The Inertia Corp Electronics Lab mini-expansion adds several twists to the game!

Experience a funky and fun scenario where movement plays out quite differently, plus additional cards only available through this expansion: two new settings cards, two moment cards, one advanced equipment, and another advanced hostile - The Bot!

Thirst is a bloodthirsty 2–4 player, competitive vampire-vs-vampire, city dominion game for ages 8+ and can be played in 30-60 minutes.

Thirst is ultimately a game of dominion in which players become powerful monstrous and bloodthirsty vampires. Players rise from the graveyard hungry to find that they are not the only creature of darkness in town. Players fight other vampire players for dominion over people and neighborhoods, vying for influence. Players must hunt wisely, as the first vampire to accrue an influence score of 10 wins, and can finally slake their Thirst!

—description from the publisher

3 Chapters: Flaschengeist Jinny is a mini expansion for 3 Chapters that came for free with every game of 3 Chapters sold at SPIEL 24.

Collect wonder lamps in Chapter 2 to exchange them for wishes from genie Jinny.

BelloLudi Kalashnikov is designed as a multiplayer game but will work just as fine with only two players or even solo. The same goes for the number of miniatures involved. It is possible to start with only around 20 miniatures, but that can easily be expanded. Similarly, the distances in BelloLudi Kalashnikov are given in inches for use with 28mm scale soldiers. Feel free to adapt to centimetres should you not have the space or when you use a smaller scale of miniatures.

This ruleset simulates battles in the period of 1975 to today, the age in which the worlds’ nations fought various conflicts. The Soviet-Afghanistan War, Iran-Iraq War, Falklands War and the Gulf Wars to name but a few. These rules can also be used for ‘what-if’ scenarios in the Cold War and the Russian-Ukrainian War.

Modern combat uses the tactic of ‘Fire and Manoeuvre’ in which the enemy is pinned in place or disorganised by the concentration of fire from the infantry and artillery. Once this is accomplished, other elements of the unit can deliver the coup de grâce. These tactics mean that teamwork and command and control (C2) are essential on the battlefield. Every unit has its own specific task and if they execute their tasks in unison, success is often achieved. The aim in this game, is not to annihilate the enemy’s units to the last man, but to inflict enough damage so that morale is reduced and the unit will become shaken or will run away in panic and might have to be removed from the tabletop. This can be achieved by a clever combination of movement, shooting and dashing charges,. The tactics are based on teamwork within a group, as well as between groups and as such, the whole army.

Your role will be that of a commander of a small group of soldiers. Should any disagreement arise concerning the rules, we suggest you roll a dice. The one who rolls the highest number is right, for now.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Liberation of South Vietnam: War Rules
description:
The Liberation of South Vietnam is a squad-based tabletop wargame rules set that works with any scale of miniatures.

Move your platoon through the jungles of Vietnam as the North Vietnamese Army and Viet Cong close in on you. Each turn, a card is drawn and randomly placed on the table, which closes in on your platoon. Only when the card can be spotted by your men will they know what the threat is, if it is a threat. A pdf to print your own cards comes with the rules.

Every turn brings new dangers with event cards that keep the enemy’s movements unknown until the last moment. With simple yet deep mechanics, you’ll need strategy, quick thinking, and careful resource management to complete your missions and bring your troops home.

This game isn’t just about combat—it’s about survival, strategy, and the ultimate challenge of winning hearts and minds in one of history’s most complex conflicts. Perfect for wargame enthusiasts and history buffs alike, this ruleset is your gateway to an authentic and gripping Vietnam War experience.

The rules’ layout parodies the American military field manuals of the Vietnam era, including handwritten notes and rule clarifications throughout the book.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rifle Squad: The British Home Guard – A Solitaire Wargame
description:
This game lets you play as the British Home Guard in World War 2. The Home Guard was developed to defend the shores of Britain against possible German invasion, which was deemed to be a real possibility, especially early in the war. Many of the Home Guard were men who were too old, and also too young, to sign up for the regular British Army. They often brought their own particular skills into their role, skills that were part of their day to day occupation.

At first, the Home Guard were poorly armed and many soldiers were equipped with tools and other melee type weapons. This game envisages an invasion by German forces a little later in the war, after the Home Guard had been better armed following the purchase of weapons from the United States. The job of the Home Guard would generally have been to delay the invasion until regular forces could arrive, and to protect vital infrastructure.

This game is a hex and counter wargame and its aim is to present a set of clear and easy to read rules, immersive Scenario Maps and clear, useful game counters. Individual Scenarios within a Campaign can be played in around an hour or so, and each one should provide almost endless replayability.

This game is a solitaire wargame. You play the game, and the enemy is controlled by the game system itself. Ideally, you will need 12 standard six-sided dice, although fewer than this will cover most situations. In addition, the number of dice needed can easily be reduced by rolling fewer dice several times as required.

It is highly recommended that the counters provided on the final page of the game are used to play this game. Simply stick them to card and cut them out. A video demonstrating how generally to do this can be found on the Mike Lambo Solitaire Book Games YouTube channel, as can a video demonstrating a full playthrough of the game.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
PIO is a visual agility card game, which consists of collecting bird cards with similar or different characteristics (color, shape, number of feathers and types of eyes) to obtain victory points. Each player will have a card that indicates the possibilities of collecting birds and another card with the characteristics or type of birds that they must collect. The player who earns the most points by collecting birds with the characteristics or missions chosen at random at the beginning of the game wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

PIO es un juego de cartas de agilidad visual, que consiste en recoger cartas de aves con características similares o diferentes, (Color, forma, cantidad de plumas y tipos de ojos) para obtener puntos de victoria. Cada jugador tendrá una carta que le indica las posibilidades de recolección de aves y otra carta con las características o tipo de aves que debe recoger. Gana el jugador que haga más puntos recolectando aves con las características o misiones elegidas al azar al inicio de la partida.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cyber Pet Quest: Plastic Thirster Promo Card
description:
An exclusive double sided promo card for backers of Cyber Pet Quest kickstarter campaign. It was packaged in a small ziplock bag and separate from the base game.

Card Type: Charm
Level 1 - Spend 5 luck and 1 time during this pet's turn you may use one of its Powers one more time.
Level 2 - Spend 4 luck and 1 time during this pet's turn you may use one of its Powers one more time.

More players, more chaos and more fun!

The Chaos expansion brings more characters, Lands, enhanced mechanics, and a multiplayer game mode to the Age of EON base game. You will be able to fight in teams (2 vs 2) during chaotic battles!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Brave + Hollow Promo Cards
description:
This is a set of three gear cards, the Hollowlink Pumpkin, the Bravelink Pumpkin, and the Sword of Doom, as well as the corresponding Seed Pattern cards and Living Glossary card.

These cards were included as promotional items with the Brave + Hollow miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Phoenix Reenactment Promo Card
description:
The Phoenix Reenactment is a new settlement event.

The card was a promotional item included with the Phoenix Hunt Reenactment, which contains a miniature and some other items that are not part of the game.

The settlement celebrates the unlikely victory over the timeless Phoenix. A whirling dance of iridescent feathers sparkles at the settlement's center enshrining the memory of the settlement's perseverance.

KARAHEX is an economic board game dedicated to the culture and history of the Karaim people. The creators of the game are the three brothers Garri, Vladimir and Alexander Babadzhan - active representatives of the Karaim youth, artists and creators of computer games.

The basis of the game is economic development - construction of buildings and connecting cities (symbolizing the connection of Karaim communities around the world). Each city is a settlement where Karaim communities existed or still exist. The buildings symbolize traditional activities that Karaites engaged in, for example: baking kybyns (Karaim dish), growing cucumbers, building a kienesa (house of prayer), midrash (religious schools), trade shops or even tobacco factories!

Each player will manage one of the families and will try to gain the greatest possible respect in their nation, for their family. In the game, you have to balance between the desire to increase income, gain respect or choose profitable trade routes based on the game card and the cards in your hand. In each new game, the locations of the cities change randomly, as well as the cards that allow you to create communities in different cities, making each game unique.

Each player has a tablet with a large number of buildings at their disposal, which gives maximum breadth and variety in creating different strategies. The most important thing to remember is that it is not money, but respect that leads to the size of your family! The game can be played by 2 to 6 players, increasing the size of the map.

—description from the publisher

KARAHEX to ekonomiczna gra planszowa poświęcona kulturze i historii narodu karaimskiego. Twórcami gry są trzej bracia Garri, Vladimir i Alexander Babadżan - aktywni przedstawiciele młodzieży karaimskiej, artyści i twórcy gier komputerowych.
Podstawą gry jest rozwój gospodarczy – budowa budynków i łączenie ze sobą miast (symbolizujące połączenie społeczności karaimskich na całym świecie). Każde miasto jest osadą, w której istniały lub istnieją do dziś społeczności karaimskie. Budynki symbolizują tradycyjne zajęcia, którymi zajmowali się Karaimi, na przykład: pieczenie kybynów (danie karaimskie), uprawa ogórków, budowa kenesy (domu modlitwy), midraszów (szkół religijnych), sklepów handlowych czy nawet fabryk tytoniu!
Każdy gracz będzie zarządzał jedną z rodzin i będzie starał się zdobyć jak największy szacunek w swoim narodzie, dla swojej rodziny. W grze musisz balansować między chęcią zwiększenia dochodów, zdobyciem szacunku lub obraniem zyskownych szlaków handlowych w oparciu o kartę gry oraz karty, które masz w ręce.
W każdej nowej partii lokalizacje miast zmieniają się losowo, a także karty, które pozwalają tworzyć społeczności w różnych miastach, czyni to każdą rozgrywką wyjątkową.
Każdy gracz ma do dyspozycji tablet z dużą liczbą budynków, co daje maksymalną szerokość i różnorodność tworzenia różnych strategii. Najważniejszą rzeczą do zapamiętania jest to, że to nie pieniądze, ale szacunek prowadzi do wielkości Twojej rodziny!
W grze może brać udział od 2 do 6 graczy zwiększający rozmiar mapy.

—description from the publisher (Polish)
publishers: Bitik. Karaimska Oficyna Wydawnicza / Karaim Publishing House

description:
Szacun brings players closer to the events, characters and curiosities of the last hundred years of independent Poland. It is a story about what the social, political and cultural life of citizens looked like and developed from regaining independence in 1918 until the jubilee year of 2018.

You won't read about this in a school textbook!

Each playing card contains unusual questions that will surely arouse your curiosity and little-known facts about culture, geography, sports and politics. And thanks to archival photos, you will feel the atmosphere of events from the last hundred years of Polish history.

Test yourself! Guess the question based on the photo, estimate your and other players' knowledge level and collect tokens. "Respect" is fun, integration and knowledge in one!

The ancient classic point trick taking game with trumps played with a special pack of 63 Sicilian Tarocchi, in a new version optimized for modern and international players, from the extraordinary brush of the cartoonist and illustrator Lelio Bonaccorso. The oldest trick-taking game played in Sicily, today is in the Register of Intangible Heritage - UNESCO of the Sicily Region.

The deck, with Its unique features set it apart from the sophisticated games played elsewhere in Italy and Europe. Starting perhaps as early as the 19th century, the number of its cards was cut down to 63. All suits have 4 court cards: Lady [J], Cavalier [C], Queen [Q] and King [K]. The 21 cards called "Trionfi" or "Tarocchi" have the role of trumps, while "U Fujutu" (i.e. The Runaway), not numbered, represents a jester – or The Fool in the other Tarot decks.

The various games that in some cases are still played today are distinguished by number of players and partnerships between them, the way of dealing the cards, the preliminary phases of setting temporary partnerships, the presence or absence of bidding for additional objectives, the scoring methods, and the aim of the game. The ranking of the cards and the system of card-point values is common to all the games.

This new edition includes for the first time a leaflet with the detailed rules (in Italian) of the version with the richest and most exciting objectives and a QR code directing to a file with rules in English.

—description from the publisher

In this card-driven area-control game you will take command of one of four asymmetric factions vying for dominance in revolutionary France. 

     Accrue political virtues to expand your influence over key locations throughout the city. 
     Call on your faction’s 24 historical figures and use their abilities to consolidate power.
     Deploy your faction’s military strength to wrest control of the streets of Paris.
     Denounce your rivals and dispatch them to the guillotine!


—description from the publisher

Various sea creatures and the water whirlpool are hidden around, waiting to be uncovered. By skillfully remembering the creatures’ locations, you can complete tasks, collect shell points, and win the round. But be careful! At any moment, the whirlpool could stir everything up.

The purpose of the game is to advance your playing pieces along the path in order to reach the "finish line", or home stretch, as quickly as possible with the roll of the dice, while at the same time trying to prevent your opponent's piece from advancing by landing on their fields as often as possible and sending them back to the starting block.

family: Game: Birds of Prey (Ad Astra Games)

description: Pack of 9 extra cards, also featured inside BANG! Dynamite Box: Claus “the Saint,” Uncle Will, Johnny Kisch, Handcuffs, New Identity, Annie Versary, Emiliano and two blank cards, to customize a Character and an Event.

boardgame: Depth Charge: A Micgame of Submarine Warfare on the Open Ocean
description: A quick 2-player print-n-play micro-game that simulates the duel of a modern submarine vs a modern surface ship. Includes three scenarios and optional rules. The goal is to eliminate ones opponent by playing tactic cards while maneuvering on a printed game boards that represent the surface and underwater.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Promo expansion. Contains 4 extra familiars: Callo, Night, Lupo, Farkas

The Reality is Murder is a murder mystery game for 23 to 30 guests and one or two hosts. It is set on a tropical island used for a reality tv show, and characters include crew, contestants and locals.

It is 2003 and Tribal is Network 123's reality show where 12 people live on Moko Shanti, a beautiful tropical island for up to 40 days. Every few days, contestants vote each other off the island during Tribal Meetings. Now, only three contestants are left, and tonight is Tribal's finale.

Tonight, everyone has returned to the beach, where the final Tribal Meeting will be broadcast live, and the winner of the $1,000,000 prize will be decided.

Unfortunately, all is not well. Jeff Thompson, Tribal's creator and director, has been found dead on the beach – a machete embedded in his chest… Find out who killed Jeff Thompson in The Reality is Murder.

The Reality is Murder lasts for about four hours and is suited to an evening. It is often played over a meal – a finger buffet if possible, to allow guests to circulate and talk in private.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Barbascura X: Evolversi Male – Espansione: Tasso del Male e Granchio del Cocco
description:
A new expansion for Barbascura X: Evolversi male. Two new animals to get evolved bad.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Una nuova espansione per il gioco di Barbascura X: Evolversi male. Due nuovi animali da fare evolvere male. Necessita del gioco base Evolversi male.

—description from the publisher

A new game from Immanuel Casto genial mind. Try to think like the others, but be careful not to be too original, if nobody thinks like you, you get no point.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un nuovo gioco dalla geniale mente di Immanuel Casto. Cerca di pensare come pensano gli altri, ma attenzione a non essere troppo originale, se nessuno la pensa come te non accumulerai alcun punto.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Barons of the Backwaters (Fan expansion to 1846: The Race for the Midwest)
description: This expansion, 1846BB, is intended to make for a longer and deeper game of 1846. This game can accommodate up to six players, has a larger bank, a longer stock market, a more robust map, S/N bonuses as well as E/W, some changes to existing private companies, some new trains, many new private companies and a new public corporation!

Not again! You’ll need to pull out all the stops if you want to avoid getting caught this time.

Vexing Letters adds 6 new cards featuring lesser-used letters. It also adds the Double Ransom variant which features two central words rather than one.

The 6 new letters are shuffled in alongside the base game to up the difficulty.

The new Double Ransom variant introduces a second row. Players may add to either row on their turn. However, if challenged, a player must provide a valid word for each row.

Guess when it happened! 500 surprising facts mixed with witty jokes from medieval feminists!
In It’s About Time, players work together or against each other to answer trivia questions about historical women and their achievements across various fields, from science to politics and the arts.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A Collection of Solo Challenges created by Michelle Skevington-Carter for Death Valley.

Enhance your game sessions with 18 solo challenges.

If you enjoy spicing up an old favorite in your solo collection, you won’t want to miss this batch of solo challenges for Death Valley. In order to win, you must meet the condition on here as well as the game’s win condition. SOme might be a little easier, such as having the same number of cards in your Journey and your Scrapbook, while others might take a lot of tries - I’m looking at you, Closely Matched. Who wants to try to win by only 1 point and repeatedly fail? Well, now you can with that challenge in play!

These originated as a weekly challenge at the Button Shy discord and after lots of requests, we’ve made them something you can carry with you and play at any time. Each will twist your game of Death Valley in a fun new way.

A Collection of Solo Challenges created by Michelle Skevington-Carter for SpaceShipped.

Enhance your game sessions with 18 solo challenges.

This is a brilliant idea, of course, and I’d be quite happy to see these sorts of things appear for ALL of the soloable Button Shy titles out there. Why not make it so you MUST buy as many Asteroids as you can afford/hold when they cost 1M? Or to take damage when the enemy collects a Xeno Crystal? This one is less about scoring conditions and more about gameplay interactions, providing even more reasons you’ll lose during your next round of SpaceShipped.

These originated as a weekly challenge at the Button Shy discord and after lots of requests, we’ve made them something you can carry with you and play at any time. Each will twist your game of SpaceShipped in a fun new way.

There’s always more work to be done on the farm. As your farm grows, make sure the most important projects are getting done.

Harvest is a 6-card expansion that introduces a variety of seasonal projects to manage. Follow each project’s specific placement rules to meet their conditions and gain the benefit of their featured blocks. Place them all successfully while still achieving your other goals to win!

Shuffle the 6 new projects and deal 3, 4, or 5 (based on your chosen difficulty) to fulfill. Each project card features 2 traditional blocks as well as a panel that indicates their placement criteria. Once per turn, a project can be placed according to standard placement rules and following any listed restrictions. The panel must remain visible throughout play and cannot be covered, though otherwise their blocks function as normal. These projects do not change scoring, but all starting projects must be placed by the end of the game to avoid a loss.

As the salmon migrate upstream, hungry bears gather and take advantage of their abundance to feed themselves.

Elevation is a 6-card expansion that adds new blocks that feature bears and salmon. Using the new placement rule, in which higher-altitude blocks must be supported by lower-altitude ones, line up the cascading rivers to ensure that no bear goes hungry.

Set up as normal, then shuffle all 6 Elevation cards into the deck.

The first card placed establishes the lowest altitude on the mountain: all subsequent cards must be placed either on the same row as the starting card OR on a higher row provided they are supported by blocks beneath them. When an expansion card is drawn, it must be the next card that you play. It may be played on its bear side or its salmon side. These blocks may not be covered later.

At the end of the game, score 2 points for each bear which is at a higher altitude than a salmon on the same river and lose 2 points for each unfed bear. Salmon do not score by themselves. These points are in addition to normal scoring.

The world was simple, man and woman measured against each other on their prowess of muscle and mind. But from the southern reaches of Rathe, dark arts and aether manipulation have again found practitioners of the forbidden warfare.

As the fight takes new shape, heroes must adapt or die. Sword and shield will not be enough to survive the Arcane Rising!

Arcane Rising is the second Flesh and Blood booster set, introducing the highly anticipated caster classes to the game, and with them, arcane damage!

An ancient pirate left behind their secret riches. Now they’re yours to keep, provided you can find a good use for them.

Buried Treasure 6-card expansion adds to your beach adventure with doubloons and treasure chests. Doubloons are a wild currency that can be used for any craft, while chests increase your hand size. Create all-new objects to celebrate your unexpected bounty!

Shuffle the 6 new cards in and play as normal. Each card features a new object to craft as well as one of the new icons (doubloons or chests).

Doubloons are wild icons that can be used in place of any other resource and also boost the value of items crafted with them by 3 points, while cards with chest icons do not count against your hand size.

boardgame: Flesh and Blood: Crucible of War
description:
Equip Your Hero, Prepare to Fight!

Throw open the armories and turn up the forge, and rally your allies from near and far. Bring all your skill, luck, and your damn best gear to bear; as you’re plunged headfirst into the Crucible of War.

Crucible of War is a supplementary booster set that introduces powerful weapons and equipment, and opens up new strategies and deck building options for all your favourite classes.

New equipment, weapons, and allegiances will be forged in the Crucible of War!

Darkness, the canvas of the universe. Light, the brushstroke of creation. A forbidden power hungers to eclipse existence, wiping the canvas clean. The soul of Rathe stands resolute, a ray of hope to the artisans of civilization.

Monarch is a standalone booster set introducing four new heroes with all new mechanics, to play out the classic conflict of light vs shadow across sealed deck, booster draft, and constructed formats.

Step right up, step right up! The greatest show in Rathe is coming to a town near you! Everfest is a supplementary booster set, designed to expand constructed with new cards for every hero in the game.

Within the bustling grounds of the Everfest carnival, all fashion of performers, peddlers, and frolicking fun is to be found. Will you find fun, fame, or good fortune in the carnival slot of your Everfest booster pack... or perhaps take center stage and become the star of the show?

Uprising is a standalone booster set that plunges fans into a brutal civil war sweeping across the harsh and unforgiving land of Volcor.

Dromai, an Imperial Illusionist able to conjure the legendary dragons of Volcor out of aether and ash faces off against Fai, a hot-headed Ninja who spearheads the Uprising against the draconian oppression of the Imperials, while Iyslander journeys from her Icy homestead in search of answers to painful questions seared deep within her mind.

Uprising is designed for exceptional booster draft, sealed deck, and constructed play, and includes incredible “Marvels” that will leave players and collectors in awe!

Venture into the vaults of Rathe and discover the origins of Flesh and Blood with History Pack 1 Black Label! Translated into French, German, Italian and Spanish for the first time, History Pack 1 Black Label celebrates the European Flesh and Blood community by re-introducing the world of Rathe in your native language. This is an ideal product for the next generation of heroes, looking to grasp the history of Rathe witnessed by those who went before.

Dynasty is an expansion booster set that takes you inside the Imperial Palace of Volcor. Play as the Emperor and defend your Dynasty against the rebellious uprising sweeping the land.

Dynasty is designed to expand constructed play with new support for all existing classes, the introduction of a brand new class, and Relics of War, a collection of Marvel weapons and equipment to proudly display in your own trophy room.

Outsiders is a standalone booster set that takes you down into the Pits, a sprawling web of underground caverns and canals, home to all manner of backstabbing and skulduggery.

Haunted by a mysterious past, the crafty Assassin Uzuri leads a band of misfits, including a frightening new version of the king-slaying Assassin Arakni, and the merciless Riptide, a trap-based Ranger.

Fan favourite Ranger Azalea returns to take out the competition, while a pair of wandering Ninjas, Katsu and Benji, embark on a dangerous mission in the underworld.

The first draftable set to feature multiple heroes of the same class, Outsiders is designed for dynamic booster draft, sealed deck, and constructed play, and includes legendary new "hybrid" cards that can be used by multiple classes!
New cards, the same black humor as always. 50 new cards to add to the H.D.P.: Hasta Donde Puedas collection you have at home.

Players choose which faction to play (brontosaurus, triceratops, pterodactyl, megalosaurus) and draw the top card from the Challenge deck to determine the winning conditions, such as three in a row, connect five, etc. Players take turns playing a card to the center of the table, trying to line up their dinosaurs, while also trying to block opponents by playing special action cards to move a card to another space or remove it from the grid. The first person to win three challenges wins.

Palabretto is a word-building game which puts your vocabulary and shrewdness to the test. Conceal your intentions, bide your time and stifle your adversaries` plans to become the highest scorer.

Palabretto is a word creation game whose objective is to score the most points after 4 rounds.
Players can participate in the creation of the same word, but beware! It is not a cooperative game, as the player who contributes the most letters to the word or completes it will get the best score.

Designed to play in Spanish, French and English languages.

—description from the designer
